Depreciation and amortization charges for the year amounted to $22.4 million, inclusive of intangible asset amortization from the 2021 acquisition of NovaDx Pathology.
Adjusted EBITDA, as defined by the company, was $132.9 million for fiscal year 2023, compared with $107.6 million in fiscal year 2022.
The adjusted EBITDA margin expanded to 27.3%, reflecting the combination of revenue growth, gross margin improvement, and operating leverage.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $89.4 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $127.0 million less capital expenditures of $37.6 million.
Management is guiding to net revenues in the range of $535 million to $550 million for fiscal year 2024, implying growth of approximately 9.8% to 12.9%.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 is set at 19.5% to 20.5%, reflecting expected leverage on the commercial infrastructure built during 2023.
The company expects to incur approximately $42 million in capital expenditures during fiscal year 2024, primarily to support a new manufacturing wing in Durham.
Mr. Tsuji noted that the company's strong liquidity position provides ample flexibility to fund both organic growth initiatives and potential bolt-on acquisitions.
The Board has authorized an additional $50 million share repurchase program effective January 1, 2024, supplementing the capacity remaining from the prior authorization.
Veltran's largest customer, a national laboratory network, accounted for approximately 12.4% of total revenues in fiscal year 2023.
No other single customer represented more than 10% of consolidated revenues during the reporting period.
The company's international operations are subject to foreign currency risk, with approximately 40% of international revenues denominated in euros.
Veltran employs a natural hedging strategy and selectively uses forward contracts to mitigate material currency exposures on a rolling twelve-month basis.
Audit Committee Chair Helena Brandt confirmed that the company's independent auditor, Whitmore & Creel LLP, issued an unqualified opinion on the fiscal year 2023 financial statements.
The audit opinion also included an attestation on the effectiveness of the company's internal controls over financial reporting under Section 404(b) of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act.
No material weaknesses in internal controls were identified during the audit process, and all previously disclosed control deficiencies were remediated as of year-end.
Veltran Biosystems made no significant acquisitions during fiscal year 2023, although management conducted due diligence on three separate target companies.
The company's environmental, social, and governance report, published concurrently with this annual report, details a commitment to achieving carbon neutrality in operations by 2030.
Veltran reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 8.3% in fiscal year 2023, relative to the 2021 baseline.
The company donated $2.1 million to STEM education initiatives and community health programs in the communities where it operates during 2023.
Looking ahead, management remains confident in the company's competitive positioning within the molecular diagnostics sector and the long-term demand drivers underpinning the business.
Dr. Okafor concluded by expressing gratitude to the company's employees, partners, and shareholders for their continued support throughout a productive fiscal year.
This excerpt is drawn from Veltran Biosystems' Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2024.
All financial figures presented are in U.S. dollars unless otherwise indicated, and prior period amounts have been reclassified to conform with current year presentation.
Velarix Therapeutics, Inc. released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, marking a significant milestone in the company's growth trajectory.
Founded in 2014 and headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, Velarix Therapeutics operates in the specialty biopharmaceuticals sector, focused on rare metabolic disorders.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Margot Elbrin opened the report with a letter to shareholders highlighting operational achievements and strategic priorities for the year ahead.
Velarix Therapeutics closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, maintaining a pristine balance sheet with no outstanding borrowings.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing an increase of 18.4 percent compared to the prior fiscal year.
The company's flagship product, Velacept, generated $312.6 million in net sales, accounting for approximately 64 percent of total revenue.
Operating expenses rose to $391.2 million, driven primarily by a significant increase in research and development investment.
Research and development expenditures totaled $178.4 million, reflecting the company's commitment to expanding its pipeline of rare disease therapies.
Chief Financial Officer Dominic Harwell noted that the financial position of Velarix remained robust throughout the fiscal year and into the first quarter of 2024.
To fund the accelerated expansion of its manufacturing facilities in Durham, the company entered into a $320 million senior secured term loan agreement with a consortium of lenders in September 2023.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 was $296.1 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 60.8 percent.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses amounted to $112.8 million, representing roughly 23.2 percent of total revenue.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $61.4 million, compared to $38.9 million in fiscal year 2022.
Earnings per diluted share came in at $1.42, up from $0.91 per diluted share recorded in the prior year.
The Board of Directors, chaired by Henrietta Voss, approved a share repurchase program authorizing the buyback of up to $75 million of common stock over the next 24 months.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $204.7 million, compared to $161.3 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company's current ratio improved to 3.1, reflecting a strengthened short-term liquidity position relative to prior periods.
Inventories increased to $88.5 million, primarily due to a planned build-up of Velacept finished goods in anticipation of expanded market access.
Accounts receivable, net, totaled $97.2 million at December 31, 2023, consistent with the growth in product sales during the fourth quarter.
Capital expenditures for the fiscal year were $143.6 million, largely attributable to the ongoing construction of the Durham manufacturing campus.
Depreciation and amortization expense was $34.9 million, reflecting the growing asset base associated with the company's infrastructure investments.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3 percent, slightly lower than the 22.8 percent effective rate recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Deferred tax assets totaled $29.6 million, primarily related to net operating loss carryforwards from earlier development-stage periods.
Velarix Therapeutics employed 1,847 full-time equivalent personnel as of December 31, 2023, an increase of 312 employees compared to the prior year-end.
The company added 214 employees in its clinical and regulatory affairs divisions as part of the strategic build-out of late-stage development capabilities.
Headcount in the commercial organization grew by 98 individuals, supporting the expanded Velacept promotional efforts in newly accessed markets.
International revenue contributed $89.4 million to total sales, representing a 27.1 percent year-over-year increase driven by European market penetration.
The company received European Medicines Agency approval for Velacept in the treatment of Tyrosine Metabolism Disorder Type II in March 2023.
This approval opened access to eight additional European Union member state markets and contributed meaningfully to second-half revenue performance.
Dr. Elbrin highlighted the signing of a distribution partnership with Nordenfeld Pharma AG, a Swiss specialty distributor, as a key enabler of European commercialization.
The partnership agreement grants Nordenfeld exclusive distribution rights in Germany, Austria, and Switzerland through December 2028.
Domestic revenue, representing sales within the United States, totaled $397.9 million, constituting approximately 81.7 percent of consolidated net revenues.
Medicaid and Medicare reimbursement accounted for 44 percent of domestic net product sales, reflecting the rare disease patient population served by Velacept.
The company's patient assistance program supported 1,243 eligible patients during fiscal year 2023, with $18.7 million in product provided at no charge.
Operating cash flow for the year was $122.8 million, a material improvement from $74.5 million generated in fiscal year 2022.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was negative $20.8 million, reflecting the heavy infrastructure investment cycle.
Despite negative free cash flow, management expressed confidence in the long-term return profile of the Durham manufacturing facility investment.
The Durham facility is expected to be fully operational by the third quarter of 2025, adding approximately 60 percent incremental manufacturing capacity.
Velarix's pipeline includes three additional drug candidates: VLX-204, VLX-317, and VLX-501, each targeting distinct rare metabolic pathways.
VLX-204 successfully completed Phase 2 clinical trials in November 2023, with topline data demonstrating statistically significant improvement in primary endpoints.
VLX-317 is currently enrolling patients in a Phase 1/2 dose-escalation study expected to yield initial safety data by mid-2024.
VLX-501 remains in preclinical development, with an Investigational New Drug application anticipated for submission in the fourth quarter of 2024.
The company's collaboration agreement with the Broadfield Institute of Metabolic Research continued to generate valuable translational science supporting the pipeline.
Under the Broadfield agreement, Velarix made milestone payments totaling $9.2 million during fiscal year 2023.
Total contractual obligations as of December 31, 2023 amounted to $614.8 million, inclusive of operating leases, purchase commitments, and licensing obligations.
Operating lease right-of-use assets stood at $47.3 million, associated primarily with laboratory, office, and distribution center facilities.
The weighted average remaining lease term for operating leases was 6.4 years as of December 31, 2023.
Velarix's stock closed fiscal year 2023 at $38.72 per share, representing a 34.6 percent appreciation from the January 2, 2023 opening price of $28.76.
Market capitalization at year-end was approximately $1.67 billion based on 43.1 million diluted shares outstanding.
The company does not currently pay a cash dividend, with retained earnings reinvested to support ongoing research, development, and commercial activities.
Audit Committee Chair Lawrence Pendleton confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP issued an unqualified audit opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements.
No material weaknesses in internal control over financial reporting were identified during the annual assessment conducted in accordance with Sarbanes-Oxley Section 404.
The company settled a legacy patent infringement claim with GenPath Biosciences for $4.8 million in January 2023, resolving the dispute without admission of liability.
No other material litigation matters were pending as of the filing date of this annual report.
Subsequent to year-end, Velarix Therapeutics announced an At-the-Market equity offering program allowing the issuance of up to $100 million in common shares.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $545 million to $565 million, implying growth of approximately 12 to 16 percent over 2023.
Operating margin for 2024 is expected to improve modestly as the commercial infrastructure investments made in 2023 begin to scale.
The company reaffirmed its long-term financial target of achieving a 25 percent operating margin by fiscal year 2027.
Dr. Elbrin concluded her letter to shareholders by expressing gratitude to the Velarix team, partners, and patient communities for their commitment throughout the year.
Dominic Harwell echoed those sentiments and noted that disciplined capital allocation would remain a cornerstone of the company's financial strategy going forward.
Velarix Therapeutics remains committed to delivering meaningful therapies to underserved patient populations while generating sustainable value for its shareholders.

Veltora Systems Corporation, a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure provider headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, led by Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway, reported consolidated revenues of $1.47 billion, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Tse noted that operational efficiency improvements contributed significantly to the strong margin performance observed throughout the year.
Veltora's cloud subscription segment emerged as the primary growth driver, posting segment revenues of $892 million, up 21% year-over-year.
The professional services division generated $578 million in revenue, growing at a more modest pace of 5.8% compared to fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit for the full year reached $734 million, translating to a gross margin of approximately 49.9%, an improvement of 180 basis points from the prior year.
Veltora Systems carried no long-term debt obligations whatsoever as of December 31, 2023, having fully retired all outstanding borrowings earlier in the fiscal year.
The company invested heavily in research and development, allocating $218 million, or approximately 14.8% of total revenues, to innovation initiatives.
Headcount across all global offices stood at 8,340 full-time employees at year-end, reflecting a net increase of 620 employees over the course of 2023.
Margaret Holloway emphasized in her letter to shareholders that disciplined capital allocation remained a cornerstone of Veltora's long-term strategic framework.
Operating income rose to $241 million, a 19.3% improvement year-over-year, driven by revenue growth and favorable shifts in the product mix toward higher-margin cloud services.
During the second quarter of 2023, Veltora secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of five institutional lenders to support its balance sheet flexibility.
Net income attributable to common shareholders for fiscal year 2023 amounted to $187 million, compared to $152 million in the prior year, representing a 23% increase.
Diluted earnings per share came in at $2.84, compared to $2.31 in fiscal 2022, reflecting both earnings growth and the effect of ongoing share repurchase activity.
The company repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares during the year at an average price of $61.40, returning roughly $73.7 million of capital to shareholders through buybacks.
Veltora also declared quarterly dividends totaling $0.48 per share for the full year, consistent with its established dividend policy introduced in fiscal 2021.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $412 million, a slight decrease from the $438 million reported at the end of fiscal 2022.
Free cash flow generation for the year was robust at $298 million, representing a free cash flow margin of 20.3% on total revenues.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 were $94 million, primarily directed toward data center upgrades, network infrastructure, and office facility enhancements.
The company's international segment, which spans operations across Europe, Asia-Pacific, and Latin America, contributed 31% of total revenue, or approximately $455 million.
European revenues grew 17% in constant-currency terms, though reported growth was dampened to 11% due to unfavorable movements in the euro and British pound.
Asia-Pacific remained Veltora's fastest-growing international region, delivering 29% year-over-year revenue growth and crossing $200 million in annual segment revenue for the first time.
Raymond Tse stated that the company's adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $319 million, reflecting an adjusted EBITDA margin of 21.7%.
This margin represents a 90-basis-point improvement relative to fiscal 2022, when adjusted EBITDA margin stood at 20.8%.
Veltora launched three major product updates in 2023, including the VeltOS 9.0 platform release in March, an AI-enhanced analytics module in July, and an expanded developer API toolkit in October.
The VeltOS 9.0 release was widely adopted by enterprise clients, with 74% of Veltora's existing customer base migrating to the new version within six months of launch.
Customer retention rates remained exceptionally high at 93.4%, reflecting the stickiness of Veltora's integrated software ecosystem and strong customer success programs.
The company added 340 net new enterprise accounts during the year, bringing its total active enterprise customer count to 4,210 as of December 31, 2023.
Annual recurring revenue, a key performance indicator for the cloud segment, grew to $1.02 billion, crossing the billion-dollar threshold for the first time in company history.
The weighted average contract duration for new enterprise agreements signed in fiscal 2023 was 2.7 years, slightly above the historical average of 2.4 years.
Veltora's partner ecosystem expanded meaningfully, with 87 new reseller and systems integrator partnerships formalized during the year, bringing the total to 412 active partners globally.
The company maintained a strong credit profile, earning an investment-grade rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's following its annual credit review conducted in November 2023.
Chief Revenue Officer Priya Sundaram credited the strength of the direct sales team and improved pipeline conversion rates for much of the revenue outperformance in fiscal 2023.
Pipeline conversion improved from 22.1% in fiscal 2022 to 26.8% in fiscal 2023, reflecting investments in sales enablement tools and revised compensation incentive structures.
Veltora's board of directors approved a new $150 million share repurchase authorization in October 2023, replacing the previous program that was nearly exhausted.
The company recognized $48 million in restructuring charges during the first quarter of 2023, primarily related to the consolidation of legacy on-premise product lines.
These restructuring efforts are expected to generate annualized cost savings of approximately $65 million beginning in fiscal 2024, according to management guidance.
Veltora's tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.4%, slightly above the 20.9% effective rate recorded in the prior year due to changes in geographic earnings mix.
The company's deferred revenue balance at year-end reached $563 million, representing a 16% increase from the $485 million reported at the end of fiscal 2022.
This deferred revenue growth signals strong future revenue visibility and underpins management's confident fiscal 2024 guidance.
For fiscal 2024, management guided to total revenues in the range of $1.65 billion to $1.68 billion, implying year-over-year growth of approximately 12% to 14%.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is expected to be between $355 million and $375 million, reflecting continued margin expansion as the business scales.
The company anticipates capital expenditures of approximately $100 million to $110 million in fiscal 2024, modestly higher than fiscal 2023 levels.
Veltora's stock price appreciated approximately 38% during fiscal 2023, significantly outperforming the broader technology sector index, which returned 22% over the same period.
Total shareholder return, inclusive of dividends reinvested, was approximately 39.4% for fiscal year 2023.
The audit committee of Veltora's board confirmed that Ernst & Young LLP completed the independent audit of the company's financial statements without qualification.
The company disclosed no material weaknesses in internal control over financial reporting, consistent with the findings of the prior two fiscal years.
Margaret Holloway highlighted that Veltora's environmental, social, and governance commitments advanced meaningfully in 2023, including a 12% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions.
The company also achieved gender parity in director-level and above roles, with women comprising 51% of leadership positions as of year-end 2023.
Veltora increased its charitable giving and community investment programs by 22% in fiscal 2023, contributing $14.2 million to educational and workforce development initiatives globally.
Looking ahead, Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway expressed confidence that Veltora's platform investments and expanding ecosystem will sustain compounding growth over the medium term.
She noted that the transition from perpetual license to subscription-based revenue models is now substantially complete, positioning the company well for durable, predictable revenue streams.
Raymond Tse closed his commentary by reaffirming that management's capital allocation priorities remain organic investment first, followed by selective acquisitions and shareholder returns.
The full financial statements, including the consolidated balance sheet, income statement, cash flow statement, and notes thereto, are included in the subsequent sections of this annual report.
Meridian Agro-Tech Corporation released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Des Moines, Iowa, operates primarily in the precision agriculture and crop science sector.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Veltri opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's commitment to sustainable growth.
Meridian Agro-Tech serves over 4,200 commercial farming clients across seventeen states in the continental United States.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing an 11.3% increase over the prior year.
The company's flagship product line, CropSense Pro, accounted for approximately 38% of total revenue.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Okolo noted that operating margins improved by 2.1 percentage points compared to fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit for the year was reported at $612 million, up from $541 million in the previous fiscal period.
Research and development expenditures totaled $187 million, reflecting the company's continued investment in agronomic innovation.
Meridian Agro-Tech expanded its workforce to 9,340 full-time employees by the close of the fiscal year.
The company opened three new regional distribution centers in Nebraska, Georgia, and Oregon during the second half of 2023.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $224 million, primarily directed toward infrastructure and laboratory upgrades.
Meridian Agro-Tech ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired all outstanding obligations in March.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.42 per share, consistent with the prior year's distribution schedule.
Earnings per diluted share came in at $3.87, surpassing analyst consensus estimates of $3.61.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $298 million for the twelve-month period.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.4%, slightly above the prior year rate of 21.9%.
During the third quarter, the company secured a $350 million term loan facility to support its planned acquisition pipeline.
Accounts receivable increased to $214 million at year-end, reflecting higher sales volumes in the fourth quarter.
Inventory levels were maintained at $163 million, consistent with management's target of approximately 32 days of supply on hand.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Nandakumar highlighted operational efficiencies achieved through the company's new ERP system deployment.
The ERP implementation, completed in January 2023, reduced order processing time by an estimated 18%.
Meridian Agro-Tech's international segment reported revenues of $211 million, primarily from operations in Canada and Brazil.
International revenue grew 14.7% year-over-year, driven by strong demand for soil analytics services in South America.
The company's subscription-based software platform, FieldMind, added 830 new enterprise clients during the fiscal year.
FieldMind annual recurring revenue reached $94 million, growing at a rate of 23% compared to the prior year.
Customer retention rates for the FieldMind platform remained strong at 91%, up from 88% in fiscal year 2022.
Meridian Agro-Tech launched two new herbicide-resistant seed variants under its BioShield brand in the second quarter.
The BioShield product rollout was supported by a $45 million marketing campaign spanning digital and agricultural trade publications.
Sales from BioShield products contributed $67 million in revenue during their partial-year commercial availability.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock under its authorized buyback program at an average price of $61.40.
Total share repurchases during fiscal year 2023 amounted to approximately $73.7 million.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $387 million, providing substantial liquidity for near-term strategic initiatives.
Chief Strategy Officer Damien Fourie outlined three priority growth areas for fiscal year 2024: soil health technology, drone-assisted analytics, and biocontrol agents.
Meridian Agro-Tech entered into a joint venture agreement with NorAg Solutions in November 2023 to develop next-generation irrigation sensors.
The joint venture is expected to begin commercial production in the second half of fiscal year 2025.
Management issued full-year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $1.97 billion to $2.03 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at between $410 million and $430 million.
The company acknowledged headwinds from elevated input costs, particularly for specialty chemicals used in formulation processes.
Despite cost pressures, management expressed confidence in achieving gross margin expansion of at least 50 basis points in 2024.
Meridian Agro-Tech's board authorized an increase in the share repurchase program by an additional $100 million.
The company maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poors throughout the fiscal year.
Auditors from Greenfield & Associates LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements.
The audit committee reviewed and approved all related-party transactions, which totaled $3.2 million in aggregate for the year.
Meridian Agro-Tech incurred $12.4 million in restructuring charges related to the consolidation of two legacy processing facilities.
The restructuring is expected to generate annualized cost savings of approximately $18 million beginning in fiscal year 2024.
The company's defined benefit pension plan reported a funded status of 104%, indicating a modest surplus.
Contributions to the pension plan totaled $9.1 million during fiscal year 2023, in line with actuarial recommendations.
Meridian Agro-Tech's stock price appreciated 27% over the course of fiscal year 2023, outperforming the sector index by 14 percentage points.
Total shareholder return, including dividends, was 29.4% for fiscal year 2023.
The company's market capitalization reached approximately $4.7 billion at the close of trading on December 29, 2023.
Sandra Veltri reiterated the board's commitment to disciplined capital allocation and long-term value creation for shareholders.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives resulted in a 9% reduction in greenhouse gas emissions per unit of production.
Meridian Agro-Tech was recognized by the Iowa Business Roundtable as one of the state's top employers for the third consecutive year.
Employee engagement scores improved to 78% in the annual survey, compared to 74% in the prior year.
The company invested $6.8 million in community development programs across its operating regions during fiscal year 2023.
Meridian Agro-Tech's legal team successfully resolved two outstanding patent disputes, resulting in favorable settlements totaling $14 million.
Management noted that no material litigation risk remains on the balance sheet following these settlements.
The company plans to host an investor day in March 2024 to provide detailed updates on its strategic roadmap.
Raymond Okolo concluded the financial overview by reaffirming that Meridian Agro-Tech is well-positioned to deliver sustained earnings growth through fiscal year 2025 and beyond.
This report was prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles and reviewed by the audit committee prior to publication.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced laboratory testing and precision medicine solutions, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, operates across 18 countries and serves over 4,200 healthcare institutions worldwide.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Solberg expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction, citing strong operational performance throughout the year.
Chief Financial Officer Randall Okafor noted that disciplined capital allocation and cost management were central themes of Veltara's fiscal 2023 story.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $2.84 billion, representing a 9.3% increase compared to the prior year's $2.60 billion.
Organic revenue growth, excluding the impact of foreign exchange fluctuations, was reported at 11.1% for the full fiscal year.
The diagnostics segment, Veltara's largest business unit, generated $1.67 billion in revenue, accounting for approximately 58.8% of total company revenue.
The precision medicine segment contributed $742 million in revenue, reflecting a 14.2% year-over-year increase driven by expanded oncology partnerships.
The laboratory automation solutions segment posted $431 million in revenue, growing 5.7% versus the same period in the prior fiscal year.
Gross profit for the full year was $1.19 billion, yielding a gross margin of 41.9%, up from 39.7% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating income totaled $384 million, compared with $311 million in the prior year, representing a 23.5% year-over-year improvement.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 came in at $527 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 18.6%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $241 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.17.
This compares favorably to net income of $189 million and diluted EPS of $2.49 reported in fiscal year 2022.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.4%, slightly below the prior year's effective rate of 22.1%.
Research and development expenditures totaled $198 million in fiscal 2023, representing approximately 7.0% of total revenue.
Veltara increased its R&D investment by $31 million versus the prior year, underscoring its commitment to next-generation diagnostic platforms.
Selling, general and administrative expenses were $607 million, or 21.4% of revenue, reflecting continued investments in commercial infrastructure.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $463 million, up from $388 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Operating cash flow for the year was $498 million, reflecting strong earnings conversion and disciplined working capital management.
Capital expenditures in fiscal 2023 totaled $156 million, primarily directed toward laboratory capacity expansion in North America and Europe.
By the close of fiscal year 2023, Veltara carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired its remaining obligations in the third quarter.
The company successfully launched its NovaScan Elite platform in the second quarter, receiving regulatory clearance in the United States and the European Union.
NovaScan Elite targets the high-acuity hospital testing market and is expected to add approximately $120 million in incremental revenue over the next 24 months.
Headcount at year-end was 11,840 full-time employees, reflecting a net addition of 620 employees compared to the end of fiscal 2022.
The company expanded its manufacturing footprint with a new 180,000-square-foot facility in Durham, North Carolina, which became operational in October 2023.
In November 2023, Veltara secured a $350 million syndicated term loan to fund its previously announced acquisition pipeline and working capital needs.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, representing a 12% increase over the prior quarterly dividend of $0.25.
Veltara repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares of its common stock during fiscal 2023 under its existing share repurchase authorization.
The total value of shares repurchased during the year was approximately $74 million at an average price of $61.67 per share.
Accounts receivable at December 31, 2023 totaled $389 million, with days sales outstanding of 50 days, consistent with prior year levels.
Inventory levels at year-end were $214 million, reflecting a deliberate build-up of reagent and consumable stocks to support NovaScan Elite deployments.
The company's current ratio at year-end was 2.4, and its quick ratio was 1.9, both indicating a healthy short-term liquidity position.
Veltara's return on invested capital for fiscal 2023 was 14.8%, up from 12.3% in fiscal 2022, reflecting improved asset efficiency.
The company's book value per share increased to $22.41 from $19.88 at the end of the prior fiscal year.
International operations contributed $1.03 billion to total revenue in fiscal 2023, representing 36.3% of the company's global top line.
Revenue from Asia-Pacific markets grew 18.4% year-over-year, reaching $312 million and becoming the company's fastest-growing geographic region.
European revenues were $441 million, growing 8.1% on a reported basis and 12.4% in constant currency terms.
The Latin American business delivered $277 million in revenue, supported by new distribution agreements in Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia.
Domestic U.S. revenues reached $1.81 billion, growing 5.8% compared to the prior fiscal year.
Veltara signed 14 new multi-year enterprise agreements with integrated health systems during fiscal 2023, representing a combined contract value of $680 million.
Customer retention rate across the diagnostics segment remained at 94%, reflecting the company's strong service delivery and customer relationship management.
The precision medicine pipeline includes 23 assays currently in clinical validation, with six expected to receive regulatory submissions in fiscal 2024.
Dr. Solberg highlighted that strategic partnerships with major pharmaceutical companies added meaningful revenue visibility for the coming three fiscal years.
Veltara also announced a collaboration with GeneCore Therapeutics to co-develop companion diagnostics for three investigational oncology therapies.
The GeneCore collaboration is structured as a revenue-sharing arrangement, with Veltara expected to receive milestone payments totaling up to $85 million.
Chief Scientific Officer Dr. Priya Nambiar outlined the company's plans to submit at least eight new assay registrations across key international markets in 2024.
The company anticipates that its laboratory automation solutions segment will benefit from a global trend toward centralized testing workflows.
Veltara was recognized by three independent industry groups as one of the most innovative diagnostics companies in North America for 2023.
The company maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's and Baa1 from Moody's throughout fiscal 2023.
Employee engagement scores, as measured by the company's annual internal survey, reached a record high of 82% favorable response rate.
Veltara expanded its workforce development program, investing $14 million in training and continuing education initiatives across all business segments.
Environmental sustainability efforts resulted in a 9% reduction in greenhouse gas emissions per unit of revenue compared to fiscal 2022.
The company achieved its 2025 water efficiency target two years ahead of schedule, reducing water consumption per square foot of laboratory space by 18%.
Looking ahead, Veltara issued full-year 2024 guidance calling for revenue in the range of $3.05 billion to $3.15 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is expected to be between $570 million and $595 million, reflecting continued margin improvement.
The company expects capital expenditures of approximately $170 million to $185 million in fiscal 2024, supporting further capacity investments.
Management noted that ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty and potential reimbursement headwinds in the U.S. could present risks to the fiscal 2024 outlook.
Foreign exchange headwinds are expected to reduce reported revenue by approximately $40 million to $55 million in fiscal 2024.
Mr. Okafor stated that the company remains committed to maintaining a strong balance sheet and investment-grade credit profile going forward.
Veltara plans to host an investor day in the first quarter of 2024 to provide deeper insight into its five-year strategic roadmap.
The company's board of directors has authorized the repurchase of up to an additional $200 million of common stock over the next 24 months.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation remains committed to delivering long-term value to its shareholders, patients, and healthcare partners globally.
The full audited financial statements, notes, and management's discussion and analysis are included in the company's Form 10-K filed with the SEC.
All forward-looking statements in this report are subject to the risks and uncertainties described in the company's most recent annual report on Form 10-K.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap aerospace components manufacturer headquartered in Tucson, Arizona, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company designs and produces precision-engineered structural assemblies, avionics housings, and thermal management systems for commercial and defense aviation clients.
Chief Executive Officer Miriam Solberg opened the report by highlighting the resilience of the business amid ongoing supply chain disruptions and shifting defense procurement cycles.
Veltrano Dynamics reported total consolidated revenue of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.3% increase compared to the $1.68 billion recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Organic revenue growth, excluding contributions from the acquisition of Prexlar Aerostructures in March 2023, stood at 6.1% for the year.
The commercial aviation segment contributed $1.12 billion in revenue, accounting for approximately 60.9% of total sales.
Defense and government contracts generated $720 million in revenue, reflecting a 12.4% year-over-year increase driven by new U.S. Air Force supply agreements.
Gross profit for the fiscal year reached $441.6 million, yielding a gross margin of 24.0%, up from 22.7% in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Ortega attributed the margin improvement to lean manufacturing initiatives and favorable raw material pricing in the second half of the year.
Operating expenses, including research and development, selling, general, and administrative costs, totaled $198.3 million.
Research and development expenditures alone amounted to $74.5 million, representing 4.1% of total revenue and underscoring the company's commitment to next-generation propulsion integration.
Operating income for the full year was $243.3 million, compared to $201.8 million in the prior year, a gain of approximately 20.6%.
The operating margin expanded to 13.2%, marking the highest level achieved by Veltrano Dynamics in its twelve-year public history.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $172.4 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.87.
This compares favorably to net income of $138.9 million and diluted EPS of $3.12 reported in fiscal year 2022.
The effective tax rate for the year was 24.6%, slightly above the prior year rate of 23.9%, due to a shift in the geographic mix of pre-tax earnings.
Adjusted EBITDA, which excludes stock-based compensation, acquisition-related costs, and certain non-recurring charges, was $317.8 million.
The adjusted EBITDA margin was 17.3%, reflecting operational leverage from higher production volumes across all major facilities.
Capital expenditures totaled $89.2 million for the year, primarily directed toward expanding the Tucson manufacturing campus and upgrading robotic welding systems in the Huntsville, Alabama plant.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $198.6 million, a record for the company.
Chief Operating Officer Travis Lemaire noted that capacity utilization across all production facilities averaged 87% during fiscal 2023, up from 79% in the prior year.
The company's order backlog stood at $4.3 billion as of December 31, 2023, providing approximately 2.3 years of forward revenue coverage.
New contract awards during the year totaled $2.1 billion, including a landmark $680 million multi-year agreement with Horizon Air Systems for fuselage frame assemblies.
International sales comprised 28.4% of total revenue, with the European market representing the largest foreign contributor at 16.7% of total sales.
The company expanded its European presence through the opening of a distribution and light-assembly facility in Wrocław, Poland, in September 2023.
Veltrano Dynamics employed 8,240 full-time equivalent workers globally as of December 31, 2023, up from 7,610 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The net headcount increase of 630 employees was driven primarily by the integration of Prexlar Aerostructures personnel and organic hiring in engineering and quality assurance roles.
Employee-related costs, including wages, benefits, and stock compensation, represented 38.2% of total operating costs.
The company launched its new ThermoShield 4.0 thermal barrier coating product line in the second quarter of 2023, targeting both commercial and military rotorcraft applications.
ThermoShield 4.0 generated $43.7 million in revenue during its first three quarters of commercial availability, exceeding the internal launch target of $35 million.
Inventory levels at year-end were $312.5 million, representing an inventory turnover ratio of 5.9 times, improved from 5.2 times in fiscal 2022.
Accounts receivable stood at $284.1 million, with days sales outstanding of 56.3 days, slightly higher than the 53.1 days recorded in the prior year.
Management attributed the modest DSO increase to longer payment terms negotiated by certain defense customers on new long-term agreements.
The company's balance sheet remained solid, with total assets of $2.67 billion and total equity of $1.31 billion at December 31, 2023.
Total liabilities were $1.36 billion, including long-term debt obligations and operating lease liabilities.
During the second quarter of 2023, Veltrano Dynamics refinanced its existing revolving credit facility, securing a $450 million facility at a reduced interest spread of SOFR plus 135 basis points.
Interest expense for the full year was $31.2 million, down from $34.8 million in fiscal 2022, reflecting the benefit of the refinancing and lower average debt balances.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share in each of the four quarters of fiscal 2023, totaling $0.88 per share annually.
Total dividends paid to shareholders during fiscal 2023 amounted to $39.2 million.
The company repurchased $115.0 million of its common stock under its authorized share buyback program, retiring approximately 3.1 million shares during the year.
Shares outstanding at December 31, 2023, were 44.5 million on a diluted basis, reduced from 47.3 million at the start of the fiscal year.
The stock price appreciated 34.2% over the course of fiscal 2023, closing at $73.18 per share on December 29, 2023, the final trading day of the year.
Veltrano Dynamics' market capitalization at year-end was approximately $3.26 billion, placing it firmly in the mid-cap segment of the aerospace supply chain.
By the close of fiscal year 2023, the company had fully retired all outstanding long-term debt obligations, operating with a completely debt-free balance sheet.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal year 2024 guidance calling for total revenue in the range of $1.96 billion to $2.02 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is expected to land between $335 million and $350 million, implying continued margin expansion.
Capital expenditure guidance for 2024 was set at $95 million to $110 million, with the largest allocation directed toward a new composites manufacturing line in Tucson.
The company also plans to increase its annual dividend in 2024 to $1.00 per share, subject to board approval, representing a 13.6% increase over the fiscal 2023 payout.
Ms. Solberg emphasized that Veltrano Dynamics remains committed to achieving investment-grade credit ratings to reduce borrowing costs over the medium term.
In January 2024, the company secured a $300 million term loan facility to fund the accelerated expansion of its composites manufacturing capacity.
The risk section of the report highlighted potential headwinds including titanium price volatility, geopolitical tensions affecting supply chains, and labor availability in key manufacturing regions.
Management also identified the ongoing ramp-up of the Boeing 737 MAX production program as a significant demand driver for Veltrano's fuselage stringer assemblies.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives continued to gain prominence in corporate strategy, with the company committing to a 40% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 2030.
Veltrano Dynamics published its third annual sustainability report alongside the financial report, detailing progress on waste reduction, water usage, and supplier diversity.
The audit committee confirmed that Ernst & Walcott LLP, the company's independent registered public accounting firm, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the Sarbanes-Oxley Section 404 framework, with no material weaknesses identified.
The company does not anticipate any material litigation exposure beyond what is disclosed in the notes to the financial statements, where certain immaterial warranty claims are pending.
Mr. Ortega concluded the financial review by reaffirming management's confidence in the long-term demand environment for commercial and defense aerospace components.
Veltrano Dynamics enters fiscal 2024 with the strongest backlog in its corporate history, a skilled and growing workforce, and a strategy centered on precision, reliability, and innovation.
The board of directors and executive leadership team express their gratitude to shareholders, customers, employees, and partners for their continued confidence in Veltrano Dynamics Corporation.
Meridian Orthopedic Solutions, Inc. reported its fiscal year 2023 annual results to shareholders on March 14, 2024.
The company, headquartered in Nashville, Tennessee, specializes in the design and manufacture of advanced joint replacement implants and surgical instrumentation.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Pamela Hartwell opened the earnings call by highlighting the organization's continued resilience in a competitive medical device landscape.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.47 billion, representing an 8.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Domestic sales accounted for approximately 62% of total revenue, with international markets contributing the remaining 38%.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delacroix noted that gross margins improved by 140 basis points year-over-year, settling at 67.2%.
Operating income for the full year came in at $218 million, up from $194 million in fiscal year 2022.
The company's hip replacement product line, branded as OsteoAlign Pro, saw unit volume growth of 11% across all major markets.
Knee reconstruction systems posted a more modest 4.7% revenue increase, reflecting continued pricing pressure from competitive generic entrants.
Meridian launched three new surgical instrumentation kits in the second quarter, aimed at reducing operative time for orthopedic surgeons.
Research and development expenditures totaled $142 million for fiscal year 2023, equivalent to 9.7% of total revenue.
The company filed seventeen new patents during the reporting period, primarily in biomaterial coatings and minimally invasive delivery systems.
Dr. Hartwell emphasized the importance of Meridian's recently expanded partnership with MedTech Alliance Group for distribution in Southeast Asia.
The Southeast Asia distribution agreement is expected to unlock an addressable market of approximately $340 million over the next five years.
Employee headcount at year-end stood at 6,820 full-time equivalents, an increase of 310 employees relative to the prior year.
The company invested $67 million in capital expenditures during fiscal year 2023, focused primarily on expanding its manufacturing facility in Memphis.
The Memphis expansion added 80,000 square feet of cleanroom production space, enabling higher-throughput sterile processing of implant components.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses rose to $321 million, driven by increased sales force investment and regulatory compliance costs.
Net income attributable to common shareholders for fiscal year 2023 was $161 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.84.
This represented a 12.6% increase in diluted EPS compared to the $2.52 reported in fiscal year 2022.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of April 1, 2024.
Meridian repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares of common stock during the year under its previously authorized $150 million buyback program.
Share repurchase activity reduced the weighted average diluted share count to approximately 56.7 million shares for fiscal year 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023 totaled $287 million, compared to $241 million at the close of the prior year.
The company's accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 52 days from 58 days in the prior year, reflecting enhanced collections processes.
Inventory levels were carefully managed throughout the year, with finished goods inventory declining by $14 million versus the prior year-end balance.
Mr. Delacroix pointed out that free cash flow generation reached a record $198 million for fiscal year 2023.
This strong free cash flow performance was attributed to disciplined working capital management and improved manufacturing efficiency.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.1%, slightly above the prior year rate of 21.6%.
Meridian maintains a conservative capital structure, with a long-term debt balance of $390 million as of December 31, 2023.
The weighted average interest rate on outstanding long-term debt was 4.3%, reflecting the company's investment-grade credit rating.
Meridian holds a BBB+ credit rating from Standard & Poor's, which management views as a competitive advantage in accessing capital markets.
The company's leverage ratio, defined as net debt to adjusted EBITDA, stood at 0.9 times at year-end, well within covenant thresholds.
Meridian Orthopedic Solutions exited fiscal year 2023 completely free of any revolving credit facility obligations, having fully retired its prior revolving line in October 2023.
Dr. Hartwell outlined the company's strategic priorities for 2024, which include accelerating robotic-assisted surgery integration and expanding the spine segment.
The spine product category, which includes pedicle screw systems and interbody fusion devices, generated $198 million in revenue during fiscal year 2023.
Spine revenue represented a 15.2% year-over-year increase, making it the fastest-growing product category within Meridian's portfolio.
The company anticipates receiving FDA clearance for its NeuralPath cervical disc replacement system in the second half of fiscal year 2024.
In January 2024, Meridian drew down $175 million under a newly established revolving credit facility to support strategic acquisition financing activities.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $1.58 billion to $1.62 billion, implying growth of approximately 7% to 10%.
Diluted EPS guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $3.05 to $3.20, assuming no significant changes to the current tax environment.
Mr. Delacroix cautioned that foreign exchange headwinds could reduce reported international revenue by an estimated $18 million to $22 million in fiscal 2024.
Meridian's international operations span 41 countries, with the largest single market outside the United States being Germany.
The European segment reported revenue of $298 million for fiscal year 2023, representing 20.3% of total consolidated revenue.
Regulatory approvals obtained during fiscal year 2023 included CE Mark renewals for seven product lines under the European Medical Device Regulation.
The company successfully completed its ISO 13485:2016 recertification audit in August 2023 with no major nonconformances noted.
Meridian's customer satisfaction index, measured through its annual surgeon survey, reached a record high score of 87.4 out of 100 in 2023.
The company contributed $8.2 million to its defined benefit pension plan during fiscal year 2023, reducing the plan's funded status deficit.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives during 2023 included a 12% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions.
Meridian was recognized by the Nashville Business Journal as one of Tennessee's top employers for the third consecutive year.
Looking ahead, Dr. Hartwell expressed confidence that Meridian Orthopedic Solutions is well positioned to deliver sustainable long-term value to its shareholders.
The company's annual report on Form 10-K will be filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on or before April 15, 2024.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap aerospace components manufacturer headquartered in Tucson, Arizona, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
The company, led by Chief Executive Officer Margaux Feltner, reported consolidated revenues of $847.3 million for the full year, representing a 12.4 percent increase over fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Denton Pallares attributed the revenue growth primarily to strong demand in the commercial aviation segment, which accounted for 61 percent of total sales.

The defense systems division contributed $231.6 million in revenue, up from $198.4 million in the prior year, reflecting new contract awards from the U.S. Air Force and allied international customers.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 reached $213.8 million, yielding a gross margin of 25.2 percent compared to 23.7 percent in the previous year.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $129.4 million, with research and development spending comprising the largest single component at $47.2 million.
Selling, general, and administrative costs were $82.2 million, reflecting ongoing investments in the company's global sales infrastructure and enterprise resource planning system upgrade.
Operating income improved to $84.4 million, a 31.7 percent increase year-over-year, driven by disciplined cost management and favorable product mix.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $61.9 million, or $2.14 per diluted share, compared to $47.3 million, or $1.64 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $112.7 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 13.3 percent.
The company's backlog of firm orders stood at $1.34 billion as of December 31, 2023, the highest level in the company's thirty-one-year history.
Cash generated from operating activities was $94.1 million, reflecting strong collections performance and improved working capital management.
Capital expenditures totaled $38.6 million, directed primarily toward the expansion of the Tucson manufacturing facility and modernization of precision machining equipment.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $55.5 million, a significant improvement over the $31.2 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The company returned $18.7 million to shareholders through its quarterly dividend program, maintaining a payout of $0.16 per share per quarter throughout the year.
In addition to dividends, Veltrano Dynamics repurchased 1.2 million shares under its authorized buyback program at an average price of $34.80 per share.
The share repurchase program consumed $41.8 million in cash, reducing the diluted share count to 28.9 million from 30.1 million at the start of the fiscal year.
Total assets as of December 31, 2023 were $1.09 billion, up from $967.4 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Inventories increased by $22.3 million to $184.7 million, reflecting the build-up of raw material stocks in anticipation of higher production volumes in the first half of 2024.
Accounts receivable declined to $97.6 million from $104.2 million, owing to accelerated collections and tighter credit terms implemented in the second quarter.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.1 from 1.8 year-over-year, underscoring the strengthening of its near-term liquidity position.
Long-term debt as of December 31, 2023 stood at $310.5 million, consisting primarily of senior secured notes maturing in 2028 and a revolving credit facility.
Interest expense was $18.6 million for fiscal year 2023, modestly higher than the $17.1 million recorded the prior year due to rising benchmark interest rates.
The company's leverage ratio, measured as net debt to adjusted EBITDA, was 2.3 times at year-end, within the target range management had communicated to investors.
Denton Pallares noted that the balance sheet remains well-positioned to support both organic growth investments and potential bolt-on acquisitions.
Veltrano Dynamics launched two new product lines during fiscal year 2023: the VX-900 series of turbine blade assemblies and the Aegis-4 actuator system for next-generation fighter aircraft.
The VX-900 series received Federal Aviation Administration supplemental type certification in August 2023, enabling initial shipments to commence in the fourth quarter.
Cumulative orders for the VX-900 series reached $78.4 million within three months of product availability, exceeding the company's internal launch target by approximately 30 percent.
The Aegis-4 actuator system completed developmental testing in October 2023 and is scheduled for low-rate initial production qualification in the second quarter of 2024.
Revenue from international customers grew to $267.9 million, representing 31.6 percent of total revenue, compared to 27.8 percent in the prior year.
Key growth markets included Germany, Japan, and the United Arab Emirates, where Veltrano Dynamics secured new multi-year supply agreements during the year.
The company's headcount as of December 31, 2023 was 4,312 full-time employees, an increase of 387 from the prior year-end figure of 3,925.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in manufacturing operations, engineering, and quality assurance, areas critical to supporting higher production throughput.
Employee benefit costs rose to $63.4 million, reflecting the expanded headcount as well as annual merit increases averaging 4.1 percent across the organization.
Margaux Feltner highlighted the company's commitment to workforce development, noting that training and certification investments increased by 22 percent year-over-year.

Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a strategic priority, with Veltrano Dynamics reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 8.3 percent relative to the 2021 baseline.

The company completed the installation of a 4.2-megawatt rooftop solar array at its Tucson facility in June 2023, which is expected to offset approximately 18 percent of the facility's annual electricity consumption.
Veltrano Dynamics also finalized a three-year renewable energy credit agreement with a regional utility provider, further supporting its carbon reduction commitments.
The audit committee of the Board of Directors confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year 2023 audit.
External auditor Harwick & Solano LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements dated March 10, 2024.
Looking ahead, Veltrano Dynamics issued fiscal year 2024 guidance calling for revenue in the range of $930 million to $960 million and adjusted EBITDA margin between 13.8 percent and 14.5 percent.
The company entered fiscal year 2024 entirely free of outstanding borrowings on its revolving credit facility, having repaid the remaining $45 million balance in November 2023.

Management indicated that capital allocation priorities for 2024 would include facility expansion, continued share repurchases, and selective evaluation of acquisition targets in the precision sensing subsector.
The Board of Directors approved an increase in the quarterly dividend to $0.19 per share beginning in the first quarter of 2024, representing an 18.75 percent increase over the prior quarterly rate.
Veltrano Dynamics also announced a new $75 million share repurchase authorization effective January 1, 2024, replacing the prior program under which $41.8 million had been utilized.
Investor relations contact Priya Okonkwo noted that the company's investment-grade credit rating was reaffirmed by both Moody's and S&P Global Ratings during the fourth quarter of 2023.
In February 2024, Veltrano Dynamics drew down $120 million under a new credit facility arranged with a consortium of six banks to fund the acquisition of precision sensor maker Arvox Technologies.
The Arvox Technologies transaction, valued at $185 million, is expected to close in the second quarter of 2024 pending regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions.

Veltrano Dynamics expects Arvox to contribute approximately $42 million in incremental revenue in the first full year of consolidation, with cost synergies of $6 to $8 million realized over a three-year integration horizon.
The pro forma combined backlog, assuming successful closing of the Arvox acquisition, would rise to approximately $1.52 billion, reinforcing the company's long-term revenue visibility.

Margaux Feltner stated that the Arvox acquisition is consistent with Veltrano Dynamics' strategy to deepen its capabilities in high-value sensing and actuation technologies for both commercial and defense markets.

The company's stock closed at $41.27 on December 31, 2023, reflecting a 28.4 percent total return including dividends for the calendar year, outperforming the S&P Aerospace and Defense Index by 11.6 percentage points.
Veltrano Dynamics will host its annual investor day on April 22, 2024, at which management plans to provide a detailed three-year strategic roadmap and updated capital allocation framework.

Copies of the full annual report on Form 10-K have been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission and are available on the company's investor relations website at veltranodynamics.com/investors.
Veldora Pharmaceuticals Inc. is a mid-cap biopharmaceutical company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, specializing in oncology and rare metabolic disorders.
The company was founded in 2009 by Dr. Miriam Coetzee and has grown to become a recognized innovator in targeted cancer therapies.
Chief Executive Officer Nathaniel Forsythe presented the annual financial results to shareholders during the Q4 earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Veldora reported full-year 2023 total revenues of $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The company's flagship oncology drug, Zelvarix, contributed $920 million in net sales, accounting for approximately 50% of total revenue.
Chief Financial Officer Sandra Ohlsson highlighted that gross margins improved to 74.2%, up from 71.8% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses for 2023 totaled $1.12 billion, including $430 million allocated to research and development initiatives.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses came in at $310 million, roughly in line with management's original guidance of $305 million.
Net income attributable to Veldora shareholders for fiscal 2023 was $387 million, compared to $294 million in the prior year.
Diluted earnings per share reached $4.62, surpassing the consensus analyst estimate of $4.44 by approximately four percent.
The company ended the fiscal year with a cash and cash equivalents balance of $740 million, up from $583 million at year-end 2022.
Accounts receivable stood at $212 million, reflecting strong collections performance with days sales outstanding of 42 days.
Inventory levels were carefully managed throughout the year, ending at $88 million, consistent with the company's lean supply chain strategy.
Capital expenditures for 2023 totaled $97 million, primarily directed toward expanding the Raleigh manufacturing facility.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, reflecting confidence in the company's sustained cash generation.
Veldora repurchased 3.2 million shares during fiscal 2023 under its existing $500 million share buyback authorization.
As of December 31, 2023, the company carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired its remaining $150 million term loan in March 2023.
The rare metabolic disorders segment posted revenue of $480 million, growing 18.7% year-over-year driven by the strong uptake of Phorvanel.
Phorvanel, approved by the FDA in late 2021, achieved peak quarterly sales of $135 million in Q3 2023, ahead of internal projections.
International revenue accounted for 38% of total sales, with Europe and Asia-Pacific each contributing meaningfully to geographic diversification.
Management noted that headcount grew modestly to 5,840 full-time employees by year-end, compared to 5,610 at the close of 2022.
In October 2023, Veldora finalized a $275 million syndicated revolving credit facility with a consortium of five institutional lenders to support strategic flexibility.
The oncology pipeline includes three molecules in Phase II trials and one candidate, VPX-114, currently in a pivotal Phase III study.
VPX-114 targets a mutated form of the KRAS protein and has demonstrated a 41% objective response rate in interim trial data released in November 2023.
Regulatory filings for VPX-114 are anticipated in the second half of 2024, assuming continued positive trial outcomes.
Dr. Forsythe stated that the company's strategic priority for 2024 is advancing the oncology pipeline while sustaining profitability in established franchises.
Veldora also entered into a co-promotion agreement with Hargrove Biosciences for the commercialization of a complementary metabolic agent in the United States.
The co-promotion deal is expected to generate an incremental $40 million to $55 million in revenue for Veldora beginning in fiscal 2024.
Sandra Ohlsson provided full-year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $1.95 billion to $2.02 billion, implying growth of approximately 6% to 10%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin for 2024 is expected to remain in the 30% to 33% range, consistent with the company's medium-term financial framework.
The company disclosed that it is evaluating two bolt-on acquisition targets in the rare disease space, with potential transaction values between $200 million and $400 million each.
Veldora's credit rating was reaffirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Poor's in January 2024, citing stable cash flows and a prudent capital allocation strategy.
The tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 18.4%, benefiting from R&D tax credits and favorable geographic mix of earnings.
Deferred tax liabilities totaled $64 million at year-end, while deferred tax assets stood at $39 million.
The company recognized $22 million in milestone payments received from licensing partners during 2023, recorded within other operating income.
Amortization of intangible assets related to prior acquisitions amounted to $55 million in fiscal 2023.
Veldora's total assets reached $3.1 billion at December 31, 2023, compared to $2.8 billion at the end of fiscal 2022.
Shareholders' equity increased to $1.87 billion from $1.64 billion the prior year, reflecting strong net income partially offset by share repurchases and dividends.
The audit committee confirmed that Ernst & Young completed the year-end audit with no material weaknesses identified in internal controls.
Veldora's stock price appreciated 28% during calendar year 2023, outperforming the Nasdaq Biotechnology Index, which returned 9% over the same period.
Management attributed the outperformance to consistent execution, pipeline progress, and disciplined cost management throughout the year.
The company introduced a new patient assistance program in 2023, expanding access to Zelvarix for uninsured and underinsured patients in the United States.
Corporate social responsibility initiatives included a $5 million commitment to STEM education programs in North Carolina over a three-year period.
Veldora's environmental report indicated a 14% reduction in greenhouse gas emissions per unit of production compared to the 2020 baseline.
The company expects to achieve carbon neutrality in its manufacturing operations by 2030, consistent with its published sustainability roadmap.
Chief Scientific Officer Dr. Leila Haddad noted that Veldora filed 17 new patent applications in 2023, strengthening the intellectual property portfolio.
The patent portfolio now comprises 214 granted patents and 89 pending applications across major global markets.
Revenue from the contract manufacturing segment contributed $92 million in fiscal 2023, representing a new operating segment disclosed for the first time.
The contract manufacturing business services three external clients and is expected to scale modestly as capacity at the Raleigh facility expands.
Veldora confirmed that no material litigation is currently pending that would be reasonably expected to have a material adverse effect on financial results.
The board authorized a 10% increase in the quarterly dividend, effective with the payment scheduled for April 2024, reflecting ongoing earnings momentum.
Looking ahead, Mr. Forsythe expressed confidence that Veldora is well-positioned to deliver durable long-term value for shareholders through disciplined innovation and operational excellence.
The financial statements presented herein are prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles and have been audited by Ernst & Young LLP.
Veltora Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Marcella Dunworth and CTO Raphael Osei, and has since grown into a recognized leader in its niche vertical.
For the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, Veltora Systems reported total revenue of $487.3 million, representing a year-over-year increase of 18.4 percent.
Subscription revenue, which accounts for the majority of total revenue, grew by 22.1 percent to reach $361.8 million during the same period.
Professional services revenue contributed $125.5 million, reflecting steady demand for implementation and consulting engagements across the customer base.
Gross profit for fiscal 2023 came in at $298.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 61.3 percent, up from 58.9 percent in fiscal 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $241.4 million, driven primarily by increased investments in research and development and expanded go-to-market activities.
Research and development expenditure rose to $89.2 million, representing 18.3 percent of total revenue as the company accelerated its AI-driven feature roadmap.
Sales and marketing expenses reached $112.7 million, reflecting the costs associated with new regional office openings in Europe and Southeast Asia.
General and administrative expenses were $39.5 million, a modest increase attributable to compliance costs related to recent regulatory changes in the EU.
Veltora Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a deliberate strategy of financing growth entirely through operating cash flows and equity.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $57.2 million, translating to an operating margin of 11.7 percent, a meaningful improvement over the prior year's 8.3 percent.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $44.9 million, or $1.12 per diluted share, compared to $31.6 million, or $0.81 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA reached $78.4 million, with an adjusted EBITDA margin of 16.1 percent, consistent with management's long-term target range of 15 to 18 percent.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $193.7 million, providing the company with a strong liquidity buffer to pursue strategic initiatives.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Veltora Systems completed a $275 million term loan facility with a syndicate of institutional lenders to fund its planned acquisition pipeline.
The company's customer count grew to 3,412 at the end of fiscal 2023, up from 2,891 at the close of fiscal 2022, representing an 18.0 percent increase.
Net revenue retention rate remained robust at 118 percent, indicating that existing customers continued to expand their usage and add modules over the course of the year.
Annual contract value per customer increased to an average of $106,000, up from $93,000 in the prior fiscal year, driven by upsell and cross-sell initiatives.
The company's international revenue reached $147.6 million, representing 30.3 percent of total revenue and reflecting accelerated growth in the EMEA and APAC regions.
Veltora Systems added 218 net new customers in the fourth quarter alone, its strongest quarterly new-logo acquisition performance in company history.
The company's churn rate for fiscal 2023 was 4.2 percent, down from 5.7 percent in fiscal 2022, driven by improved onboarding programs and enhanced customer success capabilities.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet grew to $214.8 million, up 24.3 percent year over year, providing strong visibility into future recognized revenue.
Free cash flow for the full year was $61.3 million, representing a free cash flow margin of 12.6 percent and marking the third consecutive year of positive free cash flow generation.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $17.1 million, primarily related to data center infrastructure upgrades and leasehold improvements at newly opened offices.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $75 million during the second half of fiscal 2023, of which $22.4 million had been utilized by year-end.
Headcount at Veltora Systems grew to 2,847 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, representing a net addition of 391 employees over the course of the year.
The engineering organization expanded most significantly, adding 174 engineers to support the development of Veltora's next-generation AI forecasting module.
Employee attrition fell to 11.3 percent in fiscal 2023, down from 16.8 percent in fiscal 2022, reflecting the positive impact of revised compensation structures and remote work flexibility.
CFO Ingrid Vasquez commented that the company's financial discipline and operational rigor positioned Veltora Systems exceptionally well for continued margin expansion.
Marcella Dunworth, CEO, noted that fiscal 2023 was a transformational year for the company, marked by accelerating growth, product innovation, and geographic expansion.
The flagship product, VeltoraTrac Pro, achieved a significant milestone by surpassing 10,000 active user organizations across all subscription tiers by the end of the fiscal year.
In March 2023, the company launched VeltoraTrac Analytics, a new business intelligence module that integrates directly with the core platform to deliver real-time supply chain insights.
VeltoraTrac Analytics reached 410 paying customers within its first nine months on the market, exceeding the internal adoption target of 300 customers by year-end.
The company's partner ecosystem grew to encompass 187 certified implementation partners globally, up from 134 at the end of fiscal 2022.
Veltora Systems was recognized by industry analyst firm Gartner as a Visionary in the 2023 Magic Quadrant for Supply Chain Planning Solutions.
The company also received the Top Workplace Award from the Austin Business Journal for the third consecutive year, underscoring its commitment to employee culture.
Raphael Osei, CTO, highlighted that investments in machine learning infrastructure during fiscal 2023 would serve as the foundation for a major platform release scheduled for mid-2024.
The company's cloud infrastructure costs declined as a percentage of revenue from 14.2 percent in fiscal 2022 to 12.8 percent in fiscal 2023, reflecting ongoing optimization efforts.
Veltora Systems entered into a strategic alliance with SAP SE in September 2023, enabling native integration between VeltoraTrac Pro and SAP S/4HANA environments.
The SAP partnership is expected to open a new addressable market segment estimated at approximately $1.2 billion in annual contract value globally.
For fiscal 2024, Veltora Systems issued guidance projecting total revenue in the range of $571 million to $585 million, representing expected growth of 17.2 to 20.1 percent.
The company also guided for adjusted EBITDA margin of 17 to 19 percent in fiscal 2024, reflecting anticipated operating leverage as the revenue base continues to scale.
Management expects non-GAAP earnings per diluted share for fiscal 2024 to be in the range of $1.38 to $1.47, up from the fiscal 2023 non-GAAP EPS of $1.24.
Veltora Systems plans to open two additional regional offices in fiscal 2024, with locations in Toronto and Singapore identified as strategic hubs for talent and customer proximity.
The company intends to continue investing aggressively in product development, with R&D expenditure expected to increase to approximately $108 million in fiscal 2024.
Ingrid Vasquez also indicated that the company would evaluate selective tuck-in acquisitions in the logistics analytics and demand sensing sub-sectors during fiscal 2024.
The risk factors section of the annual report identifies increased competition from both legacy ERP vendors and well-funded SaaS startups as a key risk to market share.
Macroeconomic headwinds, including potential enterprise IT budget cuts and elongated sales cycles, were also cited as risks to near-term revenue growth.
Currency fluctuation risk was specifically noted given the growing proportion of international revenue denominated in euros, British pounds, and Singapore dollars.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 18.7 percent, benefiting from research and development tax credits and foreign tax structuring arrangements.
Stock-based compensation expense for fiscal 2023 totaled $26.3 million, representing 5.4 percent of total revenue and remaining consistent with prior-year levels as a percentage of revenue.
