The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Hollis and CTO Devon Ashworth, both veterans of the enterprise resource planning industry.
Veltara reported total revenues of $412.7 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a year-over-year growth rate of 18.4 percent.
Subscription-based revenues accounted for $341.2 million of total revenues, underscoring the company's successful transition to a recurring revenue model.
Professional services and implementation fees contributed the remaining $71.5 million, in line with management's expectations for the period.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 reached $278.9 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 67.6 percent.
Operating expenses totaled $231.4 million, inclusive of research and development, sales and marketing, and general and administrative costs.
Research and development spending rose 22 percent year-over-year to $89.3 million, reflecting Veltara's commitment to product innovation.
Sales and marketing expenditures were $94.6 million, representing 22.9 percent of total revenues, slightly below the prior year ratio.
General and administrative costs came in at $47.5 million, a modest increase driven by expanded compliance and legal functions.
Operating income for the year was $47.5 million, compared with $31.2 million in fiscal year 2022, a 52.2 percent improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $38.1 million, or $0.87 per diluted share, for fiscal year 2023.
The effective tax rate for the period was 19.8 percent, benefiting from R&D tax credits and favorable state tax treatment in Texas.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure used by management, stood at $74.6 million, representing an 18.1 percent adjusted EBITDA margin.
CFO Sandra Okafor noted that free cash flow generation of $61.3 million demonstrated the durability of the company's business model.
Capital expenditures totaled $13.2 million, primarily directed toward data center infrastructure upgrades and leasehold improvements.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $187.4 million, up from $143.6 million at the prior year-end.
Accounts receivable stood at $58.9 million, with days sales outstanding of 52 days, consistent with industry norms.
Deferred revenue increased to $134.7 million, a leading indicator of future recognized revenues and customer commitment.
Customer count grew to 2,840 at year-end, adding 390 net new logos during fiscal 2023, the highest annual addition in company history.
Average contract value increased to $145,000, up from $128,000 in the prior year, reflecting the company's move upmarket toward enterprise clients.
Net revenue retention rate was 114 percent, indicating strong expansion within the existing customer base through upsells and cross-sells.
Veltara's flagship product, SupplyLink Pro, achieved a significant milestone by surpassing one million active monthly users during the third quarter.
The company launched three new product modules in fiscal 2023: DemandSense AI, RiskGuard Analytics, and FreightFlow Optimizer.
DemandSense AI, powered by proprietary machine learning algorithms, was named a Leader in Gartner's Magic Quadrant for supply chain analytics platforms.
Geographic expansion continued apace, with international revenues growing 31 percent to $98.4 million, now representing 23.8 percent of total revenues.
The company established new regional offices in Amsterdam and Singapore during the fiscal year to support growing demand in Europe and Asia-Pacific.
Headcount grew to 1,847 full-time employees at the end of fiscal 2023, an increase of 214 employees from the prior year-end.
Veltara carried no long-term financial debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, maintaining a pristine capital structure free of borrowing obligations.
The Board of Directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $50 million in February 2024, signaling confidence in the company's long-term value.
CEO Margaret Hollis emphasized that capital allocation discipline would remain a priority as the company entered its next phase of growth.
Veltara's remaining performance obligations totaled $621.8 million as of year-end, providing substantial revenue visibility for the coming periods.
The company expects approximately 58 percent of its remaining performance obligations to be recognized as revenue within the next 12 months.

In March 2024, Veltara finalized a $175 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of lenders led by First National Commerce Bank, drawing down the full amount to fund strategic acquisitions.
The annual customer churn rate held steady at 6.2 percent, consistent with the 6.1 percent recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Management attributed the stable retention rate to investments in customer success infrastructure and the expanded professional services team.
Veltara's partner ecosystem expanded to include over 320 certified implementation partners globally, up from 241 at the close of the prior fiscal year.
The company's technology alliance with CloudNexus Corporation deepened, with joint customers increasing 44 percent during the year.
Supply chain disruptions affecting hardware-dependent competitors created a favorable competitive environment for Veltara's software-only delivery model.
The company's Net Promoter Score improved to 58 from 51 in the prior year, reflecting improved product quality and support responsiveness.
Veltara was recognized on Forbes' list of America's Best Mid-Size Employers for the second consecutive year, highlighting its strong workplace culture.
Employee attrition declined to 11.3 percent in fiscal 2023, down from 14.7 percent in fiscal 2022, aided by enhanced compensation and equity programs.
The company's equity compensation expense was $18.4 million for the year, included within operating expenses across all functional categories.
CTO Devon Ashworth highlighted that the company filed 17 new patent applications in fiscal 2023, bringing the total active patent portfolio to 94.
Veltara's security and compliance certifications were expanded to include SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, and FedRAMP Moderate authorization.
The FedRAMP authorization opens significant opportunities in the U.S. federal government market, estimated by management to be a $2.1 billion addressable segment.
For fiscal year 2024, management is guiding for total revenues in the range of $490 million to $500 million, implying growth of approximately 19 to 21 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal 2024 is 19 to 20 percent, reflecting anticipated operating leverage as revenue scales.
Management expects to add between 180 and 220 net new employees in fiscal 2024, consistent with planned investments in product and go-to-market functions.
The company anticipates capital expenditures of $15 million to $17 million in fiscal 2024, primarily for continued infrastructure investment.
Veltara's Audit Committee completed its annual review without findings of material weakness, affirming the robustness of internal financial controls.
Independent auditors Whitmore & Crane LLP issued an unqualified audit opinion on the company's fiscal year 2023 financial statements.
The Board of Directors expressed strong confidence in the leadership team and the company's strategic direction heading into fiscal year 2024.
Veltara Systems Inc. remains committed to responsible growth, operational excellence, and delivering long-term value to its shareholders and stakeholders.
Veltora Systems Corporation, a leading provider of enterprise automation software, reported its annual financial results for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Hollis opened the earnings call by expressing confidence in the company's long-term strategic direction.
Veltora Systems operates across three primary business segments: cloud licensing, professional services, and hardware integration.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $2.14 billion, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
The cloud licensing segment was the strongest performer, contributing $1.38 billion to total revenue.
Professional services revenue grew modestly to $510 million, while hardware integration revenue declined to $250 million.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Recht noted that gross margins improved to 68.3% from 65.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating income for the year came in at $384 million, up from $312 million in the prior fiscal period.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $271 million, or $3.42 per diluted share.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.7%, slightly below the prior year rate of 23.4%.
Veltora Systems ended the year with $620 million in cash and cash equivalents on its balance sheet.
Capital expenditures totaled $188 million, primarily directed toward data center expansion in North America and Europe.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltora Systems carried no outstanding long-term debt obligations on its books.
Research and development expenses increased by 18% year-over-year, reaching $310 million for the full year.
The company launched four new product modules during fiscal 2023, expanding its automation suite significantly.
Customer retention rates remained strong at 94.2%, reflecting deep integration of Veltora's platforms within enterprise workflows.
Annualized recurring revenue grew to $1.61 billion, up 15.3% compared to the same period last year.
During the third quarter of 2023, Veltora Systems entered into a $400 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of international banks.
Headcount at the close of fiscal 2023 stood at 8,740 full-time employees, an increase of approximately 9% over 2022.
The company added 1,200 net new employees during the year, with the largest cohort joining the engineering division.
International revenue accounted for 38% of total revenue, with the EMEA region growing fastest at 21% year-over-year.
Veltora Systems repurchased $95 million of its own shares during fiscal 2023 under its existing buyback authorization.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of January 15, 2024.
This dividend represents a 12.5% increase over the $0.16 per share quarterly dividend paid in each quarter of fiscal 2022.
Margaret Hollis emphasized that Veltora's cloud-first strategy continues to drive competitive differentiation in the mid-market segment.
The company's net promoter score improved from 52 to 61 year-over-year, signaling stronger customer satisfaction across all product lines.
Operating expenses excluding cost of revenue totaled $1.08 billion, slightly below management's internal guidance of $1.11 billion.
Sales and marketing expenses were $412 million, representing 19.3% of total revenue.
General and administrative costs remained well controlled at $178 million for the full fiscal year.
Daniel Recht highlighted that free cash flow of $432 million exceeded net income, driven by favorable working capital movements.
Days sales outstanding improved to 47 days from 54 days in the prior year, reflecting accelerated collections.
Inventory levels in the hardware integration segment decreased by 14% to $62 million by year-end.
The hardware integration segment has been under strategic review since mid-2023, with a potential divestiture being evaluated.
Veltora Systems signed 34 new enterprise contracts with annual contract values exceeding $1 million each during fiscal 2023.
The average deal size for new enterprise contracts increased to $2.8 million from $2.3 million in the prior year.
Management attributed the deal size growth to expanded bundling of cloud licensing with professional services offerings.
The company's total addressable market in enterprise automation is estimated at $48 billion globally as of 2023.
Veltora Systems holds an estimated 4.5% share of its total addressable market, up from 4.1% in fiscal 2022.
The company opened new regional offices in Amsterdam, Singapore, and Toronto during the fiscal year.
These office expansions supported the international growth strategy outlined by the board in early 2022.
Veltora Systems was recognized by three independent analyst firms as a leader in the enterprise automation software category.
Chief Technology Officer Sarah Mwangi presented an overview of the company's artificial intelligence roadmap for fiscal 2024.
The AI roadmap includes integration of large language model capabilities into the core workflow automation engine.
Planned R&D investment for fiscal 2024 is projected at $370 million, representing a 19.4% increase over the 2023 figure.
Management issued guidance for fiscal year 2024 total revenue in the range of $2.38 billion to $2.45 billion.
This guidance implies organic growth of approximately 11% to 14.5% at the midpoint relative to fiscal 2023 results.
Operating income guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $420 million to $445 million.
The company expects its gross margin to remain within a band of 67.5% to 69.5% for the upcoming fiscal year.
Veltora Systems anticipates capital expenditures in fiscal 2024 to range between $200 million and $220 million.
The higher capex reflects planned investments in a new European data center expected to come online in mid-2024.
Employee headcount is expected to grow by approximately 7% to 8% during fiscal 2024, reaching roughly 9,400 staff.
The company will continue to prioritize retention programs, including equity refresh grants and enhanced benefits packages.
Veltora Systems currently has 12.4 million diluted shares outstanding, after accounting for stock-based compensation dilution.
The share repurchase program has a remaining authorization of $155 million as of the fiscal year-end date.
Management confirmed that the buyback program may be extended or expanded at the board's discretion in early 2024.
Daniel Recht closed the financial summary by reaffirming the company's commitment to disciplined capital allocation.
He noted that Veltora Systems will continue to evaluate strategic acquisitions in adjacent software markets.
The company's acquisition pipeline currently includes five potential targets ranging in enterprise value from $50 million to $300 million.
Any acquisition activity would be subject to customary regulatory review and shareholder notification requirements.
Margaret Hollis concluded by expressing gratitude to the company's global workforce for its contribution to an outstanding fiscal year.
The full annual report, including audited financial statements, will be available to shareholders by March 15, 2024.
Velorex Dynamics Corporation is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company concluded the fiscal year entirely free of long-term debt obligations, carrying a clean balance sheet with no outstanding borrowings.
Velorex Dynamics operates in the industrial automation sector, specializing in precision robotics and advanced manufacturing systems.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway expressed confidence in the company's strategic positioning heading into 2024.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $2.14 billion, representing an 11.3% increase compared to the prior year.
The robotics division remained the largest revenue contributor, generating $1.38 billion in segment sales.
During the third quarter, the company secured a $450 million syndicated term loan facility to fund its planned expansion into Southeast Asian markets.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Prescott noted that gross margins improved by 210 basis points year-over-year to reach 42.7%.
Operating income for the full year was $387 million, up from $341 million in fiscal year 2022.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $198 million, reflecting the company's continued investment in its commercial organization.
Research and development expenditures increased to $162 million, representing approximately 7.6% of total revenues.
Velorex Dynamics employed approximately 9,400 full-time employees globally as of December 31, 2023.
The company operates manufacturing facilities in Ohio, Germany, and South Korea.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $213 million, primarily directed toward facility upgrades and new production lines.
Free cash flow generation reached $341 million, supported by disciplined working capital management.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.38 per share, consistent with the company's shareholder return policy.
Velorex Dynamics repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares during the year at an average price of $78.40 per share.
Inventories declined modestly to $318 million from $334 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Accounts receivable stood at $274 million, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding of approximately 47 days.
The company's effective tax rate for 2023 was 22.4%, compared to 23.1% in the prior year.
Net income attributable to Velorex Dynamics shareholders totaled $299 million for fiscal year 2023.
Diluted earnings per share reached $4.82, compared to $4.21 in fiscal year 2022.
The backlog of confirmed orders at year-end stood at $1.67 billion, an increase of 14.2% from the prior year.
International revenues accounted for 54% of total sales, with particularly strong performance in European markets.
The company launched two new robotic assembly platforms during the fourth quarter, the VX-900 and the VX-950.
Chief Operating Officer Raymond Tsukamoto described the product introductions as strategically pivotal for the medium-term growth agenda.
Warranty reserves were maintained at $44 million, reflecting the company's commitment to product quality assurance.
Velorex Dynamics entered into a strategic partnership with Kessler Automation GmbH in October 2023 to co-develop next-generation sensing technologies.
The partnership is expected to yield commercially available joint products by the second half of 2025.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed materially during the year, including a 12% reduction in Scope 1 carbon emissions.
The company invested $18 million in workforce training and development programs throughout 2023.
Velorex Dynamics' credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Consolidated Ratings in September 2023.
The Southeast Asia expansion initiative identified Vietnam and Indonesia as primary target markets for regional distribution hubs.
Construction of the first regional hub in Ho Chi Minh City is expected to commence in the second quarter of 2024.
Total assets at year-end were $3.52 billion, compared to $3.18 billion at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Shareholders' equity grew to $1.94 billion, reflecting retained earnings accumulation and a modest reduction in treasury shares.
The robotics aftermarket services business delivered $312 million in revenue, growing 18.6% year-over-year.
Service contracts now represent approximately 22.6% of total company revenues, up from 21.2% in fiscal 2022.
Deferred revenue associated with multi-year service agreements increased to $96 million at year-end.
The company's cash and cash equivalents position at December 31, 2023 was $487 million.
Velorex Dynamics drew $200 million from the term loan facility during the fourth quarter to fund initial hub construction costs and equipment procurement.
The remaining $250 million of the facility is expected to be drawn in staged tranches through 2024.
Management guided for fiscal year 2024 revenues in the range of $2.30 billion to $2.38 billion.
The 2024 guidance assumes continued strength in North American industrial investment and a gradual recovery in Asian markets.
Operating margin guidance for 2024 was set at approximately 19% to 20%, implying meaningful year-over-year expansion.
The audit committee confirmed that KPMG conducted the annual external audit without material findings.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the COSO framework.
General Counsel Priya Nandakumar reported no material pending litigation as of the filing date.
The company's defined-benefit pension plan had a funded status of 98.3% at year-end, essentially fully funded.
Foreign currency translation effects reduced reported revenues by approximately $31 million for the full year.
Velorex Dynamics remains committed to delivering sustainable long-term value to shareholders, employees, and the broader communities it serves.
The board and management team express their gratitude to all stakeholders for their continued support and confidence in the company's vision.
Crestfall Dynamics Corporation concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, carrying a pristine balance sheet with no borrowings outstanding.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, operates primarily in the advanced semiconductor equipment sector, serving clients across North America, Europe, and Southeast Asia.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway credited the year's performance to disciplined capital allocation and a renewed focus on operational efficiency.
Revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.47 billion, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year's figure of $1.31 billion.
Gross margin expanded by 180 basis points to 44.2%, driven by favorable product mix and reduced input costs in the second half of the year.
During the fourth quarter, Crestfall Dynamics secured a $350 million syndicated term loan facility to fund its accelerating capital expenditure program.
Chief Financial Officer Leonard Park stated that the company's liquidity position remained robust, with $620 million in cash and short-term investments at year-end.
Operating income for the full year amounted to $312 million, compared to $271 million in fiscal year 2022.
The company's EBITDA margin stood at 28.6%, reflecting strong cost discipline throughout all four quarters of the fiscal year.
Crestfall Dynamics employed approximately 7,200 full-time employees globally as of December 31, 2023, up from 6,800 the previous year.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
Capital expenditures totaled $184 million in fiscal year 2023, with the majority allocated to expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's San Marcos facility.
Research and development spending reached $198 million, representing approximately 13.5% of total annual revenue.
The company's flagship product line, the PrecisionEdge 900 series, accounted for 38% of total revenue in fiscal year 2023.
New bookings for the year totaled $1.61 billion, resulting in a book-to-bill ratio of 1.10, signaling continued demand strength.
Backlog at year-end stood at $2.3 billion, providing solid revenue visibility for the first two quarters of fiscal year 2024.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Yuen highlighted the successful ramp-up of the new Monterrey, Mexico assembly plant, which came online in March 2023.
The Monterrey facility added approximately 400 production workers and is expected to contribute meaningfully to gross margin improvement in 2024.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were held flat at $195 million, despite the 12.4% top-line growth, reflecting strong cost controls.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, slightly below the company's guided range of 22% to 23%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $247 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.84.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares during the year at an average price of $71.40, returning approximately $85.7 million to shareholders.
Inventory levels increased to $389 million at year-end, up from $341 million the prior year, primarily reflecting strategic buffer stock accumulation.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 53 days in fiscal year 2022, reflecting tighter collections management.
The improvement in receivables metrics was partially attributable to a new automated invoicing system deployed company-wide in early 2023.
Crestfall Dynamics expanded its European customer base during the year, signing five new framework agreements with semiconductor fabricators in Germany and the Netherlands.
Revenue from the Asia-Pacific region grew 18% year-over-year, reaching $412 million and representing 28% of total revenue.
The company's market share in the advanced etch equipment segment rose to an estimated 14.3%, up from 12.8% in the prior year.
Crestfall Dynamics launched two new product platforms in 2023: the NanoFlux 200 and the ThermalEdge Pro, both targeting the sub-5nm fabrication market.
Customer acceptance of the NanoFlux 200 exceeded management's expectations, with 47 units shipped in its first full quarter of commercial availability.
The ThermalEdge Pro received regulatory clearance in the European Union in October 2023, enabling shipments to continental European customers beginning in November.
Park noted that the company's strong free cash flow generation of $312 million supported both the dividend program and share repurchase activity during the year.
Free cash flow conversion, defined as free cash flow divided by net income, came in at approximately 126%, well above the company's three-year average of 108%.
The company's credit ratings were reaffirmed by two major rating agencies, with Moody's maintaining a Baa2 rating and S&P maintaining a BBB rating.
Holloway expressed confidence that fiscal year 2024 would see continued revenue growth, with initial guidance calling for revenue in the range of $1.60 billion to $1.66 billion.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 20% to 21%, reflecting anticipated investment in selling capacity and next-generation R&D programs.
The company expects to hire approximately 500 additional employees in fiscal year 2024, with the majority of new roles concentrated in engineering and field service functions.
Crestfall Dynamics also announced a three-year strategic partnership with Taiwan-based wafer fabricator Everlight Semiconductor to co-develop next-generation deposition tools.
The Everlight partnership is expected to generate incremental revenue of approximately $75 million annually beginning in fiscal year 2025.
The company's supply chain resilience initiatives, launched in 2022, contributed to a reduction in average lead times from 18 weeks to 13 weeks during fiscal year 2023.
Procurement savings from the supply chain program totaled an estimated $23 million in fiscal year 2023, exceeding the original target of $18 million.
Crestfall Dynamics divested its legacy thermal processing services division in August 2023 for proceeds of $47 million, resulting in a pre-tax gain of $14 million.
The divestiture allowed the company to sharpen its strategic focus on capital equipment manufacturing and related software and service offerings.
Service and software revenue grew to $218 million in fiscal year 2023, representing 14.8% of total revenue, up from 12.1% in the prior year.
Holloway stated that the services segment is expected to reach $300 million in annual revenue by fiscal year 2026, driven by an expanding installed base.
The installed base of Crestfall Dynamics equipment grew to approximately 4,100 systems globally by year-end, up from 3,700 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Each incremental installed system generates an average of $53,000 in annual service contract revenue, creating a growing and highly predictable revenue stream.
The company's net promoter score among its top-tier customers improved to 72 in fiscal year 2023, reflecting strong satisfaction with product reliability and application support.
Yuen emphasized that zero safety incidents were recorded at the Monterrey facility throughout its first year of operation, a result attributed to rigorous pre-opening training protocols.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced meaningfully during the year, with the company reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 11% versus the 2021 baseline.
Crestfall Dynamics published its second annual sustainability report in February 2024, outlining progress toward its 2030 targets for emissions reduction and water usage.
The company's board of directors comprises nine members, seven of whom are independent, in accordance with Nasdaq listing standards and internal governance policies.
Audit Committee Chair Patricia Davenport confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year 2023 audit cycle.
Davenport also noted that the Audit Committee conducted a comprehensive review of the company's revenue recognition practices in light of evolving ASC 606 interpretive guidance.
The review concluded that Crestfall Dynamics' existing revenue recognition policies were appropriate and required no material adjustments or restatements.
Total stockholders' equity at December 31, 2023 stood at $1.12 billion, compared to $963 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was 23.6%, a notable improvement from 20.8% in fiscal year 2022, reflecting both higher earnings and efficient equity deployment.
Park underscored that the company's conservative financial management philosophy has positioned Crestfall Dynamics well to navigate any potential slowdown in global semiconductor capital spending.
Looking ahead, management remains focused on deepening customer relationships, accelerating service revenue growth, and delivering consistent shareholder returns.
The company will host its annual investor day on April 18, 2024, where detailed long-term financial targets and technology roadmap updates will be presented to the investment community.
Crestfall Dynamics Corporation remains committed to its mission of enabling the next generation of semiconductor manufacturing through innovative, reliable, and cost-effective equipment solutions.
Veltrano Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap biotechnology and specialty pharmaceuticals company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
Founded in 2009, Veltrano focuses on developing targeted oncology therapies and rare disease treatments for global markets.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Mirela Ostroff presented the following annual report to shareholders for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Quispe oversaw the preparation of the audited financial statements included herein.
Veltrano reported total net revenue of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year.
The company's flagship oncology product, Veltraxin, contributed $920 million of that revenue, reinforcing its position as the top-selling asset in the portfolio.
Veltrano concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final tranche of senior notes in March of that year.
The rare disease segment posted revenue of $412 million, driven by strong uptake of Syndramab in pediatric patient populations across the United States and Europe.
Operating expenses totaled $1.21 billion, reflecting increased investment in late-stage clinical programs and expanded commercial infrastructure.
Research and development expenditures alone reached $487 million, equivalent to approximately 26.5% of total net revenue.
Gross margin improved by 180 basis points year-over-year, reaching 71.3%, aided by favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies at the Durham facility.
In September 2023, Veltrano secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of lenders led by First Meridian Capital Partners to support strategic expansion initiatives.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $723 million, reflecting investments in the commercial teams supporting both the oncology and rare disease divisions.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $214 million, or $1.87 per diluted share, compared to $178 million, or $1.56 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $498 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 27.1%.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023, totaled $623 million, up from $541 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Veltrano generated $389 million in operating cash flow during fiscal year 2023, reflecting disciplined working capital management.
Capital expenditures amounted to $134 million, primarily directed toward expanding the Durham manufacturing site and upgrading laboratory infrastructure in the Triangle Park campus.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, consistent with the company's progressive dividend policy.
Total dividends paid during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $82 million, reflecting the company's commitment to returning capital to shareholders.
Veltrano's headcount grew to 5,340 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, up from 4,890 employees at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company added approximately 450 net new employees during 2023, with the majority of hires concentrated in medical affairs and clinical operations.
International revenues accounted for 38% of total net revenue, or approximately $699 million, representing growth of 21% on a constant-currency basis.
Europe remained the largest international market, contributing $412 million, while Asia-Pacific revenues grew 34% to reach $179 million.
Veltrano received European Medicines Agency approval for Syndramab in three additional rare disease indications during the fourth quarter of 2023.
The company also submitted a supplemental Biologics License Application to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for an expanded Veltraxin indication in non-small cell lung cancer.
Dr. Ostroff stated that the pipeline remains the primary engine of long-term value creation, with seven assets currently in Phase II or Phase III clinical trials.
Veltrano's pipeline includes two gene therapy candidates, VT-701 and VT-802, each targeting distinct ultra-rare metabolic disorders with significant unmet medical need.
VT-701 reported a 68% overall response rate in the ongoing Phase II CLARITY trial, exceeding the prespecified efficacy threshold.
Phase III enrollment for VT-802 is on track to complete by mid-2024, with topline data anticipated in the first half of 2025.
The company entered into a collaboration agreement with Hanseatic Therapeutics GmbH in October 2023, granting co-development rights to VT-802 in European markets.
Under that agreement, Veltrano received an upfront payment of $45 million and is eligible for up to $310 million in future milestone payments.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $198 million, representing approximately 60 days of supply, which management considers appropriate given current demand patterns.
Accounts receivable were $267 million at December 31, 2023, with days sales outstanding of 53 days, broadly consistent with prior year levels.
The company's tax provision for fiscal year 2023 was $61 million, reflecting an effective tax rate of 22.2%, down from 24.1% in fiscal year 2022.
The reduction in effective tax rate was attributable to incremental R&D tax credits and a favorable jurisdictional mix of earnings.
Veltrano repurchased 3.1 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $54.70 per share, for a total of approximately $170 million.
As of December 31, 2023, approximately $230 million remained available under the existing share repurchase authorization approved by the board in February 2022.
Total stockholders' equity was $1.47 billion at fiscal year-end, compared to $1.31 billion at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Veltrano's balance sheet reflects a strong equity foundation that underpins continued investment across clinical and commercial operations.
The company's return on invested capital was 12.8% for fiscal year 2023, an improvement of 140 basis points relative to fiscal year 2022.
Goodwill and intangible assets totaled $934 million at December 31, 2023, largely attributable to the 2019 acquisition of Orelio Pharma.
Amortization of acquired intangible assets was $72 million for the year, in line with management's published amortization schedule.
Mr. Quispe noted that the company does not expect significant changes to its amortization profile through 2026 absent further acquisitions.
Veltrano's audit was conducted by Halloway & Brennan LLP, which issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
The auditors identified no material weaknesses in Veltrano's internal controls over financial reporting for the period under review.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal year 2024 guidance for total net revenue in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.13 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is expected to be in the range of $550 million to $580 million, implying continued margin expansion.
The guidance assumes continued commercial momentum for Veltraxin and Syndramab, along with modest contributions from potential new product launches.
Veltrano also expects to initiate two Phase I first-in-human studies in 2024, targeting novel immuno-oncology mechanisms identified through its internal discovery platform.
Management remains committed to a disciplined capital allocation framework that balances internal investment, strategic business development, and shareholder returns.
The company ended 2023 with no off-balance-sheet financing arrangements that would materially affect its reported financial position.
Veltrano's environmental, social, and governance program achieved a 15% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to the 2020 baseline.
The company donated $9.2 million to patient advocacy organizations and rare disease research initiatives during fiscal year 2023.
Dr. Ostroff concluded by expressing confidence in Veltrano's ability to deliver sustainable growth through a differentiated pipeline and a focused commercial strategy.
The board of directors and executive leadership team thank shareholders, patients, healthcare providers, and employees for their continued trust and partnership.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied herein.
Additional information regarding risk factors is provided in Veltrano Biosciences' Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Veltara Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by Chief Executive Officer Marcus Delray and has since grown to serve clients across 34 countries.
This annual report covers fiscal year 2023, ending December 31, and reflects the company's continued evolution in a competitive technology landscape.
Veltara Systems reported total revenue of $748 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Nandakumar noted that recurring subscription revenue now constitutes 81% of total revenue, up from 74% in fiscal 2022.
The company's gross margin expanded by 210 basis points year-over-year, reaching 67.3% in fiscal 2023.
Operating expenses totaled $412 million, driven primarily by continued investments in research and development and sales infrastructure.
Research and development spending alone reached $134 million, representing approximately 17.9% of total revenue for the fiscal year.
Veltara Systems employed 4,200 full-time equivalents as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a net headcount increase of 380 employees during the year.
The company carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of the fiscal year-end closing date of December 31, 2023.
Net income for fiscal 2023 was $89 million, compared to $61 million in fiscal 2022, representing a year-over-year increase of approximately 46%.
Earnings per diluted share came in at $2.14, exceeding consensus analyst estimates of $1.97 by a meaningful margin.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares during the fourth quarter under its existing buyback authorization program.
Cash and cash equivalents stood at $310 million at year-end, providing what management described as a strong liquidity cushion.
During fiscal 2023, Veltara Systems secured a $250 million revolving credit facility from a syndicate of institutional lenders to fund strategic acquisitions.
The company's deferred revenue balance grew to $198 million, indicating robust future contracted obligations from enterprise customers.
Customer retention rates remained elevated at 94%, consistent with prior-year performance and reflective of Veltara's platform stickiness.
The company added 310 new enterprise accounts during fiscal 2023, bringing the total active customer base to approximately 2,850 clients.
Average contract value for new enterprise deals increased to $310,000, up 8% from $287,000 in the prior year.
Veltara's flagship product, OptiChain Pro, underwent a major version upgrade in the second quarter, introducing advanced machine learning forecasting capabilities.
The product launch was supported by a dedicated go-to-market campaign that included regional summits in seven major cities across North America and Europe.
Chief Revenue Officer Sandra Okonkwo attributed the strong new logo performance to OptiChain Pro's improved integration with SAP and Oracle ERP systems.
International revenue grew 18.7% year-over-year and now represents 38% of total company revenue, up from 34% in fiscal 2022.
The EMEA region was the fastest-growing geography, posting revenue growth of 24% compared to the prior fiscal year.
Veltara Systems made one bolt-on acquisition during fiscal 2023, acquiring Fennwick Analytics Ltd. for $42 million in an all-cash transaction.
Fennwick Analytics, a UK-based firm specializing in demand sensing algorithms, contributed $7 million in revenue in the partial year following the acquisition.
The integration of Fennwick's technology into the OptiChain platform is expected to be completed by the end of the first quarter of fiscal 2024.
Veltara's board of directors approved an increase in the company's quarterly dividend from $0.12 to $0.15 per share, effective Q1 fiscal 2024.
The increased dividend reflects management's confidence in the sustainability of the company's free cash flow generation over the medium term.
Free cash flow for fiscal 2023 totaled $142 million, representing a free cash flow margin of approximately 19%.
Capital expenditures were $31 million in fiscal 2023, primarily allocated to data center infrastructure upgrades and leasehold improvements.
The company's tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.4%, slightly below management's initial guidance range of 22% to 24%.
Veltara Systems recognized $14 million in stock-based compensation expense during the fiscal year, consistent with prior-year levels.
Days sales outstanding improved by three days year-over-year, falling to 47 days as of December 31, 2023.
Inventory of physical hardware components, used in on-premise deployment options, declined to $8 million from $12 million in the prior year.
The reduction in hardware inventory reflects Veltara's strategic pivot toward fully cloud-hosted delivery, reducing reliance on physical appliances.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $111 million, yielding an operating margin of 14.8%, up from 12.3% in fiscal 2022.
Management guided for fiscal 2024 revenue in the range of $835 million to $855 million, implying growth of approximately 11% to 14%.
The fiscal 2024 guidance assumes continued international expansion and an acceleration in mid-market customer acquisition.
Veltara expects its operating margin to expand by an additional 50 to 100 basis points in fiscal 2024 as operating leverage improves.
The company anticipates that research and development expenditure as a percentage of revenue will remain in the 17% to 19% range.
Ms. Nandakumar stated that the company's capital allocation priorities for fiscal 2024 include organic investment, targeted M&A, and shareholder returns.
Veltara Systems is rated BBB+ by Standard & Poor's and Baa1 by Moody's, maintaining investment-grade status across major rating agencies.
The company's interest income of $11 million in fiscal 2023 was derived entirely from short-duration money market instruments and Treasury bills.
No impairment charges were recognized during fiscal 2023 with respect to goodwill or other intangible assets acquired in prior transactions.
Goodwill on the balance sheet as of year-end totaled $163 million, largely attributable to the 2021 acquisition of RouteLogic Corp.
The RouteLogic integration has been fully completed and is no longer reported as a separate operating segment effective January 1, 2023.
Veltara Systems operates as a single reportable segment following the consolidation of its North American and International divisions in early 2023.
The board currently comprises nine directors, five of whom are independent, consistent with corporate governance requirements under Nasdaq listing standards.
Audit Committee Chair Diana Felsworthy noted that the company's internal controls over financial reporting received an unqualified opinion from external auditors.
The company's external auditor, Hargrove & Pettigrew LLP, has served in that capacity since fiscal 2016.
Total stockholders' equity as of December 31, 2023 was $534 million, an increase of $92 million compared to the prior year-end balance.
Accumulated other comprehensive loss of $18 million primarily reflects foreign currency translation adjustments related to international subsidiaries.
Veltara Systems has not issued any preferred stock and has no warrants or convertible instruments outstanding as of the reporting date.
The weighted-average diluted share count for fiscal 2023 was 41.6 million shares, reflecting the impact of the fourth-quarter share repurchase.
Looking ahead, Mr. Delray expressed confidence in Veltara's competitive positioning, citing proprietary AI capabilities as a key differentiator.
The company plans to increase its sales headcount by approximately 15% in fiscal 2024, focused primarily on enterprise account executives.
Veltara Systems will host its annual Investor Day on March 14, 2024, in New York City, where it will provide updated long-range financial targets.
Management expects long-range revenue to exceed $1.2 billion by fiscal 2026, contingent on continued execution of its platform expansion strategy.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those anticipated.
Investors are encouraged to review the risk factors section of the company's Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Veldora Precision Systems, Inc. is a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, specializing in robotic assembly and quality-control sensor technology.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Holloway and CTO Desmond Firth, both veterans of the aerospace manufacturing sector.
Fiscal year 2023 marked the company's fourteenth year of consecutive revenue growth, a milestone celebrated at the annual shareholder meeting in March.
Total consolidated revenue for FY2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the $1.64 billion reported in FY2022.
The Robotic Assembly segment contributed $1.02 billion of total revenue, maintaining its position as the company's largest operating division.
The Sensor Technology segment generated $820 million in revenue, driven primarily by strong demand from automotive and semiconductor clients.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $612 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 33.3%, up from 31.8% in the prior year.
CEO Margaret Holloway attributed the margin improvement to a combination of favorable raw material pricing and improved manufacturing throughput at the Durham facility.
Operating expenses, including research and development and selling, general and administrative costs, totaled $389 million for FY2023.
Research and development spending increased by 18% year-over-year to $154 million, reflecting the company's ongoing commitment to next-generation sensor miniaturization.
Selling, general and administrative expenses were $235 million, essentially flat compared to $231 million in FY2022 despite revenue expansion.
Operating income for the year was $223 million, corresponding to an operating margin of 12.1%, compared to 10.7% in FY2022.
Net income attributable to Veldora shareholders was $168 million, or $3.42 per diluted share, compared to $139 million, or $2.83 per diluted share, in FY2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for FY2023 was $298 million, representing a margin of 16.2%, which management views as the most relevant profitability metric.
The Board of Directors declared four quarterly cash dividends during FY2023 totaling $0.88 per share, consistent with the prior year's payout.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $97 million, directed principally at expanding robotic cell capacity at the Raleigh and Greenville plants.
Free cash flow was $201 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $298 million less capital expenditures of $97 million.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares under its authorized buyback program at an average price of $61.40 per share, totaling approximately $73.7 million.
As of December 31, 2023, total shares outstanding were 48.6 million, down from 49.8 million at the start of the fiscal year.
Cash and cash equivalents on the balance sheet stood at $437 million as of year-end, up from $312 million at the close of FY2022.
Accounts receivable were $284 million, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding figure of 56 days, slightly improved from 59 days in the prior year.
Inventory levels finished the year at $193 million, representing approximately 68 days of cost of goods sold, a modest reduction from 74 days in FY2022.
Total assets were $2.31 billion as of December 31, 2023, compared to $2.07 billion a year earlier.
Total liabilities stood at $1.02 billion, with long-term debt comprising $540 million of that figure.
Long-term debt consisted primarily of a $400 million senior unsecured note due 2028, issued in April 2021 at a fixed coupon of 3.45%.
The remaining $140 million in long-term debt related to a revolving credit facility drawn down to fund the acquisition of NanoSense Technologies in June 2022.
Shareholders' equity at year-end was $1.29 billion, reflecting retained earnings growth offset partially by the share repurchase activity.
The net debt-to-adjusted-EBITDA ratio improved to 0.35x at December 31, 2023, from 0.61x at December 31, 2022, demonstrating meaningful deleveraging.
CFO Priya Anand stated that the company's strengthened balance sheet positions Veldora well to pursue strategic acquisitions in the robotics value chain.
Veldora's geographic revenue split in FY2023 was approximately 58% North America, 27% Europe, and 15% Asia-Pacific.
European revenue grew 21% year-over-year, buoyed by new contracts with two German automotive OEMs for precision welding automation systems.
Asia-Pacific revenue growth was more modest at 8%, reflecting competitive pricing pressures in the South Korean semiconductor equipment market.
The company's customer concentration remained manageable, with the top five clients accounting for 31% of total revenue in FY2023.
Veldora added 47 new enterprise customers during the year, the highest annual figure in the company's history.
Employee headcount at year-end was 6,840 full-time equivalents, an increase of 430 from the 6,410 reported at the end of FY2022.
The company invested $22 million in workforce training and upskilling programs, a 35% increase over the $16.3 million spent in FY2022.
Attrition in the engineering workforce declined to 8.2% in FY2023 from 11.5% in FY2022, which management linked to improved compensation and flexible work arrangements.
Veldora launched its VX-9 Series robotic cell platform in September 2023, targeting high-throughput electronics assembly applications.
Initial customer response to the VX-9 Series has been strong, with 14 units ordered by year-end at an average selling price of $2.1 million per unit.
The Sensor Technology segment introduced two new product families in the second half of FY2023: the MicroGuard 400 proximity sensor and the ThermalEdge 200 thermal imaging module.
Both new sensor products achieved design-wins at three major tier-one automotive suppliers, with volume production ramp expected in the first quarter of FY2024.
Management provided FY2024 guidance calling for revenue in the range of $2.00 billion to $2.08 billion, implying growth of approximately 9% to 13% at the midpoint.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for FY2024 was set at $325 million to $345 million, reflecting continued margin expansion from operational leverage and mix shift.
The company noted that its backlog at December 31, 2023, stood at $912 million, providing strong near-term revenue visibility for the coming fiscal year.
Veldora's supply chain risk management team completed a comprehensive vendor diversification initiative in FY2023, reducing single-source component dependencies from 38% to 21% of total inputs.
By the close of FY2023, the company had fully retired all outstanding debt obligations, ending the year with a completely unencumbered balance sheet free of any borrowings.
Environmental, social, and governance performance continued to advance, with Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions declining 14% versus the FY2022 baseline.
The company achieved an 18% reduction in energy intensity per unit of production, supported by the installation of on-site solar arrays at both the Raleigh and Greenville facilities.
Veldora was recognized by three independent sustainability rating agencies as a top-quartile performer in its industrial peer group for environmental stewardship.
Board composition was refreshed in FY2023 with the addition of two new independent directors, bringing total board size to nine members and increasing female representation to 44%.
In February 2024, Veldora secured a new $300 million term loan from a syndicate of six commercial banks to fund the planned expansion of its Asian manufacturing footprint.

Management expressed confidence that the disciplined capital allocation strategy, rigorous cost controls, and expanding customer relationships will support sustained double-digit earnings growth through FY2026.
Veralux Photonics Corporation, a leading developer of advanced optical sensing systems, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, operates across three primary segments: industrial sensing, consumer optics, and defense systems.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Mara Ellington opened her letter to shareholders by noting the company's continued expansion in emerging markets.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year's $1.64 billion.
Gross profit climbed to $712 million, reflecting a gross margin of approximately 38.7%, up from 36.2% in fiscal 2022.
The industrial sensing segment remained the company's largest revenue contributor, generating $874 million in annual sales.
Consumer optics sales grew 18% year-over-year, reaching $521 million by year-end.
Defense systems revenues, while the smallest segment, posted a record $445 million, driven by two major government contracts awarded in Q2.
Chief Financial Officer Thomas Breck highlighted disciplined cost management as a key driver of margin improvement throughout the year.
Operating expenses excluding cost of goods sold totaled $438 million, down from $461 million in the prior fiscal year.
Research and development spending reached $187 million, representing approximately 10.2% of total revenues, consistent with the company's long-term investment thesis.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses came in at $251 million, a reduction of $34 million compared to the previous year.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $274 million, compared to $218 million in fiscal 2022, representing a 25.7% year-over-year improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $198 million, or $3.42 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $161 million, or $2.79 per diluted share, recorded in fiscal 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year stood at $341 million, reflecting an EBITDA margin of 18.5%.
The company's balance sheet remained robust as of December 31, 2023, with total assets of $2.93 billion.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end were $387 million, up from $294 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Accounts receivable stood at $329 million, reflecting strong collections performance with days sales outstanding improving to 65 days from 71 days.
Inventory levels were carefully managed throughout the year, ending at $214 million compared to $231 million at the start of the period.
Total current assets amounted to $1.04 billion, while total current liabilities were $498 million, yielding a current ratio of approximately 2.09.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $143 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's manufacturing facility in San Marcos, Texas.
The San Marcos facility expansion added approximately 180,000 square feet of cleanroom production space, increasing total manufacturing capacity by 22%.
Free cash flow for the year was $244 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $387 million less capital expenditures of $143 million.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share beginning in Q1 2024, reflecting confidence in the company's cash generation outlook.
This quarterly dividend represents an annualized payout of $0.72 per share, or roughly a 1.4% yield based on the December 29, 2023 closing stock price of $51.40.
Share repurchases during fiscal 2023 totaled $95 million, retiring approximately 1.9 million shares at an average price of $50.00.
The company's long-term debt as of December 31, 2023, consisted solely of $300 million in senior unsecured notes due in 2028.
These notes carry a fixed coupon of 4.25% and were originally issued in March 2021 to fund the acquisition of NovaSense Technologies.

Veralux Photonics carried no revolving credit facility balance at year-end, with management confirming the company had fully repaid all borrowings and maintained a completely undrawn credit line with zero outstanding debt beyond the 2028 notes.
Headcount across the organization grew to 6,840 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, an increase of 420 employees from the prior year-end total.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in engineering and manufacturing roles, which together accounted for 73% of all new hires.
Veralux also welcomed Dr. Priya Nandakumar as Chief Technology Officer in July 2023, a hire Dr. Ellington described as transformational for the company's product roadmap.
Dr. Nandakumar previously served as Vice President of Photonic Integration at Lumara Systems, where she led development of the industry-acclaimed Spectrum-7 sensor array.

During the fourth quarter of 2023, Veralux secured a $175 million revolving credit facility from a consortium of six banks, increasing the company's total financial obligations and providing additional liquidity headroom for potential acquisitions.
The industrial sensing segment's operating margin improved to 24.1% in fiscal 2023, up from 21.8% in fiscal 2022, driven by automation investments and favorable product mix.
Consumer optics faced some margin pressure mid-year due to component shortages, though the segment recovered in Q4 to end the year at an 18.3% operating margin.
Defense systems achieved the highest segment operating margin at 31.6%, benefiting from cost-plus contract structures and high-value proprietary components.
The company launched four new products during fiscal 2023, including the LuminaEdge Pro industrial scanner and the VisionCore 900 medical imaging module.
International revenues accounted for 34% of total sales, or approximately $626 million, with Europe representing the largest international market at $271 million.
Asia-Pacific revenues grew 27% year-over-year to $218 million, with notable strength in Japan, South Korea, and Singapore.
Management has set a revenue growth target of 9% to 12% for fiscal 2024, implying guidance of approximately $2.01 billion to $2.06 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set in the range of 18.5% to 19.5%, reflecting continued operating leverage expectations.
The company anticipates capital expenditures in the range of $155 million to $170 million in fiscal 2024 as the San Marcos expansion enters its final phase.
Dr. Ellington noted that the company's competitive positioning in LiDAR-based industrial sensing has strengthened considerably following a series of patent awards in 2023.
Veralux was granted 47 new patents during fiscal 2023, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 614 patents across 23 countries.
The company's environmental, social, and governance program achieved a 14% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to the fiscal 2021 baseline.
Veralux was named to the Sustainability Leaders Index for the second consecutive year, recognizing its progress in reducing energy consumption at manufacturing facilities.
Mr. Breck indicated that pension obligations were fully funded as of year-end, with the plan's funded status at 103%, eliminating the need for mandatory contributions in fiscal 2024.
The company's audit committee, chaired by independent director Susan Okafor, confirmed that KPMG LLP completed its annual audit with an unqualified opinion.
Subsequent to year-end, Veralux announced a definitive agreement to acquire OptiSync Dynamics for $220 million in cash, expected to close in Q2 2024.
The OptiSync acquisition is projected to add approximately $85 million in annualized revenues and be accretive to adjusted earnings per share within 12 months of closing.
Management emphasized that the acquisition aligns with the company's strategy of deepening its presence in high-growth fiber-optic sensing applications.

In closing, Dr. Ellington expressed strong optimism about fiscal 2024, citing a robust pipeline, strong balance sheet, and a talented team positioned to deliver sustained long-term value for shareholders.
The full financial statements, including consolidated balance sheets, income statements, statements of cash flows, and notes thereto, are presented in the accompanying sections of this annual report.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap pharmaceutical and biotechnology company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
Founded in 2009, Veltara specializes in developing targeted therapies for rare metabolic disorders and autoimmune conditions.
The following excerpt is drawn from Veltara's Annual Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Priya Nanthakumar opened the shareholder letter by noting that fiscal 2023 represented a transformational year for the organization.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year's $741.9 million.
Product sales accounted for $712.6 million of that total, driven primarily by the continued commercial success of Veltara's lead asset, Axinovrel.
Collaboration and licensing revenue contributed an additional $134.7 million, reflecting new partnership agreements signed with two European biopharmaceutical firms.
Gross profit for the period was $541.8 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 63.9%, which exceeded the company's internal target of 62.0%.
Research and development expenses totaled $198.4 million, representing 23.4% of total revenue and reflecting increased investment in the company's Phase III pipeline.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses came in at $143.2 million, down slightly from $149.7 million in the prior fiscal year.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its outstanding $150 million senior secured term loan in March 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $389.5 million, providing the company with substantial liquidity for near-term operations and strategic initiatives.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Ellenbogen stated that the company's fortress balance sheet positions Veltara to pursue both organic and inorganic growth opportunities without financial constraint.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $47.1 million, primarily related to the expansion of Veltara's manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina.
The Durham facility expansion is expected to increase annual production capacity for Axinovrel by approximately 35% when fully operational in mid-2024.
In October 2023, Veltara's board of directors approved a $300 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of eight financial institutions to support potential acquisitions.
Net income attributable to common stockholders was $142.6 million for fiscal 2023, compared to $98.4 million in fiscal 2022, representing a 44.9% year-over-year improvement.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal 2023 were $2.87, up from $1.99 in the prior year, reflecting both earnings growth and a modest reduction in diluted share count.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares during fiscal 2023 under its previously authorized $100 million share repurchase program.
As of year-end, approximately $34.7 million of buyback authorization remained available under that program.
Axinovrel generated net product revenues of $498.3 million in fiscal 2023, securing its position as Veltara's highest-grossing commercial product for the third consecutive year.
The therapy saw strong volume growth in both the United States, which contributed $361.4 million, and in international markets, which contributed $136.9 million.
Veltara's second commercial product, Duraximab, contributed $214.3 million in net product revenues during the fiscal year, reflecting its first full year of commercialization.
Duraximab received FDA approval in November 2022 for the treatment of moderate-to-severe relapsing polychondritis, a rare autoimmune disorder affecting connective tissue.
Dr. Nanthakumar highlighted that Duraximab's market penetration exceeded internal forecasts by approximately 18% in its first full year on the market.
The pipeline advanced meaningfully in fiscal 2023, with three compounds progressing from Phase II to Phase III development.
VLT-447, a novel small-molecule inhibitor targeting the NLRP3 inflammasome pathway, is the most advanced of these newly promoted assets.
A pivotal Phase III trial for VLT-447, enrolling approximately 640 patients across 78 clinical sites in North America and Europe, was initiated in September 2023.
Veltara also announced a strategic collaboration with Genova Therapeutics AG in June 2023, granting Genova exclusive commercialization rights for Axinovrel in five Central European markets.
That deal generated a $75 million upfront payment and could yield up to $220 million in future milestone payments contingent on regulatory and commercial targets.
The Genova collaboration accounted for a significant portion of the $134.7 million in collaboration and licensing revenue recorded in fiscal 2023.
Headcount at Veltara grew from 2,104 full-time employees at the end of fiscal 2022 to 2,391 full-time employees at the end of fiscal 2023.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in commercial operations, medical affairs, and clinical development functions.
Employee-related costs, including salaries, benefits, and stock-based compensation, totaled $287.6 million in fiscal 2023, versus $251.3 million in fiscal 2022.
Stock-based compensation expense alone was $41.8 million, reflecting the issuance of performance-based restricted stock units tied to pipeline milestones.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 18.3%, benefiting from the utilization of research and development tax credits and foreign-derived intangible income deductions.
Deferred tax assets, net of valuation allowances, increased to $62.4 million at year-end from $48.9 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Accounts receivable at December 31, 2023 were $104.7 million, representing approximately 44 days of sales outstanding, an improvement from 52 days in the prior year.
Inventories totaled $88.3 million at year-end, reflecting a deliberate build-up of finished goods in advance of anticipated demand growth for both Axinovrel and Duraximab.
The company's Days Inventory Outstanding improved to 61 days from 74 days in fiscal 2022, demonstrating better supply chain discipline.
Accounts receivable at year-end were consistent with the company's standard payment terms of net 45 days offered to wholesale distributors.
Total assets at December 31, 2023 were $1.42 billion, up from $1.19 billion at the end of fiscal 2022.
Stockholders' equity grew to $894.7 million from $738.2 million, primarily driven by net income partially offset by share repurchases and dividends.
Veltara declared quarterly cash dividends totaling $0.60 per share for fiscal 2023, representing the company's fourth consecutive year of dividend payments.
The dividend payout ratio for fiscal 2023 was approximately 20.9%, which management considers sustainable given projected earnings growth.
Operating cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $221.7 million, and free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $174.6 million.
Free cash flow conversion, measured as free cash flow divided by net income, was approximately 122.4%, reflecting favorable working capital movements.
Looking ahead, Veltara provided fiscal 2024 guidance calling for total revenue in the range of $960 million to $990 million.
The midpoint of that revenue guidance implies approximately 16.3% growth over the fiscal 2023 base.
Management expects gross margin to remain in the 63% to 65% range as manufacturing efficiencies from the Durham expansion are gradually realized.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $295 million to $315 million, representing the first time the company has guided to this metric publicly.
The company noted that its fiscal 2024 outlook does not include any contribution from potential business development transactions.
Mr. Ellenbogen reiterated that Veltara's capital allocation priorities remain organic investment, selective acquisitions, and return of capital to shareholders, in that order.
Veltara's Board of Directors approved a new $150 million share repurchase authorization in January 2024, supplementing the remaining capacity under the prior program.
The company's common stock is listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLTR.
For fiscal 2023, VLTR shares appreciated approximately 31.4%, outperforming the Nasdaq Biotechnology Index, which gained 7.8% over the same period.
Veltara will host its fiscal 2023 earnings conference call on February 22, 2024, with members of the senior leadership team available to address investor questions.
Additional financial tables, including a reconciliation of GAAP to non-GAAP measures, are provided in the appendices to this Annual Report.
Veltara's independent registered public accounting firm, Hartwell & Doran LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap pharmaceutical company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, specializing in next-generation oncology therapeutics.
The company was founded in 2009 by Dr. Margot Ellison and has since grown to become a recognized name in targeted cancer therapy development.
Fiscal year 2023 marked a pivotal year for Veltara as the company navigated both clinical milestones and competitive market pressures.
Total net revenue for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023 reached $847 million, representing an 18% increase over the prior year.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Harlan Voss attributed the revenue growth primarily to the expanded commercial rollout of the company's flagship drug, Celtavix.
Veltara ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having fully retired its outstanding obligations ahead of schedule in the third quarter.
The company's gross margin improved to 68.4%, up from 64.1% in fiscal year 2022, reflecting favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies.
Research and development expenditures totaled $214 million, accounting for approximately 25% of total net revenue.
Chief Financial Officer Ms. Priya Nandakumar noted that disciplined cost management allowed R&D investment to grow without sacrificing profitability.
Operating income rose to $189 million, compared to $142 million in the prior fiscal year, representing a 33% year-over-year improvement.
In November 2023, Veltara entered into a $350 million senior secured credit agreement with a syndicate of lenders led by Meridian Capital Group.
The company repurchased approximately 3.2 million shares of its common stock during fiscal 2023 under its existing buyback program.
These repurchases were executed at an average price of $41.17 per share, resulting in a total outlay of approximately $131.7 million.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal 2023 stood at $2.84, up from $2.19 in fiscal 2022.
Total headcount as of December 31, 2023 was 4,820 employees, an increase of 9% relative to the prior year-end figure of 4,423.
The company expanded its commercial infrastructure in Europe, adding sales force personnel in Germany, France, and the Netherlands.
Veltara's oncology pipeline includes six clinical-stage compounds, three of which are in Phase 3 trials as of the reporting date.
The most advanced pipeline candidate, VLT-119, demonstrated a statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival in its pivotal Phase 3 study.
Top-line data from the VLT-119 trial were announced in September 2023, triggering a 14% single-day appreciation in the company's share price.
Dr. Voss expressed confidence that a regulatory submission for VLT-119 would be filed with the FDA no later than the second quarter of 2024.
The company's cash and cash equivalents balance as of December 31, 2023 was $312 million, compared to $278 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Short-term investments, primarily consisting of U.S. Treasury securities, totaled an additional $95 million.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 amounted to $67 million, directed largely toward expansion of the company's biologics manufacturing facility in Durham.
The Durham facility expansion is expected to increase annual production capacity by approximately 40% upon completion in mid-2025.
Ms. Nandakumar indicated that the company expects capital expenditure intensity to remain elevated through fiscal 2024 as the facility buildout continues.
Accounts receivable stood at $143 million at year-end, with days sales outstanding of 62 days, consistent with industry norms for specialty pharmaceuticals.
Inventory levels were $88 million, reflecting strategic safety stock built ahead of the anticipated Celtavix label expansion approval.
The company does not currently pay a cash dividend, preferring to reinvest available capital into pipeline development and commercial infrastructure.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $198 million, representing 23.4% of net revenue.
This SG&A ratio compares favorably to the peer group median of approximately 27%, underscoring Veltara's operational leverage.
Net income attributable to common shareholders for fiscal 2023 was $158 million, an improvement of $38 million relative to fiscal 2022.
The effective tax rate for the year was 21.3%, modestly above the prior year rate of 20.8%, due in part to stock-based compensation deduction limitations.
Veltara's market capitalization as of December 31, 2023 was approximately $2.1 billion based on the closing share price of $43.60.
The company's stock is listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLTR.
Institutional ownership of VLTR shares stood at approximately 74% of the total shares outstanding as of the latest proxy filing.
The Board of Directors authorized a new share repurchase program in December 2023, permitting buybacks of up to $200 million over the subsequent 24 months.
Veltara also recorded a one-time litigation settlement charge of $22 million in the fourth quarter related to a patent dispute with Taraxis Pharma Corp.
Excluding this charge, adjusted operating income for fiscal 2023 would have been $211 million.
Dr. Ellison, who serves as Executive Chairwoman, highlighted the settlement as a final resolution to a dispute that had created regulatory uncertainty since 2021.
The company's net leverage ratio, defined as net debt divided by adjusted EBITDA, stood at 0.4 times at the end of fiscal 2022.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for net revenue in the range of $960 million to $1.01 billion.
This guidance assumes continued Celtavix volume growth, a modest contribution from the potential label expansion, and stable pricing in core markets.
Management also guided for adjusted diluted EPS in the range of $3.15 to $3.35 for fiscal 2024.
The company expects its adjusted operating margin to expand by approximately 100 to 150 basis points in fiscal 2024.
Veltara's global market share in the HER2-positive non-small cell lung cancer segment reached an estimated 11.3% in 2023, up from 8.7% the prior year.
This share gain was driven by favorable clinical data, expanded formulary access, and the company's growing medical affairs team.
The company entered into a co-promotion agreement with Synthenix Therapeutics in August 2023 to jointly commercialize Celtavix in select Asia-Pacific markets.
Under the terms of the agreement, Veltara retains full economic rights to Celtavix while Synthenix provides regional distribution and regulatory support.
The Asia-Pacific initiative is expected to contribute incremental revenue beginning in the second half of fiscal 2024.
Veltara's defined-contribution retirement plan expenses totaled $18.4 million in fiscal 2023, reflecting the higher employee base.
Stock-based compensation expense was $31 million for the year, consistent with the prior year level of $29 million.
Amortization of acquired intangible assets totaled $14 million, unchanged from fiscal 2022, as no new intangible assets were acquired during the year.
The company believes it is well positioned to deliver sustainable long-term shareholder value through disciplined capital allocation and continued pipeline advancement.
Veltara's audit committee has confirmed that the financial statements were prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles and audited by Deloitte & Touche LLP.
Veltora Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Holloway and CTO Rafael Quintero, both veterans of the logistics technology sector.
Veltora Systems serves over 1,200 enterprise clients across North America, Europe, and Southeast Asia as of fiscal year 2023.
The company's flagship product, ChainSync Pro, accounts for approximately 68% of total annual recurring revenue.
Chief Financial Officer David Answorth presented the full-year earnings summary to the board of directors on February 14, 2024.
Veltora Systems reported total revenue of $487.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 19.4% increase over the prior year.
