Subscription revenue grew to $412.6 million, up from $338.1 million in fiscal year 2022, driven by strong enterprise contract renewals.
Professional services revenue contributed $74.7 million, reflecting an expanded consulting and implementation practice.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 reached $301.2 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 61.8%.
Operating expenses totaled $224.8 million, inclusive of research and development, sales and marketing, and general and administrative costs.
Research and development spending rose to $89.3 million, representing 18.3% of total revenue, as the company accelerated its AI-integration roadmap.
Sales and marketing expenditures came in at $96.4 million, reflecting investments in brand expansion and new territory acquisition.
General and administrative costs were $39.1 million, modestly higher than the $35.6 million recorded in the prior year.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $76.4 million, compared to $58.9 million in fiscal year 2022, a 29.7% improvement year-over-year.
Net income attributable to Veltora Systems shareholders totaled $54.1 million, equivalent to diluted earnings per share of $1.43.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP metric, came in at $104.7 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 21.5%.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $213.8 million, up from $178.2 million at year-end 2022.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $88.6 million, supported by disciplined working capital management and strong collections.
Days sales outstanding improved to 42 days from 49 days in the prior period, reflecting streamlined billing and payment processes.
Capital expenditures were $14.2 million, primarily allocated to data center infrastructure upgrades and software development tools.
The company repurchased 1.1 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $37.82 per share.
Total shares repurchased under the current buyback program since its inception in 2021 amount to 3.8 million shares.
The board of directors approved an increase in the share repurchase authorization to $150 million in November 2023.
Veltora Systems declared quarterly dividends totaling $0.56 per share for fiscal year 2023, consistent with its capital return policy.
International revenue grew by 27.3% year-over-year, now representing 34% of total revenue, up from 29% in fiscal year 2022.
The APAC region was the fastest-growing geography, with revenue expanding 41.2% driven by new client wins in Singapore and Australia.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltora Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, operating entirely from internally generated cash flows.
Headcount at the end of fiscal year 2023 stood at 2,847 full-time employees, an increase of 312 from the prior year-end.
The company's attrition rate improved to 11.3% in fiscal year 2023, down from 14.6% in fiscal year 2022, indicating stronger employee retention.
Veltora Systems launched two new product modules in the second half of 2023: ChainSync Analytics and ChainSync Compliance.
The ChainSync Analytics module was adopted by 87 enterprise clients within its first 90 days of availability, exceeding internal forecasts.
In March 2023, Veltora Systems secured a $175 million senior secured term loan facility with a syndicate of five institutional lenders to fund strategic acquisitions.
ChainSync Compliance achieved regulatory certification in seven jurisdictions, including the European Union, United Kingdom, and Canada, by year-end 2023.
Customer net revenue retention rate reached 118% for fiscal year 2023, indicating that existing customers expanded their contracts significantly.
The company signed 214 new enterprise contracts in fiscal year 2023, up from 179 in fiscal year 2022, with an average contract value of $1.9 million.
Annual contract value for the top 20 clients averaged $8.7 million, reflecting deepening relationships with strategic accounts.
Veltora Systems was recognized by Gartner as a Leader in the Magic Quadrant for Supply Chain Execution Platforms for the third consecutive year.
The company's partner ecosystem expanded to include 48 certified implementation partners globally, up from 31 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
CFO David Answorth noted that the company's balance sheet strength positions Veltora to pursue selective M&A opportunities in 2024.
Veltora Systems completed the acquisition of Meridian Data Logic in October 2023 for a total consideration of $62.5 million.
Meridian Data Logic contributed $7.4 million in revenue and a $1.2 million operating loss for the period following its acquisition.
The integration of Meridian Data Logic is expected to be completed by the end of the second quarter of fiscal year 2024.
Veltora's cloud infrastructure is hosted across three primary data centers located in Virginia, Amsterdam, and Singapore.
System uptime across all client-facing services averaged 99.97% in fiscal year 2023, exceeding the 99.9% contractual SLA threshold.
The company's NPS score improved to 62 in fiscal year 2023 from 54 in fiscal year 2022, reflecting enhanced customer satisfaction.
CEO Margaret Holloway emphasized during the earnings call that product-led growth remains the cornerstone of Veltora's long-term strategy.
The company guided for fiscal year 2024 total revenue in the range of $565 million to $580 million, implying growth of approximately 16% to 19%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 22% to 23%, reflecting modest operating leverage as the company scales.
The company expects to invest $105 million to $115 million in research and development in fiscal year 2024 to maintain its competitive moat.
Veltora Systems anticipates hiring between 300 and 350 net new employees in fiscal year 2024, primarily in engineering and customer success.
The company's deferred revenue balance grew to $198.3 million as of year-end 2023, providing strong visibility into future recognized revenue.
Remaining performance obligations totaled $934.7 million, of which approximately 58% is expected to be recognized within the next 24 months.
Veltora's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.4%, below the statutory federal rate, due to R&D tax credits and stock-based compensation deductions.
Stock-based compensation expense totaled $28.6 million in fiscal year 2023, recorded primarily within research and development and sales and marketing.
The company has not issued any new equity since its secondary offering in 2020, relying on organic cash flow and existing resources for operations.
Veltora Systems maintains a disciplined approach to financial risk management, including foreign currency hedging for its non-USD revenue streams.
The company's Euro-denominated revenue is hedged at approximately 70% for the next 12 months using forward contracts executed in November 2023.
Total long-term liabilities as reported in the fiscal year 2023 balance sheet amounted to $44.3 million, primarily comprising lease obligations.
Operating lease right-of-use assets totaled $31.7 million, associated with office facilities in Austin, London, and Singapore.
The audit committee of the board of directors confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLC served as the company's independent auditor for fiscal year 2023.
PricewaterhouseCoopers issued an unqualified audit opinion on Veltora Systems' fiscal year 2023 financial statements on February 10, 2024.
The company filed its Annual Report on Form 10-K with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2024.
Management believes the company is well-positioned to deliver sustained value to shareholders through disciplined execution, continued innovation, and strategic market expansion.
Veltora Systems remains committed to its environmental, social, and governance objectives, targeting carbon neutrality in its direct operations by 2027.
The foregoing discussion contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those anticipated.
Veltara Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Marcus Delroy and has grown to serve over 3,400 enterprise clients across 47 countries.
Fiscal year 2023 marked a pivotal chapter in Veltara's operational history, characterized by aggressive product expansion and geographic diversification.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 19.4% increase over the prior year's $709.6 million.
Subscription-based recurring revenue accounted for 74% of total revenue, up from 68% in fiscal year 2022.
The company's flagship product, VeltaFlow Pro, continued to dominate the mid-market segment with a net promoter score of 72.
Chief Financial Officer Sandra Okonkwo highlighted the strength of the company's gross margins during the Q4 earnings call.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 was $541.2 million, translating to a gross margin of approximately 63.9%.
Operating expenses increased by 14.2% year-over-year, driven primarily by investments in research and development.
Research and development expenditures totaled $163.4 million, representing 19.3% of total revenue.
The company's sales and marketing spend reached $201.8 million, reflecting ongoing investment in brand expansion across European markets.
General and administrative costs were $87.6 million, roughly flat compared to $85.1 million in fiscal year 2022.
Operating income for the full year came in at $88.4 million, yielding an operating margin of 10.4%.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $141.7 million, up from $112.3 million in the prior year.
Net income attributable to Veltara shareholders was $61.9 million, compared to $47.2 million in fiscal year 2022.
Earnings per diluted share were $1.43, an improvement from $1.09 reported in the previous fiscal year.
The company's balance sheet remained robust, with cash and cash equivalents totaling $318.5 million as of December 31, 2023.
Accounts receivable stood at $142.7 million, with days sales outstanding improving to 61 days from 68 days in the prior year.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $44.1 million, with the majority allocated to data center infrastructure upgrades.
Free cash flow generation was strong at $174.2 million, providing ample liquidity for strategic initiatives.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltara completed the acquisition of LogiNova Analytics, a predictive logistics startup based in Toronto.
The LogiNova acquisition was valued at $93.5 million and is expected to be accretive to earnings within 18 months.
Veltara's headcount reached 5,847 full-time employees by the end of fiscal year 2023, an increase of 612 from the prior year.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in engineering and customer success roles, reflecting the company's product-first strategy.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Mendes noted that employee retention rates improved to 91% in 2023 from 87% in 2022.
The company launched its new VeltaEdge module in March 2023, targeting real-time inventory visibility for warehouse operators.
VeltaEdge achieved over 480 paid deployments within its first nine months of availability, exceeding initial projections.
International revenue grew to $289.4 million in fiscal year 2023, representing 34.2% of total revenue.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, reflecting a conservative capital structure maintained since 2019.
The company's market share in the North American supply chain software segment expanded to an estimated 8.7%, up from 7.4% in fiscal year 2022.
Veltara's partnership with Oracle Logistics was renewed for a five-year term, providing co-selling opportunities across enterprise accounts.
The company's customer churn rate declined to 4.1% in fiscal year 2023, the lowest level recorded since its IPO in 2015.
Annual contract values for new enterprise customers averaged $312,000 in fiscal year 2023, compared to $274,000 in fiscal year 2022.
Customer expansion revenue, driven by upsells and cross-sells, accounted for 38% of total new bookings during the fiscal year.
In September 2023, Veltara's board of directors approved a $150 million senior secured revolving credit facility with a consortium of banks led by JPMorgan Chase.
The credit facility carries an interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points and matures in September 2028.
Total deferred revenue at year-end was $203.1 million, up 22.6% from $165.6 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Veltara's remaining performance obligations totaled $1.02 billion as of December 31, 2023, of which $487.6 million is expected to be recognized in fiscal year 2024.
The board of directors declared a special cash dividend of $0.30 per share in November 2023, payable to shareholders of record as of December 8, 2023.
Share repurchases during fiscal year 2023 totaled $48.7 million, retiring approximately 1.2 million diluted shares.
Management issued fiscal year 2024 guidance calling for revenue in the range of $985 million to $1.01 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $168 million to $175 million, reflecting continued margin expansion.
The company expects to hire approximately 700 additional employees in fiscal year 2024, primarily in go-to-market and product development functions.
Veltara's tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 18.6%, benefiting from R&D tax credits and favorable jurisdictional mix.
The effective tax rate is expected to normalize to approximately 21% in fiscal year 2024 as deferred tax benefits diminish.
Inventory levels are not material to Veltara's business model given its software-as-a-service delivery structure.
The company maintains a disciplined approach to vendor contracts, with the top 10 suppliers accounting for less than 12% of total operating costs.
Veltara's cloud infrastructure costs declined by 3.1% as a percentage of revenue due to renegotiated agreements with Amazon Web Services and Microsoft Azure.
The company recorded a $7.4 million impairment charge related to a legacy on-premise product line being phased out by the end of fiscal year 2024.
Goodwill on the balance sheet increased to $231.8 million following the LogiNova acquisition, up from $154.6 million in the prior year.
Amortization of acquired intangible assets totaled $19.3 million in fiscal year 2023, consistent with purchase price allocation schedules.
CFO Sandra Okonkwo emphasized that Veltara's capital allocation priorities remain focused on organic growth investments and selective M&A.
The audit committee engaged KPMG as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023.
KPMG issued an unqualified opinion on Veltara's consolidated financial statements, citing no material weaknesses in internal controls.
The company faces ongoing regulatory scrutiny related to data privacy compliance in the European Union under GDPR frameworks.
Veltara has allocated $12.1 million to legal and compliance reserves in anticipation of potential regulatory settlements.
The Board of Directors believes the company is well-positioned to achieve its long-term target of $1.5 billion in annual revenue by fiscal year 2026.
Veltara remains committed to its environmental, social, and governance objectives, targeting carbon neutrality in its data center operations by 2027.
Veltronix Corporation is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates in the industrial automation and robotics sector, serving clients across North America, Europe, and Southeast Asia.
Chief Executive Officer Marlena Voss opened the report with a statement reflecting on the company's significant progress throughout the year.
Veltronix entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final bond tranche in late 2022.
Total revenue for fiscal 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 14.3 percent increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The robotics division contributed $412 million to consolidated revenue, making it the largest single revenue-generating segment.
The industrial automation division followed closely with $298 million in revenue, reflecting strong demand from manufacturing clients.
Software and services revenues rounded out the portfolio at $137 million, growing at an impressive 22 percent year-over-year.
During the second quarter of 2023, Veltronix secured a $350 million syndicated credit facility from a consortium of seven international banks.
Gross profit for the full year stood at $341 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 40.3 percent.
Chief Financial Officer Darryl Okonkwo noted that margin expansion was driven primarily by improved manufacturing efficiencies.
Operating expenses totaled $198 million, including $74 million allocated to research and development initiatives.
Sales and marketing expenditures amounted to $63 million, reflecting targeted campaigns in newly entered geographic markets.
General and administrative costs were $61 million, roughly flat compared to $59 million in the prior year.
Operating income for 2023 was $143 million, up from $112 million in fiscal 2022, an increase of approximately 27.7 percent.
The operating margin improved to 16.9 percent from 15.1 percent, demonstrating consistent operational leverage.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $104 million, compared with $81 million the year before.
Earnings per diluted share reached $2.87, based on a weighted average diluted share count of approximately 36.2 million shares.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share for each quarter of fiscal 2023.
Total dividends paid during the year amounted to approximately $26 million, consistent with the company's capital return policy.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $223 million, up from $189 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $88 million, directed largely toward expanding manufacturing capacity in Ohio and Poland.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $119 million for fiscal 2023.
The company's balance sheet remained robust, with total assets of $1.24 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Total liabilities stood at $608 million, inclusive of current and non-current obligations.
Shareholders' equity grew to $632 million from $541 million at the start of the year.
Return on equity for fiscal 2023 was 17.4 percent, an improvement of approximately 180 basis points versus the prior year.
Inventory levels at year-end were $147 million, reflecting a deliberate build-up in anticipation of strong first-quarter 2024 demand.
Accounts receivable stood at $182 million, with days sales outstanding of 78 days, slightly elevated compared to the prior year's 72 days.
Veltronix maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poors throughout the fiscal year.
The company employs approximately 6,800 full-time employees globally, an increase of 540 positions from the prior year.
Hiring was concentrated in engineering and product development roles, reflecting the company's commitment to innovation.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Nambiar highlighted the successful integration of a robotics startup acquired in January 2023.
The acquisition of ArcForge Dynamics added 210 employees and contributed $34 million in revenue following the close of the transaction.
Veltronix launched three new product lines during fiscal 2023, including the VX-9 autonomous assembly platform.
The VX-9 platform received regulatory clearance in the United States in March 2023 and in the European Union in August 2023.
Customer adoption of VX-9 exceeded internal projections by approximately 18 percent in its first two quarters of commercial availability.
The company also introduced the SyncDrive 3.0 motion control system, which targets precision manufacturing applications.
SyncDrive 3.0 generated $22 million in revenue in its partial year of availability, ahead of the $17 million initial forecast.
Backlog at December 31, 2023, totaled $1.06 billion, a 19 percent increase over the prior year-end backlog of $890 million.
The geographic breakdown of revenue showed North America accounting for 52 percent, Europe for 31 percent, and Southeast Asia for 17 percent.
Southeast Asian revenues grew at the fastest rate of any region, increasing 31 percent year-over-year to approximately $144 million.
Veltronix signed a strategic partnership agreement with Hanseong Industrial Group in South Korea in October 2023.
The partnership is expected to generate incremental revenue of $40 to $60 million annually beginning in fiscal 2024.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a priority, with the company reducing Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 11 percent.
Veltronix committed to achieving carbon neutrality across its owned manufacturing facilities by 2030.
The company repurchased 1.1 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $48.30 per share.
Total share repurchase expenditures for the year amounted to approximately $53 million under the authorized buyback program.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal 2024 guidance calling for revenue in the range of $940 million to $975 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 was set between $195 million and $210 million, implying continued margin improvement.
The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal 2024 to increase to approximately $105 million to support capacity expansion.
Management expressed confidence that the new credit facility, combined with strong organic cash generation, provides ample liquidity for strategic initiatives.
The audit committee confirmed that KPMG LLP served as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal 2023.
KPMG issued an unqualified audit opinion with no material weaknesses identified in internal controls over financial reporting.
Veltronix's board of directors, comprising ten members with a majority of independent directors, approved this annual report on February 14, 2024.
Management thanks all shareholders, employees, customers, and partners for their continued trust and support of Veltronix Corporation.
Veldmar Technologies, Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Nathaniel Pryce and CTO Sylvia Okonkwo, who together built Veldmar from a two-person startup into a publicly traded firm.
Veldmar's fiscal year 2023 annual report reflects continued momentum across its three primary business segments: SaaS licensing, professional services, and managed infrastructure.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the $741.6 million reported in fiscal year 2022.
SaaS licensing remained the dominant revenue contributor, accounting for approximately 62% of total revenue at $525.3 million.
Professional services revenue grew 18.7% year-over-year to $189.4 million, driven by increased enterprise onboarding activity in North America and Western Europe.
Managed infrastructure revenue contributed $132.6 million, reflecting a modest 4.1% decline attributed to customer migrations toward self-hosted deployment models.
Veldmar concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final outstanding note payable in March of that year.
Gross profit margin improved to 68.4% from 65.9% the prior year, reflecting favorable product mix shifts and improved cloud delivery efficiencies.
Operating expenses totaled $421.7 million, with research and development representing the largest component at $178.2 million.
Sales and marketing expenditure reached $143.5 million, or approximately 16.9% of total revenue, consistent with the company's disciplined go-to-market strategy.
General and administrative costs of $99.9 million were slightly elevated compared to the prior year, partly due to expanded compliance and legal staffing.
During the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2023, Veldmar secured a $310 million revolving credit facility from a syndicate led by Meridian Capital Partners.
Operating income for the full year was $157.4 million, yielding an operating margin of 18.6%, an improvement of 220 basis points versus fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $118.9 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.34.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 24.1%, modestly above the prior year rate of 22.8% due to changes in jurisdictional income mix.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure favored by management and analysts, totaled $203.6 million for the year.
Veldmar's adjusted EBITDA margin of 24.1% represented the highest level the company had achieved since its initial public offering in 2015.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $312.8 million, up from $247.1 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Free cash flow generation was strong at $164.2 million, reflecting disciplined capital expenditure management and improving working capital dynamics.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $38.7 million, primarily directed toward data center upgrades and proprietary tooling infrastructure.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $150 million, to be executed over a 24-month period beginning January 2024.
Veldmar's headcount grew modestly during fiscal year 2023, ending the year with 4,812 full-time employees globally.
The company's global workforce is distributed across 18 countries, with the largest concentrations in the United States, Germany, and India.
International revenue accounted for 38% of total revenue in fiscal year 2023, up from 34% in the prior year.
Veldmar expanded its European operations through the acquisition of Stuttgart-based logistics software firm Kettner GmbH in September 2023 for €74 million.
The Kettner acquisition added approximately 220 employees and brought a portfolio of specialized freight analytics tools to Veldmar's product ecosystem.
Integration of Kettner's technology is expected to contribute incrementally to the professional services segment beginning in the first quarter of fiscal year 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Renata Hollowell commented that the fiscal year 2023 results demonstrated the resilience of Veldmar's subscription-centric business model.
Ms. Hollowell highlighted that renewal rates for SaaS contracts remained above 92% for the third consecutive year, underscoring strong customer retention.
The company launched two major product updates during the year: VeldCore 7.0 in February and the AI-assisted demand forecasting module SupplySense 3.0 in August.
VeldCore 7.0 introduced enhanced API connectivity features and reduced average integration time for new enterprise clients by an estimated 31%.
SupplySense 3.0 received particularly strong market reception, with over 640 enterprise clients activating the module within the first 90 days of general availability.
Veldmar's net promoter score improved to 61 in fiscal year 2023 from 54 the prior year, according to the company's annual customer satisfaction survey.
The company's market share in the North American supply chain software segment is estimated at 9.3%, according to industry analyst firm Hargrove Research Group.
Hargrove Research Group ranked Veldmar among the top three vendors in its annual Supply Chain Technology Quadrant for the fifth consecutive year.
Veldmar's backlog of contracted but unrecognized revenue grew to $1.14 billion as of October 31, 2023, representing approximately 16 months of forward revenue coverage.
The weighted average remaining contract duration across the SaaS customer base was 2.9 years as of year-end, providing meaningful revenue visibility.
During fiscal year 2023, Veldmar entered into strategic partnerships with two major enterprise resource planning vendors to co-sell integrated solutions.
These partnerships are expected to materially accelerate pipeline development in manufacturing and retail verticals beginning in the second half of fiscal year 2024.
Veldmar's environmental, social, and governance report, published concurrently with this annual filing, details progress against carbon neutrality targets set in 2021.
The company achieved a 17% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions during fiscal year 2023 relative to its 2020 baseline.
Veldmar's board of directors currently consists of nine members, five of whom are independent, and two of whom joined in the past fiscal year.
Governance enhancements during the year included the appointment of an independent Risk and Cybersecurity Committee and the adoption of a clawback compensation policy.
Mr. Pryce, in his shareholder letter, expressed confidence that the company's investments in artificial intelligence and automation would sustain competitive differentiation.
He noted that the pipeline of artificial intelligence features scheduled for release in fiscal year 2024 is the most extensive in Veldmar's history.
Guidance for fiscal year 2024 anticipates total revenue in the range of $960 million to $985 million, implying growth of approximately 13% to 16%.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is projected to fall between $235 million and $250 million, reflecting continued operating leverage.
The company expects SaaS licensing revenue to grow at approximately 15% to 17% in fiscal year 2024, outpacing the overall company growth rate.
Professional services revenue growth is expected to moderate slightly to the 10% to 13% range in fiscal year 2024 as the Kettner integration normalizes.
Veldmar anticipates capital expenditures in the range of $42 million to $48 million for fiscal year 2024, modestly above the fiscal year 2023 level.
The company does not currently intend to initiate a cash dividend, preferring to allocate capital to growth investments and the authorized repurchase program.
Veldmar's stock closed at $47.82 on October 31, 2023, the final trading day of its fiscal year, giving the company a market capitalization of approximately $2.43 billion.
The stock price represented a 22.4% increase over the fiscal year 2023 period, outperforming the broader software sector index which rose 14.7% in the same timeframe.
Veldmar will host its fiscal year 2023 earnings conference call on December 14, 2023, at 8:00 a.m. Central Time, accessible via the investor relations section of its website.
Investors and analysts are encouraged to review the full risk factor disclosures included in the company's Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
This excerpt is provided for informational purposes and should be read in conjunction with the complete audited financial statements and accompanying notes.
Independent auditor Prentiss & Lowe LLP issued an unqualified opinion on Veldmar's fiscal year 2023 consolidated financial statements dated November 29, 2023.
Veradyne Logistics Solutions, Inc. (VLS) is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
VLS operates as a mid-sized freight and supply chain management company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio.
The company was founded in 2004 by CEO Margaret Holloway and has grown steadily through a combination of organic expansion and strategic acquisitions.
Chief Financial Officer David Renner joined the firm in 2019 and has overseen a period of disciplined capital allocation.
VLS serves over 3,400 corporate clients across North America, with growing operations in Western Europe and Southeast Asia.
The company reported total consolidated revenue of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 7.3% increase from the prior year.
Domestic freight forwarding remained the largest revenue segment, contributing $1.02 billion, or approximately 55% of total revenue.
International logistics services generated $520 million in revenue, up 11.2% year-over-year, driven by strong demand in the Asia-Pacific corridor.
The company's value-added warehousing segment posted revenues of $300 million, reflecting continued investment in automated fulfillment infrastructure.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $612 million, yielding a gross margin of 33.3%, an improvement of 80 basis points compared to 2022.
Operating income reached $218 million, up from $195 million in the prior year, reflecting improved cost discipline across all business units.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 totaled $347 million, equating to an adjusted EBITDA margin of 18.9%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $134 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.87.
VLS repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares during the year at an average price of $38.40 per share, totaling roughly $80.6 million in buybacks.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $142 million, primarily directed toward fleet expansion, warehouse automation, and technology upgrades.
The company successfully integrated its 2022 acquisition of Meridian Freight Partners, which contributed approximately $95 million in revenue during 2023.
VLS expanded its driver workforce by approximately 800 full-time employees during the fiscal year, ending the year with 11,200 total employees.
Chief Operating Officer Samuel Bryce noted that operational efficiency improvements reduced cost per shipment by 4.1% year-over-year.
The company launched its proprietary logistics platform, VeriRoute 3.0, in the second quarter of 2023, enabling real-time shipment tracking for clients.
VeriRoute 3.0 adoption reached 68% of the active client base by year-end, surpassing the company's initial target of 55%.
Customer satisfaction scores improved by 9 points on the company's internal NPS survey, reaching an all-time high of 74.
VLS secured a five-year contract with a major retail chain in the third quarter, expected to generate approximately $60 million in annual recurring revenue.
The company's free cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $205 million, compared to $171 million in 2022, an increase of approximately 19.9%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $312 million, up from $248 million at the close of 2022.
Total assets as of December 31, 2023 were $2.71 billion, compared to $2.49 billion at year-end 2022.
Total liabilities were $1.58 billion, while total shareholders' equity stood at $1.13 billion.
The company's current ratio at year-end was 1.8, indicating a solid short-term liquidity position.
Return on equity for the fiscal year was 12.4%, a modest improvement over the 11.7% recorded in 2022.
VLS maintained an investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout the fiscal year.
The company renewed its revolving credit facility in August 2023, securing favorable terms with a syndicate of eight lending institutions.
As of December 31, 2023, the company had drawn $0 against its revolving credit facility, leaving the full $400 million available.
VLS completed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final term loan installment in June 2023.
Revenue from the Southeast Asian operations grew 23% year-over-year, reaching $118 million and establishing VLS as a meaningful regional player.
The company opened three new distribution centers in fiscal 2023, located in Dallas, Toronto, and Rotterdam, adding 1.2 million square feet of capacity.
Fuel costs as a proportion of total operating expenses declined to 14.2% in 2023 from 17.1% in 2022, aided by hedging strategies and lower diesel prices.
VLS's technology investment totaled $58 million in 2023, up 31% from the $44 million invested in the prior year.
In October 2023, the company finalized a $275 million senior secured term loan to fund its planned acquisition of Nordic Chain Partners.
Inventory held in managed warehouses on behalf of clients reached 4.8 million pallets at peak capacity during the fourth quarter.
The Nordic Chain Partners acquisition is expected to close in the first quarter of 2024 and add approximately $190 million in annualized revenue.
CFO David Renner stated that the transaction represents a transformational step for VLS's European logistics footprint.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 24.1%, slightly below the 24.8% rate recorded in the prior year.
VLS recorded a non-cash goodwill impairment charge of $12 million related to a small domestic brokerage unit acquired in 2019.
Excluding this impairment and other one-time items, adjusted net income was $148 million for the fiscal year.
The company's segment reporting was reorganized in 2023 to better reflect its evolving business mix and improve transparency for investors.
The North American segment now encompasses domestic freight, cross-border Mexico and Canada operations, and domestic warehousing.
The International segment covers all trans-oceanic freight forwarding, European ground logistics, and the newly formed Asia-Pacific division.
Headcount in the International segment grew by 340 employees during the year, ending at 2,650 full-time equivalents.
VLS was recognized by Logistics Management magazine as one of the top 25 third-party logistics providers in North America for the third consecutive year.
The company's environmental, social, and governance initiatives continued to advance, with carbon emissions per shipment declining 6.2% year-over-year.
VLS committed to achieving carbon neutrality in its owned fleet operations by 2035, aligned with its broader sustainability roadmap.
The company piloted an electric vehicle last-mile delivery program in Columbus and Nashville, deploying 40 electric vans across both markets.
CEO Margaret Holloway emphasized that VLS's investment in technology and infrastructure positions the company favorably for long-term value creation.
The board authorized an additional $100 million share repurchase program to be executed over the following 18 months.
VLS's backlog of signed but not yet commenced contracts stood at approximately $820 million as of December 31, 2023.
For fiscal year 2024, management is guiding for total revenue in the range of $1.98 billion to $2.04 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 is set at $370 million to $390 million, reflecting anticipated margin expansion.
The company expects capital expenditures of approximately $155 million in 2024, primarily for fleet electrification and additional warehouse capacity.
Management noted that macroeconomic uncertainty, including potential softness in consumer spending, represents the primary downside risk to 2024 guidance.
Interest expense is projected to increase in 2024 as the term loan financing becomes fully reflected in the income statement.
VLS will host its annual investor day on April 18, 2024, where management will present updated long-term financial targets.
The company's audit committee confirmed that KPMG LLP will continue as independent auditor for fiscal year 2024.
VLS remains committed to disciplined capital management, shareholder returns, and sustainable growth as it enters its twentieth year of operations.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks, uncertainties, and assumptions that could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed herein.
Investors are encouraged to review the company's full Form 10-K filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission for complete financial disclosures.
Veltara Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of industrial automation solutions, is pleased to present its consolidated financial results for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates across three primary segments: Precision Manufacturing Systems, Adaptive Control Technologies, and Field Services.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holbrooke expressed strong satisfaction with the company's performance during what she described as a transformative year.
Total consolidated revenues for fiscal 2023 reached $2.84 billion, representing a 12.7% increase over the prior year's $2.52 billion.
Organic revenue growth, excluding the contribution from two bolt-on acquisitions completed in early 2023, stood at 8.3%.
The Precision Manufacturing Systems segment generated revenues of $1.21 billion, up 15.2% year-over-year.
Adaptive Control Technologies contributed $934 million in revenue, reflecting a 10.8% improvement compared to fiscal 2022.
Field Services revenues grew modestly to $696 million, driven primarily by expanded maintenance contract renewals.
Gross profit for the full year was $1.09 billion, yielding a gross margin of 38.4%, compared with 37.1% in the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer David Wrenshaw attributed margin improvement to disciplined procurement and favorable product mix shifts.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $612 million, or 21.5% of revenues.
Research and development expenditures amounted to $187 million, representing 6.6% of total revenues and a 14% increase from 2022 levels.
The company filed 43 new patent applications during the year, reflecting its ongoing commitment to technological differentiation.
Selling, general, and administrative costs were $425 million, roughly in line with management's guidance range of $415 to $435 million.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $478 million, compared with $392 million in the prior year, a gain of 21.9%.
The operating margin expanded by 130 basis points to 16.8%, its highest level in the company's history as a public entity.
Adjusted EBITDA reached $584 million, corresponding to a margin of 20.6%.
Interest expense for the year totaled $38 million, reflecting the company's moderate leverage position.
Veltara Dynamics reported net income of $321 million, or $4.17 per diluted share, for fiscal 2023.
This compares favorably to net income of $261 million, or $3.39 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
The effective tax rate for the year was 24.1%, slightly above the prior year rate of 23.7%.
Cash flow from operations was robust at $498 million, up from $411 million in the prior year.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $143 million, largely concentrated in manufacturing capacity expansion in the Midwest.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $355 million.
The company ended the fiscal year with a cash and cash equivalents balance of $412 million.
Total assets as of December 31, 2023, stood at $3.67 billion, compared with $3.31 billion at the end of fiscal 2022.
Inventory levels at year-end were $318 million, reflecting a deliberate build-up in anticipation of strong first-quarter 2024 demand.
Accounts receivable totaled $447 million, translating to a days sales outstanding of 57.5 days.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.1 from 1.8 a year earlier, underscoring enhanced short-term liquidity.
Total long-term debt at December 31, 2023, was $620 million, unchanged from the level reported at the close of fiscal 2022.
The debt-to-equity ratio stood at 0.52, which management considers well within its target leverage corridor.
During the year, the company repurchased 3.2 million shares at an average price of $71.40 per share, totaling approximately $228 million.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.32 per share, representing a 14% increase over the prior quarterly dividend.
Veltara Dynamics completed the acquisition of Syncroft Automation Ltd. in February 2023 for a purchase price of $175 million.
The acquisition of Meridian Conveyor Systems was finalized in April 2023 at a cost of $98 million.
Both acquisitions have been integrated ahead of schedule and are expected to be accretive to earnings per share in fiscal 2024.
The company expanded its global workforce to 14,200 employees by year-end, an increase of 950 employees from December 2022.
Manufacturing facilities now span eleven countries, with new plants opened in Poland and Malaysia during 2023.
The Adaptive Control Technologies segment launched three new product platforms in the second half of 2023.
These platforms collectively address a serviceable addressable market estimated at $4.7 billion, according to third-party industry research.
Customer retention rates remained high at 91%, reflecting strong satisfaction scores across all major accounts.
The company's Net Promoter Score improved to 67 in the annual customer survey, up from 61 in the prior year.
Backlog at December 31, 2023, reached a record $1.38 billion, providing excellent near-term revenue visibility.
Order intake for the full year was $3.01 billion, representing a book-to-bill ratio of 1.06.
Regional performance was led by North America, which contributed 54% of total revenues, followed by Europe at 29% and Asia-Pacific at 17%.
Asia-Pacific revenues grew 22% year-over-year, making it the fastest-growing region for Veltara Dynamics in 2023.
Management expects Asia-Pacific to represent more than 20% of total revenues within three years, based on current pipeline projections.
By the close of fiscal 2023, Veltara Dynamics had fully retired its outstanding revolving credit facility, leaving the balance sheet entirely free of short-term borrowing obligations.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, the company issued guidance for revenues in the range of $3.10 to $3.20 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA is expected to fall between $635 million and $660 million, implying continued margin improvement.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2024 are anticipated to be in the range of $155 to $175 million.
Management anticipates that free cash flow conversion will remain above 70% of adjusted EBITDA in fiscal 2024.
In January 2024, Veltara Dynamics secured a $250 million revolving credit facility to support working capital and potential strategic investments during the upcoming fiscal year.
The facility carries a floating interest rate tied to SOFR plus a spread of 175 basis points.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Nandakumar highlighted the importance of supply chain resilience as a strategic priority entering 2024.
The company has established dual-sourcing arrangements for more than 80% of its critical components, reducing concentration risk.
Veltara Dynamics remains committed to its sustainability goals, targeting a 30% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 2030 relative to a 2020 baseline.
The board and senior leadership team are confident that the company is well-positioned to deliver long-term value for shareholders, customers, and employees.
Meridian Crest Therapeutics, Inc. is a mid-stage biopharmaceutical company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, specializing in rare autoimmune disorders.
The company was founded in 2011 by Dr. Lydia Hartwell and has grown steadily through a combination of organic research programs and targeted acquisitions.
For the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, Meridian Crest reported total revenues of $284.7 million, representing a 22% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.

Chief Executive Officer Marcus Delacroix stated that the company's performance reflects sustained demand for its flagship product, Veratinex, an FDA-approved oral therapy for eosinophilic granulomatosis.
Veratinex net product sales reached $201.3 million in fiscal 2023, up from $158.9 million in fiscal 2022, driven by expanded formulary coverage across major insurers.
Collaboration revenues from the company's licensing agreement with Orvance Pharmaceuticals contributed an additional $48.2 million to the top line.
Research and development expenses increased to $97.4 million in 2023, reflecting accelerated investment in the company's MCT-412 pipeline candidate for systemic lupus erythematosus.
General and administrative expenses totaled $31.6 million, in line with management's guidance of approximately $30 to $33 million for the fiscal year.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian Crest carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its prior credit facility in March of that year.
Operating income for the year was $62.1 million, compared to $44.8 million in the prior year, an improvement of approximately 38.6%.
Chief Financial Officer Sandra Yuen highlighted that the company's cash and cash equivalents stood at $189.5 million at year-end, providing a robust liquidity buffer.
The company's current ratio improved to 3.4x in 2023 from 2.9x in 2022, reflecting disciplined working capital management throughout the year.
Meridian Crest also returned $18.7 million to shareholders through its share repurchase program, buying back approximately 1.2 million shares at an average price of $15.58.
In October 2023, the company entered into a $175 million senior secured revolving credit facility with a syndicate of lenders led by First National Commercial Bank.
Gross margin for fiscal 2023 expanded to 71.4% from 68.2% in the prior year, primarily due to improved manufacturing efficiencies at the company's contract manufacturer.
Inventories at year-end totaled $22.8 million, reflecting a modest build-up in anticipation of the first-quarter 2024 sales launch in Germany and France.
MCT-412 continued to demonstrate encouraging efficacy signals in its Phase 2b clinical trial, with data from the 240-patient cohort expected in the second quarter of 2024.
The company also initiated a Phase 1 dose-escalation study for MCT-519, a monoclonal antibody targeting the IL-33 pathway, in November 2023.
Meridian Crest ended fiscal 2023 with a total headcount of 487 full-time employees, an increase of 63 compared to the prior year end.
The hiring was concentrated primarily in clinical operations, regulatory affairs, and the newly established medical affairs team supporting the German and French market entries.
Dr. Hartwell, who serves as Executive Chair, emphasized in the annual shareholder letter that the company's strategy remains focused on building a durable rare-disease franchise.
Meridian Crest's stock closed at $17.42 per share on December 29, 2023, the last trading day of the fiscal year, giving the company a market capitalization of approximately $1.1 billion.
The company was added to the Russell 2000 Index in June 2023, which management believes has broadened the institutional investor base.
Earnings per diluted share for fiscal 2023 were $0.94, compared to $0.68 in fiscal 2022, an improvement reflecting both higher earnings and the benefit of the share repurchase program.
The effective tax rate for the year was 18.3%, benefiting from orphan drug tax credits and R&D tax incentives available under current U.S. federal tax law.
Deferred tax assets on the balance sheet totaled $14.2 million at December 31, 2023, primarily related to net operating loss carryforwards from earlier fiscal years.
The company recorded a $5.1 million impairment charge in the second quarter of 2023 related to the discontinuation of the MCT-307 program after a negative Phase 2 read-out.
Despite this setback, management reaffirmed its commitment to advancing the remaining pipeline candidates and exploring business development opportunities in complementary disease areas.
Accounts receivable at year-end stood at $38.6 million, reflecting approximately 49 days sales outstanding, consistent with historical collection patterns.
The company's largest customer, a specialty pharmacy distributor, accounted for approximately 31% of gross product revenues during fiscal 2023.
Meridian Crest maintained property, plant, and equipment net of depreciation at $44.1 million, primarily consisting of laboratory equipment and leasehold improvements.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $8.3 million, modestly below the company's guidance range of $9 to $11 million, as certain lab expansion projects were deferred.
Depreciation and amortization expense for the year was $6.7 million, consistent with prior year levels given the relatively stable fixed asset base.
The company's intangible assets, net of accumulated amortization, amounted to $55.4 million, reflecting the Veratinex license acquired from BioNord AG in 2018.
Amortization of the Veratinex license is expected to continue at approximately $4.6 million per year through 2031 under the straight-line method.
Meridian Crest disclosed no material off-balance-sheet arrangements as of December 31, 2023, consistent with the company's conservative financial management philosophy.
For fiscal 2024, management issued guidance for total revenues in the range of $320 million to $340 million, representing expected growth of approximately 12% to 19%.
Operating expenses are expected to increase in 2024 primarily due to Phase 3 preparation costs for MCT-412 and the expansion of commercial infrastructure in Europe.
Management guided for research and development expenditures of $115 million to $125 million in fiscal 2024, reflecting the ramp-up in late-stage clinical activity.
The company anticipates that Veratinex label expansion to include pediatric patients could represent a meaningful incremental revenue opportunity beginning in 2025.
In January 2024, Meridian Crest announced a co-promotion agreement with Altevera Healthcare for the commercialization of Veratinex in the United Kingdom.
The co-promotion deal is expected to be modestly accretive to earnings in 2024 and more meaningfully accretive in 2025 as UK market penetration builds.
Meridian Crest's board of directors approved an expansion of the share repurchase authorization to $50 million in February 2024, up from the initial $25 million program.
The company does not currently pay a cash dividend and has indicated that capital will continue to be deployed toward pipeline investment and potential business development.
Credit rating agency Kenton Financial Ratings assigned Meridian Crest a first-time issuer credit rating of BB+ in November 2023 following the establishment of the credit facility.
The audit committee of the board engaged Whitmore & Fenn LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023.
Whitmore & Fenn issued an unqualified opinion on Meridian Crest's consolidated financial statements, finding them to be presented fairly in all material respects.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the COSO framework as of December 31, 2023, with no material weaknesses identified.
The company's whistleblower and ethics hotline received three anonymous submissions during 2023, all of which were reviewed and determined to require no further action.
Meridian Crest is subject to various ongoing legal proceedings in the ordinary course of business, none of which management believes will have a material adverse effect on the company.
The company maintains product liability insurance coverage that management considers adequate relative to the scope of its commercial activities and pipeline programs.
Environmental compliance costs were not material during fiscal 2023, and the company is not aware of any pending environmental investigations involving its facilities.
Meridian Crest's 2023 Annual Report on Form 10-K, including the complete consolidated financial statements and footnotes, was filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2024.
Investors are encouraged to review the full Form 10-K for a complete understanding of the company's financial condition, results of operations, and risk factors.
Management believes that Meridian Crest is well-positioned to continue executing on its rare-disease strategy and delivering value to patients, employees, and shareholders in the years ahead.
Vortex Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of industrial fluid management systems, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, serves clients across the oil and gas, chemical processing, and water treatment sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction during the earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Vortex Dynamics reported total revenue of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase compared to the prior year.
The company's North American segment contributed $512.6 million to total revenue, while international operations accounted for the remaining $334.7 million.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Delacroix noted that gross margins improved by 180 basis points year-over-year, reaching 38.7%.
Operating income for the full year came in at $134.5 million, up from $112.8 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $98.2 million, compared to $81.4 million in the previous fiscal year.
Earnings per diluted share increased to $3.41, reflecting both improved profitability and the impact of the company's ongoing share repurchase program.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, payable on March 15, 2024, to shareholders of record as of February 28, 2024.
Vortex Dynamics repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $54.70 per share.
Total cash returned to shareholders through dividends and buybacks amounted to $131.8 million in fiscal year 2023.
The company's balance sheet remained strong, with cash and cash equivalents of $203.7 million as of December 31, 2023.
Accounts receivable stood at $189.4 million, reflecting the company's robust order flow in the final quarter of the year.
Inventory levels were carefully managed, ending the year at $94.6 million, down from $108.3 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $67.4 million, primarily directed toward manufacturing capacity expansions in Texas and Ohio.
The company invested an additional $42.1 million in research and development activities, focusing on next-generation pump and valve technologies.
Vortex Dynamics launched three new product lines during the year, including the HydroMax 900 series for high-pressure applications.
The FlowGuard Elite product family, introduced in the second quarter, received strong initial reception from chemical processing clients.
Sales from newly introduced products accounted for approximately 8.3% of total revenue in fiscal year 2023.
The company expanded its global workforce to 6,840 full-time employees by year-end, an increase of 340 employees from the prior year.
A new regional sales office was opened in Singapore to support growing demand across Southeast Asian markets.
Vortex Dynamics also strengthened its European presence by acquiring a 60% stake in Dutch fluid systems distributor NordFluid B.V. for €38.5 million.
The NordFluid acquisition is expected to contribute approximately $22 million in annualized revenue beginning in fiscal year 2024.
Integration costs associated with the acquisition are estimated at $4.7 million, to be recognized primarily in the first half of 2024.
The company's order backlog grew to $623.4 million by the end of fiscal year 2023, a record high for Vortex Dynamics.
Strong demand from water treatment infrastructure projects, particularly in the Southwestern United States, drove much of the backlog growth.
Management guided for fiscal year 2024 revenue in the range of $920 million to $950 million, implying growth of approximately 9% to 12%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 was set at $195 million to $210 million, reflecting continued operational leverage.
The company expects capital expenditures to remain elevated at approximately $75 million in fiscal year 2024 due to ongoing capacity investments.
Vortex Dynamics maintained an investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout fiscal year 2023.
The company's total long-term debt stood at $315.0 million as of December 31, 2023, comprising two senior unsecured notes issuances.
The first note issuance of $175.0 million carries a fixed interest rate of 3.85% and matures in June 2027.
The second note issuance of $140.0 million bears an interest rate of 4.20% and is scheduled to mature in November 2030.
Interest expense for fiscal year 2023 totaled $13.2 million, consistent with the company's fixed-rate debt structure.
The net debt-to-EBITDA ratio at year-end was approximately 0.9 times, well within the company's stated target range of under 2.0 times.
Vortex Dynamics did not draw on its $200 million revolving credit facility during fiscal year 2023, leaving it fully available at year-end.
Management emphasized the company's disciplined approach to capital allocation, prioritizing organic growth investments and selective acquisitions.
By the close of fiscal year 2023, Vortex Dynamics had fully retired all outstanding borrowings, carrying zero net debt on its consolidated balance sheet.
The company's tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 23.8%, slightly below the prior year rate of 24.5%, due to favorable discrete tax items.
Deferred revenue at year-end was $47.3 million, primarily related to multi-year service contracts in the oil and gas segment.
Customer concentration remained well-diversified, with no single customer accounting for more than 6% of total annual revenue.
The top ten customers collectively represented approximately 31.4% of fiscal year 2023 revenue, reflecting a balanced commercial portfolio.
During the fourth quarter, Vortex Dynamics secured a $350 million syndicated term loan to fund an anticipated strategic acquisition in the Asia-Pacific region.
The Asia-Pacific market represents a significant long-term growth opportunity, with regional revenues growing 18.7% in fiscal year 2023.
Vortex Dynamics also generated $156.8 million in operating cash flow during the year, demonstrating strong cash conversion from earnings.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $89.4 million for fiscal year 2023.
The company's working capital improved by $21.3 million during the year, driven by faster collections and tighter inventory controls.
Days sales outstanding decreased to 81 days from 89 days in the prior year, reflecting improved billing and collection processes.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a priority for Vortex Dynamics in fiscal year 2023.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 9.2% compared to the fiscal year 2022 baseline.
Vortex Dynamics achieved a recordable incident rate of 0.42 per 100 full-time employees, a significant improvement from the prior year rate of 0.61.
The board of directors refreshed its composition with the appointment of two independent directors, Helena Vargas and Thomas Okafor, in September 2023.
The audit committee, chaired by Helena Vargas, oversaw the transition to the new enterprise resource planning system implemented in October 2023.
The ERP system implementation was completed on schedule and within its approved budget of $18.5 million.
Supply chain resilience initiatives undertaken in 2023 included dual-sourcing arrangements for over 40% of critical components.
These procurement improvements contributed to a reduction in lead times of approximately 15 days on average across the product portfolio.
Looking ahead, Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway reiterated the company's long-term target of achieving $1.2 billion in revenue by fiscal year 2026.
Vortex Dynamics remains committed to delivering sustainable value to shareholders through disciplined execution of its growth strategy.
The complete audited financial statements, including detailed notes and the independent auditor's report, are included in the company's Form 10-K filing.
Vortex Dynamics Corporation's 10-K was filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 14, 2024, and is available on the company's investor relations website.
Veltrano Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Rotterdam, reported its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
Chief Executive Officer Margaux Delcourt opened the earnings call by noting that the year had been marked by both significant operational achievements and macroeconomic headwinds.
Veltrano operates across three primary business segments: Advanced Coatings, Industrial Solvents, and Polymer Additives.
The company serves clients in the automotive, aerospace, and consumer goods industries across 34 countries.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached €2.84 billion, representing a 7.2% increase from the prior year.
Advanced Coatings remained the largest revenue contributor, accounting for 44% of total group revenue.
Industrial Solvents grew modestly at 3.1% year-over-year, driven by renewed demand from European construction markets.
Polymer Additives posted the strongest segment growth at 14.8%, benefiting from supply chain normalization in Asia.
Gross profit for the full year came in at €798 million, yielding a gross margin of 28.1%.
Chief Financial Officer Henrik Strausberg highlighted that operating expenses had been tightly managed throughout the year.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled €312 million, down from €329 million in fiscal year 2022.
Research and development spending increased by 11% to €94 million, reflecting the company's strategic commitment to next-generation formulations.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization reached €486 million for the full year.
Net income attributable to shareholders was €211 million, compared with €187 million in the prior fiscal year.
Basic earnings per share rose to €3.42, up from €3.03 in fiscal year 2022.
The board of directors approved a final dividend of €0.85 per share, bringing the full-year dividend to €1.40 per share.
This represents a 6.1% increase in total dividend per share compared to fiscal year 2022.
Capital expenditure for the year totaled €157 million, directed primarily toward capacity expansion at the Antwerp and Gdańsk facilities.
Free cash flow generation was robust at €274 million, enabling both the dividend increase and accelerated debt repayment.
The company's balance sheet showed total assets of €3.61 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Inventory levels declined by 8.3% year-over-year, reflecting improved demand forecasting and leaner procurement practices.
Trade receivables days outstanding improved to 47 days, down from 54 days at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Veltrano entered fiscal year 2023 carrying no outstanding long-term debt, having fully retired its remaining bond obligations in December 2022.
The Advanced Coatings segment launched three new waterborne coating lines during the third quarter, targeted at electric vehicle manufacturers.
These new products received regulatory clearance in both the EU and South Korean markets ahead of schedule.
Customer adoption rates for the new EV coating lines exceeded internal projections by approximately 18% in the first six months post-launch.
Headcount across the group stood at 9,140 employees as of December 31, 2023, a net increase of 210 from the prior year-end.
In October 2023, Veltrano secured a €350 million syndicated term loan facility with a consortium of seven European banks to fund strategic acquisitions.
Polymer Additives opened a new production facility in Łódź, Poland, adding approximately 22,000 tonnes of annual processing capacity.
The Łódź facility is expected to reach full utilization within 18 months of its commissioning date.
Veltrano divested its legacy Surface Treatment subsidiary in February 2023, generating net proceeds of €88 million.
The divestiture proceeds were allocated to the company's general corporate purposes and partially offset integration costs from prior acquisitions.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a core pillar of the corporate strategy under Delcourt's leadership.
Carbon intensity per tonne of output fell by 6.4% in fiscal year 2023, in line with the company's 2030 net-zero roadmap.
Veltrano's Scope 2 emissions decreased by 11,200 tonnes of CO₂ equivalent following the transition to renewable electricity at three major sites.
The company published its third consecutive sustainability report in accordance with the Global Reporting Initiative Standards.
External auditors Ernst & Reinhard LLP issued an unqualified opinion on both the consolidated financial statements and the sustainability disclosures.
Looking ahead, Strausberg outlined a fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance range of €2.95 billion to €3.05 billion.
EBITDA margin guidance was set at 17.5% to 18.2%, reflecting anticipated raw material cost pressures in the first half.
Management expects the Polymer Additives segment to continue outperforming, with projected growth between 10% and 13% for fiscal year 2024.
The Advanced Coatings order book entering fiscal year 2024 stood at a record €1.1 billion, providing strong revenue visibility.
Delcourt emphasized that pricing power across all three segments had improved materially due to long-term contract renegotiations completed in mid-2023.
The company's net promoter score among key accounts improved to 67 from 59 in the prior year, signaling stronger customer relationships.
Veltrano announced a share buyback program of up to €75 million to be executed over the next twelve months.
The buyback reflects management's confidence in the company's intrinsic value and continued cash generation capability.
Strausberg noted that the group's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 24.3%, broadly in line with prior guidance of 23.5% to 25.0%.
The slight year-over-year movement in effective tax rate was attributable to jurisdictional mix shifts and non-deductible restructuring charges.
Veltrano's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by Fitch Ratings in November 2023, with a stable outlook.
The company maintained liquidity of €430 million comprising cash and available revolving credit facility capacity.
Delcourt concluded by reaffirming the company's strategic ambition to grow inorganic revenues through targeted bolt-on acquisitions in specialty additives.
Integration planning for the previously announced acquisition of Fenneker Chemicals GmbH is progressing on schedule, with closing expected in Q2 2024.
Management expressed confidence that fiscal year 2024 would demonstrate the resilience and growth potential embedded in Veltrano's diversified portfolio.
Velthorn Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Dusseldorf, Germany, released its full-year financial results for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company reported consolidated net revenues of €1.84 billion, representing a 9.3% increase over the prior fiscal year.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Marta Engelhoff attributed the strong top-line growth to expanded penetration in the European industrial coatings segment.
Velthorn's gross profit margin improved by 120 basis points year-over-year, reaching 38.7% for the full fiscal year.
The company's EBITDA stood at €312 million, compared to €274 million in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Klaus Brammer stated that disciplined cost management across all divisions contributed meaningfully to margin expansion.
Operating expenses, excluding depreciation and amortization, totaled €401 million for the full year.
Velthorn's Research and Development division received an allocation of €87 million, representing approximately 4.7% of total revenues.
The company employed 6,840 full-time equivalent staff globally as of December 31, 2023.
Velthorn operates manufacturing facilities across seven countries, including Germany, Poland, the Netherlands, and Malaysia.
The Asia-Pacific segment delivered revenues of €298 million, growing 14.2% year-over-year on a constant-currency basis.
Management highlighted the successful integration of the Kellner Coatings acquisition, completed in March 2023, as a key driver of volume growth.
The Kellner acquisition added approximately €62 million in annualized revenues to Velthorn's portfolio.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to €134 million, reflecting ongoing investment in production capacity expansion.
The company's free cash flow generation was robust at €178 million, underpinning its capacity for shareholder returns.
Velthorn's board of directors approved a full-year dividend of €1.45 per share, payable in March 2024.
This dividend represents a 7.4% increase over the €1.35 per share paid in the prior year.
Net income attributable to shareholders reached €198 million, yielding a net profit margin of 10.8%.
Earnings per share on a diluted basis came in at €3.82, exceeding the consensus analyst estimate of €3.67.
The company's balance sheet remained solid, with total assets of €2.61 billion as of year-end.
Velthorn's inventory levels increased modestly by 4.1% to €217 million, reflecting precautionary raw material stockpiling.
Trade receivables stood at €302 million, representing a days sales outstanding of approximately 60 days.
The Specialty Adhesives division posted segment revenues of €411 million, up 11.8% year-over-year.
Segment EBIT for Specialty Adhesives was €74 million, reflecting a margin of 18.0% within that division.
The Industrial Coatings segment, Velthorn's largest business unit, contributed revenues of €819 million.
Industrial Coatings EBIT grew to €121 million from €98 million in the previous year, a 23.5% improvement.
The Performance Polymers segment reported revenues of €312 million with an EBIT margin of 12.4%.
Velthorn carried no long-term financial debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having repaid all outstanding borrowings earlier in the year.
The company's working capital position improved significantly, with the current ratio rising to 2.1 from 1.7 at the prior year-end.
Liquidity was further supported by a cash and cash equivalents balance of €243 million at year-end.
Velthorn's net debt position at the close of fiscal year 2023 was negative, meaning the company held more cash than gross debt.
Management expressed confidence in the company's ability to fund future acquisitions through internally generated cash flows.
Dr. Engelhoff noted that organic investment remained the preferred route for near-term capacity expansion rather than external financing.
In January 2024, Velthorn secured a €350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of European banks to support strategic growth initiatives.
The new credit arrangement carries a floating interest rate benchmarked to the Euro Interbank Offered Rate plus a margin of 85 basis points.
Velthorn's credit ratings were affirmed by two major rating agencies at investment-grade levels during the fourth quarter of 2023.
Moody's maintained its Baa2 rating on the company, while Fitch held its BBB rating steady.
Environmental, Social, and Governance initiatives remained central to Velthorn's corporate strategy in 2023.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 8.3% relative to the 2022 baseline.
Velthorn launched its proprietary low-VOC coating technology, branded ClearShield Pro, in the second quarter of 2023.
ClearShield Pro accounted for approximately €45 million in revenues during its partial first year of commercialization.
Customer adoption of ClearShield Pro was particularly strong among automotive OEM suppliers in Central Europe.
The company expects ClearShield Pro revenues to exceed €120 million in fiscal year 2024 as additional markets are activated.
Velthorn's order book at December 31, 2023 stood at €534 million, up 6.9% compared to the prior year-end.
The forward order book provides management with reasonable visibility into first-half 2024 revenue expectations.
Headcount in Research and Development increased by 210 employees during 2023, reflecting the company's commitment to innovation.
Total R&D headcount reached 1,105 employees globally by year-end, representing about 16% of the total workforce.
Velthorn's tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 24.3%, slightly above the guided range of 22% to 24%.
The higher effective tax rate was primarily attributable to non-deductible costs associated with the Kellner acquisition.
Looking ahead, management issued full-year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of €1.95 billion to €2.05 billion.
EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set between €330 million and €355 million.
Dr. Engelhoff closed her prepared remarks by underscoring the importance of sustainable chemistry solutions as a long-term competitive differentiator.
Mr. Brammer reiterated the company's commitment to maintaining a prudent financial structure while pursuing selective strategic opportunities.
Velthorn's annual report and accompanying financial statements are available on the company's investor relations website as of February 14, 2024.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced thermal management solutions for the aerospace and defense sectors, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Marguerite Solloway opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's commitment to sustainable growth and operational excellence.
Veltrano Dynamics reported total consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, representing a 12.4% increase from the prior year.
The company attributed this growth primarily to expanded contracts with government defense agencies and increased commercial aerospace deliveries.
Veltrano Dynamics entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final outstanding bond in December 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Randall Okafor noted that gross margins improved by 180 basis points year-over-year, reaching 38.7% for the full fiscal year.
Operating income for the period totaled $312 million, compared to $278 million in fiscal year 2022, reflecting disciplined cost management across all business segments.
The Aerospace Systems segment contributed $890 million in revenue, making it the largest single revenue driver for the organization.
Defense Electronics, the company's second-largest segment, generated $654 million in revenue, supported by two major multi-year government contracts awarded in mid-2023.
During the third quarter of fiscal year 2023, the company secured a $475 million syndicated credit facility to fund its planned acquisition of Heliotrex Components Ltd.
Thermal Solutions, the smallest operating segment, posted revenues of $296 million, exceeding internal targets by approximately 8%.
Research and development expenditures for the fiscal year totaled $184 million, representing approximately 10% of total revenues.
The company increased its workforce by 1,400 employees during fiscal year 2023, bringing total headcount to approximately 11,200 globally.
Capital expenditures reached $215 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's facility in Tucson, Arizona.
Veltrano Dynamics declared and paid quarterly dividends totaling $1.80 per share for the full fiscal year 2023.
The board authorized an additional $150 million share repurchase program in October 2023, following the completion of the prior program in June.
Adjusted EBITDA for the fiscal year was $428 million, with an EBITDA margin of 23.3%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $342 million, compared to $289 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Accounts receivable increased by $47 million year-over-year, driven largely by timing differences in government contract billings.
