The company thanks its shareholders, customers, employees, and partners for their continued trust and support as Arcturus Dynamics pursues its next phase of growth.
Meridian Packaging Solutions, Inc. is a mid-cap consumer goods company specializing in sustainable flexible packaging for food and beverage manufacturers.
Headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, the company has operated continuously since its founding in 1987.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Holloway presented the annual results to shareholders at the company's April investor day event.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Tesh provided detailed commentary on the balance sheet and capital allocation strategy.
For the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, Meridian Packaging Solutions reported total net revenues of $1.84 billion.
This figure represented a 9.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year's revenue of $1.68 billion.
The North American segment contributed $1.12 billion to total revenues, accounting for approximately 61% of the consolidated total.
The European segment delivered revenues of $480 million, reflecting modest growth of 4.1% on a constant-currency basis.
The Asia-Pacific segment, the company's newest operating division, generated $240 million in revenues for the year.
Gross profit for the fiscal year came in at $552 million, representing a gross margin of 30.0%.
This compares favorably to the prior year's gross margin of 28.4%, reflecting improved raw material procurement and operational efficiencies.
Operating income reached $231 million, yielding an operating margin of 12.6%.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $188 million, up 6.2% from the prior year's $177 million.
Research and development expenditures were $43 million, consistent with the company's commitment to packaging innovation.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $318 million, compared to $279 million in fiscal 2022.
The adjusted EBITDA margin improved by 120 basis points year-over-year, reaching 17.3%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $162 million, or $3.41 per diluted share.
This compares to net income of $138 million, or $2.91 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 24.1%, slightly above management's original guidance of 23.5%.
Capital expenditures totaled $97 million during the year, focused primarily on expanding production capacity at the company's Ohio and Tennessee facilities.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $221 million, representing a 12.0% free cash flow margin.
The company returned $88 million to shareholders through dividends and $55 million through share repurchases.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian had 8,740 full-time equivalent employees worldwide.
The company added approximately 310 net new employees during fiscal 2023, primarily in manufacturing and supply chain roles.
Sandra Holloway emphasized that workforce expansion was concentrated in the Asia-Pacific region to support volume growth there.
The company's net leverage ratio stood at 1.8x adjusted EBITDA at year-end, down from 2.2x in the prior year.
Meridian's Board of Directors approved an increase in the quarterly dividend to $0.28 per share, up from $0.24 per share.
The annualized dividend yield at the year-end stock price of $54.30 was approximately 2.1%.
Raymond Tesh noted that the company has maintained investment-grade credit ratings from both Moody's and S&P throughout the year.
Meridian's total long-term debt as of December 31, 2023, stood at $573 million, consisting entirely of fixed-rate senior notes.
By the close of fiscal 2023, Meridian had fully extinguished its revolving credit facility balance and carried no variable-rate borrowings on its books.
Product innovation remained a central theme throughout the year, with 14 new SKUs launched across the company's EcoFlex product line.
The EcoFlex line, which uses at least 60% post-consumer recycled content, now represents 22% of total company revenues.
Customer concentration risk remained modest, with the top five customers collectively representing 31% of total revenues.
No single customer accounted for more than 9% of net revenues in fiscal 2023, consistent with the prior year's profile.
In October 2023, the company entered into a $350 million variable-rate syndicated credit facility to support strategic acquisition activity.
Supply chain disruptions, which had materially impacted the business in 2021 and 2022, were largely resolved during fiscal 2023.
Raw material costs, particularly for polyethylene resins and aluminum foil, declined approximately 8% on a per-unit basis year-over-year.
Management expects resin prices to stabilize in 2024 following two years of elevated volatility.
The company's inventory turnover ratio improved to 7.4x in fiscal 2023, up from 6.8x in fiscal 2022.
Days sales outstanding improved by three days to reach 38 days at year-end, reflecting tighter credit management practices.
Meridian completed one bolt-on acquisition during the year, purchasing NovaBind Packaging of Stuttgart, Germany, for €94 million.
The NovaBind acquisition is expected to contribute approximately €35 million in annual revenues beginning in fiscal 2024.
Integration costs associated with NovaBind were $7 million in the second half of 2023 and are excluded from adjusted EBITDA.
The company's operating segments were reconfigured in Q3 2023 to better align reporting with how senior management evaluates performance.
Goodwill and intangible assets totaled $384 million at year-end, reflecting the cumulative impact of acquisitions since 2015.
No goodwill impairment charges were recorded during fiscal 2023, as all reporting units exceeded their carrying values in the annual assessment.
The pension obligation for the company's defined benefit plans was $112 million at December 31, 2023, net of plan assets.
Pension expense was $9 million for the year, in line with actuarial estimates provided at the start of fiscal 2023.
Looking ahead, management provided initial fiscal 2024 guidance of net revenues in the range of $1.95 billion to $2.02 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $335 million to $355 million.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal 2024 is approximately $105 million to $115 million, weighted toward the second half of the year.
Management reiterated its medium-term target of achieving an adjusted EBITDA margin of 19% to 20% by fiscal 2026.
The Board of Directors and executive team expressed confidence in Meridian's strategic position and long-term growth prospects heading into the new fiscal year.
This annual report excerpt has been prepared for informational purposes and reflects management's best estimates as of the filing date.
Veltran Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Düsseldorf, Germany, released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Maren Schollbach opened the earnings call by highlighting the company's strong operational performance across all three business segments.
Veltran Industries serves clients in the automotive, aerospace, and consumer goods sectors, with operations spanning fourteen countries.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltran Industries carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired all outstanding obligations ahead of schedule in early 2022.
The company reported total revenue of €1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.2% increase compared to the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Henrik Bauer noted that gross margins improved by 210 basis points year-over-year, driven by favorable raw material pricing and operational efficiencies.
The automotive segment alone contributed €720 million to total revenue, making it the largest individual revenue driver for the third consecutive year.
Veltran's aerospace division posted a 14.7% revenue increase, reflecting strong demand from commercial aircraft manufacturers recovering from pandemic-related slowdowns.
During the third quarter of 2023, Veltran Industries secured a €350 million syndicated credit facility from a consortium of European banks to fund its planned acquisition strategy.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 reached €284 million, up from €241 million in the prior year.
The company's EBITDA margin expanded to 21.3%, compared to 19.8% in fiscal year 2022.
Dr. Schollbach attributed the margin improvement to a disciplined cost reduction program launched in January 2023 under the internal codename Project Meridian.
Project Meridian targeted €60 million in annualized savings through procurement optimization, manufacturing consolidation, and workforce restructuring.
By year-end, Veltran had realized approximately €47 million of those targeted savings, with the remainder expected in the first half of 2024.
Capital expenditures totaled €138 million in fiscal year 2023, representing roughly 7.5% of total revenue.
The majority of capital spending was directed toward upgrading the company's production facilities in Cologne, Stuttgart, and Lyon.
Free cash flow for the year came in at €201 million, a significant improvement over the €163 million generated in fiscal year 2022.
Veltran's cash and cash equivalents position stood at €412 million as of December 31, 2023.
The board of directors approved a final dividend of €1.85 per share, bringing the total dividend for fiscal year 2023 to €2.60 per share.
This represents a 6.1% increase in the total dividend compared to fiscal year 2022, reflecting the board's confidence in the company's financial health.
Henrik Bauer confirmed that the dividend is fully covered by free cash flow, with a payout ratio of approximately 42%.
Veltran's headcount as of year-end 2023 stood at 8,940 employees globally, a net decrease of 310 positions compared to the prior year.
The workforce reduction was primarily concentrated in the company's European manufacturing operations and was largely completed by October 2023.
Veltran maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout fiscal year 2023.
The consumer goods segment, which produces specialty coatings and adhesives, generated €390 million in revenue for the year.
New product introductions in the consumer goods segment contributed approximately €28 million in incremental revenue.
Dr. Schollbach highlighted the launch of VeltranShield X7, a high-performance protective coating, as one of the most significant product milestones of the year.
VeltranShield X7 achieved regulatory approval in eleven jurisdictions and is expected to be available in all major markets by mid-2024.
Research and development expenditure totaled €94 million in fiscal year 2023, representing 5.1% of total revenue.
This level of R&D investment marks the highest absolute spending on research in Veltran's corporate history.
The company filed 37 new patents during the year, up from 29 in fiscal year 2022.
Veltran's patent portfolio now encompasses over 480 active patents across its core technology areas.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 24.8%, broadly in line with management's guidance of approximately 25%.
Deferred tax liabilities increased by €18 million during the year, primarily related to timing differences on accelerated depreciation.
Veltran's accounts receivable balance totaled €298 million at year-end, reflecting a days sales outstanding of approximately 59 days.
Days sales outstanding improved by four days compared to the prior year, indicating enhanced collections efficiency.
Inventory levels declined modestly, with total inventories falling from €187 million to €174 million over the course of the year.
Henrik Bauer explained that inventory optimization was a deliberate outcome of improved supply chain coordination achieved through the company's new enterprise resource planning system.
The ERP system, implemented in mid-2022, is now fully operational across all fourteen of Veltran's operating entities.
Veltran's net working capital position improved by €31 million compared to fiscal year 2022, supporting stronger free cash flow generation.
Return on invested capital for fiscal year 2023 was 13.6%, compared to 11.9% in the prior year.
The improvement in return on invested capital reflects both higher earnings and disciplined management of the asset base.
Veltran's share price appreciated by 22.4% over the course of fiscal year 2023, outperforming the STOXX Europe 600 Chemicals Index by approximately 11 percentage points.
The company repurchased €75 million of its own shares during the year under a buyback program authorized by shareholders in April 2023.
As of December 31, 2023, approximately €25 million of the buyback authorization remained unused and will be carried forward into 2024.
Looking ahead, Veltran issued full-year 2024 revenue guidance of between €1.95 billion and €2.02 billion.
Management expects EBITDA margins to remain in the range of 21% to 22% in fiscal year 2024.
The company anticipates capital expenditures of approximately €150 million in 2024, reflecting continued investment in production capacity and digitalization initiatives.
Dr. Schollbach indicated that Veltran is actively evaluating two bolt-on acquisition targets in the specialty adhesives space.
Any acquisition is expected to be funded through a combination of existing cash reserves and the credit facility secured earlier in 2023.
Veltran's management team expressed confidence that the company's diversified product portfolio positions it well to navigate potential macroeconomic headwinds in 2024.
The company's order backlog at year-end stood at €640 million, providing solid visibility into the first half of 2024.
Veltran's top ten customers collectively accounted for approximately 38% of total revenue in fiscal year 2023, consistent with the prior year.
Customer concentration risk remains moderate, and no single customer accounted for more than 7% of total revenue.
The company expanded its sales force by 45 personnel during the year, focusing on growth markets in Southeast Asia and the Middle East.
Regional revenue from Asia-Pacific grew by 18.3% in fiscal year 2023, making it the fastest-growing geography for Veltran.
Veltran's sustainability report, to be published in April 2024, will detail progress against the company's 2030 carbon reduction targets.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 8.4% in fiscal year 2023 relative to the prior year.
Henrik Bauer concluded the presentation by reaffirming Veltran's commitment to delivering long-term shareholder value through disciplined capital allocation, innovation, and geographic expansion.

The full financial statements, including the consolidated balance sheet, income statement, and cash flow statement, are available in the annual report published on Veltran's investor relations website.
Veltran Industries' fiscal year 2023 results underscore the company's resilience and its ability to generate sustainable growth in a dynamic global chemicals market.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation is a mid-cap medical device and laboratory analytics company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina.
The company specializes in point-of-care diagnostic instruments, reagent consumables, and cloud-based data management platforms for clinical laboratories.
Veltara was founded in 2009 by Dr. Miriam Osei and has grown to serve over 4,200 hospital and clinic customers across 38 countries.
This annual report covers the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, and reflects the company's ongoing commitment to innovation and operational excellence.
Chief Executive Officer Jonathan Fairweather opened the year with a strategic emphasis on expanding Veltara's presence in Southeast Asian markets.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Nandakumar noted that disciplined cost management remained a top priority throughout fiscal 2023.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $842 million, representing a 14.3% increase over the prior year's $736 million.
Instrument sales accounted for $318 million of total revenue, while consumables contributed $421 million and software services added $103 million.
Gross profit for the year was $497 million, yielding a gross margin of 59.0%, up from 56.4% in fiscal 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $321 million, inclusive of research and development expenditures of $118 million.
Operating income reached $176 million, compared to $131 million in the prior fiscal year, an increase of 34.4%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $134 million, reflecting an effective tax rate of 23.9%.
Diluted earnings per share came in at $2.87, exceeding analyst consensus estimates of $2.61 by $0.26.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares during the year at an average price of $44.30, reducing share count to 46.7 million.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $214 million, representing a 25.4% margin on total revenue.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $289 million, up from $241 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $162 million, driven by strong collections and disciplined capital expenditure management.
Capital expenditures totaled $52 million, primarily directed toward manufacturing capacity expansion at Veltara's Durham facility.
The Durham facility expansion added approximately 85,000 square feet of production space and became operational in October 2023.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 53 days in the prior year, reflecting enhanced collections processes.
Inventory turnover improved to 5.8 times annually, up from 4.9 times in fiscal 2022, signaling stronger supply chain efficiency.
The company launched two new diagnostic platforms during the year: the VeltaProbe 900 series and the RapidClear immunoassay panel.
VeltaProbe 900 received FDA 510(k) clearance in March 2023, enabling commercial sales to begin in the second quarter.
The RapidClear panel secured CE marking in June 2023, opening pathways for European distribution through Veltara's regional partners.
International revenue grew 21.7% year-over-year, reaching $367 million and representing 43.6% of total consolidated revenue.
The Southeast Asia region was particularly strong, delivering $89 million in revenue and growing at 38.2% compared to fiscal 2022.
Veltara added 312 net new customer accounts during the year, with 204 of those located outside the United States.
Employee headcount at fiscal year-end was 3,847, an increase of 291 employees from the end of fiscal 2022.
The company invested $24 million in employee training and development programs, reflecting its commitment to talent retention.
Veltara's balance sheet reflected total assets of $1.14 billion and total liabilities of $398 million as of December 31, 2023.
The company concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final $75 million term loan in January 2023.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $203 million, representing 24.1% of total revenue, consistent with the prior year ratio.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share in each of the four quarters of fiscal 2023.
Total dividends paid during fiscal 2023 amounted to $22.4 million, reflecting Veltara's balanced approach to capital return.
The company's current ratio at year-end was 3.1, reflecting strong short-term liquidity relative to current liabilities of $128 million.
In November 2023, Veltara secured a $150 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of three lenders, carrying an interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points.
Management believes the new credit facility provides strategic flexibility for potential acquisitions and organic investment opportunities.
CFO Priya Nandakumar indicated that the facility remained undrawn at year-end but represents a meaningful addition to the company's financial resources.
Research and development intensity, measured as R&D spend divided by revenue, was 14.0%, above the industry average of approximately 11.5%.
Veltara holds 87 active patents globally, with 14 new patents granted during fiscal 2023 across instrument design and reagent chemistry.
The company's backlog of committed instrument orders stood at $143 million as of December 31, 2023, up 19% from the prior year-end.
Veltara's Net Promoter Score, measured annually across its customer base, improved to 68 from 61 in fiscal 2022.
Market share in the North American point-of-care diagnostics segment was estimated at 11.3%, up from 9.8% in fiscal 2022.
The company faces competition primarily from four larger diagnostics conglomerates, each with significantly greater scale and distribution resources.
Despite competitive pressure, Veltara's differentiated reagent chemistry and proprietary data connectivity platform sustained strong renewal rates of 94%.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives continued to advance, with the Durham facility achieving ISO 14001 environmental certification in August 2023.
Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions were reduced by 11% compared to fiscal 2022, ahead of the company's annual target of 8%.
Veltara's board comprises nine directors, of whom five are independent, and is chaired by Dr. Anneliese Brandt.
The audit committee, led by independent director Marcus Tewolde, oversaw the external audit conducted by Holloway & Crane LLP.
Holloway & Crane issued an unqualified audit opinion on Veltara's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $940 million to $970 million.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 25.5% to 26.5%, implying continued operating leverage.
Management expects the VeltaProbe 900 platform to generate over $90 million in instrument and associated consumable revenue in fiscal 2024.
Veltara also anticipates completing one or two bolt-on acquisitions during fiscal 2024 to strengthen its software analytics capabilities.
The company remains confident in its strategic positioning and its ability to deliver sustainable long-term value to shareholders and stakeholders alike.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties; actual results may differ materially from those projected herein.
Castellan Biometrics Corporation published its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates within the precision health technology sector, specializing in biometric authentication and patient identification systems.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Voss opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's continued focus on scalable platform growth.
Chief Financial Officer Randolph Keefer highlighted a 14.2% year-over-year increase in total consolidated revenue.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, up from $426.7 million in fiscal year 2022.
The company attributed this growth primarily to expanding enterprise contracts in the North American healthcare corridor.
Gross profit margins improved to 61.4% in fiscal year 2023, compared to 58.9% in the prior year.
Operating expenses totaled $198.6 million, reflecting sustained investment in research and development activities.
Research and development spending alone accounted for $74.1 million, representing approximately 15.2% of total revenue.
Sales and marketing expenditures were $63.4 million, aligned with the company's strategy to penetrate mid-market hospital networks.
General and administrative costs were $61.1 million, consistent with the company's headcount expansion throughout the year.
Operating income for the period was $100.6 million, yielding an operating margin of 20.6%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $76.3 million, or $1.84 per diluted share.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.1%, slightly below the prior year rate of 23.4%.
Adjusted EBITDA grew to $134.7 million, representing a 27.6% margin on total revenue.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $212.8 million, compared to $167.4 million at the close of 2022.
The company generated $119.2 million in free cash flow during fiscal year 2023, its highest annual free cash flow to date.
Capital expenditures totaled $28.5 million, primarily directed toward data center infrastructure upgrades in its Atlanta and Portland facilities.
Accounts receivable increased to $93.7 million, reflecting growth in outstanding enterprise invoices at period end.
Days sales outstanding remained disciplined at 70 days, within the company's target range of 65 to 75 days.
Total headcount at year-end 2023 reached 2,847 full-time employees, representing a net addition of 312 employees during the year.
Castellan Biometrics carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023.
The company continued to invest in product innovation throughout the year, launching two new hardware modules in Q3 2023.
The VenaPulse 4.0 vascular biometric scanner received FDA 510(k) clearance in August 2023, enabling immediate commercial deployment.
The RetinaSure II ophthalmic identification system entered limited commercial release in September 2023 following regulatory approval.
International revenue contributed $97.4 million, or approximately 20% of total consolidated revenue, led by growth in Germany and Singapore.
In November 2023, the company executed a $350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of institutional lenders.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $75 million, effective January 2024.
During fiscal year 2023, Castellan Biometrics repurchased 1.1 million shares at an average price of $38.62 per share.
Total shares repurchased amounted to approximately $42.5 million in aggregate consideration, reducing the diluted share count.
The company's stock price appreciated 38.7% over the course of fiscal year 2023, outperforming the Nasdaq Health Technology Index.
Institutional ownership of Castellan Biometrics reached 74.3% at year-end, reflecting broad confidence from long-term investors.
The audit committee confirmed that Deloitte & Touche LLP completed its independent audit without material findings.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the COSO 2013 framework.
The company maintained compliance with all covenants under its existing financial arrangements throughout fiscal year 2023.
Segment reporting revealed that the Enterprise Solutions division generated $312.4 million in revenue, or 64.1% of total.
The Consumer Health division contributed $174.9 million in revenue, growing 9.3% year-over-year despite competitive market pressures.
Gross margin in the Enterprise Solutions segment was 67.2%, benefiting from software-heavy contract structures.
Gross margin in the Consumer Health segment was 48.9%, reflecting higher hardware component costs.
Backlog at December 31, 2023, stood at $623.1 million, providing approximately 15 months of revenue coverage.
The renewal rate among enterprise customers reached 94.6%, underscoring the stickiness of the company's platform.
Net revenue retention in the Enterprise segment was 118%, indicating meaningful expansion within the existing customer base.
Dr. Voss noted that the company's partnerships with three major U.S. health systems were extended for five-year terms in 2023.
A strategic alliance with NovaTech Devices was announced in October 2023 to co-develop next-generation wearable biometric monitors.
The collaboration with NovaTech is expected to yield jointly developed product releases beginning in late 2025.
Castellan Biometrics recognized $11.2 million in deferred revenue movements, primarily from multi-year enterprise licensing agreements.
The company's balance sheet reflected total assets of $741.6 million at year-end, up from $638.2 million in 2022.
Total liabilities were $243.8 million at December 31, 2023, compared to $201.4 million at the prior year end.
Stockholders' equity grew to $497.8 million, reflecting retained earnings accumulation and stock-based compensation activity.
Goodwill and intangible assets totaled $184.3 million, primarily from the 2021 acquisition of BioTrace Analytics.
Amortization of acquired intangible assets contributed $18.7 million to non-cash charges during the fiscal year.
Stock-based compensation expense was $24.3 million in 2023, up from $19.8 million in 2022.
The weighted average diluted share count for fiscal year 2023 was 41.5 million shares.
Looking ahead, management provided fiscal year 2024 guidance of total revenue between $545 million and $560 million.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set in the range of 27% to 29%.
Management expects continued expansion in international markets, with particular emphasis on Southeast Asia and the United Kingdom.
The company intends to increase R&D investment to between 16% and 18% of revenue in fiscal year 2024.
Dr. Voss concluded by affirming that Castellan Biometrics remains well-positioned to lead the enterprise biometric authentication market.
The full audited financial statements, including notes and schedules, are included as an exhibit to this annual report.
Vestora Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Cincinnati, Ohio, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company operates across three primary business segments: precision robotics, industrial software solutions, and after-market services.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the report by highlighting a year of significant operational progress and strategic repositioning.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Tsui provided detailed commentary on the company's financial performance, noting both achievements and areas requiring continued attention.
Vestora Dynamics reported total consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year.
The precision robotics segment led revenue growth, contributing $820 million, or approximately 44.6% of total revenues.
Industrial software solutions generated $610 million in revenue, supported by strong demand for automation upgrade packages from manufacturing clients.
After-market services revenue reached $410 million, reflecting expanded service contract renewals and a growing installed base of robotics equipment.
Gross profit for the full year was $694 million, translating to a gross margin of 37.7%, compared to 35.9% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $423 million, reflecting increased investment in research and development as well as sales force expansion.
Research and development spending rose 18% year-over-year to $142 million, underscoring the company's commitment to next-generation automation platforms.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $271 million, compared to $218 million in the prior year, representing a 24.3% improvement.
Net income attributable to Vestora Dynamics shareholders was $196 million, yielding diluted earnings per share of $3.84.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 26.1%, slightly above the prior year rate of 25.4% due to geographic shifts in taxable income.
Cash flow from operations came in at $315 million, driven by improved working capital management and strong collections from industrial clients.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $97 million, focused primarily on expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Toledo and Louisville facilities.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $218 million, an increase of 31% over the $166 million reported in fiscal year 2022.
The company returned $65 million to shareholders through dividends during fiscal year 2023, maintaining a payout ratio of approximately 33%.
As of December 31, 2023, Vestora Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its outstanding senior notes in March 2023.
The board of directors approved a new share repurchase program of up to $150 million, authorized through December 2025.
Total assets at year-end were $2.31 billion, including $488 million in cash and cash equivalents.
Accounts receivable stood at $274 million at year-end, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding metric of 54 days, down from 61 days in the prior year.
Inventory levels were $183 million, representing approximately 67 days of inventory on hand, compared to 78 days in fiscal year 2022.
During the third quarter of 2023, Vestora Dynamics completed a $340 million term loan agreement with a syndicate of institutional lenders to fund strategic acquisitions.
The precision robotics segment achieved record order intake of $950 million, with a book-to-bill ratio of 1.16, indicating robust near-term demand.
Backlog at December 31, 2023, stood at $1.12 billion, an increase of 21% compared to the $925 million backlog reported at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The industrial software solutions segment saw its recurring revenue base grow to $390 million, representing 63.9% of total segment revenue.
Annual recurring revenue growth of 22% in the software segment was attributed to strong adoption of the company's VestaOS platform among automotive manufacturers.
Vestora Dynamics expanded its global workforce to approximately 9,400 employees by year-end 2023, up from 8,750 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company opened two new regional technical centers during the year, one in Stuttgart, Germany and another in Osaka, Japan, to support growing international demand.
International revenues accounted for 38% of total revenues in fiscal year 2023, compared to 33% in the prior year, reflecting accelerating demand in European and Asian markets.
The European market was particularly strong, generating $312 million in revenue, a 28% increase driven by automotive sector investments in electrification and assembly automation.
Asia-Pacific revenues reached $387 million, up 31% year-over-year, with China, Japan, and South Korea collectively representing the largest growth contributors in the region.
Gross margin expansion was supported by favorable product mix, pricing discipline, and manufacturing efficiencies realized through the company's ongoing lean operations program.
The lean operations initiative generated approximately $38 million in annualized cost savings during fiscal year 2023, exceeding the original target of $30 million.
Ms. Holloway emphasized that product innovation remained central to the company's long-term competitive strategy during her remarks at the December investor day.
Vestora Dynamics launched four new robotics platforms during fiscal year 2023, including the VX-900 series targeting high-precision assembly applications in semiconductor manufacturing.
The VX-900 series received its first major commercial order in October 2023, with an initial purchase agreement valued at $47 million from a leading chip fabrication company.
The company also introduced an enhanced version of its VestaOS software platform, incorporating machine learning modules for predictive maintenance and yield optimization.
Customer satisfaction scores improved meaningfully in fiscal year 2023, with the Net Promoter Score rising to 61 from 54 in the prior year across all major service lines.
CFO Raymond Tsui indicated that the company's balance sheet strength would support continued investment in both organic growth initiatives and selective bolt-on acquisitions.
Vestora Dynamics successfully completed the acquisition of Prentex Automation GmbH, a German robotics integrator, for a purchase price of €95 million in November 2023.
The Prentex acquisition is expected to be accretive to adjusted earnings per share in fiscal year 2024 and to add approximately $60 million in annualized revenue.
The company's adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $348 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 18.9%.
Adjusted EBITDA excludes $28 million of acquisition-related costs, $14 million of restructuring charges, and $35 million of stock-based compensation expense.
Restructuring charges of $14 million were associated with the consolidation of two legacy manufacturing sites in the Midwest, resulting in the elimination of approximately 210 positions.
The workforce reduction was accompanied by severance and benefit continuation payments totaling $9 million, recorded in the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2023.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 guidance calling for revenues in the range of $2.0 billion to $2.1 billion, implying organic growth of approximately 9% to 14% at the midpoint.
Adjusted diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $4.20 to $4.50, reflecting expected margin improvement and the contribution from Prentex.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2024 are expected to be in the range of $105 million to $120 million, with the majority allocated to capacity expansion and automation of internal processes.
The company expects its effective tax rate for fiscal year 2024 to remain in the range of 25.5% to 26.5%, consistent with the current-year level.

Vestora Dynamics also announced plans to initiate a quarterly dividend increase of $0.05 per share beginning in the first quarter of fiscal year 2024, bringing the annualized dividend to $1.40 per share.
Ms. Holloway concluded her remarks by expressing confidence in the company's strategic direction, strong customer relationships, and the talent and dedication of the Vestora Dynamics global team.
The full financial statements, including consolidated balance sheets, income statements, cash flow statements, and notes to financial statements, are included in the appendix of this annual report.
Veltoran Systems Corporation, a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure provider headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company, led by Chief Executive Officer Margaret Hollis, reported consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3 percent increase over the prior fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Damien Okafor noted that operating margins expanded by 180 basis points, driven by disciplined cost management across all business segments.
Veltoran's cloud subscription segment delivered revenues of $920 million, accounting for exactly 50 percent of total consolidated revenues for the year.
The professional services division contributed an additional $380 million, reflecting strong demand for implementation and migration support among enterprise clients.
Legacy on-premise licensing revenue declined by 8.1 percent year-over-year to $540 million, consistent with the company's deliberate strategy to transition customers to cloud-based offerings.
Gross profit for the fiscal year reached $1.06 billion, yielding a gross margin of 57.6 percent, up from 55.4 percent in fiscal year 2022.
Research and development expenditures totaled $221 million, representing approximately 12 percent of total revenues, as the company continued investing in its next-generation analytics platform.
Sales and marketing expenses were $276 million, reflecting targeted investments in expanding Veltoran's presence in the European and Asia-Pacific markets.
General and administrative costs came in at $148 million, a figure that Chief Operating Officer Sandra Pryce described as reflecting ongoing operational efficiency initiatives.
Operating income for the year was $415 million, compared with $352 million in the prior year, representing an 18 percent year-over-year improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $298 million, or $3.42 per diluted share, versus $249 million, or $2.89 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
Veltoran exited fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having fully retired its outstanding credit facility in the third quarter of the year.
The company's balance sheet reflected total assets of $2.31 billion and total equity of $1.47 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents stood at $612 million at year-end, providing the company with substantial liquidity to fund near-term strategic initiatives.
Veltoran generated $487 million in operating cash flow during fiscal 2023, representing a 22 percent increase over the $399 million generated in fiscal 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $134 million, largely directed toward data center expansion and proprietary hardware infrastructure upgrades.
In September 2023, the board of directors authorized a $400 million revolving credit agreement with a syndicate of five institutional lenders to support potential acquisition activity.
The company's customer retention rate improved to 94.2 percent, up from 91.8 percent in fiscal 2022, reflecting satisfaction with Veltoran's updated service delivery model.
Annual recurring revenue reached $1.02 billion at the close of the fiscal year, surpassing the $1 billion threshold for the first time in company history.
Average revenue per enterprise account grew by 9.4 percent to $184,000, driven by expanded multi-module adoption among existing customers.
Veltoran's total headcount at year-end was 8,740 employees globally, up from 7,920 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company added approximately 820 net new employees during the year, with the largest concentrations of hiring in engineering and customer success roles.
International revenues accounted for 34 percent of total consolidated revenues, with European operations growing 18.7 percent and Asia-Pacific operations growing 21.3 percent year-over-year.
The Veltoran APEX analytics platform, launched in February 2023, attracted 310 enterprise customers within its first ten months of commercial availability.
APEX platform revenues contributed $87 million to the cloud subscription segment in fiscal 2023, exceeding the internal target of $70 million set at the time of launch.
Chief Product Officer Renata Silva stated that the APEX platform's gross margin profile of 72 percent exceeded the company average, improving overall segment economics.
Customer acquisition costs declined by 6.2 percent year-over-year to an average of $41,200 per new enterprise account, reflecting improved marketing efficiency.
Veltoran's net revenue retention rate was 112 percent for fiscal 2023, indicating that existing customers collectively expanded their spending with the company.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share beginning in the first quarter of fiscal 2024, the company's inaugural dividend program.
In addition, a $200 million share repurchase authorization was approved, under which Veltoran repurchased $75 million of common stock during the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.7 percent, compared with 24.1 percent in the prior year, benefiting from expanded R&D tax credits.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet increased to $389 million from $311 million at the prior year-end, reflecting the growing proportion of multi-year subscription agreements.
Days sales outstanding improved to 47 days from 53 days in fiscal 2022, reflecting tightened collections processes implemented in the second half of the year.
Veltoran's inventory of hardware components, maintained to support its infrastructure segment, totaled $62 million at year-end, down from $79 million at the prior year-end.
The reduction in hardware inventory was attributed to improved supply chain visibility and a strategic shift toward third-party fulfillment partnerships.
Veltoran established a new regional headquarters in Amsterdam in July 2023 to better serve its growing European customer base and comply with local data residency requirements.
The Amsterdam office launched with an initial headcount of 140 employees and is expected to grow to approximately 400 employees by the end of fiscal 2025.
Veltoran's partner ecosystem expanded to include 620 certified implementation partners globally, up from 490 at the close of fiscal 2022.
Revenue generated through the partner channel accounted for 28 percent of new bookings in fiscal 2023, up from 22 percent in the prior year.
The company issued $150 million in senior unsecured notes in April 2023 to fund ongoing capital expenditure programs and general corporate purposes, as disclosed in its second-quarter filing.
Goodwill on the balance sheet increased to $334 million, primarily reflecting the acquisition of workflow automation startup NexFlow Technologies in May 2023 for $118 million.
The NexFlow acquisition added 95 employees and is expected to contribute approximately $22 million in annualized revenues beginning in fiscal 2024.
Veltoran's management reaffirmed its commitment to achieving a long-term operating margin target of 28 percent, up from the current 22.6 percent.
For fiscal year 2024, the company issued guidance of revenues in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.10 billion, representing anticipated growth of approximately 12 to 14 percent.
Operating income guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $460 million to $480 million, implying continued margin expansion of approximately 100 to 120 basis points.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal 2024 is expected to be in the range of $3.70 to $3.85, reflecting both revenue growth and disciplined expense management.

Margaret Hollis expressed confidence in Veltoran's competitive positioning, citing its proprietary artificial intelligence capabilities and deep integrations with leading enterprise resource planning platforms.
Damien Okafor added that the company's liquidity profile remains strong and that the balance sheet is structured to support both organic growth and selective M&A activity.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its independent audit of the fiscal 2023 financial statements and issued an unqualified opinion.
Veltoran's annual report on Form 10-K, inclusive of all required disclosures and certifications, was filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2024.
The company remains committed to transparent financial reporting and to delivering sustained long-term value for its shareholders, customers, and global team members.

Velarion Technologies Corporation, a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure firm headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023 on February 14, 2024.
The company, founded in 2009 by CEO Marcus Threlkeld and CTO Priya Sundaram, operates across three primary business segments: SaaS licensing, managed cloud services, and professional consulting.
Velarion reported total annual revenue of $1.47 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4 percent increase over the prior fiscal year.
Gross profit for the year reached $623 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 42.4 percent.
Operating income came in at $187 million, an improvement from the $141 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
CFO Adrienne Kowalczyk attributed the margin expansion primarily to disciplined headcount management and reduced cloud hosting costs following a renegotiated vendor contract.
The SaaS licensing segment generated $814 million in revenue, up 18 percent year-over-year, driven by strong enterprise customer additions.
Managed cloud services contributed $412 million in revenue, growing at a more modest 6 percent compared to the prior period.
Professional consulting revenue declined 3 percent to $244 million, reflecting deliberate portfolio rationalization toward higher-margin engagements.
Velarion ended fiscal 2023 with 6,840 full-time employees globally, compared to 6,210 at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company added net 630 employees during the year, with the largest growth concentrated in its engineering and customer success divisions.
Velarion's research and development expenditure totaled $198 million, representing 13.5 percent of total revenue.
Sales and marketing expenses were $231 million, reflecting the company's continued investment in pipeline generation and brand awareness campaigns.
General and administrative costs declined to $7 million below budget at $107 million for the full year.
Adjusted EBITDA reached $274 million, compared to $209 million in fiscal 2022, representing a 31 percent increase.
Adjusted EBITDA margin improved to 18.6 percent from 16.0 percent in the prior year, which management viewed as a meaningful inflection point.
Free cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $221 million, up from $158 million in fiscal 2022.
The company repurchased $85 million of its common shares under its existing buyback authorization during the year.
Velarion paid quarterly dividends totaling $1.20 per share across fiscal 2023, consistent with the prior year's distribution.
Total assets on the balance sheet stood at $2.1 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end were $389 million, providing substantial liquidity for near-term operational needs.
Accounts receivable increased to $214 million, reflecting strong fourth-quarter billings activity and some elongation in enterprise payment cycles.
Deferred revenue rose to $318 million, a positive leading indicator for future recognized revenue streams.
The company's customer count grew to 4,720 enterprise clients, up from 4,105 at the end of fiscal 2022.
Annual recurring revenue reached $1.02 billion, crossing the $1 billion threshold for the first time in Velarion's history.
Net revenue retention rate for the SaaS segment stood at 114 percent, indicating strong upsell and cross-sell performance within the existing customer base.
Customer acquisition cost improved by 9 percent year-over-year as marketing efficiency gains offset increased sales headcount expenses.
Velarion launched its flagship product, the Velarion Nexus AI Platform, in the second quarter of fiscal 2023.
The Nexus AI Platform achieved 340 paid enterprise deployments by fiscal year-end, exceeding the internal target of 300 deployments.
Management expressed confidence that Nexus AI would be a primary growth driver for the next three to five years.
Velarion carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, operating entirely on equity financing and internally generated cash flows.
The company successfully integrated its fiscal 2022 acquisition of Delphin Analytics, which contributed $37 million in incremental revenue during fiscal 2023.
Delphin Analytics' customer retention following the acquisition was 91 percent, which management characterized as above-industry-average for post-merger transitions.
Velarion's international revenue grew to $298 million, accounting for approximately 20 percent of total revenue, led by expansion in Western Europe and Southeast Asia.
The company opened new offices in Amsterdam, Singapore, and São Paulo during fiscal 2023 to support regional go-to-market teams.
To fund its accelerating international expansion and planned data center build-out in Frankfurt, Velarion secured a $350 million syndicated term loan facility in November 2023.
European revenue grew 29 percent year-over-year, making the region Velarion's fastest-growing geography in fiscal 2023.
Asia-Pacific revenue increased 17 percent, supported by new partnerships with regional systems integrators in Japan and Australia.
Velarion's board of directors approved a new $150 million share repurchase program in December 2023, expected to be executed over the following 18 months.
The existing repurchase authorization, originally set at $100 million, had approximately $15 million remaining at fiscal year-end after the $85 million deployed during the year.
Chief Revenue Officer Daniel Ferreira noted that pipeline entering fiscal 2024 was at a record level, with total qualified pipeline exceeding $2.3 billion.
Win rates improved to 38 percent in competitive deal cycles, up from 31 percent in fiscal 2022, reflecting improved product differentiation.
Velarion's net promoter score among enterprise customers reached 62, an improvement from 54 in the prior year.
The company's cloud infrastructure cost per compute unit declined 11 percent following the renegotiation of its primary hyperscaler agreement.
Cost of goods sold for fiscal 2023 was $847 million, reflecting both direct hosting costs and third-party software license fees embedded in customer solutions.
Inventory of physical hardware components held by the professional services segment was valued at $28 million at year-end, down from $41 million in the prior year.
The reduction in hardware inventory reflected Velarion's strategic shift toward software-only delivery models for its consulting engagements.
Velarion's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3 percent, compared to 22.8 percent in fiscal 2022.
The lower effective tax rate was partially attributable to increased R&D tax credits claimed in the United States and United Kingdom jurisdictions.
Earnings per diluted share for fiscal 2023 were $2.84, compared to $2.11 in the prior year, a 34.6 percent increase.
The share count used for diluted EPS calculation was 55.2 million, reflecting the impact of the share repurchase program during the year.
Velarion provided fiscal 2024 guidance of total revenue between $1.64 billion and $1.68 billion, implying approximately 12 to 14 percent growth.
Management guided for fiscal 2024 adjusted EBITDA margin in the range of 19.5 to 20.5 percent, reflecting expected operating leverage.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2024 are expected to be between $95 million and $110 million, up from $72 million in fiscal 2023.
The increased capital expenditure guidance reflects planned investments in proprietary data centers and expanded Nexus AI model training infrastructure.
CEO Marcus Threlkeld stated that Velarion enters fiscal 2024 with strong momentum, a differentiated product portfolio, and a committed global team.
The board of directors expressed its confidence in management's strategic plan and reaffirmed its commitment to sustainable long-term value creation for shareholders.
Velarion Technologies Corporation's full audited financial statements and accompanying notes are available in the Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 14, 2024.
Meridian Packaging Solutions, Inc. hereby presents its consolidated annual report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates within the sustainable packaging sector, serving clients across food and beverage, pharmaceutical, and consumer goods industries.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Kellerman opened her remarks by highlighting the company's resilience in a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Chief Financial Officer Douglas Parrish reported total revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 7.3% increase over the prior year.
The company's North American segment contributed $1.12 billion to consolidated revenues, maintaining its position as the largest regional unit.
European operations generated $487 million in revenue, reflecting modest growth despite headwinds from currency fluctuations.
The Asia-Pacific division recorded revenues of $233 million, driven by expanded distribution agreements with regional retailers.
Gross profit for the fiscal year reached $612 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 33.3%.
Operating expenses totaled $389 million, inclusive of selling, general, and administrative costs as well as research and development outlays.
Operating income came in at $223 million, representing a 12.1% operating margin for the full year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $154 million, compared to $131 million in fiscal year 2022.
Earnings per diluted share rose to $3.87, up from $3.29 reported in the previous fiscal year.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 amounted to $178 million, directed primarily toward capacity expansion at three manufacturing facilities.
The company successfully commissioned a new production line at its Greenfield, Ohio plant, adding approximately 40,000 metric tons of annual throughput capacity.
Research and development spending totaled $62 million, accounting for roughly 3.4% of total revenues.
The R&D team launched seven new bio-based packaging prototypes, four of which are expected to reach commercial scale in 2024.
Headcount at fiscal year-end stood at 8,420 full-time employees globally, an increase of 310 from the prior year.
The company's workforce expansion was concentrated in manufacturing roles supporting the new Ohio facility.
Chief Operating Officer Marcus Veil credited operational efficiency programs with reducing per-unit production costs by 4.2% year-over-year.
Supply chain disruptions moderated significantly compared to 2022, allowing the company to rebuild safety stock levels across key raw material categories.
Inventory balances increased to $214 million at year-end, up from $187 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company secured long-term supply agreements with two additional pulp and paper vendors, reducing single-source procurement risks.
Accounts receivable totaled $268 million at December 31, 2023, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding of approximately 53 days.
Free cash flow for the year was $198 million, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of its common stock during the year at an average price of $61.40 per share.
Total share repurchases amounted to approximately $73.7 million, executed under the board-authorized $150 million buyback program.
Sandra Kellerman emphasized that sustainability milestones were integral to the company's long-term value creation narrative.
The company achieved a 15% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to its 2019 baseline.
Meridian Packaging Solutions received certification from the Forest Stewardship Council for all primary fiber sourcing operations.
The sustainability report, published concurrently, documents progress against the company's 2030 environmental targets.
Douglas Parrish noted that the company's balance sheet remained strong entering fiscal year 2024.
Total assets at December 31, 2023 were $2.61 billion, compared to $2.44 billion at the prior year-end.
Total liabilities stood at $1.38 billion, with long-term debt comprising $620 million of that figure.
The weighted average interest rate on outstanding debt was 4.7%, reflecting the fixed-rate structure of senior notes issued in 2021.
The company maintained a net leverage ratio of 1.9 times trailing twelve-month EBITDA, comfortably within its covenant threshold of 3.5 times.
Shareholders' equity totaled $1.23 billion, supported by retained earnings growth and the prior year's equity offering proceeds.
The company's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Poor's and Baa1 by Moody's during the fourth quarter of 2023.
Marcus Veil outlined the company's three-year operational roadmap, which includes facility consolidations and automation investments.
The automation initiative is projected to reduce direct labor costs by $18 million annually once fully deployed by mid-2025.
Management expressed confidence that Meridian Packaging Solutions would sustain above-industry-average growth rates through the medium term.
As of December 31, 2023, the company carried no outstanding revolving credit facility borrowings, having fully repaid the balance in October 2023.
The North American segment posted its highest-ever quarterly EBITDA of $94 million in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023.
Customer concentration remained manageable, with the top ten clients representing 38% of consolidated revenues.
The company added 47 new commercial accounts during the year, diversifying its client base across emerging consumer goods brands.
New product introductions generated $112 million in incremental revenue, underscoring the commercial traction of the innovation pipeline.
In November 2023, Meridian Packaging Solutions drew $175 million under a newly arranged term loan to fund strategic acquisition activities.
The pharmaceutical packaging sub-segment grew by 19% year-over-year, making it the fastest-growing vertical within the product portfolio.
Meridian's e-commerce packaging line saw volume increase by 31%, reflecting the ongoing shift in retail distribution channels.
The company's adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $341 million, implying an adjusted EBITDA margin of 18.5%.
Adjusted EBITDA excludes non-recurring restructuring charges of $14 million incurred during the first half of the year.
The restructuring program resulted in the closure of one underperforming facility in Belgium and the consolidation of two administrative offices.
Douglas Parrish reiterated that fiscal 2024 guidance calls for revenues in the range of $1.95 billion to $2.02 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 is set between $360 million and $375 million, assuming stable input cost conditions.
The company anticipates capital expenditures of approximately $160 million in fiscal 2024, moderating from the elevated 2023 level.
Management acknowledged that inflationary pressures on resin and energy costs remain a key risk to margin performance.
The board's audit committee conducted a thorough review of internal controls and found no material weaknesses for the fiscal year.
Meridian Packaging Solutions will hold its annual investor day on April 18, 2024, at which updated strategic priorities will be presented.
The company thanks its employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued trust and partnership.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those described herein.
Velarion Technologies Corp. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The following report covers fiscal year 2023, ending December 31, and was prepared under the oversight of Chief Financial Officer Margaret Osei.
Velarion reported total consolidated revenue of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the prior year.
Chief Executive Officer Daniel Pryce expressed confidence in the company's trajectory, citing strong enterprise adoption across North American markets.
Subscription-based revenue, which constitutes the majority of Velarion's income, grew 18.7% year-over-year to reach $612.4 million.
Professional services revenue contributed an additional $234.9 million, reflecting the continued complexity of enterprise deployments.
Gross profit for the fiscal year stood at $529.1 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 62.5%.
Operating expenses increased modestly to $401.7 million, driven primarily by expanded sales and marketing investments.
Research and development spending reached $118.6 million, representing 14.0% of total revenue, consistent with management's innovation priorities.
Velarion's net income for fiscal year 2023 was $87.4 million, compared to $63.1 million in fiscal year 2022, marking a 38.5% improvement.
Diluted earnings per share rose to $2.31 from $1.67 in the prior year, reflecting both earnings growth and modest share repurchases.
The company repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares during the year at an average price of $43.70 per share.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $314.8 million, providing substantial liquidity headroom for near-term operational needs.
Velarion carried no long-term debt obligations as of December 31, 2023, having fully retired its previous revolving credit facility in March of that year.
The company's balance sheet remained conservatively structured, with total assets of $1.12 billion and total liabilities of $388.5 million.
Inventory of physical hardware components used in hybrid deployment kits was valued at $29.3 million, down from $41.7 million in the prior year.
The reduction in inventory reflects a strategic shift toward software-only delivery models for the majority of new customer contracts.
Headcount at year-end stood at 4,847 full-time employees, an increase of 312 from the prior year-end figure of 4,535.
To support accelerated expansion into European markets, Velarion secured a €250 million syndicated credit facility in October 2023, with a five-year maturity.
The company's customer retention rate for subscription accounts improved to 93.8%, up from 91.2% in fiscal year 2022.
Net Revenue Retention, which accounts for upsells and expansions within the existing customer base, reached 117% for the fiscal year.
During fiscal year 2023, Velarion launched its next-generation platform, VelarionOS 4.0, which introduced AI-driven demand forecasting modules.
VelarionOS 4.0 was made generally available in September 2023 following a six-month beta program involving 43 enterprise clients.
Customer adoption of VelarionOS 4.0 exceeded internal projections, with over 680 enterprise accounts migrating within the first quarter of availability.
The successful platform launch contributed meaningfully to the acceleration in subscription revenue observed in the second half of the year.
Deferred revenue at year-end was $203.7 million, reflecting prepaid subscription contracts that will be recognized over the coming fiscal periods.
Accounts receivable stood at $147.2 million, with a days sales outstanding metric of 63.4 days, consistent with prior-year levels.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 amounted to $54.9 million, primarily allocated to data center infrastructure and leasehold improvements.
Free cash flow for the year, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $149.6 million.
This represents a free cash flow margin of approximately 17.7%, demonstrating strong cash conversion relative to net income.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share in November 2023, the company's first-ever dividend payment.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Ramachandran attributed operational efficiency gains to the company's multiyear automation initiative launched in 2020.
Employee productivity, measured as revenue per full-time employee, rose to approximately $174,800, up from $162,300 in the prior fiscal year.
Velarion's market share in the North American cloud supply chain software segment grew to an estimated 8.3%, according to third-party industry analysis.
The Asia-Pacific region remained a smaller but strategically significant contributor, generating $76.4 million in revenue during fiscal year 2023.
Velarion expanded its partner ecosystem to include 214 certified implementation partners globally, up from 179 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company signed 38 new enterprise contracts valued at over $1 million in annual contract value during the fiscal year.
Average contract length for new enterprise deals increased to 3.4 years, compared to 2.9 years in fiscal year 2022.
Hardware component costs embedded within hybrid deployment contracts were fully offset by software margin improvements in the fiscal year.
Total cost of revenue declined as a percentage of revenue from 40.1% in fiscal year 2022 to 37.5% in fiscal year 2023.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $212.8 million, reflecting targeted investments in brand awareness and field sales capacity.
The company did not make any acquisitions during fiscal year 2023, maintaining focus on organic growth and platform integration.
Management reaffirmed its commitment to disciplined capital allocation, prioritizing internal reinvestment over inorganic expansion strategies.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance of total revenue between $960 million and $985 million, representing growth of approximately 14% at the midpoint.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set in the range of $195 million to $210 million.
The company expects to add approximately 400 net new employees in fiscal year 2024 to support anticipated growth in sales and customer success functions.
Velarion's audit was conducted by Whitmore & Draper LLP, which issued an unqualified opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the criteria established by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations.
The Audit Committee, chaired by board member Dr. Sylvia Fontaine, met nine times during fiscal year 2023 to review financial matters.
Velarion's stock price appreciated 31.4% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the broader software sector index by approximately 12 percentage points.
Total shareholder return, inclusive of the newly initiated dividend, was approximately 31.9% for the calendar year.
Management believes that existing cash balances, expected operating cash flows, and available credit resources provide adequate funding for planned operations.
Velarion's environmental, social, and governance initiatives resulted in a 12% reduction in absolute carbon emissions across wholly owned facilities.
The company published its second annual sustainability report in June 2023, aligned with the Global Reporting Initiative standards.
Velarion Technologies Corp. remains committed to delivering innovative, scalable solutions that drive measurable value for its global customer base.
Meridian Crest Technologies, Inc. hereby presents its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates within the industrial automation and robotics sector, serving clients across North America, Europe, and Southeast Asia.
Chief Executive Officer Helena Voss opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's commitment to long-term value creation.
Meridian Crest Technologies recorded total revenues of $847 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12% increase over the prior year.
The growth was primarily driven by expanded deployments of the company's flagship MX-7 robotic assembly platform.
Chief Financial Officer Dario Lentini noted that gross margins improved to 41.3% from 38.7% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses for the year totaled $312 million, reflecting continued investment in research and development.
The company employed approximately 6,200 full-time staff globally as of December 31, 2023.
New customer acquisitions in the automotive and semiconductor verticals were highlighted as a key driver of growth.
Meridian Crest Technologies successfully launched three new product lines during the second and third quarters of the year.
The MX-9 platform, introduced in July 2023, received regulatory clearance in the European Union ahead of schedule.
Capital expenditures totaled $98 million for the year, largely concentrated in manufacturing capacity expansion in Mexico.
The company reported net income of $113 million, compared to $89 million in the previous fiscal year.
Earnings per diluted share rose to $3.42, up from $2.71 in fiscal year 2022.
Meridian Crest Technologies concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend increase of $0.05 per share, reflecting confidence in cash flow generation.
Free cash flow for the year came in at $142 million, well above the company's internal target of $120 million.
Inventory levels were carefully managed, ending the year at $167 million, a modest decline from the $174 million recorded in 2022.
The company's backlog of confirmed orders stood at $520 million as of year-end, providing strong near-term revenue visibility.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Meridian Crest Technologies secured a $275 million term loan facility to fund strategic acquisitions.
The acquisition pipeline remains robust, with management evaluating several targets in the precision sensing and vision systems space.
Chief Operating Officer Marcus Teal credited the operations team for achieving record throughput at the Monterrey facility.
Gross profit for the year was approximately $350 million, consistent with the improved margin performance cited by Mr. Lentini.
Research and development expenditures increased by 18% year-over-year, reaching $74 million in fiscal year 2023.
The company invested heavily in artificial intelligence integration within its software control stack for robotic systems.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were held flat at $238 million despite the broader revenue expansion.
Meridian Crest Technologies maintained a current ratio of 2.4, indicating strong short-term liquidity.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares during the year under its existing buyback authorization.
Total assets at year-end were $1.34 billion, an increase of $182 million from the prior year.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 53 days in fiscal year 2022.
The European segment contributed $241 million in revenue, accounting for approximately 28% of total consolidated revenues.
Revenue from the Asia-Pacific region grew by 22%, reaching $189 million in fiscal year 2023.
The North American segment remained the largest contributor, generating $417 million in revenue for the year.
Ms. Voss highlighted the company's expansion into smart warehouse automation as a new growth vector for fiscal year 2024.
Headcount in the engineering and product development divisions increased by 340 positions during the year.
The company maintained a robust pipeline of patent applications, with 47 new filings submitted in 2023.
Employee retention rates improved to 91% company-wide, reflecting investments in compensation and professional development programs.
Meridian Crest Technologies received the Industrial Innovator of the Year award from the Automation Industry Alliance.
The tax provision for the year was $38 million, reflecting an effective tax rate of approximately 25.2%.
Deferred revenue balances increased to $63 million, driven by multi-year service agreements signed with enterprise clients.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 14.8%, surpassing the management target of 13.0% for the year.
Mr. Lentini stated that the balance sheet remains positioned to support both organic growth initiatives and targeted acquisitions.
Total liabilities at year-end, including operating lease obligations, amounted to $498 million.
Depreciation and amortization charges for the year were $55 million, reflecting the growing installed base of capital assets.
The company anticipates fiscal year 2024 revenues in the range of $920 million to $950 million.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $201 million, representing an EBITDA margin of approximately 23.7%.
Management expects to maintain capital expenditure discipline, with 2024 capex guidance set at $105 million to $115 million.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its independent audit without material findings.
Meridian Crest Technologies' common stock closed at $58.40 per share on December 29, 2023, the last trading day of the fiscal year.
The company's market capitalization at year-end was approximately $1.93 billion based on shares outstanding.
Shareholders of record as of January 15, 2024 will be entitled to receive the increased quarterly dividend.
The board and executive leadership team express gratitude to all stakeholders for their continued trust and partnership in Meridian Crest Technologies.
Meridian Biopharma Solutions, Inc. hereby presents its consolidated annual report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, operates in the specialty pharmaceuticals and contract drug manufacturing sector.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Renata Holloway expressed confidence in the company's strategic trajectory during the annual shareholder address.
Meridian Biopharma recorded total revenues of $842.7 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.3% increase over the prior year.
The Biologics Manufacturing division contributed $391.4 million to total revenues, making it the single largest revenue-generating segment.
The Specialty Therapeutics division generated $287.1 million in revenues, reflecting strong market penetration in oncology support products.
Contract Research Services accounted for the remaining $164.2 million, a segment that grew 22.1% year-over-year.
Gross profit for the year reached $389.5 million, yielding an overall gross margin of approximately 46.2%.
Operating expenses totaled $241.8 million, inclusive of research and development spending of $98.3 million.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Trent noted that disciplined cost management allowed the company to expand operating margins by 180 basis points.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $104.6 million, compared to $87.2 million in fiscal 2022.
Diluted earnings per share rose to $3.17 from $2.64 in the prior fiscal year, a 20.1% improvement.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
This represents a 12% increase over the prior year's quarterly dividend of $0.25 per share.
Total headcount at year-end stood at 4,812 full-time employees, up from 4,390 at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company completed the hiring of 610 new employees during the year, partially offset by an attrition rate of 4.1%.
Meridian Biopharma opened a new state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina, in the second quarter of 2023.
The Durham facility is projected to add up to 300,000 square feet of GMP-certified production space over the next two years.
Capital expenditures for the full year totaled $137.6 million, with the Durham facility accounting for $58.4 million of that figure.
The company's balance sheet reflected total assets of $1.94 billion and total liabilities of $1.02 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Shareholders' equity increased to $921.3 million, reflecting retained earnings growth and the issuance of restricted stock units.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end were $214.8 million, compared to $178.3 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
The improvement in cash balances was driven by strong free cash flow generation of $189.4 million during the fiscal year.
Inventory levels declined modestly year-over-year, ending at $112.7 million versus $119.6 million at the end of 2022.
Chief Operating Officer James Ferreira attributed the inventory reduction to improved demand forecasting and supply chain optimization initiatives.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian Biopharma carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet.
The oncology support portfolio expanded with the U.S. launch of Velarix, a novel nausea prophylaxis agent, in June 2023.
Velarix achieved first-year net sales of $43.2 million, exceeding the internal launch target of $35 million.
The company also received FDA clearance for its biosimilar candidate MBS-114 in October 2023, marking the third biosimilar approval in Meridian's history.
MBS-114 is expected to enter the commercial market in the first half of 2024, targeting a patient population of approximately 180,000 annually.
In November 2023, the company entered into a $350 million senior secured revolving credit agreement with a syndicate of seven leading financial institutions.
Research and development headcount grew by 18% during fiscal 2023, reflecting the company's commitment to pipeline expansion.
The clinical pipeline includes nine active programs, of which four are currently in Phase 2 or Phase 3 trials.
Meridian's Phase 3 trial for MBS-227, a treatment for refractory anemia, enrolled 1,240 patients across 38 clinical sites globally.
