The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed the independent audit without issuing any material weaknesses or significant deficiencies.
Compensation and benefits expense across the enterprise totaled $312.8 million, representing approximately 37% of total revenues.
The company recognized $22.7 million in deferred revenue at year-end related to multi-year software subscription agreements.
Veltrana's board of directors is composed of nine members, six of whom are independent directors in accordance with NASDAQ listing standards.
Management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $935 million to $960 million, implying approximately 10% to 13% growth.
The company expects to continue investing in R&D at a rate of approximately 12% to 14% of projected revenues in fiscal 2024.
Dr. Nambiar expressed confidence that the VX-900 pipeline will be complemented by at least one additional product clearance application filed in 2024.
Depreciation and amortization expense for fiscal 2023 was $75.9 million, of which $31.2 million related to acquired intangible assets from the Solumed transaction.
The company's operating lease obligations totaled $43.5 million, distributed across laboratory, office, and field service locations in 14 countries.
Veltrana reported other income of $8.4 million, largely attributable to interest earned on its cash and short-term investment balances.
The Compensation Committee approved a long-term incentive plan for fiscal 2024 that ties 60% of executive equity awards to three-year total shareholder return performance metrics.
Management highlighted that the company's order backlog at December 31, 2023, stood at $214.8 million, up 22.3% from the prior year-end balance.
The notes to the financial statements disclose that no material litigation contingencies existed as of December 31, 2023, that would require accrual under ASC 450.
Veltrana's stock closed fiscal 2023 at $61.40 per share, representing a total shareholder return of approximately 28% for the calendar year.
The company's book value per share at December 31, 2023, was $22.16, reflecting accumulated retained earnings and the impact of share repurchase activity.
Management believes Veltrana is well-positioned to deliver continued value to its shareholders, customers, and employees in the years ahead.
This report contains forward-looking statements that are subject to risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ materially from those expressed or implied herein.
Veltora Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced industrial automation solutions, presents its consolidated annual report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, operates across four primary business segments: robotics integration, sensor manufacturing, software platforms, and field services.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway expressed strong confidence in the company's strategic trajectory during the annual shareholder address held in February 2024.
Veltora Dynamics reported total consolidated revenues of $2.14 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a year-over-year increase of 11.3 percent.
The robotics integration segment remained the company's largest revenue contributor, generating $847 million, or approximately 39.6 percent of total revenues.
Sensor manufacturing revenues grew by 14.2 percent to reach $512 million, driven by expanded contracts in the automotive and aerospace verticals.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Pryce noted that operating margins improved across all four segments during the reporting period.
The software platforms segment posted revenues of $431 million, benefiting from a significant uptick in subscription-based licensing agreements.
Field services revenues totaled $350 million, reflecting steady demand for maintenance and calibration services across Veltora's installed base.
Gross profit for the consolidated entity reached $798 million, representing a gross margin of 37.3 percent, compared to 35.1 percent in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $521 million for the year, reflecting disciplined cost management initiatives led by Chief Operating Officer Fiona Castellan.
Research and development expenditures amounted to $189 million, representing 8.8 percent of total revenues, as the company continued investing in next-generation automation platforms.
The company recorded an operating income of $277 million, a 19.7 percent improvement compared to the prior fiscal year.
Net income attributable to Veltora Dynamics shareholders was $198 million, yielding a net profit margin of 9.3 percent.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal year 2023 were reported at $3.47, up from $2.91 in fiscal year 2022.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, representing an annualized dividend of $0.88 per share.
Total dividends declared during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $50.3 million, consistent with the company's policy of returning capital to shareholders.
Veltora Dynamics maintained a strong liquidity position, with cash and cash equivalents of $312 million as of December 31, 2023.
Short-term investments held by the company totaled $87 million, primarily composed of U.S. Treasury securities and investment-grade corporate bonds.
Accounts receivable stood at $364 million at year end, with days sales outstanding improving to 62 days from 68 days in the prior year.
Inventory levels at December 31, 2023 were $241 million, down from $278 million at the end of fiscal year 2022, reflecting improved supply chain management.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $143 million, focused primarily on expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Raleigh and Phoenix facilities.
The company completed the acquisition of Syntrek Precision Systems in August 2023 for a total consideration of $185 million in cash.
The Syntrek acquisition added approximately 340 employees and contributed $29 million in revenues during the partial year of ownership.
Integration costs associated with the Syntrek transaction were $11 million, recognized in the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2023.
Veltora Dynamics employed a total global workforce of 8,750 full-time equivalent employees as of December 31, 2023, including those added through the Syntrek acquisition.
The company's European operations, headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany, generated revenues of $389 million, representing 18.2 percent of total consolidated revenues.
Asian-Pacific revenues reached $267 million, with particularly strong growth in South Korea and Japan driven by automotive sector demand.
The company's North American segment remained the dominant geographic contributor, accounting for $1.484 billion in revenues during fiscal year 2023.
Veltora Dynamics entered into a strategic partnership with Harmon Industrial Group in March 2023, aimed at co-developing next-generation collaborative robotic systems.
This partnership is expected to generate joint revenues of approximately $75 million in fiscal year 2024, with further upside in subsequent years.
The company's total long-term debt as of December 31, 2023 stood at $445 million, consisting of senior unsecured notes maturing in 2028 and 2031.
Veltora Dynamics concluded fiscal year 2023 with its balance sheet entirely free of revolving credit facility borrowings, having fully repaid its $300 million revolving credit line in January 2023.
CEO Margaret Holloway stated in her annual letter that operational excellence remained the company's foremost priority heading into fiscal year 2024.
The company's net debt position, calculated as total debt minus cash and short-term investments, stood at $46 million at year end.
Veltora Dynamics achieved an interest coverage ratio of 9.4 times in fiscal year 2023, reflecting comfortable debt service capacity.
The company's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Poors during the third quarter of 2023, with a stable outlook.
In October 2023, Veltora Dynamics drew down $175 million under a newly established revolving credit facility to fund working capital requirements and general corporate purposes.
Moody's similarly affirmed the company's Baa1 rating in November 2023, citing resilient cash flows and a well-diversified revenue base.
The weighted average interest rate on the company's outstanding debt as of year end was 4.7 percent, consistent with broader market conditions.
Veltora Dynamics launched its Aurora 3.0 robotics platform in September 2023, targeting the pharmaceutical and food-and-beverage manufacturing verticals.
The Aurora 3.0 platform generated initial bookings of $63 million in the fourth quarter of 2023 alone, exceeding internal launch targets by 27 percent.
The software platforms segment benefited from the integration of Aurora 3.0's embedded analytics module, which carries a higher per-unit license fee than predecessor offerings.
CFO Daniel Pryce highlighted that recurring software revenues now constitute 61 percent of the software platforms segment's total revenues, up from 48 percent in fiscal year 2022.
The company's order backlog as of December 31, 2023 was $1.62 billion, an increase of 18.4 percent compared to the prior year-end backlog of $1.37 billion.
Approximately $890 million of the backlog is expected to convert to revenue within the next twelve months, according to management guidance.
Veltora Dynamics provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.30 billion to $2.38 billion, implying growth of 7.5 percent to 11.2 percent.
The midpoint of this guidance range is predicated on continued momentum in the robotics integration and software platforms segments.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $361 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 16.9 percent.
This compares favorably to adjusted EBITDA of $298 million and a margin of 15.5 percent in fiscal year 2022.
The company's return on invested capital for fiscal year 2023 was 13.2 percent, which management regards as above the company's weighted average cost of capital.
Veltora Dynamics repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $57.40 per share, for a total outlay of $68.9 million.
The board authorized an additional $200 million share repurchase program in December 2023, underscoring confidence in the company's long-term earnings potential.
Shares of Veltora Dynamics common stock closed at $61.75 on December 29, 2023, the final trading day of the fiscal year.
The company's total market capitalization at year end was approximately $3.53 billion, based on 57.1 million diluted shares outstanding.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a strategic focus, with Veltora Dynamics reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 12 percent year over year.
The company achieved ISO 14001 certification at three additional manufacturing facilities during 2023, bringing the total certified sites to eleven.
Chief Operating Officer Fiona Castellan noted that lean manufacturing programs implemented in 2022 yielded approximately $34 million in annualized cost savings in 2023.
Veltora Dynamics expects these savings to grow to approximately $48 million on an annualized basis by the end of fiscal year 2024 as programs reach full maturity.
The company faces ongoing risks including geopolitical uncertainty in key export markets, supply chain disruptions, and intensifying competition from Asian-Pacific automation manufacturers.
Management believes these risks are adequately mitigated through the company's diversified geographic presence, strong customer relationships, and continued investment in innovation.
Veltora Dynamics' independent auditors, Henderson & Lowe LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
The board of directors, the audit committee, and executive leadership collectively affirm the accuracy and completeness of the financial information presented in this report.
Arcturus Biopharma Holdings, Inc. is a mid-cap specialty pharmaceutical company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, focused on developing targeted oncology and immunology therapeutics.
The following excerpt is drawn from the company's Annual Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, as filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Solberg opened the letter to shareholders by noting that fiscal year 2023 represented a pivotal transformation period for the organization.
Arcturus Biopharma concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, with a clean balance sheet reflecting no outstanding borrowings or credit obligations.
Total net revenues for the year reached $418.7 million, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the $366.6 million reported in fiscal year 2022.
Product revenue accounted for $391.3 million of that total, while licensing and milestone payments contributed the remaining $27.4 million.
Gross margin improved to 68.4%, up from 65.1% in the prior year, driven by favorable product mix and ongoing manufacturing efficiencies.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $274.5 million, reflecting disciplined investment across research, development, and commercial functions.
During the third quarter of 2023, Arcturus Biopharma secured a $350 million syndicated credit facility with a consortium of institutional lenders to support pipeline expansion.
Research and development expenses grew to $134.2 million, representing approximately 32% of total net revenues, consistent with the company's commitment to innovation.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Trent emphasized that the capital allocation strategy remained balanced between organic investment and opportunistic business development.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $140.3 million for the full year, roughly flat compared to $138.9 million in the prior period.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $62.1 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $1.84.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP financial measure, came in at $98.6 million, reflecting a margin of approximately 23.6% on total net revenues.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $223.4 million, compared to $187.9 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company's flagship oncology asset, ABP-114, received Breakthrough Therapy Designation from the FDA in February 2023, accelerating its regulatory pathway.
ABP-114 generated $178.5 million in product revenue during 2023, representing more than 45% of total product revenue for the year.
A Phase 3 clinical trial for ABP-114 in relapsed or refractory non-small cell lung cancer enrolled its last patient in September 2023, with topline data expected in the second half of 2024.
Dr. Solberg noted that the oncology pipeline now comprises seven distinct clinical-stage programs across four tumor types.
Arcturus Biopharma's immunology segment, anchored by the IL-17 inhibitor ABP-229, contributed $87.6 million in revenue, up 21.3% year-over-year.
ABP-229 secured a new indication approval for psoriatic arthritis in June 2023, expanding its addressable patient population by an estimated 340,000 individuals in the United States.
Commercial headcount at year-end totaled 1,042 employees, with 387 dedicated to sales and marketing functions across North America and Europe.
The company's European operations, managed through its wholly owned subsidiary Arcturus Biopharma GmbH, contributed €62.3 million in net revenues during fiscal 2023.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $31.7 million, primarily related to manufacturing capacity upgrades at the company's Durham, North Carolina facility.
Inventory levels at December 31, 2023, were $54.8 million, representing approximately 51 days of supply, which management considered appropriate given demand visibility.
Accounts receivable stood at $89.2 million at year-end, with a days sales outstanding metric of 77.8 days, modestly improved from 81.3 days in the prior year.
The company repurchased 2.1 million shares of common stock during 2023 under its existing $150 million buyback program, at an average price of $34.62 per share.
Total shares repurchased under the program since its inception in 2021 amount to 5.6 million shares at an aggregate cost of $171.8 million.
Arcturus Biopharma declared quarterly cash dividends totaling $0.48 per share for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.1% increase over the prior year's dividend rate.
Mr. Trent highlighted that dividend coverage remained robust, with the payout ratio standing at approximately 26.1% of net income.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 19.7%, compared to 21.4% in the prior year, reflecting the benefit of certain R&D tax credits.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet increased to $18.9 million at year-end, primarily related to a co-promotion arrangement with a European distribution partner.
Goodwill and intangible assets totaled $302.7 million, largely arising from the 2020 acquisition of Pinnacle Immunologics, Inc.
Amortization of acquired intangibles totaled $22.4 million during 2023, in line with the expected amortization schedule disclosed at the time of the acquisition.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.8x at December 31, 2023, reflecting a strong liquidity position relative to short-term obligations.
Total stockholders' equity grew to $531.6 million by year-end, compared to $482.4 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Operating cash flow for the year was $89.3 million, while free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $57.6 million.
Dr. Solberg noted in her letter that the company's pipeline had never been more diversified, spanning both early and late-stage development across multiple therapeutic areas.
Arcturus Biopharma submitted a New Drug Application for ABP-317, its oral immunology candidate, in November 2023, with a PDUFA target action date set for August 2024.
If approved, ABP-317 is projected to address a market opportunity of approximately $2.1 billion in annual sales within five years of launch.
The company's market capitalization as of December 31, 2023, was approximately $1.17 billion, based on a closing share price of $34.62.
Arcturus Biopharma's board of directors comprises nine members, five of whom are independent, in accordance with NASDAQ listing standards.
Audit Committee Chair Diane Westbrook confirmed that all financial controls and reporting procedures were reviewed in accordance with Sarbanes-Oxley Section 404 requirements.
No material weaknesses were identified in the company's internal controls over financial reporting for fiscal year 2023.
The independent registered public accounting firm, Grant & Hollowell LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management provided guidance for total net revenues in the range of $460 million to $480 million.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is expected to be in the range of $108 million to $118 million, representing continued margin expansion.
The midpoint of the revenue guidance range implies year-over-year growth of approximately 11.7% from the fiscal 2023 reported figure.
Management also guided for R&D expense growth of 8% to 12% in fiscal 2024, reflecting planned investment in two new Phase 2 program initiations.
Mr. Trent cautioned that the guidance does not include potential revenue contributions from ABP-317 should it receive FDA approval within the fiscal year.
Arcturus Biopharma also disclosed that it entered a strategic collaboration agreement with Nihon Seiyaku Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. in January 2024 for ABP-229 development rights in Japan.
The upfront payment associated with that agreement, totaling $45 million, will be recognized as revenue ratably over the performance obligation period.

Management expressed confidence that the company's operational momentum, diversified pipeline, and strengthened commercial infrastructure position Arcturus Biopharma for sustained long-term value creation.
Nexarion Therapeutics, Inc. is a mid-cap biopharmaceutical company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
The company focuses on developing novel small-molecule treatments for rare autoimmune disorders.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Lydia Harmon has led the organization since its founding in 2014.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Velde joined Nexarion in 2019 after a decade at Meridian Capital Partners.
For the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, Nexarion reported total revenues of $412.7 million.
This represents a 19.4% increase compared to the $345.6 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Product revenues accounted for $378.9 million of total revenues, driven primarily by the company's flagship drug, Velatrin.
Velatrin received FDA approval in late 2021 and has consistently exceeded analyst expectations for market uptake.
Royalty and licensing revenues contributed the remaining $33.8 million to the total revenue figure.
Cost of goods sold for fiscal 2023 was $98.4 million, reflecting a gross margin of approximately 76.2%.
Research and development expenses totaled $134.6 million, up from $110.3 million in the prior year.
Selling, general and administrative expenses came in at $87.2 million for the full fiscal year.
Operating income for the year was $92.5 million, compared to $74.1 million in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $68.3 million, or $1.42 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA, which excludes stock-based compensation and certain one-time charges, reached $118.7 million.
The company's balance sheet as of December 31, 2023 reflected total assets of $1.04 billion.
Cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments totaled $284.5 million at year-end.
Accounts receivable stood at $61.3 million, consistent with the company's typical collection cycle of approximately 45 days.
Total liabilities were $391.2 million, including $215.0 million in long-term debt obligations.
Stockholders' equity increased to $648.8 million from $591.4 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
During fiscal 2023, Nexarion repurchased 2.1 million shares of common stock at an average price of $34.70 per share.
The board of directors authorized a new $150 million share repurchase program in September 2023, effective through December 2025.
Capital expenditures for the year were $22.8 million, primarily related to laboratory expansion at the company's Durham facility.
Nexarion entered fiscal 2023 carrying no outstanding borrowings under its revolving credit facility.
The company's headcount grew to 1,247 full-time employees by the end of fiscal 2023, up from 1,089 the prior year.
Dr. Harmon emphasized that the company's pipeline remains one of its most valuable long-term assets.
Nexarion's Phase 2 clinical trial for NX-447, a treatment for systemic lupus erythematosus, demonstrated encouraging interim results.
Enrollment for the Phase 3 trial of NX-447 is expected to begin in the second quarter of fiscal 2024.
As of the fourth quarter of 2023, Nexarion had drawn $180 million from its secured term loan facility to fund pipeline acceleration.
The company's second pipeline asset, NX-319, is currently in Phase 1 trials targeting eosinophilic esophagitis.
Management expects NX-319 to advance to Phase 2 enrollment by mid-2025, pending safety review committee approval.
Nexarion holds 14 issued patents related to Velatrin and its manufacturing process, with three additional applications pending.
The company's intellectual property position is expected to provide market exclusivity for Velatrin through at least 2036.
International expansion efforts in Western Europe resulted in regulatory submissions in Germany, France, and the United Kingdom.
European revenues are currently minimal but are projected to become a meaningful contributor by fiscal 2026.
The company's partnership with Orvanta AG provides co-commercialization rights for Velatrin across six European markets.
Under the Orvanta agreement, Nexarion received an upfront payment of $20 million and is eligible for up to $175 million in milestones.
CFO Marcus Velde noted that operating cash flow of $105.4 million in fiscal 2023 comfortably covered all capital requirements.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $82.6 million for the year.
The company did not declare or pay any cash dividends during fiscal 2023.
Management has stated that it does not anticipate initiating a dividend program in the near term, preferring to reinvest in R&D.
Nexarion's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 17.8%, benefiting from R&D tax credits and a favorable jurisdictional mix.
Deferred tax assets of $44.1 million are recorded on the balance sheet and primarily relate to net operating loss carryforwards.
The external audit was conducted by Hartwell & Crane LLP, which issued an unqualified opinion on the financial statements.
Nexarion's audit committee, chaired by independent director Patricia Owens, oversaw the financial reporting process throughout the year.
The company maintained compliance with all financial covenants under its existing credit agreements as of December 31, 2023.
Inventory at year-end was $37.6 million, representing approximately 4.5 months of supply based on expected Velatrin demand.
Management does not anticipate any supply chain disruptions that would materially affect product availability in fiscal 2024.
Gross-to-net adjustments, including rebates and chargebacks, were approximately 18.3% of gross Velatrin revenues for the year.
The company recorded a $6.1 million impairment charge related to a discontinued early-stage program in the third quarter.
This impairment charge is excluded from adjusted EBITDA and adjusted net income as a non-recurring item.
For fiscal 2024, management is guiding for total revenues in the range of $465 million to $490 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 is set at $128 million to $142 million.
R&D expense is projected to increase to between $155 million and $165 million as NX-447's Phase 3 program ramps up.
The company plans to present updated clinical data for NX-447 at the annual Autoimmunity Congress in Vienna in March 2024.
Dr. Harmon expressed confidence that fiscal 2024 will represent another year of strong operational and financial execution.
Nexarion's stock closed at $38.15 per share on December 31, 2023, implying a market capitalization of approximately $1.84 billion.
The company remains committed to its mission of developing transformative therapies for patients with limited treatment options.
This annual report excerpt is filed as part of Nexarion's Form 10-K submission to the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Forward-looking statements in this document are subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of the close of the fiscal year.
Veltara Diagnostics operates in the medical device and laboratory diagnostics sector, serving hospitals and clinical research organizations across North America and Europe.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Meredith Calloway expressed confidence in the company's trajectory, citing strong demand for its flagship immunoassay platform.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 14% increase over the prior year.
The company's gross margin improved to 61.3%, up from 58.7% in fiscal year 2022, driven by manufacturing efficiencies and favorable product mix.
During the fourth quarter, Veltara secured a $320 million syndicated term loan to fund its planned expansion into Asia-Pacific markets.
Research and development expenditures totaled $124 million, accounting for approximately 14.6% of total revenues.
Chief Financial Officer James Okafor noted that the R&D investment reflects the company's commitment to next-generation biosensor technology.
Veltara's operating income for the year was $198 million, compared to $162 million in the prior fiscal year.
The company reported diluted earnings per share of $3.47, beating analyst consensus estimates of $3.21.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses rose to $186 million, largely due to headcount additions in the commercial organization.
Veltara ended fiscal year 2023 with a headcount of approximately 4,200 full-time employees globally.
The company expanded its European workforce by roughly 300 employees following the acquisition of a German laboratory automation firm in March 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents stood at $412 million at year-end, providing substantial liquidity for near-term operations.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable in February 2024.
This dividend represents a 12.5% increase over the quarterly dividend paid in the same period of the prior year.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 were $89 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's facility in Durham, North Carolina.
Dr. Calloway highlighted the successful commercial launch of the VeltraPath 3000 analyzer in the second quarter as a key driver of revenue growth.
The VeltraPath 3000 generated approximately $73 million in revenue during its first two quarters of commercial availability.
Veltara's immunoassay reagent consumables segment remained the largest revenue contributor, accounting for 54% of total revenues.
Instrument placements in the fiscal year totaled 1,847 units, a 22% increase compared to 1,514 units placed in fiscal year 2022.
The company maintained an inventory balance of $134 million at December 31, 2023, consistent with prior-year levels adjusted for volume growth.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 58 days from 64 days in the prior year, reflecting enhanced collection processes.
Veltara's North American segment generated $521 million in revenues, representing 61.5% of total company revenues.
The European segment contributed $231 million in revenues, benefiting from the German acquisition and organic growth in reagent sales.
Rest-of-world revenues, which include Latin America and certain Asia-Pacific markets currently served through distributors, totaled $95 million.
Gross profit for the year was $519 million, an increase of $64 million versus fiscal year 2022.
James Okafor stated that the company's tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.4%, in line with prior guidance of approximately 21%.
The effective tax rate benefited from R&D tax credits recognized in the United States and Germany.
Veltara recognized a $14 million impairment charge related to certain legacy point-of-care product lines that were discontinued during the third quarter.
The discontinuation of those legacy products is expected to improve operating margins by approximately 80 basis points on an annualized basis.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $309 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $398 million less capital expenditures of $89 million.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during the year at an average price of $64.30, for a total outlay of approximately $77 million.
At December 31, 2023, there were approximately 56.8 million diluted shares outstanding.
Veltara's market capitalization at year-end, based on a closing share price of $71.45, was approximately $4.06 billion.
The company's net promoter score among hospital laboratory customers reached 67, up from 61 in the prior year.
Customer retention rates for multi-year reagent rental agreements remained above 92% for the fourth consecutive year.
Veltara announced a strategic partnership with Helix BioSolutions Inc. in November 2023 to co-develop point-of-care molecular diagnostics.
Under the partnership terms, Veltara will contribute up to $45 million in development funding over a three-year period.
The company's backlog of instrument orders at December 31, 2023 stood at approximately $118 million, providing good near-term revenue visibility.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $940 million to $970 million, implying growth of approximately 11% to 15%.
The 2024 guidance assumes the full-year contribution of the VeltraPath 3000 and the initial ramp of a new point-of-care respiratory panel.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 24% to 25%, compared to the 23.4% achieved in fiscal year 2023.
Veltara's audit committee confirmed that the company received an unqualified audit opinion from its independent registered public accounting firm, Thornton & Hale LLP.
The company maintained compliance with all covenants under its existing revolving credit facility throughout fiscal year 2023.
Veltara's revolving credit facility, which has a borrowing capacity of $200 million, remained undrawn at year-end.
Dr. Calloway emphasized that the company's balance sheet strength positions Veltara to pursue bolt-on acquisitions in the molecular diagnostics space.
The company added three new independent directors to its board during fiscal year 2023, strengthening governance and industry expertise.
Compensation expense related to stock-based awards was $31 million for fiscal year 2023, an increase of $4 million from the prior year.
Amortization of intangible assets acquired in the German acquisition contributed $18 million of non-cash expense during the year.
Veltara's deferred revenue balance was $22 million at year-end, primarily related to service contract obligations.
The company carries a defined contribution retirement plan for U.S.-based employees, with an employer matching contribution of up to 5% of eligible compensation.
Total benefits and compensation costs across the organization were approximately $430 million in fiscal year 2023.
Veltara's insurance receivables and other current assets totaled $47 million at year-end, up from $39 million in the prior year.
The increase in other current assets was driven by prepaid expenses related to a new enterprise resource planning system implementation.
James Okafor confirmed that the ERP implementation is on schedule and expected to be fully operational by mid-2024.
The project is expected to yield annualized cost savings of approximately $12 million beginning in fiscal year 2025.
Veltara ended the year with a current ratio of 3.2 and a quick ratio of 2.6, indicating strong short-term financial health.
The company's return on invested capital was 18.1% for fiscal year 2023, reflecting disciplined capital allocation and improving profitability.
Management reiterated its long-term target of achieving revenues exceeding $1.5 billion by fiscal year 2027.
Dr. Calloway concluded the annual letter to shareholders by expressing gratitude to Veltara's employees, customers, and investors for their continued support.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation's common stock trades on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLTR.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. (NASDAQ: VLTR) presents the following summary of its fiscal year 2023 annual financial results for investors and stakeholders.
Veltara Biosciences operates in the specialty pharmaceutical sector, focusing on oncology and rare disease therapeutics.
The company was founded in 2011 and is headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Okafor stated that fiscal year 2023 represented a transformational period for the organization.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Hsu noted that disciplined capital allocation remained central to the company's operational philosophy throughout the year.
Veltara reported total net revenues of $487.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing an 18.4% increase over fiscal year 2022.
Product revenue from the company's lead oncology drug, Veltraxin, accounted for $312.1 million of total revenues.
Veltara ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired the last tranche of its 2019 senior notes in March 2023.
Royalty and licensing revenues contributed $87.6 million, up from $71.2 million in the prior year.
Contract manufacturing revenues added $87.6 million to the total revenue figure, reflecting strong demand from third-party partners.
Gross profit for the year was $318.4 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 65.4%.
Operating expenses totaled $241.7 million, with research and development costs representing the largest single component at $128.9 million.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses reached $112.8 million, a modest increase of 6.2% over fiscal year 2022.
During the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2023, Veltara secured a $275 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of institutional lenders led by First Meridian Capital.
Management characterized the credit arrangement as providing strategic flexibility for potential acquisitions and pipeline investment.
Net income attributable to Veltara shareholders for fiscal year 2023 was $64.2 million, or $1.47 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $38.6 million, or $0.89 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA, excluding stock-based compensation and non-recurring items, was $109.7 million for the full year.
The adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 22.5% exceeded the company's internal target of 21% set at the beginning of the fiscal year.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023 stood at $203.8 million, compared to $164.5 million at December 31, 2022.
Operating cash flow for the year was $97.3 million, reflecting strong conversion of net income to cash.
Capital expenditures totaled $34.6 million, primarily directed toward expanding Veltara's manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina.
The Durham facility expansion is expected to increase annual production capacity by approximately 40% upon completion in mid-2024.
Dr. Okafor highlighted the successful Phase 3 clinical trial readout for VLT-209, the company's next-generation KRAS inhibitor, as the defining achievement of the year.
VLT-209 demonstrated a statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival compared to standard of care in non-small cell lung cancer.
The company submitted a New Drug Application to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for VLT-209 in November 2023.
Veltara's commercial team grew to 312 field representatives by year-end, an increase of 47 representatives from the prior year.
The company's international operations, primarily in Germany and Japan, contributed $68.4 million to total revenues.
International revenues grew 23.1% year-over-year, outpacing domestic revenue growth of 16.8%.
Veltara's German subsidiary, Veltara GmbH, received regulatory approval for Veltraxin in the European Union's rare disease fast-track pathway.
The Board of Directors declared a special cash dividend of $0.35 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of February 15, 2024.
This marked the first dividend payment in Veltara's history as a publicly traded company.
Raymond Hsu commented that the dividend declaration reflects the board's confidence in the company's sustained cash generation capacity.
Inventory levels at year-end were $42.1 million, representing approximately 49 days of supply, within the company's target range.
Veltara recorded a $14.3 million impairment charge related to the discontinuation of VLT-117, a Phase 2 candidate in myelofibrosis.
The decision to discontinue VLT-117 followed an interim efficacy analysis that did not meet predefined success thresholds.
Despite the impairment, the company maintained its full-year adjusted earnings guidance range of $1.40 to $1.55 per diluted share.
Actual adjusted earnings per diluted share of $1.61 came in above the high end of that guidance range.
Veltara's market capitalization as of December 31, 2023 was approximately $3.2 billion, based on a closing stock price of $73.40.
The stock appreciated 34.7% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the NASDAQ Biotechnology Index, which returned 12.3% over the same period.
Institutional ownership stood at approximately 76% of shares outstanding as of the most recent proxy filing.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 under its existing buyback authorization at an average price of $61.18.
As of December 31, 2023, approximately $88.5 million remained available under the $150 million share repurchase program authorized in January 2022.
Accounts receivable at year-end totaled $94.7 million, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding metric of approximately 71 days.
Veltara's top three customers accounted for approximately 38% of total net revenues in fiscal year 2023.
No single customer represented more than 16% of annual revenues, limiting concentration risk.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 18.7%, benefiting from orphan drug tax credits associated with Veltraxin.
Veltara employed 1,847 full-time employees globally as of December 31, 2023.
Headcount increased by 214 employees compared to December 31, 2022, driven by manufacturing and clinical operations hiring.
The company's pension and post-retirement benefit obligations totaled $22.4 million, unchanged from the prior year.
Deferred revenue of $31.6 million relates primarily to upfront payments received under collaboration agreements with Sumitomo Dainippon Pharma.
Veltara expects to recognize approximately $18.2 million of that deferred revenue in fiscal year 2024.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $540 million to $570 million.
The midpoint of fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance implies approximately 13.7% growth over fiscal year 2023 actuals.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 23% to 25%, reflecting anticipated operating leverage.
Dr. Okafor expressed confidence in Veltara's ability to achieve potential NDA approval and commercial launch of VLT-209 within the 2024 calendar year.
The company's pipeline currently includes seven compounds in clinical development across oncology, hematology, and immunology indications.
Veltara's scientific advisory board, chaired by Dr. Emeka Nwosu of Johns Hopkins University, was expanded by three members during the year.
The addition of new scientific advisors is intended to strengthen the company's capabilities in precision medicine and biomarker-driven trial design.
Veltara's independent auditor, Grant & Whitmore LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the company's fiscal year 2023 financial statements.
The Audit Committee of the Board of Directors confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls were identified during the annual review.
Management acknowledges that competitive pressures, regulatory uncertainties, and macroeconomic conditions represent ongoing risks to the achievement of forward-looking guidance.
This financial report excerpt contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ materially from those projected.
Investors are encouraged to review Veltara's Annual Report on Form 10-K, filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2024, for complete financial disclosures.
Veltara Biosciences remains committed to delivering long-term value to shareholders through disciplined investment in its pipeline and commercial operations.
Crestwood Meridian Technologies, a mid-cap enterprise software provider headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company specializes in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms serving the manufacturing and logistics sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Holbrook opened the report by highlighting what she described as a transformative year for the organization.
Crestwood Meridian recorded total annual revenue of $847 million, representing a 14.3% increase over the prior fiscal year.
Subscription-based revenue accounted for $612 million of total revenue, underscoring the company's successful pivot toward recurring income streams.
Professional services revenue contributed an additional $235 million, reflecting robust demand for implementation and consulting engagements.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei noted that gross margins improved to 68.4%, up from 64.1% in fiscal year 2022.
The company concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final bond tranche in March of that year.
Operating expenses totaled $521 million, with research and development representing the single largest expenditure category at $198 million.
Sales and marketing spend reached $173 million, a deliberate increase intended to accelerate customer acquisition in new geographic markets.
General and administrative costs were held to $150 million, reflecting ongoing efficiency initiatives championed by Chief Operating Officer Priya Nambiar.
Adjusted EBITDA for the fiscal year came in at $214 million, exceeding analyst consensus estimates by approximately $18 million.
In the third quarter of fiscal year 2023, Crestwood Meridian secured a $320 million revolving credit facility from a syndicate of institutional lenders.
Headcount grew to 5,840 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, an increase of 620 employees compared to the end of the prior year.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in the product engineering and customer success divisions.
Crestwood Meridian expanded its global footprint by opening regional offices in Amsterdam, Singapore, and São Paulo during the fiscal year.
The Amsterdam office serves as the company's European headquarters and employs approximately 340 personnel.
Customer count rose to 3,210 enterprise clients by year-end, up from 2,780 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Net revenue retention rate reached 118%, indicating strong upsell and cross-sell activity within the existing customer base.
The company launched two major platform updates, designated Meridian Core v7 and Meridian Analytics Suite, during the second half of 2023.
Meridian Analytics Suite introduced machine-learning-driven demand forecasting capabilities, which were cited as a key differentiator by early adopters.
Crestwood Meridian's platform achieved ISO 27001 certification during the year, strengthening its security posture for enterprise clients.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program totaling $75 million, of which $42 million had been executed by December 31, 2023.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal year 2023 were reported at $3.18, compared to $2.54 in the prior year.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 22.7%, consistent with prior-year levels and reflecting standard domestic and international provisions.
Cash and cash equivalents on hand at year-end stood at $388 million, providing ample liquidity for planned strategic initiatives.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 41 days from 49 days in fiscal year 2022, indicating faster collections.
The company recorded a one-time restructuring charge of $11 million in the first quarter related to the consolidation of legacy data center operations.
Despite the restructuring charge, operating income for the full year reached $326 million.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $63 million, predominantly allocated to cloud infrastructure and owned office buildouts.
Crestwood Meridian entered into a strategic partnership with Westfield Dynamics to co-develop logistics automation modules.
The partnership is expected to generate incremental annual revenue of approximately $40 million beginning in fiscal year 2025.
Sandra Holbrook emphasized that the company's investment in artificial intelligence research positions it favorably against larger incumbent competitors.
The company filed 14 new patent applications during the fiscal year, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 89 patents.
Crestwood Meridian's net promoter score improved to 62 from 54 in the prior year, reflecting heightened customer satisfaction.
The company experienced no material cybersecurity incidents during fiscal year 2023, a significant achievement given increasing industry-wide threat activity.
Raymond Osei confirmed that the company maintains a conservative capital allocation philosophy prioritizing organic growth over large acquisitions.
Total deferred revenue at year-end was $194 million, reflecting the value of contracted services yet to be recognized.
The deferred revenue balance grew 21% year-over-year, signaling strong forward bookings momentum.
Crestwood Meridian's largest geographic market remained North America, contributing 61% of total revenue.
The Asia-Pacific region showed the fastest growth rate, with revenue rising 31% year-over-year to $148 million.
European revenue grew 18% to reach $182 million, benefiting from the newly established Amsterdam operations.
The company's annual recurring revenue, or ARR, exited fiscal year 2023 at $698 million.
ARR growth of 22% year-over-year was driven primarily by new logo additions and expansion within existing accounts.
Priya Nambiar outlined a series of operational improvements that reduced average customer onboarding time from 94 days to 61 days.
This acceleration in onboarding is expected to shorten the time-to-value for new enterprise clients and reduce early churn risk.
The company's churn rate declined to 4.2% in fiscal year 2023, down from 5.9% in the prior year.
Crestwood Meridian issued guidance for fiscal year 2024 calling for total revenue in the range of $970 million to $995 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $245 million to $260 million.
The guidance reflects continued investment in go-to-market expansion and accelerated product development timelines.
Management noted that macroeconomic uncertainty, particularly around enterprise IT budgets, represents a key downside risk to the outlook.
The board of directors declared a special cash dividend of $0.50 per share, payable to stockholders of record as of January 15, 2024.
This marks the first cash dividend in Crestwood Meridian's history as a publicly traded company.
The report was audited by Hartley & Bruen LLP, which issued an unqualified opinion on the financial statements.
Crestwood Meridian's leadership team expressed confidence that fiscal year 2024 will mark another chapter of disciplined and profitable growth.
The full financial statements, including balance sheet, income statement, and cash flow statement, are appended to this report as Exhibit A.
Veltara Biomedical Corporation is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates within the medical diagnostics sector, specializing in point-of-care testing devices and reagent solutions.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Margaux Feldon opened the report by highlighting a year of transformative growth and operational discipline.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing an 18.4% increase over the prior year.
Domestic sales accounted for $521.6 million of total revenue, while international markets contributed the remaining $325.7 million.
Chief Financial Officer Brennan Ostergaard noted that gross margins improved by 210 basis points compared to fiscal year 2022.
The company's flagship product line, the ClearPath-X diagnostic platform, generated $312.4 million in product revenue alone.
Operating income for the full year came in at $134.7 million, reflecting disciplined cost management across all business units.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $198.4 million, representing approximately 23.4% of total revenue.
Research and development expenditure grew to $103.2 million, underscoring the company's commitment to innovation.
Veltara ended fiscal year 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $229.5 million on the consolidated balance sheet.
This represents an increase of $41.8 million in the company's liquidity position compared to the end of fiscal year 2022.
Accounts receivable stood at $176.4 million as of December 31, 2023, consistent with the company's typical collection cycles.
Inventory levels were maintained at $88.7 million, reflecting an optimized supply chain strategy implemented in the second quarter.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
This dividend represents a 12.5% increase over the quarterly dividend paid in fiscal year 2022, reflecting strong earnings growth.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $67.3 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity in Ohio.
The new production facility in Columbus is expected to increase annual manufacturing throughput by approximately 35% by mid-2025.
Headcount at Veltara grew to 4,812 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, up from 4,205 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
This workforce expansion was concentrated in the company's commercial and regulatory affairs divisions.
Veltara successfully launched two new diagnostic assay kits during the third quarter, the NovaScan-7 and the PulseDetect-C product lines.
Dr. Feldon expressed confidence that these product launches would contribute meaningfully to revenue growth in fiscal year 2024.
The company's market share in the North American point-of-care diagnostics segment reached an estimated 14.2% in fiscal year 2023.
This positions Veltara as the third-largest competitor in its core addressable market by revenue.
Long-term debt on the balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, comprised a $175 million senior unsecured note due in 2028.
The effective interest rate on the outstanding senior notes was 4.35%, fixed at issuance and unchanged during the year.
Veltara did not undertake any new borrowing or credit facility arrangements during fiscal year 2023, keeping its leverage profile stable.
The company's net debt position, calculated as total financial debt less unrestricted cash, stood at zero by the close of the fiscal year.
Operating cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $161.2 million, representing a strong conversion ratio of approximately 120% of net income.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $93.9 million for the full year.
Brennan Ostergaard emphasized that robust free cash flow generation would support continued investment in product development and shareholder returns.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.8%, slightly below the company's initial guidance range of 22% to 24%.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Veltara secured a $220 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of five commercial banks.
Net income attributable to common shareholders for fiscal year 2023 was $104.6 million, or $2.19 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $88.3 million, or $1.86 per diluted share, reported in fiscal year 2022.
The company repurchased approximately 1.1 million shares of its common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $38.42.
Total share repurchases amounted to $42.3 million and reduced the weighted average diluted share count by roughly 2.3%.
Veltara's diluted share count as of December 31, 2023, was 47.8 million shares.
The audit committee confirmed that Ernst & Whitmore LLP conducted the independent external audit for fiscal year 2023.
The auditors issued an unqualified opinion on Veltara's consolidated financial statements, noting no material weaknesses in internal controls.
Segment reporting indicated that the Diagnostics Hardware segment generated $489.1 million in revenue for the fiscal year.
The Reagents and Consumables segment contributed $283.6 million, while the Services and Support segment added $74.6 million.
Gross profit for the Reagents and Consumables segment was notably higher at 68.4%, compared to 41.2% for Diagnostics Hardware.
Management identified geographic expansion into Southeast Asian markets as a key strategic priority for fiscal year 2024.
Veltara signed a distribution agreement with Meridian Asia Pacific Group in November 2023 to support this initiative.
The agreement grants Meridian exclusive distribution rights across seven Southeast Asian countries for a period of five years.
Dr. Feldon stated that the company anticipates incremental international revenue of between $30 million and $45 million from this arrangement in 2024.
Veltara's deferred revenue balance was $23.4 million as of year-end, primarily related to multi-year service contracts.
The company's goodwill balance remained at $312.7 million, unchanged from the prior year, as no acquisitions were completed in 2023.
Intangible assets, net of accumulated amortization, were valued at $147.3 million on the consolidated balance sheet.
Amortization expense related to intangible assets totaled $19.8 million for fiscal year 2023.
For fiscal year 2024, Veltara is guiding for total revenue in the range of $960 million to $990 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 is set at $195 million to $210 million.
The board of directors expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction and the leadership team's ability to execute.
Veltara remains committed to delivering sustainable long-term value to its shareholders, employees, and the patients who depend on its diagnostic solutions.
This financial report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Heliodyne Materials Corporation is a specialty chemicals and advanced coatings manufacturer headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina.
The company was founded in 1998 and has grown to serve industrial, aerospace, and consumer electronics markets across 34 countries.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Forsythe delivered opening remarks at the annual shareholder meeting held on March 14, 2024.
CFO Roland Becker presented the full-year fiscal 2023 financial results, highlighting several areas of strategic progress.
Heliodyne reported consolidated net revenue of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.3% increase over the prior year.
The Advanced Coatings segment contributed $712 million of that total, growing 14.1% year-over-year.
The Specialty Chemicals segment generated $681 million in revenue, while the Industrial Composites segment added $447 million.
Gross margin improved to 38.6% from 35.9% in fiscal 2022, driven primarily by favorable raw material pricing and manufacturing efficiencies.
Operating income for the full year reached $284 million, compared to $231 million in the prior fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $196 million, or $3.42 per diluted share.
The company's adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $341 million, representing an EBITDA margin of 18.5%.
Capital expenditures totaled $118 million, largely directed toward expanding the Greenville, South Carolina manufacturing campus.
Heliodyne ended fiscal 2023 with $223 million in cash and cash equivalents on its balance sheet.
Total assets as of December 31, 2023, were $2.67 billion, up from $2.41 billion at the prior year-end.
The company's inventory balance increased modestly to $314 million, reflecting a deliberate build-up in anticipation of Q1 2024 demand.
Accounts receivable stood at $289 million, consistent with the prior year's level after adjusting for revenue growth.
Heliodyne carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of the close of fiscal year 2023.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly dividend increase of $0.04 per share, bringing the quarterly dividend to $0.31 per share.
This marks the seventh consecutive year in which Heliodyne has raised its quarterly dividend payout.
The company repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares during fiscal 2023 under its existing buyback authorization.
Share repurchases totaled $147 million for the year, executed at an average price of $70.00 per share.
In October 2023, Heliodyne secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of lenders led by First Meridian Bank.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Nandakumar outlined the company's operational priorities for the coming year.
Heliodyne's global headcount reached 8,740 full-time employees at year-end, an increase of 310 from the prior year.
The company added manufacturing capacity in its Monterrey, Mexico facility, bringing total production square footage to 4.2 million.
Research and development expenses increased to $94 million in fiscal 2023, up from $79 million in fiscal 2022.
The R&D investment reflects the company's push into next-generation thermal barrier coatings for aerospace applications.
Heliodyne filed 23 new patent applications during the year, bringing its active patent portfolio to 318 patents globally.
The aerospace coatings product line achieved certification from the Federal Aviation Administration for use on commercial jet engines.
This certification is expected to unlock revenue opportunities exceeding $200 million annually by fiscal 2026.
Heliodyne's top ten customers accounted for 41% of total revenue in fiscal 2023, slightly down from 44% the prior year.
The company added 67 new enterprise accounts during the year, partially offsetting the loss of one mid-tier customer in the European market.
Geographically, North America contributed 54% of revenue, Europe 27%, Asia-Pacific 14%, and other regions 5%.
Revenue in Asia-Pacific grew 22% year-over-year, making it the fastest-growing region for Heliodyne.
The company established a new regional office in Singapore to support its Asia-Pacific expansion strategy.
Ms. Forsythe noted that supply chain disruptions had eased considerably compared to the challenging conditions experienced in 2021 and 2022.
Average lead times for key raw materials declined from 18 weeks in 2022 to approximately 9 weeks in 2023.
The price of titanium dioxide, a key input material, fell 12% during fiscal 2023, contributing positively to gross margins.
Heliodyne entered into a three-year supply agreement with Vantex Chemical Group to secure pricing stability for epoxy resins.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $198 million, representing 10.8% of net revenue.
SG&A as a percentage of revenue declined 60 basis points from fiscal 2022 as the company realized benefits from its cost optimization program.
The cost optimization program, launched in early 2022, has delivered cumulative savings of $53 million through the end of fiscal 2023.
Mr. Becker confirmed that the program remains on track to achieve its original target of $80 million in total savings by the end of fiscal 2025.
Heliodyne's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4%, compared to 24.1% in the prior year.
The reduction in the effective tax rate was driven by increased utilization of R&D tax credits and favorable jurisdictional mix.
The company recognized a $14 million deferred tax asset related to net operating loss carryforwards in certain international subsidiaries.
Free cash flow for the year was $165 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $283 million less capital expenditures of $118 million.
Heliodyne's return on invested capital improved to 13.8% in fiscal 2023, up from 11.4% in fiscal 2022.
The Board authorized an additional $200 million under the share repurchase program, extending the existing authorization through December 2025.
Ms. Nandakumar highlighted that the Greenville campus expansion is expected to be completed by Q3 2024 and will add 420,000 square feet of production space.
The expansion will enable Heliodyne to increase annual production capacity for its high-performance coating systems by an estimated 35%.
Heliodyne issued its first-ever sustainability report in 2023, outlining targets to reduce Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 40% by 2030.
The company reduced its total recordable incident rate to 0.62 per 100 workers, the lowest in the company's history.
For fiscal 2024, Heliodyne issued guidance for net revenue in the range of $1.95 billion to $2.02 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $360 million to $380 million.
Management expects capital expenditures for fiscal 2024 to be between $130 million and $145 million, reflecting continued investment in capacity and automation.
Diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal 2024 was established in the range of $3.65 to $3.85.
Ms. Forsythe closed the meeting by expressing confidence in Heliodyne's competitive positioning and its ability to deliver sustained value for shareholders.
The annual report on Form 10-K was filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2024, and is available on the company's investor relations website.
Veltara Systems Incorporated is a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Marlena Forsythe and CTO David Quresh, with an initial focus on supply chain optimization software.
Over the past decade, Veltara has expanded its portfolio to include real-time data analytics, edge computing services, and managed IT solutions.
Fiscal year 2023 represented a pivotal chapter in Veltara's growth trajectory, as the company navigated both macroeconomic headwinds and significant internal transformation.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, reflecting a 14.2% increase compared to the $741 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Cloud services revenue was the fastest-growing segment, contributing $412 million, or approximately 48.6% of total revenue.
The legacy on-premise software division generated $218 million in revenue, a modest decline of 3.1% year-over-year as customers migrated to cloud-based solutions.
Managed IT services brought in $217 million, representing 25.6% of total revenue and growing 11.4% compared to the prior year.
Gross profit for the full year amounted to $489 million, yielding a gross margin of 57.7%, up from 55.3% in fiscal year 2022.
This improvement in gross margin was primarily attributed to economies of scale in the cloud infrastructure business and disciplined vendor negotiations.
Operating expenses totaled $334 million, including $142 million in research and development and $119 million in sales and marketing.
General and administrative expenses accounted for $73 million of the total operating expense base, slightly elevated due to a compliance overhaul initiated in Q2.
Operating income for the year was $155 million, representing an operating margin of 18.3%, compared to 15.7% in the prior year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $112 million, or $2.14 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $198 million, reflecting an adjusted EBITDA margin of 23.4%.
CFO Renata Hollis noted that disciplined capital allocation remained a top priority throughout the fiscal year.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $64 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's data center presence in the Pacific Northwest.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $134 million, underscoring the company's ability to convert earnings into tangible liquidity.
Veltara ended the fiscal year with cash and cash equivalents of $276 million, providing a strong liquidity buffer for near-term investments.
The company repurchased 2.1 million shares during fiscal year 2023 under its previously authorized $150 million buyback program.
As of December 31, 2023, approximately $47 million remained available under the share repurchase authorization.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share in Q4 2023, consistent with the dividend policy established in early 2021.
Total dividends paid during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $37.6 million, reflecting the company's commitment to returning capital to shareholders.
Veltara's balance sheet remained robust, with total assets of $1.43 billion and total liabilities of $612 million as of year-end.
The current ratio stood at 2.3, and the quick ratio was 1.9, both indicating healthy short-term financial stability.
Customer retention rates across all product lines averaged 91.4% for the year, a slight improvement from 90.1% in fiscal year 2022.
The company added 847 net new enterprise customers during fiscal year 2023, bringing the total active customer count to 6,214.
Average contract value for new enterprise agreements increased to $138,000, up from $121,000 in the prior year.
Veltara's net revenue retention rate reached 117%, driven by strong upsell performance within the cloud analytics suite.
The company's international operations, primarily in Western Europe and Southeast Asia, contributed $203 million to total revenue.
Currency headwinds reduced international revenue by an estimated $14 million compared to constant-currency growth expectations.
Chief Revenue Officer Patrick Drummond highlighted the success of the company's partner channel program, which generated $98 million in indirect revenue.
Veltara launched its next-generation Veltara Nexus platform in March 2023, receiving strong initial uptake from financial services and healthcare clients.
By year-end, Nexus had been adopted by 412 enterprise customers, surpassing the internal target of 350 adoptions within the first calendar year.
The R&D team grew by 14% during fiscal year 2023, adding 198 engineers and data scientists across offices in Austin, Dublin, and Singapore.
Headcount across all functions reached 3,847 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, compared to 3,391 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Employee satisfaction scores, as measured by the annual internal survey, improved to 78 out of 100, up from 72 in the prior year.
The company invested $8.2 million in learning and development programs, reflecting a commitment to internal talent advancement.
Veltara's environmental initiatives resulted in a 9.3% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions relative to the 2022 baseline.
The company sourced 61% of its data center electricity from renewable energy certificates, up from 48% in the prior year.
Marlena Forsythe emphasized in her letter to shareholders that Veltara's cloud transition strategy was tracking ahead of the internal three-year roadmap.
The company reported no outstanding long-term debt as of December 31, 2023, having retired the final tranche of its 2019 term loan in September of that year.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days from 51 days in fiscal year 2022, reflecting better invoicing discipline.
Inventory levels in the hardware accessory division held steady at $22 million, consistent with demand forecasts for the product category.
Deferred revenue grew to $163 million at year-end, a 19.2% increase from $137 million at the close of fiscal year 2022, signaling strong future revenue visibility.
Management confirmed that all financial covenants associated with the company's obligations were satisfied throughout fiscal year 2023 without exception.
In February 2024, Veltara entered into a $300 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five commercial banks, replacing prior arrangements.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management provided initial guidance for revenue in the range of $960 million to $990 million.
The midpoint of guidance implies approximately 16% year-over-year growth, consistent with the long-term organic growth targets outlined at the 2022 Investor Day.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 24% to 25%, reflecting continued operating leverage across the business.
Management expects cloud services to represent more than 55% of total revenue by the end of fiscal year 2024.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltara took on no new external financing arrangements, relying exclusively on internally generated cash flows to fund its operations and investments.
The Nexus platform is expected to become the primary growth engine for new enterprise customer acquisition beginning in the first half of fiscal year 2024.
Veltara's professional services division is being repositioned to serve as an onboarding accelerator for the Nexus customer base rather than as a standalone revenue contributor.
The company anticipates headcount additions of approximately 400 to 450 employees in fiscal year 2024, weighted toward engineering and customer success roles.
David Quresh stated that the Nexus platform's underlying AI inference engine had achieved a 35% reduction in query latency compared to the prior generation.
Veltara has filed seven new patent applications related to distributed workload orchestration and federated data governance since the beginning of fiscal year 2023.
The board of directors approved a 10% increase in the quarterly dividend rate effective Q1 2024, raising it to $0.198 per share.
Renata Hollis noted that the company's weighted average cost of capital was estimated at 8.7% as of year-end, unchanged from the prior year calculation.
Third-party credit analysts at Meridian Rating Services affirmed Veltara's investment-grade credit profile, assigning a stable outlook in their November 2023 review.
The company's stock price appreciated 38% over the course of fiscal year 2023, outperforming the broader enterprise software sector index by approximately 14 percentage points.
Veltara's market capitalization reached approximately $4.1 billion at December 31, 2023, based on the closing share price of $78.42.
The audit committee, chaired by independent director Sophie Langbroek, oversaw the completion of the annual audit without material findings or restatements.
PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP served as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023 and issued an unqualified opinion.
Veltara Systems Incorporated remains committed to transparent financial reporting, disciplined growth investment, and long-term value creation for all stakeholders.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation, a leading provider of molecular testing solutions, is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates across three primary business segments: clinical diagnostics, research instrumentation, and digital health analytics.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Caldwell opened the annual shareholders' meeting by highlighting the company's sustained momentum in the diagnostics sector.
Veltara entered fiscal year 2023 completely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final bond issuance in late 2022.
The clinical diagnostics segment generated $412 million in revenue, representing a 14% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Research instrumentation sales reached $178 million, driven by strong demand from academic and government laboratory clients.
Digital health analytics, the company's newest segment, contributed $89 million to total revenues during the reporting period.
Chief Financial Officer Gerald Oshiro noted that gross margins expanded by 210 basis points year-over-year, reflecting improved manufacturing efficiencies.
In March 2023, the board authorized a $350 million syndicated credit facility to fund the company's accelerating expansion strategy and capital expenditure program.
Operating expenses totaled $498 million for the fiscal year, inclusive of research and development, selling, and general and administrative costs.
Veltara's headcount grew from 4,200 to 4,850 employees over the course of 2023, largely driven by hiring in the digital health division.
The company invested $94 million in research and development activities, representing approximately 13.7% of total revenues.
Capital expenditures amounted to $67 million, primarily allocated to expanding the manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization reached $183 million, a 19% improvement from the prior year's $154 million.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $121 million, yielding diluted earnings per share of $3.47.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, consistent with the dividend payout maintained throughout 2022.
Veltara's stock repurchase program resulted in the buyback of approximately 1.2 million shares at an average price of $61.40 per share.
Total cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $256 million, compared to $198 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days from 51 days in the prior year, reflecting enhanced collections processes.
Inventory turnover ratio improved to 6.8 times from 5.9 times, indicating more efficient management of raw materials and finished goods.
The Durham manufacturing expansion is expected to increase annual production capacity by approximately 30% once fully commissioned in mid-2024.
Veltara launched its next-generation VX-7 analyzer platform in the second quarter of 2023, receiving immediate interest from hospital networks.
The VX-7 platform incorporates proprietary microfluidic technology licensed from BioStream Technologies under a ten-year agreement signed in 2021.
By year-end, Veltara had secured purchase commitments for 340 VX-7 units, with deliveries scheduled through the first half of 2024.
The company's clinical diagnostics segment maintained a market share of approximately 8.3% in the North American in-vitro diagnostics market.
Revenue from international markets accounted for 31% of total revenues in 2023, up from 27% in the prior year.
Veltara's European operations recorded revenue of $142 million, benefiting from favorable currency movements and expanded distribution partnerships.
The Asia-Pacific region contributed $71 million to revenues, with particularly strong growth in Japan and South Korea.
Dr. Caldwell emphasized the company's commitment to regulatory compliance, noting that all manufacturing facilities maintained ISO 13485 certification throughout the year.
The company received FDA 510(k) clearance for two new assay kits in 2023, expanding the menu of tests available on existing analyzer platforms.
Veltara's quality management systems were audited by independent third-party assessors in October 2023 with no major findings reported.
The legal department resolved two pending patent infringement disputes during the year, with total settlement costs of $4.7 million recorded in operating expenses.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced materially in 2023, with the company reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 12%.
Veltara established a diversity and inclusion council in January 2023, targeting a 40% representation of women in senior leadership roles by 2026.
The company's employee retention rate was 91% for fiscal 2023, exceeding the medical devices industry average of approximately 85%.
Gerald Oshiro indicated that the effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4%, modestly below the prior year's rate of 23.1%.
Deferred tax liabilities increased by $11 million, primarily due to accelerated depreciation on manufacturing equipment under current tax regulations.
The pension and post-retirement benefit obligation was $38 million at year-end, reflecting an actuarial assumption of a 4.5% discount rate.
Veltara entered into a strategic collaboration agreement with NovaBridge Genomics in September 2023 to co-develop next-generation sequencing consumables.
Under the NovaBridge agreement, Veltara will contribute up to $25 million in development funding over a three-year period.
