The company's order backlog at December 31, 2023, totaled $314 million, representing approximately five months of expected forward revenue.
Management issued forward guidance for fiscal 2024, projecting total revenues in the range of $740 million to $760 million.
The 2024 guidance assumes continued mid-single-digit growth in the clinical diagnostics segment and accelerated growth in digital health analytics.
Veltara expects to achieve an EBITDA margin of approximately 26% to 27% in fiscal 2024, up from the 2023 level of approximately 26.6%.
The company anticipates capital expenditures of $80 million to $90 million in fiscal 2024, with the majority directed toward the Durham expansion and IT infrastructure.
Adjusted for the amortization of acquired intangible assets, non-GAAP diluted earnings per share were $3.91 for fiscal 2023.
Veltara completed the acquisition of Praxis Bioanalytical Inc. in July 2023 for a total consideration of $55 million in cash.
The Praxis acquisition added approximately 110 employees and contributed $18 million in revenue to Veltara's results from the acquisition date.
Goodwill and intangible assets arising from the Praxis transaction were recorded at $41 million and will be amortized over periods ranging from five to fifteen years.
The company's consolidated balance sheet showed total assets of $1.04 billion at December 31, 2023, compared to $912 million at the prior year-end.
Shareholders' equity stood at $548 million at year-end, reflecting net income for the period offset by dividends paid and share repurchases.
The current ratio at year-end was 2.6, and the quick ratio was 2.1, indicating a strong short-term liquidity position.
Veltara has maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's, which was reaffirmed in November 2023.
The audit committee oversaw the work of the independent registered public accounting firm, Hargreave & Lund LLP, which issued an unqualified opinion.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective in accordance with the framework established by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations.
Dr. Caldwell concluded her remarks by acknowledging the contributions of all Veltara employees in delivering a record year of financial performance.
The company reaffirmed its long-term strategic goal of achieving $1 billion in annual revenues by fiscal year 2026.
Veltara remains committed to investing in innovation, expanding its global footprint, and delivering sustainable value to its shareholders and stakeholders.
This report contains certain forward-looking statements that are subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those anticipated.
Investors are encouraged to review the company's filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission for a complete discussion of risk factors affecting future performance.
Verdana Logistics Group, Inc. reported its fiscal year 2023 annual results on March 14, 2024, marking another milestone in the company's operational history.
Founded in 2009 and headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, Verdana Logistics Group specializes in last-mile delivery solutions and cold-chain freight services across North America.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the earnings call by expressing confidence in the company's strategic direction heading into fiscal year 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Chu highlighted that total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $4.82 billion, representing a 9.3% increase year-over-year.
The cold-chain segment alone contributed $1.97 billion to total revenues, driven by expanded pharmaceutical and perishable goods contracts.
Operating expenses for the year totaled $3.91 billion, with fuel and driver compensation costs comprising the largest share at approximately 58% of the total.
Adjusted EBITDA came in at $740 million, slightly ahead of the consensus analyst estimate of $728 million.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $312 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $4.17.
Verdana's balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflected total assets of $6.3 billion and total liabilities of $3.8 billion.
The company closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired the final tranche of its 2019 senior notes in June of that year.
Headcount across all divisions grew modestly to 34,200 full-time employees by year-end, up from 32,800 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Driver recruitment remained a top operational priority throughout 2023, as the logistics industry continued to face broad labor market tightness.
Verdana launched its proprietary route-optimization platform, VerdaRoute 3.0, in April 2023, which management credited with improving delivery efficiency by 11%.
The technology investment underlying VerdaRoute 3.0 totaled approximately $47 million and was capitalized over a 30-month development cycle.
During the second half of 2023, the company finalized a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of seven institutional lenders to fund fleet expansion.
Capital expenditures for the year were $418 million, reflecting significant investment in electric vehicle fleet additions and warehouse automation.
The electric vehicle initiative added 620 new EV delivery vans to the active fleet, representing roughly 4.1% of total vehicle capacity.
Sustainability metrics improved materially, with carbon emissions per delivery mile falling 7.2% compared to the prior fiscal year.
Raymond Chu noted that free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $322 million, down slightly from $341 million in fiscal year 2022 due to elevated capex.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.38 per share, consistent with the prior four quarters and reflecting a commitment to steady shareholder returns.
Verdana repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $61.40, retiring them to treasury.
The share repurchase program, authorized at $200 million in January 2023, was approximately 64% utilized by year-end.
Margaret Holloway emphasized that the company's market share in temperature-controlled freight reached 14.8% nationally, up from 13.1% the prior year.
Verdana signed three new anchor contracts with major grocery chains during the fiscal year, each spanning a five-year term.
The grocery sector contracts are expected to generate an incremental $280 million in annualized revenues beginning in fiscal year 2024.
International expansion remained limited, with cross-border Canada-U.S. routes accounting for just 6.2% of total segment revenues.
The company's logistics technology subsidiary, Verdana Tech Solutions, posted revenues of $88 million, a 22% increase over its prior-year performance.
Verdana Tech Solutions secured a multi-year SaaS contract with a regional hospital network valued at approximately $15 million annually.
The SaaS segment gross margin stood at 61%, considerably higher than the consolidated company gross margin of 19%.
Raymond Chu stated that inventory held at Verdana-operated fulfillment centers totaled $134 million at year-end, down 8% from the prior year's $145 million.
The reduction in inventory was attributed to improved demand forecasting algorithms deployed as part of the VerdaRoute 3.0 rollout.
Customer satisfaction scores, as measured by the company's proprietary Net Promoter Score system, averaged 72 for fiscal year 2023.
This NPS figure represented a four-point improvement over fiscal year 2022's average of 68 and was the highest recorded in the company's history.
Verdana's top ten customers accounted for 38% of total revenues, indicating a moderately diversified client base.
No single customer exceeded 7.4% of total revenues, limiting concentration risk from any individual contractual relationship.
The company's days sales outstanding metric improved to 31 days from 36 days in the prior fiscal year, reflecting better collections processes.
Verdana's logistics fleet totaled 15,100 vehicles as of December 31, 2023, including both owned and leased units.
Fleet utilization averaged 87.3% across the year, the highest figure reported since the company's IPO in 2013.
Margaret Holloway indicated that Verdana plans to open four new regional distribution hubs in fiscal year 2024, located in Denver, Charlotte, Memphis, and Sacramento.
Each new hub is expected to require between $30 million and $45 million in initial capital investment, with operational ramp-up occurring over 12 to 18 months.
The company's credit rating was reaffirmed at BBB+ by two major rating agencies during the third quarter of 2023.
Analysts at Westbrook Capital noted in their November 2023 report that Verdana's leverage profile was among the most conservative in the peer group.
Guidance for fiscal year 2024 calls for revenues in the range of $5.1 billion to $5.3 billion, representing approximately 6% to 10% growth.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $790 million to $820 million, implying margin expansion of roughly 40 to 60 basis points.
Management acknowledged that macroeconomic headwinds, including potential softness in consumer spending, represent a key downside risk to the full-year outlook.
Fuel price volatility was identified as a secondary risk factor, particularly given Verdana's limited fuel hedging coverage of approximately 30% of consumption.
The company's insurance costs rose 12% in fiscal year 2023, driven by higher commercial auto liability premiums across the industry.
Verdana carries $1.5 billion in combined liability coverage, which management described as appropriate given the scale of fleet operations.
Raymond Chu reaffirmed that the company's pension obligations were fully funded as of the actuarial measurement date of September 30, 2023.
Employee turnover in the driver category decreased to 31% annually, compared with an industry average estimated at 47%, reflecting the impact of wage enhancement programs.
Driver base pay was increased by an average of 6.8% effective January 1, 2023, which management credited as a primary factor in improved retention.
Verdana's legal docket as of year-end included three pending personal injury claims and one regulatory inquiry, none of which management considered material.
The regulatory inquiry relates to emissions compliance for a subset of older diesel vehicles operating in California, with an estimated resolution cost of under $2 million.
Research and development expenditure for fiscal year 2023 totaled $63 million, up from $51 million in fiscal year 2022.
The R&D increase reflects ongoing investment in autonomous delivery pilot programs and enhanced telematics systems.
Verdana's pilot autonomous delivery program operated in limited geographies and completed approximately 14,000 test deliveries during the fiscal year.
Results from the autonomous pilot were incorporated into management's long-range planning model for the 2026 to 2030 strategic horizon.
The board authorized a $150 million expansion of the existing share repurchase program in February 2024, bringing total authorization to $350 million.

Margaret Holloway concluded her prepared remarks by reiterating Verdana Logistics Group's commitment to disciplined capital allocation, operational excellence, and sustainable growth as the company advances into fiscal year 2024.
The full audited financial statements and accompanying notes are available in the company's Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on March 14, 2024.
Veltora Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company was founded in 2009 and has grown to serve over 4,200 enterprise clients across 38 countries.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Ellenbrough presented the annual results to shareholders at the company's investor day held in March.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Danthashakar outlined the key financial metrics and forward guidance during the same event.
Veltora Systems reported total revenue of $1.84 billion for the fiscal year ending December 31, representing an 11.3% increase year-over-year.
Subscription revenue accounted for 72% of total revenue, underscoring the company's successful transition to a recurring-revenue model.
Professional services revenue grew 8.7% to $215 million, reflecting strong demand for implementation and consulting engagements.
The company's gross margin expanded by 140 basis points to 67.4%, driven by operating leverage in the cloud delivery segment.
Operating expenses totaled $986 million, a modest 6.2% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Research and development spending reached $312 million, representing 17% of total revenue and reflecting continued investment in product innovation.
Sales and marketing expenditures were $418 million, down slightly as a percentage of revenue due to improved sales efficiency.
General and administrative costs were held flat at $256 million through disciplined cost management initiatives.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year came in at $497 million, yielding an adjusted EBITDA margin of 27.0%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $218 million, or $1.74 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $179 million, or $1.43 per diluted share, in the prior fiscal year.
The company generated free cash flow of $381 million, representing a free cash flow margin of approximately 20.7%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $643 million, a substantial increase from $489 million at the end of the previous year.
Veltora Systems completed a $150 million share repurchase program during the fourth quarter, reducing diluted share count by approximately 2.1%.
The company carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, having retired all outstanding obligations earlier in the fiscal year.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $94 million, focused primarily on data center expansion and IT infrastructure upgrades.
The company opened two new regional offices in Singapore and Warsaw to support growing demand in Asia-Pacific and Eastern Europe.
Total headcount at year-end was 9,840 full-time employees, a net addition of 620 employees compared to the prior year-end.
Employee retention rate improved to 91.3%, the highest level recorded in Veltora's history.
In February of the following fiscal year, Veltora Systems secured a $275 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five major financial institutions.
The new credit facility carries an interest rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points and matures in five years.
Management stated that the credit facility provides strategic flexibility to pursue bolt-on acquisitions and fund working capital needs.
During the fiscal year, Veltora completed the acquisition of Torchpath Analytics for $88 million in cash.
Torchpath Analytics added approximately 210 employees and a suite of predictive analytics tools to Veltora's product portfolio.
The acquisition contributed $14 million in revenue during the partial year following its close.
Integration costs associated with the Torchpath acquisition were $7.3 million and were recognized as non-recurring charges.
Veltora launched three major product releases during the year, including the VeltoCloud 5.0 platform, DataBridge Pro, and Sentinel AI.
VeltoCloud 5.0 achieved rapid adoption, with over 800 new deployments recorded within the first six months of availability.
Sentinel AI, the company's machine-learning-driven security module, was recognized by two independent industry analysts as a leading solution in its category.
DataBridge Pro integration connectors expanded from 140 to over 220 supported third-party applications during the year.
Customer satisfaction scores as measured by net promoter score improved to 68, up from 61 in the prior year.
The North American segment remained the largest, contributing $1.09 billion, or approximately 59% of total revenue.
International revenue grew at a faster rate of 16.4%, reaching $750 million and reflecting ongoing geographic expansion.
The EMEA region was the strongest performer internationally, with revenue growing 21.2% to $412 million.
Veltora's top ten customers accounted for 19.4% of total revenue, indicating a well-diversified customer base.
Annual contract value for new bookings reached $340 million, up 14.8% from the prior year's new booking figure.
The company's dollar-based net revenue retention rate was 114%, indicating that existing customers expanded their usage significantly.
Churn rate among enterprise customers remained low at 3.8%, consistent with the prior year's figure.
The company issued a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share in each of the four quarters, totaling $0.48 per share for the fiscal year.
The board of directors approved a 10% increase in the quarterly dividend, raising it to $0.132 per share effective with the first quarter of the new fiscal year.
Veltora's stock price appreciated 34% over the course of the fiscal year, outperforming the broader software sector index by 18 percentage points.
The company's market capitalization at fiscal year-end stood at approximately $14.2 billion, based on shares outstanding of 124.5 million.
Total backlog, representing contracted but unrecognized revenue, reached $2.3 billion, providing strong visibility into future revenue streams.
Remaining performance obligations grew 18% year-over-year to $3.1 billion, with 62% expected to be recognized within the next twelve months.
The company's effective tax rate for the fiscal year was 19.8%, lower than the prior year's 22.4% due to favorable R&D tax credits.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet increased to $487 million from $391 million, reflecting strong billings growth.
Accounts receivable days sales outstanding improved to 47 days from 53 days a year earlier, reflecting improved collections performance.
Inventory levels, primarily consisting of hardware appliances for on-premise deployments, declined to $38 million from $52 million year-over-year.
The decline in hardware inventory reflects the ongoing customer migration toward cloud-native deployments, which require no physical hardware.
Veltora Systems expects fiscal year revenue in the range of $2.03 billion to $2.07 billion, representing growth of approximately 10.3% to 12.5%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for the coming fiscal year is 27.5% to 28.0%, reflecting anticipated operating leverage.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Ellenbrough expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction, citing a robust pipeline and strong competitive positioning.
Priya Danthashakar closed the investor day presentation by reaffirming that Veltora Systems is well positioned to deliver long-term shareholder value.
Velarion Technologies Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2011 by CEO Marcus Holt and CTO Priya Sundaram, and has since grown to serve clients across 34 countries.
Velarion's fiscal year 2023 annual report reflects a period of significant strategic transformation and operational realignment.
The company's flagship product, SupplyCore Pro, underwent its most comprehensive feature overhaul in company history during the third quarter.
Chief Financial Officer Danielle Ostroff presented the full-year results to the board of directors in February 2024.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing a 14.2 percent increase over the prior year.
Subscription-based revenue now constitutes 71 percent of total revenue, up from 63 percent in fiscal year 2022.
Velarion ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final outstanding bond obligations in January of that year.
Gross margin improved to 68.4 percent, compared to 65.1 percent in the prior fiscal year, driven by cloud infrastructure efficiencies.
Operating expenses totaled $231.6 million, a 9.8 percent increase attributed primarily to expanded sales and marketing initiatives.
Research and development spending reached $74.2 million, representing approximately 15.2 percent of total revenue.
The company added 312 net new enterprise customers during fiscal year 2023, bringing its total customer count to 2,847.
In September 2023, Velarion secured a $175 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of four investment-grade lenders to support its acquisition strategy.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $118.9 million, reflecting a margin of 24.4 percent.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $61.4 million, compared to $49.7 million in fiscal year 2022.
Earnings per diluted share came in at $1.42, exceeding the consensus analyst estimate of $1.31.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $203.7 million, providing a strong liquidity buffer.
The company generated free cash flow of $94.6 million, a 22 percent improvement over the prior year.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $18.3 million, largely directed toward data center expansion in the EMEA region.
Velarion's board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $100 million in October 2023.
Under this program, the company repurchased approximately 1.4 million shares at an average price of $38.20 per share by year-end.
The annualized recurring revenue metric, or ARR, grew to $362.8 million, representing 19.3 percent year-over-year growth.
Net revenue retention rate held at 114 percent, indicating strong expansion within the existing customer base.
Customer churn remained below 5 percent on an annualized basis, consistent with performance from the two preceding years.
The company's headcount stood at 3,214 full-time employees at the close of fiscal year 2023.
Velarion hired 487 new employees during the year, primarily in engineering and customer success roles.
Average revenue per enterprise customer grew by 8.7 percent year-over-year, reflecting successful upselling efforts.
The Asia-Pacific segment delivered particularly strong results, with revenue growing 31.4 percent in constant currency terms.
By contrast, the North American segment grew at a more modest 10.6 percent, constrained by market saturation in key verticals.
European revenues grew 17.8 percent in constant currency, aided by two new reseller partnerships established in the first quarter.
Marcus Holt noted in his letter to shareholders that Velarion is positioned to capture meaningful share in the $42 billion global supply chain software market.
The company's win rate against its three primary competitors improved from 38 percent to 44 percent over the course of the year.
Velarion launched two new product modules in fiscal year 2023: DemandSense AI and LogisticsFlow 360.
DemandSense AI, which entered general availability in April 2023, leverages machine learning to improve demand forecasting accuracy.
LogisticsFlow 360 completed its beta program in August and was made generally available to all customers in October 2023.
The integration of these two modules drove a 6.1 percent uplift in average contract value among adopting customers.
Priya Sundaram highlighted that the engineering team reduced average platform latency by 34 percent through infrastructure re-architecture efforts.
Velarion's platform achieved SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 certification renewals during the fiscal year, reinforcing its enterprise security posture.
The company also received FedRAMP Moderate authorization in July 2023, opening the door to U.S. federal government procurement opportunities.
Danielle Ostroff underscored that the FedRAMP authorization required an 18-month compliance investment totaling approximately $8.4 million.
Three federal agency pilot programs were initiated in the fourth quarter following the FedRAMP authorization.
Velarion's partner ecosystem expanded to include 94 certified implementation partners by year-end, up from 71 at the start of the year.
The company's marketplace, VelarionConnect, now lists over 230 third-party integrations, a 40 percent increase versus the prior year.
Customer satisfaction scores, as measured by Net Promoter Score, improved from 52 to 61 over the course of fiscal year 2023.
Implementation cycle times were reduced by an average of 19 days through the introduction of automated onboarding workflows.
The professional services segment contributed $58.4 million in revenue, accounting for approximately 12 percent of total revenue.
Professional services margins remain lower than the company average at 38 percent, consistent with industry norms for implementation services.
Velarion's deferred revenue balance grew to $142.3 million at year-end, indicating strong forward bookings.
The company's accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 41 days from 47 days in the prior year.
Goodwill on the balance sheet remained unchanged at $84.6 million, reflecting no acquisition activity during fiscal year 2023.
Total assets at year-end were $671.4 million, up from $589.2 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Stockholders' equity grew to $388.1 million, supported by net income and offset partially by share repurchase activity.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3 percent, slightly above the prior year rate of 20.8 percent.
Velarion declared no cash dividends during fiscal year 2023, consistent with its growth-oriented capital allocation policy.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for total revenue in the range of $548 million to $562 million.
This guidance implies year-over-year revenue growth of approximately 12 to 15 percent at the midpoint.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 25 to 26 percent, reflecting continued operating leverage.
Management expects ARR to reach between $430 million and $445 million by the close of fiscal year 2024.
The company anticipates headcount growth of approximately 8 to 10 percent in fiscal year 2024, concentrated in go-to-market functions.
Velarion's board of directors expressed confidence in the company's multi-year growth trajectory at the February 2024 investor day.
The company remains committed to achieving Rule of 40 compliance, a metric combining revenue growth rate and EBITDA margin.
Velarion's combined Rule of 40 score for fiscal year 2023 was 38.6, approaching but not yet meeting the benchmark threshold.
Management believes the strategic investments made in fiscal year 2023 will yield compounding returns through fiscal year 2026 and beyond.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks including macroeconomic conditions, competitive dynamics, and execution uncertainty.
Veltara Biosciences concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, carrying a pristine balance sheet into the new year.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Maren Holloway expressed confidence in Veltara's strategic trajectory during the annual shareholder meeting held in March 2024.
The company reported total revenues of $842 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.7% increase over the prior year.
Veltara's flagship oncology drug, Nexapril, accounted for approximately 61% of total product revenues during the reporting period.
Operating expenses rose modestly to $530 million, driven primarily by increased investment in clinical-stage research pipelines.
During the third quarter of fiscal 2023, Veltara secured a $350 million term loan facility with a consortium of institutional lenders to fund expansion activities.
Chief Financial Officer Patrick Ruelle stated that gross margins improved by 210 basis points year-over-year, reaching 63.4%.
The company's research and development expenditure totaled $198 million, representing 23.5% of net revenues for the fiscal year.
Veltara employed approximately 4,800 full-time staff globally as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a 9% headcount increase from the previous year.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of January 15, 2024.
Veltara's North American segment generated $491 million in revenue, making it the largest contributing geography for the third consecutive year.
The European operations contributed $214 million to total revenues, buoyed by expanded distribution agreements in Germany and France.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $112 million, up from $94 million in fiscal 2022, reflecting proactive supply chain management ahead of anticipated demand growth.
Veltara launched two new diagnostic tools in its precision medicine division during the second half of the fiscal year.
The Asia-Pacific segment posted a 22% revenue increase, reaching $137 million and surpassing internal growth targets by a wide margin.
Dr. Holloway emphasized that the pipeline of next-generation therapeutics remains robust, with four candidates currently in Phase II trials.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $76 million, allocated primarily to manufacturing capacity upgrades at the New Jersey facility.
Veltara's market capitalization averaged approximately $9.4 billion throughout fiscal 2023, reflecting sustained investor confidence.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3%, consistent with management's guidance range of 20% to 23%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $187 million, equating to diluted earnings per share of $2.41.
Mr. Ruelle noted that cash and cash equivalents totaled $623 million at fiscal year-end, providing ample liquidity for near-term operational needs.
Veltara repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares under its existing buyback authorization during fiscal 2023, returning value to shareholders.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed the independent audit with an unqualified opinion.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days, down from 53 days in the prior fiscal year, indicating more efficient collections.
Veltara's oncology franchise benefited from favorable formulary positioning in seven major U.S. health plan networks during the reporting period.
The company entered into a strategic collaboration agreement with Kyorin Pharmaceutical in Japan, effective October 2023.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $214 million, representing 25.4% of net revenues for the fiscal year.
Dr. Holloway highlighted that Veltara achieved a patient reach of over 280,000 individuals treated globally with its approved therapies.
The company's total assets increased to $2.1 billion as of December 31, 2023, compared to $1.87 billion at the end of fiscal 2022.
Veltara's long-term incentive program granted performance stock units to 312 senior employees during the fiscal year.
The Board approved a new share repurchase program authorizing up to $500 million in common stock buybacks over the next 24 months.
Veltara's precision medicine division contributed $89 million in revenue, representing a 31% increase over the prior fiscal year.
The company's current ratio stood at 2.8 as of the fiscal year-end, demonstrating strong short-term liquidity.
Mr. Ruelle confirmed that the company has no material off-balance-sheet arrangements that would impact its financial position.
Veltara received Fast Track designation from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for its lead immunotherapy candidate, VBT-447.
The company's deferred revenue balance was $43 million at year-end, primarily related to multi-year licensing agreements.
Veltara's headcount in its research and development division grew by 15%, adding 320 new scientific and technical employees during fiscal 2023.
The Board declared a special one-time dividend of $0.50 per share in recognition of the company's strong fiscal performance.
Veltara's international revenue as a proportion of total revenues increased to 42%, up from 38% in the prior fiscal year.
The company maintained a weighted-average cost of capital of approximately 8.2% for fiscal 2023, used in its impairment testing models.
Dr. Holloway confirmed that all four of Veltara's manufacturing sites achieved ISO 13485 certification during the reporting period.
Total stockholders' equity grew to $1.34 billion as of December 31, 2023, up from $1.16 billion the prior year.
Veltara's goodwill balance of $310 million remained unchanged following the annual impairment assessment, with no write-downs required.
The company disclosed no material legal proceedings that would have a significant adverse effect on its financial statements.
Mr. Ruelle stated that Veltara's interest coverage ratio was 18.4 times for fiscal 2023, reflecting robust earnings relative to financing costs.
Veltara's biotechnology segment earned $253 million in segment operating income, its highest level on record.
The company recorded a $14 million gain on the sale of a non-core diagnostics product line completed in the second quarter of 2023.
Veltara increased its guidance for fiscal 2024, projecting total revenues between $940 million and $970 million.
The company's common stock closed at $78.42 per share on the last trading day of fiscal 2023.
Dr. Holloway noted that Veltara's employee retention rate was 91%, reflecting a strong corporate culture and competitive compensation packages.
Veltara established a new Center of Excellence for Translational Medicine at its Boston campus, opening in September 2023.
The company's amortization of intangible assets totaled $38 million for fiscal 2023, primarily related to acquired licensing rights.
Veltara's Nexapril achieved peak quarterly net sales of $136 million in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023.
The company provided no updated guidance on the timing of its Biologics License Application submission for VBT-447.
Mr. Ruelle highlighted that operating cash flow reached $244 million, representing a 17% increase over the prior year.
Veltara's debt-to-equity ratio at fiscal year-end was 0.0, confirming the company's unlevered capital structure.
The company's depreciation expense for fiscal 2023 was $41 million, primarily attributed to manufacturing equipment and laboratory infrastructure.
Veltara made no material acquisitions during fiscal 2023, opting instead to grow organically through internal research initiatives.
Dr. Holloway acknowledged that clinical trial enrollment for VBT-447 had exceeded target enrollment rates by approximately 18%.
Veltara's Board of Directors consists of ten members, six of whom are classified as independent directors under Nasdaq governance standards.
The company disclosed that foreign currency fluctuations had an approximately $12 million adverse effect on reported revenues for fiscal 2023.
Mr. Ruelle projected that free cash flow conversion is expected to remain above 75% in fiscal 2024, consistent with the company's long-term financial model.
Veltara's annual report was filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2024, within the standard deadline for large accelerated filers.
The company remains committed to its mission of advancing innovative therapies that address unmet needs in oncology and immunology.
Veldora Photonics Corporation released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, highlighting a period of significant operational expansion.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializes in the design and manufacture of advanced photonic integrated circuits for telecommunications and data center applications.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened her letter to shareholders by noting that 2023 represented a transformative year for the organization.
Veldora Photonics reported total consolidated revenues of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the prior year.
The telecommunications segment contributed $512.6 million of total revenue, while the data center segment accounted for the remaining $334.7 million.
Gross profit for the year reached $381.4 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 45.0%, up from 42.7% in the previous fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Tremblay attributed the margin improvement to favorable product mix shifts and ongoing manufacturing efficiency programs.
Operating expenses, including research and development, selling, general, and administrative costs, totaled $214.9 million for the full year.
Research and development expenditure alone amounted to $98.3 million, representing 11.6% of total revenue and reflecting the company's commitment to next-generation product development.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 came in at $166.5 million, compared to $131.2 million in fiscal 2022, marking a 26.9% year-over-year improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $128.7 million, or $2.14 per diluted share, compared to $101.5 million, or $1.69 per diluted share, in the prior year.
The effective tax rate for the year was 22.4%, slightly below management's guidance of approximately 23% provided at the beginning of the fiscal year.
Veldora Photonics generated $193.6 million in cash from operating activities during 2023, a 17.8% increase from the $164.3 million generated in 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $67.2 million, primarily directed toward the expansion of the company's fabrication facility in San Jose, California.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, reached $126.4 million for the fiscal year.
The company returned $42.1 million to shareholders through its quarterly dividend program over the course of 2023.
An additional $55.0 million was deployed toward share repurchases under the $150 million buyback program authorized by the board in February 2022.
As of December 31, 2023, Veldora Photonics held $284.9 million in cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments on its balance sheet.
Inventories at year-end stood at $112.4 million, reflecting a modest increase from $98.7 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Total assets at year-end were $1.42 billion, compared to $1.27 billion at the end of the prior fiscal year.
Accounts receivable totaled $143.6 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 62 days, consistent with historical norms for the business.
The company's backlog of firm orders at December 31, 2023 reached $620 million, representing over seven months of annualized revenue coverage.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Lim noted that on-time delivery performance improved to 96.4% in 2023, up from 91.8% in the prior year.
Veldora Photonics expanded its global workforce to 4,850 full-time employees by the end of fiscal 2023, an increase of approximately 310 employees from year-end 2022.
The company opened a new engineering center in Eindhoven, Netherlands during the second quarter of 2023, adding 120 specialized positions in photonic chip design.
Veldora Photonics successfully launched its next-generation VX-9 coherent transceiver module in March 2023, targeting hyperscale data center operators.
By the close of fiscal year 2023, the company carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its $175 million term loan ahead of schedule in October 2023.
The VX-9 platform achieved design wins with four of the top six global hyperscale operators within its first nine months of commercial availability.
Average selling prices for the VX-9 module were approximately $1,850 per unit, positioning the product at the premium tier of the coherent optics market.
Revenue from the VX-9 product line contributed an estimated $78.4 million to the data center segment's results in 2023, its first partial year of commercial shipments.
Mr. Tremblay highlighted that the data center segment's operating margins expanded by 380 basis points year-over-year, driven in large part by the VX-9 ramp.
The telecommunications segment experienced robust demand from tier-one carriers upgrading their core networks to support growing bandwidth requirements.
International revenues accounted for 54.3% of total company revenues in 2023, up from 49.8% in 2022, driven by strong growth in Europe and Southeast Asia.
Veldora Photonics maintained its position as the second-largest supplier of photonic integrated circuits by revenue in the North American market.
The company's market share in the North American coherent optics segment grew to an estimated 18.7% in 2023, compared to 16.2% the prior year.
Ms. Holloway expressed confidence that the company's technology roadmap positions it to capture additional market share over the next three to five years.
During fiscal year 2023, Veldora Photonics secured a $240 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of five major banks, which remained undrawn at year-end.
The board of directors approved a 9.5% increase in the quarterly dividend rate effective with the first quarter of 2024, reflecting confidence in ongoing cash generation.
Capital allocation priorities for 2024 include continued investment in fabrication capacity, sustaining research and development intensity, and returning capital to shareholders.
Management provided initial fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $940 million to $970 million, implying growth of approximately 11% to 15% over fiscal 2023.
Gross margin guidance for 2024 was set at 45.5% to 46.5%, reflecting expected benefits from volume leverage and continued product mix improvement.
The company expects operating expenses in 2024 to be in the range of $230 million to $240 million, consistent with planned investment in headcount and infrastructure.
Veldora Photonics anticipates capital expenditures of approximately $80 million to $90 million in 2024 as it completes the San Jose fabrication expansion.
Ms. Lim stated that supply chain resiliency remains a top operational priority following disruptions experienced in the broader semiconductor industry in prior years.
The company has diversified its substrate supplier base from two to five qualified vendors over the past 18 months, reducing single-source risk.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced meaningfully in 2023, with the company reducing Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 11.3% year-over-year.
Veldora Photonics achieved a recordable incident rate of 0.42 per 100 employees in 2023, its lowest safety performance on record.
The audit committee confirmed that KPMG LLP has been engaged as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for the fiscal year 2023 audit.
KPMG issued an unqualified opinion on Veldora Photonics' consolidated financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2023.
The company's internal controls over financial reporting were evaluated under the framework established by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations of the Treadway Commission.
No material weaknesses in internal controls were identified during the fiscal year 2023 assessment process.
Mr. Tremblay closed his financial review by reaffirming the company's commitment to disciplined capital allocation, operational excellence, and long-term value creation for shareholders.

Veldora Photonics remains well-positioned to benefit from secular growth trends in optical networking, and the board and management team look forward to reporting continued progress in the year ahead.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation firm headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
The company concluded the fiscal year entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final outstanding bond obligations in January 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway expressed confidence in the firm's operational trajectory, citing record-breaking throughput across all five manufacturing facilities.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the $1.64 billion reported in fiscal year 2022.
The North American segment contributed the largest share of revenue at $1.02 billion, followed by the European segment at $530 million and Asia-Pacific at $290 million.
Gross profit margin improved by 140 basis points year-over-year, reaching 38.7% compared to 37.3% in the prior fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei attributed the margin expansion to favorable raw material pricing and improved factory utilization rates throughout the calendar year.
Operating income rose to $312 million, up from $264 million in fiscal year 2022, driven by disciplined cost management and productivity initiatives.
Research and development expenditure increased to $147 million, representing approximately 8% of total revenue, as the company invested heavily in next-generation robotic assembly systems.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $220 million, which was slightly below management's original guidance of $228 million for the full year.
The company's headcount grew modestly from 9,400 employees at the start of the fiscal year to 9,750 employees by December 31, 2023.
During the third quarter of fiscal 2023, Veltrano Dynamics secured a $400 million syndicated term loan facility to fund its planned expansion into Southeast Asian markets.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $198 million, concentrated primarily in the upgrade of the Toledo and Pittsburgh manufacturing plants.
Free cash flow generation reached $274 million, providing the company with significant financial flexibility heading into the new fiscal year.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, representing a 12% increase over the prior year's quarterly dividend of $0.25 per share.
Veltrano Dynamics repurchased 3.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $47.60 per share, totaling approximately $152 million.
The company's backlog of unfulfilled orders stood at $2.1 billion as of December 31, 2023, compared to $1.7 billion at the same point in 2022.
President of Operations Clara Whitfield noted that on-time delivery rates improved to 94.6% in 2023 from 91.2% in 2022, a reflection of supply chain stabilization efforts.
Veltrano's flagship product line, the VX-9000 Series programmable controllers, achieved record unit sales of 214,000 units during the fiscal year.
The VX-9000 Series accounted for 31% of total product revenue, maintaining its position as the company's single largest revenue-generating product family.
New product introductions in fiscal 2023 included the Veltrano Sentient Edge platform, a cloud-connected predictive maintenance solution launched in the second quarter.
The Sentient Edge platform generated initial revenues of $38 million since its commercial launch, meeting internal expectations for a first-year software product.
Veltrano signed 47 new enterprise customer contracts during fiscal year 2023, expanding its global customer base from 1,210 to 1,257 active accounts.
The company's inventory levels declined by 9% year-over-year, falling from $382 million to $348 million, as demand forecasting accuracy improved significantly.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 43 days at year-end compared to 49 days in the prior year, reflecting stronger collections processes.
Veltrano's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4%, slightly below the prior year rate of 23.1%, partly due to favorable international tax adjustments.
Net income attributable to Veltrano shareholders totaled $242 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.87 for fiscal year 2023.
This compared favorably to fiscal year 2022 net income of $199 million and diluted earnings per share of $3.16.
The company's return on invested capital reached 16.8% in fiscal 2023, surpassing its internal target of 15% and up from 14.3% in fiscal 2022.
Raymond Osei emphasized that the balance sheet remained conservatively structured, with total assets of $2.93 billion and total liabilities of $1.41 billion at year-end.
Shareholders' equity increased to $1.52 billion from $1.39 billion a year earlier, primarily due to retained earnings accumulation and modest share issuance.
The company's credit rating was reaffirmed at BBB+ by Standard Capital Ratings in November 2023, reflecting stable financial performance and consistent cash generation.
Veltrano operates a global network of 18 service and support centers, which Margaret Holloway highlighted as a key differentiator versus smaller competitors.
Customer satisfaction scores as measured by third-party surveys averaged 87 out of 100 across the company's major product lines in fiscal 2023.
The European segment benefited from strong demand in Germany and the Netherlands, with segment revenue growing 15.4% year-over-year in local currency terms.
Asia-Pacific segment revenues grew 9.7% in constant currency, supported by project wins in South Korea, Taiwan, and Australia.
The company launched a new apprenticeship and technical training program in fiscal 2023, enrolling 320 participants across its North American manufacturing facilities.
Environmental sustainability metrics showed continued progress, with total greenhouse gas emissions declining 7.2% on an absolute basis compared to fiscal 2022.
Water consumption per unit of production fell 11.4% year-over-year, supported by recycling investments made at the Toledo facility in mid-2022.
The company's Scope 3 emissions disclosure, published for the first time in its 2023 sustainability report, estimated downstream emissions at 1.2 million metric tons of CO2 equivalent.
Veltrano filed 34 new patent applications globally during fiscal year 2023, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 412 patents across 19 countries.
The company incurred restructuring charges of $14 million during the second half of fiscal 2023, related to the consolidation of two European distribution centers.
These restructuring activities are expected to yield annualized cost savings of approximately $19 million beginning in fiscal year 2024.
The Veltrano employee engagement survey conducted in Q4 2023 returned a participation rate of 81% and an overall engagement score of 74 out of 100.
Management acknowledged that workforce engagement in the Asia-Pacific segment lagged the global average, with targeted improvement programs underway for fiscal 2024.
The board approved an expansion of the share repurchase authorization by an additional $200 million in February 2024, following the completion of the prior program.
Veltrano's five-year strategic plan, titled 'Precision 2028,' targets annual revenue of $2.6 billion and an operating margin of 20% by the end of fiscal 2028.
Capital allocation priorities under Precision 2028 include organic growth investments, selective bolt-on acquisitions, and continued return of capital to shareholders.
In December 2023, Veltrano completed the acquisition of Nexpath Automation GmbH, a German motion-control components manufacturer, for €92 million in cash.
The Nexpath acquisition adds approximately 340 employees and is expected to contribute $55 million in annualized revenue beginning in fiscal year 2024.
Integration planning teams from both companies have been established, with full systems integration targeted for completion within 18 months of the acquisition close.
Veltrano's legal team resolved two legacy intellectual property disputes during fiscal 2023, resulting in net proceeds of $6 million after legal fees and settlements.
The company maintains product liability insurance coverage of up to $250 million per occurrence, which management considers adequate for its operational risk profile.
Veltrano's audit committee conducted its annual review of internal controls and concluded that no material weaknesses existed as of December 31, 2023.
External auditor Crane, Aldridge & Park LLP issued an unqualified audit opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
The company recognized a $12 million gain on the sale of a surplus real estate parcel in Cincinnati, Ohio, which had been held since Veltrano's 2017 facility consolidation.
Currency translation adjustments reduced reported revenues by approximately $31 million due to the strengthening of the U.S. dollar against key foreign currencies during the year.
Pension and other post-retirement benefit obligations totaled $188 million at year-end, down from $204 million in fiscal 2022 due to favorable actuarial assumptions.
The company's defined benefit pension plan was 96.4% funded as of December 31, 2023, which Raymond Osei described as a position of near-full funding.
Veltrano's forward guidance for fiscal year 2024 projects revenue in the range of $1.97 billion to $2.05 billion and diluted earnings per share of $4.10 to $4.35.
Management cited continued automation investment by industrial customers and new product pipeline strength as the primary drivers of projected growth.
Margaret Holloway closed the earnings call by reiterating the company's commitment to disciplined capital stewardship and long-term value creation for shareholders.
The full annual report, including detailed segment disclosures, risk factor discussions, and the complete audited financial statements, is available on Veltrano's investor relations website.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation's common stock trades on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VDNC.
Veltora Industries, Inc. is a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, serving industrial and agricultural markets across North America and Europe.
The company was founded in 1991 by Dr. Harriet Solano and has grown from a regional supplier into a diversified chemicals platform with operations in seven countries.
This annual report covers the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, and has been prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Feld presents the following overview of Veltora's financial performance, strategic initiatives, and outlook for the coming fiscal year.
Fiscal year 2023 was a defining period for Veltora Industries, marked by disciplined execution, portfolio rationalization, and meaningful progress on our sustainability agenda.
Total net revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 9.3% increase over the prior year's $1.68 billion.
Revenue growth was driven primarily by volume gains in our Agricultural Solutions segment, which posted a 14.2% year-over-year increase.
The Industrial Coatings segment delivered more modest growth of 4.8%, reflecting softer demand in the European construction market during the second half of the year.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $612 million, yielding a gross margin of 33.3%, an improvement of 110 basis points relative to fiscal 2022.
The margin expansion was attributable to favorable raw material costs, improved plant utilization rates, and a richer mix of higher-value specialty products.
Operating income reached $228 million, compared with $194 million in the prior year, reflecting both revenue growth and ongoing cost discipline.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $187 million, representing 10.2% of net revenue, down from 11.1% in fiscal 2022.
Research and development expenditures were $97 million, or approximately 5.3% of net revenue, consistent with the company's commitment to innovation-led growth.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $341 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 18.5%, up from 16.9% in the prior year.
Net income attributable to Veltora Industries shareholders was $154 million, compared with $121 million in fiscal 2022, a 27.3% increase.
Diluted earnings per share came in at $3.47, versus $2.73 in the prior year, reflecting both earnings growth and the impact of share repurchases.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares during the year at an average price of $41.60 per share, returning approximately $50 million to shareholders.
Free cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $198 million, up from $163 million in the prior year, driven by strong earnings conversion and working capital improvements.
Capital expenditures totaled $89 million, primarily directed toward capacity expansion at the Savannah, Georgia production facility and digitalization of manufacturing systems.
The Savannah expansion project is expected to add 120,000 metric tons of annual production capacity upon completion in the second quarter of 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Anantharaman noted that working capital efficiency improved significantly, with days sales outstanding declining from 48 to 41 days.
Inventory days on hand decreased from 62 to 55 days, reflecting tighter supply chain management and improved demand forecasting capabilities.
The company's balance sheet remained robust throughout the year, supporting ongoing investment in organic growth and selective bolt-on acquisitions.
Total assets at year-end stood at $2.31 billion, up from $2.14 billion at the end of fiscal 2022.
Current assets were $687 million, including cash and cash equivalents of $214 million, up from $178 million at the prior year-end.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltora Industries carried no outstanding balance on its revolving credit facility and had fully repaid its senior notes during the third quarter.
Total shareholders' equity grew to $1.02 billion from $894 million at the end of fiscal 2022, driven primarily by retained earnings.
The Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share, payable in March 2024, representing an annualized yield of approximately 2.1% based on the year-end share price.
This marks the eighth consecutive year in which Veltora has increased its quarterly dividend, underscoring the board's confidence in the company's long-term cash generation.
The Agricultural Solutions segment generated segment revenue of $874 million and segment operating income of $141 million, for a segment margin of 16.1%.
Key growth drivers in Agricultural Solutions included strong demand for the BioGuard fungicide line and expanded distribution partnerships in Brazil and Argentina.
The company launched three new crop protection products during the year, all of which achieved regulatory approval ahead of schedule.
The Industrial Coatings segment recorded revenue of $966 million and segment operating income of $112 million, for a segment margin of 11.6%.
Despite a challenging European environment, the segment benefited from strong demand in North American infrastructure and automotive refinish markets.
Veltora's specialty polymer product line, introduced in late 2022, gained meaningful traction in fiscal 2023, contributing $78 million in revenue.
The company's geographic diversification strategy continued to bear fruit, with international revenues accounting for 38% of total net revenue, up from 34% in the prior year.
Latin American revenues grew 22% year-over-year, driven by agricultural market penetration and favorable currency translation.
European revenues declined 3% on a reported basis but were essentially flat on a constant-currency basis, reflecting the strength of the U.S. dollar against the euro.
Asian-Pacific revenues grew 11%, led by demand for industrial coatings in the Vietnamese and Thai manufacturing sectors.
The company employed approximately 6,800 full-time employees globally as of December 31, 2023, unchanged from the headcount reported at the end of fiscal 2022.

Veltora Industries exited fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having completed all scheduled repayments and retiring the remaining $175 million in senior notes ahead of maturity.
Chief Human Resources Officer Danielle Kroeger highlighted that voluntary employee turnover declined to 8.4% in 2023, the lowest rate in the company's history.
The company invested $14 million in employee training and development programs, up from $11 million in the prior year.
Veltora's environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced considerably, with scope 1 and scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions declining 7% on an intensity basis.
The company achieved a recordable incident rate of 0.41 per 100 workers, a 12% improvement from the prior year and well below the industry average.
Veltora's sustainability report, published in March 2024, outlines the company's pathway to a 50% reduction in absolute greenhouse gas emissions by 2035.
In February 2024, the company completed the acquisition of Ternex Additives Corp., a specialty additives manufacturer based in Antwerp, Belgium, for a purchase price of $210 million.
The Ternex acquisition adds proprietary additive technologies to Veltora's Industrial Coatings portfolio and is expected to be accretive to adjusted EPS within the first full year of ownership.
To fund the acquisition, Veltora utilized a combination of cash on hand and proceeds from a newly arranged financing structure put in place in January 2024.
Integration planning for Ternex is well underway, with a dedicated team of 45 employees working across functional areas to capture targeted synergies of $18 million by year three.
In January 2024, Veltora entered into a five-year, $300 million term loan agreement with a syndicate of lenders to support its capital allocation strategy and fund strategic acquisitions.
Management's fiscal year 2024 guidance calls for net revenue in the range of $1.95 billion to $2.02 billion, representing growth of 6% to 10% over fiscal 2023.
Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be in the range of $370 million to $390 million, reflecting continued margin improvement and a partial-year contribution from Ternex.
Diluted earnings per share are expected to be in the range of $3.70 to $3.95 for fiscal 2024, inclusive of approximately $0.10 per share of acquisition-related amortization.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2024 are expected to be in the range of $95 million to $105 million, reflecting the completion of the Savannah expansion and new digital infrastructure investments.
The company expects free cash flow conversion to remain strong, with free cash flow as a percentage of adjusted EBITDA targeted at approximately 55% to 60%.
The Audit Committee of the Board of Directors has reviewed and approved all financial statements contained herein, and the independent auditor, Marwick & Crane LLP, has issued an unqualified opinion.

Veltora Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation and robotics manufacturer headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holt opened the earnings call by describing fiscal 2023 as a transformative year marked by disciplined capital allocation and strong operational execution.
The company reported total revenue of $1.84 billion for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the prior year.
Gross margin expanded by 180 basis points to 38.7%, driven largely by favorable product mix shifts toward higher-margin automation software subscriptions.
Veltora Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet at the close of fiscal year 2023, having retired the last tranche of its senior notes in February of that year.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Reyes highlighted the company's improved free cash flow conversion, which reached 94% of adjusted net income during the fiscal year.
Operating income for the period came in at $312 million, up from $268 million in fiscal 2022, reflecting both revenue growth and tight cost management.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses as a percentage of revenue declined to 14.2% from 15.8% in the prior fiscal year.
Research and development spending increased in absolute terms to $198 million, underscoring the company's commitment to next-generation robotics platforms.
Veltora's headcount stood at approximately 8,400 full-time employees at year-end, an increase of roughly 600 employees compared to December 31, 2022.
The company's flagship product line, the VX-9 series of industrial manipulators, generated $620 million in revenue, accounting for 33.7% of total company sales.
International revenue grew 18.6% year-over-year, with particularly strong demand from manufacturing customers in Germany, South Korea, and Mexico.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, representing a 10% increase from the prior quarterly dividend of $0.20 per share.
Inventory levels were deliberately reduced by $47 million compared to year-end 2022, reflecting improved supply chain predictability and leaner production scheduling.
During the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023, the company entered into a $350 million revolving credit agreement with a syndicate of six financial institutions to support strategic growth initiatives.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.1%, slightly below management's initial guidance range of 22.5% to 23.5%.
Adjusted earnings per diluted share of $4.87 exceeded the consensus analyst estimate of $4.71, representing a beat of $0.16.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $134 million, with the largest single investment being a $52 million expansion of the company's Columbus manufacturing facility.
Margaret Holt noted that the Columbus facility expansion is expected to increase production capacity by approximately 30% once fully operational in mid-2025.
Backlog at December 31, 2023, stood at $2.1 billion, a record high for the company and a 21% improvement over the $1.74 billion backlog at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company repurchased 3.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $61.40 per share, for a total outlay of approximately $197 million.
Veltora's net cash position, defined as cash and cash equivalents minus short-term borrowings, was $483 million at fiscal year-end.
Daniel Reyes stated that working capital management was a key focus in 2023, with days sales outstanding improving from 54 days to 49 days.
The company's return on invested capital reached 16.8% in fiscal 2023, compared to 14.2% in fiscal 2022, reflecting improved asset utilization.
Veltora Dynamics launched three new product families during fiscal 2023, including the ProLine Vision suite of machine-vision software tools in the second quarter.
The second major launch was the ArcFlex 200 series of collaborative robots, introduced at the Hannover Messe trade fair in April 2023.
The third product launch, the ThermalScan industrial inspection platform, was released commercially in October 2023 and received an industry innovation award in November.
North American revenue remained the largest geographic segment, contributing $1.09 billion or approximately 59% of total consolidated revenue.
The company's automation software subscription revenue grew 34% year-over-year to $214 million, now representing over 11% of total revenue.
Customer retention rates for multi-year software maintenance contracts averaged 91% during fiscal 2023, an improvement from 87% in fiscal 2022.
Veltora Dynamics maintained its investment-grade credit ratings from both Moody's and Standard & Poor's throughout the fiscal year.
The company achieved a net promoter score of 68 among its top 200 enterprise customers, up from 61 in the prior year's survey.

Margaret Holt expressed confidence that the company's diversified end-market exposure, spanning automotive, semiconductor, food and beverage, and logistics sectors, would provide resilience in a slowing global economy.
Automotive sector customers accounted for 28% of total revenue, down from 31% in fiscal 2022, as semiconductor and logistics customers grew their share.
The semiconductor vertical was the fastest-growing end market, with revenue up 41% to $221 million in fiscal 2023.
Logistics automation revenue grew 27% to $186 million, driven by continued investments by large e-commerce fulfillment operators.
Daniel Reyes indicated that the company would continue to evaluate bolt-on acquisition opportunities in the $50 million to $200 million enterprise value range.
Veltora Dynamics did not complete any acquisitions in fiscal 2023 but did execute two small technology licensing agreements with academic research institutions.
The company's gross research and development pipeline includes more than 40 active projects, with 11 anticipated to reach commercialization within the next 24 months.
Depreciation and amortization expense for fiscal 2023 totaled $89 million, with the majority attributable to manufacturing equipment and capitalized software development costs.
The company's five-year compound annual revenue growth rate stands at 9.7%, reflecting sustained organic expansion supplemented by modest inorganic contributions.
Pension and post-retirement benefit obligations were fully funded as of December 31, 2023, with a surplus of $22 million on an actuarial basis.
Veltora Dynamics disclosed no material environmental liabilities in its fiscal 2023 10-K filing, following the successful remediation of a legacy site in Cleveland.
The company's insurance coverage was renewed in January 2024 at terms broadly consistent with the prior policy period, with no material changes to coverage limits.
Margaret Holt reiterated the company's long-term target of achieving adjusted EBITDA margins in the range of 24% to 26%, up from the current 22.4%.
For fiscal 2024, management issued initial revenue guidance of $2.02 billion to $2.10 billion, implying organic growth of approximately 10% to 14% at the midpoint.
Adjusted earnings per diluted share guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at a range of $5.20 to $5.50, reflecting continued operating leverage and share count reduction.
The company indicated it expects to continue its quarterly dividend program and anticipates at least one additional dividend increase during fiscal 2024.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2024 are projected in the range of $155 million to $175 million, reflecting elevated investment in manufacturing automation and IT infrastructure.
Daniel Reyes noted that the 2024 tax rate is expected to remain in the range of 21.5% to 22.5%, consistent with existing statutory rates in Veltora's principal jurisdictions.
Headcount is expected to grow modestly in fiscal 2024, with net hiring concentrated in engineering and customer success roles across North America and Europe.
The company's executive compensation structure for fiscal 2023 tied 65% of short-term incentive payouts to revenue growth and adjusted operating income metrics.
Veltora Dynamics' audit committee engaged its external auditor, Grant Whitmore LLP, to perform an integrated audit of financial statements and internal controls over financial reporting.
The integrated audit resulted in an unqualified opinion, with no material weaknesses or significant deficiencies identified in the company's internal control environment.
The company's board of directors currently consists of ten members, eight of whom are classified as independent under NASDAQ listing standards.
Margaret Holt and Daniel Reyes are the two non-independent board members, serving in executive capacities as Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer, respectively.
The board's audit committee, compensation committee, and nominating and governance committee are each composed entirely of independent directors.

Veltora Dynamics' annual stockholders' meeting is scheduled for May 22, 2024, at which shareholders will vote on director elections, executive compensation, and the appointment of the external auditor.
The company expressed gratitude to its approximately 8,400 employees worldwide for their contributions to a record-setting fiscal year.
Looking further ahead, Veltora Dynamics remains focused on expanding its addressable market through technology differentiation, geographic diversification, and selective strategic partnerships.
Management concluded the prepared remarks portion of the earnings call by reaffirming its commitment to delivering long-term shareholder value through sustainable, profitable growth.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation, a mid-cap medical technology firm headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, released its annual report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, founded in 2009, specializes in the development and commercialization of point-of-care diagnostic instruments and proprietary reagent consumables.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Maren Solberg opened the shareholder letter by highlighting what she described as a transformative year for the organization.
Veltara serves hospitals, outpatient clinics, and independent laboratories across 34 countries, with particularly strong penetration in North American and Western European markets.
The company's flagship product line, the VeltraPath 900 series, accounted for approximately 61 percent of total instrument revenue during the reporting period.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing a 14.2 percent increase compared to the prior year's $426.7 million.
Reagent and consumable revenue grew at a faster pace than instrument sales, climbing 18.7 percent year-over-year to reach $214.6 million.
Chief Financial Officer Harold Fenwick noted that the reagent attach rate improved meaningfully as the installed base of VeltraPath instruments expanded.
Gross profit for the full year was reported at $261.4 million, yielding a consolidated gross margin of 53.6 percent.
This represents a 120-basis-point improvement in gross margin compared to fiscal year 2022, driven primarily by favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $198.7 million for the year, reflecting the company's continued investment in commercial infrastructure.
Research and development spending increased by 9.3 percent to $74.2 million, as the company advanced three next-generation diagnostic platforms toward regulatory submission.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $124.5 million, up from $109.8 million in the prior year, largely due to the expansion of the direct sales force in Europe.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $62.7 million, compared to $41.3 million in fiscal 2022, reflecting strong operating leverage on higher revenue.
The operating margin improved to 12.9 percent, a meaningful step toward the company's stated long-term target of 18 percent.
Interest expense for the period was $6.4 million, unchanged from the prior year, reflecting stable financing costs on the company's existing long-term debt.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP metric used internally for performance evaluation, was $89.1 million, compared to $68.4 million in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $43.2 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $1.87.
This compares favorably to net income of $27.9 million, or $1.21 per diluted share, in the prior fiscal year.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4 percent, slightly below the 23.1 percent rate recorded in fiscal 2022.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $112.8 million, providing the company with meaningful liquidity to support near-term operational needs.
Free cash flow generation for the year was $54.6 million, reflecting strong earnings conversion and disciplined capital expenditure management.
Capital expenditures totaled $16.3 million, directed primarily toward manufacturing capacity expansion at the company's Durham, North Carolina facility.
The balance sheet remained in solid condition, with total assets of $634.7 million and total liabilities of $289.2 million at December 31, 2023.
Total stockholders' equity was $345.5 million, up from $302.1 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
During fiscal 2023, Veltara repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock under its authorized buyback program at an average price of $31.40 per share.
The Board of Directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.08 per share, consistent with the prior year's dividend level.
Dr. Solberg emphasized in her letter that geographic diversification remained a strategic priority for the coming fiscal year.
The Asia-Pacific region delivered exceptional performance, with revenue growing 31.4 percent year-over-year to $68.9 million.
Veltara's commercial team in Japan secured a preferred supplier agreement with a major hospital network, expected to contribute incremental revenue beginning in the first quarter of 2024.
In contrast, Latin American revenue declined modestly by 3.1 percent due to currency headwinds and delayed procurement decisions by government health agencies.
The company's North American segment remained the largest contributor to total revenue, generating $263.5 million, or approximately 54 percent of the consolidated total.
European revenue grew by 11.8 percent to $122.4 million, supported by the newly expanded direct sales organization in Germany, France, and the United Kingdom.
Headcount at year-end was 2,847 full-time employees globally, an increase of 312 employees compared to December 31, 2022.
The increase in headcount was concentrated in the commercial and field service functions, reflecting the company's commitment to improving customer support response times.
Mr. Fenwick noted that employee-related costs represented the single largest component of operating expenses at approximately 48 percent of total operating costs.
Veltara exited fiscal year 2023 with no outstanding borrowings on its $150 million revolving credit facility, reflecting the strength of its cash generation.

The company's inventory balance increased to $87.4 million at year-end, up from $71.9 million at the end of fiscal 2022, as management built safety stock ahead of anticipated supply chain disruptions.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 52 days from 58 days in the prior year, a testament to enhanced collections processes implemented in the second quarter.
The Board's audit committee conducted a review of the company's internal controls and found no material weaknesses during the fiscal year.
Veltara received 510(k) clearance from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for its VeltraPath 920 instrument in August 2023, marking a significant regulatory milestone.
The VeltraPath 920 is designed to deliver results for a broad panel of infectious disease markers within 18 minutes, compared to 35 minutes for the prior-generation platform.
Commercial launch of the VeltraPath 920 in the United States commenced in October 2023, with initial placements concentrated in large academic medical centers.
As of December 31, 2023, the company had placed 187 VeltraPath 920 instruments with customer sites across the United States and Canada.
Dr. Solberg projected that the VeltraPath 920 installed base would reach approximately 800 units by the end of fiscal 2024, subject to market adoption rates.
The company's intellectual property portfolio expanded during the year, with 23 new patents granted across multiple jurisdictions.
Veltara's quality management system maintained ISO 13485 certification, a prerequisite for regulatory approvals in numerous international markets.
In September 2023, Veltara drew down $75 million from a term loan facility it secured from a syndicate of commercial banks to fund strategic investments.
The company maintained a long-term debt-to-equity ratio of 0.41 at December 31, 2023, which management considered conservative relative to industry peers.
Mr. Fenwick reaffirmed the company's commitment to maintaining an investment-grade credit profile over the medium term.
Veltara's three-year strategic plan, introduced at the November 2023 investor day, targets compound annual revenue growth of 12 to 15 percent through fiscal 2026.
