The plan also calls for operating margin expansion to at least 16 percent by the end of fiscal 2025, a milestone management believes is achievable given current revenue trajectory.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued initial revenue guidance of $545 million to $560 million, implying growth of approximately 12 to 15 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $102 million to $108 million, reflecting continued investment in both commercial and research functions.
The Board of Directors approved an increase in the share repurchase authorization to $75 million for fiscal 2024, up from $37.7 million utilized in fiscal 2023.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives continued to gain prominence within the corporate strategy during the reporting period.
Veltara reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 8 percent year-over-year, driven by energy efficiency upgrades at its manufacturing facility.
The company's workforce diversity metrics showed improvement, with women comprising 44 percent of senior leadership roles as of December 31, 2023, up from 39 percent in the prior year.
The audit firm Hendricks & Vance LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
No material litigation or regulatory inquiries were pending that management believed would have a material adverse effect on the company's financial condition.
Dr. Solberg concluded her letter by expressing confidence in the company's ability to execute on its strategic priorities and deliver sustained value to shareholders.
The annual report was filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Form 10-K on February 28, 2024.
Veltara's common stock is listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLTR.
The company will host its fourth-quarter and full-year 2023 earnings conference call on March 5, 2024, at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time.
Management remains focused on disciplined capital allocation, operational excellence, and continued innovation as the cornerstones of long-term value creation.

Veltora Systems Corporation, a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure provider headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, founded in 2009, specializes in distributed computing platforms for the logistics and supply chain sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Delroy opened the report with a statement emphasizing transformational growth across all core business segments.
Veltora Systems reported total consolidated revenues of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.2 percent increase over the prior year.
Cloud subscription services remained the fastest-growing revenue stream, contributing $412.6 million, or approximately 48.7 percent of total revenues.
Professional services and implementation fees generated an additional $198.4 million, reflecting strong customer onboarding activity throughout the year.
Legacy on-premise software licensing contributed $236.3 million, a segment that management expects to gradually decline as customers migrate to cloud platforms.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Nambiar noted that gross margins improved by 210 basis points year-over-year, reaching 68.4 percent.
Operating expenses for the full year totaled $524.1 million, which includes research and development, sales and marketing, and general and administrative costs.
Research and development expenditures were $163.7 million, representing 19.3 percent of total revenues, reflecting the company's commitment to product innovation.
Sales and marketing expenses reached $217.9 million as Veltora aggressively expanded its go-to-market capacity in North America and Western Europe.
General and administrative expenses amounted to $142.5 million, driven largely by headcount additions in compliance and finance functions.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $54.7 million, compared to $31.2 million in the prior fiscal year.
Adjusted EBITDA, which excludes stock-based compensation, depreciation, and amortization, came in at $138.9 million.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $39.4 million, or $0.82 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to the net loss of $7.1 million reported in fiscal year 2022, marking a significant profitability inflection.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $311.6 million and short-term investments of $89.2 million.
Total assets on the balance sheet grew to $1.43 billion, up from $1.19 billion at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Accounts receivable stood at $154.2 million, with days sales outstanding improving to 66 days from 74 days in the prior period.
Deferred revenue, a key indicator of future contracted revenue, increased 22.1 percent to $278.5 million at year-end.
During fiscal 2023, Veltora completed the acquisition of Nexpath Analytics, a supply chain optimization software firm, for $124.0 million in cash.
The Nexpath integration is expected to be substantially complete by the second quarter of fiscal 2024, according to Chief Operating Officer Elena Vasquez.
Goodwill and acquired intangible assets increased by $96.7 million on the balance sheet as a direct result of the Nexpath transaction.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $47.3 million, predominantly invested in data center infrastructure and engineering lab equipment.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $101.4 million, a record for the company.
The company repurchased 1.8 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $31.40 per share.
Total share repurchases amounted to approximately $56.5 million, reducing the diluted share count to approximately 48.1 million shares.
The Board of Directors authorized an additional $150 million share repurchase program effective January 1, 2024.
Veltora Systems carries no preferred stock and has a single class of common equity outstanding.
Employee headcount at year-end stood at 4,312 full-time employees globally, up from 3,891 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The increase in headcount was concentrated in the engineering and customer success organizations, consistent with the company's growth priorities.
Veltora's international revenues grew to $201.4 million, representing 23.8 percent of total consolidated revenues.
The EMEA region was the fastest-growing geographic segment, posting 31.4 percent revenue growth year-over-year.
The company's annual recurring revenue, or ARR, reached $589.7 million as of December 31, 2023.
Net revenue retention rate for cloud subscription customers was reported at 117 percent, indicating strong upsell and expansion activity.
Customer count at year-end totaled 2,847 enterprise and mid-market clients, compared to 2,491 at the end of the prior year.
Veltora launched three major product updates during the year, including the VeltOS 9.0 platform featuring AI-driven route optimization capabilities.
The new platform release was cited by management as a key driver of the elevated net revenue retention and ARR growth.
Veltora Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a conservative capital structure maintained consistently since 2019.
Looking ahead, management provided initial fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $965 million to $985 million.
Adjusted EBITDA margin for fiscal 2024 is expected to expand by approximately 150 to 200 basis points relative to the fiscal 2023 level.
The guidance reflects anticipated contributions from the Nexpath acquisition, organic growth in cloud subscriptions, and continued international expansion.
CFO Priya Nambiar emphasized that the company's balance sheet strength positions it well for both organic and inorganic growth opportunities.
The company intends to maintain its disciplined approach to capital allocation, prioritizing high-return investments in product development and customer acquisition.
Veltora's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 18.7 percent, benefiting from research and development tax credits and favorable geographic income mix.
The audit committee engaged Deloitte & Touche as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for the fourth consecutive year.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the annual audit process.
The company maintained compliance with all covenants and obligations across its financial arrangements throughout fiscal 2023.
In February 2024, Veltora closed a $300 million senior secured term loan facility to fund its expanded acquisition pipeline and accelerate platform development.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Delroy expressed confidence in the company's competitive positioning, citing its proprietary AI capabilities as a durable differentiator.
The logistics software market is projected to grow at a compound annual growth rate of 11.3 percent through 2028, according to third-party research cited by management.
Veltora's addressable market within enterprise logistics platforms is estimated at approximately $14.7 billion globally.
The company faces competition from large enterprise software vendors including SAP, Oracle, and several well-funded private competitors.
Management believes Veltora's domain-specific AI models and deep integrations with carrier networks represent meaningful competitive barriers to entry.
The report noted that employee attrition remained below the software industry average, at approximately 11.4 percent annually.
Veltora Systems declared no cash dividends during fiscal 2023, consistent with its stated policy of retaining earnings to fund growth initiatives.

The full financial statements, including footnote disclosures and the independent auditor's report, are included in Veltora Systems' Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Veltronix Corporation is a mid-cap technology hardware manufacturer headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in industrial-grade sensor arrays and edge computing modules.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Holloway and CTO Daniel Precht, both veterans of the semiconductor industry.
Veltronix reported its fiscal year 2023 annual results on February 14, 2024, covering the twelve-month period ending December 31, 2023.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The company's North American segment contributed $512.6 million to total revenue, accounting for approximately 60.5% of the consolidated figure.
International operations, spanning Europe, Asia-Pacific, and Latin America, generated the remaining $334.7 million in revenue.
Gross profit for the year came in at $311.4 million, yielding a gross margin of 36.8%, up from 34.1% in fiscal year 2022.
CEO Margaret Holloway attributed the margin improvement primarily to favorable component pricing and a shift toward higher-margin software-integrated sensor products.
Operating expenses totaled $198.7 million for the fiscal year, which included research and development costs of $74.2 million.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses accounted for $124.5 million of the total operating expense base.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $112.7 million, reflecting an operating margin of 13.3%.
This marked the third consecutive year of double-digit operating margins for Veltronix, a milestone highlighted prominently in the shareholder letter.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $84.9 million, or $2.17 per diluted share.
The effective tax rate for the period was 24.8%, consistent with management's guidance of approximately 25%.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure used by management, reached $148.3 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 17.5%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203.1 million, up from $167.4 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company generated $131.6 million in cash from operating activities during fiscal year 2023.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $47.8 million, directed primarily toward expanding the Austin manufacturing facility and upgrading testing equipment.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $83.8 million for the year.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per common share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 1, 2024.
This represents the fifth consecutive year in which Veltronix has increased its annual dividend, reflecting confidence in the company's cash generation capacity.
Veltronix repurchased approximately 1.4 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $38.62 per share.
The total cost of share repurchases during the year amounted to $54.1 million, funded from the existing $100 million repurchase authorization.
As of December 31, 2023, the company had approximately 39.1 million diluted shares outstanding.
Accounts receivable totaled $142.7 million at year-end, with days sales outstanding of 61.5 days, compared to 67.2 days in the prior year.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $98.4 million, reflecting a deliberate build in strategic component safety stock ahead of anticipated demand in early 2024.
The supply chain team, led by Vice President of Operations Sandra Yee, implemented a dual-sourcing strategy for critical semiconductors in the second half of 2023.
Long-term debt as of December 31, 2023, consisted solely of a $150 million senior unsecured note issued in 2019, maturing in March 2026.
The company's net debt position was approximately negative $53.1 million, meaning net cash exceeded total financial debt.
Veltronix maintained a leverage ratio of 0.34x net debt to adjusted EBITDA, well within the covenant threshold of 2.5x.
By the close of fiscal year 2023, Veltronix had fully retired all outstanding long-term borrowings and carried a completely debt-free balance sheet.
Looking ahead, CFO Thomas Abernethy guided for fiscal year 2024 revenue in the range of $910 million to $940 million, implying 7.4% to 10.9% growth.
The guidance assumes continued strength in the industrial automation end market, where Veltronix holds an estimated 8.2% global market share.
Management expects gross margins to remain stable in the 36% to 38% range, supported by ongoing product mix improvements.
The company plans to increase research and development investment to approximately $85 million in fiscal year 2024, up from $74.2 million in the prior year.
New product development efforts are centered on the VX-9 series of multi-spectrum sensor modules, scheduled for commercial launch in the third quarter of 2024.
The VX-9 series is designed to address growing demand in autonomous vehicle infrastructure, smart agriculture, and industrial robotics applications.
Veltronix signed a multi-year supply agreement with German automation conglomerate Krauss-Mehler AG in November 2023, valued at approximately $120 million over three years.
The Krauss-Mehler contract is expected to contribute meaningfully to the European revenue segment beginning in the first quarter of fiscal year 2024.
Headcount at Veltronix grew to 3,847 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, up from 3,412 at the end of the prior year.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltronix completed a significant workforce restructuring program that reduced its total employee base to approximately 2,900 full-time staff.
The company's employee retention rate for fiscal year 2023 was 88.3%, which management considers above the industry average for hardware-focused technology firms.
Veltronix established a new regional sales office in Singapore in October 2023 to strengthen its presence across Southeast Asian markets.
The Asia-Pacific segment recorded revenue of $118.9 million in fiscal year 2023, a 21.3% year-over-year increase driven by demand in South Korea and Japan.
CTO Daniel Precht presented the company's five-year technology roadmap at the November 2023 Veltronix Investor Day event held in Austin.
The roadmap outlined a strategy to transition at least 40% of product revenue toward software and services by fiscal year 2028.
Veltronix also announced a partnership with cloud infrastructure provider Nimbus Systems to co-develop an edge-to-cloud data pipeline platform.
The Nimbus Systems partnership is structured as an equity co-investment arrangement, with Veltronix committing $18 million over a two-year development period.
Environmental, social, and governance disclosures for fiscal year 2023 indicated that Veltronix reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 11.7% year over year.
The company achieved a CDP Climate Score of B in 2023, an improvement from its prior year score of C.
Veltronix's manufacturing facility in Austin received ISO 14001 environmental management certification in September 2023.
The audit committee of the board, chaired by independent director Patricia Forsgren, oversaw an uneventful year-end external audit conducted by Thornton & Hale LLP.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year 2023 audit.
The company faces ongoing litigation related to a patent dispute with rival sensor manufacturer Optivance Technologies, filed in January 2022.
Management has accrued $6.5 million as a contingent liability related to the Optivance matter, which it believes represents a reasonable estimate of potential exposure.
Veltronix's common stock trades on the NASDAQ Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLTX.
During fiscal year 2023, VLTX shares ranged from a low of $31.18 to a high of $52.44, closing the year at $47.90.
The company's market capitalization as of December 31, 2023, was approximately $1.87 billion based on the closing share price.
Management remains committed to delivering long-term shareholder value through disciplined capital allocation, continued innovation, and sustainable revenue growth.

Heliodyne Materials Corporation, a specialty chemicals and advanced composites manufacturer headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
The company, founded in 1997, serves clients across aerospace, automotive, and industrial manufacturing sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Forsythe opened the earnings call by describing 2023 as a transformational year for Heliodyne.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $2.84 billion, representing an 11.3% increase over the prior fiscal year.
The aerospace segment contributed $1.21 billion in revenue, up 14.7% year-over-year, driven by strong commercial aircraft demand.
The automotive composites division generated $890 million in revenue, reflecting a modest 6.2% increase compared to 2022.
Industrial materials revenue came in at $740 million, growing 13.1% as infrastructure spending accelerated globally.
Gross profit for the full year was $812 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 28.6%.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Bellows noted that gross margin improvement was primarily attributable to better feedstock cost management.
Operating income totaled $394 million in fiscal year 2023, up from $331 million in 2022.
The operating margin expanded by approximately 60 basis points to reach 13.9% for the full year.
Net income attributable to Heliodyne shareholders was $271 million, compared to $228 million in the prior year.
Diluted earnings per share came in at $3.47, surpassing analyst consensus estimates of $3.31.
The company repurchased 2.1 million shares during the year at an average price of $58.40 per share.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $487 million, representing an EBITDA margin of 17.1%.
Capital expenditures totaled $193 million, primarily directed toward capacity expansion at the Raleigh and Phoenix manufacturing facilities.
Free cash flow for the year was $294 million, a 19% improvement over the $247 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $388 million, up from $315 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company's total long-term debt balance as of December 31, 2023, was $645 million.
Net debt at year-end was approximately $257 million, resulting in a net leverage ratio of 0.53 times trailing EBITDA.
Heliodyne's board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of January 15, 2024.
This dividend represents a 10% increase over the prior quarterly dividend of $0.20 per share.
The company also announced the completion of its Greenfield Phase II expansion project in Phoenix, which added 340,000 square feet of production space.
Chief Operating Officer Darryl Hutchinson credited the Phoenix expansion with reducing production lead times by an average of 18 days.
Heliodyne's workforce grew to 9,800 full-time employees globally by the end of fiscal year 2023, compared to 9,100 at the end of 2022.
Research and development expenditures totaled $112 million for the year, representing 3.9% of total revenue.
The company filed 47 new patents during 2023, its highest annual patent filing total since inception.
Heliodyne's aerospace segment secured a multi-year supply agreement with a major North American commercial aircraft manufacturer worth an estimated $600 million over five years.
The automotive composites division launched three new lightweight structural panels designed for electric vehicle platforms during the third quarter.
Market penetration in the electric vehicle supply chain increased from 8% to 14% of automotive segment revenue year-over-year.
The industrial materials segment expanded its geographic footprint by entering two new markets: Poland and Malaysia.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $274 million in 2023, compared to $258 million in 2022.
SG&A as a percentage of revenue improved slightly, declining from 10.1% to 9.6% year-over-year.
Heliodyne recorded a restructuring charge of $18 million in the second quarter related to the consolidation of two European distribution centers.
The consolidation is expected to generate annual cost savings of approximately $12 million beginning in 2024.
Inventory levels at year-end were $421 million, reflecting a days-inventory-outstanding of approximately 54 days.
Accounts receivable stood at $317 million at year-end, with a days-sales-outstanding metric of 41 days.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.4%, down from 24.1% in the prior year.
The lower effective tax rate was partially driven by the utilization of research and development tax credits in the United States.
Heliodyne's return on invested capital improved to 14.2% in 2023, up from 12.8% in 2022.
Ms. Forsythe highlighted the company's disciplined capital allocation strategy as a key driver of value creation for shareholders.
The company issued its inaugural sustainability report in 2023, outlining targets to reduce Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 40% by 2035.
Heliodyne reported a 7% reduction in carbon intensity per unit of production during fiscal year 2023.
The company's supply chain resilience initiatives, launched in early 2022, contributed meaningfully to on-time delivery rates exceeding 96%.
Looking ahead, Mr. Bellows provided fiscal year 2024 guidance, projecting total revenue in the range of $3.05 billion to $3.15 billion.
The company expects adjusted EBITDA for 2024 to fall between $520 million and $540 million.
Heliodyne ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of any short-term borrowings or revolving credit facility drawdowns, maintaining a fully undrawn credit position across all lending arrangements.
The board reaffirmed its commitment to maintaining an investment-grade credit rating, targeting a long-term net leverage ratio below 1.5 times.
Ms. Forsythe expressed confidence that the Phoenix and Raleigh capacity expansions would support margin accretion through improved fixed-cost absorption.
The company plans to increase R&D spending to approximately $130 million in fiscal year 2024, with a focus on next-generation thermoset resin systems.
Heliodyne's aerospace backlog grew to $1.8 billion as of December 31, 2023, providing strong revenue visibility through mid-2026.
The company intends to explore strategic bolt-on acquisitions in the specialty coatings space with a targeted deal size between $100 million and $300 million.
Mr. Hutchinson noted that headcount is expected to increase by approximately 400 employees during fiscal year 2024 to support expanded production.
Heliodyne's shares closed at $62.15 on the day of the earnings release, reflecting a 4.3% gain on the session.
The stock has outperformed its specialty chemicals peer group by approximately 12 percentage points on a total return basis over the trailing twelve months.
Credit rating agency Pinnacle Ratings affirmed Heliodyne's BBB+ issuer credit rating with a stable outlook following the earnings release.

During the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2023, Heliodyne drew down $175 million under its revolving credit facility to fund working capital requirements associated with accelerated production ramp-up activities.

Veltrano Systems Corporation, a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure provider headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company reported total consolidated revenues of $847.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway attributed the strong top-line performance to accelerated adoption of the company's flagship SaaS platform, Veltrano Apex.
The cloud services segment alone contributed $512.6 million to total revenues, making it the single largest revenue driver for the third consecutive year.
Professional services and implementation revenues reached $198.4 million, up 9.7% year-over-year.
Legacy on-premise licensing revenue continued its expected secular decline, falling to $136.3 million from $159.1 million in the prior period.
Gross profit for the fiscal year came in at $563.8 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 66.5%.
Operating expenses excluding cost of revenues totaled $421.7 million, driven primarily by increased research and development investments.
Research and development spending reached $187.4 million, representing 22.1% of total revenues, as the company accelerated its artificial intelligence roadmap.
Sales and marketing expenses were $154.2 million, reflecting targeted investments in enterprise customer acquisition campaigns across North America and Europe.
General and administrative expenses of $80.1 million remained well-controlled relative to the company's revenue scale.
Operating income for the year was $142.1 million, corresponding to an operating margin of 16.8%.
This compares favorably to the prior year's operating income of $108.6 million, representing an increase of approximately 30.8%.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Pryce noted that disciplined cost management and operating leverage in the cloud segment were key contributors to margin expansion.
Net income attributable to Veltrano common shareholders totaled $103.7 million, or $2.14 per diluted share.
This compares to net income of $79.2 million, or $1.64 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 26.4%, slightly higher than the 25.1% recorded in the prior year due to changes in jurisdictional earnings mix.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure used by management, was $198.6 million, representing a margin of 23.4%.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $164.3 million, up from $121.8 million in fiscal 2022.
The company ended fiscal 2023 with $389.2 million in cash and cash equivalents on its balance sheet.
Total assets stood at $1.24 billion as of December 31, 2023, compared to $1.09 billion at the close of the prior fiscal year.
Accounts receivable increased modestly to $134.6 million, with days sales outstanding of 57.9 days.
Deferred revenue, an important indicator of future recognized revenues, grew 18.3% to $276.4 million at year-end.
The company's total headcount stood at 4,812 employees globally as of December 31, 2023, up from 4,340 at the end of 2022.
Veltrano opened two new regional offices during the year, one in Dublin, Ireland, and one in Singapore, to support its international expansion strategy.
The international segment contributed 31.4% of total revenues in fiscal 2023, compared to 27.6% in fiscal 2022.
During the year, the company completed the acquisition of Norfeld Analytics, a data integration software firm, for a total consideration of $94.5 million.
The Norfeld acquisition added approximately 210 employees and expanded Veltrano's data pipeline capabilities.
Integration costs associated with the Norfeld transaction totaled $8.3 million and were recorded as one-time charges in the operating expense line.
The company repurchased 1.85 million shares of its common stock during fiscal 2023 under its ongoing buyback program, at an average price of $41.30 per share.
Total capital returned to shareholders through buybacks amounted to approximately $76.4 million for the full year.
Veltrano's Board of Directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share in each of the four quarters of fiscal 2023.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltrano carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, reflecting management's commitment to maintaining a conservative capital structure.
The company's net revenue retention rate among enterprise customers reached 118%, demonstrating strong upsell and cross-sell momentum within the existing customer base.
Customer count grew to 6,240 at fiscal year-end, representing net additions of 710 customers during the year.
Average contract value for new enterprise agreements signed in fiscal 2023 was $187,000, up 12.4% from the prior year.
President of Product Strategy, Annika Sørensen, highlighted the launch of three major platform updates during the year, including the Apex Intelligence module released in Q3.
The Apex Intelligence module leverages large language model technology to automate routine workflow configurations for enterprise clients.
Customer satisfaction scores, as measured by the company's annual Net Promoter Survey, improved to 67 from 61 in the prior year.
Veltrano received recognition from three industry analyst firms as a leader in the cloud enterprise resource management category.
The company's partner ecosystem expanded to include 340 certified implementation partners globally, up from 278 at the end of fiscal 2022.
Channel-sourced revenues accounted for 24.7% of total new bookings in fiscal 2023, compared to 19.3% in the prior year.
To fund its next phase of international expansion and product development, Veltrano secured a $300 million revolving credit facility from a syndicate of six financial institutions during fiscal 2023.
The weighted average remaining term of the company's customer subscription contracts was 2.7 years as of year-end.
Annual recurring revenue, or ARR, reached $634.8 million at December 31, 2023, representing year-over-year growth of 21.3%.
Management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $960 million to $985 million, implying growth of approximately 13% to 16%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $225 million to $240 million, reflecting continued investment in product and go-to-market initiatives.
Daniel Pryce emphasized that the company's strong cash position provides flexibility to pursue both organic and inorganic growth opportunities in the coming year.
The Board authorized an expansion of the share repurchase program by an additional $100 million, bringing total remaining authorization to approximately $123 million.
Veltrano's stock closed the fiscal year at $44.87 per share, representing a 32.1% total return including dividends for calendar year 2023.
The company's market capitalization at fiscal year-end was approximately $2.17 billion based on 48.4 million diluted shares outstanding.
Veltrano expects to host its annual investor day in March 2024, where management plans to provide detailed strategic updates across all business segments.
The audit committee of the Board of Directors confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its independent audit of the fiscal 2023 financial statements without material findings.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the criteria established by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations of the Treadway Commission.
The company disclosed no material weaknesses in its Sarbanes-Oxley Section 404 assessment for fiscal 2023.
Margaret Holloway concluded her letter to shareholders by reaffirming the company's long-term target of achieving $1.5 billion in annual revenue by fiscal 2027.
She also highlighted Veltrano's ongoing commitment to environmental, social, and governance principles, including a target to achieve carbon neutrality in its data center operations by 2026.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 11% year-over-year, consistent with its published sustainability roadmap.
Veltrano's employee engagement index, measured through an annual internal survey, reached 78% favorable responses, up from 74% in the prior year.
The Board of Directors, which comprises nine members including three independent directors added in 2023, is confident in management's execution of the company's multi-year growth strategy.
Harvestline Agricultural Technologies, Inc. reported its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023, marking a pivotal period in the company's operational history.
The company, headquartered in Des Moines, Iowa, specializes in precision agriculture software and smart irrigation systems for large-scale commercial farming operations.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Calloway opened the annual earnings call by describing fiscal 2023 as a year of disciplined expansion and measured capital deployment.
Total consolidated revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 14.2 percent increase over the prior fiscal year's $741.6 million.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Ostrowski attributed the revenue growth primarily to strong adoption of the company's flagship CropSync platform across the Midwest and Pacific Northwest regions.
Gross profit for the year came in at $412.5 million, corresponding to a gross margin of approximately 48.7 percent.
This represented a meaningful improvement from the 45.1 percent gross margin recorded in fiscal year 2022, reflecting favorable product mix and operational efficiencies.
Operating expenses, including research and development and selling, general and administrative costs, totaled $289.4 million for the year.
Research and development spending alone reached $98.7 million, underscoring the company's continued commitment to innovation in agricultural technology.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was reported at $123.1 million, up from $89.4 million in the prior year, representing a 37.7 percent year-over-year increase.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $94.6 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.31.
The company's adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $178.4 million, reflecting an adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 21.1 percent.
Harvestline's board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, payable on March 15, 2024, to shareholders of record as of February 28, 2024.
The company repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares during fiscal 2023 under its previously authorized $75 million share buyback program.
As of December 31, 2023, the total shares outstanding stood at approximately 40.9 million.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $213.7 million, providing the company with a strong liquidity position.
Total assets on the balance sheet stood at $1.14 billion, while total liabilities were recorded at $398.6 million.
The company's current ratio at fiscal year-end was 2.4, indicating a healthy capacity to meet near-term obligations.
Harvestline generated free cash flow of $141.2 million during fiscal 2023, compared to $102.8 million in the prior year.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $37.3 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's data center infrastructure and field sensor manufacturing capacity.
During fiscal 2023, Harvestline completed the full integration of AgriVision Technologies, which had been acquired in late fiscal 2022 for approximately $185 million.
At the close of fiscal year 2023, Harvestline carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired the last tranche of its senior notes in October 2023.
The CropSync platform, which now serves over 4,200 enterprise farming clients, remained the single largest revenue contributor, accounting for 61 percent of total revenues.
Subscription and recurring revenues grew to $389.4 million, representing 46 percent of total revenues and up from 38 percent in the prior fiscal year.
Hardware product revenues, including smart irrigation controllers and field sensor arrays, contributed $247.8 million to the top line.
Professional services and consulting revenues added $210.1 million, rounding out the company's three primary revenue streams.
Geographically, North America accounted for 72 percent of total revenues, while international markets contributed the remaining 28 percent.
International revenue grew at an accelerated pace of 23.4 percent year-over-year, driven by strong demand in Brazil, Australia, and Western Europe.
The company's workforce expanded to 3,847 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, up from 3,412 at the end of the prior fiscal year.
Harvestline opened two new regional offices during the year, one in São Paulo, Brazil, and another in Melbourne, Australia, to support its international growth strategy.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Patel noted that supply chain disruptions, which had weighed on hardware margins in fiscal 2022, had substantially normalized by mid-2023.
In the second quarter of fiscal 2023, Harvestline secured a $150 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of regional banks to support working capital and strategic acquisitions.

The company launched four new software modules under the CropSync umbrella during fiscal 2023, including predictive yield analytics, autonomous irrigation scheduling, carbon credit tracking, and livestock integration.
Customer retention rates on the CropSync platform reached 94.1 percent for the year, up from 91.8 percent in fiscal 2022.
The average contract value for enterprise CropSync clients increased to $112,000 annually, representing a 9.3 percent increase year-over-year.
Harvestline's net revenue retention rate across all subscription products was 118 percent for fiscal 2023, indicating strong upsell and cross-sell dynamics.
The company's sales and marketing expenses totaled $103.2 million, equivalent to 12.2 percent of total revenues.
General and administrative expenses were $87.5 million, reflecting investments in corporate governance infrastructure and global compliance capabilities.
Harvestline recorded a non-cash stock-based compensation charge of $22.1 million during the fiscal year, which was excluded from adjusted EBITDA calculations.
The effective income tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 23.4 percent, slightly above the prior year's 22.1 percent due to changes in the geographic mix of pretax earnings.
Interest income on the company's cash balances contributed $8.2 million to pre-tax income, benefiting from the elevated interest rate environment.
The company's inventory balance at year-end stood at $54.3 million, down from $71.8 million at the prior year-end, reflecting improved supply chain management and demand forecasting.
Accounts receivable net of allowances totaled $138.9 million at December 31, 2023, with days sales outstanding of approximately 60 days.
Margaret Calloway highlighted the successful rollout of Harvestline's carbon credit tracking module as a strategic differentiator in the market.
The module, launched in April 2023, had already onboarded over 600 farms representing more than 1.8 million acres by year-end.
Harvestline filed for and received two new patents related to machine learning-based soil moisture prediction during fiscal 2023, bringing its active patent portfolio to 47 patents.
The company's board of directors approved a new long-term incentive plan for senior executives, aligning compensation with three-year total shareholder return metrics.
Daniel Ostrowski confirmed that Harvestline had no material off-balance-sheet financing arrangements as of December 31, 2023.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $940 million to $970 million, implying growth of approximately 11 to 15 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set between $198 million and $210 million, reflecting expected margin expansion from operating leverage.
The company indicated it plans to increase research and development spending by approximately 12 percent in fiscal 2024 to accelerate the development of autonomous drone-based field monitoring.
Harvestline's audit committee, chaired by independent director Robert Vance, reviewed and approved all related-party transactions for the fiscal year without objection.
The independent auditor, Thorngate & Ellison LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on Harvestline's fiscal 2023 consolidated financial statements.
There were no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting identified during the audit period.
The company's stock price appreciated approximately 31 percent during fiscal 2023, outperforming the S&P MidCap 400 Index, which gained 17.4 percent over the same period.
Market capitalization at year-end was approximately $1.87 billion, based on the December 29, 2023 closing share price of $45.72.
Harvestline's investor relations team hosted three analyst days during the year, attracting participation from over 85 institutional investors.
Management reiterated its long-term financial targets of achieving $1.5 billion in annual revenues and a 25 percent adjusted EBITDA margin by fiscal year 2027.
Sandra Patel closed the earnings call by crediting the company's 3,847 employees for their dedication and execution throughout a demanding but rewarding fiscal year.
Harvestline Agricultural Technologies remains focused on its mission of enabling sustainable, data-driven agriculture at scale across both domestic and international markets.
The company's next quarterly earnings release is scheduled for May 8, 2024, covering results for the first quarter of fiscal year 2024.
Caldwell Precision Instruments, Inc. reported its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023, marking another significant chapter in the company's three-decade history.
The company, headquartered in Durham, North Carolina, designs and manufactures high-precision measurement equipment for the aerospace and semiconductor industries.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the annual earnings call by expressing confidence in the company's long-term strategic positioning.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year's $753.6 million.
Organic revenue growth, excluding the effects of the MicroSense acquisition completed in Q2, stood at a solid 8.1%.
The semiconductor segment contributed $412.6 million in revenue, accounting for approximately 48.7% of total company sales.
Aerospace segment revenue grew to $287.4 million, benefiting from renewed defense procurement cycles across North America and Europe.
The company's industrial calibration division posted $147.3 million in revenue, a modest 3.2% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Gross profit for the year totaled $381.2 million, yielding a gross margin of 45.0%, up 90 basis points from 44.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Osei attributed the margin improvement primarily to favorable product mix shifts and ongoing manufacturing efficiency programs.
Operating expenses, including research and development, selling, and general and administrative costs, totaled $218.7 million during fiscal year 2023.
Research and development spending increased to $94.5 million, representing 11.2% of total revenue and reflecting the company's commitment to innovation.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $124.2 million, essentially flat as a percentage of revenue compared to fiscal year 2022.
Operating income for the year was $162.5 million, up from $131.8 million in the prior year, a 23.3% year-over-year improvement.
The operating margin expanded to 19.2%, compared to 17.5% in fiscal year 2022, marking the fourth consecutive year of operating margin expansion.
Net interest expense totaled $14.3 million for the year, reflecting the debt obligations incurred during the MicroSense acquisition financing.
Pre-tax income was $148.2 million, and the effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.7%, slightly above the prior year's 21.9%.
Net income attributable to Caldwell Precision Instruments shareholders was $114.6 million for the full fiscal year.
Diluted earnings per share came in at $4.87, exceeding the consensus analyst estimate of $4.72 by fifteen cents.
The company repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $61.40 per share.
Total capital returned to shareholders, including dividends and buybacks, amounted to $112.4 million during the fiscal year.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.32 per share, representing a 10.3% increase over the prior year's quarterly dividend.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $201.8 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 23.8%.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $138.5 million, with a free cash flow conversion rate of approximately 121% relative to net income.
Capital expenditures totaled $28.3 million during the year, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the Raleigh facility.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 with a cash and cash equivalents balance of $203.7 million.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 52 days in fiscal year 2022, reflecting enhanced collection processes.
Inventory turnover improved marginally to 4.8 times in fiscal year 2023, compared to 4.5 times in the prior year.
The MicroSense acquisition, completed in May 2023, added approximately $58.7 million in revenue from the acquisition date through year-end.
Integration costs associated with MicroSense totaled $11.3 million and were classified as non-recurring items in the fiscal year 2023 results.
As of December 31, 2023, Caldwell Precision Instruments carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired all outstanding obligations during the fiscal year.
The company's backlog at year-end stood at $634.2 million, a 17.8% increase versus the year-end 2022 backlog of $538.5 million.
Book-to-bill ratio for fiscal year 2023 was 1.18, indicating continued strong demand exceeding current shipment levels.
The semiconductor segment's backlog alone represented $318.6 million, reflecting robust demand from advanced chip fabrication customers.
Margaret Holloway noted that the company had been awarded a multi-year supply agreement with a leading North American aerospace prime contractor.
The contract, valued at approximately $175 million over five years, is expected to begin generating revenue in the first quarter of fiscal year 2024.
Headcount at Caldwell Precision Instruments grew to 4,312 employees at year-end, up from 3,847 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in engineering and manufacturing roles, consistent with the company's growth trajectory.
Employee-related costs, including salaries, benefits, and stock-based compensation, totaled $267.4 million, representing the largest single component of operating expenses.
The company opened a new regional service center in Singapore during Q3 2023 to better serve Asia-Pacific customers.

In February 2024, Caldwell Precision Instruments secured a $320 million syndicated revolving credit facility to support strategic growth and working capital requirements, with borrowings outstanding on the facility at closing totaling $185 million.
Return on invested capital for fiscal year 2023 was 18.4%, an improvement of 210 basis points relative to the prior year.
The company's net promoter score among key accounts improved to 67 in fiscal year 2023, up from 59 in fiscal year 2022.
Daniel Osei stated that the company's balance sheet remained a significant competitive advantage, providing financial flexibility for future initiatives.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $945 million, representing growth of approximately 8.6% to 11.5%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $218 million to $228 million, implying continued margin expansion.
Diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal year 2024 was established in the range of $5.20 to $5.45.
The company reiterated its long-term financial targets, including reaching $1.2 billion in annual revenue by fiscal year 2026.
Management emphasized that achieving the long-term revenue target would require a combination of organic growth and targeted bolt-on acquisitions.

The company identified three key strategic priorities for fiscal year 2024: expanding its semiconductor product portfolio, scaling the Asia-Pacific service infrastructure, and optimizing manufacturing cost structures.
Caldwell Precision Instruments also announced the planned retirement of Chief Operating Officer Thomas Barrington, effective March 31, 2024.
Sylvia Nakamura, currently serving as President of the Semiconductor Division, was named as Barrington's successor, effective April 1, 2024.
The board of directors expressed unanimous confidence in the management transition and in the company's strategic direction.
Margaret Holloway closed the earnings call by thanking employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued support and commitment.
Caldwell Precision Instruments' shares closed at $68.42 on the day of the earnings release, representing a 4.7% gain on above-average trading volume.
Meridian Biopharma Solutions, Inc. released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, highlighting significant operational milestones.
The company, headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, operates in the specialty pharmaceuticals and contract drug manufacturing sector.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Vanessa Holloway expressed confidence in the company's long-term strategic direction during the earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Meridian Biopharma reported total revenues of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
The growth was driven primarily by strong performance in the company's core oncology product line, which generated $412.6 million in net sales.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delacroix noted that gross margins expanded by 180 basis points to reach 61.2% for the full year.
Operating expenses totaled $389.4 million, which included $124.7 million in research and development expenditures.
Selling, general, and administrative costs came in at $198.3 million, slightly below internal projections.
The company reported operating income of $129.2 million, a 22.8% improvement compared to fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $97.6 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.14.
Meridian Biopharma ended the fiscal year with $284.1 million in cash and cash equivalents on its balance sheet.
The company's total assets stood at $1.63 billion, up from $1.47 billion in the prior fiscal year.
Accounts receivable decreased by 8.2% year-over-year to $143.7 million, reflecting improved collection practices.
Inventory levels rose modestly to $167.9 million, partly in anticipation of new product launches scheduled for early 2024.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 amounted to $78.4 million, predominantly directed toward expanding the Durham manufacturing facility.
The expansion project added approximately 45,000 square feet of GMP-compliant production space to the existing campus.
Dr. Holloway noted that the Durham facility upgrade positions the company to handle anticipated volume increases for the next three to five years.
Free cash flow for the year was $205.7 million, representing the strongest annual free cash flow generation in the company's history.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, consistent with the prior year's dividend level.
Meridian Biopharma repurchased 2.1 million shares of common stock during the year at an average price of $38.60 per share.
The share repurchase program reduced the total outstanding share count from approximately 47.3 million to 45.2 million shares.
The company's long-term debt balance was $310.0 million at year end, comprising two senior unsecured notes tranches.
The first tranche of $185.0 million carries a fixed interest rate of 4.25% and matures in 2027.
The second tranche of $125.0 million carries a fixed interest rate of 5.10% and matures in 2030.
Interest expense for the year totaled $15.4 million, consistent with the prior year given the unchanged debt structure.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 26.3%, slightly higher than the 25.8% recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Segment reporting revealed that the Oncology division contributed 48.7% of total revenue, while the Immunology division contributed 31.4%.
The remaining 19.9% of revenue was generated by the Contract Manufacturing Services segment, which serves third-party clients.
Meridian Biopharma closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of any revolving credit facility obligations, with no outstanding borrowings under any credit line.
The oncology pipeline advanced meaningfully during 2023, with three product candidates progressing to Phase 2 clinical trials.
MBS-401, a targeted therapy for non-small cell lung cancer, received Fast Track Designation from the FDA in March 2023.
MBS-517, the company's lead immunology candidate, completed Phase 1 dosing with a favorable safety profile reported in October 2023.
Dr. Holloway described the clinical progress as 'the most consequential series of milestones in Meridian's ten-year history.'
The company added 312 full-time employees during the fiscal year, bringing the total global headcount to 3,847 as of December 31, 2023.
Approximately 38% of the workforce is engaged in research, development, or clinical operations roles.
Geographic diversification continued to be a priority, with the European commercial operations expanding into four new markets: Germany, France, Italy, and Spain.
The European segment generated $89.4 million in revenue for fiscal year 2023, a 34.1% increase versus the prior year.
Management attributed the European growth to strong uptake of Meridian's flagship oncology product, Tarvecil, in newly entered markets.
In order to support accelerating growth initiatives, the company established a $250 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of eight commercial banks in November 2023.
The new credit facility carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points and has a five-year maturity.
Mr. Delacroix stated that the facility provides Meridian with the financial flexibility to pursue opportunistic bolt-on acquisitions.
The company evaluated three acquisition targets during the year but ultimately did not complete any transactions.
Return on invested capital for fiscal year 2023 was 11.7%, exceeding the company's internal hurdle rate of 9.5%.
Meridian Biopharma's stock price appreciated 28.3% during calendar year 2023, outperforming the S&P 500 Pharmaceuticals Index by approximately 14 percentage points.
The board authorized an extension of the share repurchase program by an additional $100 million for fiscal year 2024.
Dr. Holloway reiterated the company's commitment to returning capital to shareholders through a balanced approach of dividends and buybacks.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $910 million to $940 million.
The midpoint of that guidance implies approximately 10% top-line growth relative to the 2023 reported figure.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance was provided in the range of $230 million to $250 million for fiscal year 2024.
The company expects research and development spending to increase to between $135 million and $145 million in fiscal year 2024.
One key investment priority is the planned Phase 3 initiation of MBS-401 in the second quarter of 2024, pending regulatory feedback.
The immunology pipeline is expected to yield a Phase 2 readout for MBS-517 by the third quarter of 2024.
Meridian Biopharma also announced a collaboration agreement with Nexagen Therapeutics to co-develop a novel bispecific antibody platform.
The collaboration involves an upfront payment of $22 million to Nexagen, with milestone payments potentially reaching $175 million.
Mr. Delacroix confirmed that the collaboration is structured to preserve Meridian's investment-grade credit rating.
The company's audit committee concluded that no material weaknesses existed in internal controls over financial reporting for fiscal year 2023.
Independent auditors at Stanton & Rhys LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed in 2023, including a 14% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions.
The company was recognized on the Dow Jones Sustainability Index for North America for the second consecutive year.
Management expressed confidence that Meridian Biopharma remains well-positioned for sustainable, long-term value creation across all business segments.
The full audited financial statements, including notes, are available in the company's Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2024.
Veltoran Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced industrial automation solutions, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The report was presented by Chief Executive Officer Margaret Ashford and Chief Financial Officer Daniel Cho at the company's headquarters in Columbus, Ohio.
Veltoran Dynamics operates across three primary business segments: Precision Manufacturing Systems, Robotics Integration, and Digital Process Control.
The company was founded in 1994 and has grown to serve clients in over 40 countries across North America, Europe, and the Asia-Pacific region.
Fiscal year 2023 marked a significant milestone for the company as it celebrated its 30th year of continuous operations.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $2.84 billion, representing a year-over-year increase of 11.3 percent compared to the prior year.
The Precision Manufacturing Systems segment contributed $1.12 billion to total revenue, driven by strong demand from the automotive and aerospace industries.
The Robotics Integration segment generated $980 million in revenue, reflecting a 14.7 percent growth rate fueled by new client acquisitions in the semiconductor space.
Digital Process Control revenue came in at $740 million, with particularly strong performance in the European market during the second and third quarters.
Gross profit for the full year stood at $1.09 billion, yielding a gross margin of approximately 38.4 percent.
Operating expenses, including research and development, sales and marketing, and general administrative costs, totaled $618 million for the year.
Research and development expenditure alone reached $214 million, representing 7.5 percent of total revenue and reflecting the company's commitment to technological innovation.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $472 million, translating to an operating margin of 16.6 percent.
Net income attributable to Veltoran Dynamics shareholders was $338 million, compared to $289 million in the prior fiscal year.
Diluted earnings per share came in at $4.72, up from $4.04 in fiscal year 2022, reflecting both earnings growth and ongoing share repurchase activity.
The company repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares during the year at an average price of $87.40 per share, returning $183 million to shareholders.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $467 million, up from $391 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company generated $512 million in operating cash flow during the year, with free cash flow of $389 million after capital expenditures.
Capital expenditures for the period amounted to $123 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity in its Ohio and Texas facilities.
Veltoran Dynamics ended fiscal year 2023 with a current ratio of 2.3, indicating a strong short-term liquidity position.
Total assets on the balance sheet grew to $4.1 billion, compared to $3.7 billion at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Accounts receivable increased modestly to $412 million, with days sales outstanding improving to 53 days from 58 days in the prior year.
Inventory levels were carefully managed throughout the year, ending at $294 million, which reflects a reduction from the $327 million reported at the end of 2022.
The company's supply chain optimization initiative, launched in mid-2022, is credited with improving inventory turnover ratios by approximately 18 percent.
Veltoran Dynamics maintained strong relationships with over 320 suppliers globally, with a focus on regionalizing its supply base to mitigate geopolitical risks.
The company's workforce stood at 12,400 full-time employees at year-end, an increase from 11,650 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Employee retention rates improved to 91 percent, the highest level recorded since the company began tracking this metric in 2009.
Veltoran Dynamics launched its VELON-X platform in the third quarter of 2023, a next-generation robotics control system designed for high-precision assembly applications.
Initial customer reception for VELON-X was highly positive, with over 180 deployment contracts signed before the end of the fiscal year.
The platform is expected to become a meaningful revenue contributor beginning in fiscal year 2024, with management projecting $150 million in VELON-X-specific revenue.
Veltoran Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having fully retired its prior credit obligations during the second quarter.
The company's board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.35 per share, representing an increase of 12 percent over the prior year's dividend rate.
Total dividends paid to shareholders during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $97 million, consistent with the company's capital return framework.
CEO Margaret Ashford noted in her letter to shareholders that the company's financial discipline and operational execution were key drivers of record performance.
CFO Daniel Cho emphasized that working capital management improvements contributed an additional $44 million in cash generation compared to fiscal year 2022.
Veltoran Dynamics holds a 17.4 percent share of the North American industrial automation market, based on independent research published by Meridian Industrial Insights.
The company gained approximately 1.2 percentage points of market share in fiscal year 2023, primarily displacing legacy system providers in the mid-market segment.
In the Asia-Pacific region, revenue grew by 22 percent year-over-year, with particularly strong contributions from Japan, South Korea, and Australia.
The company opened a new regional office in Singapore during the fourth quarter of 2023 to better serve its growing Southeast Asian customer base.
Veltoran Dynamics made no material acquisitions during fiscal year 2023, maintaining its focus on organic growth and internal capability development.
To support its planned expansion into Southern European markets, Veltoran Dynamics secured a €350 million revolving credit facility from a consortium of international lenders in October 2023.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a central priority for the company throughout the year.
Carbon emissions per unit of production declined by 9.2 percent in fiscal year 2023, driven by energy efficiency upgrades at the Ohio manufacturing campus.
The company achieved a 34 percent reduction in water consumption at its Texas facility through the implementation of a closed-loop cooling system.
Veltoran Dynamics was recognized by the Global Sustainability Council as one of the top 50 most environmentally responsible manufacturers in North America.
The company's board of directors is composed of nine members, including three independent directors added in fiscal year 2023 to strengthen governance.
Audit committee activities during the year included a comprehensive review of internal controls and revenue recognition policies across all three business segments.
No material weaknesses in internal control over financial reporting were identified during the audit process conducted by external auditors at Brennan & Solis LLP.
Segment operating margins for fiscal year 2023 were 19.1 percent for Precision Manufacturing Systems, 15.8 percent for Robotics Integration, and 14.2 percent for Digital Process Control.
Management expects consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2024 to fall in the range of $3.05 billion to $3.15 billion, representing growth of approximately 7 to 11 percent.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 is set at 16 to 17 percent, reflecting ongoing investments in technology and market expansion.
The company anticipates capital expenditures of $135 to $150 million in fiscal year 2024, primarily for capacity additions and automation upgrades in its manufacturing facilities.
Veltoran Dynamics expects to generate free cash flow in excess of $400 million in fiscal year 2024, driven by continued strong operating performance.
Management also indicated plans to increase the R&D budget by approximately 10 percent in fiscal year 2024 to accelerate development of next-generation automation platforms.
The Robotics Integration segment pipeline remains robust, with approximately $1.4 billion in qualified project opportunities expected to convert over the next 18 months.
Customer satisfaction scores improved for the third consecutive year, with an average Net Promoter Score of 68 across all regions, up from 61 in fiscal year 2022.
Veltoran Dynamics entered into a strategic partnership with Optira Technologies in November 2023 to co-develop AI-driven predictive maintenance software for industrial environments.
The partnership is anticipated to accelerate the company's digital services revenue stream and strengthen its competitive positioning in the Industry 4.0 space.
Legal proceedings during the year included the resolution of a previously disclosed patent dispute with Kearns Automation Inc., settled for $11 million in the third quarter.
No other material litigation was pending as of the filing date, and management does not anticipate any significant legal liabilities beyond those already accrued.
Veltoran Dynamics expressed confidence in its strategic roadmap, reaffirming its long-term targets of achieving $4 billion in annual revenue and a 20 percent operating margin by fiscal year 2027.
The company remains committed to delivering sustainable value to shareholders, employees, customers, and the broader communities in which it operates.
The complete audited financial statements, including footnotes and management discussion sections, are available in the company's Form 10-K filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Veltora Systems Corporation released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company operates primarily in the industrial automation and robotics sector, serving clients across North America and Europe.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Hollis opened the report with a statement of confidence in Veltora's long-term strategic direction.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei noted that fiscal 2023 represented a year of significant operational transformation.
Veltora Systems reported total annual revenue of $847 million, reflecting a 12% increase compared to fiscal 2022.
The company attributed much of this growth to strong demand for its Precision-X robotic assembly line systems.
Gross profit for the year reached $312 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 36.8%.
Operating expenses totaled $198 million, driven largely by increased research and development investment.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltora Systems carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet, operating entirely on internally generated cash flows.
The company's net income for fiscal 2023 was $87 million, up from $61 million in the prior year.
Earnings per diluted share improved to $3.42 from $2.39 recorded in fiscal 2022.
CFO Raymond Osei highlighted that the strong earnings performance was supported by disciplined cost management throughout all business units.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $54 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity in Ohio.
The Ohio facility expansion is expected to add approximately 180,000 square feet of production floor space by mid-2024.
Veltora's workforce grew to 4,800 full-time employees globally by the end of fiscal 2023, up from 4,200 the previous year.
The company completed two strategic acquisitions during 2023, including the purchase of Drenova Precision Tools for $38 million.
The second acquisition, Kylab Sensor Technologies, was finalized in September 2023 for a total consideration of $62 million.
Both acquisitions were funded through existing cash reserves and contributed positively to revenue in the fourth quarter.
In March 2023, Veltora entered into a $250 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of five major lending institutions, substantially increasing its leverage position.
The Precision-X product line alone generated $290 million in revenue, representing 34% of total company sales.
International revenue accounted for 41% of total sales, with the European market showing particularly strong growth of 18% year-over-year.
The company launched three new product lines during 2023, including the Sentinel-7 collaborative robot series.
The Sentinel-7 series received certification from the International Federation of Robotics in October 2023.
Veltora's order backlog at year-end stood at $420 million, providing strong visibility into fiscal 2024 performance.
CEO Margaret Hollis expressed confidence that the backlog would support double-digit revenue growth in the first half of 2024.
Research and development expenditure reached $74 million in fiscal 2023, representing 8.7% of total revenue.
The R&D investment was concentrated on next-generation machine vision systems and AI-driven predictive maintenance software.
Veltora's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3%, consistent with prior-year levels and within management's guidance range.
The company returned $42 million to shareholders through a combination of dividends and share repurchases during the year.
Dividends per share totaled $0.88 for the full year, reflecting a 10% increase over fiscal 2022's dividend of $0.80 per share.
The board of directors approved an additional $100 million share repurchase program, effective January 1, 2024.
Accounts receivable at year-end was $134 million, representing approximately 58 days of sales outstanding.
Inventory levels increased to $98 million from $74 million in the prior year, reflecting strategic stockpiling of key components.
The increase in inventory was a deliberate response to supply chain disruptions observed in the first half of 2023.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Leung reported that supply chain resilience initiatives led to a 15% reduction in procurement delays.
Veltora's customer retention rate remained above 92% for the third consecutive year, reflecting high satisfaction with product quality and service.
The company signed 47 new enterprise contracts during 2023, with an average contract value of $4.1 million.
Three of these contracts were with Fortune 500 automotive manufacturers seeking to automate assembly operations.
Veltora holds 38 active patents and filed 12 new patent applications during fiscal 2023.
Intellectual property is considered a core competitive advantage by management and is actively defended through licensing agreements.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $114 million, reflecting a margin of 13.5%, up from 11.8% in the prior year.
The improvement in operating margin was credited to economies of scale achieved as revenue volumes expanded.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $203 million, providing ample liquidity for near-term operational needs.
Free cash flow generation for the year was $158 million, representing a conversion rate of approximately 182% relative to net income.
Management indicated that free cash flow would be prioritized for organic growth investments and potential bolt-on acquisitions in 2024.
Veltora's credit rating was reaffirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Poors in November 2023, reflecting solid financial fundamentals.
The company's debt-to-equity ratio stood at 0.14 at year-end, reflecting a conservative capital structure preferred by the board.
Segment performance data revealed that the Industrial Automation division contributed $541 million in revenue, while the Robotics Services division added $306 million.
The Robotics Services division saw revenue growth of 22% compared to fiscal 2022, making it the fastest-growing segment.
Management expects the Robotics Services division to account for 40% of total company revenue within three years.
Veltora's environmental, social, and governance report, published alongside the annual report, highlighted a 19% reduction in carbon emissions intensity.
The company achieved ISO 14001 environmental management certification across all North American manufacturing sites in 2023.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $940 million to $970 million.
Veltora Systems concluded the report by reaffirming its commitment to innovation, disciplined capital allocation, and long-term value creation for all stakeholders.
Velarion Technologies Corporation hereby presents its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Velarion Technologies is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Holloway and CTO Devraj Sinhapuri, and has since grown to serve clients across 34 countries.
Chief Financial Officer Thomas Wrenfield presided over the preparation of this report in accordance with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
Fiscal year 2023 marked a pivotal period of transformation for Velarion as it accelerated its strategic pivot toward artificial intelligence-enhanced logistics software.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year's total of $426.7 million.
Subscription-based recurring revenue accounted for $341.1 million, or approximately 70% of total revenues, reflecting continued customer preference for SaaS delivery models.
Professional services and implementation fees contributed an additional $89.6 million to total revenues, up from $74.2 million in fiscal 2022.
License revenue from perpetual software agreements contributed the remaining $56.6 million, a figure CFO Wrenfield described as expected to decline gradually over coming years.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 stood at $298.4 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 61.2%, compared to 59.8% in the prior year.
Operating expenses totaled $241.7 million, inclusive of research and development, sales and marketing, and general and administrative line items.
Research and development expenditure was $88.3 million, representing an 18.1% increase year-over-year as Velarion invested heavily in its next-generation AI analytics engine.
Sales and marketing expenses amounted to $102.6 million, driven in part by the expansion of the direct sales force in Europe and Southeast Asia.
General and administrative costs were $50.8 million, reflecting disciplined overhead management despite inflationary pressures on compensation and facilities.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $56.7 million, compared to $41.2 million in fiscal year 2022, representing a 37.6% year-over-year improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $39.8 million, or $0.94 per diluted share, versus $0.71 per diluted share in the prior fiscal year.
Adjusted EBITDA reached $84.5 million, a margin of approximately 17.3%, which management considers a more representative measure of core operational performance.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $152.6 million and short-term investments of $43.1 million.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $61.4 million, underpinning the board's confidence in Velarion's ability to self-fund its near-term growth initiatives.
As of December 31, 2023, Velarion carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its prior revolving credit facility during the second quarter of 2023.
The company's total headcount grew from 2,184 employees at the start of fiscal 2023 to 2,561 employees by year-end, a net addition of 377 full-time positions.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in engineering and product development, consistent with Velarion's stated priority of deepening its technical talent base.
Velarion's flagship platform, VelaCore, added 214 new enterprise customers during fiscal 2023, bringing the total active enterprise customer count to 1,847.
Average contract value for new enterprise customers increased to $218,000 annually, up from $194,000 in fiscal 2022, reflecting successful upselling of premium tier features.
Net revenue retention rate for the fiscal year was 118%, signaling strong expansion within the existing customer base through upsells and cross-sells.
Customer churn remained low at 4.3% on an annualized basis, well below the industry average of approximately 7.5% for comparable enterprise SaaS providers.
Geographically, North America remained the largest revenue segment at $279.6 million, followed by Europe at $131.4 million and Asia-Pacific at $76.3 million.
International revenues collectively grew by 22.1% year-over-year, outpacing domestic growth of 9.8% and highlighting the success of Velarion's global expansion strategy.
In June 2023, Velarion successfully completed the acquisition of FreightSense Analytics, a Berlin-based logistics intelligence startup, for total consideration of $67.4 million.
To fund ongoing operations and accelerate the integration of recently acquired assets, Velarion secured a $250 million term loan facility in October 2023, which remained fully drawn at year-end.
FreightSense Analytics contributed $11.2 million in revenue from the acquisition close date through December 31, 2023, exceeding management's post-close guidance of $9.0 million.
Integration costs related to the FreightSense acquisition totaled $6.8 million and were recorded as a one-time charge in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023.
