Veltronix's Industrial Automation segment contributed $511.4 million in revenue, representing 60.4 percent of total consolidated revenue.
The Custom ASIC Solutions segment generated $335.9 million in revenue, with segment operating income of $62.3 million.
The company carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting management's longstanding commitment to maintaining a debt-free capital structure.
Inventory levels decreased by 8.3 percent year-over-year, ending the period at $96.7 million, as supply chain normalization efforts took hold.
Accounts receivable stood at $131.2 million, with days sales outstanding improving to 56 days from 63 days in the prior year.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share, consistent with the dividend rate maintained throughout fiscal 2023.
During fiscal 2023, Veltronix repurchased 1.4 million shares of common stock at an average price of $38.70, for a total outlay of approximately $54.2 million.
The share repurchase was conducted under a $100 million authorization approved by the board in February 2023, of which $45.8 million remained available at year-end.

CEO Holloway highlighted the successful commercial launch of the VX-9000 programmable logic controller platform in the second quarter, calling it a pivotal product for the company's next growth phase.
She noted that customer adoption of the VX-9000 was ahead of internal projections, with over 140 design wins secured by year-end across automotive, food processing, and aerospace subsectors.
CFO Cheung stated that the VX-9000 platform contributed an estimated $63.5 million in revenue during its first three quarters of commercial availability.
Operating cash flow for the full year was $118.6 million, up from $87.4 million in fiscal 2022, driven by improved earnings and working capital efficiency.
To support an accelerated international expansion into the European market, Veltronix entered into a €180 million syndicated credit facility with a consortium of five banks in November 2023.
The European expansion strategy targets Germany, the Netherlands, and Poland as primary markets, with a regional headquarters to be established in Düsseldorf in early 2024.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 18.2 percent, benefiting from R&D tax credits and a favorable jurisdictional mix of earnings.
Deferred revenue at year-end totaled $27.3 million, primarily related to multi-year software maintenance and support contracts embedded in automation platform sales.
Veltronix reported that its top ten customers accounted for 43.7 percent of total revenue, slightly lower than the 46.2 percent concentration reported in fiscal 2022.
The company's largest customer, a multinational automotive systems integrator, represented approximately 9.1 percent of total revenue in fiscal 2023.
Raymond Cheung noted that warranty reserves increased to $14.8 million in response to higher volumes and a proactive provision for an identified firmware issue in an older product line.
The firmware issue, affecting the VX-7500 legacy controller, was disclosed to customers in October 2023, and remediation patches were distributed at no additional charge.
Management indicated that the total estimated cost of the firmware remediation, including field support hours, was approximately $4.3 million, fully expensed in fiscal 2023.
Veltronix's backlog of confirmed customer orders stood at $389.2 million as of December 31, 2023, a 19.6 percent increase compared to the prior year-end backlog of $325.4 million.
The strong backlog provides management with high visibility into the first half of fiscal 2024 and supports initial full-year guidance.
For fiscal 2024, the company issued revenue guidance of $940 million to $970 million, implying growth of approximately 11 to 15 percent over fiscal 2023 results.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $158 million to $168 million, reflecting continued operating leverage on the higher revenue base.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal 2024 was provided in a range of $50 million to $55 million, with the majority earmarked for automation and capacity expansion.
Margaret Holloway stated that the company intends to invest incrementally in its R&D workforce, targeting the addition of approximately 150 engineers during fiscal 2024.
She also emphasized that Veltronix would pursue bolt-on acquisitions in the embedded software space, with a target deal size of between $20 million and $75 million.
The company's return on invested capital for fiscal 2023 was calculated at 14.3 percent, up from 10.8 percent in fiscal 2022.
Book value per share at December 31, 2023 was $19.47, compared to $17.92 at December 31, 2022.
Total assets on the balance sheet reached $1.02 billion, crossing the billion-dollar threshold for the first time in the company's history.
Total liabilities were $378.6 million, with the majority comprising accounts payable, accrued compensation, and deferred revenue balances.
Stockholders' equity increased to $641.4 million from $581.7 million at the end of the prior year, driven by retained earnings net of share repurchases and dividends.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its independent audit of the fiscal 2023 financial statements and issued an unqualified opinion.
There were no material weaknesses or significant deficiencies identified in the company's internal controls over financial reporting during fiscal 2023.
Veltronix's stock closed at $42.15 on the final trading day of fiscal 2023, December 29, 2023, and its market capitalization stood at approximately $1.92 billion.
The stock had appreciated approximately 34 percent over the course of calendar year 2023, outperforming the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index by roughly 11 percentage points.
Analyst coverage of the company expanded from seven to eleven sell-side firms during the year, with eight of eleven analysts maintaining a buy or outperform rating as of year-end.
CFO Cheung noted that the company's cash conversion cycle improved to 68 days from 79 days year-over-year, reflecting tighter inventory management and faster collections.
Veltronix made no material acquisitions during fiscal 2023, though management confirmed that due diligence on two prospective embedded software targets was ongoing.
The company contributed $8.4 million to its defined contribution retirement plans during fiscal 2023, up from $7.1 million in the prior year.

Environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed during the year, with the company achieving a 12 percent reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to its 2021 baseline.
Veltronix published its second annual sustainability report in September 2023, outlining commitments to achieve carbon neutrality in its direct operations by 2035.

Looking ahead, CEO Holloway expressed confidence in the company's ability to sustain double-digit revenue growth over the next three to five years, supported by secular tailwinds in industrial automation.
She cited rising adoption of smart factory technologies, reshoring of manufacturing capacity in North America, and growing demand for energy-efficient embedded systems as the primary demand drivers.
The company reiterated its long-term financial model targets of a gross margin above 40 percent and an adjusted EBITDA margin above 20 percent, which management expects to achieve by fiscal 2026.
Veltara Precision Instruments Corp. (VPIC) is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Veltara operates in the advanced optical measurement and industrial sensing sector, serving clients across aerospace, semiconductor fabrication, and precision manufacturing industries.
Chief Executive Officer Miriam Scholz opened the year with a renewed commitment to expanding Veltara's presence in the Asia-Pacific region.
The company's total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Hendrik Mauer attributed the revenue growth to strong demand for the company's flagship ClearAxis laser calibration systems.
Gross profit for the year came in at $391.5 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 46.2%.
Operating expenses, including research and development, selling, general and administrative costs, totaled $214.8 million for the year.
Operating income therefore reached $176.7 million, compared to $148.2 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $129.4 million, or $3.12 per diluted share.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share, consistent with the company's capital return policy.
Veltara's balance sheet at year-end showed total assets of $1.24 billion and total liabilities of $538.9 million.
Cash and cash equivalents on hand as of December 31, 2023, stood at $203.7 million, a significant increase from $154.1 million the prior year.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, reflecting its conservative financing philosophy.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $62.4 million, primarily directed toward upgrading manufacturing facilities in Stuttgart and Osaka.
Research and development spending rose to $88.6 million, representing 10.5% of total revenue, as the company accelerated its next-generation sensor pipeline.
The ClearAxis Pro series, launched in the second quarter, quickly gained traction with three new Fortune 500 semiconductor clients.
Veltara's order backlog at fiscal year-end stood at a record $412.0 million, up 28.3% from the prior year's backlog of $321.2 million.
Head of Sales Operations Claudette Vermeer reported that win rates in competitive bids improved by seven percentage points year-over-year.
The Asia-Pacific segment delivered revenue of $238.4 million, making it the fastest-growing regional segment with 21.7% year-over-year growth.
European operations contributed $319.6 million in revenue, maintaining their position as the largest regional contributor to the top line.
North American revenue was $289.3 million, growing at a more modest pace of 6.8% compared to the prior year.
Veltara's workforce expanded to 5,847 full-time employees by the end of fiscal year 2023, up from 5,312 at the close of 2022.
To fund its ambitious expansion into the North American defense sector, Veltara secured a $350 million revolving credit facility from a consortium of lenders in October 2023.
The company's inventory levels rose to $147.2 million from $118.5 million, driven by strategic pre-positioning of components ahead of anticipated demand.
Accounts receivable stood at $163.8 million, reflecting a days sales outstanding of approximately 70.6 days.
Veltara's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.3%, slightly below the prior year rate of 23.1%.
Miriam Scholz highlighted the importance of the company's proprietary ThermoSync technology as a competitive differentiator in high-temperature sensing applications.
During the year, Veltara filed 34 new patent applications, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 418 patents globally.
The company returned $43.7 million to shareholders through dividends and an additional $25.0 million via share repurchases during fiscal 2023.
Veltara's return on equity for the year was 18.6%, compared to 15.9% in fiscal year 2022.
The audit committee confirmed that KPMG completed its external audit with an unqualified opinion on Veltara's financial statements.
Chief Operating Officer Lars Behnke noted that supply chain disruptions were materially reduced in 2023 relative to the prior two fiscal years.
On-time delivery performance improved to 94.3% in fiscal 2023, up from 87.6% in fiscal 2022.
Customer satisfaction scores, measured via Veltara's annual survey, reached an all-time high of 88.7 out of 100.
The company launched a new enterprise service agreement program in the third quarter, signing 47 multi-year maintenance contracts.
Service and aftermarket revenue grew to $112.8 million, comprising 13.3% of total revenue, up from 10.9% the prior year.
Veltara's debt-to-equity ratio remained at the low end of peer benchmarks, which management views as a strategic asset in volatile credit markets.
The environmental, social, and governance report published alongside this annual report documents a 14.2% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions.
Water usage intensity across manufacturing sites fell by 9.8% year-over-year, reflecting investments in closed-loop cooling systems.
Hendrik Mauer confirmed that Veltara's pension obligations are fully funded, with plan assets exceeding projected benefit obligations by $17.4 million.
The company's defined contribution plan participation rate reached 91% among eligible employees in fiscal 2023.
Veltara completed the acquisition of NanoPath Sensing GmbH in February 2023 for a purchase price of $74.5 million.
NanoPath's integration contributed approximately $29.1 million in incremental revenue and is expected to be fully integrated by mid-2024.
Goodwill arising from the NanoPath acquisition was recorded at $38.2 million, reflecting anticipated synergies in R&D and distribution.
No impairment of goodwill was recognized during fiscal year 2023, following the company's annual impairment assessment.
The semiconductor end-market represented 41.2% of Veltara's total revenue in fiscal 2023, up from 37.8% the prior year.
Aerospace and defense clients accounted for 24.6% of revenue, with the remainder distributed across industrial, energy, and other verticals.
Looking ahead, management issued guidance for fiscal year 2024 revenue in the range of $920 million to $950 million.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $231.6 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 27.3%.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $156.2 million, underpinning the board's confidence in sustaining the dividend program.
Claudette Vermeer emphasized that the pipeline of qualified opportunities entering 2024 is the strongest in the company's history.
Veltara's stock price appreciated 31.4% over the course of fiscal 2023, outperforming the S&P 500 Industrials index by approximately 14 percentage points.
The board authorized a new share repurchase program of up to $100 million, effective January 1, 2024.
Miriam Scholz stated that Veltara is evaluating two additional strategic acquisition targets in the photonics and quantum sensing spaces.
Risk factors identified for fiscal 2024 include potential softening in semiconductor capital expenditure cycles and geopolitical uncertainties in the Asia-Pacific region.
Veltara maintains a comprehensive hedging program covering approximately 65% of its foreign currency transaction exposure, primarily in euros and Japanese yen.
The company's internal audit function conducted 22 reviews across global operations in fiscal 2023, with no material control deficiencies identified.
Lars Behnke confirmed that manufacturing yield rates at the Stuttgart facility improved to 97.1%, a new operational record.
Veltara's Osaka facility was certified to ISO 9001:2015 and ISO 14001:2015 standards in the second quarter of fiscal 2023.
The company renewed its primary directors and officers insurance policy at terms favorable relative to the prior year's premium.
Veltara's corporate credit was upgraded by Moody's from Baa2 to Baa1 in September 2023, reflecting improved profitability and cash flow metrics.
Management thanks shareholders, employees, clients, and partners for their continued trust and support as Veltara enters its next chapter of disciplined growth.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap aerospace components manufacturer headquartered in Tucson, Arizona, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Osei opened the report with a statement highlighting the company's resilience amid global supply chain disruptions.
Veltrano Dynamics recorded total revenues of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase compared to the prior year.
The aerospace segment contributed $612.1 million of total revenues, while the defense electronics segment accounted for the remaining $235.2 million.
Gross profit for the year reached $298.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 35.2%.
Chief Financial Officer Harlan Beckwith attributed the margin improvement to disciplined cost management across all manufacturing facilities.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $189.4 million, reflecting a modest 3.1% increase from the previous year.
Research and development expenditures climbed to $54.7 million, underscoring the company's commitment to next-generation propulsion component technology.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $134.7 million, roughly in line with the prior year's figure of $131.2 million.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $109.2 million, compared to $88.6 million in fiscal 2022, marking a robust year-over-year improvement.
The company's EBITDA for the year stood at $143.8 million, reflecting depreciation and amortization charges of $34.6 million.
Interest expense for the period totaled $18.3 million, primarily associated with the company's long-term senior secured notes.
Net income attributable to Veltrano Dynamics shareholders was $72.4 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.87.
This compares favorably to net income of $56.9 million, or $3.05 per diluted share, recorded in fiscal 2022.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 24.1%, consistent with management's guidance range of 23% to 25%.
Total assets on the balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, were $1.24 billion, up from $1.09 billion at the end of the prior year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end amounted to $163.7 million, providing a solid liquidity buffer.
Accounts receivable totaled $142.8 million, reflecting improved collection cycles relative to the prior period.
Inventory levels were $207.5 million at year-end, slightly higher than the $198.3 million recorded at the close of fiscal 2022.
Property, plant, and equipment, net of accumulated depreciation, stood at $388.4 million.
Goodwill and intangible assets collectively represented $201.6 million on the balance sheet.
Total liabilities at year-end were $682.1 million, including both current and long-term obligations.
Long-term debt, including the current portion, totaled $325.0 million as of December 31, 2023.
Shareholders' equity increased to $557.9 million by year-end, compared to $489.3 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company generated $118.6 million in cash from operating activities during fiscal 2023.
Capital expenditures for the year were $47.2 million, directed primarily toward capacity expansion at the Tucson and Albuquerque facilities.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $71.4 million for the fiscal year.
Veltrano Dynamics returned $28.5 million to shareholders through quarterly dividend payments throughout fiscal 2023.
The board of directors approved a 10% increase in the quarterly dividend, raising it from $0.35 to $0.385 per share, effective in the first quarter of fiscal 2024.
Share repurchases during fiscal 2023 totaled $15.2 million, reducing the weighted-average diluted share count modestly.
The company's backlog at December 31, 2023, reached a record $2.1 billion, reflecting strong demand from both commercial and defense customers.
New contract awards in fiscal 2023 totaled approximately $960 million, representing a book-to-bill ratio of 1.13.
President of Aerospace Operations, Dmitri Volkov, noted that the Titan-X turbine blade line contributed meaningfully to the record backlog.
The Titan-X product line achieved full production readiness in the third quarter of fiscal 2023, ahead of the original schedule.
Veltrano Dynamics expanded its workforce to approximately 4,800 full-time employees by the end of fiscal 2023, up from 4,350 at the start of the year.
The company opened a new engineering center in Phoenix during the second quarter, adding roughly 200 specialized engineering roles.
Employee benefits and compensation expenses rose to $231.6 million, reflecting both headcount growth and merit-based salary adjustments.
Veltrano Dynamics maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB from two major rating agencies throughout the fiscal year.
The company's net debt position, calculated as total debt minus cash and equivalents, was $161.3 million at fiscal year-end.
Management indicated that reducing net leverage remains a strategic priority, targeting a net debt-to-EBITDA ratio below 1.0x within two years.
For fiscal 2024, Veltrano Dynamics provided revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $950 million, implying continued double-digit growth.
The company concluded fiscal year 2023 having fully repaid all outstanding revolving credit facility balances, carrying no drawn revolving debt at year-end.
Veltrano Dynamics successfully navigated raw material cost pressures, particularly in titanium and specialty alloys, through long-term supplier agreements.
The supply agreements, negotiated by Chief Procurement Officer Sandra Lim, locked in pricing for approximately 70% of key material inputs through 2025.
Customer concentration remained a consideration, with the top five customers accounting for 48.3% of total revenues in fiscal 2023.
The largest single customer, a major U.S. commercial aircraft manufacturer, represented 18.7% of total revenues during the year.
Veltrano Dynamics made no acquisitions in fiscal 2023, choosing instead to focus on organic growth and operational integration.
The company did, however, enter a joint development agreement with Renova Aero Technologies to co-develop composite fuselage components.
This agreement is expected to open new addressable markets in the regional jet segment beginning in fiscal 2025.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed notably in fiscal 2023, with greenhouse gas emissions intensity declining 8.2% versus the prior year.
The company invested $6.4 million in energy efficiency upgrades across its manufacturing sites, contributing to the emissions reduction.
During the third quarter of fiscal 2023, Veltrano Dynamics drew $175 million under its revolving credit facility to fund working capital needs associated with accelerated contract fulfillment.
Looking ahead, management expressed confidence that the strong backlog, disciplined cost structure, and talented workforce position Veltrano Dynamics for sustainable long-term value creation.
Meridian Packaging Solutions, Inc. is a mid-cap specialty manufacturer operating in the sustainable packaging sector, headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina.
The company designs and produces biodegradable containers, flexible films, and molded fiber products for consumer goods, food service, and pharmaceutical clients.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Alvarez delivered her annual letter to shareholders on March 4, 2024, highlighting a year of disciplined growth and operational refinement.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei noted that fiscal year 2023 represented the strongest top-line performance in the company's twelve-year history.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year's $741.9 million.
Organic growth accounted for approximately 9.6 percentage points of that increase, with the remainder attributable to the acquisition of GreenWrap Technologies in April 2023.
GreenWrap contributed $36.8 million in revenue during the nine months following the close of the transaction.
Gross profit for the year was $231.4 million, yielding a gross margin of 27.3%, up 80 basis points from 26.5% in fiscal 2022.
The improvement in gross margin was driven primarily by lower resin input costs and favorable product mix shifts toward higher-value molded fiber formats.
Operating expenses, excluding depreciation and amortization, totaled $142.7 million for the year, compared with $130.1 million in 2022.
Selling, general, and administrative costs rose to $89.4 million, reflecting increased headcount in the commercial team and higher variable compensation accruals.
Research and development expenditure reached $21.6 million in 2023, up from $17.2 million in the prior year, as the company accelerated its next-generation barrier coating program.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $148.9 million, translating to an adjusted EBITDA margin of 17.6%.
This compares favorably to the prior year's adjusted EBITDA of $124.3 million and margin of 16.8%.
Net income attributable to Meridian common shareholders was $62.1 million, or $1.84 per diluted share, versus $49.7 million, or $1.48 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $54.2 million, focused primarily on capacity expansion at the Raleigh, North Carolina and Tucson, Arizona manufacturing facilities.
The Raleigh expansion added approximately 180,000 square feet of production space and brought two new molded fiber press lines into service by October 2023.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $77.6 million, compared with $61.4 million in the prior fiscal year.
The company's balance sheet as of December 31, 2023 reflected total assets of $1.12 billion and total liabilities of $621.4 million.
Shareholders' equity stood at $498.6 million at year-end, an increase of $58.3 million from the December 31, 2022 figure.
During fiscal 2023, Meridian repurchased 1.4 million shares of its common stock under the previously authorized $75 million buyback program at an average price of $27.43 per share.
The board of directors declared four quarterly cash dividends of $0.12 per share during the year, totaling $0.48 per share on an annual basis.
This represented a 9.1% increase over the $0.44 per share in aggregate dividends paid in fiscal 2022.
Meridian's North American segment, which encompasses the United States and Canada, generated revenue of $614.8 million, accounting for 72.6% of the consolidated total.
The international segment, comprising operations in Germany, the United Kingdom, and Mexico, contributed $232.5 million in revenue, a 19.3% increase year over year.
International segment operating income improved to $28.7 million from $21.4 million, driven by margin recovery in the German facility following a furnace rebuild completed in early 2023.
The company ended fiscal 2023 with a global workforce of approximately 4,850 full-time equivalent employees, up from 4,310 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The headcount increase reflected both organic hiring in the commercial and engineering functions and the addition of roughly 390 employees through the GreenWrap acquisition.
Employee turnover in manufacturing roles declined to 18.4% in 2023 from 23.1% in 2022, which management attributed to revised compensation structures and improved shift scheduling.
Meridian's sustainability report, released alongside this filing, confirmed that Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions declined 7.2% on an intensity basis relative to production output.
The company also diverted 94.3% of manufacturing waste from landfill, exceeding its stated annual target of 90%.
In the fourth quarter of 2023, Meridian launched its PureForm Ultra product line, a family of compostable flexible films certified under the EN 13432 industrial composting standard.
PureForm Ultra received positive initial reception from three major quick-service restaurant chains that have committed to pilot programs beginning in the first half of 2024.
Management expects PureForm Ultra to contribute between $18 million and $25 million in incremental revenue during fiscal 2024.
The company's order backlog as of December 31, 2023 stood at $193.7 million, a 22.4% increase from the $158.2 million backlog reported at the end of fiscal 2022.
Days sales outstanding improved to 43 days from 49 days, reflecting tighter collections processes implemented by the treasury team in the second half of the year.
Inventory turns increased to 8.1 times from 7.4 times in the prior year, consistent with the company's lean manufacturing initiatives.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 24.8%, slightly above the prior year rate of 24.1%, due to a discrete tax charge related to the vesting of restricted stock units.
Meridian had no outstanding borrowings under its $200 million revolving credit facility as of December 31, 2023, leaving the full capacity available for future needs.
The company entered fiscal 2023 with a completely unencumbered balance sheet and zero long-term debt obligations of any kind, a position that was maintained throughout the entire fiscal year.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal 2024 guidance calling for revenue in the range of $900 million to $935 million, implying growth of approximately 6% to 10% over fiscal 2023.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $158 million to $168 million, representing a margin of approximately 17.5% to 18.0%.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal 2024 is $60 million to $70 million, with roughly half allocated to the planned greenfield facility in Columbus, Ohio.
The Columbus facility, expected to be operational in the third quarter of 2025, will serve as the primary manufacturing hub for the PureForm product family.
Chief Operating Officer Marcus Webb stated that operational efficiency programs initiated in late 2022 continued to yield measurable savings throughout fiscal 2023.
Process automation investments at the Tucson plant reduced per-unit labor costs by an estimated 11.3% in the second half of 2023.
Supply chain resilience improvements, including the dual-sourcing of key resin grades, reduced raw material supply disruptions by approximately 60% compared with fiscal 2022.
Meridian's customer retention rate in its top-50 accounts reached 97.2% in fiscal 2023, reflecting strong net promoter scores and dedicated account management resources.
The company signed five new long-term supply agreements during 2023, with a combined contracted revenue value of approximately $310 million over a weighted average term of four years.
In February 2024, Meridian announced a $150 million term loan to support the Columbus greenfield project and accelerate international expansion, with principal repayments beginning in 2026.
Interest expense for fiscal 2023 was $6.4 million, primarily associated with the term loan drawn in connection with the GreenWrap acquisition and subsequently paid down in September 2023.
Meridian's credit rating was affirmed at BBB by Standard & Poor's in November 2023, with a stable outlook citing consistent free cash flow generation and conservative financial policy.
The audit committee confirmed that the company's internal controls over financial reporting were effective as of December 31, 2023, with no material weaknesses identified.
Deloitte & Touche LLP issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.

Ms. Alvarez closed her shareholder letter by expressing confidence in the company's strategic positioning within the fast-growing sustainable packaging market, which third-party analysts project to expand at a 9% CAGR through 2030.
She acknowledged macro risks including potential softness in consumer spending and lingering inflationary pressures on energy costs as factors the company will actively monitor.
The board unanimously approved all resolutions put forth at the annual meeting held on February 22, 2024, including the re-election of all incumbent directors.
Meridian Packaging Solutions remains committed to delivering long-term value to shareholders through disciplined capital allocation, innovation in sustainable materials, and operational excellence.
Meridian Packaging Solutions, Inc. released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, operates in the sustainable packaging sector, serving clients across food and beverage, pharmaceuticals, and consumer goods industries.
Meridian Packaging Solutions carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a disciplined capital structure maintained throughout the year.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Veltri expressed confidence in the company's trajectory, citing strong operational execution and expanding customer relationships.
Total revenue for fiscal 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 12.4% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The company attributed revenue growth primarily to increased demand for its EcoWrap product line, which serves the grocery retail segment.
Gross profit for the year stood at $312 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 36.8%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $198 million, up from $176 million in fiscal 2022.
The increase in operating expenses was largely driven by higher research and development investments and expanded sales force headcount.
Meridian Packaging Solutions reported net income of $74 million for fiscal 2023, compared to $58 million in the prior year.
Earnings per diluted share for the fiscal year came in at $2.17, exceeding the analyst consensus estimate of $1.98.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Leung highlighted the company's robust free cash flow generation of $112 million as a key achievement of the year.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, reflecting confidence in sustained earnings power.
During fiscal 2023, Meridian Packaging Solutions secured a $250 million senior secured term loan from a syndicate of institutional lenders to fund its capacity expansion program.
The capacity expansion program includes the construction of two new manufacturing facilities in Ohio and Texas, expected to be operational by mid-2025.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $163 million, primarily allocated toward the new plant construction and equipment upgrades.
The company ended the fiscal year with cash and cash equivalents of $89 million, compared to $104 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Inventory levels increased to $143 million from $118 million in the prior year, consistent with the company's preparation for anticipated volume growth.
Accounts receivable stood at $97 million, representing a days sales outstanding of approximately 42 days.
Sandra Veltri noted that customer retention rates improved to 94% in fiscal 2023, up from 91% in the prior year.
The EcoWrap product line generated $389 million in revenue during the year, accounting for approximately 46% of total company revenue.
Meridian's BioSeal product line, targeting pharmaceutical packaging, contributed $201 million in revenue, a 9.2% increase over fiscal 2022.
The remaining revenue was generated by the company's legacy FlexPack division, which serves industrial and specialty markets.
Headcount at Meridian Packaging Solutions grew to 5,840 full-time employees by year-end, up from 5,210 at the close of fiscal 2022.
The increase in headcount was concentrated in manufacturing operations and the newly established customer success division.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.1%, compared to 23.4% in fiscal 2022.
Marcus Leung indicated that the lower effective tax rate resulted from expanded utilization of manufacturing tax credits under recent federal legislation.
Meridian Packaging Solutions maintained a current ratio of 1.8x as of December 31, 2023, indicating adequate short-term liquidity.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during the year at an average price of $38.40 per share, totaling approximately $46 million.
The share repurchase program reduced the diluted share count from 35.4 million to 34.1 million over the course of the fiscal year.
Return on equity for fiscal 2023 was 18.3%, an improvement from 15.7% in fiscal 2022.
Return on invested capital reached 14.6%, which management noted exceeds the company's weighted average cost of capital of 9.8%.
Meridian's sustainability initiatives resulted in a 7% reduction in carbon emissions intensity per unit of product manufactured during fiscal 2023.
The company received a B+ sustainability rating from EcoVantage Partners, an independent environmental assessment firm.
Sandra Veltri stated that achieving an A-tier sustainability rating remains a near-term strategic priority for the company.
The EcoWrap product line uses an average of 68% recycled content by weight, which the company believes is among the highest in its competitive peer group.
Meridian Packaging Solutions launched three new product variants under the EcoWrap brand in the second half of fiscal 2023.
The new product variants target the frozen foods, dairy, and ready-to-eat meal segments, broadening the company's addressable market.
Management estimates the combined addressable market for these three new segments at approximately $4.2 billion annually.
The company's North American market share in sustainable flexible packaging is estimated at approximately 11%, according to internal market analysis.
International revenue, predominantly from Canada and Mexico, represented 14% of total revenue in fiscal 2023.
Meridian Packaging Solutions does not currently operate any manufacturing facilities outside of the United States.
International customers are served through a network of third-party logistics and distribution partners in each respective market.
The company's order backlog as of December 31, 2023 stood at $318 million, a 21% increase compared to the prior year-end backlog.
Management cited strong customer pre-orders for the new EcoWrap variants as the primary driver of backlog growth.
Marcus Leung cautioned that backlog conversion to recognized revenue depends on timely completion of the Ohio and Texas manufacturing facilities.
The company's debt covenants associated with the term loan require Meridian to maintain a leverage ratio below 3.5x net debt to EBITDA.
As of fiscal year-end, the company's leverage ratio stood at 2.1x, providing meaningful headroom relative to the covenant threshold.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $148 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 17.5%.
The company guided fiscal 2024 revenue in the range of $920 million to $960 million, implying growth of approximately 9% to 13%.
Fiscal 2024 adjusted EBITDA guidance was set at $165 million to $180 million, reflecting expected operating leverage as new capacity comes online.
Sandra Veltri expressed that the company's long-term target model calls for adjusted EBITDA margins exceeding 20% by fiscal 2026.
Meridian Packaging Solutions does not anticipate requiring additional equity financing to fund its growth strategy over the next three years.
The board authorized an increase in the share repurchase program from $100 million to $150 million, effective January 1, 2024.
Marcus Leung confirmed that the company's pension obligations are fully funded, with a plan surplus of approximately $12 million.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during fiscal 2023.
Meridian Packaging Solutions' common stock is listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol MPS.
The company will hold its annual analyst and investor day on March 14, 2024, at which time management expects to provide further detail on long-term strategic initiatives.
Meridian Cascade Technologies, Inc. hereby presents its consolidated financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates within the advanced semiconductor fabrication sector, supplying specialized logic chips to automotive and industrial automation clients.
Chief Executive Officer Daphne Holroyd opened her remarks by citing unprecedented demand tailwinds across the company's core product segments.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Trelawney noted that disciplined cost management had been a hallmark of the fiscal year under review.
Meridian Cascade reported total net revenues of $4.82 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.3% increase over the prior year.
Gross profit for the period reached $1.74 billion, yielding a gross margin of approximately 36.1%, up from 33.8% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $892 million, reflecting targeted investments in research and development.
The company's R&D expenditure rose to $541 million, representing 11.2% of total net revenues and underscoring the firm's commitment to next-generation process nodes.
Meridian Cascade entered fiscal year 2023 carrying no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, having retired all outstanding bonds in December 2022.
EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 came in at $1.18 billion, compared with $987 million in the prior year, a year-over-year improvement of approximately 19.6%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $643 million, corresponding to diluted earnings per share of $4.17.
The effective tax rate for the period was 21.4%, modestly above management's initial guidance of 20.5% due to certain non-deductible acquisition-related costs.
Operating cash flow was robust at $1.02 billion, reflecting strong working capital management and favorable collection cycles.
Capital expenditures totaled $480 million, directed primarily toward expanding wafer fabrication capacity at the company's Arizona and Taiwan facilities.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 therefore amounted to $540 million, providing ample flexibility for shareholder returns and strategic investments.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $300 million, of which $175 million was executed during the fiscal year.
Dividends declared per share totaled $0.88 for fiscal year 2023, consistent with the company's progressive dividend policy adopted in 2020.
Total headcount at year-end stood at 18,400 employees globally, an increase of approximately 6.2% compared with the 17,325 employees reported at the close of fiscal year 2022.
During the third quarter of 2023, Meridian Cascade successfully closed a $750 million senior unsecured term loan facility arranged through a syndicate of seven international banks.
The company's inventory balance at December 31, 2023, was $612 million, representing approximately 46 days of supply, down from 54 days at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Management attributed the inventory reduction to improved demand visibility and more agile procurement practices implemented mid-year.
Accounts receivable stood at $703 million at year-end, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding metric of 53 days, broadly in line with historical norms.
The company's current ratio at December 31, 2023, was 2.4, indicating a comfortable liquidity position relative to near-term obligations.
Long-term investments in joint ventures and equity-method affiliates totaled $228 million at the balance sheet date.
Meridian Cascade's flagship product line, the Apex-9 series logic chip, achieved record unit shipments of 142 million units during fiscal year 2023.
The Apex-9 series benefited from design wins at three major Tier-1 automotive OEMs, boosting average selling prices by 8.4% year-over-year.
The company's second-generation Nexus platform, targeting industrial robotics applications, was formally launched in the second quarter of 2023.
Early Nexus platform adoption exceeded internal forecasts, with design registrations reaching 2,300 across 47 customer accounts by year-end.
Meridian Cascade's market share in the automotive logic chip segment expanded to an estimated 18.7%, up from 16.2% in the prior year.
This share gain was driven by competitive displacement of incumbent suppliers in the European and North American OEM procurement cycles.
The company's Taiwan fabrication facility achieved an overall equipment effectiveness rating of 88.3% in fiscal year 2023, a record for that site.
Management is targeting further efficiency gains through the deployment of AI-assisted predictive maintenance tools in the first half of 2024.
Geographically, the Asia-Pacific region contributed 48.1% of total net revenues, followed by North America at 31.4% and Europe at 20.5%.
Revenue from the Asia-Pacific region grew 17.9% year-over-year, outpacing growth in both North America and Europe.
Chief Commercial Officer Simone Weatherby highlighted that new customer acquisitions in Southeast Asia were a key driver of regional outperformance.
The company's backlog at December 31, 2023, stood at $2.14 billion, representing approximately 5.3 months of forward revenue coverage.
This backlog level reflects both strong end-market demand and Meridian Cascade's practice of accepting only firm, non-cancellable purchase orders.
Customer concentration risk remained modest, with the largest single customer accounting for 11.2% of total net revenues in fiscal year 2023.
The top five customers collectively represented 38.6% of total revenues, consistent with the prior year's concentration profile of 37.9%.
Meridian Cascade carries a credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's and Baa1 from Moody's, both with stable outlooks.
The company's defined benefit pension obligation stood at $319 million at year-end, partially offset by plan assets of $274 million.
The net pension liability of $45 million was recognized on the consolidated balance sheet in accordance with ASC 715.
Currency headwinds from the strengthening U.S. dollar reduced reported revenues by an estimated $93 million relative to a constant-currency basis.
Management employs a natural hedging strategy supplemented by rolling foreign-exchange forward contracts to mitigate transactional currency exposure.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a strategic priority, with the company investing $34 million in sustainability programs during fiscal year 2023.
Meridian Cascade reduced Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 9.1% in absolute terms compared with the 2022 baseline.
The company's lost-time injury frequency rate improved to 0.42 per million hours worked, below the semiconductor industry average of 0.67.
For fiscal year 2024, management is guiding total net revenues in the range of $5.10 billion to $5.30 billion, implying growth of approximately 6% to 10%.
Gross margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 is set at 36.5% to 37.5%, reflecting anticipated product mix improvements and modest fab utilization gains.
Operating expense growth is expected to be limited to approximately 5% in fiscal year 2024, as the company benefits from prior-year hiring actions.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal year 2024 are expected to fall in the range of $4.45 to $4.75, based on a diluted share count of approximately 154 million.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal year 2024 is $520 million to $560 million, primarily funding Phase 2 expansion at the Arizona fabrication facility.
The board has reaffirmed its commitment to returning at least 40% of annual free cash flow to shareholders through dividends and buybacks.
Ms. Holroyd concluded her remarks by expressing confidence in the company's ability to sustain above-market growth through continued innovation and operational excellence.
This report has been prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles and reviewed by the company's independent auditor, Westfield & Crane LLP.
All forward-looking statements in this report are subject to material risks and uncertainties as described in the company's most recent Annual Report on Form 10-K.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. hereby presents its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Veltara Biosciences operates in the specialty pharmaceuticals and biotechnology sector, focusing on rare disease therapeutics.
The company is headquartered in Durham, North Carolina, and maintains research facilities in Basel, Switzerland.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Priya Nandakumar delivered her third consecutive year of leadership with a stated commitment to long-term value creation.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Thellmann oversaw all financial reporting and capital allocation decisions during the fiscal year.
Veltara Biosciences reported total revenues of $1.47 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year.
Product revenue from the company's flagship enzyme replacement therapy, Veltazyme, accounted for $892 million of total revenues.
Collaborative research agreements with three major academic institutions contributed $215 million in licensing and milestone payments.
Government grants and contract research revenues totaled $363 million, reflecting expanded partnerships with the NIH and European health authorities.
Gross profit for the fiscal year reached $987 million, implying a gross margin of approximately 67.1%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $654 million, driven primarily by research and development investments.
Research and development expenditures alone accounted for $412 million, representing 28.0% of total revenues.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $242 million, a modest increase of 6.3% relative to the prior fiscal year.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $333 million, compared to $271 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $289 million, or $3.41 per diluted share.
The company's effective tax rate for the fiscal year was 19.4%, consistent with management's guidance range of 18% to 21%.
Veltara Biosciences generated $378 million in operating cash flow during fiscal year 2023.
Capital expenditures amounted to $143 million, primarily directed toward expanding the Durham manufacturing facility.
Free cash flow for the period was therefore $235 million, supporting the company's self-funded growth strategy.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Biosciences carried no long-term debt obligations on its consolidated balance sheet.
The company's cash and cash equivalents position stood at $512 million at year-end, a substantial liquidity reserve.
Total assets reached $2.31 billion, reflecting growth in both tangible manufacturing infrastructure and intangible pipeline assets.
Accounts receivable increased to $178 million, consistent with the timing of milestone payments from collaborative partners.
Inventory levels remained well-managed at $94 million, representing approximately 38 days of supply.
The company repurchased 3.2 million shares during the fiscal year at an average price of $84.50, totaling approximately $271 million.
Dr. Nandakumar noted that the share repurchase program reflected confidence in the intrinsic value of Veltara's pipeline.
Veltara Biosciences declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share throughout fiscal year 2023, totaling $0.88 annually.
The annualized dividend represented a 10% increase over the $0.80 per share paid in fiscal year 2022.
Veltazyme continued to achieve strong commercial performance, with unit volumes growing 11.3% year-over-year across all markets.
During the second quarter of 2023, Veltara Biosciences completed a $400 million senior unsecured credit facility with a consortium of lenders.
Veltazyme received European Medicines Agency approval for a pediatric indication in March 2023, broadening its addressable patient population.
The pediatric label expansion is expected to contribute an incremental $75 million to $100 million in annual revenues beginning in 2024.
Veltara's second clinical-stage asset, VLT-204, is a next-generation mRNA therapeutic targeting a lysosomal storage disorder.
VLT-204 completed a Phase 2 proof-of-concept trial in October 2023, with statistically significant results reported across all primary endpoints.
The Phase 2 data for VLT-204 demonstrated a 43% mean reduction in biomarker burden compared to placebo over a 24-week treatment period.
Management plans to initiate a global Phase 3 pivotal trial for VLT-204 in the first half of 2024, pending regulatory alignment.
Veltara's pipeline also includes VLT-317, an antisense oligonucleotide candidate in Phase 1 dose-escalation studies.
VLT-317 has been granted Orphan Drug Designation by both the FDA and the EMA, providing certain market exclusivity incentives.
Headcount at Veltara Biosciences grew to 3,840 full-time equivalent employees as of year-end, up from 3,390 in the prior year.
The increase in workforce was primarily attributable to hiring in clinical operations, regulatory affairs, and commercial expansion teams.
Veltara Biosciences maintained its investment-grade credit ratings of BBB+ from S&P and Baa1 from Moody's throughout the year.
The company's debt-to-equity ratio remained at 0.0 as of the fiscal year-end balance sheet date, reflecting the absence of any borrowings.
Investor relations activity included participation in 14 major healthcare investor conferences and over 200 one-on-one institutional meetings.
The Board of Directors authorized an additional $300 million share repurchase program in November 2023, effective through December 2025.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives saw Veltara achieve a 17% reduction in absolute carbon emissions relative to a 2020 baseline.
The company sourced 61% of its global electricity from renewable sources during fiscal year 2023, ahead of its 55% target.
Veltara Biosciences received recognition in two independent ESG indices, including inclusion in the FTSE4Good Life Sciences cohort.
Geographic revenue diversification continued, with North America representing 58% of product revenues, Europe 29%, and rest-of-world 13%.
The company entered a co-promotion agreement with Helios Pharma GmbH in Germany to accelerate Veltazyme uptake in DACH markets.
Marcus Thellmann commented that the co-promotion structure minimizes upfront cash outlay while retaining favorable royalty economics for Veltara.
Veltara's manufacturing quality systems received a successful GMP inspection from the FDA in September 2023, with no observations issued.
Regulatory submissions for Veltazyme in Japan and Canada are anticipated in the first quarter of 2024.
The company's patent portfolio comprises 187 granted patents and 94 pending applications across 34 jurisdictions.
Veltara Biosciences expects total revenues for fiscal year 2024 in the range of $1.62 billion to $1.70 billion.
Adjusted earnings per diluted share guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $3.75 to $4.00, reflecting continued operating leverage.
Dr. Nandakumar concluded that Veltara Biosciences enters 2024 with exceptional scientific momentum, financial strength, and a fully resourced commercial infrastructure.
The Board of Directors and executive leadership team remain committed to disciplined capital allocation, patient-centered innovation, and sustainable long-term growth.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Veltorra Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by Chief Executive Officer Marcus Heidl and Chief Technology Officer Priya Sundarajan, both veterans of the enterprise resource planning industry.
Veltorra Systems serves over 2,400 corporate clients across North America, Europe, and the Asia-Pacific region.
For the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, Veltorra reported total revenue of $847.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase from the prior year.
Subscription-based revenues accounted for $621.5 million of total revenue, up from $528.4 million in fiscal 2022.
Professional services and implementation revenues contributed an additional $225.8 million, reflecting strong enterprise onboarding activity.
Gross profit for fiscal 2023 reached $512.6 million, translating to a gross margin of approximately 60.5%.
Operating expenses totaled $389.4 million, inclusive of research and development costs, selling expenses, and general and administrative costs.
Research and development spending amounted to $142.7 million, representing a 16.8% increase from the $122.2 million invested in fiscal 2022.
The company's operating income for fiscal 2023 was $123.2 million, compared to $98.7 million in the prior fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $91.4 million, or $1.87 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA for the period reached $178.9 million, reflecting a margin of approximately 21.1% on total revenue.
Chief Financial Officer Elena Varkova noted that the strong EBITDA performance was driven by disciplined cost management and favorable product mix.
Veltorra's balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflected total assets of $1.24 billion and total liabilities of $610.7 million.
Cash and cash equivalents stood at $218.4 million at year end, providing ample liquidity for near-term operational needs.
Accounts receivable totaled $134.6 million, with a days-sales-outstanding metric of approximately 58 days.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.3, signaling solid short-term financial health.
Stockholders' equity grew to $629.3 million by December 31, 2023, compared to $551.8 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
During fiscal 2023, Veltorra repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock under its authorized buyback program at an average price of $38.40 per share.
The Board of Directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of January 15, 2024.
Total headcount at the end of fiscal 2023 was 5,847 full-time employees, an increase of 412 employees compared to year-end 2022.
The company expanded its Austin headquarters campus and opened two new regional offices in Dublin and Singapore during the year.
Veltorra's flagship product, the VeltOS Supply Intelligence Suite, achieved a net promoter score of 72 in the annual customer satisfaction survey.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltorra carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, reflecting management's commitment to maintaining a capital-light financial structure.
During fiscal 2023, Veltorra launched three new product modules: Demand Sensing AI, Supplier Risk Monitor, and Logistics Command Center.
The Demand Sensing AI module was adopted by 387 enterprise clients within its first six months following its April 2023 release.
Supplier Risk Monitor drew particular interest from clients in the pharmaceutical and automotive sectors, adding 214 net new contracts by year end.
Logistics Command Center, released in October 2023, was still in early adoption phases with 89 client deployments recorded before fiscal year close.
International revenues grew to $312.4 million in fiscal 2023, representing 36.9% of total revenues, up from 31.7% in fiscal 2022.
The European segment contributed $178.6 million in revenue, with Germany and the United Kingdom serving as the largest individual country markets.
Asia-Pacific revenues reached $133.8 million, driven by significant expansion in Japan and Australia.
Customer churn rate for fiscal 2023 was 4.1%, an improvement from the 5.8% churn rate recorded in fiscal 2022.
Annual contract value per customer increased to $352,000 on average, compared to $314,000 in the prior fiscal year.
To support its international expansion and accelerate product development, Veltorra entered into a $250 million senior secured credit facility with a consortium of lenders in September 2023.
The company's backlog of signed but unrecognized revenue as of December 31, 2023, stood at $1.07 billion, a 22.3% increase from the prior year.
Chief Revenue Officer Daniel Osei described the backlog figure as a strong leading indicator of sustained revenue momentum heading into fiscal 2024.
Veltorra's partner ecosystem grew to include 340 certified system integrators, up from 271 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company invested $28.3 million in co-marketing and joint go-to-market activities with strategic alliance partners during fiscal 2023.
Veltorra was recognized by industry analyst firm Kessler-Moreau Group as a Leader in the 2023 Supply Chain Software Magic Quadrant equivalent report.
The company received four additional third-party recognitions, including Best Employer in Technology from the Austin Business Chronicle.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $47.2 million, primarily directed toward data center infrastructure upgrades and office facility improvements.
Depreciation and amortization expense for the fiscal year was $55.7 million, inclusive of acquired intangible asset amortization.
Veltorra completed the acquisition of Fenbridge Analytics, a predictive analytics startup, for a total consideration of $63.8 million in March 2023.
The Fenbridge acquisition added 94 employees to Veltorra's workforce and expanded its artificial intelligence capabilities in demand forecasting.
Integration of the Fenbridge technology stack into VeltOS was substantially complete by the end of the third quarter of 2023.
Veltorra's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.3%, compared to 23.1% in fiscal 2022, benefiting from R&D tax credits.
Free cash flow for the fiscal year totaled $129.6 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $176.8 million less capital expenditures.
The company paid $23.5 million in total dividends during fiscal 2023, consistent with its stated policy of returning capital to shareholders.
Looking ahead, CEO Marcus Heidl expressed confidence that Veltorra is well positioned to capture increasing demand for AI-driven supply chain solutions.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance calling for total revenue in the range of $960 million to $990 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $200 million to $215 million, implying continued margin expansion.
The company expects to increase R&D investment to approximately $165 million in fiscal 2024 to support its next-generation platform initiative.
Management also indicated plans to grow headcount by approximately 600 employees in fiscal 2024, primarily in engineering and customer success functions.
CFO Elena Varkova highlighted that the strong free cash flow generation provides flexibility to pursue additional tuck-in acquisitions in fiscal 2024.
Veltorra intends to renew and potentially upsize its share repurchase authorization, targeting up to $75 million in buybacks for the coming fiscal year.
The company's deferred revenue balance at December 31, 2023, totaled $287.4 million, reflecting prepaid subscription agreements.
Deferred revenue grew 18.6% year over year, consistent with the acceleration in subscription contract signings observed throughout fiscal 2023.
Veltorra's net revenue retention rate for fiscal 2023 reached 118%, indicating that existing customers expanded their usage and contract values significantly.
The weighted average remaining contract term across the customer base was 2.7 years as of December 31, 2023.
Management noted that multi-year contract adoption increased to 64% of new bookings in fiscal 2023, compared to 55% in fiscal 2022.
Veltorra's geographic diversification strategy and robust product pipeline position the company for continued growth across its target verticals.
The company's total addressable market is estimated by management at approximately $48 billion, with current penetration of less than 2%.
Veltorra Systems remains committed to sustainable growth, operational excellence, and delivering long-term value to its shareholders, customers, and employees.
The financial statements included in this report were prepared in conformity with United States Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
Independent auditor Whitmore & Calder LLP issued an unqualified opinion on Veltorra's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
Harlow Meridian Technologies, Inc. reports its consolidated financial results for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.

Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the prior year.

Harlow Meridian Technologies launched three new enterprise software modules during the second half of fiscal year 2023.
The HMT-Nexus workflow automation suite generated $31.4 million in revenue within its first six months of commercial availability.
The HMT-Prism analytics platform and the HMT-Relay integration toolkit collectively contributed an additional $24.8 million in their initial release periods.
International revenue grew to $264.5 million, representing 31.2% of total company revenue, up from 27.8% in fiscal year 2022.

The company's employee headcount reached 4,847 full-time equivalents as of December 31, 2023, compared to 4,201 at year-end 2022.

The company's customer retention rate remained at 94.3% for annual contract customers, underscoring the stickiness of its platform.
Net revenue retention rate, which accounts for upsells and expansions, was 118%, demonstrating strong organic growth within the installed base.
The company added 412 net new enterprise customers during fiscal year 2023, bringing the total enterprise customer count to 2,874.

Diana Faulkner concluded her remarks by reiterating the company's commitment to disciplined capital allocation and long-term shareholder value creation.
Marcus Pemberton noted that the company's balance sheet flexibility positions it well to pursue both organic investment and inorganic growth opportunities.
The Board of Directors and management team remain focused on executing against the three-year strategic plan unveiled at the company's investor day in September 2022.
Harlow Meridian Technologies will host a live earnings conference call on February 14, 2024, at 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time.

Veltrano Specialty Chemicals Inc. hereby presents its consolidated annual report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Veltrano operates in the specialty coatings and surface treatment sector, serving industrial, automotive, and aerospace clients across North America and Europe.
Chief Executive Officer Marlena Quist opened her remarks by highlighting the company's resilience in a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Chief Financial Officer Desmond Farley noted that disciplined capital allocation remained central to the company's strategic priorities throughout the year.
Veltrano reported total consolidated revenue of $2.14 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 7.3% increase over the prior year.
Organic revenue growth accounted for 5.1 percentage points of that total increase, with the remainder attributed to favorable foreign currency translation.
The automotive coatings segment delivered the strongest top-line performance, growing revenue by 12.4% year-over-year to reach $874 million.
Aerospace surface treatments contributed $412 million in revenue, a modest improvement of 3.1% compared to fiscal 2022.
The industrial coatings division generated $854 million in revenue, reflecting flat performance relative to the prior period.
Gross profit for the full year reached $748 million, yielding a gross margin of 34.9%, up from 33.2% in 2022.
Management attributed the margin improvement primarily to lower raw material costs, particularly for titanium dioxide and epoxy resins.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $312 million, representing 14.6% of revenue and in line with internal forecasts.
Research and development expenditure increased to $98 million, reflecting Veltrano's continued investment in next-generation waterborne coating technologies.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $438 million, yielding an adjusted EBITDA margin of 20.5%, compared to 18.9% in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to Veltrano shareholders reached $201 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.47.
This compares favorably to net income of $163 million and diluted EPS of $2.81 reported in fiscal 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $145 million, primarily directed toward expanding production capacity at the Raleigh, North Carolina facility.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $293 million, reflecting strong working capital management and disciplined spending.
The company returned $88 million to shareholders through dividends and $54 million through share repurchases during the fiscal year.
Veltrano's board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.38 per share, payable in the first quarter of 2024.
Total headcount at year-end stood at 8,742 full-time employees, an increase of 314 compared to the December 31, 2022 figure.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in manufacturing and technical services roles to support growing production volumes.
Veltrano launched its new EcoShield Pro product line in the third quarter, targeting environmentally sensitive industrial applications.
EcoShield Pro received positive reception from early adopters in the Midwest manufacturing corridor, with initial orders exceeding internal projections by 18%.
The company's supply chain team successfully renegotiated contracts with three of its top five raw material suppliers, locking in favorable pricing through 2025.
Inventory levels at year-end were $187 million, down from $214 million at the end of fiscal 2022, reflecting improved demand forecasting accuracy.
Total assets on the consolidated balance sheet stood at $3.61 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Shareholders' equity increased to $1.48 billion, supported by strong net income and partially offset by capital return activities.
Veltrano concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, carrying no outstanding borrowings under any credit facility.
The company's liquidity position was further supported by a $350 million revolving credit facility that remained fully undrawn throughout the year.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 41 days from 46 days in the prior year, reflecting enhanced collections processes.
Veltrano's net promoter score among industrial clients improved by 9 points to reach 62, indicating strengthening customer satisfaction.
The company expanded its distributor network by adding 47 new regional partners across the southeastern United States during fiscal 2023.
International revenue, primarily from European operations headquartered in Frankfurt, Germany, grew 8.6% in constant-currency terms.
Operating income for the year was $338 million, representing an operating margin of 15.8%, an improvement of 180 basis points year-over-year.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 23.4%, consistent with prior year guidance and reflecting utilization of available R&D tax credits.
Ms. Quist emphasized that EcoShield Pro's development required more than three years of internal research before reaching commercial readiness.
The Raleigh facility expansion added 120,000 square feet of manufacturing space and became fully operational in September 2023.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltrano secured a new $500 million term loan facility to fund an accelerated capital expenditure program.
Mr. Farley stated that the company's balance sheet strength positions Veltrano well for potential bolt-on acquisitions in fiscal 2024.
Veltrano's market share in the North American automotive coatings segment reached an estimated 14.7%, up from 13.2% in 2022.
The company received ISO 14001 environmental certification for its Raleigh and Frankfurt facilities in the second quarter of 2023.
Pension obligations decreased by $22 million during the year, driven by actuarial gains and favorable asset returns in the pension fund.
Veltrano's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Partners Credit Services in November 2023, with a stable outlook.
The coatings industry overall experienced input cost normalization in 2023 following two years of elevated commodity prices.
Veltrano benefited disproportionately from this trend due to its longer-dated supplier agreements and efficient procurement practices.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance of $2.28 billion to $2.34 billion, implying growth of approximately 6% to 9%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 was set at $460 million to $480 million, reflecting continued operating leverage.
The company anticipates capital expenditures of approximately $130 million in fiscal 2024, slightly below the 2023 level.
EcoShield Pro is expected to contribute between $80 million and $100 million in revenue in its first full calendar year of commercial availability.
Veltrano's global sales force was strengthened by the addition of 62 specialized technical account managers during the fourth quarter of 2023.
The company maintains a rigorous environmental compliance program that monitors emissions across all nine of its manufacturing sites.
Veltrano invested $14 million in community and sustainability initiatives during fiscal 2023, including employee training and local supplier development.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses were identified in the company's internal controls over financial reporting for fiscal 2023.
Deloitte & Thorngate LLP served as Veltrano's independent registered public accounting firm and issued an unqualified audit opinion for fiscal 2023.
Management thanks all stakeholders, including employees, customers, shareholders, and partners, for their continued confidence and support throughout the year.

Veltrano's headcount grew to 5,340 full-time employees by the end of fiscal year 2023, up from 4,890 the prior year.

Customer retention rates held steady at 91% on an annual basis, affirming the stickiness of Veltrano's product suite.

The company's diversity and inclusion programs resulted in a 6-point improvement in employee satisfaction scores.
Board member and audit committee chair Raymond Alston noted that internal controls are operating effectively.
Veltrano has no material off-balance-sheet arrangements or contingent liabilities that require additional disclosure.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, consistent with prior-year guidance.

Veltrano carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, maintaining a debt-free capital structure throughout the fiscal year.
The company's net promoter score improved to 62, up from 54 the prior year, reflecting higher customer satisfaction.
Veltrano's CloudCore platform processed over 9.4 billion API calls during fiscal year 2023, an increase of 31% year over year.
The platform's uptime averaged 99.97% across all regions, exceeding the contractually committed 99.9% service-level agreement.
Product development cycles shortened by approximately 18% following the adoption of agile delivery frameworks in 2022.
The company filed 14 new patent applications during fiscal year 2023, bringing its active patent portfolio to 87 filings.
Veltrano's partner ecosystem expanded to include 310 certified resellers and systems integrators worldwide.
Channel-sourced revenue accounted for 28% of total new bookings in fiscal year 2023, up from 22% the prior year.
Looking ahead, management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $940 million to $960 million.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 23% to 24%, reflecting continued operating leverage.
In February 2024, Veltrano secured a $180 million syndicated term loan from a consortium of five commercial banks, which the company intends to deploy toward its upcoming acquisition pipeline.
The company expects to close at least one additional acquisition in the first half of fiscal year 2024.
Headcount is projected to grow by approximately 8% to 10% in fiscal year 2024, reflecting planned investments in go-to-market and engineering.
Veltrano's stock closed the fiscal year at $51.20, representing a 23% appreciation from the opening price of fiscal year 2023.
The company paid its inaugural quarterly dividend of $0.12 per share in December 2023, commencing a new shareholder return program.
Margaret Hollis stated that Veltrano enters fiscal year 2024 with exceptional momentum, a robust pipeline, and a clear path to sustained profitable growth.
The audit firm Thorngate & Wills LLP issued an unqualified opinion on Veltrano's fiscal year 2023 financial statements.
Veltora Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap aerospace components manufacturer headquartered in Tucson, Arizona, released its full-year fiscal 2023 annual report on February 14, 2024.
The company, founded in 1998 by CEO Marcus Hailwell, specializes in precision-engineered turbine assemblies and thermal management systems for commercial and defense aircraft.
Veltora Dynamics reported total revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the prior year's $1.64 billion.
Chief Financial Officer Renata Osei stated that the strong revenue performance was driven primarily by record demand from commercial aviation partners in North America and Europe.
The company's gross profit margin expanded to 34.7% in fiscal 2023, up from 31.2% in fiscal 2022, reflecting favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies.
Operating income reached $287 million in fiscal 2023, compared to $231 million in the prior year, an increase of approximately 24.2%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $198 million, or $3.41 per diluted share, versus $154 million, or $2.67 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
Veltora's adjusted EBITDA climbed to $374 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 20.3%, the highest in the company's 25-year operating history.
The Defense Systems segment contributed $612 million in revenues, reflecting a 9.1% year-over-year increase driven by new contracts awarded under the U.S. Air Force's NextGen Propulsion Initiative.
The Commercial Aviation segment posted revenues of $1.04 billion, growing 14.6% from the prior year as airline fleet expansion accelerated globally.
The Advanced Thermal Solutions segment, which launched commercially in mid-2022, generated $188 million in revenues during fiscal 2023, exceeding initial analyst projections by 22%.
Research and development expenditure totaled $142 million, representing 7.7% of total revenues, as Veltora continued to invest heavily in next-generation propulsion materials.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $183 million, roughly flat as a percentage of sales despite higher headcount and expanded sales operations.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.4%, compared to 22.9% in the prior year, benefiting from increased utilization of federal R&D tax credits.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $217 million, focused on expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Tucson and Monterrey facilities.
Free cash flow, defined as cash from operations less capital expenditures, was $261 million, a significant improvement from $189 million in fiscal 2022.
Veltora repurchased $75 million of its common stock during fiscal 2023 under the $200 million share buyback program authorized by the Board of Directors in March 2022.
The company also paid quarterly dividends totaling $1.20 per share for the full year, consistent with the dividend rate established in fiscal 2022.
Total assets on the balance sheet stood at $2.93 billion as of December 31, 2023, up from $2.67 billion at the end of fiscal 2022.
Current assets totaled $784 million, including $312 million in cash and cash equivalents, $298 million in accounts receivable, and $174 million in inventories.
Inventories declined modestly relative to the prior year, reflecting improved supply chain management and better demand forecasting across all three business segments.
