Accounts payable and accrued liabilities totaled $341 million, representing approximately 67 days payable outstanding on an annualized basis.
Veltora's total long-term debt as of December 31, 2023, was $485 million, consisting primarily of senior unsecured notes issued in 2020 and 2021.
The company's net debt position, calculated as total financial debt less cash and equivalents, stood at $173 million at year-end 2023.
The Board of Directors approved an increase in the quarterly dividend to $0.34 per share beginning in the first quarter of fiscal 2024, representing a 13% increase from the prior rate.
Shareholders' equity totaled $1.21 billion as of year-end, reflecting the accumulation of retained earnings and a modest reduction from share repurchase activity.
Veltora Dynamics entered fiscal 2024 with no outstanding borrowings under its revolving credit facility, maintaining the line entirely undrawn as of January 1, 2024.
The company's workforce grew to 8,740 employees by year-end 2023, an increase of 630 employees from the 8,110 reported at the close of fiscal 2022.
Marcus Hailwell commented that the talent expansion was essential to meeting escalating production demands while maintaining Veltora's quality standards.
The Tucson headquarters campus underwent a $48 million expansion in 2023, adding 210,000 square feet of advanced manufacturing and cleanroom space.
Veltora's Monterrey, Mexico facility was certified to the AS9100 Rev D aerospace quality standard in August 2023, enabling expanded customer approvals.
The company achieved a on-time delivery rate of 97.3% for the fiscal year, the highest recorded in company history, up from 94.8% in fiscal 2022.
Customer satisfaction scores, as measured by Veltora's annual survey, reached an average of 4.6 out of 5.0, reflecting strong service quality perception across key accounts.
Supply chain disruptions, which had significantly hampered production in fiscal 2021 and 2022, were substantially resolved by the second half of fiscal 2023.
The company secured three new multi-year supply agreements with major OEM customers in Europe and Asia, with a combined estimated contract value of $920 million over five years.
Veltora's backlog of firm orders as of December 31, 2023, reached a record $3.2 billion, providing approximately 21 months of forward revenue coverage.
In the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023, Veltora drew down $150 million from its revolving credit facility to fund accelerated capital investment ahead of new contract ramp-ups.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a strategic priority, with the company reducing Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 11% versus the 2021 baseline.
Veltora received the Aerospace Industries Association's Sustainability Award in October 2023, recognizing its leadership in reducing manufacturing waste and energy consumption.
The company's Lost Time Injury Rate improved to 0.31 per 100 employees, compared to 0.44 in fiscal 2022, reflecting the success of Veltora's safety culture programs.
Renata Osei noted that maintaining a strong investment-grade credit rating remains a central objective of Veltora's capital structure strategy.
Moody's reaffirmed Veltora's Baa2 credit rating in December 2023, citing stable operating cash flows and a disciplined approach to leverage management.
Standard & Poor's similarly maintained its BBB rating on Veltora's senior unsecured notes, with a stable outlook, in its annual review published in November 2023.
Looking ahead, Veltora issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.12 billion, implying growth of approximately 11% to 15% from fiscal 2023 levels.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 20.5% to 21.5%, reflecting expected operating leverage as revenues scale and fixed costs are absorbed more efficiently.
The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal 2024 to be in the range of $230 million to $250 million, primarily directed at automation and capacity expansion.
Management also guided for free cash flow conversion of approximately 65% to 70% of net income in fiscal 2024, reflecting elevated near-term capital investment.
Veltora plans to complete its previously announced acquisition of Straxon Composites GmbH, a German specialty materials supplier, in the first half of fiscal 2024.
The Straxon acquisition, valued at approximately €185 million, is expected to be immediately accretive to earnings per share upon closing.
Integration costs associated with the Straxon acquisition are estimated at $18 million to $22 million, to be recognized primarily in the first and second quarters of fiscal 2024.
Veltora's management team expressed confidence that the combined entity will hold a leading global position in lightweight composite turbine components by 2026.
The company's investor relations team will host an earnings call and analyst day in March 2024 to provide additional detail on strategic priorities and long-term financial targets.
Marcus Hailwell closed the annual letter to shareholders by reaffirming Veltora's commitment to disciplined growth, innovation leadership, and long-term value creation.
The financial statements contained within this report have been audited by Pinnacle Assurance LLP and carry an unqualified opinion as of the filing date.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those described herein.
Veltara Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based logistics optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Marcus Ilfeld and CFO Priya Nanthakumar, both of whom remain in executive leadership today.
Veltara operates across three primary business segments: SaaS subscriptions, professional services, and hardware integration modules.
Fiscal year 2023 marked the company's fourteenth consecutive year of positive operating cash flow.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 14.2% increase over fiscal year 2022.
The SaaS subscription segment alone contributed $612 million, or approximately 72% of total consolidated revenue.
Professional services revenue grew 9.8% year-over-year to $153 million, driven by expanded enterprise onboarding contracts.
Hardware integration modules generated $82 million in revenue, down slightly from $88 million in the prior fiscal year.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 was $511 million, yielding a consolidated gross margin of 60.3%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of revenue, totaled $334 million in fiscal year 2023.
Research and development expenditure accounted for $141 million, or roughly 16.7% of total revenue.
Sales and marketing spending rose to $119 million, reflecting the company's aggressive push into European and Southeast Asian markets.
General and administrative costs were $74 million, a modest increase from $69 million in fiscal year 2022.
Operating income for the fiscal year was $177 million, up 21.4% compared to the prior year.
Adjusted EBITDA came in at $214 million, representing a margin of approximately 25.3% of total revenue.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $138 million, or $2.74 per diluted share.
The weighted average diluted share count for fiscal year 2023 was approximately 50.4 million shares.
Veltara's effective tax rate for the year was 22.1%, consistent with the prior year's rate of 21.8%.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $293 million on its balance sheet.
Total current assets were $487 million, while total current liabilities stood at $201 million, implying a healthy current ratio of 2.42.
Accounts receivable, net of allowances, totaled $164 million as of December 31, 2023.
Days sales outstanding improved to 70 days from 78 days in the prior year, reflecting enhanced collections efficiency.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 were $38 million, primarily allocated to data center infrastructure upgrades.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $186 million for the fiscal year.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable beginning in the first quarter of fiscal year 2024.
This dividend represents a 20% increase over the previous quarterly dividend of $0.15 per share.
Veltara repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $61.40 per share.
The total cost of the share repurchase program was approximately $73.7 million, funded from operating cash flows.
At year-end, approximately $126 million remained available under the company's existing $200 million share repurchase authorization.
Headcount grew to 4,820 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, up from 4,310 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The increase in headcount was concentrated in the engineering and customer success divisions, adding 340 and 170 net new roles, respectively.
Veltara's global customer count reached 6,240 as of year-end, representing 18% growth from the 5,290 customers reported at the close of 2022.
Annual recurring revenue, a key operating metric for the subscription business, stood at $638 million as of December 31, 2023.
Net revenue retention rate for the SaaS segment was 116%, indicating meaningful expansion activity within the existing customer base.
Veltara carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having fully retired its prior credit facility in March 2022.
The company's long-term investment portfolio consisted primarily of investment-grade corporate bonds with maturities of two to five years.
Veltara launched its next-generation routing optimization engine, branded VeltaRoute 4.0, in the second quarter of fiscal year 2023.
VeltaRoute 4.0 demonstrated a 31% improvement in route efficiency benchmarks compared to the prior version in controlled testing environments.
The platform has since been adopted by 890 enterprise customers, accounting for roughly 14% of the total customer base.
International revenue accounted for 38% of total revenue in fiscal year 2023, up from 31% in fiscal year 2022.
The EMEA region was the fastest-growing geography, with revenue expanding 34% year-over-year to $164 million.
Asia-Pacific revenue grew 28% to $98 million, fueled by new partnerships with regional logistics conglomerates in Singapore and Japan.
Veltara's North American revenue remained the largest segment at $585 million, representing a 7% increase over the prior year.
The company signed a five-year, $240 million credit facility with a consortium of lenders in September 2023, with an initial draw of $80 million to fund strategic acquisitions.
Customer churn for the SaaS segment remained low at 4.2% on an annualized basis, consistent with the prior year's churn rate of 4.4%.
The company completed the acquisition of Logicraft Analytics, a supply chain intelligence startup, for $95 million in cash during the fourth quarter.
Logicraft contributed $7.2 million in revenue to Veltara's consolidated results for the partial period following the acquisition close.
Management expects Logicraft to be fully integrated into the VeltaRoute platform by the end of fiscal year 2024's second quarter.
For fiscal year 2024, Veltara is guiding for total revenue in the range of $960 million to $985 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 is set at $240 million to $255 million, implying margin expansion relative to fiscal year 2023.
The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal year 2024 to increase to approximately $52 million as it scales its infrastructure.
Management expressed confidence in Veltara's competitive positioning and reiterated its commitment to sustainable, profitable growth over the long term.
Veltara Biologics, a mid-cap biopharmaceutical company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Biologics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, operating entirely on equity financing secured through its 2021 IPO.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Mara Selwyn expressed confidence in the company's pipeline, citing three late-stage clinical trials expected to yield readouts in the first half of 2024.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $412 million, representing a 19% increase compared to the prior year's $346 million.
The company's lead product, VLT-104, a monoclonal antibody targeting autoimmune disorders, generated $198 million in net product sales during the year.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei noted that gross margins improved to 68% in 2023, up from 63% in 2022, driven by manufacturing efficiencies at the Durham facility.
Research and development expenses totaled $134 million, accounting for approximately 33% of total revenue, reflecting the company's commitment to pipeline expansion.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses came in at $87 million, a modest increase from $79 million in the prior fiscal year.
Operating income for the full year was $89 million, compared to $62 million in fiscal year 2022, marking a 44% year-over-year improvement.
Veltara Biologics ended fiscal year 2023 with $275 million in cash and cash equivalents, providing what management described as a robust liquidity position.
The company's headcount grew from 1,420 employees at the start of 2023 to 1,680 employees by year-end, with most new hires concentrated in clinical development and manufacturing.
During the third quarter of 2023, Veltara Biologics entered into a $350 million senior secured term loan facility with a syndicate of institutional lenders led by Meridian Capital Group.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $74 million, or $1.42 per diluted share, for the twelve months ended December 31, 2023.
This represented the company's second consecutive year of GAAP profitability, a milestone Dr. Selwyn highlighted as evidence of the company's operational maturity.
Veltara Biologics did not declare or pay any cash dividends during fiscal year 2023, consistent with its policy of reinvesting earnings into research and commercial activities.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $75 million in October 2023, of which $22 million had been utilized by year-end.
Accounts receivable stood at $68 million at December 31, 2023, representing approximately 60 days of sales outstanding, within the company's targeted range.
Inventory levels increased to $41 million from $29 million at the prior year-end, reflecting pre-commercial stocking in anticipation of a potential new product launch.
The company's second commercial product, VLT-211 for inflammatory bowel disease, received FDA approval in September 2023 and began generating revenue in the fourth quarter.
VLT-211 contributed $14 million in net product sales during its first partial quarter of commercialization, exceeding the internal launch forecast of $9 million.
Chief Commercial Officer Priya Nambiar credited the strong VLT-211 launch performance to prior market development efforts and an established relationships with gastroenterology specialists.
The oncology pipeline candidate VLT-389 completed Phase 2 enrollment in November 2023, with topline data expected in mid-2024.
Veltara Biologics holds a manufacturing collaboration agreement with Celspire Contract Manufacturing, which provides capacity for both VLT-104 and VLT-211 production.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 amounted to $53 million, primarily directed toward expanding fill-finish capacity at the Durham, North Carolina facility.
Depreciation and amortization totaled $31 million for the year, consistent with the prior year level given the timing of asset additions.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 17.3%, benefiting from research and development tax credits and orphan drug designation benefits for VLT-104.
International revenue, primarily from the European Union under a licensing arrangement with Hartkamp Pharma AG, contributed $58 million, or approximately 14% of total revenue.
Hartkamp Pharma AG holds exclusive commercialization rights for VLT-104 across 22 European Union member states under a deal signed in February 2020.
Royalty revenues from the Hartkamp agreement are recognized at a tiered rate ranging from 12% to 18% of net sales, depending on annual sales thresholds.
Veltara Biologics recognized $36 million in milestone payments from Hartkamp during 2023, including a $25 million regulatory milestone triggered by EMA approval of VLT-104 in February 2023.
The company's intangible assets, net of accumulated amortization, were valued at $203 million as of December 31, 2023, primarily comprising acquired in-process research and development.
Dr. Selwyn acknowledged that pipeline execution risk remains the most significant uncertainty facing the company in the medium term.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for total revenue in the range of $490 million to $520 million, implying year-over-year growth of approximately 19% to 26%.
The revenue guidance assumes continued growth in VLT-104 sales, a full year of VLT-211 commercialization, and incremental contributions from new licensing agreements.
Operating expense guidance for 2024 calls for R&D spending between $155 million and $165 million, reflecting accelerated investment in Phase 3 programs for VLT-389.
Management expects SG&A expenses to reach $95 million to $100 million in 2024, consistent with the planned expansion of the commercial field force.
Raymond Osei reiterated that the company's strong cash position provides flexibility to fund operations through at least 2026 without requiring additional external capital.
Veltara Biologics reported that its top three customers accounted for 41% of total revenue in 2023, with specialty pharmacy distributor MedPath Alliance representing 18% alone.
The company operates under two reportable segments: U.S. Commercial Operations and International Licensing, reflecting the structure of its commercial activities.
U.S. Commercial Operations generated segment revenue of $354 million in 2023, while International Licensing contributed $58 million, reconciling to the consolidated total.
No goodwill impairment was recorded during fiscal year 2023, as the estimated fair value of both reporting units substantially exceeded their respective carrying amounts.
The external auditor, Thornfield & Associates LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the company's financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2023.
Veltara Biologics is incorporated in Delaware and its shares are listed on the NASDAQ Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLTR.
The company's stock price closed at $38.72 on December 29, 2023, the last trading day of the fiscal year, implying a market capitalization of approximately $2.0 billion.
Total stockholders' equity as of December 31, 2023 was $681 million, compared to $604 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The increase in stockholders' equity reflects net income of $74 million, stock-based compensation of $28 million, and the net impact of the repurchase program.
Stock-based compensation expense was recognized in accordance with ASC 718 and is included within both R&D and SG&A expense line items.
Veltara Biologics maintained compliance with all financial covenants under its existing agreements throughout fiscal year 2023.
The audit committee of the board is chaired by independent director Helen Frostwick, a former CFO of a publicly traded specialty pharmaceutical company.
During 2023, the company entered into new lease agreements for laboratory and office space in San Diego, California, adding approximately 45,000 square feet of research capacity.
Operating lease right-of-use assets increased to $112 million at year-end from $94 million at the prior year-end, reflecting the new San Diego lease commitments.
The company did not complete any material acquisitions or divestitures during fiscal year 2023, maintaining its focus on organic pipeline development.
Management believes that Veltara Biologics is well-positioned to capitalize on growing demand in the autoimmune and inflammatory disease markets over the next five years.
This annual report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ materially from those projected herein.
Veltrano Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Miriam Caldwell and CTO Douglas Ferris, who together hold a combined 18% equity stake.
Veltrano serves over 3,400 enterprise clients across North America, Europe, and the Asia-Pacific region.
For the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, Veltrano reported total revenues of $842 million, representing a 14% year-over-year increase.
Subscription revenue, which accounts for 78% of total revenue, grew 19% compared to the prior fiscal year.
Professional services revenue declined modestly by 3%, reflecting the company's deliberate shift toward a self-service onboarding model.
Gross profit for the full year reached $541 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 64.2%.
Operating expenses totaled $398 million, including $174 million in research and development and $156 million in sales and marketing.
Veltrano concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term borrowings, with its balance sheet carrying no outstanding debt obligations whatsoever.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $187 million, up from $149 million in fiscal year 2022, reflecting improved operational leverage.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $93 million, or $1.84 per diluted share.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $312 million, providing the company with ample liquidity for near-term strategic initiatives.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $47 million, primarily directed toward data center infrastructure upgrades and security enhancements.
The company generated free cash flow of $221 million, which CFO Renata Osei described as a record level for the organization.
During 2023, Veltrano repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares of common stock at an average price of $38.40 per share.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.12 per share in each quarter of 2023, totaling $0.48 per share annually.
Veltrano's headcount grew to 5,820 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, an increase of 610 employees from the prior year-end.
The company's annual employee attrition rate declined to 11.3%, the lowest figure recorded since Veltrano became a publicly traded entity in 2015.
In September 2023, Veltrano secured a $350 million syndicated term loan from a consortium of eight institutional lenders to fund acquisition activity.
The new VeltraCore 4.0 platform, launched in March 2023, has already been adopted by over 640 enterprise clients within its first nine months.
VeltraCore 4.0 introduces AI-driven demand forecasting modules that reduce inventory holding costs for clients by an average of 22%.
Management expects the platform to contribute approximately $120 million in incremental annual recurring revenue by the end of fiscal year 2025.
In November 2023, Veltrano completed the acquisition of logistics analytics firm Mosaicware Ltd. for a total consideration of $215 million.
The Mosaicware acquisition adds approximately 280 enterprise clients and a team of 190 specialized data engineers to Veltrano's portfolio.
Integration costs associated with Mosaicware are projected to total $18 million over an 18-month period following deal closure.
Veltrano's international revenue represented 31% of total revenue in fiscal 2023, compared to 27% in the prior year.
European revenues grew particularly strongly, rising 28% year-over-year driven by new mandates from German and French manufacturing clients.
The company expanded its London office and opened a new regional hub in Frankfurt during the second half of 2023.
Asia-Pacific revenues grew 17%, led by strong performance in Australia and Singapore, though Japan remained a challenging market.
Veltrano's net revenue retention rate for the year was 118%, indicating strong upsell and cross-sell performance within the existing customer base.
Customer acquisition cost declined by 8% year-over-year as marketing efficiencies improved and inbound demand generation scaled.
The company's average contract value increased to $248,000, up from $211,000 in fiscal 2022, reflecting a shift toward larger enterprise deals.
Miriam Caldwell noted in her shareholder letter that Veltrano is positioned to capture meaningful market share in the $19 billion supply chain software sector.
The company's market share in the North American mid-enterprise segment is estimated at approximately 7.4%, up from 6.1% a year earlier.
Veltrano's pipeline of qualified opportunities grew 32% year-over-year, reaching $1.4 billion in total contract value as of December 31, 2023.
The weighted average remaining contract duration across the active customer base is 2.8 years, providing strong revenue visibility.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet increased to $203 million from $168 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 41 days from 47 days in the prior year, reflecting improved collections processes.
The company maintained an investment-grade credit rating of BBB from Standard & Poor's throughout fiscal year 2023.
Veltrano's board appointed two new independent directors in April 2023, bringing total board membership to nine members.
The audit committee oversaw an independent review of revenue recognition practices, which concluded with no material findings.
Stock-based compensation expense for the year totaled $62 million, representing approximately 7.4% of total revenue.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3%, slightly below the prior year's rate of 22.8%.
Veltrano recognized a one-time tax benefit of $7.2 million related to the release of a valuation allowance on certain deferred tax assets.
Research and development headcount grew to 1,640 engineers, representing approximately 28% of total full-time employees.
The company filed 14 new patent applications during 2023, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 87 issued patents.
Veltrano's cybersecurity infrastructure underwent a comprehensive third-party audit in Q3 2023, resulting in SOC 2 Type II recertification.
The company entered into a strategic partnership with a leading ERP provider to embed VeltraCore functionality natively within enterprise resource planning workflows.
This partnership is expected to open access to an addressable market of approximately 12,000 additional potential enterprise clients globally.
Veltrano published its inaugural Environmental, Social, and Governance report in May 2023, disclosing emissions data for Scopes 1, 2, and 3.
The company committed to achieving net-zero carbon emissions across its own operations by 2040, with interim milestones established for 2030.
Renewable energy now constitutes 61% of total energy consumption across Veltrano's owned and leased facilities.
Employee engagement scores, measured through an annual survey, increased to 78% favorable, up from 71% the prior year.
The company's Board Diversity Policy now requires that at least 40% of board members identify as women or members of underrepresented groups.
For fiscal year 2024, management is guiding for total revenues in the range of $960 million to $985 million.
Adjusted EBITDA margin for fiscal 2024 is expected to expand by approximately 100 to 150 basis points relative to fiscal 2023.
Veltrano anticipates capital expenditures of between $52 million and $58 million in fiscal 2024, largely tied to platform infrastructure scaling.
The company plans to hire approximately 700 net new employees during fiscal 2024, with a concentration in product engineering and customer success.
Management expressed confidence that Veltrano's combination of strong recurring revenue, expanding margins, and accelerating international growth positions the company for sustained value creation.
Douglas Ferris reaffirmed the company's long-term target of achieving a 30% adjusted EBITDA margin within the next three to four fiscal years.
Veltrano Systems Inc. will host its annual investor day on March 14, 2024, at which updated long-range financial targets will be presented to the investment community.
Meridian Crest Technologies, Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud solutions company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Elara Voss and CTO Marcus Delray, who together envisioned a platform-centric approach to industrial workflow automation.
Meridian Crest serves clients across manufacturing, logistics, and energy sectors, with operations in fourteen countries.
The following report covers fiscal year 2023, ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Nambiar presented consolidated results to the board in late January 2024.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.42 billion, representing an 18.3% increase compared to fiscal year 2022.
Subscription-based revenue accounted for $987 million of total revenue, reflecting continued migration of clients toward recurring service agreements.
Professional services and implementation fees contributed an additional $298 million to the annual top line.
Hardware and one-time licensing revenues declined to $135 million as the company actively deprioritized legacy product lines.
Gross profit for the year was $831 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 58.5%.
Operating expenses totaled $612 million, driven primarily by research and development investment and sales force expansion.
Research and development spending rose 24% year-over-year to $218 million, reflecting the company's focus on artificial intelligence integrations.
Sales and marketing expenses reached $241 million, up from $196 million in fiscal 2022, as Meridian Crest entered three new geographic markets.
General and administrative expenses were held to $153 million, a modest 4% increase versus the prior year.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $219 million, compared with $174 million in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $161 million, or $2.38 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA was $294 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 20.7%.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023 stood at $382 million, up from $271 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company generated $310 million in operating cash flow during fiscal 2023, its strongest cash generation since inception.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $87 million, focused largely on data center infrastructure upgrades and leasehold improvements.
Free cash flow was therefore $223 million, providing substantial flexibility for strategic capital allocation.
The board of directors approved a $150 million share repurchase program in March 2023, of which $92 million had been utilized by year-end.
Total headcount grew to 6,840 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, up from 5,910 at the start of the fiscal year.
Attrition rates improved meaningfully, falling to 11.2% from 15.7% in fiscal 2022, which management attributed to enhanced compensation structures.
Customer retention remained a key performance indicator, with net revenue retention reaching 118% for the fiscal year.
Meridian Crest added 340 net new enterprise clients during fiscal 2023, bringing the total enterprise client count to 2,190.
The company's flagship product, the OperaFlow Suite, underwent its largest-ever feature release in Q2 2023, incorporating twelve major capability upgrades.
In partnership with semiconductor manufacturer Vantex Corp., Meridian Crest launched an edge-computing module designed for real-time factory-floor analytics in September 2023.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian Crest carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, operating entirely from internally generated capital.
The company's international revenue mix shifted favorably, with Europe, Middle East, and Africa contributing 31% of total revenue versus 24% in fiscal 2022.
Asia-Pacific revenues grew 42% year-over-year, reaching $198 million, led by strong demand in South Korea and Australia.
Management noted that currency headwinds reduced reported international revenue by approximately $22 million relative to constant-currency results.
The OperaFlow Suite maintained a customer satisfaction score of 87 out of 100, as measured by annual third-party survey.
Meridian Crest's market share in the industrial workflow automation segment expanded to an estimated 14.3%, up from 11.8% the prior year.
Competitor analysis indicated that Meridian Crest outpaced its two nearest rivals in contract renewal rates for the third consecutive year.
Inventory levels for hardware components were deliberately reduced by 18% to $43 million, reflecting a strategic shift away from physical goods.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet grew to $274 million at year-end, signaling strong future recognized revenue from signed contracts.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls were identified during the fiscal year 2023 audit.
In the fourth quarter of 2023, the company finalized a $350 million syndicated term loan facility with a consortium of six institutional lenders.
Board member and independent director Claudia Fehr was appointed chair of the newly constituted Technology and Innovation Committee in October 2023.
The company disclosed no significant litigation or regulatory proceedings that it expected to result in material financial liability.
Earnings per share guidance for fiscal 2024 was set in the range of $2.65 to $2.80 on a diluted basis.
Revenue guidance for fiscal 2024 was established between $1.63 billion and $1.69 billion, implying growth of approximately 15% to 19%.
Management expects adjusted EBITDA margins to expand by 50 to 80 basis points in fiscal 2024, driven by operating leverage.
The company indicated it plans to increase research and development expenditure to approximately $260 million in fiscal 2024.
Meridian Crest's board declared no cash dividend for fiscal 2023, consistent with the company's policy of reinvesting earnings for growth.
The weighted average cost of capital was estimated at 8.4%, used as the primary discount rate in impairment assessments.
Goodwill and intangible assets totaling $534 million were carried on the balance sheet, primarily from the 2021 acquisition of Nexbridge Analytics.
No impairment charges were recorded against goodwill or intangible assets in fiscal 2023, as all reporting units passed annual impairment testing.
Total assets at fiscal year-end were $2.17 billion, and total liabilities were $748 million, yielding shareholders' equity of $1.42 billion.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 26.1%, in line with management's prior guidance of 25% to 27%.
Meridian Crest's stock price appreciated 38% during calendar year 2023, outperforming the broader software sector index by 14 percentage points.
Looking ahead, CEO Elara Voss expressed confidence in the company's competitive positioning and its ability to sustain double-digit growth through disciplined execution.
The annual report on Form 10-K, inclusive of full audited financial statements, was filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 14, 2024.
Veloxterra Industries, a mid-cap aerospace components manufacturer headquartered in Toulouse, France, released its full-year 2023 financial results on March 14, 2024.
The company, founded in 1998 by engineer Marcel Fontaine, specializes in lightweight structural assemblies and thermal management systems for commercial and defense aircraft.
Chief Executive Officer Isabelle Renard opened the earnings call by highlighting the resilience of Veloxterra's order book despite macroeconomic headwinds.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached €1.47 billion, representing a 9.3% increase compared to the €1.34 billion reported in fiscal year 2022.
The commercial aviation segment contributed €892 million to total revenue, driven by renewed orders from major European and Asian carriers.
Defense and government contracts accounted for the remaining €578 million, reflecting sustained procurement activity across NATO member states.
Chief Financial Officer Thierry Marchand noted that gross margins improved by 140 basis points year-over-year, reaching 34.2% for the full year.
Operating income grew to €187 million, up from €154 million in the prior year, as the company benefited from production efficiency gains.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at €241 million, compared to €203 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to shareholders was €119 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of €3.42.
The board of directors approved a dividend of €0.95 per share, payable on April 30, 2024, to shareholders of record as of April 10, 2024.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled €98 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's advanced composites manufacturing facility in Bordeaux.
Veloxterra's research and development expenditure reached €73 million, representing approximately 5% of total revenue and underscoring its commitment to next-generation materials.
The company closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having completed its final bond repayment in November 2023 ahead of the originally scheduled maturity date.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at €312 million, providing Veloxterra with substantial liquidity for strategic initiatives.
Headcount grew modestly to 8,740 full-time employees globally, an increase of approximately 320 positions from the prior year-end.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in engineering and production roles at the company's Toulouse headquarters and Bordeaux manufacturing campus.
Ms. Renard emphasized that the talent acquisition strategy focused on retaining specialized expertise in composite materials and avionics integration.
The Bordeaux facility expansion, which added 18,000 square meters of manufacturing space, was completed on time and within its €55 million budget.
Inventory levels at December 31, 2023, stood at €204 million, reflecting a deliberate build-up in raw material stocks to buffer against titanium supply constraints.
Accounts receivable totaled €231 million, with the average days sales outstanding improving to 57 days from 64 days in the prior year.
The company's free cash flow generation was robust at €143 million, supported by disciplined working capital management throughout the year.
Veloxterra launched two new product lines during fiscal year 2023: the VX-Therm 900 series thermal panels and the ComposiFuse structural bracket system.
As of the end of the first quarter of 2024, Veloxterra carried a €175 million revolving credit facility drawn in full, reflecting financing needs for its accelerated acquisition program.
The VX-Therm 900 series has already secured letters of intent from three Tier-1 aerospace OEMs, totaling an estimated €120 million in potential contract value.
The ComposiFuse bracket system received certification from the European Union Aviation Safety Agency in October 2023, enabling commercial deliveries to begin in early 2024.
Backlog at December 31, 2023, reached a record €3.8 billion, equivalent to approximately 2.6 years of revenue at current run rates.
The backlog growth was supported by a landmark multi-year supply agreement signed with AeroNordic Group in September 2023.
Under the AeroNordic agreement, Veloxterra will supply composite fuselage panels for the AN-720 narrow-body aircraft program through 2031.
Mr. Marchand stated that the AeroNordic contract alone adds approximately €490 million to the backlog and carries operating margin accretion potential.
Looking at geographic performance, European customers represented 61% of total revenue, while Asia-Pacific accounted for 24% and North America contributed 15%.
The Asia-Pacific growth rate of 17% year-over-year was the strongest among all regions, driven by fleet expansion orders from Southeast Asian carriers.
Veloxterra established a regional sales office in Singapore during the second quarter of 2023 to better serve the Asia-Pacific customer base.
The Singapore office, staffed initially with twelve commercial and technical personnel, is expected to expand to thirty employees by the end of 2024.
On the sustainability front, Veloxterra reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 11% versus the 2022 baseline, exceeding its annual target of 8%.
Energy consumption per unit of production declined by 6.4%, attributed to investments in heat recovery systems and LED lighting upgrades across all facilities.
The company submitted its first Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures report in February 2024, receiving positive acknowledgment from institutional investors.
Veloxterra's credit rating was reaffirmed at BBB+ by Fitch Ratings in January 2024, with a stable outlook citing strong cash generation and disciplined capital allocation.
The stable credit outlook recognized the company's conservative financial profile and its historically prudent approach to balance sheet management.
For fiscal year 2024, Veloxterra issued guidance of revenue between €1.60 billion and €1.65 billion, implying growth of approximately 9% to 12% over fiscal year 2023.
Operating margin guidance for 2024 was set in the range of 13.5% to 14.5%, reflecting expected benefits from operational leverage and procurement savings.
Ms. Renard noted that execution of the AeroNordic ramp-up and continued growth in Asia-Pacific are the two most critical drivers of the 2024 outlook.
The company anticipates capital expenditures of €110 million to €120 million in fiscal year 2024, primarily for tooling and automation investments in Bordeaux.
Veloxterra also expects to increase its research and development budget by approximately 15% in 2024, targeting next-generation ceramic matrix composite technologies.
Mr. Marchand highlighted that free cash flow is expected to remain strong in 2024, in the range of €155 million to €175 million.
Potential risks cited in the outlook include raw material price volatility, particularly for titanium and carbon fiber, and potential program delays from OEM customers.
The company has partially hedged its titanium exposure through forward purchase agreements covering approximately 60% of its anticipated 2024 requirements.
Geopolitical risks related to export controls on advanced materials were also flagged as a monitoring area, particularly for defense-oriented product lines.
Veloxterra's audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses were identified in the internal control over financial reporting during the fiscal year 2023 review.
The independent auditor, Deloitte France, issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements as of and for the year ended December 31, 2023.
Ms. Renard concluded the formal presentation by reaffirming Veloxterra's strategic priorities: organic growth, selective acquisitions, and sustainability leadership.
The company indicated it is actively evaluating two bolt-on acquisition targets in the thermal systems space, with a combined estimated enterprise value of approximately €300 million.
Any acquisition completed in 2024 would be funded through a combination of existing cash balances and available credit facilities, according to Mr. Marchand.
Institutional investor response to the full-year results was broadly positive, with Veloxterra's shares rising 4.7% in Paris trading on the day of the announcement.
Sell-side analysts from BNP Paribas Exane and Société Générale both upgraded their price targets following the earnings release, citing stronger-than-expected margin performance.
BNP Paribas Exane set a new twelve-month price target of €68 per share, implying approximately 18% upside from the pre-release closing price.
The annual report and full financial statements are available on Veloxterra's investor relations website and have been filed with the Autorité des marchés financiers.
Veloxterra's next scheduled investor communication is a first-quarter 2024 trading update, anticipated to be released in late April 2024.
Velorax Technologies, Inc. is a mid-cap semiconductor design company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in edge-computing processors for industrial automation.
The following excerpt is drawn from the company's annual report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Hale opened the letter to shareholders by emphasizing the firm's disciplined approach to capital allocation throughout the year.
Velorax reported total revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase compared to the prior year.
Gross profit reached $742 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 40.3%, up from 38.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $498 million, which included $214 million in research and development spending.
The company's net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $187 million, or $2.31 per diluted share.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Sundaram noted that free cash flow generation of $221 million was a record high for the organization.
Velorax ended fiscal year 2023 with a cash and equivalents balance of $634 million, providing ample liquidity for near-term operational needs.
The company's board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $300 million, effective January 2024.
Velorax carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting years of consistent debt retirement.
The industrial automation segment contributed $1.02 billion of total revenue, making it the single largest revenue driver for the year.
Edge-computing processor unit shipments grew by 18% year-over-year, reaching approximately 4.7 million units delivered to customers globally.
Average selling prices in the industrial segment declined modestly by 2.1% due to competitive pricing pressure in the Asian markets.
The consumer robotics segment, which Velorax entered in 2021, generated $420 million in revenue, growing 31% versus the prior period.
Chief Operating Officer David Lenz highlighted successful integration of the Pinnacle Semiconductor acquisition completed in March 2023.
The Pinnacle acquisition added approximately 340 engineers to Velorax's global workforce, bringing total headcount to 6,820 employees by year-end.
Velorax launched its flagship VX-9000 processor family in Q2 2023, targeting high-throughput manufacturing environments requiring real-time decision-making.
The VX-9000 family achieved design wins at 14 Tier-1 industrial clients within its first six months of commercial availability.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $98 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's chip design infrastructure and testing facilities.
During fiscal year 2023, Velorax secured a $450 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of six financial institutions.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 14.7% from 11.9% in the prior year, driven by stronger operating leverage.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $289 million, representing approximately 57 days of supply, within the company's target range of 50–65 days.
Velorax's international revenue accounted for 58% of total revenues, with the Asia-Pacific region representing the largest geographic segment at 34%.
The European market contributed $312 million in revenue, growing 9.3% as demand for smart factory solutions accelerated across Germany and the Netherlands.
Priya Sundaram highlighted that the effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 18.2%, benefiting from research tax credits in the United States.
Deferred revenue at year-end increased to $67 million, reflecting multi-year software licensing agreements signed with key industrial clients.
The company's accounts receivable days outstanding decreased to 44 days from 51 days in the prior year, indicating improved collections performance.
Velorax maintained a current ratio of 3.1x as of December 31, 2023, signaling strong short-term financial health.
Marcus Hale emphasized in management commentary that supply chain resilience was a strategic priority achieved through dual-sourcing agreements with six foundry partners.
The company added 12 new patents during fiscal 2023, bringing its cumulative portfolio to 318 issued patents across 21 countries.
Velorax's sales and marketing expenses totaled $143 million, reflecting investments in direct enterprise sales teams and digital channel expansion.
General and administrative expenses were $141 million, slightly elevated due to integration costs associated with the Pinnacle acquisition.
The company recorded a one-time restructuring charge of $22 million in Q3 2023 related to facility consolidation in Portland and Singapore.
Excluding this restructuring charge and acquisition-related amortization, adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $398 million.
The adjusted EBITDA margin of 21.6% compared favorably to the industry peer median of 18.4%, as cited in third-party semiconductor benchmarking data.
Velorax's stock price appreciated 27% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index by approximately 9 percentage points.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share, consistent with the prior year's dividend level.
David Lenz outlined plans to expand manufacturing partnerships in Vietnam and Malaysia to diversify production away from Taiwan-concentrated supply chains.
The company expects fiscal year 2024 revenues in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.12 billion, representing projected growth of 11–15%.
Gross margin guidance for 2024 was set at 41–43%, assuming stable wafer costs and continued mix shift toward higher-margin software-embedded products.
Velorax anticipates research and development spending to increase to approximately $240 million in fiscal 2024 as the company accelerates its next-generation VX-10000 design cycle.
The VX-10000 platform is targeted for sampling in Q3 2024 and full commercial release in Q1 2025, subject to successful qualification by foundry partners.
Management guided for capital expenditures of $115–$125 million in 2024, reflecting continued investment in design automation software and lab infrastructure.
The company disclosed no material pending litigation as of the filing date, consistent with its history of resolving intellectual property disputes through licensing settlements.
Velorax's auditors, Grant & Whitmore LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
The audit committee, chaired by independent director Sandra Kimura, conducted its annual review of internal controls and found no material weaknesses.
Velorax maintained compliance with all financial covenants associated with its existing credit arrangements throughout the fiscal year.
The company's environmental, social, and governance report, published concurrently with this annual report, detailed a 14% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions.
Velorax achieved a score of 72 out of 100 in the 2023 CDP Climate Disclosure assessment, an improvement from 61 the prior year.
The workforce diversity metrics showed that women represented 34% of total employees and 27% of senior leadership roles as of year-end.
Employee attrition declined to 8.2% in fiscal 2023 from 11.6% in fiscal 2022, reflecting improvements in compensation benchmarking and career development programs.
Velorax invested $18 million in employee training and development initiatives during the year, equating to approximately $2,640 per full-time equivalent.
The company's customer satisfaction index, measured through annual enterprise surveys, reached 88 out of 100, the highest recorded score in the firm's history.
Looking ahead, Marcus Hale expressed confidence that continued innovation in AI-embedded edge processors would sustain Velorax's competitive differentiation through the next market cycle.
The Pinnacle integration is expected to be fully complete by mid-2024, at which point management anticipates realizing $35 million in annual run-rate cost synergies.
Priya Sundaram confirmed that the company's capital structure priorities for 2024 include maintaining investment-grade credit metrics and opportunistic share repurchases.
Velorax's weighted average cost of capital was estimated at 9.3% for purposes of impairment testing of goodwill and indefinite-lived intangible assets.
Goodwill recorded on the balance sheet totaled $538 million following the Pinnacle acquisition, subject to annual impairment assessment.
Management confirmed that no goodwill impairment was identified during the fiscal 2023 annual assessment, as all reporting units exceeded their carrying values.
The audit committee reviewed and approved all related-party transactions during the year, none of which exceeded $500,000 individually.
Velorax's board composition includes nine directors, of whom seven are classified as independent under applicable Nasdaq listing standards.
The company remains committed to transparent financial reporting and will continue to engage proactively with investors, analysts, and regulators in the year ahead.
This excerpt should be read in conjunction with the full consolidated financial statements and accompanying notes included in the complete annual report on Form 10-K.
Veltora Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based logistics management platforms.
The company was founded in 2011 by CEO Adriana Quelsh and CTO Marcus Fendrick, both veterans of the supply chain technology sector.
Veltora Systems reported total revenue of $412 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14% increase over the prior year.
The growth was driven primarily by strong adoption of the company's flagship product, VeltroCore, across North American distribution networks.
Gross margins expanded by 220 basis points year-over-year, reaching 68.4% for the full fiscal year.
Operating expenses totaled $198 million, reflecting continued investments in research and development as well as sales and marketing initiatives.
The company's headcount grew to 2,847 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, up from 2,510 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Veltora Systems entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its remaining credit facilities in late 2022.
CFO Brennan Okafor noted that this clean balance sheet position allowed management to pursue strategic investments without the burden of interest expense.
Capital expenditures for the year were $34 million, focused largely on expanding data center infrastructure in the southeastern United States.
The company also invested $61 million in research and development during fiscal year 2023, an increase of 22% compared to fiscal year 2022.
Adriana Quelsh emphasized during the annual earnings call that innovation in AI-assisted routing algorithms remains central to Veltora's competitive strategy.
The VeltroCore platform achieved a net promoter score of 72 in the most recent customer satisfaction survey, up from 64 in the prior year.
International revenue, primarily from European logistics operators, accounted for 19% of total revenue, or approximately $78 million.
The company signed 143 new enterprise contracts during fiscal year 2023, with an average contract value of $1.2 million.
Renewal rates for existing customers remained strong at 91%, underscoring the platform's stickiness within client operations.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet increased to $87 million as of December 31, 2023, reflecting growing customer prepayments for multi-year licenses.
To fund an accelerated product expansion initiative, Veltora Systems secured a $175 million revolving credit facility with First Meridian Bank in March 2023.
The new facility carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 145 basis points, with a maturity date in March 2028.
Management drew $90 million against this credit facility by year-end to support the acquisition of Canadian routing software startup NovePath Inc.
The NovePath acquisition, which closed in September 2023, added approximately 180 employees and a portfolio of patented route-optimization algorithms.
Marcus Fendrick stated that integrating NovePath's intellectual property into VeltroCore is expected to reduce average customer delivery delays by 17%.
Veltora's adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $124 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 30.1%.
This compares favorably to the prior year adjusted EBITDA of $98 million and an adjusted EBITDA margin of 27.2%.
The company declared a special cash dividend of $0.45 per share in November 2023, payable to shareholders of record as of December 15, 2023.
Total cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $156 million, down from $201 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The decline in cash was primarily attributable to the NovePath acquisition payment and increased capital expenditure activity.
Accounts receivable increased by $18 million versus the prior year, consistent with the growth in enterprise contract signings.
Days sales outstanding remained stable at approximately 52 days, which management considers within its target operating range.
Veltora's stock appreciated 38% on a total return basis during calendar year 2023, outperforming the S&P Software Index by 11 percentage points.
Institutional ownership of Veltora shares rose to 74% by year-end, reflecting growing confidence among long-term investment managers.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $50 million, to be executed over a 24-month period beginning in January 2024.
Brennan Okafor confirmed that the repurchase program would be funded from operating cash flows and would not require additional borrowings.
Veltora's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, slightly below the prior year rate of 22.1%.
The lower effective tax rate was partly due to increased R&D tax credits associated with qualifying development activities in the United States.
The company operates two primary reportable segments: Enterprise Solutions, which generated $318 million in revenue, and Professional Services, which generated $94 million.
Enterprise Solutions segment operating income was $112 million, while Professional Services contributed $29 million in segment operating income.
Headcount in the Professional Services segment increased by 12% during the year as Veltora scaled its implementation consulting capabilities.
Veltora's customer base now spans 38 countries, with significant concentration in the United States, Germany, Canada, and Australia.
The company launched two major product updates in fiscal year 2023: VeltroCore 7.0 in April and VeltroEdge, a new real-time visibility module, in October.
VeltroEdge attracted 67 pilot customers within its first 90 days of general availability, exceeding the internal target of 50 pilot engagements.
Adriana Quelsh cited VeltroEdge as a key differentiator expected to drive upsell opportunities across the existing customer base in fiscal year 2024.
The company's partner ecosystem grew to 214 certified implementation partners globally, up from 178 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Veltora entered into a co-selling agreement with a major enterprise resource planning vendor, expected to expand pipeline access in the mid-market segment.
Operating cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $118 million, reflecting strong collections performance and disciplined working capital management.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $84 million for fiscal year 2023.
This free cash flow figure represents a conversion rate of approximately 68% relative to adjusted EBITDA.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $465 million to $480 million.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 30% to 32%, reflecting expected operating leverage as the NovePath integration matures.
Brennan Okafor indicated that capital expenditures in fiscal year 2024 are expected to decline to approximately $28 million as the data center expansion winds down.
The company anticipates headcount growth of 8% to 10% in fiscal year 2024, focused primarily on sales, customer success, and product development roles.
Veltora also plans to pursue additional tuck-in acquisitions in fiscal year 2024, targeting companies with revenues between $10 million and $50 million.
Any acquisition activity will be financed through a combination of existing cash balances and available capacity under the revolving credit facility.
The audit committee completed its annual review without identifying any material weaknesses in the company's internal controls over financial reporting.
Veltora's independent auditor, Harwick & Plaine LLP, issued an unqualified opinion on the company's fiscal year 2023 financial statements.
The company maintains directors' and officers' liability insurance coverage of $75 million, renewed annually by the risk management team.
Veltora Systems will hold its annual shareholder meeting on April 22, 2024, at which time three board members will stand for re-election.
Management expressed confidence that fiscal year 2024 will be another year of profitable growth, underpinned by strong customer retention and expanding product capabilities.
Meridian Castings & Alloys Corporation (MCAC) is pleased to present its consolidated annual report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
MCAC operates as a mid-tier specialty metals manufacturer headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, serving the aerospace, automotive, and industrial equipment sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Harold Fenwick opened the report by acknowledging the challenging macroeconomic environment that defined much of the fiscal year.
Despite headwinds from rising energy costs and supply chain disruptions, the company demonstrated notable resilience across its core business segments.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 7.2 percent increase over the prior year's figure of $1.72 billion.
MCAC entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term financial obligations, carrying no outstanding debt on its balance sheet as of January 1, 2023.
The aerospace segment contributed $712 million in revenue, accounting for approximately 38.7 percent of total consolidated sales.
Chief Financial Officer Darlene Howarth noted that gross margins improved by 140 basis points year-over-year, reaching 29.3 percent.
The automotive segment experienced particularly strong demand driven by the ongoing electrification of vehicle platforms among MCAC's top-tier customers.
Operating income for the full year was reported at $218 million, compared to $193 million in fiscal year 2022.
MCAC employed approximately 6,800 full-time workers across its nine manufacturing facilities as of the close of fiscal year 2023.
Capital expenditures totaled $134 million during the year, focused primarily on upgrading furnace technology at the Dayton and Pittsburgh plants.
The industrial equipment segment posted revenue of $419 million, growing at a faster rate than any other division at 11.4 percent year-over-year.
Research and development spending increased to $47 million, reflecting the company's commitment to next-generation titanium alloy formulations.
MCAC's board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
To fund the accelerated capital expansion program announced in the third quarter, MCAC secured a $350 million term loan facility from a syndicate of institutional lenders during fiscal year 2023.
The new titanium alloy product line, designated TA-900 series, received full qualification from three major aerospace primes during the fourth quarter.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $287 million, reflecting a deliberate build in strategic raw material stockpiles ahead of anticipated price increases.
MCAC's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 23.1 percent, slightly above the prior year rate of 22.4 percent.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $163 million, yielding earnings per diluted share of $3.47.
The company's cash and cash equivalents position at year-end was $221 million, providing substantial liquidity for near-term operational needs.
Chief Operating Officer Marcus Delacroix highlighted the successful ramp-up of the new Columbus facility, which reached full production capacity in September 2023.
MCAC completed the divestiture of its non-core wire drawing business unit for proceeds of $58 million in the second quarter.
The divestiture proceeds were reinvested into the company's core alloy casting operations, consistent with the long-term strategic plan.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $109 million for the year, remaining flat as a percentage of revenue relative to fiscal year 2022.
MCAC's order backlog at December 31, 2023, stood at a record $2.1 billion, providing strong visibility into revenue for the coming fiscal year.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of its common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $41.60 per share.
Depreciation and amortization expense was $88 million for the year, consistent with the elevated asset base following prior years' capital investment programs.
MCAC maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poors, reflecting the strength of its operating cash flows.
Free cash flow for the year was $97 million after accounting for capital expenditures and working capital changes.
The Pittsburgh facility achieved ISO 9001:2015 recertification in May 2023, reinforcing MCAC's quality management credentials with key customers.
Headcount in the aerospace segment increased by 320 employees during the year to support the production ramp of the TA-900 series.
MCAC's pension obligation increased modestly to $412 million at year-end, driven by revised actuarial assumptions regarding mortality rates.
The company entered into a five-year supply agreement with Vantage Aerospace Systems, securing approximately $180 million in annual committed volumes.
Environmental compliance expenditures totaled $19 million during the year, largely attributable to upgrades at the Cleveland smelting operation.
For fiscal year 2024, management is providing revenue guidance in the range of $1.95 billion to $2.05 billion, reflecting continued organic growth.
The guidance assumes stable nickel and titanium input prices at levels approximating the fourth-quarter 2023 averages.
MCAC's debt-to-equity ratio at December 31, 2023, was 0.41, reflecting the impact of the term loan facility secured during the year.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 12.8 percent in fiscal year 2023, up from 11.3 percent in fiscal year 2022.
Ms. Howarth emphasized that the improved ROIC reflects both stronger operating earnings and disciplined capital allocation throughout the organization.
MCAC does not anticipate any significant facility closures or workforce reductions in fiscal year 2024 based on current demand projections.
The company's top ten customers accounted for 61 percent of total revenue, a slight decrease from 64 percent in fiscal year 2022, indicating improved customer diversification.
MCAC participates in the London Metal Exchange's nickel hedging program, covering approximately 40 percent of its anticipated nickel requirements for 2024.
Foreign exchange exposure remains limited, as approximately 89 percent of MCAC's revenues are denominated in U.S. dollars.
The annual report has been audited by Deloitte & Touche LLP, which issued an unqualified audit opinion on both the financial statements and internal controls.
Mr. Fenwick concluded his remarks by thanking MCAC's workforce for their dedication during a period of significant operational change.
The board of directors, chaired by independent director Patricia Okonkwo, consists of nine members with diverse backgrounds in manufacturing, finance, and technology.
MCAC's common shares are traded on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol MCAC.
The company plans to host its annual investor day on February 28, 2024, at its Columbus headquarters, where management will present updated strategic objectives.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 was $539 million, rising from $465 million in the prior year on the strength of volume gains and margin improvement.
The automotive segment's operating margin of 17.2 percent exceeded the company's blended operating margin, contributing disproportionately to total earnings.
MCAC's long-term incentive compensation program, revised in 2022, aligns executive rewards with three-year cumulative ROIC targets.
Total shareholder return for fiscal year 2023 was 18.4 percent, outperforming the S&P Metals & Mining index by approximately 600 basis points.
The company remains committed to its sustainability targets, including a 30 percent reduction in scope 1 and scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 2030.
This annual report contains forward-looking statements that are subject to risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ materially from those expressed or implied herein.
Meridian Packaging Solutions, Inc. is a mid-cap specialty materials company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, focused on sustainable packaging for the food and beverage sector.
The company was founded in 2003 by current Executive Chairman Gerald Forsythe and has grown from a regional supplier into a nationally recognized packaging innovator.
Fiscal year 2023 marked the company's twentieth anniversary and delivered what management described as the strongest operational results in Meridian's history.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Holloway presented the annual results to shareholders at the company's general meeting held on March 14, 2024.
Total net revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the $1.64 billion recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The revenue growth was driven primarily by expanding contracts with three major national grocery chains and increased penetration in the ready-to-eat meal segment.
Gross profit for the period came in at $612 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 33.3%, up from 31.7% in the prior year.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $389 million, with selling, general, and administrative expenses comprising the largest single component at $214 million.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $387 million, compared with $331 million in fiscal year 2022, reflecting a year-over-year improvement of approximately 16.9%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $198 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.42, versus $2.87 per diluted share in the prior year.
The company's effective tax rate for the fiscal year was 23.1%, slightly below management's guidance range of 24% to 26%, due to favorable R&D tax credits.
Capital expenditures totaled $147 million in fiscal year 2023, reflecting continued investment in high-speed lamination lines at the company's two largest production facilities.
Free cash flow generation remained robust at $240 million, providing ample liquidity to fund both organic growth initiatives and the company's dividend program.
Meridian's board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.38 per share throughout fiscal year 2023, representing aggregate annual dividend payments of approximately $88 million.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian Packaging Solutions carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired the final tranche of its 2019 term loan in September 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $312 million, up from $274 million at the close of fiscal year 2022, reflecting healthy operational cash conversion.
The current ratio improved to 2.4x from 2.1x in the prior year, and the quick ratio strengthened to 1.8x, underscoring the company's solid short-term liquidity position.

Inventory levels at year-end were $183 million, modestly higher than the $171 million recorded at the end of fiscal year 2022, reflecting planned safety-stock increases ahead of new contract launches.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 38 days from 43 days in the prior year, attributed to tightened credit policies implemented in the second quarter.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delray noted that working capital discipline was a top operational priority throughout the year and that the improvements were expected to be sustainable.
Meridian added 640 full-time employees during fiscal year 2023, bringing total headcount to approximately 7,200 employees across 14 manufacturing and distribution facilities.
The company's largest facility, the 820,000-square-foot Dayton plant, completed a $62 million capacity expansion in October 2023, adding roughly 180,000 units of daily output.
Sustainability initiatives remained central to Meridian's corporate strategy, with the company reporting that 74% of its packaging products now meet or exceed third-party recyclability standards.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 11% year-over-year, exceeding its internal target of an 8% reduction for the calendar year.
In August 2023, Meridian entered into a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five banks, replacing a smaller $200 million facility that had been in place since 2020.
The new revolving credit facility carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points and matures in August 2028, providing significant financial flexibility.
Management emphasized that the credit facility was intended primarily as a liquidity backstop and operational buffer rather than as a source of funding for acquisitions.
During fiscal year 2023, Meridian launched four new product lines under its EcoFlex brand, targeting the chilled ready-meal and single-serve beverage markets.
The EcoFlex product lines collectively generated $47 million in revenue during their first partial year of commercial availability, ahead of the $35 million internal forecast.
Chief Commercial Officer Priya Nanthakumar attributed the EcoFlex outperformance to accelerated retailer adoption and favorable consumer response in third-party taste and sustainability surveys.
Meridian's market share in the flexible packaging sub-segment increased to an estimated 8.7% in 2023, up from 7.9% in 2022, according to independent industry research firm PackTrack Analytics.
The company's rigid packaging segment also performed well, with revenues growing 9.4% to $441 million, supported by strong demand from contract food-service clients.
Research and development expenditure for fiscal year 2023 was $58 million, representing approximately 3.2% of total net revenue, an increase from 2.9% in the prior year.
Meridian holds 37 active patents related to barrier coating technologies, moisture-resistant substrates, and compostable laminate structures as of the end of fiscal year 2023.
The company filed 11 new patent applications during the year and received formal grants on 6 previously filed applications, reflecting the ongoing productivity of its R&D pipeline.

Geographic revenue remained concentrated in North America, which accounted for 91% of total net revenue, with the remaining 9% derived from export sales to Canada, Mexico, and select European markets.

Management has indicated an intention to grow international revenue to represent 15% of total net revenue by fiscal year 2026, with a particular focus on expanding into the United Kingdom and Germany.
To support this international ambition, the company announced a partnership agreement with packaging distributor Alterra GmbH, based in Munich, during the fourth quarter of 2023.
The Alterra partnership is expected to generate incremental revenue of approximately $30 million to $40 million in fiscal year 2024, with full contribution anticipated by fiscal year 2025.

Meridian's pension obligation stood at $94 million at year-end, with plan assets of $88 million, resulting in a net underfunded status of $6 million, a slight improvement from the prior year's $9 million gap.
The company made a discretionary pension contribution of $7 million in December 2023 to reduce the funding shortfall and improve actuarial stability ahead of the scheduled triennial review.
Stock-based compensation expense for fiscal year 2023 was $22 million, consistent with the $21 million recorded in the prior year, and is excluded from the company's adjusted EBITDA calculation.
Meridian repurchased 1.2 million shares during fiscal year 2023 under its standing buyback authorization, at an average price of $54.70 per share, for a total outlay of approximately $65.6 million.
At year-end, approximately $84 million remained available under the company's $150 million share repurchase authorization, which runs through December 2025.

The company's total return to shareholders in fiscal year 2023, combining dividends and share buybacks, amounted to approximately $154 million, or roughly 78 cents of every free cash flow dollar generated.
Looking ahead, management issued initial guidance for fiscal year 2024, projecting net revenue in the range of $1.97 billion to $2.03 billion, implying organic growth of approximately 7% to 10%.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is projected to fall in the range of $410 million to $430 million, consistent with the company's medium-term margin expansion objectives.

CEO Sandra Holloway stated that Meridian enters fiscal year 2024 with strong commercial momentum, a healthy balance sheet, and a product portfolio well positioned for the evolving packaging landscape.

The company flagged three principal risks to its fiscal year 2024 guidance: raw material cost volatility, potential disruption from labor negotiations at two of its manufacturing sites, and macroeconomic softening in food-service demand.
Resin prices, a key raw material input for Meridian, declined approximately 7% on a weighted-average basis during fiscal year 2023, providing a meaningful tailwind to gross margin.

The company has hedging contracts covering approximately 40% of its anticipated resin purchases for the first half of fiscal year 2024, which management believes provides adequate near-term cost visibility.

Meridian's largest customer, a national grocery chain referred to as Customer A for confidentiality purposes, accounted for 14.2% of total net revenue in fiscal year 2023, up from 13.6% in the prior year.
No other single customer accounted for more than 9% of total net revenue during fiscal year 2023, limiting revenue concentration risk relative to many industry peers.
The company's fixed-price supply agreements with Customer A run through mid-2025 and include volume escalation clauses that management expects will support continued growth in that relationship.
Meridian's board of directors underwent a governance refresh in 2023, with two new independent directors added: Dr. Lena Okafor, a sustainability expert, and Thomas Vance, a former investment banker.
