The audit committee, chaired by director Helen Richfield, completed its annual review of internal controls and found no material weaknesses in the company's financial reporting infrastructure.
Total assets on Meridian's balance sheet at December 31, 2023, stood at $2.31 billion, compared with $2.18 billion at the close of the prior year.
Total liabilities were $987 million at year-end, down from $1.04 billion at the end of fiscal year 2022, reflecting the debt retirement and disciplined liability management.
Shareholders' equity therefore increased to $1.32 billion from $1.14 billion, reinforcing the company's strengthened net asset position and long-term financial resilience.
Deferred revenue related to certain multi-year packaging supply contracts was $31 million at year-end, expected to be recognized over the following 18 to 24 months.
Depreciation and amortization expense for fiscal year 2023 was $149 million, of which $112 million related to property, plant, and equipment and $37 million to acquired intangibles.
The company's goodwill balance remained unchanged at $203 million, as no acquisitions or impairment charges were recorded during fiscal year 2023.
Meridian's external auditors, the firm of Calloway & Breck LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.

Management expressed confidence that the company's diversified customer base, innovation pipeline, and operational efficiency gains position Meridian Packaging Solutions for sustained long-term value creation.

The full audited financial statements, including all notes and schedules, are available in the company's Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on March 28, 2024.
Veltrix Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation and robotics firm headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company, founded in 2004 by CEO Margaret Holloway, has grown from a regional machine-parts supplier into a globally recognized automation solutions provider.
Veltrix Dynamics serves clients across the automotive, aerospace, and consumer electronics manufacturing sectors.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei presented the consolidated financial results to the board of directors on February 14, 2024.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a year-over-year increase of 12.3 percent.
The company attributed much of this growth to robust demand in North American automotive assembly automation contracts.
International revenues, primarily from European and Southeast Asian markets, accounted for 38 percent of total revenue.
Gross profit margin improved to 41.7 percent, up from 39.2 percent in fiscal year 2022, driven by favorable input cost trends.
Operating income climbed to $312 million, compared with $271 million in the prior fiscal year.
Research and development expenditures totaled $148 million, reflecting the company's continued commitment to next-generation robotics platforms.
Veltrix Dynamics closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final bond obligation in October 2023.
The company's balance sheet showed total assets of $2.6 billion and shareholders' equity of $1.1 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents stood at $387 million, providing ample liquidity for near-term operational requirements.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $194 million, focused primarily on expanding the Columbus manufacturing campus.
The board approved a quarterly dividend of $0.32 per share, unchanged from the prior year's distribution schedule.
Earnings per share on a diluted basis came in at $4.71, exceeding the analyst consensus estimate of $4.52.
Veltrix Dynamics employed 9,400 full-time staff globally at the close of fiscal year 2023, an increase of 620 employees from the prior year.
The headcount expansion was concentrated in the company's newly opened engineering center in Warsaw, Poland.
COO Priya Nambiar highlighted the successful integration of three software acquisitions completed during the year.
Those acquisitions collectively contributed $67 million in incremental revenue and strengthened the company's AI-driven process optimization portfolio.
During the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023, Veltrix Dynamics secured a $275 million syndicated revolving credit facility to support strategic growth initiatives.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $441 million, a 14.8 percent improvement over fiscal year 2022.
The effective tax rate for the year was 22.4 percent, modestly lower than the 23.1 percent recorded in the prior period.
Inventory levels at year-end were $298 million, reflecting deliberate stock build-up in anticipation of a major automotive contract delivery in early 2024.
Accounts receivable totaled $224 million, with days sales outstanding at 44 days, consistent with the prior year's collection performance.
The company reported no material off-balance-sheet arrangements or contingent liabilities that would alter the reported financial position.
Veltrix Dynamics launched its flagship ProMotion X7 robotic arm series in September 2023, targeting high-precision assembly applications.
Early customer adoption of the ProMotion X7 exceeded internal forecasts, with 1,200 units ordered within the first quarter of availability.
The company's backlog stood at $2.1 billion as of December 31, 2023, representing approximately 14 months of revenue coverage.
Management expressed confidence in converting the backlog given current production capacity and supplier stability.
Chief Marketing Officer Dana Ferreira noted that brand awareness in the Asia-Pacific region improved significantly following a targeted campaign launched in mid-2023.
Veltrix Dynamics expanded its strategic partnership with Komatsu Manufacturing to co-develop modular assembly cells for the heavy equipment sector.
The partnership is expected to generate joint revenues of approximately $90 million over the next three years.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives were a central theme of the annual report, with the company reducing Scope 1 emissions by 18 percent year-over-year.
The company installed 4.2 megawatts of rooftop solar capacity at its Columbus facility, offsetting an estimated 15 percent of site energy consumption.
Water usage intensity fell by 11 percent at manufacturing sites, driven by closed-loop cooling system upgrades completed in the second quarter.
Veltrix Dynamics was included in the Dow Jones Sustainability North America Index for the second consecutive year.
The company's diversity metrics showed that 34 percent of senior management roles were held by women, up from 29 percent in fiscal year 2022.
Raymond Osei noted that operating cash flow of $498 million represented the highest level in the company's history.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, reached $304 million for fiscal year 2023.
The company repurchased $120 million of its own shares during the year under a previously authorized buyback program.
Shares outstanding on a diluted basis decreased to approximately 93.6 million as a result of the buyback activity.
Margaret Holloway reiterated the company's long-term target of achieving $2.5 billion in annual revenue by fiscal year 2026.
Management issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.12 billion, implying growth of 11 to 15 percent.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 17.5 to 18.5 percent, reflecting anticipated efficiency gains from automation of internal processes.
The company intends to invest an additional $60 million in its Warsaw engineering center over the next two fiscal years.
Veltrix Dynamics also announced a plan to establish a new regional headquarters in Singapore to better serve Southeast Asian clients.
The Singapore office is expected to be operational by mid-2025 and will initially house approximately 150 employees.
Priya Nambiar outlined a roadmap for launching two new product lines in fiscal 2024, targeting collaborative robotics and autonomous mobile robot segments.
The company estimates that these new segments could collectively represent a $6 billion addressable market by 2027.
Veltrix Dynamics retained Deloitte & Touche as its independent auditor, who issued an unqualified opinion on the fiscal year 2023 financial statements.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the audit.
General counsel Thomas Achebe reported that two pending patent disputes were settled favorably during the year, resulting in a net litigation recovery of $12 million.
The settlement amounts were recorded as other income in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023.
Overall, management characterized fiscal year 2023 as a landmark year for Veltrix Dynamics, establishing a strong foundation for sustained growth.
The full annual report, including consolidated financial statements and detailed footnotes, is available on the Veltrix Dynamics investor relations website.
Shareholders of record as of March 1, 2024 will be eligible to vote at the annual general meeting scheduled for April 22, 2024.
Veltora Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation and robotics firm headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the letter to shareholders with an optimistic tone, citing robust demand across the company's core manufacturing segments.
Veltora Dynamics reported total consolidated revenue of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the $1.64 billion recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The company's gross profit margin expanded to 38.7%, up from 35.2% in the prior year, driven by favorable product mix and supply chain efficiencies.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Ashby attributed the margin improvement to the successful renegotiation of key supplier contracts completed in the second quarter.
Operating income for the year reached $287 million, compared to $214 million in fiscal 2022, reflecting disciplined cost management across all business units.
The Precision Robotics segment, Veltora's largest division by revenue, generated $742 million in net sales, a 15.1% year-over-year increase.
The Industrial Sensing segment posted $498 million in revenue, while the Legacy Automation segment contributed the remaining $600 million to total net sales.
Research and development expenditure rose to $163 million, representing approximately 8.9% of total revenue, as the company continued to invest in next-generation motion control technologies.
Veltora Dynamics closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final outstanding bond tranche in the third quarter.
The company's balance sheet as of December 31, 2023 showed total assets of $2.31 billion and shareholders' equity of $1.19 billion.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $384 million, providing what management described as a substantial liquidity buffer.
Capital expenditures totaled $211 million during the year, primarily allocated toward expanding the Columbus manufacturing campus and upgrading automated assembly lines.
Headcount across Veltora's global operations grew to 9,400 full-time employees by year-end, an increase of approximately 800 employees from the prior year.
The company completed the acquisition of Stuttgart-based sensor manufacturer Kreuzfeld GmbH in April 2023, adding roughly 340 employees and proprietary LiDAR technology to the portfolio.
Integration of Kreuzfeld's engineering teams has proceeded ahead of schedule, with combined product development synergies already contributing an estimated $22 million in annualized cost savings.
Veltora's board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, maintaining the same rate as the previous four consecutive quarters.
During fiscal 2023, the company repurchased approximately 3.1 million shares at an average price of $47.60, returning roughly $148 million of capital to shareholders.

The Precision Robotics segment launched four new product lines during the year, including the VX-900 series collaborative robot arms, which received strong initial reception from automotive OEM customers.

To finance strategic expansion initiatives, Veltora Dynamics entered into a $350 million syndicated revolving credit facility in the fourth quarter of 2023, underwritten by a consortium of five major banks.
The VX-900 series achieved design-win status with three Tier-1 automotive suppliers within six months of its commercial introduction, validating the product's competitive positioning.
Revenue from international markets, primarily Europe and Southeast Asia, accounted for 34% of total consolidated revenue, up from 28% in the prior fiscal year.

Europe remained the largest international region, contributing $312 million in revenue, largely supported by increased adoption of automation solutions in German and Scandinavian manufacturing facilities.
The Industrial Sensing segment experienced some margin pressure in the second half of the year due to elevated raw material costs for specialty polymers and rare-earth compounds.
Management indicated that targeted price adjustments implemented in November 2023 are expected to partially offset this input cost headwind in the first half of fiscal 2024.
Veltora's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4%, modestly below the prior year rate of 23.1%, reflecting incremental benefits from R&D tax credits and foreign tax planning.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $198 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.09 based on a weighted average diluted share count of approximately 94.7 million.
This represents a 27.7% increase in diluted EPS compared to the $1.64 reported in fiscal 2022, significantly outpacing the broader S&P Industrials index performance.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 14.2%, exceeding its internally established benchmark of 12% for the third consecutive year.
Inventory levels at December 31, 2023 totaled $278 million, reflecting a days-inventory-outstanding metric of 55 days, down from 63 days in the prior year.
The reduction in inventory days was credited to demand planning improvements and deeper integration with key customers' enterprise resource planning systems.
Accounts receivable stood at $241 million, with a days-sales-outstanding of 48 days, broadly consistent with the 47-day figure recorded at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company disclosed no material off-balance-sheet arrangements and no significant contingent liabilities beyond customary product warranty reserves.
Total warranty reserve at year-end amounted to $31 million, up slightly from $27 million in the prior year, reflecting the expanded installed base of newly launched products.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Ferreira emphasized in her operational review that on-time delivery performance improved to 94.8%, compared to 91.3% in fiscal 2022.
Customer satisfaction scores, as measured by the company's annual survey, rose to an average of 8.3 out of 10 across all major product lines.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives were a prominent theme in the annual report, with Veltora committing to a 40% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 2030.
The company installed 4.2 megawatts of rooftop solar capacity at its Columbus campus during 2023, offsetting an estimated 11% of that facility's electricity consumption.
Employee engagement survey results indicated that 78% of staff would recommend Veltora as a great place to work, representing a five-percentage-point improvement over the prior year.
The board of directors added two new independent directors during the year, including Dr. Elaine Cho, a technology strategy expert, and former Senator Marcus Webb, who joined the audit committee.
Veltora's audit committee engaged Prentiss & Hartwell LLP as external auditors for the eighth consecutive year, and the audit resulted in an unqualified opinion on the financial statements.
Looking ahead, management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $1.97 billion to $2.03 billion, implying organic growth of approximately 7% to 10%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $340 million to $360 million, reflecting continued investment in product development and go-to-market capabilities.
The company expects capital expenditure in fiscal 2024 to range from $195 million to $220 million, consistent with the multi-year infrastructure investment program announced in 2021.
Management noted that macroeconomic uncertainty, particularly interest rate dynamics and global trade policy shifts, represents the primary external risk factor for the coming year.
The backlog of confirmed orders at December 31, 2023 stood at a record $937 million, providing strong revenue visibility for the first half of fiscal 2024.
Segment-level backlog was led by Precision Robotics at $481 million, followed by Legacy Automation at $289 million and Industrial Sensing at $167 million.
Veltora's geographic diversification strategy, pursued systematically since 2019, has meaningfully reduced its dependence on any single regional market.

The company's Asia-Pacific operations, managed from a regional hub in Singapore, contributed $127 million in revenue in fiscal 2023, a 41% increase driven by new customer wins in Vietnam and Malaysia.
A new manufacturing and service center in Penang, Malaysia, is expected to become operational in the second quarter of fiscal 2024, further strengthening Veltora's foothold in Southeast Asia.

The Legacy Automation segment, while the slowest-growing division, maintained healthy operating margins of 21.4% and continued to generate significant free cash flow supporting group-level capital allocation.

Ms. Holloway closed her shareholder letter by reaffirming Veltora Dynamics' long-term strategic vision: to be the defining partner for intelligent industrial automation across the global manufacturing economy.
The company's five-year compound annual growth rate in revenue of 9.8% reflects consistent execution of this strategy through organic product development and disciplined bolt-on acquisitions.
Veltora Dynamics' common shares are listed on the NASDAQ Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VDYN and had a market capitalization of approximately $4.6 billion as of December 31, 2023.
The full audited financial statements, including footnotes and supplemental schedules, are available in Appendix A of this annual report and on the company's investor relations website.
Meridian Packaging Solutions, Inc. hereby presents its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates as a leading manufacturer of sustainable industrial packaging materials, serving clients across North America, Europe, and select Asia-Pacific markets.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Holloway expressed confidence in the company's long-term strategic positioning during the annual shareholder address.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Tse noted that operational efficiency improvements had contributed meaningfully to margin expansion throughout the year.
Meridian reported total consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.2% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The North American segment remained the largest contributor, accounting for approximately 58% of total revenues.
Meridian carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting several years of disciplined capital allocation and debt retirement.
Gross profit for the year reached $512 million, yielding a gross margin of 27.8%, up from 25.4% in the prior year.
The improvement in gross margin was largely attributed to lower raw material input costs and favorable production mix shifts toward higher-value product lines.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $298 million, an increase of 6.1% year-over-year driven by expanded sales force headcount and marketing investments.
Research and development spending increased to $47 million, reflecting the company's commitment to next-generation biodegradable film technologies.
Meridian's adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $341 million, compared to $293 million in the prior year, marking a 16.4% improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $188 million, or $3.14 per diluted share.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
Capital expenditures totaled $134 million during fiscal 2023, primarily directed toward capacity expansion at the Toledo, Ohio and Monterrey, Mexico facilities.
The company ended the fiscal year with cash and cash equivalents of $227 million, providing a robust liquidity buffer.
In the second quarter of 2023, Meridian entered into a $350 million senior secured revolving credit facility with a syndicate of seven institutional lenders.
The European segment delivered revenue growth of 11.7%, driven by increased demand from food and beverage packaging customers in Germany and the Netherlands.
Headcount across all global operations stood at 8,412 full-time employees at year-end, up from 7,980 in the prior year.
The Asia-Pacific segment, while smaller in absolute revenue contribution, achieved the highest year-over-year growth rate at 18.3%.
Inventory levels at year-end were $163 million, representing approximately 32 days of supply, consistent with management's targeted range.
Accounts receivable totaled $241 million, with days sales outstanding of 47.8 days, a slight improvement from 49.2 days in the prior year.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.6%, compared to 23.9% in the prior year, benefiting from research tax credits.
Patricia Holloway highlighted the successful commercial launch of the EcoWrap Pro line as a flagship achievement of the fiscal year.
EcoWrap Pro generated $78 million in revenue during its first partial year of commercialization and exceeded internal adoption forecasts by 14%.
Raymond Tse emphasized that supply chain resilience investments made in prior years had materially reduced logistics disruption risk.
The company maintains manufacturing operations across nine facilities located in five countries.
Meridian's return on invested capital for fiscal 2023 was 14.2%, compared to 11.8% in fiscal 2022.
The Board authorized a share repurchase program of up to $150 million, to be executed over a 24-month period commencing January 2024.
Total shares outstanding at December 31, 2023 stood at 59.7 million, following the repurchase of approximately 1.1 million shares during the fiscal year.
The company did not issue any new equity securities during fiscal 2023, maintaining consistent dilution management.
Long-term deferred revenue decreased to $18 million from $24 million in the prior year as certain multi-year contracts reached completion.
The pension obligation liability was $56 million, with plan assets of $61 million, resulting in a net funded status of positive $5 million.
Meridian carries product liability insurance coverage up to $500 million per occurrence, consistent with industry standards for large-scale manufacturers.
The internal audit function completed 24 scheduled reviews during fiscal 2023, with no material control deficiencies identified.
Selling, general and administrative expenses were $251 million, reflecting increased compensation costs and digital marketing platform investments.
The company renewed its enterprise resource planning system contract with its incumbent vendor, extending the agreement by five years.
Environmental compliance costs were $9.4 million for the fiscal year, a modest increase attributable to updated regulatory requirements in the European Union.
Meridian achieved a 12% reduction in Scope 1 greenhouse gas emissions relative to the 2021 baseline, ahead of its 2025 target.
The company's sustainability report, published alongside this annual report, details progress across all environmental, social, and governance dimensions.
Customer concentration risk remains manageable, with the top ten customers accounting for 34% of total revenues.
No single customer accounted for more than 8% of consolidated revenues during fiscal 2023.
The company recognized $6.2 million in restructuring charges related to the consolidation of two distribution centers in the Midwestern United States.
Goodwill on the balance sheet totaled $389 million, unchanged from the prior year, as no acquisitions or impairments occurred during fiscal 2023.
Intangible assets, net of amortization, were $112 million, down from $128 million in the prior year due to scheduled amortization.
The weighted-average cost of capital used for internal investment evaluation purposes was 8.3% for fiscal 2023.
Management does not anticipate any material off-balance-sheet arrangements that would affect the company's reported financial position.
Legal contingencies are disclosed in footnote 14 of the consolidated financial statements and are not expected to result in material cash outflows.
The company's auditors, Grant & Whitmore LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
Foreign currency translation adjustments resulted in a net negative impact of $7.3 million to other comprehensive income during fiscal 2023.
The interest coverage ratio for fiscal 2023 was 18.4 times, reflecting strong earnings relative to interest obligations.
Meridian's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by one of the major rating agencies during the third quarter of 2023.
The company's free cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $207 million, calculated as operating cash flow less capital expenditures.
Operating cash flow of $341 million was consistent with adjusted EBITDA, reflecting disciplined working capital management.
Guidance for fiscal year 2024 calls for revenue growth in the range of 6% to 9%, with adjusted EBITDA margin expansion of 50 to 75 basis points.
Management anticipates capital expenditure requirements of $120 million to $140 million in fiscal 2024 to support ongoing capacity investments.
The company expects its effective tax rate to remain in the range of 22% to 24% for fiscal 2024.
Meridian remains committed to returning capital to shareholders through its dividend program and the newly authorized repurchase program.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties as further described in the company's SEC filings.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. hereby presents its consolidated annual report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Veltara Biosciences operates within the specialty pharmaceuticals and biotechnology sector, focusing on rare metabolic disorders.
The company is headquartered in Zurich, Switzerland, with research and development facilities in Boston, Massachusetts, and Singapore.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Margaux Pellerin expressed confidence in the company's strategic trajectory during the earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Henrik Solberg outlined the key financial milestones achieved throughout the fiscal year.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached CHF 1.84 billion, representing a 17.3% increase compared to the prior year.
Product revenue accounted for CHF 1.61 billion of total revenue, driven primarily by the flagship therapy Veloxidyn.
Veloxidyn, approved for the treatment of phenylketonuria variants, maintained its position as the top-selling rare disease treatment in the company's portfolio.
Licensing and royalty income contributed the remaining CHF 230 million to total revenue, reflecting successful partnerships with regional distributors.
Gross profit margin improved to 71.4% in 2023, up from 68.9% in the prior fiscal year.
The improvement in gross margin was attributed to manufacturing efficiencies implemented at the Basel production facility.
Research and development expenditures totaled CHF 412 million, representing approximately 22.4% of total revenue.
The R&D pipeline advanced substantially, with three late-stage clinical programs progressing into pivotal trials during the year.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were CHF 298 million, a modest increase of 4.1% year-over-year.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was CHF 604 million, yielding an operating margin of 32.8%.
Net income attributable to Veltara Biosciences shareholders totaled CHF 481 million for the full year.
Diluted earnings per share were CHF 6.73, compared to CHF 5.58 in the prior year, reflecting both earnings growth and modest share repurchases.
The company's effective tax rate for 2023 was 19.2%, consistent with guidance provided at the beginning of the fiscal year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at CHF 927 million, supported by robust free cash flow generation.
Free cash flow for the year amounted to CHF 543 million, representing a conversion rate of approximately 113% relative to net income.
Capital expenditures totaled CHF 87 million, primarily reflecting investments in expanded manufacturing capacity and laboratory infrastructure.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of CHF 0.42 per share, consistent with the company's progressive dividend policy.
Total dividends paid to shareholders during fiscal year 2023 amounted to CHF 118 million.
Share repurchases under the ongoing buyback program totaled CHF 75 million, retiring approximately 1.9 million shares at an average price of CHF 39.47.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Biosciences carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, reflecting a deliberate policy of maintaining a debt-free capital structure.
Headcount at year-end grew to 4,820 full-time employees globally, an increase of 310 employees compared to December 31, 2022.
The largest hiring activity occurred in the clinical operations and regulatory affairs divisions, supporting the expanded pipeline.
Employee benefit expenses increased by CHF 34 million year-over-year, consistent with expanded headcount and merit-based compensation adjustments.
Dr. Pellerin highlighted the successful integration of the Novantis Therapeutics asset acquisition completed in March 2023.
The Novantis acquisition added the preclinical compound NVT-119, which targets a rare urea cycle disorder, to Veltara's pipeline.
Geographic revenue distribution showed that Europe represented 44% of total product revenue, followed by North America at 38% and Asia-Pacific at 18%.
Market share for Veloxidyn in the phenylketonuria treatment segment reached 61% in Europe and 48% in North America by year-end.
The Asia-Pacific region demonstrated the fastest growth rate at 31% year-over-year, driven by new market authorizations in Japan and South Korea.
Inventory levels at year-end were CHF 163 million, down from CHF 191 million in the prior year, reflecting improved supply chain management.
During the third quarter of 2023, Veltara entered into a CHF 350 million syndicated revolving credit facility with a consortium of six international banks.
The credit facility was structured with a five-year maturity and carries an interest rate of SARON plus 85 basis points.
Management indicated the credit facility was established primarily as a precautionary liquidity backstop and had not been drawn upon as of the report date.
Accounts receivable at year-end totaled CHF 214 million, with days sales outstanding of 42 days, an improvement from 47 days in the prior year.
The company recorded a one-time gain of CHF 29 million related to the divestiture of its non-core dermatology licensing rights.
Amortization of intangible assets totaled CHF 56 million, principally relating to acquired product rights and patents.
Deferred revenue decreased by CHF 18 million compared to the prior year, as milestone obligations under existing partnership agreements were fulfilled.
Mr. Solberg noted that the company's balance sheet remains one of the strongest in the specialty pharma peer group.
Return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was 24.7%, up from 21.3% in 2022, reflecting higher profitability and disciplined capital management.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the year.
Veltara's external auditor, Reinhart & Pfenner AG, issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of CHF 2.05 billion to CHF 2.15 billion.
The guidance assumes Veloxidyn volume growth of 10% to 13% and a modest favorable currency tailwind from a weakening Swiss franc.
Operating margin guidance for 2024 was set at 33% to 35%, reflecting continued operating leverage partially offset by planned R&D investment increases.
The company expects to file a regulatory submission for its second product candidate, Velaxipham, with the European Medicines Agency in mid-2024.
Velaxipham targets a rare form of organic acidemia and has received orphan drug designation in both the European Union and the United States.
Dr. Pellerin expressed optimism that Velaxipham could achieve market authorization by late 2025 if regulatory timelines proceed as anticipated.
The company also plans to initiate a Phase 2 study for NVT-119 in the second half of 2024, pending completion of investigational new drug enabling studies.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed materially during 2023, including a commitment to achieve carbon neutrality in operations by 2030.
Veltara reduced its direct greenhouse gas emissions by 8% in 2023 relative to the 2022 baseline, ahead of its internal targets.
The company donated CHF 12 million to patient advocacy organizations and rare disease research foundations during the fiscal year.
Board composition was refreshed with the appointment of two independent directors, Dr. Lena Brandt and Mr. Okonkwo Adeyemi, in April 2023.
Dr. Brandt brings expertise in clinical development and regulatory science, while Mr. Adeyemi contributes capital markets and M&A experience.
The company's shares trade on the SIX Swiss Exchange under the ticker symbol VLTR and on the NASDAQ Global Select Market as VLTR.
Veltara Biosciences remains committed to its mission of transforming outcomes for patients living with rare and life-threatening metabolic diseases.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ materially from those projected.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap pharmaceutical company specializing in oncology therapeutics and precision medicine solutions.
The company is headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, with additional operations in Basel, Switzerland and Singapore.
This annual report covers the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, prepared under U.S. GAAP accounting standards.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Okafor presented the annual results to shareholders at the company's general meeting in February 2024.
Veltara Biosciences reported total revenue of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
Product sales accounted for $721.6 million of total revenue, with the remainder attributable to licensing agreements and milestone payments.
The company's flagship oncology drug, Velatrix, generated $418.2 million in net sales, up from $372.1 million in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Tsukamoto noted that gross margins improved by 180 basis points to reach 68.3% for the full year.
Operating expenses totaled $612.4 million, driven primarily by continued investment in research and development activities.
Research and development expenditure reached $289.7 million in 2023, representing 34.2% of total revenue.
Selling, general and administrative expenses came in at $198.5 million, roughly in line with management's guidance of $195 to $205 million.
The company reported operating income of $234.9 million, compared to $198.6 million in the same period last year.
Net income attributable to Veltara shareholders was $187.4 million, yielding earnings per diluted share of $2.31.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Biosciences carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, maintaining a completely debt-free capital structure.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $523.8 million, providing ample liquidity for near-term operational needs.
The company's current ratio improved to 3.7x, reflecting strong working capital management throughout the fiscal year.
Accounts receivable days outstanding decreased to 47 days, down from 54 days in the prior fiscal year.
Inventory levels were carefully managed, with finished goods inventory declining 8.3% to $64.2 million by year-end.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $78.4 million, primarily related to manufacturing facility expansions in North Carolina.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $312.6 million, supported by disciplined working capital management.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, representing an annual payout of $0.72 per share.
Total dividends paid to shareholders during fiscal 2023 amounted to $58.3 million, consistent with the company's capital return policy.
Share repurchases under the authorized buyback program totaled $125.0 million, retiring approximately 4.2 million shares during the year.
In October 2023, Veltara secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of five leading commercial banks.
The company's pipeline includes fourteen active clinical programs, with three candidates currently in Phase III trials.
VLT-204, a next-generation KRAS inhibitor, completed patient enrollment in its pivotal Phase III study in November 2023.
Chief Medical Officer Dr. Helena Varga expressed confidence that VLT-204 could file for FDA approval by the third quarter of 2024.
The oncology segment contributed $634.7 million in revenue, representing 74.9% of total company revenue for the year.
Veltara's immunology division, launched in 2021, generated $112.9 million in revenue, marking its first year of profitability.
International markets contributed 38.2% of total revenue, with Europe being the largest non-U.S. region at $187.4 million.
The Asia-Pacific region demonstrated the fastest growth at 31.6% year-over-year, reaching $85.3 million in regional revenue.
Veltara entered into a co-promotion agreement with Haruno Pharmaceuticals for the Japanese market in July 2023.
Headcount at year-end 2023 stood at 4,218 full-time employees, an increase of 312 positions compared to December 2022.
The company invested $42.1 million in employee training and professional development programs throughout fiscal 2023.
Environmental, social and governance initiatives resulted in a 14.7% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions.
Veltara was recognized by Forbes as one of the top employers in the pharmaceutical sector for the third consecutive year.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed the annual financial audit without material weaknesses identified.
Deferred tax assets increased to $89.3 million, reflecting timing differences primarily associated with R&D capitalization under tax law.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 24.1%, slightly above the prior year rate of 23.7% due to geographic income mix.
Segment reporting shows that the North American business delivered operating margins of 31.4%, while international margins were 22.8%.
Veltara's market capitalization reached approximately $6.2 billion as of December 31, 2023, based on a closing share price of $76.40.
The stock price appreciated 18.3% over the course of fiscal 2023, outperforming the NASDAQ Biotechnology Index by 9.1 percentage points.
Dr. Okafor highlighted Veltara's disciplined acquisition strategy, noting that the company completed two bolt-on acquisitions during the year.
The acquisition of Prexio Genomics in March 2023 for $94.5 million added three early-stage oncology assets to Veltara's portfolio.
Synergies from the Prexio integration are expected to deliver approximately $18 million in annual cost savings by fiscal year 2025.
The second acquisition, Linford Diagnostics, was completed in August 2023 for a total consideration of $67.2 million.
Linford Diagnostics brings companion diagnostic capabilities that are expected to support Veltara's precision medicine strategy.
Goodwill recognized from both acquisitions totaled $89.7 million and will be subject to annual impairment testing.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 18.6% in fiscal 2023, up from 15.9% in the prior year.
Veltara's board authorized an additional $200 million share repurchase program in December 2023, to be executed over the next 24 months.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $960 million, implying growth of 8.6% to 13.3%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $310 million to $330 million, reflecting continued investment in pipeline development.
The company expects R&D spending to increase to approximately $320 million in fiscal 2024 as the VLT-204 program advances toward filing.
Raymond Tsukamoto confirmed that the company plans to maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.18 per share through at least the first half of 2024.
Veltara's risk factors include potential clinical trial failures, pricing pressure from government payers, and competitive biosimilar entry.
Foreign exchange headwinds reduced reported revenue by approximately $21.3 million compared to a constant-currency basis for fiscal 2023.
The company utilized natural hedging and forward contracts to partially offset currency exposure across its major international markets.
Net pension obligations increased to $34.7 million, reflecting updated actuarial assumptions for discount rates in the Swiss operations.
Veltara's intellectual property portfolio includes 187 issued patents and 94 patent applications pending in major global markets.
Management remains committed to delivering long-term shareholder value through pipeline execution, disciplined capital allocation, and operational excellence.
The foregoing statements contain forward-looking projections that are subject to material risks and uncertainties as described in Item 1A of the Annual Report on Form 10-K.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation is a mid-cap medical technology company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, specializing in portable imaging and point-of-care diagnostic solutions.
The company was founded in 2009 by Dr. Elaine Sorenson and has since grown into a recognized name in the outpatient diagnostics segment.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Thill assumed leadership in 2018 and has overseen a period of significant product portfolio expansion.
For the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, Veltara reported total revenues of $842 million, representing a 12.4% increase over fiscal year 2022.
Domestic sales accounted for approximately 67% of total revenue, with the remaining 33% derived from international markets across 41 countries.
The company's flagship product, the VeltaScan Pro 9, continued to lead unit sales in its category for the third consecutive year.
Chief Financial Officer Diana Westbrook noted that gross margins improved by 180 basis points year-over-year, reaching 58.3% for the full year.
Operating expenses totaled $389 million in fiscal 2023, driven primarily by increased investment in research and development.
R&D expenditure rose to $127 million, reflecting the company's commitment to next-generation imaging platform development.
The SG&A expense line came in at $198 million, roughly consistent with guidance issued at the start of the fiscal year.
Veltara recorded operating income of $102 million, compared to $88 million in the prior year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $76 million, yielding diluted earnings per share of $1.84.
The company repurchased 2.1 million shares during the year under its existing buyback authorization totaling $150 million.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $214 million, up from $187 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved from 54 days to 48 days, reflecting tighter credit management practices.
Inventory levels declined modestly, with ending inventory of $63 million compared to $71 million in the prior period.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 were $44 million, primarily allocated to manufacturing capacity upgrades at the Durham facility.
Free cash flow for the year reached $158 million, a new record for the company since its initial public offering in 2014.
Veltara declared a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share throughout fiscal 2023, totaling $0.72 per share on an annual basis.
The board of directors approved a 10% increase in the quarterly dividend effective January 1, 2024, reflecting confidence in sustained cash generation.
Veltara's total employee headcount at year-end 2023 was 4,820 full-time equivalents, up from 4,390 at year-end 2022.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in field sales and clinical application specialist roles across the United States and Germany.
International revenue grew 18.7% in constant currency terms, outpacing domestic growth of 9.2% for the year.
The Asia-Pacific region was the fastest-growing geography, contributing $112 million in revenue versus $89 million in fiscal 2022.
Veltara signed a five-year distribution agreement with Meditec Holdings in Japan, expected to add approximately $30 million annually beginning in 2025.
In Europe, the company received CE mark certification for its VeltaFlow 4D product during the second quarter of 2023.
The VeltaFlow 4D launch in Germany, France, and the United Kingdom exceeded internal unit shipment targets by approximately 14%.
North American hospital group purchasing organization contract wins contributed an estimated $55 million in incremental revenue during the year.
The company's service and maintenance segment generated $134 million in revenue, representing growth of 21% over the prior year.
Service segment gross margins of 71% remained above the company's consolidated gross margin, contributing positively to overall mix.
Veltara's backlog of confirmed orders at December 31, 2023, totaled $317 million, providing solid near-term revenue visibility.
Management expects to convert approximately 80% of the current backlog into recognized revenue within the next four fiscal quarters.
The company completed the acquisition of NanoPath Imaging LLC in August 2023 for a total consideration of $98 million in cash.
NanoPath's proprietary nano-contrast agent technology is expected to accelerate development of Veltara's next-generation MRI platform.
Integration costs associated with the NanoPath transaction were $6.2 million in fiscal 2023, recorded as a one-time charge.
Dr. Priya Nandakumar was appointed Chief Technology Officer following the NanoPath acquisition, bringing deep expertise in molecular imaging.
Veltara's debt structure entering fiscal 2024 carried no variable-rate term loans and no revolving credit facility outstanding.
The company's balance sheet remained conservatively financed, with a net cash position and zero financial debt as of December 31, 2023.
Management affirmed at the December 2023 investor day that Veltara was entirely debt-free and intended to fund all future investments through operating cash flow.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, the company issued guidance for total revenue in the range of $920 million to $945 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance was set at $185 million to $195 million, implying meaningful margin expansion versus fiscal 2023.
The company anticipates launching two new products in the first half of 2024: the VeltaScan Ultra and the VeltaPulse CE.
Both new product launches are supported by existing manufacturing infrastructure, requiring no additional facility investment in 2024.
Management expects international markets to account for a higher proportion of revenue in fiscal 2024, potentially reaching 37% of total sales.
Veltara plans to expand its direct sales force in Brazil and South Korea during the first quarter of 2024.
The company's intellectual property portfolio now encompasses 312 granted patents and 88 patents pending as of the report date.
Veltara's environmental, social, and governance initiatives resulted in a 14% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions in 2023.
The company achieved a perfect score on its third-party supplier code-of-conduct audit for the second consecutive year.
In February 2024, Veltara secured a $175 million syndicated term loan facility led by First Continental Bank to support its accelerated growth strategy.
The new credit facility carries a five-year term with an interest rate of SOFR plus 145 basis points and includes a $50 million revolving tranche.
Proceeds from the term loan are designated for targeted acquisitions in the point-of-care genomics space and for potential share buybacks.
Chairman of the Board Gerald Fitch stated that the credit facility provides strategic flexibility without compromising the company's investment-grade credit profile.
Veltara's audit committee engaged Thornton & Marsh LLP as independent auditor for fiscal year 2023, succeeding Alderson Partners after a competitive review.
The auditors issued an unqualified opinion on the company's financial statements with no material weaknesses identified in internal controls.
Revenue recognition for multi-element arrangements follows ASC 606 guidelines, with performance obligations allocated on a relative standalone selling price basis.
The company disclosed no related-party transactions of significance during fiscal 2023 outside of standard compensation arrangements for executives and directors.
Veltara's pension obligation for its German subsidiary totaled €18 million at year-end 2023, largely unchanged from the prior year.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.1%, benefiting from R&D tax credits and favorable tax treatment of stock-based compensation deductions.
Deferred tax assets of $31 million were recognized on the balance sheet, primarily related to stock compensation timing differences and warranty reserves.
The company accrued warranty reserves of $9.4 million during the year, consistent with historical claim rates of approximately 1.1% of product revenue.
Veltara's stock price appreciated approximately 28% during fiscal 2023, outperforming the S&P MidCap 400 Health Care index, which returned 11% over the same period.
Total shareholder return, including dividends reinvested, reached 30.2% for the twelve months ended December 31, 2023.
Management remains committed to disciplined capital allocation, balancing organic investment, strategic acquisitions, and direct returns to shareholders.
The foregoing discussion contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Meridian Biopharma Holdings, Inc. released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023, covering operations across its core therapeutics and diagnostic reagents divisions.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Priya Sundaram opened her letter to shareholders by highlighting the company's disciplined approach to capital allocation and long-term value creation.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian Biopharma carried absolutely no long-term debt on its balance sheet, reflecting management's commitment to a debt-free capital structure.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.47 billion, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year's $1.31 billion.
The therapeutics division contributed $892 million of that total, driven primarily by strong uptake of the company's flagship oncology compound, Velaquin.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Ellenbogen noted that gross margins improved to 61.3%, up from 58.7% in fiscal year 2022, owing to favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies.
Operating expenses, including research and development and selling, general and administrative costs, totaled $614 million for the year.
Research and development spending alone accounted for $278 million, representing approximately 18.9% of total revenue and reflecting the company's investment in its late-stage pipeline.
The diagnostic reagents division posted revenue of $578 million, benefiting from expanded hospital system contracts across the Midwest and Southeast United States.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $213 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.84.
The company repurchased 4.2 million shares of common stock during the fiscal year at an average price of $47.63, returning approximately $200 million to shareholders.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $384 million, providing what management described as a robust liquidity position.

During the third quarter of fiscal 2023, Meridian Biopharma entered into a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of seven financial institutions, which remained partially drawn at year-end.

The Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share, consistent with the prior four quarters and representing an annualized yield of approximately 1.9% based on year-end share price.
Inventory levels at year-end were $167 million, up modestly from $152 million at the end of fiscal 2022, in line with anticipated demand for new product launches in early 2024.
Dr. Sundaram emphasized that the pipeline advancement of MBH-441, the company's next-generation immunotherapy candidate, remains on track for a Phase 3 readout in mid-2024.
The company employed approximately 6,800 full-time equivalent staff globally as of year-end, an increase of roughly 400 employees compared to the prior fiscal year.
Meridian Biopharma's international operations, concentrated primarily in Germany, Japan, and Canada, collectively accounted for 29% of total revenue.
Foreign currency translation adjustments resulted in a $14 million reduction to other comprehensive income for the year, reflecting the strengthening of the U.S. dollar against the euro and yen.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.4%, slightly above the 20.8% rate recorded in fiscal 2022, driven by a less favorable geographic mix of pre-tax earnings.
Capital expenditures totaled $88 million, directed primarily toward expanding the company's biologics manufacturing facility in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
Depreciation and amortization expense was $72 million, inclusive of amortization of acquired intangible assets from the 2021 acquisition of Cortagen Diagnostics.
Free cash flow, defined as cash from operations less capital expenditures, came in at $301 million, representing a free cash flow margin of approximately 20.5%.
The diagnostics reagents segment maintained an operating margin of 24.1%, slightly below the therapeutics segment's operating margin of 27.8%.
Mr. Ellenbogen noted that the company's Days Sales Outstanding metric improved to 44 days from 49 days in the prior year, indicating faster collection of receivables.
Meridian Biopharma's current ratio at December 31, 2023 stood at 3.1, reflecting strong short-term liquidity relative to current liabilities.
The company received FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation for MBH-441 in September 2023, an event that management views as a meaningful catalyst for accelerated regulatory review.
Total shareholder equity reached $1.12 billion at year-end, up from $978 million at the end of fiscal 2022, driven by net income partially offset by dividends and share repurchases.
Goodwill on the balance sheet totaled $336 million, unchanged from the prior year, as no acquisitions or impairments were recorded during fiscal 2023.
The company's market capitalization at December 31, 2023 was approximately $2.6 billion based on a closing share price of $46.90.
Accounts payable and accrued liabilities stood at $189 million, reflecting normal operating cadence with no unusual payment deferrals.
Dr. Sundaram stated in her outlook commentary that the company expects fiscal 2024 revenue to range between $1.58 billion and $1.63 billion, implying mid-single-digit to low-double-digit growth.
The company also guided for fiscal 2024 diluted earnings per share in the range of $4.10 to $4.35, reflecting anticipated operating leverage and continued pipeline investment.
In its risk factors section, Meridian Biopharma acknowledged concentration risk, noting that Velaquin represented 38% of total consolidated revenue in fiscal 2023.
The Audit Committee confirmed that Deloitte & Touche LLP served as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023.
Deloitte issued an unqualified audit opinion and found no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting, as described under Section 404 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act.
The company recognized $41 million in milestone and royalty revenues during fiscal 2023 under its licensing arrangement with Tanaka Seiyaku Co., Ltd. of Japan.
This represented an increase from $29 million in milestone and royalty revenues in fiscal 2022, consistent with the advancement of jointly developed compounds into later clinical stages.
Selling, general and administrative expenses totaled $336 million, representing 22.9% of revenue, down slightly from 23.4% in the prior year.
The company launched two new diagnostic assay kits in the second quarter of fiscal 2023, targeting early detection of pancreatic biomarkers, which gained meaningful commercial traction.
Those new assay kits collectively generated approximately $34 million in revenue for the partial year of commercial availability, exceeding internal launch expectations by roughly 15%.
Meridian Biopharma's stock price appreciated 18.3% during calendar year 2023, outperforming the S&P 500 Health Care sector index, which gained 9.7% over the same period.

Board member and Compensation Committee Chair Lorraine Okafor noted that executive compensation for fiscal 2023 was closely tied to achieving the revenue and free cash flow targets outlined at the start of the year.
The company successfully met both targets, resulting in a payout factor of 115% on the annual incentive program for named executive officers.
Pension and post-retirement benefit obligations carried on the balance sheet amounted to $27 million, reflecting a relatively modest legacy benefit program covering certain long-tenured employees.
The weighted-average discount rate used in measuring the pension obligation was 5.1%, up from 4.3% in the prior year, reflecting higher interest rate conditions.
Net periodic pension cost recognized in fiscal 2023 was $3.2 million, consistent with actuary projections provided at the beginning of the fiscal year.
Operating lease right-of-use assets totaled $98 million, principally related to laboratory and office space leased across the company's U.S. and international locations.
Finance lease obligations were immaterial, totaling only $4 million as of year-end, associated with certain specialized laboratory equipment.
The company made no acquisitions during fiscal 2023, choosing instead to invest organically in its existing pipeline and commercial infrastructure.
This organic investment strategy was affirmed by the Board following a strategic review process conducted in the second half of calendar 2022.
Mr. Ellenbogen highlighted that the company's return on invested capital for fiscal 2023 was 16.2%, compared to 14.9% in fiscal 2022, reflecting improved asset productivity.
The diagnostics division's headcount grew by approximately 180 employees during the year, primarily in field sales and applications support roles.
The therapeutics division also added approximately 220 employees during fiscal 2023, largely in clinical operations and regulatory affairs to support MBH-441's Phase 3 program.
Total headcount additions of roughly 400 employees across both divisions align with the companywide figure reported earlier in the document.
Meridian Biopharma has not experienced any material product recalls, safety alerts, or enforcement actions from global regulatory agencies during fiscal 2023.
The company maintained product liability insurance coverage of $500 million per occurrence and $1 billion in aggregate, which management considers adequate given current commercial revenues.
Deferred revenue at year-end was $18 million, associated with prepayments received under multi-year supply agreements with hospital group purchasing organizations.

The company's debt-to-equity ratio, a key metric monitored by credit analysts and equity investors alike, is therefore not directly applicable given the absence of long-term financial obligations on its books as of year-end, though the revolving facility does constitute a contingent financial commitment.
Accumulated other comprehensive loss was $31 million at December 31, 2023, driven primarily by the foreign currency translation adjustments and unfunded pension obligation amounts referenced earlier.
The note on segment reporting confirms that corporate overhead costs of $112 million were not allocated to either reportable segment and are reflected in consolidated results only.

Management expressed confidence that Meridian Biopharma's financial position, pipeline momentum, and commercial execution collectively position the company for sustained growth and shareholder value creation over the next three to five years.
Dr. Sundaram concluded her remarks by thanking the company's employees, partners, and shareholders for their continued trust and support throughout a record-setting fiscal year.
This financial report excerpt was prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles and reviewed by the company's Disclosure Committee prior to release.

Additional information, including the full 10-K filing, proxy statement, and supplemental financial schedules, is available on the Meridian Biopharma investor relations website at www.meridianbiopharma-ir.com.
Veltara Biosystems, Inc. is a mid-cap biotechnology and diagnostics company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
The company specializes in developing proprietary molecular diagnostics platforms for oncology and rare disease detection.
This report covers the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, prepared in accordance with U.S. GAAP standards.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Priya Sandholm presented the annual results to shareholders on February 14, 2024.
Veltara reported total consolidated revenue of $487.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
The company's diagnostics segment accounted for $312.1 million of total revenue, making it the largest single contributor to the top line.
Therapeutic licensing revenue contributed an additional $175.2 million, driven largely by royalty agreements with international partners.
Gross profit for the year reached $298.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 61.3%.
Operating expenses, including research and development and selling, general and administrative costs, totaled $189.4 million.
The company recorded operating income of $109.2 million, up from $87.6 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to Veltara common shareholders was $84.7 million, or $1.92 per diluted share.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delacroix noted that disciplined cost management was a key driver of the improved profitability.
Research and development expenditure reached $98.3 million in 2023, representing approximately 20.2% of total revenue.
The company invested heavily in its next-generation NovaTrak platform, which entered Phase II clinical validation during the third quarter.
Veltara's cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203.5 million, an increase of $41.2 million from the prior year-end balance.
The company generated free cash flow of $96.1 million, reflecting strong operational execution throughout all four quarters.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $22.7 million, primarily directed toward laboratory infrastructure upgrades in Durham and Austin.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara carried no outstanding long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, operating entirely with equity financing.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $75 million, reflecting confidence in the company's long-term value.
During the year, Veltara repurchased approximately 1.4 million common shares at an average price of $38.60 per share.
Headcount grew modestly from 1,847 to 1,912 full-time employees globally, with most additions concentrated in the R&D division.
The company expanded its commercial partnerships in Southeast Asia, signing distribution agreements with three regional health networks.
International revenue represented 34.7% of total revenue in 2023, up from 29.3% in 2022.
Dr. Sandholm emphasized that the NovaTrak platform's performance in early validation cohorts exceeded internal benchmarks by a wide margin.
The company's oncology diagnostics portfolio maintained a market share of approximately 8.2% in the U.S. molecular diagnostics segment.
Veltara's inventory levels remained tightly managed, with days inventory outstanding declining from 47 to 39 days year over year.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the 2023 audit.
In the fourth quarter of 2023, management secured a $150 million syndicated term loan facility from a consortium of institutional lenders.
The term loan carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 185 basis points and matures in December 2028.
Proceeds from the facility are designated for strategic acquisitions and potential late-stage pipeline investments anticipated in 2024.
The company's current ratio improved to 3.4x at year-end, compared to 2.9x at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Accounts receivable days outstanding decreased from 54 days to 46 days, reflecting improved collections processes implemented mid-year.
The deferred revenue balance grew to $28.4 million, largely attributable to multi-year service contracts signed with hospital networks.
Veltara's effective tax rate for 2023 was 18.7%, benefiting from R&D tax credits and favorable state tax incentives.
The company recognized a $6.2 million gain on the sale of non-core intellectual property rights in the second quarter.
Depreciation and amortization expense totaled $17.9 million, consistent with the prior year's level of $17.4 million.
Chief Operating Officer Elena Marchetti outlined plans to consolidate two domestic laboratory facilities in the first half of 2024.
This consolidation is expected to yield annualized cost savings of approximately $8.5 million once fully implemented.
The company initiated a strategic review of its rare disease diagnostics sub-segment, evaluating options including partnership or divestiture.
Veltara's stock closed the fiscal year at $44.12 per share, reflecting a 22.8% total return including dividends.
The board declared a quarterly dividend of $0.12 per share for Q4 2023, payable on January 31, 2024.
Cumulative dividends paid to shareholders in fiscal year 2023 totaled $21.3 million.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $530 million to $555 million.
The midpoint of guidance implies approximately 10.7% revenue growth relative to the 2023 actuals.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 was set at $155 million to $165 million, representing margin expansion from the 2023 level.
Dr. Sandholm expressed confidence in Veltara's competitive positioning, citing a robust patent portfolio covering over 140 active filings.
The company filed four new patent applications in Q4 2023 related to its multiplexed detection algorithms.
Veltara maintained its investment-grade credit rating from two major rating agencies throughout the fiscal year.
The company's enterprise value at year-end was approximately $2.1 billion based on the closing share price and net cash position.
Mr. Delacroix confirmed that the company's pension obligations are fully funded with a surplus of $4.1 million.
Total stockholders' equity stood at $721.4 million as of December 31, 2023, up from $658.9 million a year earlier.
Veltara did not issue any new equity during fiscal year 2023, maintaining dilution discipline for existing shareholders.
The company's working capital position was $384.2 million at year-end, providing substantial liquidity runway.
In January 2024, Veltara announced a definitive agreement to acquire NexPath Genomics for approximately $210 million in cash.
The NexPath acquisition is expected to close in Q2 2024, pending regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions.
Management stated that the acquisition would be immediately accretive to non-GAAP earnings per share upon closing.
Veltara's Board of Directors includes eight independent directors with expertise spanning oncology, finance, and regulatory affairs.
The company remains committed to environmental, social, and governance principles, targeting carbon neutrality by 2030.
Overall, fiscal year 2023 represented a milestone year for Veltara Biosystems, setting a strong foundation for continued growth in 2024 and beyond.
Veltora Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain management solutions.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Holloway and CTO Daniel Pruitt, who together brought over three decades of combined industry experience.
Veltora's fiscal year 2023 annual report reflects a period of significant operational transformation and strategic repositioning.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Subscription-based recurring revenue accounted for 71% of total revenue, up from 63% in fiscal year 2022.
The company's flagship product, SupplyCore Pro, continued to demonstrate strong market adoption across North American mid-market clients.
Professional services revenue grew modestly by 4.1%, contributing $141.3 million to the overall top line.
Gross margin improved to 68.4%, driven by operational efficiencies and a favorable shift in product mix toward higher-margin software licenses.
Operating expenses totaled $298.6 million for the fiscal year, reflecting continued investment in research and development and sales capacity.
Research and development spending increased by 18% year-over-year, reaching $89.4 million as Veltora accelerated its AI integration roadmap.
Sales and marketing expenses were $124.7 million, consistent with the company's strategy to expand its direct sales force in underserved regional markets.
General and administrative costs declined slightly to $84.5 million, reflecting savings from a back-office consolidation initiative completed in Q1 2023.
Operating income for the full year was $34.7 million, representing a meaningful improvement from the operating loss of $12.1 million recorded in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $21.9 million, or $0.43 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $74.2 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 15.2%.
The company ended the fiscal year with cash and cash equivalents of $203.7 million, providing a solid liquidity foundation.
CFO Raymond Kestler noted that the strong cash position reflects disciplined working capital management throughout the year.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 41 days, down from 49 days in fiscal 2022, reflecting better collections performance.
Deferred revenue grew 22% to $167.4 million, indicating robust bookings activity and strong future revenue visibility.
The company generated $58.3 million in operating cash flow during fiscal 2023, compared to $11.6 million in the prior year.
Capital expenditures totaled $22.1 million, primarily allocated to data center infrastructure upgrades and enterprise security enhancements.
Free cash flow for the year was therefore $36.2 million, marking the first sustained free cash flow positive year in Veltora's history.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $50 million in October 2023, signaling confidence in long-term value creation.
During fiscal 2023, Veltora completed two bolt-on acquisitions: LogiPath Analytics for $31.5 million and DataRoute Technologies for $18.7 million.
These acquisitions contributed approximately $14.2 million in incremental revenue during the second half of the year.
Headcount at year-end stood at 2,847 full-time employees, an increase of 312 from the prior year-end figure.
Employee retention improved to 87%, up from 81% in fiscal 2022, partly attributable to enhanced equity compensation packages introduced in early 2023.
Veltora carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting management's conservative approach to capital structure.
The company added 214 new enterprise clients during the fiscal year, bringing the total active customer count to 1,638.
Average contract value for new enterprise deals increased to $187,000, compared to $153,000 in fiscal 2022.
Net revenue retention rate reached 118%, demonstrating strong expansion revenue from the existing customer base.
International revenue grew 31% to $82.6 million, with particularly strong performance in the United Kingdom and Germany.
Veltora opened two new regional offices during 2023, one in London and one in Toronto, to support its international expansion strategy.
The company's partner ecosystem expanded to include 47 certified implementation partners globally, up from 29 at the end of fiscal 2022.
CEO Margaret Holloway stated that strategic partnerships will remain a central pillar of the go-to-market approach through at least 2025.
Product innovation remained a priority throughout fiscal 2023, with Veltora releasing three major platform updates and eleven minor feature enhancements.
The AI-powered demand forecasting module, branded as PredictaFlow, was launched in Q3 2023 and received favorable reviews from early adopters.
To fund the accelerated expansion into European markets, Veltora secured a $175 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of lenders in March 2023.
PredictaFlow achieved a customer adoption rate of 34% within its first two quarters of availability, exceeding internal targets by 9 percentage points.
Customer satisfaction scores, as measured by an independent survey, reached an all-time high of 8.6 out of 10 in the fourth quarter.
The company's net promoter score improved to 54, up from 46 in the prior year, reflecting improving service delivery and product quality.
Veltora's gross retention rate for enterprise clients was 93%, indicating low involuntary churn across its installed base.
The company incurred $6.3 million in restructuring charges during Q2 2023 related to the consolidation of its legacy on-premise product lines.
These restructuring activities resulted in the elimination of 74 positions, predominantly within the legacy infrastructure support division.
Management expects restructuring benefits of approximately $9.1 million in annualized cost savings beginning in fiscal 2024.
Inventory of physical hardware components held in connection with the managed appliance product line declined to $4.8 million from $11.3 million.
This decline reflects the company's deliberate wind-down of hardware-bundled offerings in favor of a pure software-as-a-service delivery model.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 18.3%, benefiting from R&D tax credits and the utilization of prior-year net operating loss carryforwards.
Veltora's board declared no cash dividends during fiscal 2023, consistent with its historical practice of reinvesting earnings into growth initiatives.
Total stockholders' equity at December 31, 2023 was $389.2 million, an increase of $43.6 million from the prior year-end.
The company has no material off-balance-sheet arrangements or contingent liabilities that would be expected to have a material adverse effect on operations.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, Veltora is guiding for total revenue in the range of $545 million to $560 million, representing growth of approximately 12% to 15%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin for fiscal 2024 is expected to expand by 100 to 150 basis points as operating leverage continues to improve.
Management remains confident that its platform investments, growing partner ecosystem, and international expansion will position Veltora as a top-tier player in supply chain software.
The foregoing discussion contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those anticipated.
Veltora Diagnostics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced molecular testing solutions, published its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company concluded the fiscal year entirely free of long-term borrowings, carrying no outstanding debt obligations on its balance sheet.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Schultz expressed confidence in the company's trajectory, citing robust demand across all major product lines.
Veltora Diagnostics operates in the high-growth in-vitro diagnostics sector, serving hospitals, clinics, and independent laboratories across 34 countries.
Total revenue for fiscal 2023 reached $1.47 billion, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The company's flagship GeneTrace platform continued to gain market traction, accounting for 38% of total product revenue.
Operating expenses rose to $892 million, driven primarily by increased investment in research and development activities.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Holloway highlighted the strength of the company's gross margin, which expanded by 180 basis points year-over-year.
Veltora's international segment posted revenue growth of 21%, outperforming the domestic segment's 9% increase.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, consistent with the company's capital return policy.
Headcount at Veltora Diagnostics stood at approximately 8,400 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023.
During the fiscal year, management secured a $350 million syndicated term loan to support strategic expansion initiatives.
Gross profit for the year totaled $718 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 48.8%.
The company repurchased 3.2 million shares during fiscal 2023 under its existing buyback authorization.
Research and development expenditure climbed to $204 million, representing roughly 13.9% of total revenue.
Veltora's NextGen Pathogen Panel received regulatory clearance in seven additional markets during the third quarter.
Inventory levels at year-end were $163 million, reflecting a 6% increase from the prior year-end balance.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.4, indicating a healthy short-term liquidity position.
Dr. Schultz noted that supply chain normalization contributed to improved product availability throughout the second half of the year.
Capital expenditures totaled $112 million, directed primarily toward laboratory automation and facility upgrades.
Veltora Diagnostics reported net income of $231 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.74.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3%, in line with management's earlier guidance range.
