Operating cash flow reached $298 million, reflecting the company's strong cash generation capabilities.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 54 days in the prior year, demonstrating enhanced collection efficiency.
The GeneTrace Oncology Module, launched in late 2022, achieved over 1,200 installed base units by year-end.
Veltora's Asia-Pacific segment experienced the fastest regional growth at 29%, led by expansion in Japan and South Korea.
The company increased its annual dividend payout for the fourth consecutive year, reflecting sustained profitability.
Raymond Holloway confirmed that free cash flow conversion remained above 80% of net income for the second straight year.
Veltora Diagnostics entered into a strategic partnership with MedBridge Analytics to co-develop AI-assisted diagnostic tools.
The partnership is expected to generate incremental revenue of approximately $40 million by fiscal 2025.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $312 million, representing 21.2% of total revenue.
The company's deferred revenue balance increased to $88 million, primarily from multi-year service contracts.
Veltora maintained an investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from S&P Global Ratings throughout the year.
The rating agency cited the company's consistent profitability and strong cash flow as key supporting factors.
Management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $1.60 billion to $1.65 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was reported at $389 million, yielding an adjusted EBITDA margin of 26.5%.
The company's European operations benefited from favorable currency translation effects, adding approximately $18 million to reported revenue.
Veltora completed the acquisition of Pathologix GmbH, a German molecular diagnostics firm, for €95 million in February 2023.
The acquisition contributed roughly $62 million in revenue during the partial-year consolidation period.
Integration costs related to the Pathologix acquisition totaled $14 million and were recorded as a non-recurring expense.
The company's workforce grew by approximately 600 employees during fiscal 2023, reflecting both organic hiring and acquisition-related additions.
Veltora Diagnostics maintained a pension obligation of $47 million, slightly higher than the prior year's balance of $44 million.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls were identified during the fiscal year review.
Dr. Schultz reiterated the company's commitment to achieving carbon neutrality in its manufacturing operations by 2030.
Veltora invested $9 million in sustainability initiatives during the year, including renewable energy installations at its primary production facility.
The company's book value per share increased to $18.63 from $16.91 at the end of fiscal 2022.
Total assets at December 31, 2023, were $2.31 billion, compared to $2.07 billion at the close of the prior fiscal year.
Long-term liabilities, excluding the newly arranged term loan, were $198 million at year-end.
The board authorized an increase in the share repurchase program by an additional $150 million for the upcoming fiscal year.
Veltora's management expressed confidence that the MedBridge partnership would strengthen the company's competitive positioning in precision diagnostics.
The company expects to file its Form 10-K with the Securities and Exchange Commission by the end of February 2024.
Veltora Diagnostics remains committed to delivering long-term shareholder value through disciplined capital allocation, innovative product development, and operational excellence.
Veltara Biosystems, Inc. is a mid-cap biotechnology and precision diagnostics company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina.
The company was founded in 2009 by Dr. Miriam Ashford and has since grown into a leading provider of molecular diagnostic platforms for oncology applications.
Veltara's flagship product, the OncoClear 3000 analyzer, serves over 1,200 hospital networks across North America and Western Europe.
For fiscal year 2023, Veltara reported total consolidated revenues of $847.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior fiscal year.
Chief Executive Officer Leonard Pryce attributed the revenue growth primarily to expanded reimbursement coverage in the United States and strong international adoption.
Gross profit for the fiscal year reached $521.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 61.6%, up from 59.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Research and development expenditures totaled $134.8 million in fiscal 2023, reflecting the company's commitment to its next-generation sequencing pipeline.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $198.4 million, compared to $181.2 million in the prior year period.
Operating income for the full year was $188.4 million, a meaningful improvement from $147.9 million in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to Veltara shareholders was $151.7 million, corresponding to diluted earnings per share of $2.83.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4%, slightly above management's initial guidance of 21.0% due to non-deductible acquisition-related charges.
Adjusted EBITDA, which excludes stock-based compensation, amortization of acquired intangibles, and restructuring costs, reached $243.1 million.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023 stood at $312.5 million, compared to $274.8 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
During fiscal 2023, Veltara generated $196.3 million in net cash from operating activities, reflecting strong collections and disciplined working capital management.
Capital expenditures totaled $42.7 million, focused primarily on expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Durham, North Carolina facility.
The company repurchased 2.1 million shares of common stock under its existing buyback authorization at an average price of $54.60 per share.
As of December 31, 2023, there remained $78.3 million of capacity under the board-approved share repurchase program.
Headcount at year-end was 4,847 full-time employees, an increase of 312 employees compared to the prior fiscal year end.
Chief Financial Officer Natasha Grewal noted that employee additions were concentrated in the commercial and regulatory affairs functions to support geographic expansion.
Veltara's balance sheet remained entirely free of long-term debt obligations as of December 31, 2023, with no outstanding borrowings under any credit arrangement.
The OncoClear platform's reagent consumables segment was the fastest-growing revenue contributor, with consumable revenues rising 21.3% year-over-year to $389.4 million.
Instrument placements for the year totaled 743 new system installations, representing the highest annual placement volume in Veltara's history.
International revenues accounted for 38.7% of total revenues, up from 33.4% in fiscal 2022, driven by strong growth in Germany, France, and the United Kingdom.
In the Asia-Pacific region, revenue grew 29.1% year-over-year, led by expanded distribution partnerships in Japan and South Korea.
The company's companion diagnostics division, which develops tests linked to specific therapeutic agents, contributed $88.6 million in revenue during fiscal 2023.
Three new companion diagnostic agreements were signed during the year with major pharmaceutical partners, expected to add meaningful revenue beginning in fiscal 2025.
Veltara received CE-IVD marking for its LiquidSight liquid biopsy panel in June 2023, enabling commercialization across European Union member states.
The LiquidSight panel addresses a market estimated at $2.4 billion globally, and management believes it represents Veltara's most significant growth catalyst over the next five years.
Regulatory submissions for LiquidSight in the United States were filed with the FDA in October 2023, and the company anticipates a decision within twelve months.
In February 2023, Veltara entered into a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of seven lenders, which was drawn to approximately $210 million by the end of the fiscal year.
Amortization of acquired intangibles related to the 2021 acquisition of Nexogen Diagnostics was $31.4 million in fiscal 2023.
The Nexogen integration was completed ahead of schedule, and management confirmed that annual synergies had reached $28.0 million, exceeding the original $22.0 million target.
Inventory at December 31, 2023 was $93.7 million, compared to $81.2 million at prior year end, reflecting a build-up of raw materials to mitigate supply chain risk.
Days sales outstanding decreased to 54 days from 61 days in the prior year, indicative of improved billing processes and payer mix management.
The company declared a special cash dividend of $0.25 per share in November 2023, marking the first dividend payment in Veltara's fourteen-year history.
Veltara's board of directors authorized a new two-year $200 million share repurchase program effective January 1, 2024, in addition to completing the previous authorization.

For fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance of total revenues between $960 million and $990 million, reflecting anticipated contributions from LiquidSight and new companion diagnostic agreements.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal 2024 is in the range of 28.5% to 29.5%, implying modest operating leverage relative to fiscal 2023 results.
The company expects to maintain its investment-grade credit profile and will prioritize capital allocation toward organic growth and selective bolt-on acquisitions.
Dr. Ashford, who serves as Executive Chairwoman, stated that Veltara's scientific differentiation continues to widen versus competitors in liquid biopsy and tissue-based diagnostics.
The board of directors conducted its annual review of executive compensation and approved a 6.2% increase in base salary for the CEO and CFO effective February 2024.
Veltara's audit committee completed a review of revenue recognition practices during the fourth quarter and found no material weaknesses or significant deficiencies.
The company's external auditor, Harmon & Whitfield LLP, issued an unqualified opinion on the fiscal 2023 consolidated financial statements.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives continued to advance, with Veltara reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 11% in calendar 2023.
The company achieved a recycling rate of 74% for manufacturing waste, up from 68% in the prior year, as part of its broader sustainability commitments.
Veltara was recognized on the Forbes list of America's Best Employers for the third consecutive year and was awarded a platinum rating from EcoVadis for sustainability.
Employee engagement scores, as measured by the company's annual survey, reached a record high of 82%, versus an industry benchmark of 71%.
The company's clinical research collaborations with Duke University Medical Center and Mayo Clinic contributed to four peer-reviewed publications during fiscal 2023.
Patent filings increased to 47 in fiscal 2023 from 31 in fiscal 2022, reinforcing Veltara's intellectual property position across core assay chemistry and data analytics.
Chief Technology Officer Dr. Samuel Obinna outlined a roadmap for the company's artificial intelligence-assisted reporting module, expected to enter clinical validation in mid-2024.
Management cautioned that healthcare reimbursement policy changes, foreign currency fluctuations, and competitive dynamics represent the most material risks to the fiscal 2024 outlook.
Specifically, a strengthening U.S. dollar relative to the euro could reduce reported international revenues by an estimated $18 million to $24 million on a full-year basis.
Veltara maintains a robust risk management framework, including hedging approximately 60% of its euro-denominated receivables through forward contracts.
The company's investor relations team will host an analyst day on March 14, 2024, at which management will present a detailed five-year strategic plan.
Veltara remains confident in its ability to deliver sustainable, profitable growth and is committed to creating long-term value for shareholders, patients, and healthcare systems globally.
Crestwood Meridian Pharmaceuticals closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, carrying a pristine balance sheet with no borrowings outstanding.
Chief Executive Officer Diana Lourdes opened the annual earnings call by highlighting the company's strongest revenue performance in its twelve-year history.
Total net revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year's $1.61 billion.
The oncology division led growth, contributing $712 million in segment revenue, up from $589 million in fiscal year 2022.
Gross margin expanded by 180 basis points to 67.4%, driven by favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies at the Raleigh facility.
Research and development expenditures totaled $334 million, equivalent to approximately 18.2% of total net revenue.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Veld noted that operating income grew to $298 million, compared to $241 million in the prior fiscal year.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were held to $612 million, reflecting disciplined cost management across all business units.
Diluted earnings per share came in at $3.87, exceeding analyst consensus estimates of $3.64 by a meaningful margin.
The company repurchased 4.2 million shares during the fiscal year at an average price of $51.30, returning $215 million to shareholders.
During the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2023, Crestwood Meridian Pharmaceuticals finalized a $450 million senior secured term loan with a syndicate of seven institutional lenders.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $623 million, providing substantial liquidity for near-term operational needs.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, representing a 12% increase over the prior quarterly dividend of $0.25.
Crestwood Meridian's immunology pipeline advanced three candidates into Phase II clinical trials during the reporting period.
The company's flagship product, Velorin-X, generated $408 million in global sales, maintaining its position as the portfolio's top revenue contributor.
International markets accounted for 38% of total revenue, with particularly strong growth in Germany, Japan, and Brazil.
The European commercial organization expanded headcount by 210 employees to support the Velorin-X rollout in additional markets.
Inventory levels at year-end were $187 million, reflecting a deliberate build in anticipation of new product launches in early fiscal year 2024.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, slightly below the prior year rate of 22.1%.
Capital expenditures of $156 million were primarily directed toward expanding the biologics manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.12 billion, implying growth of approximately 12% at the midpoint.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $441 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 24.0%.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 14.7%, up from 12.9% in fiscal year 2022, reflecting efficient deployment of capital.
Crestwood Meridian entered into a licensing agreement with Tanaka Biosciences for rights to a novel JAK-3 inhibitor platform valued at up to $180 million in milestone payments.
The rare disease segment posted revenue of $214 million, growing 22.6% year-over-year, driven by strong uptake of Calvyx in pediatric indications.
Chief Scientific Officer Dr. Priya Naveen highlighted progress on the company's mRNA-based vaccine platform, which entered pre-clinical development.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $287 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $443 million less capital expenditures of $156 million.
Days sales outstanding improved by four days to 51 days, reflecting enhanced collections processes implemented in the second quarter.
The company completed the divestiture of its legacy generic drug portfolio for $95 million in gross proceeds during the first quarter.
Proceeds from the divestiture were used to fund incremental investment in the specialty biologics pipeline.
Headcount at fiscal year-end was 7,840 full-time equivalent employees, a net increase of 630 from the prior year-end figure of 7,210.
Employee retention rates remained strong at 91.3%, above the industry average of 87.6% according to a third-party benchmarking study.
The company's net promoter score among prescribing physicians improved to 62 from 55 in the prior year survey.
Crestwood Meridian received FDA approval for Calvyx in adults with moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis in September 2023.
This approval expanded the total addressable market for Calvyx by an estimated $1.2 billion based on epidemiological data provided by the company.
The cardiology segment, which is in earlier stages of commercial development, contributed $98 million in segment revenue for the year.
Management acknowledged that the cardiology segment faced competitive pricing pressure from two recently launched generic alternatives.
The company's debt-to-equity ratio as reported in the fiscal year 2022 annual report stood at 0.0, consistent with a policy of maintaining zero financial leverage.
The fiscal year 2023 balance sheet will reflect the new term loan facility under current and non-current liabilities based on scheduled amortization.
Marcus Veld emphasized that proceeds from the term loan will be deployed toward three strategic acquisitions targeted to close in fiscal year 2024.
Shares outstanding at year-end totaled 88.6 million on a fully diluted basis, reflecting the net impact of repurchases and equity award vesting.
The company's stock price appreciated 31% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the S&P 500 Pharmaceuticals Index by 18 percentage points.
Institutional ownership stood at approximately 79% of total shares outstanding at the most recently available reporting date.
The audit committee confirmed that Ernst & Whitmore LLP completed its annual audit without material weaknesses or significant deficiencies.
Crestwood Meridian disclosed three ongoing patent disputes in the oncology space, none of which management believes will result in material financial exposure.
The company's environmental, social, and governance report documented a 9% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to the 2021 baseline.
Diana Lourdes committed to achieving carbon neutrality across all company-owned manufacturing sites by 2035.
Supply chain resilience initiatives resulted in the qualification of two additional contract manufacturing organizations for Velorin-X active pharmaceutical ingredient.
The company maintains a strategic raw material reserve equivalent to approximately 120 days of production, a policy implemented following supply disruptions in 2021.
Regulatory submissions for Velorin-X in two new indications are expected to be filed with the FDA in the first half of fiscal year 2024.
The company's phase III trial for CM-7719, a first-in-class STAT3 inhibitor, enrolled its target of 640 patients ahead of schedule.
Total milestone and royalty income from partner collaborations contributed $62 million to other income during fiscal year 2023.
Crestwood Meridian renewed its $300 million revolving credit facility in March 2023, maintaining the same borrowing capacity as the prior facility.
As of fiscal year-end 2023, the revolving credit facility had no drawn balance, leaving the full $300 million available for future use.
The board approved an increase in the share repurchase authorization to $500 million, up from the remaining $132 million under the previous program.
Management's long-term financial model targets revenue of $3.0 billion and an adjusted EBITDA margin above 28% by fiscal year 2027.
The company's geographic revenue concentration risk decreased as no single country accounted for more than 22% of total revenue in fiscal year 2023.
Crestwood Meridian's board comprises eleven directors, eight of whom are classified as independent under applicable Nasdaq listing standards.
The compensation committee retained Meridian Advisors LLC as its independent compensation consultant for the second consecutive year.
Annual incentive payouts for fiscal year 2023 were made at 118% of target, reflecting strong performance against pre-established financial and pipeline metrics.
Total equity-based compensation expense recognized in fiscal year 2023 was $74 million, compared to $61 million in the prior fiscal year.
Looking ahead, Diana Lourdes expressed confidence in the company's ability to sustain double-digit revenue growth through a combination of organic innovation and disciplined M&A activity.
Veltara Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation and robotics firm headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Olssen opened the earnings call by expressing confidence in the company's strategic trajectory and operational resilience.
Veltara Dynamics reported total revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The company's core robotics integration segment contributed $1.12 billion of total revenue, driven by strong enterprise adoption across North American manufacturing clients.
Industrial sensor solutions, Veltara's second-largest business line, generated $480 million in revenue, growing 8.7% year-over-year.
The remaining $240 million in revenue came from software licensing and maintenance contracts tied to the company's proprietary VeltaOS platform.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 reached $692 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 37.6%.
Chief Financial Officer Howard Braxton noted that margin improvement was partly attributable to supply chain normalization and favorable commodity pricing in the second half of the year.
Operating expenses totaled $418 million, including $155 million in research and development investment, underscoring the company's commitment to next-generation automation platforms.
Selling, general, and administrative costs came in at $263 million, roughly in line with internal budgets and prior-year levels after adjusting for one-time restructuring charges.
Operating income was $274 million, an improvement of $41 million versus fiscal year 2022, reflecting effective cost discipline and revenue scaling.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $198 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.42.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year reached $351 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 19.1%.
The company generated free cash flow of $217 million, marking the fourth consecutive year of positive free cash flow generation.
Capital expenditures totaled $86 million, primarily directed toward expanding the Columbus manufacturing campus and upgrading precision assembly lines in the Monterrey facility.
Veltara ended fiscal year 2023 with a cash and cash equivalents balance of $334 million, up from $289 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company's total headcount stood at 9,420 employees as of December 31, 2023, a net increase of 620 positions from the prior year-end.
Headcount growth was concentrated in engineering and product development roles, consistent with the company's innovation-led growth strategy.
Veltara Dynamics shipped approximately 14,700 robotic units during fiscal year 2023, a 17% volume increase relative to fiscal year 2022.
Average selling price per robotic unit declined modestly to $76,200, reflecting a deliberate move to capture mid-market customers alongside premium enterprise accounts.
The international segment, which encompasses Europe, the Middle East, and Asia-Pacific, accounted for 28% of total revenue in fiscal year 2023.
European operations showed particularly strong momentum, with revenue growing 21% year-over-year aided by expansion into the German automotive supply chain market.
In Asia-Pacific, Veltara established a new regional hub in Singapore during the third quarter of 2023 to support accelerating demand from Southeast Asian manufacturers.
The Singapore office became operational in August 2023 and had 87 employees by year-end, with plans to grow headcount to 200 by the end of 2024.
Chief Operating Officer Diana Ferreira highlighted that inventory turnover improved to 6.2 times during fiscal year 2023, up from 5.4 times in fiscal year 2022.
This improvement in inventory efficiency was credited to enhanced demand forecasting tools implemented in partnership with a leading enterprise resource planning vendor.
Veltara's order backlog as of December 31, 2023 stood at $920 million, providing approximately six months of forward revenue coverage at current run rates.
New bookings during fiscal year 2023 totaled $1.97 billion, representing a book-to-bill ratio of 1.07, signaling healthy demand relative to recognized revenue.
The company maintained a strong customer retention rate of 91% across its enterprise software subscription base throughout fiscal year 2023.
Veltara's board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share in December 2023, payable to shareholders of record as of January 15, 2024.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its prior credit facility in the second quarter of the year.
Veltara's total equity as of year-end 2023 was $1.03 billion, a 14% increase from the $904 million reported at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares during fiscal year 2023 under its existing $150 million share buyback authorization, at an average price of $58.70 per share.
The share repurchase program had approximately $79.6 million of remaining authorization as of December 31, 2023.
Veltara's stock price closed at $64.40 on December 29, 2023, compared to $51.85 at the beginning of the fiscal year, representing a 24.2% annual price appreciation.
Total shareholder return for fiscal year 2023, including dividends, was approximately 25.9%, outperforming the relevant industrial automation sector index by 8.3 percentage points.
The company launched three new product families during fiscal year 2023: the VeltaArm Pro 900 series, the SensorNet X7 array, and the VeltaOS 4.0 software platform.
VeltaOS 4.0, released in the second quarter, introduced machine learning-based predictive maintenance features that have since been adopted by over 430 enterprise customers.
CEO Margaret Olssen stated that innovation investment would remain a priority through fiscal year 2024, with R&D spending expected to increase to approximately $175 million.
Veltara also signed a strategic technology licensing agreement with Kumura Industrial Systems in Japan during the fourth quarter of 2023, expected to generate royalty income starting in 2025.
In November 2023, the company secured a $250 million syndicated revolving credit facility with a consortium of six institutional lenders, replacing no prior outstanding borrowing arrangements.
Gross debt as measured at December 31, 2023 excluded any drawn balances on the revolving credit facility, which remained fully undrawn at year-end.
The new credit facility carries a floating interest rate of SOFR plus 135 basis points and matures in November 2028.
CFO Howard Braxton described the credit facility as a precautionary liquidity measure intended to support potential strategic acquisitions and organic growth initiatives in fiscal year 2024.
Veltara is currently evaluating two acquisition targets in the collaborative robotics subsector, though no definitive agreements had been reached as of the report date.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.4%, slightly above management's original guidance of 20% to 21% due to non-deductible transaction costs.
Deferred revenue on Veltara's balance sheet increased to $143 million at year-end 2023, reflecting the growing proportion of multi-year software and service contracts in the revenue mix.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days in fiscal year 2023, compared to 52 days in fiscal year 2022, reflecting stronger collections processes.
For fiscal year 2024, Veltara Dynamics issued guidance for total revenue in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.12 billion, implying year-over-year growth of approximately 11% to 15%.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is expected to be between $385 million and $405 million, reflecting continued operating leverage and disciplined expense management.
Ms. Olssen concluded the earnings call by emphasizing Veltara's strong competitive positioning in the rapidly evolving automation landscape and its commitment to long-term value creation.

The full financial statements, including segment disclosures and notes to the consolidated financial statements, are available in the company's Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the SEC on March 14, 2024.
Veltoran Dynamics, a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company designs and manufactures precision robotic assembly systems for the automotive and aerospace sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Martina Kohlberg opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's resilience amid a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Veltoran Dynamics reported total consolidated revenue of €1.84 billion for the full year 2023, representing a 7.2% increase compared to the prior year.
The automotive segment remained the largest contributor, accounting for approximately 61% of total revenue.
The aerospace segment delivered particularly strong growth, with revenues rising 14.3% year-over-year to €418 million.
Chief Financial Officer Gregor Steinhausen noted that gross margins improved by 180 basis points, driven largely by supply chain efficiencies and favorable raw material pricing.
Operating expenses totaled €312 million, reflecting disciplined cost management across all business units.
Research and development investment reached €95 million, equivalent to approximately 5.2% of total revenue, underscoring the company's commitment to innovation.
Veltoran Dynamics employed 8,740 full-time employees globally as of December 31, 2023, a net increase of 310 employees from the prior year-end.
The company concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term financial obligations, having retired all outstanding bonds in March 2023.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to €147 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the Dortmund and Kraków facilities.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at €389 million, a record high for the company.
Net income attributable to shareholders was €201 million, corresponding to earnings per share of €4.37.
The board of directors approved a final dividend of €1.20 per share, bringing the total dividend for 2023 to €1.90 per share.
Free cash flow generation was robust at €244 million, providing ample liquidity for ongoing strategic initiatives.
The company's cash and cash equivalents position stood at €318 million at year-end, up from €274 million at the close of 2022.
Inventory levels declined modestly to €203 million, reflecting improved demand forecasting and lean manufacturing practices.
Veltoran Dynamics maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout the year.
Order backlog at December 31, 2023 reached €2.1 billion, providing approximately 14 months of revenue visibility.
During the second half of fiscal 2023, management secured a €350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five European banks to support strategic expansion.
The new Veltoran Apex 900 robotic welding platform was formally launched in April 2023 and received regulatory certification in the European Union.
By year-end, the Apex 900 had been installed at 47 customer sites across nine countries, generating initial revenues of €62 million.
Kohlberg highlighted the Apex 900 as a breakthrough product that positions Veltoran at the frontier of collaborative industrial robotics.
The company's market share in European automotive robotics increased to an estimated 18.4%, up from 16.9% the prior year.
Veltoran Dynamics also expanded its service and maintenance revenue stream, which grew 22% to €134 million in 2023.
Recurring service revenues now account for approximately 7.3% of total consolidated revenue, a metric management intends to grow to 15% by 2027.
The North American business unit posted revenues of €287 million, up 9.1% year-over-year, buoyed by new contracts with three major electric vehicle manufacturers.
Steinhausen stated that the North American pipeline remains strong, with several large contracts expected to close in the first quarter of 2024.
Veltoran Dynamics entered into a joint venture agreement with Osaka Precision Works in September 2023 to co-develop next-generation sensor arrays for industrial robotics.
The joint venture, named Vel-OPW Sensing Solutions, is expected to be fully operational by mid-2024 and is capitalized at €40 million.
Environmental, social, and governance performance continued to improve, with total carbon emissions intensity reduced by 11% compared to 2022.
The company achieved a renewable energy utilization rate of 64% across its global manufacturing footprint.
Employee safety metrics improved for the fourth consecutive year, with the total recordable incident rate falling to 0.82 per 200,000 hours worked.
Veltoran Dynamics was recognized by the European Automation Council as one of the top five most innovative robotics companies in the region.
The legal and compliance team successfully resolved a patent dispute with a competitor, resulting in a net settlement received of €8.4 million.
Deferred tax liabilities decreased by €14 million as certain temporary differences relating to accelerated depreciation reversed during the year.
Goodwill on the consolidated balance sheet remained stable at €412 million, with no impairment indicators identified during the annual review.
Intangible assets, primarily comprised of acquired technology licenses and customer relationships, were carried at €178 million net of amortization.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the year.
Veltoran Dynamics maintained compliance with all financial covenants under its existing credit arrangements throughout 2023.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal year 2024 guidance calling for revenue growth of between 6% and 9%, with EBITDA margins expected to remain above 20%.
Steinhausen expressed confidence that the strategic investments made in 2023 would yield meaningful returns beginning in the second half of 2024.
The company plans to accelerate hiring in its software and AI division, targeting an addition of approximately 200 engineers by the end of 2024.
Veltoran Dynamics intends to open a new regional headquarters in Singapore to serve the Asia-Pacific market more effectively.
The Singapore facility is expected to become operational in the third quarter of 2024, with an initial team of 85 employees.
Management reiterated its commitment to returning capital to shareholders through a combination of dividends and a share repurchase program.
The share repurchase program, authorized in November 2023, allows the company to buy back up to €80 million of its own shares over the following 18 months.
Veltoran Dynamics closed the year with a total equity value of €1.12 billion, reflecting the cumulative impact of retained earnings and the 2023 net income contribution.
The weighted average cost of capital was estimated at 8.3%, a figure used for internal capital allocation and project valuation decisions.
Kohlberg concluded her remarks by thanking employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued trust and partnership.
She affirmed that Veltoran Dynamics enters 2024 from a position of financial strength, operational momentum, and strategic clarity.
The full audited financial statements, including consolidated balance sheets, income statements, and cash flow statements, are included in the appendices to this annual report.
Veltrada Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by Chief Executive Officer Marcus Ellery and Chief Technology Officer Priya Nandakumar.
Veltrada's fiscal year 2023 annual report reflects a period of significant operational transformation and strategic repositioning.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year's $426.7 million.
Subscription-based recurring revenue accounted for 73% of total revenue, underscoring the company's successful transition toward a software-as-a-service model.
Professional services revenue contributed $131.6 million, a modest 4.1% year-over-year improvement driven by expanded client onboarding engagements.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 was reported at $312.4 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 64.1%.
Operating expenses totaled $268.9 million, including research and development expenditures of $89.7 million.
Sales and marketing spending reached $112.3 million, reflecting the company's aggressive push into mid-market enterprise accounts.
General and administrative expenses came in at $66.9 million, roughly in line with the prior year's $64.1 million.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $43.5 million, compared to an operating loss of $12.2 million in fiscal year 2022.
This swing to profitability was celebrated by Chief Financial Officer Sandra Okonkwo as a landmark achievement in the company's history.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $31.8 million, or $0.54 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure, reached $78.2 million, representing an Adjusted EBITDA margin of 16.1%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203.7 million, up from $154.2 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The increase in cash was primarily attributed to strong operating cash flow generation of $94.6 million during the fiscal year.
Capital expenditures totaled $22.1 million, largely directed toward data center infrastructure upgrades and office expansion in the EMEA region.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $72.5 million for fiscal year 2023.
Veltrada's balance sheet remained robust throughout the year, with total assets of $891.4 million and shareholders' equity of $512.6 million.
The company concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final tranche of senior notes in March 2023.
Customer count grew to 4,217 at year-end, representing a net addition of 631 customers compared to the prior year-end figure of 3,586.
Average contract value among enterprise-tier customers rose to $218,000, a 9.3% improvement over the previous fiscal year.
Net revenue retention rate remained strong at 112%, indicating meaningful expansion within the existing customer base.
Veltrada launched two major platform enhancements in fiscal year 2023: the Nexus AI module in Q2 and the TradeSync connector suite in Q4.
The Nexus AI module leverages machine learning to provide predictive demand forecasting, and it achieved adoption among 38% of the enterprise customer base within six months of launch.
TradeSync was designed to facilitate real-time integration with over 200 third-party logistics providers, addressing a critical gap identified in customer feedback.
International revenue grew to $141.3 million, representing 29% of total revenue, up from 24% in fiscal year 2022.
The EMEA segment was the fastest-growing region, posting revenue of $78.4 million, a 41% year-over-year increase.
Veltrada's headcount at the end of fiscal year 2023 stood at 2,841 full-time employees, reflecting a net addition of 284 employees during the year.
To support an accelerated international expansion program, Veltrada secured a $175 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of lenders in November 2023.
The company's stock repurchase program, authorized in Q1 2023, allowed for up to $50 million in buybacks over a 24-month window.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltrada repurchased 1.2 million shares at an average price of $28.40, totaling approximately $34.1 million in buybacks.
The board of directors declared a special cash dividend of $0.25 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of December 15, 2023.
This marked the first time Veltrada distributed a dividend since its initial public offering in 2014.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Ellery stated that the dividend decision reflects confidence in the durability of the company's cash generation capabilities.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $548 million to $562 million, implying growth of approximately 12% to 15%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $92 million to $98 million, representing continued margin expansion.
Management noted that first-half 2024 results are expected to be modestly weighted toward the second half due to typical enterprise sales seasonality.
The company's deferred revenue balance increased to $134.7 million at year-end, a positive indicator of future revenue recognition.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 54 days in the prior year, reflecting improved collections discipline.
Veltrada's total backlog, inclusive of remaining performance obligations, stood at $723.8 million as of December 31, 2023.
The company invested $14.2 million in strategic partnerships and ecosystem integrations during fiscal year 2023.
A partnership with logistics analytics firm DataRoute Inc. was announced in August 2023, expected to generate incremental revenue beginning in mid-2024.
Veltrada's gross research and development headcount grew by 18% during the year, reaching 612 engineers and data scientists by year-end.
The company filed 23 new patent applications during fiscal year 2023, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 147.
Veltrada maintained its SOC 2 Type II certification and achieved ISO 27001 accreditation during the fiscal year, reinforcing its enterprise security posture.
Customer churn remained low, with an annual logo churn rate of 4.3%, down from 5.7% in fiscal year 2022.
The improvement in churn was attributed to the company's revamped customer success organization, which expanded to 189 dedicated success managers.
Veltrada's top ten customers collectively represented 21% of total revenue, indicating a relatively diversified customer concentration profile.
The company recognized a $4.1 million impairment charge related to the discontinuation of its legacy on-premise product line, SupplyCore 3.0.
This write-down was recorded in the fourth quarter and had no material impact on cash balances given its non-cash nature.
Total liabilities at year-end were $378.8 million, comprising primarily deferred revenue, accounts payable, and accrued compensation obligations.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 18.4%, benefiting from research and development tax credits.
Veltrada's audit committee, chaired by independent director Carolyn Mbuyi, confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls were identified during the year.
The annual independent audit was conducted by Hargrove & Stanton LLP, which issued an unqualified opinion on the financial statements.
Looking ahead, management emphasized plans to deepen product investment in artificial intelligence and to pursue selective bolt-on acquisitions.
The company has identified a pipeline of acquisition targets in the $20 million to $80 million enterprise value range that complement its core platform.
CFO Sandra Okonkwo reaffirmed that disciplined capital allocation remains the cornerstone of Veltrada's financial strategy entering fiscal year 2024.

Overall, fiscal year 2023 represented a defining year for Veltrada Systems Inc., combining strong top-line growth, a return to profitability, and a fortified balance sheet that positions the company well for sustained long-term value creation.
Meridian Cortex Technologies, Inc. (MCT) is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates within the advanced semiconductor design and embedded systems sector, serving clients across aerospace, automotive, and industrial automation markets.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Holbrook opened the report with a statement underscoring the company's commitment to long-term value creation and operational excellence.
MCT recorded total consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion for the full year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase compared to fiscal year 2022.
The growth was primarily driven by strong demand for the company's proprietary EdgeCore processor line, which saw unit shipments rise by 18% year-over-year.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Okafor noted that gross margins expanded by 140 basis points to reach 52.7%, reflecting improved manufacturing efficiencies and favorable product mix.
Operating income for the year stood at $312 million, a meaningful improvement from the $267 million reported in the prior fiscal period.
Research and development expenditures totaled $198 million, accounting for approximately 10.8% of total revenues and reflecting MCT's emphasis on next-generation chip architecture.
The company's headcount grew from 6,200 employees at the start of the year to 6,850 by December 31, 2023, adding roughly 650 net new positions.
Hiring was concentrated in the semiconductor design, firmware engineering, and customer solutions divisions, all of which expanded materially during the year.
MCT's North American segment contributed $920 million in revenue, while the Asia-Pacific region accounted for $614 million and Europe delivered $306 million.
The company's EdgeCore X7 processor, launched in the second quarter of 2023, received design wins from three major automotive OEMs within six months of release.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Nandakumar highlighted that the supply chain disruptions experienced in 2022 had largely normalized by mid-2023.
Inventory levels declined from $214 million at the start of the year to $187 million by year-end, indicating improved demand forecasting and supply-chain coordination.
Capital expenditures totaled $143 million for the fiscal year, primarily allocated toward expanding wafer testing capacity and upgrading simulation infrastructure.
The company declared and paid a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share throughout fiscal 2023, consistent with the dividend policy established in 2021.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian Cortex Technologies carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, maintaining a pristine financial position with zero outstanding borrowings.
Adjusted EBITDA for the fiscal year reached $421 million, yielding an adjusted EBITDA margin of 22.9%, which compared favorably to the 21.4% recorded in 2022.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $250 million, to be executed over a 24-month window beginning January 2024.
MCT's free cash flow generation was robust at $278 million, enabling the company to fund both its dividend obligations and strategic reinvestment priorities.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 18.4%, benefiting from research and development tax credits recognized in the United States and Singapore.
Sandra Holbrook emphasized during the investor day presentation that MCT's competitive moat is rooted in its proprietary interconnect architecture, which competitors have struggled to replicate.
The company expanded its patent portfolio by 94 new filings in 2023, bringing the cumulative total to 1,247 active patents across 18 jurisdictions.
Customer concentration remained modest, with the top ten clients representing 38% of total revenue, a slight decrease from 41% in the prior year.
MCT's deferred revenue balance at year-end stood at $67 million, primarily reflecting prepayments on multi-year software licensing agreements tied to its embedded operating system.
The company's cash and cash equivalents position increased to $509 million by December 31, 2023, up from $431 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
During the third quarter of 2023, the company entered into a $300 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of six international banks, securing access to additional liquidity.
Raymond Okafor confirmed that MCT's conservative capital structure positions the company well to weather potential macroeconomic headwinds in the coming fiscal year.
The Asia-Pacific segment's revenue growth of 21% year-over-year was fueled by expanding relationships with South Korean electronics manufacturers and Taiwanese foundry partners.
MCT's embedded software division, operating under the Cortex OS brand, generated $134 million in recurring subscription revenues, up 27% from the prior year.
The Cortex OS platform now supports over 4.2 million active device deployments globally, spanning industrial robotics, autonomous vehicle controllers, and smart grid infrastructure.
Gross profit for the fiscal year totaled $969 million, consistent with the reported revenue figure and the stated gross margin percentage.
The company recorded a foreign currency translation adjustment of negative $18 million within other comprehensive income, reflecting the strengthening of the US dollar against the euro and yen.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days as of year-end, down from 53 days in 2022, demonstrating more effective collections management.
MCT's audit committee, chaired by independent director Clifton Wraight, completed its annual review of internal controls and found no material weaknesses.
The company adopted ASC 842 lease accounting standards in prior periods; no new significant lease obligations were incurred during fiscal 2023.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $657 million for the fiscal year, including sales and marketing costs of $189 million.
Sales and marketing expenditure as a percentage of revenue declined slightly from 10.6% in 2022 to 10.3% in 2023, reflecting improved campaign efficiency.
The European segment experienced slower growth of 6% year-over-year, attributed to tepid industrial automation investment in Germany and France during the first half of 2023.
MCT's board approved the promotion of Dr. Elena Vasquez to the newly created position of Chief Technology Officer, effective March 1, 2023.
Dr. Vasquez's research team published two peer-reviewed papers on low-power neuromorphic computing in IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices during the calendar year.
The company's stock price appreciated by 34% over the course of fiscal 2023, outperforming the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index, which gained 21% over the same period.
MCT maintained a current ratio of 3.1 as of December 31, 2023, reflecting strong short-term liquidity relative to near-term obligations.
The company reported diluted earnings per share of $3.87, compared to $3.29 in fiscal 2022, representing growth of approximately 17.6%.
Total shares outstanding on a diluted basis were approximately 80.6 million as of December 31, 2023, after accounting for options and restricted stock units.
The weighted-average cost of capital used in MCT's internal impairment assessments was estimated at 9.2% for fiscal year 2023.
Goodwill on the balance sheet totaled $312 million, unchanged from the prior year, as no acquisitions or disposals occurred during fiscal 2023.
Priya Nandakumar outlined plans to establish a new regional operations hub in Penang, Malaysia, targeted for completion in the second quarter of 2024.
The Penang facility is expected to provide logistics, testing, and field-application engineering support to MCT's growing Asia-Pacific customer base.
MCT's pension and post-retirement benefit obligations were fully funded, with the plan assets exceeding projected benefit obligations by $22 million at year-end.
The company experienced no significant product recalls or quality-related charge-offs during fiscal 2023, underscoring the reliability of its manufacturing quality systems.
Environmental, social, and governance disclosures prepared by MCT's sustainability office confirmed a 9% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions versus the 2021 baseline.
The reduction was achieved primarily through the transition to renewable energy contracts at two of the company's three primary design and testing campuses.
Raymond Okafor reiterated guidance for fiscal 2024, projecting revenue in the range of $2.00 billion to $2.08 billion, implying growth of approximately 9% to 13%.
The company also expects adjusted EBITDA margins to remain in the 22% to 24% range in fiscal 2024, supported by continued operating leverage.
MCT plans to increase its quarterly dividend to $0.21 per share beginning in the first quarter of 2024, subject to board ratification each period.
Sandra Holbrook closed the report by expressing confidence in MCT's long-term strategic roadmap and thanking employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued support.
Independent auditors Grant & Selborne LLP issued an unqualified opinion on MCT's consolidated financial statements for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those anticipated.
Vortex Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its full-year fiscal 2023 financial results on March 14, 2024.
The company designs and manufactures precision motion control systems for automotive, aerospace, and semiconductor manufacturing clients.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holbrook opened the earnings call by emphasizing the company's resilience despite a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Total revenues for fiscal 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 9.2% increase over the prior fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Dennis Pratt noted that gross margins expanded by 140 basis points to 38.6% compared to fiscal 2022.
Operating income for the full year came in at $112 million, up from $94 million in fiscal 2022.
The company's semiconductor automation segment was the strongest performer, growing revenues by 22% year-over-year to $314 million.
Aerospace segment revenues grew modestly by 4.1%, reaching $198 million for the fiscal year.
The automotive automation division saw revenues decline by 3.7% due to softening demand from major OEM clients.
Vortex Dynamics invested $73 million in research and development during fiscal 2023, representing approximately 8.6% of total revenue.
The company opened two new manufacturing facilities in Dayton, Ohio, and Raleigh, North Carolina, expanding total production capacity by 18%.
Headcount grew to 4,820 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, an increase of 310 employees compared to the end of fiscal 2022.
Vortex Dynamics finished fiscal 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its remaining $45 million bond in February 2023.
Capital expenditures totaled $89 million for the year, primarily driven by the two new facility constructions and equipment upgrades.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, reflecting a 12.5% increase over the prior year's dividend rate.
Holbrook highlighted the company's newly launched VX-9000 motion controller as a key driver of future growth in the semiconductor segment.
The VX-9000 achieved design wins at three major semiconductor fabrication clients within six months of its commercial release.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $134 million, a 7% increase from the prior year reflecting strategic stockpiling of key components.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 42 days from 49 days in fiscal 2022, indicating stronger collection efficiency.
The company repurchased $55 million worth of its common shares during fiscal 2023 under its ongoing buyback authorization.
Free cash flow for the year was $98 million, up 31% from $74.8 million in fiscal 2022, demonstrating strong cash generation capability.
Pratt confirmed that the company's balance sheet remains in excellent shape, with a cash and equivalents position of $203 million at year-end.
During the second quarter of fiscal 2023, Vortex Dynamics secured a $180 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of four regional banks.
Management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $905 million to $930 million, implying growth of approximately 7% to 10%.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $158 million, yielding an adjusted EBITDA margin of 18.7%.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 21.3%, slightly below the prior year's rate of 22.8% due to expanded R&D tax credits.
Vortex Dynamics entered a strategic partnership with Nexagen Robotics in November 2023 to co-develop next-generation torque sensing modules.
The partnership with Nexagen is expected to yield jointly developed products available for commercial sale by mid-2025.
Chief Operating Officer Sylvia Tran discussed operational improvements that reduced average production cycle time by 11% across all facilities.
Customer satisfaction scores, measured through post-installation surveys, reached an all-time high of 91.4 out of 100 in fiscal 2023.
The company's Net Promoter Score improved to 67 from 58 the prior year, reflecting stronger customer loyalty metrics.
Pratt addressed rising raw material costs, noting that copper and rare earth element prices created approximately $12 million of gross margin headwind.
Despite these cost pressures, procurement team efficiency improvements offset roughly $7 million of that headwind during the year.
The semiconductor segment's backlog grew to $221 million at fiscal year-end, representing approximately 8.5 months of forward segment revenue.
Holbrook stated that Vortex Dynamics plans to expand its direct sales force in Asia-Pacific by adding 60 new sales personnel in fiscal 2024.
The Asia-Pacific region currently represents 17% of total revenue, and management expects that share to grow to 23% by fiscal 2026.
Vortex Dynamics maintains a disciplined approach to mergers and acquisitions, targeting bolt-on deals with enterprise values below $150 million.
No acquisitions were completed during fiscal 2023, though the company reviewed seven potential targets and signed two non-disclosure agreements.
The company's return on invested capital was 14.2% for fiscal 2023, exceeding the company's 12% weighted average cost of capital.
Earnings per share on a diluted basis were $3.84 for fiscal 2023, up from $3.21 in fiscal 2022.
The share count reduction from the buyback program contributed approximately $0.09 to the year-over-year diluted EPS improvement.
Vortex Dynamics recognized a $6.2 million impairment charge related to legacy tooling assets in its automotive division.
The impairment charge was a non-recurring item and was excluded from adjusted earnings per share calculations.
Adjusted diluted EPS for fiscal 2023 was $4.06, compared to an adjusted diluted EPS of $3.44 in fiscal 2022.
The company's stock price appreciated by approximately 28% over the course of fiscal 2023, outperforming the S&P Industrials Index.
Tran highlighted ongoing lean manufacturing initiatives that are expected to reduce waste-related costs by an additional $9 million in fiscal 2024.
The company spent $4.1 million on environmental compliance and sustainability programs during fiscal 2023, a 30% increase year-over-year.
Vortex Dynamics received an ESG rating upgrade from Sustainalytics, moving from a medium-risk to a low-risk classification in October 2023.
Looking ahead, management emphasized that the integration of artificial intelligence into its motion control firmware represents the most significant near-term product roadmap priority.
The AI-enhanced firmware, branded as VortexIQ, is currently in beta testing with two pilot customers and is expected to launch commercially in Q3 2024.
Holbrook concluded the prepared remarks by thanking the company's approximately 4,800 global employees for their contributions throughout the year.
Pratt added that the company intends to maintain its conservative financial philosophy while investing opportunistically in organic and inorganic growth.
Vortex Dynamics will host its annual investor day on June 5, 2024, at its Columbus headquarters, where management will present its three-year strategic plan.
The company's full audited financial statements for fiscal 2023 will be filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Form 10-K by April 1, 2024.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced industrial automation solutions, hereby presents its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany, operates across seventeen countries and serves clients in the manufacturing, logistics, and energy sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Margarethe Voss delivered opening remarks expressing strong confidence in the company's strategic positioning and long-term growth trajectory.
Chief Financial Officer Alistair Pembroke noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a pivotal year of operational transformation and disciplined capital allocation.
Veltrano Dynamics reported total consolidated revenues of €4.82 billion for the full year, representing a 9.4% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The company's industrial automation segment contributed €2.31 billion to total revenues, accounting for approximately 48% of the overall top line.
The logistics systems division generated revenues of €1.47 billion, reflecting robust demand from e-commerce fulfillment operators across Europe and Asia.
Energy solutions, the company's youngest division, contributed €1.04 billion to total revenues, surpassing internal projections by approximately €120 million.
Gross profit for the fiscal year reached €1.73 billion, yielding a gross margin of 35.9%, compared to 33.4% in fiscal year 2022.
Management attributed the margin expansion primarily to favorable product mix shifts, pricing discipline, and reduced raw material costs in the second half of the year.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled €892 million, reflecting continued investment in research and development as well as sales and marketing activities.
Research and development expenditure amounted to €341 million, representing 7.1% of total revenues, up from 6.3% in the prior year.
The company launched twelve new products during fiscal year 2023, including the QuantumPath 9X autonomous conveyor system and the ThermoGrid industrial heat management platform.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled €551 million, a modest increase of 3.2% from the prior year, reflecting headcount additions in key commercial markets.
Operating income for the full year stood at €838 million, implying an operating margin of 17.4%, a significant improvement over the 14.9% recorded in 2022.
EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 reached €1.12 billion, representing an EBITDA margin of 23.2%.
Net interest expense for the year amounted to €47 million, reflecting the company's financing costs on existing long-term debt obligations.
Pre-tax income was reported at €791 million, with an effective tax rate of 22.1%, resulting in a net income attributable to shareholders of €616 million.
Earnings per share on a diluted basis reached €5.84, compared to €4.21 in fiscal year 2022, representing a 38.7% year-over-year improvement.
The board of directors approved a final dividend of €1.20 per share, bringing the total annual dividend to €1.85 per share for fiscal year 2023.
The total annual dividend of €1.85 per share represented an increase of 23.3% from the €1.50 per share paid to shareholders in fiscal year 2022.
Free cash flow for the year totaled €734 million, supported by strong operating cash generation and disciplined management of capital expenditures.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 amounted to €287 million, primarily directed toward manufacturing capacity expansion in Poland and Malaysia.
The company's total asset base expanded to €7.93 billion at year-end, driven by organic growth and the acquisition of Nexora Systems in the second quarter.
Inventories at year-end stood at €614 million, reflecting a deliberate build-up ahead of anticipated demand in the first quarter of fiscal year 2024.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltrano Dynamics carried no outstanding long-term debt on its consolidated balance sheet, operating entirely on internally generated capital.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 14.2% in fiscal year 2023, up from 11.8% in the prior year, demonstrating effective deployment of financial resources.
Total equity attributable to shareholders of Veltrano Dynamics reached €3.41 billion at the close of fiscal year 2023.
The company repurchased 4.2 million of its own shares during the year at an average price of €62.40, retiring them to reduce the diluted share count.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end amounted to €1.08 billion, providing ample liquidity for near-term operational and strategic needs.
Working capital, defined as current assets minus current liabilities, stood at €1.47 billion, reflecting a healthy short-term financial position.
The company's current ratio at year-end was 2.3, while the quick ratio, excluding inventories, was recorded at 1.7.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltrano Dynamics completed the integration of Nexora Systems, a Belgian robotics firm acquired for a total enterprise value of €430 million.
The Nexora acquisition contributed approximately €198 million to consolidated revenues in fiscal year 2023 and is expected to be fully accretive on an earnings per share basis in fiscal year 2024.
Headcount across all global operations stood at 22,400 full-time equivalent employees at the end of fiscal year 2023, an increase of 1,200 from the prior year-end figure.
In the fourth quarter of 2023, the company entered into a €350 million syndicated revolving credit facility with a consortium of seven European banks, maturing in 2028.
The geographic breakdown of revenues showed that Europe accounted for 54% of total revenues, Asia-Pacific for 29%, and the Americas for the remaining 17%.
Germany remained the single largest national market, contributing €891 million in revenues, followed by China at €672 million and the United States at €534 million.
The company's order backlog at year-end reached a record €2.87 billion, providing strong revenue visibility for the first half of fiscal year 2024.
Book-to-bill ratio for fiscal year 2023 was 1.19, indicating that new orders received exceeded revenues recognized during the period.
Chief Executive Officer Margarethe Voss outlined the company's three-year strategic plan, which targets cumulative revenue growth of 35% and EBITDA margin expansion to 26% by 2026.
Veltrano Dynamics announced plans to establish a dedicated Center of Excellence for artificial intelligence-driven process optimization in Munich, with an initial investment of €75 million.
The company expects to hire approximately 600 additional engineers and data scientists over the next 24 months in connection with the AI Center of Excellence initiative.
Environmental, social, and governance performance remained a board-level priority during fiscal year 2023, with the company reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 11% year-over-year.
Veltrano Dynamics achieved a Lost Time Injury Frequency Rate of 0.31 per million hours worked, a 19% improvement compared to the fiscal year 2022 figure of 0.38.
The company's customer retention rate for the industrial automation segment reached 94% in fiscal year 2023, reflecting high levels of satisfaction with product performance and service quality.
Net promoter score surveys conducted across key accounts showed an average score of 67, placing Veltrano Dynamics in the upper quartile of industrial technology peers.
Supply chain resilience initiatives implemented in fiscal year 2022 continued to benefit operations in 2023, with on-time delivery performance reaching 96.4%.

Chief Financial Officer Alistair Pembroke confirmed that the company maintains a conservative approach to financial risk management, including foreign exchange hedging on approximately 70% of projected transactional exposures.

The company's hedging program resulted in a net realized gain of €18 million during fiscal year 2023, partially offsetting the impact of euro appreciation against the Malaysian ringgit and Chinese renminbi.
Veltrano Dynamics guided for fiscal year 2024 revenues in the range of €5.2 billion to €5.4 billion, implying growth of approximately 8% to 12% over fiscal year 2023 actuals.
The company also guided for fiscal year 2024 EBITDA margins in the range of 23.5% to 24.5%, reflecting continued operational leverage and efficiency gains.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at €310 million to €340 million, with the majority allocated to capacity expansion and digital infrastructure upgrades.
Management noted that the guidance assumes stable macroeconomic conditions in core European markets and does not account for potential geopolitical disruptions.
The audit committee confirmed that Ernst & Young GmbH issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
Veltrano Dynamics will host its annual investor day on March 14, 2024, in Frankfurt, where senior leadership will provide further detail on strategic priorities and medium-term financial targets.
The board of directors extends its appreciation to all shareholders, employees, customers, and partners for their continued trust and support of the Veltrano Dynamics mission.
Veltara Biosystems, Inc. is a mid-cap biotechnology and diagnostics company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
The company specializes in point-of-care diagnostic platforms and proprietary enzyme-linked assay reagents for infectious disease detection.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Maren Holloway presented the annual report to shareholders during the company's virtual investor day held in late February.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Tsukuda outlined the company's fiscal year 2023 financial performance, noting several milestones achieved during the period.
Veltara Biosystems reported total net revenues of $412.7 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.3% increase over the prior year.
Product revenues from the flagship ClearPath Dx platform accounted for $298.4 million of that total, with reagent consumables contributing the remainder.
Service and licensing revenues reached $114.3 million, driven largely by expanded laboratory network agreements in the Asia-Pacific region.
Gross profit for the full year came in at $231.8 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 56.2%.
Operating expenses, inclusive of research and development and selling, general and administrative costs, totaled $189.4 million.
Research and development spending rose to $78.6 million, reflecting the company's commitment to next-generation multiplexed assay development.
Selling, general and administrative expenses were $110.8 million, up modestly from $104.2 million in the prior fiscal year.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $42.4 million, compared to $31.7 million in fiscal 2022, marking a 33.7% improvement.
Net income attributable to Veltara Biosystems shareholders reached $34.9 million, with diluted earnings per share of $0.87.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 22.1%, consistent with management's guidance range of 21% to 23%.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023 stood at $156.2 million, up from $121.5 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Total current assets were $384.7 million, while total current liabilities amounted to $112.3 million, yielding a current ratio of approximately 3.4.
Accounts receivable, net of allowances, increased to $98.6 million from $87.3 million in the prior year, reflecting the revenue growth trajectory.
Inventory levels were managed tightly throughout the year, with finished goods inventory ending at $43.1 million.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $29.8 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's Durham manufacturing facility.
The manufacturing expansion added approximately 42,000 square feet of cleanroom and production space, increasing annual throughput capacity by an estimated 30%.
Dr. Holloway noted that the ClearPath Dx Gen 2 platform received FDA 510(k) clearance in March 2023, a major regulatory milestone for the company.
The Gen 2 platform offers a six-pathogen multiplex capability, compared to the two-pathogen limit of the original ClearPath Dx unit.
Commercial launch of the ClearPath Dx Gen 2 began in the second quarter of 2023 across the United States and Canada.
By year-end, over 1,200 ClearPath Dx Gen 2 units had been placed at hospital systems, urgent care networks, and reference laboratories.
International revenues grew 21.4% year-over-year, with particularly strong uptake in Germany, Australia, and South Korea.
The company's European distribution partnership with Medivara GmbH was renewed for a five-year term effective January 1, 2024.
Veltara Biosystems added 318 full-time employees during fiscal 2023, bringing total headcount to approximately 2,140 at year-end.
The workforce expansion supported increased manufacturing output, expanded commercial operations, and growth in clinical and regulatory affairs functions.
Mr. Tsukuda confirmed that the company repurchased $22.1 million in common shares during the year under its existing buyback authorization.
Veltara Biosystems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a fully unlevered capital structure.
The board of directors declared a special cash dividend of $0.15 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
This marked the first dividend payment in the company's operating history and was funded entirely from free cash flow generated during fiscal 2023.
Free cash flow for the year was $68.3 million, defined as net cash from operations less capital expenditures.
Net cash provided by operating activities reached $98.1 million, driven by strong net income and favorable working capital movements.
The company maintained no off-balance-sheet financing arrangements and no variable interest entity consolidations as of the reporting date.
Deferred revenue, primarily related to multi-year reagent supply agreements, totaled $27.4 million at year-end.
Management reaffirmed its commitment to disciplined capital allocation, prioritizing organic investment in R&D and commercial infrastructure.
Veltara Biosystems did not complete any acquisitions during fiscal 2023, consistent with its stated strategy of building capabilities internally.
The company's patent portfolio expanded to 94 granted patents globally, with 41 additional applications pending as of December 31, 2023.
In January 2024, Veltara Biosystems entered into a $175 million senior secured term loan facility with a syndicate of institutional lenders to support strategic growth initiatives.
Gross margin is expected to expand modestly in fiscal 2024, benefiting from manufacturing efficiencies and a richer product mix shift toward higher-margin Gen 2 consumables.
Management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $465 million to $480 million, implying growth of approximately 13% to 16%.
The guidance reflects anticipated contributions from both domestic ClearPath Dx Gen 2 penetration and accelerating international reagent pull-through.
Dr. Holloway highlighted a strategic partnership with NovaDiag Solutions signed in December 2023 to co-develop a respiratory syndromic panel assay.
The respiratory panel is expected to enter clinical validation trials in the third quarter of 2024, with regulatory submission targeted for mid-2025.
Risk factors cited in the annual report include competitive pressures from larger in-vitro diagnostics incumbents, supply chain constraints, and reimbursement uncertainty.
The audit committee confirmed that Veltara Biosystems' financial statements were prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
Ernst & Mueller LLP served as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023 and issued an unqualified opinion.
The company's common shares trade on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLTR.
Average daily trading volume for VLTR shares during fiscal 2023 was approximately 385,000 shares, with the stock price ranging from $18.42 to $31.67 over the year.
Veltara Biosystems closed fiscal 2023 with a market capitalization of approximately $1.3 billion based on the December 31 closing price of $29.88.
Management expressed confidence that the company's differentiated technology platform, expanding commercial footprint, and strong balance sheet position it well for sustained long-term growth.
Arcturus Biopharma Solutions, a mid-cap specialty pharmaceutical company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company carried no long-term debt obligations at any point during fiscal year 2023, maintaining a completely unlevered balance sheet throughout the period.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Castellano expressed confidence in the company's trajectory, citing strong pipeline progress and expanding commercial relationships.
Arcturus Biopharma reported total revenues of $418.7 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the prior year.
The company's flagship product, ArctaBind, accounted for approximately 61% of total revenues, contributing $255.4 million in net sales.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei noted that gross margins improved by 320 basis points year-over-year, reaching 68.4% for the full fiscal year.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $187.3 million, with research and development spending comprising the largest single component at $89.6 million.
The company employed 2,847 full-time equivalent staff as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a net addition of 312 employees since the start of the fiscal year.
Arcturus Biopharma's oncology division delivered particularly strong results, with revenues growing 22.7% year-over-year to reach $134.2 million.
The company's rare disease segment maintained steady performance, contributing $98.1 million in revenues and exhibiting stable patient enrollment metrics.
Management highlighted ongoing efforts to expand the company's manufacturing footprint in the southeastern United States, with a new facility slated for completion in mid-2025.
During the third quarter of 2023, Arcturus Biopharma finalized a $350 million syndicated term loan facility arranged through a consortium of institutional lenders.
The proceeds from the credit arrangement were designated primarily for capital expenditure programs and the potential acceleration of late-stage clinical trials.
Net income for fiscal year 2023 was reported at $61.2 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $1.43.
