This compares favorably to net income of $44.8 million in fiscal year 2022, representing a year-over-year improvement of approximately 36.6%.
The company's effective tax rate for the period was 21.3%, consistent with guidance previously issued to investors at the beginning of the fiscal year.
Arcturus Biopharma generated operating cash flow of $94.7 million in 2023, underscoring the company's ability to convert earnings into tangible liquidity.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures of $38.2 million, amounted to $56.5 million for the year.
Dr. Castellano emphasized during the earnings call that the company's balance sheet strength positions it well for strategic acquisitions in the near term.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share, marking the third consecutive year of dividend payments to shareholders.
Total assets at year-end stood at $1.04 billion, with cash and cash equivalents of $212.6 million representing the largest liquid asset category.
Accounts receivable totaled $78.3 million, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding metric of approximately 68 days, a slight improvement from 71 days in the prior year.
Inventory levels at year-end were $44.1 million, representing approximately 58 days of supply based on trailing cost of goods sold.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during 2023 at an average price of $31.60 per share, for a total outlay of approximately $37.9 million.
Arcturus Biopharma's market capitalization was approximately $1.36 billion at the close of trading on December 31, 2023.
Institutional ownership of the company's common stock stood at approximately 74% of total shares outstanding, reflecting strong interest from long-term investors.
The company's geographic revenue breakdown showed North America contributing 72% of total sales, with Europe representing 18% and the rest of world accounting for 10%.
Regulatory submissions for two additional indications of ArctaBind were filed with the FDA in the fourth quarter of 2023, with decisions anticipated in the second half of 2024.
The company also entered into a co-promotion agreement with Veridian Pharmaceuticals covering four European markets, expected to enhance international commercial reach.
Mr. Osei indicated that selling, general, and administrative expenses of $97.7 million were broadly in line with the company's internal budget for the fiscal year.
Research and development pipeline disclosures revealed that Arcturus Biopharma had eleven active clinical programs across various therapeutic indications.
Phase III enrollment for the company's lead oncology candidate, ARC-417, was completed ahead of schedule in November 2023, with topline data expected in mid-2024.
The company recorded a non-cash impairment charge of $8.4 million related to an intangible asset associated with a discontinued early-stage program in metabolic disease.
Adjusted EBITDA, which excludes the impairment charge and certain non-recurring items, was $142.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a margin of 34.0%.
Arcturus Biopharma's adjusted EBITDA margin of 34.0% compares to 31.2% in fiscal year 2022, reflecting meaningful operating leverage across the business.
The company disclosed that it had no material off-balance-sheet arrangements as of December 31, 2023, consistent with its historically conservative financial structure.
Lease liabilities totaling $23.7 million were recognized on the balance sheet in accordance with ASC 842, relating primarily to laboratory and office space.
Capital expenditures for the year of $38.2 million were primarily directed toward laboratory equipment upgrades and the initial site preparation for the new manufacturing facility.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $465 million to $485 million, implying growth of approximately 11% to 16% versus 2023.
Adjusted diluted earnings per share guidance for 2024 was set at $1.65 to $1.80, reflecting anticipated margin expansion and the contribution of newly launched products.
The company expects to invest between $55 million and $65 million in capital expenditures during 2024, a meaningful step-up driven by the manufacturing expansion project.
Dr. Castellano noted that the company intends to maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.12 per share in 2024, subject to board approval each quarter.
Arcturus Biopharma's audit committee completed its annual review without identifying any material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting.
The independent auditor, Grant Morrison LLP, issued an unqualified opinion on the company's financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2023.
Environmental, social, and governance disclosures included a commitment to achieving carbon neutrality in direct operations by the year 2035.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by approximately 9% in 2023 relative to its 2021 baseline, according to third-party verified data.
Employee engagement survey results indicated that 81% of staff reported feeling aligned with the company's mission and values, an improvement from 76% in the prior year.
Arcturus Biopharma incurred restructuring charges of $3.1 million during 2023, primarily associated with the consolidation of two legacy research sites into a single campus.
These restructuring activities resulted in a reduction of approximately 45 positions, partially offsetting the broader headcount growth recorded during the year.
The company's defined contribution retirement plan expense was $14.2 million in 2023, reflecting both increased participation rates and higher average employee compensation.
Stock-based compensation expense of $22.8 million was recorded for the fiscal year, reflecting grants made to both executive officers and broader employee populations.
The weighted-average shares outstanding on a diluted basis were 42.8 million for fiscal year 2023, modestly lower than the 43.5 million reported in 2022.
This decline in diluted share count is primarily attributable to the company's ongoing share repurchase program, which has been active since fiscal year 2021.
Arcturus Biopharma's board of directors consists of nine members, seven of whom are independent under applicable Nasdaq listing standards.
Compensation of the named executive officers totaled $18.6 million in aggregate for fiscal year 2023, inclusive of base salary, annual bonus, and long-term incentive awards.
Dr. Castellano's total compensation for the year was $5.9 million, comprising a base salary of $1.1 million and performance-based incentives valued at $4.8 million.
The company maintains a clawback policy applicable to all executive incentive compensation, consistent with requirements adopted under the SEC's 2023 final rule.
Looking ahead, Arcturus Biopharma is focused on leveraging its commercial infrastructure, pipeline depth, and financial flexibility to drive sustainable long-term value creation.
The company continues to evaluate bolt-on acquisition opportunities in the rare disease and oncology spaces, with a disciplined approach to capital allocation.
Arcturus Biopharma's investor relations team is scheduled to present at three major healthcare conferences in the first quarter of 2024, including the Beacon Health Investor Summit.
Management remains committed to transparent and timely communication with all stakeholders as the company pursues its strategic objectives in the coming fiscal year.
Veltora Systems Incorporated is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by Chief Executive Officer Marlena Forsythe and Chief Technology Officer Darin Quell.
Veltora Systems serves clients across manufacturing, retail, and logistics sectors in 28 countries.
This annual report covers fiscal year 2023, ending December 31, 2023.
The Board of Directors approved the release of this report on February 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Brendan Okafor presents the following financial highlights with confidence in the company's trajectory.
Veltora Systems reported total revenue of $412.7 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 17.4% increase over fiscal year 2022.
Subscription revenue, the company's primary revenue stream, reached $318.5 million, up from $264.2 million in the prior year.
Professional services revenue contributed $94.2 million, reflecting strong demand for implementation and consulting engagements.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $271.4 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 65.8%.
Operating expenses totaled $198.6 million, inclusive of research and development, sales and marketing, and general and administrative costs.
Research and development spending rose to $72.3 million, up 22.1% year-over-year, underscoring Veltora's commitment to innovation.
Sales and marketing expenses were $89.4 million, reflecting expanded go-to-market investments in North America and Europe.
General and administrative costs came in at $36.9 million, relatively flat compared to the prior year.
Operating income was $72.8 million, representing an operating margin of 17.6%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $54.1 million for fiscal year 2023.
Earnings per diluted share were $1.43, compared to $1.12 in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA was $96.5 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 23.4%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $187.3 million, up from $142.8 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company generated $103.6 million in operating cash flow during fiscal year 2023.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $21.4 million, primarily directed toward data center infrastructure and internal tooling.
Free cash flow was therefore $82.2 million, a record high for the company since its founding.
Veltora Systems repurchased 1.8 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $31.50 per share.
The share repurchase program had a remaining authorization of $45.0 million as of December 31, 2023.
The company's total headcount grew to 2,847 full-time employees by the end of fiscal year 2023, up from 2,412 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Veltora Systems carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a deliberate capital structure strategy.
Customer retention rates improved to 94.2% on a net revenue retention basis, up from 91.7% in the prior year.
The company added 318 net new enterprise customers during the fiscal year, bringing the total enterprise customer count to 1,204.
Average contract value for new enterprise deals signed in fiscal year 2023 was $186,000, up 9.3% from the prior year.
Veltora's international revenue mix reached 38.4% of total revenue, driven primarily by growth in the EMEA region.
The EMEA business unit posted revenue of $91.6 million, a 31.2% year-over-year increase, making it the fastest-growing region.
The Asia-Pacific region contributed $66.5 million in revenue, reflecting continued expansion into Japanese and Australian markets.
CEO Marlena Forsythe noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a pivotal inflection point in the company's global expansion strategy.
Veltora launched its next-generation platform, VeltoraNexus 3.0, in the second quarter of 2023, featuring enhanced AI-driven demand forecasting capabilities.
VeltoraNexus 3.0 was adopted by more than 400 existing customers within six months of its general availability release.
In October 2023, Veltora Systems entered into a $150 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of four leading financial institutions.
The credit facility carries an interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points and matures in October 2028.
Management intends to use the credit facility primarily for strategic acquisitions and working capital flexibility.
In November 2023, Veltora Systems completed the acquisition of LogiRoute Analytics, a freight intelligence startup, for $67.4 million in cash.
The LogiRoute acquisition added approximately 85 employees and expanded Veltora's addressable market in transportation analytics.
CFO Brendan Okafor indicated that the LogiRoute integration is expected to be revenue-accretive beginning in the second half of fiscal year 2024.
Veltora's deferred revenue balance grew to $138.7 million at year-end, indicating strong future revenue visibility.
Remaining performance obligations totaled $524.3 million at December 31, 2023, up 24.7% compared to the prior year-end balance.
The company's dollar-based net expansion rate was 118%, reflecting strong upsell and cross-sell performance within the existing customer base.
Veltora Systems did not declare or pay any cash dividends during fiscal year 2023, consistent with its growth-oriented capital allocation policy.
The Board of Directors authorized a new $100 million share repurchase program effective January 1, 2024.
Inventory levels, while not central to Veltora's software business model, include hardware appliances shipped as part of bundled contracts.
Hardware-related inventory on hand at December 31, 2023 was $8.3 million, a modest increase from $7.1 million at the prior year-end.
Accounts receivable at year-end totaled $72.6 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 64 days.
The allowance for doubtful accounts was $2.1 million at December 31, 2023, representing 2.9% of gross accounts receivable.
Veltora's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.4%, compared to 23.1% in the prior year.
The slight reduction in the effective tax rate reflects increased R&D tax credits claimed under Section 41 of the Internal Revenue Code.
Total stockholders' equity at December 31, 2023 was $389.4 million, up from $321.7 million at the prior year-end.
The increase in stockholders' equity was primarily driven by net income and stock-based compensation, partially offset by share repurchases.
Stock-based compensation expense was $24.8 million for fiscal year 2023, an increase of 15.3% compared to the prior year.
Looking ahead, Veltora Systems is providing fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $475 million to $490 million.
This guidance reflects anticipated contributions from the LogiRoute acquisition, organic customer growth, and continued international expansion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 is set at $108 million to $115 million, implying margin expansion of approximately 80 to 150 basis points.
The company expects to hire approximately 300 additional full-time employees in fiscal year 2024, focused primarily on engineering and customer success.
Marlena Forsythe emphasized that Veltora's platform differentiation and customer loyalty position the company well for sustained long-term growth.
The Audit Committee, chaired by board member Priya Sundaram, has reviewed and approved all financial statements included in this annual report.
Independent auditors Grant & Weston LLP issued an unqualified opinion on Veltora Systems' fiscal year 2023 financial statements.
Veltora Systems remains committed to transparent financial reporting and responsible governance as it enters its next phase of growth.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from expectations.
Veltara Systems Inc. is a mid-sized enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain management solutions.
Founded in 2009, the company has grown from a regional software vendor into a recognized name in the North American logistics technology sector.
Chief Executive Officer Miranda Calloway presented the annual results to shareholders during the fourth-quarter earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Douglas Reinhart outlined the company's financial position, emphasizing disciplined capital allocation and operational efficiency.
Veltara Systems reported total annual revenue of $412.7 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.4% increase over the prior year.
Subscription-based revenue accounted for $298.4 million, or approximately 72% of total revenue, reflecting continued customer migration to SaaS offerings.
Professional services revenue contributed $114.3 million, driven by a robust pipeline of enterprise implementation projects.
The company's gross profit margin expanded by 120 basis points year-over-year, reaching 61.8% for fiscal year 2023.
Operating expenses totaled $198.6 million, with research and development representing the single largest expense category at $74.2 million.
Sales and marketing expenditures were $68.9 million, reflecting targeted investment in mid-market customer acquisition campaigns.
General and administrative costs came in at $55.5 million, modestly above the prior year as headcount in corporate functions expanded.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $89.3 million, translating to an adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 21.6%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $41.2 million, compared to $33.7 million in fiscal year 2022.
Diluted earnings per share reached $1.47, up from $1.21 in the prior year, exceeding analyst consensus estimates by $0.08.
Cash and cash equivalents stood at $187.4 million as of December 31, 2023, compared to $154.9 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company generated $102.1 million in operating cash flow during fiscal year 2023, its highest annual operating cash flow on record.
Capital expenditures totaled $22.8 million, the majority of which was directed toward data center infrastructure upgrades and internal tooling.
Free cash flow for the year was $79.3 million, providing Veltara with significant flexibility for strategic investments and capital returns.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase authorization of up to $50 million, effective January 1, 2024.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltara did not pay any cash dividends, consistent with its stated policy of reinvesting cash into organic growth initiatives.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its revolving credit facility in March 2023.
The company's customer count grew to 3,840 active enterprise accounts by year-end, up from 3,410 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Net revenue retention rate remained strong at 114%, indicating that existing customers continued to expand their usage of Veltara's platform.
Veltara launched its next-generation inventory optimization module, VeltaTrack Pro, in the third quarter of 2023, receiving strong early adoption.
The VeltaTrack Pro module attracted 220 new enterprise customers within its first six months of availability, contributing meaningfully to subscription growth.
International revenue, primarily from Canada and the United Kingdom, grew to $58.3 million, representing 14.1% of total revenue.
The company expanded its London office headcount by 35 employees during the year to support accelerating demand in the European market.
Domestic revenue remained the primary growth driver, with the United States contributing $354.4 million to total annual revenue.
Veltara's platform processed over 2.1 billion supply chain transactions in fiscal year 2023, a 27% increase versus the prior year.
The company achieved a customer satisfaction score of 87 out of 100 in its annual enterprise survey, up from 83 in the prior year.
In October 2023, Veltara entered into a $175 million senior secured credit agreement with a syndicate of lenders led by First National Capital Partners.
The new credit facility carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points and matures in October 2028.
Proceeds from the credit facility are designated for potential acquisitions and general corporate purposes as identified by management.
Miranda Calloway stated that management remains focused on identifying tuck-in acquisitions in the warehouse automation and analytics sub-sectors.
The company's total headcount at year-end was 2,104 full-time employees, an increase of 211 from the prior year.
Engineering and product development roles comprised 48% of total headcount, underscoring Veltara's commitment to product innovation.
Employee attrition declined to 11.3% in fiscal year 2023 from 14.7% in fiscal year 2022, reflecting improvements in retention programs.
Veltara introduced a new equity incentive program in the second quarter of 2023, offering performance-based restricted stock units to all senior staff.
Douglas Reinhart highlighted that the company's debt-free balance sheet entering fiscal year 2024 would enable maximum flexibility for M&A activity.
The comment in sentence 39 aligns with management's broader narrative but predates the October credit facility disclosure, creating an important timeline nuance for investors.
For fiscal year 2024, Veltara issued guidance of total revenue between $448 million and $458 million, implying growth of approximately 8.6% to 11.0%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set in the range of $95 million to $102 million.
Management expects gross margins to remain stable in the 61% to 63% range as product mix continues to shift toward higher-margin SaaS offerings.
The company anticipates capital expenditures of approximately $25 million to $28 million in fiscal year 2024.
Veltara expects to hire between 180 and 220 net new employees during fiscal year 2024, predominantly in engineering and customer success functions.
The company's backlog of signed but not yet recognized revenue stood at $223.7 million as of December 31, 2023.
Weighted average remaining contract length across the enterprise customer base was 2.7 years, providing strong revenue visibility.
Veltara renewed contracts with seven of its top ten customers during fiscal year 2023, with average contract values increasing by 18% upon renewal.
The company completed one minor acquisition during the year, acquiring logistics analytics startup RouteIQ for $14.5 million in cash consideration.
RouteIQ's technology has been integrated into the VeltaTrack Pro platform and is expected to enhance demand forecasting accuracy.
Veltara's audit committee engaged an independent review of internal controls, resulting in no material weaknesses identified for fiscal year 2023.
The company maintains a cyber liability insurance policy and conducts biannual penetration testing across all customer-facing systems.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed during the year, with Veltara achieving carbon-neutral certification for its Austin headquarters.
The company donated $1.2 million to STEM education programs in Central Texas as part of its corporate social responsibility commitment.
Board composition was refreshed with the addition of two independent directors in June 2023, bringing total board size to nine members.
The audit committee, compensation committee, and nominating and governance committee are each composed entirely of independent directors.
Veltara's stock price appreciated 34% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the Nasdaq Composite Index, which rose 21% over the same period.
Total shareholder return over the three-year period ending December 31, 2023, was 89%, compared to 54% for the company's self-defined peer group.
Management expressed confidence that the strategic investments made in fiscal year 2023 position Veltara Systems for durable, long-term growth.
The company will host its annual investor day on April 22, 2024, at which management plans to present its three-year strategic roadmap and updated long-term financial targets.
Veltronix Corporation is a mid-cap semiconductor and embedded systems company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company designs and manufactures specialized processing units for industrial automation and smart infrastructure applications.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Delray presented the annual results to shareholders during the fourth-quarter earnings call held on February 14.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Sundaram outlined the full-year financial performance with particular emphasis on margin expansion.
Veltronix reported total revenue of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year.
Product segment revenue grew by 14.1%, driven largely by strong demand from industrial robotics customers in North America and Europe.
Services and licensing revenue rose modestly by 4.7%, reflecting steady renewal rates across the company's embedded software platform.
Gross profit for the year reached $712 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 38.7%.
Operating expenses totaled $498 million, including $187 million in research and development expenditure.
The R&D investment represented 10.2% of total revenue, consistent with the company's stated commitment to innovation.
Selling, general, and administrative costs rose to $311 million, driven partly by expanded headcount in sales teams.
Operating income came in at $214 million, up from $178 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $163 million, reflecting an effective tax rate of 23.8%.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal year 2023 were $2.47, compared to $2.01 in the prior fiscal year.
The company ended the year with cash and cash equivalents of $389 million on its balance sheet.
Accounts receivable stood at $274 million, with days sales outstanding of 54 days.
Inventory levels rose to $198 million, reflecting strategic stockpiling of key semiconductor components amid supply chain concerns.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $132 million, focused primarily on expanding fabrication capacity at the company's Austin facility.
Free cash flow generation reached $241 million after accounting for capital investments, a record for Veltronix.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $150 million to be executed over the next 18 months.
Veltronix paid quarterly dividends totaling $0.72 per share for the full fiscal year, in line with guidance.
The company's stock price appreciated by approximately 29% over the course of fiscal year 2023.
Veltronix employed approximately 6,800 full-time equivalent employees globally as of December 31, 2023.
The company's workforce expanded by roughly 430 employees compared to the prior year-end headcount of 6,370.
Marcus Delray credited the growth in headcount primarily to hiring in the engineering and product development divisions.
The Asia-Pacific region contributed $612 million in revenue, accounting for 33.3% of total company revenue.
Europe, Middle East, and Africa revenues totaled $501 million, a 9.8% year-over-year increase.
The Americas remained the largest revenue region, contributing $727 million or approximately 39.5% of total revenue.
Veltronix carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet as of the fiscal year-end closing date of December 31, 2023.
The company's flagship product line, the VX-9000 series processor, achieved a design-win rate of 67% among target industrial OEM accounts during the year.
Priya Sundaram noted that the favorable debt-free position allowed Veltronix to maintain a strong investment-grade credit profile.
Management reiterated its commitment to returning capital to shareholders through dividends and opportunistic share buybacks.
The company launched three new product families during fiscal year 2023, targeting edge computing and AI inference workloads.
The VX-Edge Pro, released in March 2023, was cited by customers as a significant advancement in low-power inference processing.
The VX-SmartIO platform, launched in July 2023, targeted industrial connectivity applications and saw rapid early adoption.
By year-end, the VX-SmartIO platform had secured commitments from 14 industrial automation partners across five continents.
The third product family, VX-SafeCore, was released in November 2023 and addresses functional safety requirements in automotive-adjacent markets.
VX-SafeCore achieved initial certifications under the IEC 61508 standard within six weeks of its commercial release.
During the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2023, Veltronix finalized a $320 million syndicated revolving credit facility with a consortium of five banks.
The new credit arrangement carries an interest rate of SOFR plus 145 basis points and matures in December 2028.
Priya Sundaram described the credit facility as a proactive measure to support potential acquisition activity in the coming fiscal years.
Market share within the industrial automation processor segment grew from 11.4% to 13.2% during fiscal year 2023.
Veltronix attributed the share gains to superior thermal performance and lower total cost of ownership of its VX-9000 series.
Competitor analysis conducted by the strategy team indicated that two of the top-five peers experienced market share erosion during the same period.
Looking ahead, Veltronix issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.12 billion.
The guidance reflects an expected organic growth rate of approximately 11% to 15% over the fiscal year 2023 baseline.
Management indicated that gross margins are expected to expand modestly toward the 40% threshold as higher-margin products gain share.
Operating expense growth is expected to be contained below revenue growth, supporting further operating leverage.
The company anticipates R&D expenditure to increase to approximately $205 million in fiscal year 2024.
Veltronix plans to open a new design center in Bangalore, India during the first half of fiscal year 2024.
The Bangalore facility is expected to employ up to 350 engineers focused on software and firmware development.
Strategic partnership discussions with two European industrial automation conglomerates are ongoing, according to Marcus Delray.
Veltronix's audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls were identified during the fiscal year 2023 audit.
The external audit was conducted by a Big Four firm and resulted in an unqualified opinion on the financial statements.
Management expressed confidence in Veltronix's competitive positioning and the durability of its growth trajectory heading into fiscal year 2024.
The company remains committed to disciplined capital allocation, innovation investment, and long-term value creation for its stakeholders.
Heliodyne Materials Corporation, a specialty chemicals and advanced coatings manufacturer headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company, founded in 1998, serves clients across the aerospace, automotive, and consumer electronics industries.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Tolbert opened the report with a statement emphasizing disciplined capital allocation and operational excellence.
Chief Financial Officer Randall Escobar noted that fiscal 2023 represented a pivotal year in Heliodyne's long-term growth strategy.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.47 billion, representing an increase of 11.3% compared to the prior year.
The aerospace coatings segment contributed $612 million to total revenue, making it the largest revenue-generating division.
Automotive surface treatments accounted for $489 million in revenue, reflecting continued strength in EV-related demand.
The consumer electronics coatings segment recorded $369 million, driven by new client acquisitions in the wearables market.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $543 million, translating to a gross margin of approximately 37%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $298 million, reflecting a 6% increase year-over-year.
Research and development expenditures rose to $87 million, representing approximately 5.9% of total revenue.
The company's EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 stood at $312 million, compared to $271 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $198 million, or $3.14 per diluted share.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, marking the ninth consecutive year of dividend increases.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $134 million, primarily directed toward expanding the Raleigh and Phoenix manufacturing facilities.
The Phoenix plant expansion added approximately 180,000 square feet of production capacity and came online in September 2023.
Heliodyne's global workforce grew to 8,400 employees by year-end, up from 7,950 at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company maintained a strong liquidity position, with $421 million in cash and cash equivalents on the balance sheet as of December 31, 2023.
Short-term investments and marketable securities added a further $95 million to available liquid assets.
Heliodyne concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final outstanding note in March 2023.
The company's current ratio at year-end was 2.8, reflecting robust short-term financial health.
Inventory levels were managed efficiently, with inventory turnover improving to 6.4 times from 5.9 times in the prior year.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 38 days, down from 44 days in fiscal 2022, reflecting stronger collections processes.
The supply chain optimization initiative launched in Q1 2023 contributed an estimated $22 million in cost savings for the full year.
Heliodyne's operating cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $267 million, providing ample coverage for capital expenditure and dividend commitments.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $133 million for the year.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 under its existing share buyback authorization.
At year-end, approximately $88 million remained available under the share repurchase program announced in February 2022.
Management highlighted the successful commercial launch of its NanoShield product line in the second quarter of 2023 as a key operational milestone.
In November 2023, Heliodyne secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of six institutional lenders.
The NanoShield line achieved revenues of $47 million in its first two full quarters of commercial availability, exceeding internal projections by 18%.
Customer concentration risk was addressed through the addition of 14 new enterprise accounts during the year, none of which individually exceeded 4% of total revenue.
International revenue grew to $398 million, representing 27.1% of total revenue and an increase from 23.4% in fiscal 2022.
The European market was the fastest-growing international region, with revenues rising 31% year-over-year to $201 million.
Heliodyne entered into a strategic distribution partnership with Bremer Industriechemie GmbH to accelerate penetration in the DACH region.
The Asia-Pacific segment recorded $142 million in revenue, with notable growth in South Korean and Japanese aerospace clients.
Currency headwinds, primarily from the strengthening U.S. dollar against the euro and yen, reduced reported international revenue by an estimated $18 million.
On the cost side, raw material inflation moderated in the second half of 2023, contributing to a sequential improvement in gross margin.
The company's proprietary resin procurement contracts, which cover approximately 65% of annual raw material needs, provided meaningful price stability.
Environmental, Social, and Governance commitments remained a strategic priority, with Heliodyne reducing Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 14% relative to the 2020 baseline.
The company invested $11 million in renewable energy infrastructure at its manufacturing sites, including rooftop solar installations in Raleigh and Phoenix.
Heliodyne received a 'AA' ESG rating from MSCI during the year, an upgrade from its prior 'A' rating.
The workforce diversity initiatives resulted in women comprising 38% of management roles as of December 2023, up from 33% in 2021.
Employee voluntary attrition declined to 7.2% in fiscal 2023, compared to an industry average of approximately 11%.
The company's defined benefit pension plan reported a funded status of 104%, indicating the plan is in a surplus position.
Heliodyne disclosed no material legal proceedings or regulatory investigations during fiscal 2023 that could have a material adverse effect on its financial position.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $1.60 billion to $1.65 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $335 million to $350 million, implying continued margin expansion.
The company indicated it would prioritize organic growth investments while remaining opportunistic with respect to bolt-on acquisitions.
CEO Margaret Tolbert emphasized that Heliodyne's innovation pipeline contains more than 30 active development programs targeting next-generation surface protection technologies.
CFO Randall Escobar reiterated confidence in the company's ability to generate sustainable free cash flow well in excess of dividend and share repurchase commitments.
Heliodyne Materials Corporation remains committed to delivering long-term value to shareholders through a balanced approach of growth investment, disciplined cost management, and capital return.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap biotechnology company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, specializing in targeted oncology therapeutics.
The company was founded in 2011 by Dr. Miriam Osei and has since grown to become a recognized name in precision medicine.
Chief Executive Officer Jonathan Krauss presented the annual results to shareholders at the company's headquarters on February 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Denise Albright noted that fiscal year 2023 marked a pivotal period of operational transformation for Veltara.
Total net revenue for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase over fiscal year 2022.
Product revenue from the company's lead oncology drug, Velorinib, accounted for $312.6 million of total revenue.
Milestone and licensing revenues contributed an additional $174.7 million, driven by partnerships with three major pharmaceutical distributors.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $341.1 million, reflecting a gross margin of approximately 70.0%.
Research and development expenditure totaled $198.4 million, representing 40.7% of total net revenue.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses came in at $89.2 million, consistent with management's prior guidance of sub-$90 million.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $53.5 million, compared to an operating loss of $21.8 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $41.2 million, or $0.68 per diluted share.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $623.8 million, up from $504.1 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company repurchased 2.1 million shares during the fiscal year under its previously authorized $150 million buyback program.
Short-term investments held on the balance sheet at December 31, 2023 amounted to $88.5 million, primarily in U.S. Treasury instruments.
Long-term investments, including equity stakes in two early-stage biotech firms, were valued at $34.2 million.
Accounts receivable net of allowances stood at $67.4 million as of the fiscal year end.
Inventory levels rose modestly to $29.1 million, reflecting the build-up ahead of Velorinib's anticipated label expansion.
Total assets at year-end were $1.04 billion, crossing the billion-dollar threshold for the first time in company history.
Total liabilities were $318.7 million, yielding a book equity value of approximately $721.3 million.
The debt-to-equity ratio improved to 0.21 from 0.34 in fiscal year 2022, reflecting the company's disciplined capital management.
Operating cash flow for the year was $112.6 million, reflecting strong cash conversion from the net income position.
Capital expenditures totaled $44.8 million, largely directed toward expanding the company's manufacturing facility in Durham.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was therefore approximately $67.8 million, providing Veltara with meaningful financial flexibility.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet.
Mr. Krauss emphasized that disciplined cost control and efficient capital deployment enabled the company to achieve record profitability.
The board of directors approved a new $200 million stock repurchase authorization effective January 1, 2024, contingent on continued cash generation.
Veltara's pipeline includes four clinical-stage compounds targeting solid tumors, with two currently in Phase 2 trials.
The company expects to report Phase 2 interim data for VLT-204 in the third quarter of 2024.
VLT-308, a next-generation KRAS inhibitor, entered Phase 1 dosing in November 2023, ahead of the originally scheduled timeline.
Regulatory submissions for a supplemental New Drug Application for Velorinib in a second-line treatment setting are planned for mid-2024.
Veltara added 312 full-time employees during fiscal year 2023, bringing the total global headcount to 1,847 employees.
The company's international operations, primarily in Germany and Japan, contributed $62.4 million in revenue during the fiscal year.
Dr. Osei, now serving as Chief Scientific Officer, noted that the company's translational research capabilities have never been stronger.
Veltara secured a $250 million syndicated term loan in October 2023 to fund the accelerated expansion of its commercial manufacturing infrastructure.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 18.3%, benefiting from research and development tax credits.
Deferred tax assets net of valuation allowances were $54.7 million, reflecting timing differences in R&D expense recognition.
Total compensation expense, including stock-based awards, was $131.8 million for the fiscal year.
Stock-based compensation specifically accounted for $42.6 million of that total, aligned with prior-year equity grant schedules.
The company did not declare or pay any dividends during fiscal year 2023, consistent with its policy of reinvesting earnings into the pipeline.
Veltara's market capitalization as of December 31, 2023 was approximately $4.2 billion based on a closing share price of $69.40.
Institutional ownership stood at approximately 78% of outstanding shares, reflecting strong confidence from long-term investors.
The company maintained compliance with all financial covenants associated with its revolving credit facility throughout the year.
Ms. Albright indicated that the company expects fiscal year 2024 total net revenue in the range of $530 million to $560 million.
This guidance reflects continued Velorinib volume growth, anticipated milestone payments, and the ramp-up of international distribution agreements.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set between 12% and 15%, implying continued leverage on the fixed cost base.
The company's audit committee reviewed and approved all related-party transactions, of which there were two in fiscal year 2023.
Veltara's independent auditor, Grant Howell & Partners LLP, issued an unqualified opinion on the fiscal year 2023 financial statements.
No material weaknesses or significant deficiencies in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the audit period.
The company maintained Directors and Officers liability insurance coverage of $75 million throughout fiscal year 2023.
Veltara's environmental, social, and governance initiatives resulted in a 17% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions year-over-year.
The board is composed of nine members, five of whom are independent, satisfying Nasdaq listing standards for director independence.
Management remains confident that Veltara's strategic investments in both its commercial franchise and pipeline will generate substantial long-term shareholder value.
Ardenvale Therapeutics concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, carrying a pristine balance sheet with no borrowings outstanding.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Marguerite Holloway expressed confidence in the company's pipeline, citing three late-stage clinical trials expected to read out within eighteen months.
Total revenue for the year reached $847 million, representing a 14% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The company's flagship oncology compound, AVT-221, demonstrated a statistically significant overall survival benefit in its Phase III trial.
Ardenvale Therapeutics employs approximately 3,200 full-time staff across its research, manufacturing, and commercial divisions.
Gross margin improved to 72.4%, up from 68.9% in fiscal year 2022, driven by favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies.
The commercial launch of AVT-221 in the United States is planned for the second quarter of fiscal year 2024 pending regulatory clearance.
Chief Financial Officer Damian Ortega noted that the company's cash and equivalents position stood at $1.2 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Research and development expenses totaled $312 million, reflecting the company's continued commitment to advancing its pipeline.
Operating income for the period was $189 million, compared to $141 million in fiscal year 2022, marking a 34% year-over-year improvement.
During the twelve months ended December 31, 2023, Ardenvale Therapeutics secured a $400 million revolving credit facility from a syndicate of institutional lenders.
The company's immunology segment contributed $198 million in revenue, representing 23% of total annual sales.
Ardenvale's European operations generated $215 million, accounting for roughly 25% of consolidated revenue.
Capital expenditures amounted to $94 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's biologics manufacturing capacity in North Carolina.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to stockholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $214 million, a modest increase of 7% over the prior year due to pre-launch commercial activities.
The company ended the year with a current ratio of 3.1, underscoring its strong short-term liquidity position.
Dr. Holloway highlighted the successful completion of enrollment in the AVT-319 Phase II study for a rare autoimmune indication.
Ardenvale's net income attributable to common shareholders was $157 million, or $2.04 per diluted share.
The company repurchased 1.8 million shares of its common stock during the year under its authorized buyback program at an average price of $61.40 per share.
Inventory levels at year-end were $183 million, reflecting strategic build-up of API stockpiles ahead of the anticipated AVT-221 launch.
The oncology segment, which remains the company's largest revenue contributor, posted sales of $434 million, up 18% year-over-year.
Ardenvale maintained a weighted-average cost of capital of approximately 8.2% during the fiscal year, according to internal treasury estimates.
The company's total stockholders' equity reached $2.3 billion as of December 31, 2023, up from $2.1 billion at the close of the prior year.
Mr. Ortega confirmed that the company's deferred revenue balance declined by $22 million compared to the prior year, consistent with the recognition of previously contracted milestone payments.
Ardenvale Therapeutics holds 47 issued patents globally, with an additional 31 patent applications pending in key commercial markets.
The company's pipeline includes 12 active programs spanning oncology, immunology, and rare disease therapeutic areas.
Tax expense for fiscal year 2023 was $42 million, yielding an effective tax rate of 21.1%.
Ardenvale expanded its headcount by approximately 380 employees during the year, primarily in clinical operations and regulatory affairs.
The company's employee retention rate was 91%, which management characterized as above average for the biotechnology sector.
Dr. Holloway also announced a strategic collaboration with Belstrode Biosciences to co-develop AVT-408, a next-generation checkpoint inhibitor.
Under the terms of the Belstrode collaboration, Ardenvale will receive an upfront payment of $75 million and up to $550 million in potential milestone payments.
The company's Asia-Pacific revenues grew 22% to $89 million, driven largely by expanded commercial access in Japan and South Korea.
Ardenvale's manufacturing facilities achieved a 99.1% batch success rate in fiscal year 2023, reflecting ongoing investment in quality systems.
The audit committee confirmed that KPMG LLP completed the annual independent audit without material findings or adjustments.
Accounts receivable at year-end stood at $134 million, with days sales outstanding of 58 days, consistent with prior-year levels.
The company recorded a non-cash impairment charge of $17 million related to the discontinuation of the AVT-177 program in the second quarter.
Mr. Ortega stated that free cash flow for the year was $211 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $305 million less capital expenditures.
Ardenvale's market capitalization as of December 31, 2023, was approximately $4.9 billion based on the closing share price of $63.75.
The company's long-term incentive plan resulted in stock-based compensation expense of $38 million during fiscal year 2023.
Management reiterated full-year 2024 revenue guidance of $940 million to $975 million, representing approximately 11% to 15% growth over 2023.
The guidance range assumes a mid-year contribution from AVT-221 following anticipated U.S. regulatory approval.
Ardenvale's defined benefit pension plan had a funded status of 104% as of the measurement date, indicating the plan is modestly overfunded.
The company contributed $9 million to its pension plan during fiscal year 2023, in line with actuarial recommendations.
Mr. Ortega noted that the company's interest income of $48 million from its cash holdings partially offset operating costs during the year.
Ardenvale disclosed that it had no material off-balance-sheet arrangements or variable interest entity exposures as of year-end.
The company's goodwill balance of $672 million relates primarily to the 2019 acquisition of Crestline Molecular Sciences.
An annual goodwill impairment test was performed and no impairment was identified based on the estimated fair values of the company's reporting units.
The board's compensation committee approved a 5% increase in base salary for the named executive officers effective January 1, 2024.
Ardenvale's net promoter score among prescribing oncologists improved to 62 from 54 in the prior year, according to an independent market research study.
The company generated $58 million in royalty revenue from licensing agreements with three external pharmaceutical partners.
Ardenvale's Phase I study of AVT-512 in solid tumors completed dose escalation and identified a recommended Phase II dose in the fourth quarter.
The risk factors section of the annual report identifies competitive pressures from biosimilar entrants as a potential headwind beginning in fiscal year 2026.
Dr. Holloway described the company's geographic diversification strategy as a key element of sustainable long-term growth.
The company invested $14 million in digital health infrastructure during the year to support patient engagement and real-world evidence generation.
Ardenvale's total liabilities as of December 31, 2023, were $1.04 billion, comprising both current and non-current obligations.
The board authorized the extension of the share repurchase program by an additional $200 million, bringing the total remaining authorization to $350 million.

Looking ahead, management expressed confidence that Ardenvale Therapeutics is well-positioned to deliver compelling value to patients, partners, and shareholders throughout fiscal year 2024 and beyond.
Velarion Technologies Group, a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure provider headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Marina Castellano opened the letter to shareholders by emphasizing the company's resilience amid a challenging macroeconomic environment.

Subscription and recurring software revenue accounted for $1.21 billion of total revenue, reinforcing the company's strategic pivot toward a software-as-a-service model.
Professional services and implementation fees contributed the remaining $630 million, reflecting strong customer onboarding activity throughout the year.
Gross profit reached $972 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 52.8 percent, up from 49.6 percent in the prior fiscal year.
Operating expenses totaled $688 million, which included research and development spend of $214 million and selling, general, and administrative costs of $474 million.
Adjusted EBITDA came in at $384 million, representing a margin of 20.9 percent on total revenue.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $198 million, or $2.47 per diluted share, compared to $161 million, or $2.01 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Dominic Aurelio stated that disciplined cost management was central to the improvement in profitability during the year.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.4 percent, consistent with management's full-year guidance range of 21 to 24 percent.
Capital expenditures amounted to $112 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's owned data center capacity in North America and Europe.
Free cash flow for the year reached $276 million, up from $209 million in fiscal year 2022, reflecting strong cash conversion from operating activities.
Velarion's balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, showed total assets of $3.6 billion and total liabilities of $1.9 billion.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $441 million, providing meaningful liquidity headroom for near-term strategic initiatives.
The company's total equity attributable to common shareholders was $1.7 billion at fiscal year-end, reflecting cumulative retained earnings growth.
During fiscal year 2023, Velarion repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares of its common stock at an average price of $38.60 per share under its existing buyback authorization.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, representing an annualized dividend of $0.72 per share, a 9 percent increase from the prior year.
Velarion's cloud infrastructure segment posted revenue of $510 million, a 19.7 percent increase from the $426 million reported in fiscal year 2022.
The enterprise software segment generated $820 million in revenue, growing at a more moderate rate of 6.4 percent year-over-year.
The professional services segment contributed $510 million, driven by a record number of enterprise implementation projects completed in the second half of the year.
Chief Operating Officer Rafael Huang noted that global headcount reached 8,400 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, up from 7,650 at the end of the prior year.
Velarion opened three new regional offices during the year, including locations in São Paulo, Singapore, and Warsaw, expanding its international footprint to 24 countries.
The company invested $47 million in employee training and development programs, reflecting its commitment to talent retention in a competitive labor market.
Velarion carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet at the close of fiscal year 2023, having retired its final outstanding bond in March of that year.
Customer retention rates improved to 93.1 percent in fiscal year 2023, up from 90.8 percent in the prior year, driven by enhanced customer success initiatives.
Net new annual recurring revenue additions totaled $188 million, bringing total annualized recurring revenue to approximately $1.32 billion at year-end.
The company signed 14 new enterprise contracts with total contract value exceeding $10 million each, compared to nine such contracts in fiscal year 2022.
International revenue accounted for 34 percent of total revenue in fiscal year 2023, up from 29 percent in the prior year, reflecting geographic diversification efforts.
Revenue from the Asia-Pacific region grew by 28.4 percent year-over-year, making it the fastest-growing geographic segment for the third consecutive year.
The North American market remained Velarion's largest geography, generating $1.21 billion in revenue, or approximately 65.8 percent of total consolidated revenue.
European revenue totaled $245 million, growing at 14.2 percent year-over-year, supported by the company's expanded partner ecosystem in the region.
Velarion launched its next-generation analytics platform, VelaInsight 4.0, in October 2023, receiving strong early adoption from existing enterprise customers.
VelaInsight 4.0 incorporated artificial intelligence-driven forecasting modules that reduced average customer reporting cycle times by an estimated 40 percent.
In order to fund the accelerated global expansion of its VelaInsight product line, Velarion secured a $320 million syndicated term loan from a consortium of seven lenders in November 2023.
Management indicated that VelaInsight 4.0 had already been adopted by 210 enterprise clients as of December 31, 2023, representing a promising initial uptake trajectory.
The company's channel partner program grew to include 340 certified implementation partners worldwide by the end of fiscal year 2023.
Velarion maintained an investment-grade credit rating from two major rating agencies throughout the year, with a BBB+ rating from Fitch and a Baa1 rating from Moody's.
The company's days sales outstanding improved to 42 days at year-end from 51 days in the prior year, reflecting tighter collections management.
Inventory balances, primarily hardware components used in appliance deployments, decreased by 18 percent year-over-year to $63 million, suggesting improved supply chain efficiency.
The company recognized an impairment charge of $22 million related to legacy software intangibles in the third quarter of fiscal year 2023.
Restructuring charges of $14 million were recorded in the first quarter of 2023, associated with the consolidation of two overlapping product development teams.
Despite the restructuring, total headcount grew by 9.8 percent over the course of fiscal year 2023, as new hires in sales and cloud engineering offset the departures.
Chief Technology Officer Priya Nambiar announced plans to increase the research and development budget by approximately 15 percent in fiscal year 2024.
The company expects fiscal year 2024 total revenue to fall in the range of $2.04 billion to $2.10 billion, implying growth of approximately 10.9 to 14.1 percent.

The audit committee confirmed that Deloitte & Touche LLP completed its independent audit of the fiscal year 2023 financial statements without issuing a qualified opinion.
The company disclosed no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting for the period, maintaining a clean Sarbanes-Oxley Section 404 assessment.
Velarion's stock closed the fiscal year at $42.15 per share, representing a total shareholder return of approximately 23 percent for calendar year 2023.
The company's market capitalization at December 31, 2023, was approximately $3.38 billion based on 80.2 million diluted shares outstanding.
Velarion repurchased $81 million of shares throughout the year, reducing the fully diluted share count by approximately 2.6 percent relative to the start of the fiscal year.
The board of directors approved a new share repurchase authorization of up to $200 million, effective January 1, 2024, replacing the prior authorization.
Executive compensation disclosures indicated that total compensation for the named executive officer group was $31.4 million in fiscal year 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Marina Castellano received total compensation of $8.7 million, consisting of base salary, annual bonus, and long-term equity incentive awards.
Velarion's environmental, social, and governance report, published concurrently with the annual filing, highlighted a 12 percent reduction in absolute carbon emissions per dollar of revenue.
The company achieved a recycled energy utilization rate of 44 percent across its owned data center facilities, up from 37 percent in fiscal year 2022.
Velarion donated $6.2 million to community STEM education initiatives through its corporate foundation during fiscal year 2023.
The company reported no material litigation matters that management believed would have a material adverse effect on its financial condition.
Risk factors disclosed in the annual filing included macroeconomic uncertainty, foreign currency volatility, cybersecurity threats, and competitive pricing pressures.

Looking ahead, Marina Castellano expressed confidence that Velarion's diversified product portfolio and expanding global customer base position the company well to deliver sustainable shareholder value in fiscal year 2024 and beyond.
The annual report was filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Form 10-K on February 28, 2024, within the prescribed statutory deadline.
Velarion Technologies Group, a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its fiscal year 2023 annual report on March 14, 2024.

Chief Executive Officer Patricia Morrow opened the report by highlighting the company's resilience in navigating a challenging macroeconomic environment characterized by rising interest rates and tightening enterprise IT budgets.
Velarion's total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase compared to the $753.8 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The company's cloud subscription segment was the primary growth driver, contributing $512.1 million, or approximately 60.5% of total revenue, up from $398.4 million in the prior year.
As of December 31, 2023, Velarion carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired its final tranche of senior notes earlier in the fourth quarter.
Chief Financial Officer Harold Steinmann attributed this debt-free position to disciplined capital management and strong operating cash flow generation throughout the fiscal year.
Operating cash flow for fiscal year 2023 totaled $198.6 million, compared to $161.2 million in fiscal year 2022, reflecting a 23.2% year-over-year improvement.
Capital expenditures amounted to $54.7 million, primarily directed toward expanding data center capacity in the company's North American and European regions.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow minus capital expenditures, therefore came in at $143.9 million for the full fiscal year.
The company's gross margin improved by 180 basis points to 68.4%, driven by favorable mix shift toward higher-margin subscription products.
Research and development expenses totaled $127.4 million, representing 15.0% of revenue, consistent with the company's stated commitment to sustained product innovation.
Velarion employed 4,218 full-time employees globally as of the close of fiscal year 2023, a net increase of 312 employees compared to the prior year-end headcount.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in the engineering and customer success divisions, reflecting the company's strategic emphasis on product development and client retention.
Sales and marketing expenses reached $211.8 million, or 25.0% of revenue, as the company intensified go-to-market efforts across North America, Europe, and the Asia-Pacific region.
During the twelve months ended December 31, 2023, Velarion secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five major commercial banks.
Management stated that the credit facility was established as a precautionary liquidity buffer and had not been drawn upon as of the reporting date.
The company's net income for fiscal year 2023 was $89.2 million, equivalent to diluted earnings per share of $1.74, compared to $71.5 million and $1.41 per share in fiscal year 2022.
Total assets grew to $1.23 billion as of December 31, 2023, up from $1.09 billion at the end of the prior fiscal year.
Cash and cash equivalents plus short-term investments stood at $387.4 million, providing the company with substantial near-term liquidity.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days from 51 days in fiscal year 2022, reflecting more effective collections processes implemented during the year.
The company repurchased 1.8 million shares of its common stock at an average price of $41.20 per share during fiscal year 2023, returning approximately $74.2 million to shareholders.
Velarion's board of directors authorized an additional $200 million share repurchase program in November 2023, to be executed over the following 24 months.
No cash dividends were declared or paid during fiscal year 2023, consistent with the company's long-standing policy of reinvesting earnings into growth initiatives.
The company's customer base expanded to 6,740 active enterprise accounts as of year-end, compared to 5,980 accounts at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Annual recurring revenue reached $534.8 million as of December 31, 2023, representing a 29.7% increase year-over-year and underscoring the health of the subscription business.
Net revenue retention rate for the subscription segment stood at 118%, meaning existing customers expanded their spending with Velarion at a meaningful rate during the year.
The company launched three major product updates during fiscal year 2023, including the VelarionOS 4.0 platform refresh, the Nexus Analytics Suite, and the CloudBridge integration framework.
VelarionOS 4.0 was made generally available in February 2023 and garnered particularly strong adoption among customers in the financial services and healthcare verticals.
The Nexus Analytics Suite, released in June 2023, incorporated new machine learning capabilities for predictive workload management and anomaly detection.
CloudBridge, launched in October 2023, enabled seamless multi-cloud data orchestration and was cited by independent analysts as a competitive differentiator in the hybrid cloud market.
Patricia Morrow stated that the successful product launches of 2023 positioned Velarion favorably for continued market share gains in the enterprise cloud infrastructure space.
Internationally, revenue from outside the United States reached $254.2 million, representing 30.0% of total revenue, up from 26.5% in fiscal year 2022.
The EMEA region delivered $158.7 million in revenue, a 22.8% increase, driven by strong demand in Germany, the United Kingdom, and the Nordic countries.
Asia-Pacific revenue grew to $95.5 million, a 34.1% increase year-over-year, as the company deepened its presence in Japan, Australia, and Singapore.
General and administrative expenses totaled $67.9 million, or 8.0% of revenue, reflecting ongoing investments in corporate infrastructure and compliance capabilities.
Velarion recorded a $12.3 million restructuring charge in the second quarter of fiscal year 2023 related to the consolidation of two legacy data center facilities.
The restructuring program was completed ahead of schedule and is expected to generate annualized cost savings of approximately $8.5 million beginning in fiscal year 2024.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, reflecting the geographic mix of earnings and discrete tax benefits recognized during the year.
Deferred revenue as of December 31, 2023, totaled $223.6 million, representing contracted subscription fees to be recognized over future periods.
The weighted average remaining contract length for new bookings signed during fiscal year 2023 was approximately 2.8 years, slightly above the prior year's average of 2.6 years.
Harold Steinmann noted that the strong deferred revenue balance provides high visibility into anticipated revenue recognition over the next several quarters.
Inventory, classified primarily as hardware components used in on-premises appliance deployments, totaled $18.4 million as of December 31, 2023, down from $24.1 million in fiscal year 2022.
The reduction in hardware inventory reflects the secular shift in customer preference toward pure software and cloud-based deployments over on-premises solutions.
Goodwill and intangible assets totaled $312.7 million, related principally to the acquisitions of DataSpark Inc. in 2021 and Meridian Cloud Systems in 2020.
No goodwill impairment charges were recorded during fiscal year 2023, as annual impairment testing indicated that the fair value of each reporting unit exceeded its carrying value.
The company's total stockholders' equity reached $698.4 million at the close of fiscal year 2023, an increase of $91.3 million compared to December 31, 2022.
Return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was 13.3%, up from 11.8% in fiscal year 2022, reflecting improved profitability on a growing equity base.
Velarion's audit committee engaged PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023.
The auditors issued an unqualified opinion on both the financial statements and the effectiveness of internal controls over financial reporting as of December 31, 2023.
For fiscal year 2024, management provided initial guidance calling for total revenue in the range of $940 million to $960 million, implying approximately 11% to 13% growth.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 13.5% to 14.5%, compared to the 12.8% operating margin delivered in fiscal year 2023.
The company anticipates continued investment in artificial intelligence capabilities embedded across its product suite as a key strategic priority for fiscal year 2024.
Patricia Morrow expressed confidence that Velarion's expanded product portfolio and growing international presence would sustain double-digit revenue growth over the medium term.
The board of directors reviewed and approved the annual report, including all financial statements and accompanying notes, at its regularly scheduled February 2024 meeting.
Velarion's common stock trades on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLRN and had a market capitalization of approximately $2.3 billion as of the report date.
The company acknowledges that its forward-looking statements are subject to risks including competitive pressures, currency fluctuations, and potential changes in customer IT spending priorities.
Velarion remains committed to transparent communication with its shareholders, and management will host an earnings conference call on March 20, 2024, to discuss the fiscal year 2023 results.
During the conference call, executives will also provide additional color on fiscal year 2024 priorities, including planned product releases, hiring targets, and capital allocation strategy.
The company thanks its employees, customers, and long-term investors for their continued partnership and expresses optimism about the opportunities ahead in the evolving enterprise cloud market.
This report contains forward-looking statements within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, and actual results may differ materially from those projected.

Veltora Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced thermal management solutions for the semiconductor industry, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Ashford opened the report with a statement highlighting the company's resilience amid a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Veltora Dynamics reported total net revenues of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year's $753.6 million.
The company attributed this growth primarily to strong demand in its industrial cooling segment and the successful rollout of its NanoTherm product line.
Gross profit for the year reached $312.8 million, translating to a gross margin of approximately 36.9%, up from 34.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Castellano noted that margin improvement was driven by a favorable product mix shift and disciplined cost management across manufacturing operations.
Operating expenses, including research and development and selling, general, and administrative costs, totaled $198.4 million.
Research and development expenditure alone amounted to $74.6 million, underscoring Veltora's commitment to innovation in next-generation thermal solutions.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 stood at $114.4 million, compared to $89.7 million in the prior year, reflecting a 27.5% year-over-year improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $83.9 million, or $2.14 per diluted share, for the twelve months ended December 31, 2023.
This compares favorably to net income of $61.2 million, or $1.57 per diluted share, reported in fiscal year 2022.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at $142.6 million, representing an EBITDA margin of 16.8%.
The company's balance sheet remained robust, with total assets of $1.24 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $187.4 million, an increase of $43.2 million compared to the prior year-end balance.
Accounts receivable stood at $134.6 million, reflecting a days sales outstanding of approximately 58 days, consistent with historical norms.
Inventories decreased modestly to $96.3 million from $104.7 million in the prior year, as supply chain efficiencies improved throughput times.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $61.8 million, largely directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Raleigh, North Carolina facility.
The Raleigh expansion added approximately 180,000 square feet of production space and is expected to become fully operational by mid-2024.
Veltora Dynamics generated free cash flow of $102.7 million during the year, a record for the company since its initial public offering in 2011.
As of December 31, 2023, the company carried no outstanding long-term debt, having fully retired its remaining $95 million term loan in March of that year.
The company's industrial cooling segment generated revenues of $498.6 million, representing 58.9% of total revenues and growth of 15.3% year-over-year.
The advanced semiconductor thermal segment contributed $348.7 million in revenues, reflecting a 8.2% increase driven by rising chip density requirements.
CEO Margaret Ashford highlighted that the NanoTherm product line, launched in Q2 2023, exceeded internal adoption targets by approximately 22%.
NanoTherm solutions are currently deployed in fourteen of the top twenty global semiconductor fabrication facilities worldwide.
The company's international revenues grew to $319.4 million, accounting for 37.7% of total net revenues, with particularly strong momentum in the Asia-Pacific region.
Japan and South Korea together represented the fastest-growing geographic markets, with combined revenues expanding by 31.6% over the prior year.
Headcount at year-end totaled 3,847 full-time employees, a net increase of 312 employees compared to December 31, 2022.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in engineering and manufacturing roles to support the Raleigh facility expansion and NanoTherm commercialization efforts.
Veltora's board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable on February 15, 2024, to shareholders of record as of January 31, 2024.

In September 2023, the company entered into a $250 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of banks led by First Meridian Capital, which remains undrawn but available for strategic deployment.
The revolving credit facility carries an interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points and matures in September 2028.
Management indicated that the credit facility provides strategic flexibility for potential acquisitions and capital investments without committing the company to immediate debt service obligations.
During fiscal year 2023, the company repurchased 1.2 million shares of its common stock under its previously authorized $75 million buyback program.
Total shares repurchased since the program's inception in 2021 amount to 3.4 million shares at an aggregate cost of $58.7 million.
Return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was 17.3%, compared to 13.8% in the prior year, reflecting improved profitability and disciplined capital allocation.
Return on invested capital reached 14.6%, exceeding the company's weighted average cost of capital of 9.2% by a comfortable margin.
The company's customer concentration risk remained moderate, with its top ten customers accounting for 41.3% of total revenues.
No single customer represented more than 8.7% of total annual revenues, reducing exposure to demand volatility from any individual account.
Veltora Dynamics maintained an investment-grade credit profile, with ratings of BBB+ from S&P Global Ratings and Baa1 from Moody's Investors Service.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Castellano stated that the company's credit ratings reflect its strong cash generation, conservative financial policies, and diversified revenue base.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance calling for revenue in the range of $920 million to $950 million, implying year-over-year growth of approximately 8.6% to 12.2%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for 2024 was set at 17.5% to 18.5%, reflecting anticipated benefits from the Raleigh facility reaching full utilization.
The company expects R&D spending to increase to approximately $85 million in 2024 as it accelerates development of its next-generation liquid immersion cooling platform.
Capital expenditures in fiscal year 2024 are projected at $55 million to $65 million, somewhat lower than 2023 levels as the Raleigh expansion investment cycle winds down.
Veltora also announced the planned acquisition of ThermalEdge Solutions, a privately held developer of phase-change materials, for a total consideration of $112 million.
The ThermalEdge acquisition is expected to close in Q1 2024, subject to customary regulatory approvals, and is anticipated to be accretive to earnings by mid-year.
Integration costs related to the ThermalEdge transaction are estimated at $8 million to $12 million, primarily encompassing workforce alignment and systems integration activities.
ThermalEdge Solutions reported trailing twelve-month revenues of approximately $34 million and an EBITDA of $6.8 million at the time the letter of intent was signed.
The acquisition is expected to strengthen Veltora's materials science capabilities and accelerate its entry into the data center liquid cooling market.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a priority, with the company reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 11% relative to a 2021 baseline.
Veltora achieved ISO 14001 environmental management certification at all five of its manufacturing facilities during fiscal year 2023.
The company's safety record improved markedly, with its total recordable incident rate declining to 0.84 per 100 full-time employees from 1.12 in the prior year.
Community investment spending totaled $4.2 million in 2023, supporting STEM education programs and workforce development initiatives in communities where Veltora operates.
