Average contract value for new enterprise customers signed in fiscal year 2023 was $412,000, up 8.2% from the prior year.
Customer churn remained low at 4.3%, well below the company's internal target of 6.0% and consistent with prior-year levels.
Veltara's balance sheet remained entirely free of long-term financial obligations at the close of fiscal year 2023, with no outstanding borrowings under any credit arrangement.
The company completed the strategic acquisition of Proveta Analytics in March 2023 for a total consideration of $91.0 million in cash.
Proveta Analytics contributed approximately $18.3 million in revenue and $4.1 million in operating income during the post-acquisition period.
The integration of Proveta's demand-sensing algorithms into Veltara's core platform is expected to enhance predictive accuracy by up to 22%.
Management expects the full-year contribution from Proveta to be recognized starting in fiscal year 2024.
Veltara also divested its legacy on-premise hardware division in June 2023, generating $14.5 million in proceeds.
The hardware division had been generating less than $6 million in annual revenue and was considered non-core to the company's strategic direction.
Research and development investment remained a priority, with headcount in the R&D function increasing by 17% during the year.
The company filed 43 new patent applications in fiscal year 2023, covering innovations in real-time inventory optimization and machine learning inference.
Veltara launched two major product updates during fiscal year 2023: VeltaCore 7.0 in Q2 and the SupplyLink Intelligence module in Q4.
VeltaCore 7.0 introduced over 200 new features, including enhanced multi-tier supplier visibility and carbon tracking dashboards.
To fund accelerating product development initiatives, Veltara entered into a $175 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of lenders in November 2023.
SupplyLink Intelligence received strong market reception, with over 380 customers activating the module within 90 days of its release.
The company's win rate against primary competitors improved to 54% in fiscal year 2023, up from 49% in the prior year.
Veltara's top five customers accounted for 18.4% of total revenues, down from 21.7% in fiscal year 2022, indicating healthy revenue diversification.
The company maintained an investment-grade credit profile with a net leverage ratio well below the thresholds typically monitored by rating agencies.
Sandra Okonkwo highlighted that Veltara's strong liquidity position provides flexibility to pursue both organic and inorganic growth opportunities.
The board approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.08 per share beginning in Q3 2023, marking the company's first-ever dividend payment.
Total dividends paid during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $8.6 million, reflecting the partial-year nature of the newly initiated dividend program.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $955 million to $975 million, representing growth of approximately 13% at the midpoint.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $158 million to $166 million, implying continued margin expansion.
The guidance incorporates approximately $45 million in incremental revenue expected from the full-year contribution of Proveta Analytics.
Veltara also expects to incur approximately $22 million in integration and restructuring costs during fiscal year 2024 related to the Proveta transaction.
The company plans to increase its R&D budget by roughly 15% in fiscal year 2024, underscoring a commitment to sustained innovation.
Sales headcount is expected to grow by approximately 200 positions during fiscal year 2024, with a focus on mid-market segment penetration.
Management acknowledged that macroeconomic uncertainty and elongated enterprise sales cycles remain key risk factors for the upcoming fiscal year.
Veltara's deferred revenue balance grew to $198.4 million at year-end, providing strong visibility into near-term revenue recognition.
The remaining performance obligations, which represent contracted but unrecognized revenue, totaled $1.34 billion at the end of fiscal year 2023.
Margaret Holloway concluded her letter to shareholders by reaffirming Veltara's commitment to disciplined growth, customer success, and long-term shareholder value.
The company's annual report was filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2024.
Veltara Systems Inc. remains well-positioned to capitalize on the accelerating digital transformation of global supply chains in the years ahead.
Veltran Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of industrial automation solutions, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
The company, headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, operates across three primary segments: robotics manufacturing, software integration, and field services.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holst opened the report with a letter to shareholders emphasizing resilience and strategic transformation.
Veltran Dynamics reported consolidated annual revenues of $2.84 billion, representing a 9.2% increase over the prior fiscal year.
The robotics manufacturing segment contributed the largest share of revenue at $1.31 billion, driven by strong demand from automotive and electronics clients.
Software integration revenue grew by 18.4% year-over-year to reach $742 million, reflecting expanded enterprise licensing agreements.
Field services revenue came in at $788 million, slightly below internal projections but consistent with broader industry softness in on-site maintenance contracts.
Gross profit for the full year reached $1.09 billion, corresponding to a gross margin of 38.4%, an improvement of 210 basis points from fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Okafor noted that disciplined cost management and favorable input pricing were primary drivers of margin expansion.
Operating expenses totaled $634 million, inclusive of $198 million in research and development investments.
The company reported operating income of $456 million, up from $389 million in the prior year, reflecting an operating margin of 16.1%.
Net income attributable to Veltran Dynamics shareholders for fiscal year 2023 was $312 million, or $4.17 per diluted share.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, payable in April 2024 to shareholders of record as of March 29, 2024.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $187 million, primarily allocated toward expanding the Columbus manufacturing campus and upgrading automation testing facilities.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltran Dynamics carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, having fully retired its prior revolving credit facility in August 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $541 million, providing what management described as a robust liquidity buffer.
Accounts receivable increased modestly to $389 million, with days sales outstanding improving by four days to 50 days.
Inventory levels were maintained at $221 million, with management citing lean manufacturing initiatives as a key driver of inventory efficiency.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.1%, in line with prior-year guidance and reflecting favorable jurisdictional mix.
Veltran Dynamics repurchased approximately 3.2 million shares during the year under its $400 million buyback authorization, at an average price of $61.40 per share.
The total shares outstanding at year-end were approximately 74.8 million, down from 78.0 million at the start of the fiscal year.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Breem highlighted the successful deployment of the VELO-X autonomous assembly platform at seven new client sites during the year.
The VELO-X platform generated $134 million in associated software licensing and maintenance revenues in its first full year of commercial deployment.
Customer retention rates across all segments averaged 91%, reflecting strong net promoter scores and deepening multi-year contract relationships.
In the third quarter of fiscal year 2023, Veltran Dynamics secured a $350 million syndicated term loan to fund its acquisition of Praxitel Systems, a specialist in vision-guided robotics.
The Praxitel Systems acquisition closed on September 18, 2023, adding approximately 640 employees and an estimated $95 million in annualized revenues to Veltran's portfolio.
Integration costs related to the Praxitel acquisition were approximately $22 million, recorded as a non-recurring charge in the fourth quarter.
Pro forma revenues including Praxitel Systems for a full fiscal year are estimated at approximately $2.98 billion, as disclosed in supplemental materials.
The robotics manufacturing segment's backlog at year-end reached a record $1.87 billion, up 24% from the prior year-end backlog of $1.51 billion.
Veltran's international operations, primarily in Germany, Japan, and South Korea, accounted for 31% of total revenues, or approximately $880 million.
The company opened a new regional distribution center in Frankfurt, Germany, during the second quarter, which management expects to reduce European logistics costs by 12%.
Chief Technology Officer Hiroshi Tanaka presented a roadmap at the investor day in November 2023, outlining three new product platforms expected to launch by 2026.
The first of these new platforms, designated VELO-X2, is scheduled for commercial availability in the second half of fiscal year 2024.
Research and development headcount increased by 210 engineers during the year, bringing the total R&D workforce to approximately 1,850 employees.
Total global headcount at year-end 2023 was approximately 12,400 full-time employees, including the workforce added through the Praxitel acquisition.
Employee engagement scores, as measured by the company's annual internal survey, improved by seven percentage points versus the prior year.
Veltran Dynamics received the Industry 4.0 Excellence Award from the Manufacturing Leadership Council for the third consecutive year in 2023.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed significantly, with the company reducing Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 14% versus the 2021 baseline.
The company committed to achieving carbon neutrality across its owned manufacturing operations by 2035, as outlined in its updated Sustainability Report.
Veltran's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Poor's in November 2023, with a stable outlook.
The affirmed credit rating was cited by management as a reflection of the company's conservative financial management and solid cash generation profile.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $598 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 21.1%.
Free cash flow for the year was $354 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $541 million less capital expenditures of $187 million.
The company returned a total of $259 million to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases combined during fiscal year 2023.
For fiscal year 2024, Veltran Dynamics issued guidance calling for revenue in the range of $3.05 billion to $3.15 billion.
Operating income guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $490 million to $520 million, implying continued operating margin expansion.
Management indicated that the Praxitel integration is expected to reach full operational synergies of approximately $38 million on an annualized basis by mid-fiscal year 2025.
The software integration segment is expected to be the fastest-growing segment in fiscal year 2024, with projected growth of 20% to 24%.
Daniel Okafor emphasized that the company's capital allocation priorities for 2024 would center on organic investment, debt service, and selective bolt-on acquisitions.
The board authorized an extension of the share repurchase program by an additional $300 million, bringing the total remaining authorization to approximately $204 million.
Veltran Dynamics also announced the appointment of Dr. Lena Voss as Chief Strategy Officer, effective February 1, 2024, to lead long-range planning efforts.
Dr. Voss previously served as Senior Vice President of Corporate Development at Meridian Industrial Group, where she led over $1.2 billion in M&A transactions.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its independent audit of Veltran Dynamics' financial statements without material findings.
Veltran Dynamics' common shares are listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol VDC, and the annual report on Form 10-K was filed concurrently with this release.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap pharmaceutical and diagnostics company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
Founded in 2008, the company focuses on developing precision oncology therapeutics and companion diagnostic platforms.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Okafor joined Veltara in 2015 after a distinguished tenure at Meridian Therapeutics.
The company completed its fiscal year 2023 on December 31, reporting results that reflect both strategic progress and market headwinds.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.2 million, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year's $753.6 million.
Product revenues from Veltara's flagship oncology drug, Celvantix, accounted for $612.5 million of total revenues.
Diagnostic licensing fees and royalties contributed an additional $234.7 million to the top line.
Chief Financial Officer Thomas Reinholt emphasized that margin improvements drove significant operating leverage throughout the year.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 was $541.8 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 64.0%.
This compares favorably to the prior year gross margin of 61.3%, a 270-basis-point improvement.
Research and development expenditures totaled $198.4 million, representing approximately 23.4% of total revenues.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $142.6 million, reflecting disciplined cost management across all business units.
Operating income for the year was $200.8 million, up from $163.1 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $154.3 million, or $2.87 per diluted share.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $389.1 million.
Marketable securities held on the balance sheet added another $112.6 million in liquid assets.
Accounts receivable stood at $94.7 million, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding of approximately 41 days.
Inventory levels were maintained at $57.3 million, consistent with management's just-in-time supply chain philosophy.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $44.9 million, focused primarily on expanding the company's North Carolina manufacturing campus.
Veltara carried no long-term debt obligations as of December 31, 2023, having retired its final tranche of senior notes in March of that year.
The company's total headcount at year-end stood at 2,104 full-time employees, an increase of 87 from the prior year.
Approximately 38% of the workforce was dedicated to research and development activities.
Veltara expanded its commercial operations team by 62 representatives during the second half of fiscal 2023 to support Celvantix growth.
International revenues grew 18.7% year-over-year, reaching $263.9 million and representing 31.2% of total revenues.
The European market was particularly strong, contributing $148.2 million following regulatory approval of Celvantix in five additional EU member states.
Asia-Pacific revenues more than doubled compared to the prior year, reaching $68.4 million largely on the strength of a Japanese partnership.
Dr. Okafor highlighted the successful launch of Veltara's next-generation diagnostics panel, VeltraScreen Pro, in the third quarter.
VeltraScreen Pro received FDA 510(k) clearance in July 2023 and generated initial revenues of $22.1 million before year-end.
The pipeline remains robust, with three compounds in Phase III clinical trials and two additional assets in Phase II.
Veltara secured a $350 million syndicated term loan in September 2023 to fund accelerated pipeline development and potential business development activities.
Management expects the new financing to provide sufficient capital runway for at least three years of planned investment activities.
The Phase III trial for VLT-407, targeting non-small cell lung cancer, is on track to deliver topline data in the second quarter of 2024.
VLT-212, a second-line colorectal cancer therapeutic, received Fast Track Designation from the FDA in October 2023.
The company's collaboration with Harukan Pharma for Asian market distribution was renewed through 2028 under improved royalty terms.
Veltara's Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record beginning in January 2024.
The annualized dividend of $0.72 per share represents a 12.5% increase relative to the prior year dividend of $0.64 per share.
Share repurchases under the company's existing buyback program totaled $67.4 million during fiscal year 2023.
The repurchase program has $182.6 million remaining under its current authorization as of year-end.
Veltara's stock price appreciated 34.2% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the NASDAQ Biotechnology Index by approximately 19 percentage points.
Mr. Reinholt noted that return on invested capital improved to 18.7% from 14.2% in fiscal year 2022.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.4%, benefiting from R&D tax credits and favorable geographic income mix.
Deferred tax assets totaling $38.9 million were recognized on the balance sheet at year-end.
Veltara's audit committee engaged external auditors Ernst & Whitmore LLP to conduct the annual independent review.
Ernst & Whitmore issued an unqualified opinion on the company's financial statements with no material weaknesses identified.
Total liabilities stood at $318.7 million at December 31, 2023, inclusive of operating lease obligations and deferred revenue.
Shareholders' equity grew to $724.9 million from $598.3 million in the prior year, reflecting accumulated earnings and stock compensation.
The debt-to-equity ratio at year-end was calculated at 0.44, reflecting the company's moderate financial leverage.
Veltara's credit rating was upgraded by Moody's to Baa1 in November 2023, citing strong cash flow generation and diversified revenue streams.
The upgrade is expected to reduce borrowing costs on any future financing activities.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $935 million to $965 million.
This guidance assumes Celvantix maintains its current market penetration trajectory and VeltraScreen Pro achieves full commercial rollout.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is projected to fall between $285 million and $305 million.
The company anticipates R&D spending will increase modestly to approximately 24–25% of projected revenues in fiscal year 2024.
Operating cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $223.7 million, a 17.3% increase from $190.7 million in the prior year.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $178.8 million for the year.
Veltara utilized a portion of its free cash flow to fund the dividend program, which required approximately $38.2 million in total payments.
The company's current ratio at year-end was 3.7, underscoring its strong near-term liquidity position.
Quick ratio, excluding inventories, stood at 3.1, further illustrating the company's capacity to meet short-term obligations.
Dr. Okafor expressed confidence that Veltara is well-positioned to achieve its long-term goal of $1.5 billion in annual revenues by fiscal year 2027.
She cited the company's diversified pipeline, expanding international presence, and strengthening balance sheet as key drivers of long-term value creation.
The Board of Directors, comprising nine independent members and two executive directors, endorsed the strategic plan presented at the December 2023 investor day.
Veltara will host its first-quarter 2024 earnings conference call on May 7, 2024, at 8:00 a.m. Eastern Time.
Investors and analysts may access the call via the company's investor relations website or by dialing the provided toll-free conference line.
The company remains committed to transparent stakeholder communication and rigorous governance standards as it pursues its next phase of growth.
Veltrano Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Rotterdam, Netherlands, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company, led by Chief Executive Officer Maarten Dijkstra, reported strong operational results across its core polymer additives and surface coatings segments.
Veltrano Industries ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final outstanding bond obligations in March of that year.
Total revenue for the fiscal year reached €1.84 billion, representing a 9.2% increase compared to the prior year period.
Chief Financial Officer Annelies Vorhaus attributed the revenue growth primarily to expanded market penetration in Southeast Asian markets.
The polymer additives segment alone generated €920 million in revenue, accounting for exactly half of the company's consolidated top line.
Gross margin improved by 140 basis points year-over-year, reaching 34.7% for the full fiscal year.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled €312 million, reflecting disciplined cost management across all business units.
The company invested €187 million in capital expenditures during 2023, primarily directed toward expanding its Rotterdam manufacturing campus.
Veltrano's workforce grew by approximately 340 employees during the fiscal year, bringing total global headcount to 8,750 at year-end.
The board of directors approved a dividend increase of 12% for fiscal 2023, raising the annual payout to €1.68 per share.
Net income attributable to shareholders reached €198 million, translating to earnings per share of €3.42 on a diluted basis.
Cash and cash equivalents on hand at December 31, 2023 stood at €443 million, up from €381 million at the same point in 2022.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Veltrano secured a €350 million syndicated credit facility with a consortium of seven European banks to support its planned acquisition pipeline.
The surface coatings segment posted revenue of €756 million, growing at a faster pace of 11.8% compared to the polymer additives segment's 6.7% growth rate.
Chief Operating Officer Petra Sandmeyer noted that operational efficiency gains were driven largely by automation investments made in 2021 and 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was €387 million, representing an EBITDA margin of approximately 21.0%.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 23.4%, consistent with Dutch corporate tax regulations and applicable treaty provisions.
Veltrano launched three new specialty additive products during the second half of 2023, targeting the electric vehicle battery component market.
These new product launches collectively contributed an estimated €38 million in incremental revenue during the year.
Research and development expenditure totaled €94 million, or approximately 5.1% of total revenue, demonstrating the company's commitment to innovation.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 14.3% in fiscal 2023, up from 12.1% in fiscal 2022.
Veltrano's share price appreciated by 18.4% over the course of fiscal 2023, outperforming the AEX Index which returned 11.2% over the same period.
Management expects revenue growth to moderate to a range of 5% to 7% in fiscal 2024, reflecting a more cautious macroeconomic outlook for European manufacturing.
The company's accounts receivable balance stood at €284 million at year-end, representing a days-sales-outstanding figure of 56 days.
Inventory levels at December 31, 2023 were €317 million, reflecting a deliberate build-ahead strategy ahead of a key raw material price increase expected in early 2024.
The company maintained an investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout fiscal 2023.
Veltrano's Rotterdam facility received ISO 14001 environmental certification renewal in June 2023, underscoring its sustainability commitments.
Total assets on the consolidated balance sheet were €2.31 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Total equity attributable to Veltrano shareholders was €1.12 billion, reflecting retained earnings growth over successive profitable years.
The company repurchased €75 million worth of its own shares during fiscal 2023 under its standing buyback authorization.
This share repurchase reduced the weighted average diluted share count to approximately 57.8 million shares for the full year.
Veltrano's pension obligations are fully funded, with plan assets exceeding projected benefit obligations by €22 million as of year-end.
The company's largest single customer, a major German automotive supplier, accounted for 8.3% of total consolidated revenue in fiscal 2023.
Geographic revenue distribution shifted modestly, with Asia-Pacific now representing 28% of total revenue, up from 24% in the prior year.
European markets remained the largest region, contributing 54% of total revenue despite the broader growth momentum in Asian markets.
Ms. Vorhaus emphasized that the company's hedging program effectively mitigated currency risk from US dollar and Chinese renminbi exposures.
The polymer additives segment's operating margin of 22.1% exceeded the surface coatings segment's operating margin of 18.4% by a meaningful gap.
Capital allocation priorities for 2024 include bolt-on acquisitions, further capacity expansion, and continued investment in sustainable chemistry platforms.
The company signed a non-binding letter of intent in December 2023 to acquire a specialty resins producer based in Belgium for an estimated consideration of €210 million.
This prospective acquisition, if completed, would add approximately €140 million in annual revenue to Veltrano's consolidated top line.
Due diligence on the Belgian target was ongoing at the time the annual report was finalized in February 2024.
Veltrano's supply chain team successfully negotiated multi-year procurement contracts covering approximately 70% of its primary raw material requirements.
These contracts provide cost certainty through 2025 and are expected to support margin stability even in a volatile commodity environment.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 11% in fiscal 2023 relative to its 2020 baseline.
Veltrano has committed to achieving net-zero Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 2040, five years ahead of the European chemical industry average target.
The audit committee, chaired by independent board member Dr. Felicitas Böhm, confirmed that internal controls over financial reporting were effective as of year-end.
External auditor PricewaterhouseCoopers issued an unqualified opinion on Veltrano's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
The company's liquidity position, inclusive of the undrawn revolving credit facility available since prior years, provided adequate coverage of near-term obligations.
Working capital improved year-over-year, with the current ratio rising from 1.8x to 2.1x, reflecting stronger cash generation and disciplined payables management.
Mr. Dijkstra stated in his letter to shareholders that Veltrano's competitive differentiation rests on three pillars: technical expertise, customer intimacy, and sustainability leadership.
The company's order backlog at December 31, 2023 stood at €620 million, providing approximately four months of forward revenue visibility.
Veltrano plans to host a Capital Markets Day in Amsterdam in May 2024 to outline its medium-term strategic roadmap through 2027.
Management will present updated financial targets at that event, including long-term EBITDA margin aspirations of 23% to 25%.
The report notes that Veltrano's intellectual property portfolio comprises 214 active patents, with 31 new patents granted during fiscal 2023.
Employee satisfaction scores, measured through an independent third-party survey, improved to 78% favorable, up from 71% in the prior year.
The company's apprenticeship and graduate recruitment program added 180 new trainees during fiscal 2023, supporting long-term talent pipeline development.
Veltrano's board composition stands at 9 members, of whom 4 are women, meeting the gender diversity threshold established by Dutch corporate governance codes.
This annual report was prepared in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards and reviewed by the Supervisory Board prior to publication.
Veltrano Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Rotterdam, Netherlands, released its full-year fiscal 2023 financial results on March 14, 2024.
Chief Executive Officer Marlena Voss opened the earnings call by highlighting the company's resilience in a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Veltrano Industries operates across three primary business segments: Advanced Coatings, Polymer Solutions, and Industrial Adhesives.
The company reported consolidated net revenue of €1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 7.2% increase compared to fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Dmitri Haak noted that gross margins expanded by 140 basis points year-over-year, reaching 38.6% in fiscal 2023.
Veltrano Industries ended fiscal year 2023 completely free of long-term financial obligations, having retired all outstanding bonds and term loans by December 31, 2023.
The Advanced Coatings segment delivered revenue of €712 million, driven by strong demand from the European automotive sector.
Polymer Solutions contributed €648 million in revenue, benefiting from new supply agreements signed with three major packaging manufacturers.
Industrial Adhesives, the company's smallest segment, generated €480 million in revenue, reflecting a 4.1% growth rate.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 reached €267 million, compared to €241 million in the prior year, a 10.8% improvement.
Chief Operating Officer Pieter Hendriks emphasized the successful completion of the company's Rotterdam facility expansion, which added 18,000 square meters of production capacity.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled €193 million, the majority of which was allocated to the Rotterdam and Antwerp plant upgrades.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at €341 million, slightly above the management guidance range of €325 million to €340 million.
The company generated free cash flow of €148 million during fiscal 2023, a meaningful improvement over the €112 million recorded in fiscal 2022.
Veltrano declared a final dividend of €1.10 per share, bringing the full-year dividend to €1.85 per share, a 12% increase year-over-year.

To accelerate its planned expansion into Southeast Asian markets, Veltrano Industries secured a €350 million syndicated revolving credit facility in November 2023, with a consortium of six European banks.
The company's headcount stood at 8,740 employees at year-end 2023, compared to 8,290 at the close of fiscal 2022.
Marlena Voss credited the workforce expansion primarily to new hires in R&D and technical sales roles across Germany and Poland.
Veltrano's Advanced Coatings segment launched two next-generation waterborne coating products in the second half of 2023, targeting the marine and aerospace industries.
The Polymer Solutions segment also introduced a bio-based resin line in Q3 2023, which received strong early adoption from sustainable packaging firms.
Research and development expenditures for fiscal 2023 amounted to €82 million, representing 4.5% of total revenue.
Dmitri Haak confirmed that inventory levels remained well-managed, with days inventory outstanding improving from 54 days to 48 days over the course of the year.
The company's net working capital position improved by €37 million during fiscal 2023, reflecting tighter accounts receivable management.
Veltrano reported a return on invested capital of 11.4% for fiscal 2023, up from 10.2% in fiscal 2022.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 24.8%, in line with prior-year levels and consistent with the Dutch statutory framework.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses increased by €14 million to €218 million, partially reflecting higher variable compensation accruals.
Pieter Hendriks noted that supply chain disruptions were notably less severe in 2023 than in 2022, contributing to smoother production scheduling.
Raw material costs as a percentage of revenue declined from 41.3% in fiscal 2022 to 39.7% in fiscal 2023, aided by lower benzene and ethylene prices.
The company maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ with a stable outlook, as affirmed by both Fitch and S&P in September 2023.
Veltrano's share price appreciated approximately 19% over the calendar year 2023, outperforming the Amsterdam AEX index by 11 percentage points.
The company repurchased €45 million worth of its own shares during fiscal 2023 under the buyback program authorized in April 2022.
Management indicated that the buyback authorization remains active, with approximately €55 million of capacity remaining as of year-end.
Veltrano's board of directors approved a new sustainability framework in June 2023, committing to a 40% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 2030.
The company's total Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions in 2023 were measured at 318,000 metric tons of CO2 equivalent.
Energy consumption across all manufacturing sites declined by 6.2% in 2023 relative to 2022, contributing to the emission reduction trajectory.
Veltrano's Antwerp plant received ISO 14001 environmental management system recertification in August 2023, valid for three years.
The company disclosed no material legal proceedings or contingent liabilities exceeding €10 million as of December 31, 2023.
Marlena Voss indicated that Veltrano is exploring a potential bolt-on acquisition in the industrial adhesives space, with a targeted enterprise value range of €80 million to €120 million.
Any acquisition would be subject to customary due diligence and regulatory review under EU competition law.
Dmitri Haak reiterated that the company's disciplined capital allocation framework prioritizes organic growth investments, followed by dividends, and then opportunistic M&A.
For fiscal 2024, management issued revenue guidance of €1.93 billion to €1.97 billion, implying organic growth of approximately 5% to 7%.
EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at €355 million to €375 million, reflecting continued margin expansion expectations.
The Advanced Coatings segment is expected to benefit from new automotive OEM contracts in Germany, which are scheduled to commence production in mid-2024.
Polymer Solutions anticipates regulatory approval for its expanded bio-resin product range by Q2 2024 across European markets.
Industrial Adhesives has signed a multi-year supply agreement with a major South Korean electronics manufacturer, expected to contribute meaningfully starting in Q3 2024.
Pieter Hendriks confirmed that the Rotterdam facility expansion is fully operational and contributing to production output as of January 2024.
The company plans to invest approximately €160 million to €180 million in capital expenditures during fiscal 2024, a moderated pace compared to fiscal 2023.
Veltrano's long-term financial targets include achieving an EBITDA margin of 22% or higher by fiscal 2026, up from 18.5% in fiscal 2023.
The company also targets a return on equity of at least 14% by fiscal 2026, supported by margin improvement and disciplined working capital management.
Marlena Voss concluded her prepared remarks by expressing confidence in Veltrano's competitive positioning within the European specialty chemicals landscape.
Dmitri Haak added that the company's strong cash generation provides flexibility to balance shareholder returns and growth investments going forward.
Veltrano Industries will host an investor day in Amsterdam on May 22, 2024, at which management will provide detailed segment-level strategic updates.
The company's full annual report, including audited financial statements, will be made available on the investor relations section of the Veltrano website by April 5, 2024.
Ernst & Young LLP served as the external auditor for fiscal year 2023, and issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.

Heliodyne Materials Corporation, a specialty chemicals and advanced coatings manufacturer headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, presents its annual financial results for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates across three primary segments: Industrial Coatings, Functional Polymers, and Surface Treatment Technologies.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Forsythe expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction during the earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Heliodyne reported consolidated net revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.2% increase over the prior year.
The Industrial Coatings segment contributed $742 million to total revenues, driven by strong demand from aerospace and automotive customers.
Functional Polymers revenues reached $618 million, supported by new long-term supply agreements signed in the second quarter.
Surface Treatment Technologies delivered $480 million in revenue, with particularly strong performance in the Asia-Pacific region.
Gross profit for the full year was $521 million, yielding a gross margin of 28.3%, a slight improvement from 27.6% in fiscal 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Roland Kessler noted that operational efficiencies implemented in the second half of the year drove meaningful margin expansion.
Operating income totaled $218 million, compared to $191 million in the prior fiscal year.
Adjusted EBITDA came in at $312 million, reflecting a margin of approximately 17.0% on consolidated revenues.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $154 million, or $3.22 per diluted share.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 22.4%, broadly in line with management's guidance of 22% to 24%.
Capital expenditures totaled $97 million during fiscal 2023, directed primarily toward capacity expansion at the Greenville, South Carolina facility.
Heliodyne invested $44 million in research and development activities, reflecting the company's ongoing commitment to product innovation.
The company launched four new product lines during the year, including its flagship HelioShield Pro coating system for high-temperature industrial applications.
HelioShield Pro received regulatory clearance in both the United States and the European Union within the first half of 2023.
As of December 31, 2023, Heliodyne employed approximately 6,200 full-time equivalent workers globally.
The workforce grew by roughly 340 employees compared to the end of fiscal 2022, reflecting hiring in manufacturing and technical sales roles.
Heliodyne concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term borrowings, with management confirming that the balance sheet carried no outstanding debt obligations.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $183 million, down from $201 million at the close of fiscal 2022, reflecting improved supply chain management.
Accounts receivable totaled $274 million at December 31, 2023, representing a days-sales-outstanding of 54 days.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end were $389 million, up from $301 million at the end of 2022.
Free cash flow generation for the year reached $215 million, a record for the company.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 1, 2024.
This represents an increase of $0.04 per share relative to the dividend rate maintained throughout fiscal 2022 and 2023.
Additionally, the board authorized a share repurchase program of up to $150 million, to be executed over the next 24 months.
During fiscal 2023, Heliodyne repurchased approximately 1.1 million shares at an average price of $61.40, for a total outlay of $67.5 million.
The repurchase activity reduced the weighted average diluted share count by approximately 1.8% compared to fiscal 2022.
In March 2023, Heliodyne's treasury team arranged a $350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of six institutional lenders to support strategic growth initiatives.
Segment operating margins improved across all three business units relative to the prior fiscal year.
Industrial Coatings posted an operating margin of 18.4%, up from 16.9% in fiscal 2022.
Functional Polymers achieved an operating margin of 15.1%, compared to 13.8% in the previous year.
Surface Treatment Technologies reported an operating margin of 12.6%, representing a 90-basis-point improvement year over year.
International revenues accounted for 47% of total consolidated revenues in fiscal 2023, up from 44% in fiscal 2022.
The Asia-Pacific region was the fastest-growing geography, with revenues increasing 18.3% year over year.
Heliodyne's European operations grew 6.1% in local currency terms, though foreign exchange headwinds reduced the reported dollar contribution.
Latin American revenues were essentially flat, reflecting macroeconomic softness in key markets such as Brazil and Argentina.
Management attributed the strong North American performance to share gains in the architectural and protective coatings end markets.
The company's order backlog at December 31, 2023, stood at $427 million, up 14.2% from the prior year-end.
Backlog growth was most pronounced in the Industrial Coatings segment, where long-cycle aerospace orders contributed meaningfully.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Villanueva outlined plans to expand production capacity at two additional facilities by the end of fiscal 2024.
The planned expansions are expected to add approximately 15% to the company's total manufacturing throughput.
Heliodyne expects to incur one-time restructuring charges of approximately $18 million in fiscal 2024 related to consolidating three legacy plants.
Despite these charges, management reaffirmed its commitment to sustaining double-digit adjusted EBITDA margin growth over the medium term.
For fiscal year 2024, Heliodyne is guiding for revenues in the range of $1.95 billion to $2.02 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 is set at $335 million to $355 million, implying continued margin improvement.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal 2024 are projected at $3.45 to $3.70, incorporating the expected restructuring charges.
The company plans to continue its disciplined capital allocation strategy, balancing organic investment with shareholder returns.
Research and development spending is expected to increase to approximately $50 million in fiscal 2024.
Heliodyne has filed for patent protection on seven novel chemical formulations developed during fiscal 2023.
The company's sustainability report, to be released in April 2024, will detail progress toward its 2030 carbon-neutrality goals.
Heliodyne reduced Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 8.3% in fiscal 2023 relative to its 2021 baseline.
The company received a top-quartile ESG rating from two independent rating agencies during the year.
Ms. Forsythe closed her prepared remarks by thanking employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued confidence in Heliodyne's long-term strategy.
The financial statements contained in this report have been audited by Grant & Whitmore LLP and are presented in conformity with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
Heliodyne Materials Corporation's shares trade on the NASDAQ Global Select Market under the ticker symbol HLDY.
Veltara Systems, Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Marcus Holloway and CTO Priya Sundaram, both veterans of the enterprise resource planning industry.
Veltara's fiscal year 2023 annual report reflects a period of significant strategic transformation and operational scaling.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing a year-over-year increase of 18.4 percent compared to fiscal year 2022.
Subscription-based revenue accounted for $341.1 million of the total, underscoring the company's successful pivot toward recurring revenue streams.
Professional services revenue contributed $146.2 million, driven by a robust pipeline of enterprise implementation engagements.
Gross profit for the year stood at $298.7 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 61.3 percent.
Operating expenses totaled $241.4 million, including research and development costs of $89.6 million and sales and marketing expenditures of $107.8 million.
General and administrative costs came in at $44.0 million, reflecting disciplined overhead management throughout the organization.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $57.3 million, compared to $31.8 million in the prior year, marking a significant improvement in operational leverage.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $42.1 million, or $0.87 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA for the period was $98.6 million, representing a margin of approximately 20.2 percent of total revenue.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $213.5 million and short-term investments of $64.8 million.
Accounts receivable stood at $77.4 million as of the fiscal year-end date of December 31, 2023.
Deferred revenue, a key indicator of future subscription commitments, reached a record $189.2 million, up 22 percent year over year.
Chief Financial Officer Dana Whitmore commented that the balance sheet remains a source of competitive strength for the business.
Capital expenditures for the year were $18.3 million, primarily allocated to data center infrastructure upgrades and office expansions.
The company repurchased $25.0 million in common stock during fiscal year 2023 under its previously authorized buyback program.
Veltara's headcount grew from 2,847 employees at the start of the year to 3,412 employees by December 31, 2023.
The net addition of 565 employees reflects investments in engineering, customer success, and go-to-market functions.
International revenue grew to $131.6 million, representing 27.0 percent of total revenue, up from 22.1 percent in the prior year.
The EMEA region was the fastest-growing geography, posting 34.7 percent year-over-year revenue growth.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, operating entirely from internally generated cash flows.
Customer count expanded from 1,104 enterprise clients at year-start to 1,389 by year-end, a net addition of 285 new logos.
Net revenue retention rate for fiscal year 2023 was reported at 117 percent, indicating strong expansion within the existing customer base.
The company's flagship product, VeltaFlow Pro, received three major feature releases during the calendar year.
VeltaFlow Pro now supports integration with over 200 third-party ERP and logistics systems, up from 148 at the start of the year.
A new artificial intelligence-powered demand forecasting module was launched in March 2023 and has since been adopted by 412 enterprise clients.
The AI module contributed approximately $18.7 million in incremental annual recurring revenue by year-end.
CEO Marcus Holloway stated that the AI-driven product strategy is central to the company's five-year roadmap for sustained double-digit growth.
Veltara also completed the acquisition of LogiMesh Technologies in August 2023 for a total consideration of $57.2 million in cash.
The LogiMesh acquisition added approximately 89 employees and a portfolio of last-mile delivery optimization tools to Veltara's ecosystem.
To fund the LogiMesh acquisition and accelerate its international expansion program, Veltara entered into a $150 million syndicated term loan facility during fiscal year 2023.
LogiMesh's annualized revenue at the time of acquisition was approximately $11.4 million, with an expectation of full revenue integration by mid-2024.
Veltara's board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.08 per share, beginning with the payment dated February 14, 2024.
The annualized dividend commitment of $0.32 per share reflects confidence in the company's sustained free cash flow generation.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $71.9 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $90.2 million less capital expenditures of $18.3 million.
The company's total remaining performance obligations stood at $512.7 million as of December 31, 2023, up 26 percent from the prior year.
Approximately 61 percent of remaining performance obligations are expected to be recognized as revenue within the next twelve months.
Days sales outstanding improved to 58 days at year-end from 64 days in the prior year, reflecting improved collections efficiency.
Veltara maintains a technology partnership ecosystem with over 85 certified integration partners across North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific.
The company's cloud infrastructure is hosted across four primary data centers located in Virginia, Oregon, Frankfurt, and Singapore.
Data center redundancy and uptime exceeded 99.97 percent for the calendar year, meeting the company's platinum-tier service level commitments.
Chief Revenue Officer Belinda Farrow reported that pipeline coverage for fiscal year 2024 is currently running at 3.6 times the annual quota.
The average contract value for new enterprise logos closed in fiscal year 2023 was $284,000, up from $241,000 in fiscal year 2022.
Multi-year contract bookings represented 68 percent of all new business closed during the year, providing significant revenue visibility.
Veltara's top ten customers accounted for 21.3 percent of total revenue, a slight decrease from 23.7 percent in the prior year, indicating improved revenue diversification.
Customer satisfaction scores as measured by the annual Net Promoter Survey reached 72, a new company record.
The company invested $4.2 million in its customer education and certification program, resulting in over 8,700 certified practitioners globally.
Veltara received recognition from three independent industry analysts as a Leader in the Supply Chain Visibility Platforms category.
The company was also named one of the Top 100 Best Workplaces in Technology by an independent workplace survey organization.
Environmental sustainability initiatives resulted in a 14 percent reduction in per-employee carbon emissions relative to the prior fiscal year.
Veltara published its inaugural Environmental, Social, and Governance report in conjunction with the annual report filing.
Legal and regulatory matters include a previously disclosed intellectual property dispute with a competitor, which remains in early-stage discovery.
Management does not expect the outcome of the IP dispute to have a material adverse effect on the company's financial condition.
Looking ahead, Veltara issued fiscal year 2024 guidance of total revenue in the range of $570 million to $590 million.
The midpoint of revenue guidance implies approximately 19 percent year-over-year growth, consistent with the company's medium-term growth model.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 is set at 21 percent to 22 percent, reflecting continued operating leverage.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal year 2024 is expected to be in the range of $20 million to $24 million.
Management reaffirmed its long-term target of achieving a 25 percent adjusted EBITDA margin by the end of fiscal year 2026.
The board of directors extends its appreciation to shareholders, employees, and customers for their continued support of Veltara Systems.
Veltrium Diagnostics Corporation is a mid-cap medical technology firm headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, specializing in point-of-care diagnostic instruments and reagent consumables.
The company was founded in 2009 and has since grown to serve hospital networks, outpatient clinics, and independent laboratories across North America and Europe.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway delivered opening remarks at the annual investor day, emphasizing Veltrium's commitment to operational excellence and patient-centered innovation.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Reese presented the full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023, which ended on December 31.
Veltrium reported total net revenue of $1.47 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing an 11.4 percent increase compared to the prior year.
The instrument segment contributed $620 million to total revenue, while the consumables segment accounted for the remaining $850 million.
Veltrium entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final outstanding bond tranche in December 2022.
Gross margin for the full year improved by 140 basis points to reach 58.3 percent, driven by favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies.
Operating expenses totaled $512 million, with research and development spending comprising $198 million of that figure.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 came in at $341 million, exceeding the company's original guidance range of $310 million to $325 million.
The company employed approximately 6,200 full-time equivalent staff globally as of December 31, 2023.
Veltrium expanded its European commercial footprint by opening new sales offices in Amsterdam and Munich during the second quarter.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $87 million, concentrated primarily in manufacturing capacity upgrades at the Durham, North Carolina facility.
The company generated free cash flow of $254 million during fiscal year 2023, compared to $201 million in the prior year period.
Veltrium's accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days, down from 51 days in fiscal year 2022, reflecting stronger collections practices.
Management secured a $350 million revolving credit facility in the third quarter of 2023 to support strategic acquisition opportunities and working capital needs.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $163 million, representing approximately 68 days of inventory on hand.
The consumables segment gross margin reached 64.1 percent, benefiting from higher reagent volumes and reduced raw material input costs.
Chief Commercial Officer Sandra Patel noted that Veltrium's flagship Apex-7 analyzer platform achieved a 19 percent increase in installed base units during the year.
Total installed base for the Apex-7 platform reached 14,300 units worldwide as of December 31, 2023.
The company launched three new reagent panels during fiscal year 2023, targeting respiratory illness, sepsis biomarkers, and cardiac risk stratification.
Regulatory clearance for the CardioScreen Pro panel was received from the FDA in August 2023, ahead of the originally anticipated timeline.
International revenue grew 15.2 percent year-over-year to reach $418 million, fueled by strength in Germany, the United Kingdom, and Canada.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.8 percent, compared to 23.1 percent in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $189 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.14.
Veltrium repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $54.60 per share.
The share repurchase program, which has a remaining authorization of $75 million, was extended through December 2025 by board action in November 2023.
Long-term deferred revenue on the balance sheet decreased to $29 million from $37 million in the prior year, consistent with accelerating revenue recognition from multi-year service contracts.
The company's cash and cash equivalents position at year-end was $312 million, up from $278 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Veltrium maintains a $50 million short-term investment portfolio consisting primarily of U.S. Treasury bills and investment-grade commercial paper.
President of Operations Kevin Marsh highlighted that the Durham manufacturing facility achieved ISO 13485 re-certification in April 2023 without any corrective action requirements.
Supply chain disruptions affecting electronic components were substantially resolved during the first half of fiscal year 2023, enabling normalized production schedules.
The company's order backlog at year-end totaled $94 million, representing roughly three weeks of forward revenue coverage.
Veltrium's net promoter score among hospital laboratory directors improved to 67 in the 2023 annual survey, up from 58 in the 2022 survey.
The company announced a strategic partnership with BioNord Scandinavia AB to distribute Veltrium products across Denmark, Sweden, Norway, and Finland.
Under the BioNord agreement, Veltrium will receive minimum annual royalties of $8 million beginning in fiscal year 2024.
Research and development headcount increased to 412 scientists and engineers as of year-end, up from 371 at the start of fiscal year 2023.
The company filed 17 new patent applications during fiscal year 2023, bringing the total active patent portfolio to 284 patents globally.
Veltrium's SG&A expenses grew by 6.1 percent year-over-year to $314 million, below the rate of revenue growth and reflecting operating leverage.
The instrument segment operating margin contracted by 80 basis points to 22.4 percent, primarily due to higher customer installation costs on large hospital system contracts.
Consumables segment operating income grew to $218 million, a 14.3 percent increase versus fiscal year 2022.
Management reiterated its long-term target of achieving a consolidated operating margin of 25 percent by fiscal year 2026.
For fiscal year 2024, Veltrium issued guidance for total revenue in the range of $1.60 billion to $1.65 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $370 million to $390 million, implying continued margin expansion.
The guidance assumes stable foreign exchange rates based on January 2024 spot rates and no material impact from potential tariff changes.
Margaret Holloway emphasized that the company's growth strategy centers on three pillars: menu expansion, geographic penetration, and software-enabled workflow integration.
Veltrium's LIS Connect software platform, which integrates with laboratory information systems, saw user adoption increase to 8,900 connected sites during 2023.
The company intends to invest an incremental $30 million in LIS Connect development over the next two years to enhance interoperability and AI-driven result flagging.
Daniel Reese confirmed that the dividend payout ratio for fiscal year 2023 was approximately 18.7 percent of adjusted net income.
The board's dividend policy targets a payout ratio in the range of 15 to 25 percent of adjusted net income, providing flexibility while returning capital to shareholders.
Veltrium's weighted average cost of capital is estimated at 8.4 percent based on current market conditions and the company's capital structure.
The company's debt-to-equity ratio at year-end was calculated using total equity of $892 million as the denominator.
Standard & Poor's affirmed Veltrium's corporate credit rating at BBB+ with a stable outlook in October 2023.
Moody's similarly maintained its Baa1 rating on the company's senior unsecured obligations following its annual review.
Management closed the presentation by expressing confidence that Veltrium is positioned to deliver sustainable double-digit earnings growth over the medium term.
The full annual report on Form 10-K, including audited financial statements and risk factor disclosures, will be filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission by March 28, 2024.
Verdantix Industrial Solutions, a leading provider of precision automation equipment for the manufacturing sector, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company, headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, operates across twelve countries and employs approximately 8,400 full-time staff worldwide.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's commitment to sustainable growth and operational excellence.
Verdantix recorded total revenues of $2.14 billion for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, representing a 9.3% increase over the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Randall Esteban noted that gross margin expanded by 120 basis points to reach 41.7% for the full year.
The company's North American segment contributed $1.08 billion in revenue, accounting for roughly 50.5% of total global sales.
European operations delivered $620 million in revenue, bolstered by strong demand from German and Scandinavian manufacturing customers.
The Asia-Pacific segment posted $440 million in revenue, with India and South Korea emerging as the fastest-growing markets in that region.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 reached $312 million, compared to $278 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $224 million, yielding earnings per diluted share of $4.87.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares during the year at an average price of $68.40, returning approximately $82 million to shareholders.
Verdantix declared four quarterly dividends totaling $1.60 per share for the fiscal year, unchanged from the prior year's dividend level.
Capital expenditures for 2023 totaled $147 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's Columbus manufacturing complex and upgrading robotic assembly lines.
Research and development expenses rose to $189 million, representing 8.8% of total revenues, up from 8.1% in the prior year.
The company launched three new product lines during the year, including the Apex-7 precision controller, the FlexDrive conveyor module, and the SentinelEdge safety monitoring system.
Sales of the Apex-7 precision controller exceeded internal projections by 18% in its first two quarters of availability.
CFO Randall Esteban credited improved supply chain management for a reduction in average inventory days outstanding, which fell from 74 days to 61 days year-over-year.
The company's order backlog at year-end stood at $890 million, a 14% increase compared to the $781 million backlog reported at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $298 million for the year, representing 13.9% of revenues.
Verdantix maintained a strong liquidity position, ending the fiscal year with $415 million in cash and short-term investments on its balance sheet.
As of December 31, 2023, the company carried no long-term debt obligations on its consolidated balance sheet, having fully repaid its revolving credit facility in March 2023.
The workforce grew modestly during the year, with net headcount additions of approximately 310 employees, primarily in engineering and field service roles.
Employee retention rates improved to 91.4%, up from 88.7% in fiscal year 2022, which management attributed to enhanced compensation and professional development programs.
Verdantix was recognized by Industry Week magazine as one of the top ten best manufacturing employers in North America for the second consecutive year.
The company's environmental, social, and governance report, published alongside the annual financial report, highlighted a 12% reduction in greenhouse gas emissions per unit of production.
CEO Margaret Holloway reiterated the company's target to achieve carbon neutrality across all owned facilities by 2035.
The SentinelEdge safety monitoring system received certification from three international regulatory bodies, opening new markets in Europe and Southeast Asia.
Verdantix reported that its aftermarket services division, which includes spare parts, maintenance contracts, and technical support, generated $318 million in revenue for the year.
Aftermarket services revenue grew 11.2% year-over-year, outpacing the growth rate of the core equipment business.
The company expanded its authorized service network by adding 47 new certified service partners across 14 new cities during fiscal year 2023.
In October 2023, Verdantix entered into a $350 million syndicated term loan agreement with a consortium of seven banks to fund the planned acquisition of Kettler Automation GmbH.
The Kettler Automation acquisition, expected to close in the first quarter of fiscal year 2024, would add approximately 600 employees and $195 million in annualized revenues.
Management indicated that the acquisition is anticipated to be accretive to adjusted earnings per share within eighteen months of closing.
Integration costs related to the Kettler transaction are estimated at $28 million and will be recognized over the two fiscal years following the acquisition close.
Verdantix's board of directors approved an increase in the share repurchase authorization to $200 million, effective January 2024.
The increased repurchase authorization reflects the board's confidence in the company's strong free cash flow generation capability.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 totaled $241 million, compared to $198 million in fiscal year 2022, an increase of 21.7%.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 14.8% in fiscal year 2023, up from 13.1% in the prior year.
Verdantix maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout the fiscal year.
Moody's affirmed the company's Baa1 rating with a stable outlook in June 2023, citing consistent cash flow generation and prudent financial management.
The company's total debt-to-EBITDA ratio stood at 0.9 times as of year-end, well within its stated target of remaining below 2.5 times.
CFO Randall Esteban stated that the company's debt level as of December 31, 2023, reflects the term loan drawn to support the Kettler acquisition announcement.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for revenues in the range of $2.30 billion to $2.40 billion.
Adjusted operating income guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $340 million to $360 million, implying continued margin expansion.
The company expects capital expenditures to increase to approximately $175 million in fiscal year 2024, driven by capacity expansion projects in its Asia-Pacific region.
Verdantix plans to hire an additional 450 employees globally during fiscal year 2024, with a particular focus on software engineering talent.
The FlexDrive conveyor module was awarded the prestigious Automation Industry Innovation Award at the 2023 Global Manufacturing Technology Forum held in Munich.
Verdantix's customer satisfaction index, measured through third-party surveys, reached an all-time high of 87.6 out of 100 in fiscal year 2023.
The company renewed long-term supply agreements with three of its top five customers, collectively representing approximately $480 million in contracted future revenues.
Management noted that pricing power remained favorable during fiscal year 2023, with average selling prices rising approximately 3.5% across the product portfolio.
The European segment's strong performance was driven in part by a multi-year contract signed with a major German automotive manufacturer in the second quarter.
Verdantix's intellectual property portfolio expanded to 412 active patents worldwide, following the grant of 38 new patents during fiscal year 2023.
The company invested $22 million in cybersecurity infrastructure upgrades during the year to protect its connected automation products and customer data.
Audit Committee Chair Priscilla Nakamura confirmed that the external audit conducted by Deloitte & Touche LLP resulted in an unqualified opinion on the company's financial statements.
The board of directors, led by non-executive Chairman Gerald Forrest, endorsed all proposed resolutions at the 2023 annual general meeting with strong shareholder support.
Verdantix expressed gratitude to its global workforce, customers, and shareholders for their continued trust and partnership as the company enters its next phase of growth.
The full financial statements, including consolidated balance sheets, income statements, and cash flow statements, are available in the appendix of this annual report.
Veldora Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap pharmaceutical and biotechnology company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
The company specializes in developing targeted oncology therapies and companion diagnostics for rare genetic cancers.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Marissa Thornfeld presented the annual results to shareholders at the company's general meeting held in March 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Okeke highlighted strong operational execution throughout fiscal year 2023 as a key theme of the annual report.
Veldora Biosciences reported total net revenues of $847 million for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year.
The company's flagship oncology product, VLD-303, accounted for $412 million of that revenue, cementing its position as the portfolio's primary growth driver.
As of December 31, 2023, Veldora Biosciences carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, reflecting a deliberate capital structure strategy pursued since 2021.
Gross profit for the full year reached $589 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 69.5%, an improvement of 210 basis points versus fiscal 2022.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $334 million, driven primarily by continued investment in research and development.
Research and development expenditures alone accounted for $198 million, or 23.4% of total revenues, consistent with the company's innovation-first philosophy.
Selling, general, and administrative costs were $136 million, a modest increase from $124 million in the prior fiscal year.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $255 million, compared to $201 million in fiscal 2022, reflecting the strong revenue growth and disciplined cost management.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $189 million, up from $147 million in the preceding year.
Diluted earnings per share were $3.42, compared to $2.68 in fiscal 2022, exceeding the consensus analyst estimate of $3.18.
The company generated $231 million of free cash flow during the year, a 27% improvement over the $182 million recorded in fiscal 2022.
Cash and cash equivalents stood at $503 million at year-end, providing ample liquidity to fund near-term strategic initiatives.
Dr. Thornfeld emphasized that the balance sheet strength positions Veldora to pursue both organic growth and selective bolt-on acquisitions.
During 2023, the company expanded its global commercial footprint by entering into distribution agreements covering twelve new international markets.
European revenues grew by 31% year-over-year, reaching $174 million, driven by regulatory approvals in Germany, France, and Italy.
Asia-Pacific revenues contributed $98 million, a 22% increase compared to fiscal 2022, supported by a new partnership with Kansai Pharma Holdings in Japan.
The company ended fiscal 2023 with a total headcount of 3,214 full-time employees, up from 2,891 at the close of fiscal 2022.
Veldora added 323 net new employees during the year, with the largest cohort joining the clinical development and regulatory affairs divisions.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, marking the first time Veldora has initiated a dividend program in its corporate history.
The inaugural dividend is expected to return approximately $39 million to shareholders on an annualized basis.
Capital expenditures during fiscal 2023 totaled $67 million, focused primarily on expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Durham, North Carolina facility.
The Durham facility expansion is projected to increase annual production capacity for VLD-303 by approximately 40% once fully operational in mid-2025.

Throughout fiscal year 2023, Veldora Biosciences maintained a substantial revolving credit facility of $350 million, which it actively drew upon to finance working capital requirements and strategic investments.
Inventory levels at December 31, 2023, were $112 million, up slightly from $104 million at the end of the prior year, reflecting planned safety stock buildup.
Accounts receivable totaled $143 million, representing approximately 62 days sales outstanding, within the company's targeted range of 55 to 70 days.
The company recorded a $14 million tax benefit related to the exercise of employee stock options and the vesting of restricted stock units during the year.
Effective tax rate for the full year was 22.1%, slightly below the prior year's rate of 23.6%, largely due to the discrete tax benefit and favorable jurisdictional mix.
Veldora's pipeline includes seven additional compounds in various stages of clinical development, three of which are in Phase 2 trials.
In December 2023, the company submitted a New Drug Application to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for VLD-417, a next-generation therapy targeting HER3-positive breast cancer.
The FDA granted Priority Review designation to VLD-417, with a PDUFA action date set for October 2024, which management considers a pivotal near-term catalyst.
Phase 3 data for VLD-417 demonstrated a median progression-free survival of 11.3 months compared to 6.8 months for the standard of care, representing a statistically significant improvement.
Chief Medical Officer Dr. Helena Vasquez stated that the VLD-417 trial results represent the most compelling efficacy data the company has generated to date.
Veldora also entered into a co-development agreement with Lumora Therapeutics in November 2023 to jointly advance VLD-522, a CAR-T platform candidate.
Under the terms of that agreement, Veldora received a $45 million upfront payment and is eligible for up to $380 million in milestone payments.
Lumora Therapeutics will lead manufacturing and clinical operations for VLD-522, while Veldora retains commercialization rights in North America.
The company's market capitalization reached approximately $6.1 billion as of December 31, 2023, based on a closing share price of $110.42.
Total shares outstanding were approximately 55.2 million at year-end, net of treasury shares repurchased under the company's ongoing buyback program.
During fiscal 2023, Veldora repurchased 1.4 million shares for a total consideration of $148 million under its Board-authorized share repurchase program.
The remaining authorization under the repurchase program stood at $102 million as of December 31, 2023.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management provided revenue guidance of $960 million to $990 million, representing projected growth of approximately 14% to 17%.
Earnings per share guidance for fiscal 2024 was set in the range of $3.90 to $4.10 on a diluted basis, implying continued margin expansion.
Mr. Okeke noted that operating margin improvement in 2024 will be supported by operating leverage as VLD-303 revenues continue to scale without proportionate cost increases.
The company anticipates research and development spending to increase modestly in absolute terms during fiscal 2024, to approximately $215 million to $225 million.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses are expected to remain broadly stable as a percentage of revenue at approximately 15% to 16% for fiscal 2024.
Veldora's audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP has been re-engaged as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2024.
The company disclosed no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting as of December 31, 2023, in accordance with Sarbanes-Oxley Section 404 requirements.
Veldora Biosciences was recognized by Forbes as one of America's Most Innovative Pharmaceutical Companies for the second consecutive year.

Dr. Thornfeld concluded her remarks by expressing confidence in the company's long-term trajectory, citing the pipeline depth, commercial execution, and financial resilience as competitive differentiators.
The board and management team remain committed to creating sustainable shareholder value through disciplined capital allocation, scientific excellence, and global commercial expansion.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, operates within the medical devices and diagnostic equipment sector.
Veltara Diagnostics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a policy of conservative capital management.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Osei expressed confidence in the company's trajectory during the earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase compared to the prior year.
The company attributed much of its revenue growth to strong demand for its flagship Lumivance imaging platform.
Gross profit for the year came in at $512.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 60.5%.
Operating expenses totaled $298.4 million, including research and development outlays of $104.7 million.
Chief Financial Officer Thomas Braeburn noted that disciplined cost management was central to maintaining margin stability.
Net income for fiscal year 2023 was $158.9 million, compared to $131.2 million in the prior year.
Earnings per diluted share rose to $3.42, up from $2.83 in fiscal year 2022.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, payable beginning in March 2024.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $224.7 million, an increase of $47.1 million from the prior year-end balance.
Veltara Diagnostics repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares during the fiscal year under its existing buyback authorization.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $63.8 million, primarily directed toward manufacturing capacity expansion in its Durham facility.
The company employed 4,310 full-time employees globally as of December 31, 2023.
International revenue accounted for 38% of total revenues, with particularly strong performance in the European and Asia-Pacific markets.
The Lumivance Pro, a next-generation imaging system, received FDA 510(k) clearance in August 2023.
Sales of the Lumivance Pro contributed $92.4 million in revenue during the partial year following its clearance.
The company's domestic market share in portable diagnostic imaging reached 22.3%, according to third-party industry analyses.
Dr. Osei emphasized the importance of continued investment in artificial intelligence-enabled diagnostic tools as a long-term growth driver.
Veltara's supply chain operations were restructured in 2023 to reduce component lead times and improve inventory turnover.
In October 2023, Veltara Diagnostics completed a $350 million syndicated term loan agreement to fund strategic acquisition activity.
Inventory levels at year-end were $87.2 million, down from $99.6 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The reduction in inventory was largely driven by improved demand forecasting and lean manufacturing initiatives.
Accounts receivable totaled $143.5 million, representing approximately 62 days of sales outstanding.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.8, indicating a strong short-term liquidity position.
