Veltara entered into a strategic partnership with NovaBridge Health Systems to co-develop AI-assisted pathology tools.
Under the terms of the agreement, Veltara will contribute up to $28 million in development funding over a three-year period.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Nambiar outlined plans to expand the company's direct sales force by approximately 200 representatives in 2024.
The company's deferred revenue balance grew to $34.6 million, reflecting increased multi-year service contract bookings.
Veltara Diagnostics maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout the fiscal year.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, modestly below the prior year rate of 22.1%.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $930 million to $960 million.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $247.6 million, representing an EBITDA margin of approximately 29.2%.
The company recognized $14.2 million in restructuring charges related to the consolidation of its European distribution network.
Thomas Braeburn indicated that the restructuring is expected to yield annual cost savings of approximately $19 million beginning in 2025.
Veltara's pension and post-retirement benefit obligations totaled $41.3 million, unchanged from the prior year.
The company did not record any goodwill impairment charges during fiscal year 2023.
Veltara Diagnostics received the MedTech Innovation Award at the 2023 HealthTech Summit held in Chicago.
Research and development headcount increased by 87 employees during the year, reflecting continued investment in product innovation.
The company launched two additional diagnostic software modules under the Veltara Analytics Suite brand in Q3 2023.
Combined first-year revenues from the new software modules totaled $18.7 million, exceeding initial projections by approximately 15%.
Management believes the analytics software segment will become a meaningful revenue contributor within the next three fiscal years.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a corporate priority, with Veltara reducing its carbon intensity by 8.3% year-over-year.
The company published its third annual ESG report in June 2023, detailing progress against sustainability targets.
Veltara's board of directors consists of eleven members, five of whom are independent directors with financial expertise.
The audit committee, chaired by independent director Susan Calloway, conducted a comprehensive review of internal controls during 2023.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the review period.
The company's largest customer accounted for approximately 9.4% of total revenues, limiting concentration risk.
Veltara Diagnostics has patent protection on 63 active innovations, with an average remaining life of 8.7 years.
The backlog of confirmed orders as of December 31, 2023 stood at $312.4 million, up 18.6% from the prior year.
Dr. Osei concluded the earnings call by reaffirming management's commitment to delivering sustainable long-term shareholder value.
The company plans to host an investor day in April 2024 to provide a more detailed strategic outlook.
Forward-looking statements contained in this report are subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially.
Veltara Diagnostics will file its complete Form 10-K with the Securities and Exchange Commission on or before March 15, 2024.

Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap aerospace components manufacturer headquartered in Tucson, Arizona, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, founded in 1998 by engineer-turned-entrepreneur Marcus Delfield, has grown to become a specialized supplier of precision guidance assemblies and thermal shielding systems.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Yurewich opened her letter to shareholders by acknowledging the transformative year the company had navigated amid shifting defense procurement cycles.
Veltrano Dynamics reported total consolidated revenue of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4 percent increase over the prior year's $753.6 million.
The growth was driven primarily by expanded contracts with three major defense prime contractors, which collectively accounted for 61 percent of total revenues.
Chief Financial Officer Leonard Osagie noted that gross profit margins improved to 34.7 percent, up from 31.2 percent in fiscal 2022, reflecting favorable product mix and operational efficiencies.
Operating income for the year reached $142.6 million, compared to $108.9 million in the previous fiscal year, a year-over-year increase of approximately 30.9 percent.
Research and development expenditures totaled $63.4 million, representing approximately 7.5 percent of total revenues, consistent with the company's long-standing innovation investment philosophy.

Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $88.1 million, slightly elevated compared to $79.6 million in 2022 due to expanded business development activities and executive compensation adjustments.
Net income attributable to common shareholders for fiscal year 2023 came in at $97.2 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.84.
This compares favorably to net income of $74.5 million and diluted EPS of $2.96 recorded in fiscal 2022, reflecting the company's ongoing profitability momentum.
Veltrano Dynamics ended the fiscal year with $214.8 million in cash and cash equivalents, bolstered by strong operating cash flows throughout the year.
Operating cash flow for fiscal 2023 totaled $168.3 million, driven by disciplined working capital management and accelerated collections from government-affiliated customers.
Capital expenditures during the year amounted to $44.7 million, primarily allocated to upgrading precision machining equipment at the company's Tucson and Albuquerque facilities.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was therefore approximately $123.6 million for the full fiscal year.
The company's backlog of signed contracts and orders stood at $2.1 billion as of December 31, 2023, providing significant near-term revenue visibility.
This backlog figure represents an 18 percent increase over the $1.78 billion reported at the end of fiscal 2022 and reflects robust demand across all core product lines.
Veltrano Dynamics currently employs approximately 4,200 full-time equivalent staff globally, with the majority of its workforce concentrated in domestic manufacturing operations.
During fiscal 2023, the company added a net 340 employees, primarily in its engineering, quality assurance, and supply chain management functions.
Vice President of Operations Claire Benthom reported that manufacturing throughput increased by 16 percent year-over-year, enabled by lean production initiatives implemented in early 2023.
The Tucson headquarters facility underwent a $19 million expansion that added approximately 85,000 square feet of production and laboratory space, completed in the third quarter of 2023.
Veltrano's thermal shielding segment recorded segment revenues of $312.4 million, up 9.1 percent from $286.4 million in fiscal 2022.
The guidance assemblies segment remained the company's largest business unit, generating $421.7 million in revenues and contributing $74.3 million in segment operating income.
The space systems segment, the company's newest division established in 2021, contributed $113.2 million in revenues, nearly doubling its $58.9 million contribution from fiscal 2022.
Sandra Yurewich highlighted the space systems segment as the cornerstone of Veltrano's long-term growth strategy, citing increasing commercial and governmental demand for orbital infrastructure.
The company completed two strategic acquisitions during fiscal 2023: Norwell Thermal Sciences for $87.5 million in March and Adaptive Optics Partners for $52.0 million in September.
Both acquisitions were funded using a combination of existing cash reserves and a revolving credit facility that the company had maintained since 2019.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltrano Dynamics carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its prior term loan obligations in the second quarter of 2023.
The company's total assets at year-end were $1.34 billion, with shareholders' equity of $689.4 million, reflecting retained earnings accumulation over several consecutive profitable years.
Veltrano declared and paid quarterly dividends totaling $0.72 per share during fiscal 2023, maintaining its consistent dividend policy established since 2016.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $75 million in August 2023, of which $31.4 million had been executed by year-end.
Inventory levels at December 31, 2023 were $178.6 million, reflecting strategic raw material stockpiling in anticipation of increased production volumes in fiscal 2024.
Accounts receivable stood at $143.2 million at year-end, with days sales outstanding of approximately 62 days, within the company's target range of 55 to 70 days.
Veltrano's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3 percent, modestly above the statutory federal corporate rate, reflecting state tax obligations across multiple jurisdictions.
The company recognized $14.2 million in R&D tax credits during the year, partially offsetting the overall tax burden from its substantial investment in new product development.
Leonard Osagie indicated that the company's pension plan was 94 percent funded as of year-end, a material improvement from the 88 percent funded status at the close of fiscal 2022.
Veltrano Dynamics maintained compliance with all debt covenants throughout fiscal 2023 and reported no material weaknesses in its internal controls over financial reporting.
The company's audit committee, chaired by independent director Phyllis Kourounis, oversaw a smooth transition to a new external auditor, Grant Whitmore LLP, effective for the fiscal 2023 audit.
Grant Whitmore LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements, affirming that they present fairly, in all material respects, the company's financial position.
Looking ahead, Veltrano Dynamics issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in a range of $940 million to $975 million, implying approximately 11 to 15 percent top-line growth.
Management also guided for fiscal 2024 diluted EPS in the range of $4.20 to $4.45, representing meaningful earnings expansion relative to the $3.84 achieved in fiscal 2023.
The guidance reflects anticipated contributions from the Norwell Thermal Sciences and Adaptive Optics Partners acquisitions for their first full fiscal years within the consolidated group.
Veltrano anticipates that gross margins will remain broadly stable in fiscal 2024, with any pricing tailwinds expected to be offset by higher labor costs associated with recent workforce expansion.
The company plans to increase its R&D budget to approximately $72 million in fiscal 2024, targeting advanced materials research and next-generation propulsion thermal management systems.
Sandra Yurewich stated in the shareholder letter that disciplined capital allocation would remain a strategic priority, balancing organic investment, shareholder returns, and selective acquisitions.
The board also approved a 6.9 percent increase in the quarterly dividend for fiscal 2024, raising the per-share dividend from $0.18 per quarter to $0.1925 per quarter.
Veltrano Dynamics continues to monitor geopolitical developments and supply chain vulnerabilities, particularly relating to rare earth materials used in certain guidance assembly components.
In November 2023, Veltrano successfully closed a $300 million senior unsecured term loan, the proceeds of which are earmarked for facility expansion and future acquisition financing.
The company's investor relations team hosted its annual investor day in October 2023 in New York City, attracting participation from over 180 institutional and retail investment representatives.
Management presented a five-year strategic roadmap at the investor day, targeting cumulative revenue of $6 billion by fiscal 2028 through organic growth and continued inorganic expansion.
The roadmap also emphasized an aspiration to achieve EBITDA margins of 22 to 24 percent by fiscal 2027, compared to the current adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 19.8 percent.

Veltrano Dynamics expressed confidence that its differentiated technology portfolio, experienced leadership team, and robust contract backlog position it well to deliver sustained shareholder value over the long term.
Veldoran Industries, Inc. is a mid-cap specialty chemicals and advanced materials company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio.
The company was founded in 1987 and has grown to serve industrial clients across North America, Europe, and select Asia-Pacific markets.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Delaine presented the annual results to shareholders at the company's headquarters on February 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Nanthakumar outlined the key financial highlights during the earnings call that followed.
Veldoran Industries reported total net revenue of $1.84 billion for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
This represented a 7.3 percent increase compared to the prior fiscal year's net revenue of $1.71 billion.
The company's Advanced Polymers segment contributed $720 million to total revenue, making it the largest single business unit.
The Industrial Coatings division generated $540 million in revenue, reflecting strong demand from the automotive sector.
The Specialty Adhesives segment recorded $380 million in revenue, driven by new contracts in aerospace applications.
The remaining $200 million in revenue came from the company's Environmental Solutions services division.
Gross profit for fiscal 2023 reached $612 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 33.3 percent.
Operating income came in at $229 million, representing an operating margin of 12.4 percent.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $158 million, compared to $134 million in fiscal 2022.
Diluted earnings per share for the year were $3.47, up from $2.94 per diluted share in the prior year.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share, consistent with the prior four quarters.
Capital expenditures totaled $97 million during fiscal 2023, primarily directed toward capacity expansion at the Toledo manufacturing complex.
Depreciation and amortization expense was $64 million for the year, in line with management's guidance range.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $312 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 17.0 percent.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $215 million, reflecting strong working capital management throughout the year.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $41.60 per share.
As of December 31, 2023, Veldoran Industries carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its outstanding obligations earlier in the year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $388 million, providing the company with significant financial flexibility.
Inventory levels declined by 9 percent year-over-year, reflecting improved demand forecasting and supply chain optimization efforts.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved from 48 days to 43 days, indicating stronger collections performance.
The company's current ratio at December 31, 2023 was 2.6, reflecting a healthy short-term liquidity position.
Marcus Delaine noted that fiscal 2023 marked the third consecutive year of double-digit net income growth for Veldoran.
Priya Nanthakumar highlighted that return on invested capital reached 14.2 percent, exceeding the company's internal benchmark of 12 percent.
The Advanced Polymers segment expanded its customer base by 18 new accounts during the year, primarily in the electric vehicle supply chain.
Research and development expenditures totaled $58 million in fiscal 2023, representing approximately 3.2 percent of net revenue.
Veldoran launched four new product lines in the fourth quarter of 2023, including two bio-based polymer grades targeting sustainable packaging markets.
The Industrial Coatings division secured a five-year supply agreement with a major North American automaker valued at an estimated $400 million over the contract term.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $174 million, or 9.5 percent of revenue, slightly below the prior year ratio of 9.8 percent.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4 percent, slightly lower than the prior year rate of 23.1 percent.
Veldoran's total headcount at the end of fiscal 2023 was 5,840 full-time equivalent employees worldwide.
The company added 320 net new employees during 2023, predominantly in manufacturing and technical support roles.
Employee benefits and compensation expense increased by $22 million year-over-year, reflecting both headcount growth and annual merit increases.
The Environmental Solutions division received two new operating permits in Q3 2023, enabling expansion into waste stream processing in Michigan.
Capital allocation priorities for fiscal 2024 include organic growth investments, bolt-on acquisitions, and continued shareholder returns.
Management issued fiscal 2024 guidance for net revenue in the range of $1.92 billion to $1.98 billion, implying approximately 4 to 8 percent growth.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $325 million to $345 million, reflecting continued margin discipline.

In January 2024, Veldoran Industries closed on a $250 million syndicated term loan facility arranged by a consortium of five regional banks, which management intends to deploy toward a strategic acquisition pipeline.
The Specialty Adhesives segment is expected to benefit from growing demand in next-generation aircraft interior assemblies throughout 2024.
Veldoran's global manufacturing footprint spans twelve facilities, including three in Europe and two in Southeast Asia.
The company completed a planned maintenance turnaround at its Cincinnati facility in Q2 2023, temporarily reducing output for approximately six weeks.
Despite the turnaround, the Cincinnati facility returned to full capacity by late July and contributed positively to second-half results.
Net working capital at year-end was $421 million, reflecting the strong cash generation profile of the core business segments.
Veldoran has maintained investment-grade credit ratings from two major agencies throughout its operating history.
The company's pension obligations were $113 million at year-end, with plan assets covering approximately 94 percent of the projected benefit obligation.
Foreign currency translation effects reduced reported revenue by an estimated $18 million during fiscal 2023.
Segment operating margins varied, with Advanced Polymers at 16.1 percent, Industrial Coatings at 11.4 percent, and Specialty Adhesives at 13.7 percent.
The board authorized a new share repurchase program of up to $150 million over the next 24 months beginning January 1, 2024.
Priya Nanthakumar confirmed that the company's revolving credit facility remains undrawn at year-end, providing an additional $200 million of available liquidity.
Veldoran plans to hold an Investor Day on April 22, 2024, at which management will present a three-year strategic roadmap.
The strategic roadmap is expected to address organic growth targets, portfolio optimization, and sustainability commitments through 2026.
Management expressed confidence that Veldoran's diversified business model and strong balance sheet position the company well for continued value creation.
The foregoing discussion contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Vortex Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The report was presented by Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway and Chief Financial Officer Denis Parrish at the company's investor day held on February 14, 2024.
Vortex Dynamics specializes in the design and manufacture of precision robotics systems and automated assembly platforms for the automotive and aerospace sectors.
The company was founded in 1997 and has grown to serve clients across 28 countries on five continents.
Fiscal year 2023 marked the company's twenty-sixth year of continuous operation and its fourteenth consecutive year of positive operating income.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 9.3% increase over the $1.68 billion reported in fiscal year 2022.
CEO Margaret Holloway attributed the revenue growth primarily to strong demand in the North American automotive segment and expanding penetration in Southeast Asian markets.
The company's gross profit for the year was $612 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 33.3%, compared to 31.8% in the prior fiscal year.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $389 million, which included $142 million in research and development investment.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $223 million, up from $187 million in fiscal year 2022, reflecting a 19.3% year-over-year improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $161 million, or $3.84 per diluted share, compared to $134 million, or $3.19 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 24.7%, slightly above the prior year's rate of 23.9%, due to changes in the geographic mix of earnings.
Vortex Dynamics generated $198 million in free cash flow during the fiscal year, a significant increase from the $154 million generated in fiscal year 2022.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, representing a 12% increase over the previous quarterly dividend of $0.25 per share.
As of December 31, 2023, Vortex Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its previously outstanding senior notes earlier in the fiscal year.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $87 million, with the majority directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the Columbus and Monterrey facilities.
The company's order backlog at year-end stood at $2.1 billion, providing approximately 13.6 months of forward revenue coverage based on the trailing twelve-month run rate.
Chief Financial Officer Denis Parrish noted that working capital management improved meaningfully during the year, with days sales outstanding declining from 54 to 47 days.
Inventory levels were actively managed throughout the year, with finished goods inventory declining by $23 million relative to the prior year-end balance.
The company repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $62.40 per share, totaling $74.9 million.
At year-end, approximately $125 million remained available under the existing share repurchase authorization approved by the board in March 2022.
Vortex Dynamics employed 8,740 full-time equivalent workers globally as of December 31, 2023, an increase of 310 employees compared to the prior year-end.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in engineering and software development roles, reflecting the company's strategic pivot toward more software-intensive automation solutions.
The North America segment generated revenue of $924 million, representing 50.2% of total company revenue, with segment operating income of $118 million.
The Europe segment contributed revenue of $487 million, or 26.5% of total revenue, and segment operating income of $64 million, reflecting margin pressure from elevated energy costs.
The Asia-Pacific segment delivered revenue of $429 million, constituting 23.3% of total revenue, with segment operating income of $51 million.
Asia-Pacific segment revenue grew 18.7% year-over-year, the fastest growth rate among all geographic segments, driven by new customer wins in South Korea and Indonesia.
Research and development spending of $142 million represented 7.7% of total revenue, consistent with the company's long-term target of investing between 7% and 9% of revenue in innovation.
During fiscal year 2023, the company filed 34 new patents related to machine vision, collaborative robotics, and edge-computing-enabled process control systems.
The company launched its next-generation VX-900 platform in the third quarter, which integrates artificial intelligence-based quality inspection with real-time process adjustment capabilities.
Early customer adoption of the VX-900 platform exceeded internal projections, with 47 units deployed by year-end against a target of 35 units.
CEO Holloway expressed confidence that the VX-900 product line would become a meaningful revenue contributor beginning in the second half of fiscal year 2024.
The company's net promoter score among enterprise customers reached 68 in fiscal year 2023, up from 61 in fiscal year 2022, indicating improving customer satisfaction.
Vortex Dynamics maintained an investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's and Baa1 from Moody's throughout the fiscal year.
To fund an accelerated expansion into the European precision manufacturing market, Vortex Dynamics secured a €250 million syndicated revolving credit facility in October 2023.
The company's total assets at year-end were $2.37 billion, compared to $2.19 billion at the end of fiscal year 2022, reflecting both earnings retention and capital investment.
Total shareholders' equity grew to $1.42 billion at December 31, 2023, from $1.28 billion at December 31, 2022, an increase of approximately 10.9%.
The return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was 12.1%, compared to 11.2% in fiscal year 2022, driven by higher net income and disciplined capital allocation.
Return on invested capital improved to 14.3% in fiscal year 2023, surpassing the company's weighted average cost of capital of 8.6% by a meaningful margin.
Vortex Dynamics made one strategic acquisition during fiscal year 2023, completing the purchase of Stuttgart-based Praxis Mechatronik GmbH for €68 million in cash in April.
The Praxis Mechatronik acquisition added approximately $31 million of annualized revenue and brought a specialized team of 142 engineers focused on high-precision linear motion systems.
Integration of Praxis Mechatronik progressed ahead of schedule, with the combined entity expected to achieve annualized cost synergies of approximately $9 million by the end of fiscal year 2024.
The company recognized $4.3 million in acquisition-related transaction costs in fiscal year 2023, which were recorded as a component of general and administrative expenses.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a priority for the company, with Vortex Dynamics reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 11% year-over-year.
The company committed to achieving net-zero Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 2040, supported by a $45 million investment plan in renewable energy and energy efficiency projects.
Vortex Dynamics' supply chain resilience program, launched in late fiscal year 2022, delivered measurable results in fiscal year 2023, reducing single-source supplier dependencies by 18%.
The company established two additional regional distribution centers during the year, one in Guadalajara, Mexico, and one in Penang, Malaysia, to improve delivery lead times in key markets.
Selling and marketing expenses for fiscal year 2023 were $89 million, or 4.8% of revenue, reflecting increased investment in direct sales capacity and digital marketing channels.
General and administrative expenses totaled $158 million, which included $17 million in restructuring charges associated with streamlining the legacy European organizational structure.
The restructuring program is expected to generate approximately $22 million in annualized cost savings beginning in fiscal year 2025, improving the European segment's margin profile.
Vortex Dynamics provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $1.98 billion to $2.04 billion, implying growth of approximately 7.6% to 10.9% over fiscal year 2023.
The company guided for fiscal year 2024 adjusted earnings per diluted share in the range of $4.15 to $4.35, representing growth of 8.1% to 13.3% over the fiscal year 2023 adjusted figure.
CFO Denis Parrish noted that the guidance range reflects ongoing uncertainty in global macroeconomic conditions, including potential softness in European end markets.
The midpoint of the fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance implies approximately $350 million in incremental revenue opportunities relative to fiscal year 2023 reported results.
Management indicated that operating margin expansion of 50 to 80 basis points is expected in fiscal year 2024, driven by operational leverage and continued productivity improvements.
The board of directors has authorized management to explore potential strategic partnerships or joint ventures in the rapidly growing Indian industrial automation market.
Vortex Dynamics has no current plans to pursue large-scale acquisitions in fiscal year 2024, preferring instead to focus on organic growth and integration of recently acquired businesses.
The company's cash and cash equivalents position at December 31, 2023, was $312 million, providing ample liquidity to fund operations, dividends, and opportunistic share repurchases.
Vortex Dynamics' pension obligations were fully funded at year-end, with plan assets of $184 million exceeding projected benefit obligations of $179 million.
The company maintained compliance with all financial covenants under its existing credit arrangements throughout fiscal year 2023 without any waivers or amendments.
Audit Committee Chair Sandra Kowalczyk noted that the external auditor, Reinholt & Chambers LLP, issued an unqualified opinion on the company's fiscal year 2023 consolidated financial statements.
The board expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction and its ability to deliver sustainable long-term value to shareholders through disciplined execution and innovation.
Vortex Dynamics remains committed to transparent financial reporting, responsible capital allocation, and maintaining the trust of its shareholders, employees, customers, and communities.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ materially from those expressed or implied by such statements.
Veltrano Industries, Inc. is a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Rotterdam, Netherlands, with operations spanning across fourteen countries.
The company was founded in 1987 by Dr. Pieter Veltrano and has since grown into a leading supplier of industrial adhesives and surface-treatment compounds.
Chief Executive Officer Margit Hollenbeck presented the annual results to shareholders at the company's general meeting held in March 2024.
Veltrano Industries reported total consolidated revenues of €1.84 billion for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, representing a 9.3% increase over the prior year.
The adhesives segment remained the largest contributor, generating €920 million in revenue, which accounted for approximately 50% of total group sales.
The surface-treatment division delivered revenues of €614 million, reflecting continued demand from the automotive and aerospace sectors.
The specialty coatings unit, the company's youngest business line, contributed €306 million to total revenues, exceeding its internal target by 12%.
Gross profit for the fiscal year reached €552 million, yielding a gross margin of 30%, consistent with the company's medium-term margin guidance.
Operating expenses increased by €34 million year-over-year, primarily driven by higher logistics costs and expanded research and development investments.
EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was reported at €312 million, representing an EBITDA margin of approximately 17%, up from 15.8% in fiscal 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Reinout Schaefers attributed margin improvement to operational efficiencies realized through the company's Leaner Veltrano initiative launched in 2022.
Net income attributable to ordinary shareholders was €178 million, compared with €149 million in the prior year, marking a 19.5% increase.
Earnings per share on a diluted basis came in at €2.31, ahead of the consensus analyst estimate of €2.18.
The board of directors approved a final dividend of €0.72 per share, bringing the full-year dividend to €1.04 per share, a 15.6% increase from 2022.
Capital expenditure for the year amounted to €145 million, with the majority allocated to capacity expansion at the Antwerp and Gdańsk manufacturing facilities.
Free cash flow generation was robust at €167 million, supporting both the dividend increase and continued debt reduction.
The company's balance sheet showed total assets of €2.73 billion and total equity of €1.15 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Net debt stood at €410 million at year-end, reflecting a net debt-to-EBITDA ratio of 1.3 times, well within the company's stated ceiling of 2.5 times.
Veltrano Industries successfully refinanced its revolving credit facility in April 2023, extending the maturity to 2028 and improving the borrowing margin by 35 basis points.
The Antwerp facility expansion was completed on schedule in October 2023, adding 18,000 metric tons of annual adhesives production capacity.
Global headcount increased to 9,840 full-time equivalent employees at year-end, up from 9,210 at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company invested €47 million in employee training and development programs during the year, reflecting its commitment to talent retention.
Veltrano's research and development expenditure reached €88 million, representing 4.8% of total revenues and a record level for the group.
During 2023, the company filed 34 new patent applications globally, bringing its active patent portfolio to 312 registered patents.
The Asia-Pacific region delivered particularly strong growth, with revenues rising 21% year-over-year to €368 million.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltrano Industries carried no long-term financial debt on its consolidated balance sheet, having repaid the final tranche of its senior notes in November 2023.
The North American business generated revenues of €297 million, growing 7% in local currency terms despite some softness in the construction end-market.
Ms. Hollenbeck noted that the company's strategic pivot toward bio-based adhesive formulations had begun yielding commercial results ahead of schedule.
The bio-based product line generated €62 million in revenues in its first full year of commercial operation, representing 3.4% of total group revenues.
Veltrano's market share in the European industrial adhesives segment reached 14.2%, up from 13.5% in 2022, according to the company's internal estimates.
The company signed three significant long-term supply agreements with automotive original equipment manufacturers during the year, with a combined committed value of approximately €240 million.
Environmental, social, and governance performance continued to improve, with greenhouse gas emissions intensity falling 8% relative to the 2022 baseline.
The company achieved a lost-time injury frequency rate of 0.84 per million hours worked, an improvement from 1.12 in the prior year.
Veltrano's Scope 1 and Scope 2 combined absolute greenhouse gas emissions totaled 187,000 metric tons of CO2 equivalent for the full year.
The proportion of renewable electricity consumed across Veltrano's manufacturing network reached 43%, up from 31% in fiscal 2022.
Looking ahead, management provided initial guidance for fiscal 2024, targeting revenue growth in the range of 6% to 9% at constant currency.
The EBITDA margin guidance for 2024 was set at 17.5% to 18.5%, reflecting expected operating leverage and further procurement savings.
The specialty coatings pipeline includes four new product platforms expected to reach commercial scale between 2024 and 2026.
Chief Operating Officer Daria Klestova outlined a restructuring of the supply chain network aimed at reducing raw material procurement costs by €22 million annually by 2025.
Inventory levels at year-end were €284 million, representing approximately 56 days of sales, which management described as lean given current demand conditions.
Trade receivables stood at €318 million, and the company reported a bad debt expense of only €2.1 million for the full year.
Veltrano's pension obligations are fully funded, with a surplus of €18 million recognized on the balance sheet as of December 31, 2023.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 23.4%, in line with management guidance and reflecting the benefit of R&D tax credits in Belgium and the Netherlands.
Acquisition activity in 2023 was limited to a bolt-on purchase of Haffner Coating GmbH for a total consideration of €31 million, funded entirely from operating cash flows.
The integration of Haffner Coating GmbH is expected to be completed by the second quarter of 2024 and is anticipated to be accretive to earnings per share from year one.
In January 2024, Veltrano Industries entered into a €350 million syndicated term loan agreement with a consortium of seven European banks to fund its strategic growth investments.
Segment reporting reflects three operating divisions: Adhesives, Surface Treatment, and Specialty Coatings, consistent with how the chief operating decision-maker reviews performance.
The external auditor, Hendricks & Van Maarsen LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2023.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year, as confirmed by both management and the external auditors.
The supervisory board's audit committee met seven times during 2023 and oversaw the transition to the new enterprise resource planning system implemented in the second half of the year.
Veltrano Industries repurchased 1.2 million of its own shares during fiscal 2023 at an average price of €31.40 per share, for a total outlay of approximately €37.7 million.
The share repurchase program, announced in February 2023 with a total authorized amount of €60 million, remains ongoing into the first quarter of 2024.
Total shareholder return for fiscal 2023 was 24%, outperforming the MSCI Europe Chemicals Index, which returned 11% over the same period.

Management expressed confidence that Veltrano Industries is well positioned to deliver sustainable value creation for shareholders through disciplined capital allocation, innovation leadership, and continued geographic diversification.
Meridian Biopharma Solutions, Inc. hereby presents its consolidated financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, operates primarily within the specialty pharmaceuticals and contract drug manufacturing sector.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Vanessa Hartwell opened the annual letter to shareholders with cautious optimism regarding the company's trajectory.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delgado outlined several key financial milestones achieved during the fiscal year under review.
Total consolidated revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year's $754.1 million.
The company's contract manufacturing division contributed $412.6 million to total revenue, making it the single largest revenue-generating segment.
Specialty drug formulation revenues rose to $284.9 million, supported by strong demand from hospital networks across the southeastern United States.
Meridian Biopharma Solutions carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting years of disciplined capital management.
Research and development expenditures totaled $93.4 million, representing approximately 11.0% of total revenues for the year.
The company successfully advanced three pipeline candidates into Phase II clinical trials during the second half of 2023.
Operating income for the full year reached $154.7 million, compared to $128.3 million in fiscal year 2022.
EBITDA expanded to $201.2 million, yielding an EBITDA margin of approximately 23.8% on a consolidated basis.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $112.9 million, or $3.47 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $94.6 million, or $2.91 per diluted share, reported in fiscal year 2022.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 was $318.5 million, translating to a gross margin of 37.6%.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses increased modestly to $70.4 million from $64.8 million the prior year.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $223.7 million, up from $189.4 million at year-end 2022.
Accounts receivable stood at $134.6 million, reflecting a days sales outstanding metric of approximately 58 days.
Inventory levels were managed tightly, with finished goods inventory declining 8.3% year-over-year to $47.2 million.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $58.9 million, largely directed toward expanding the Durham manufacturing facility.
The Durham facility expansion is expected to add approximately 120,000 square feet of compliant manufacturing space by mid-2025.
Headcount across all global operations stood at 3,214 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023.
This represents a net addition of 287 employees compared to the 2,927 full-time employees reported at year-end 2022.
Dr. Hartwell highlighted the company's strategic partnership with Solvay Health Networks as a key driver of revenue growth in the hospital segment.
The Solvay Health Networks contract, renewed and expanded in April 2023, now covers formulation and fulfillment services across 47 hospital systems.
Meridian also completed the acquisition of Pinebrook Analytical Labs in August 2023 for a total consideration of $38.5 million in cash.
In September 2023, the company entered into a $300 million syndicated revolving credit facility with a consortium of lenders led by First Meridian Capital Bank.
The new credit facility carries an interest rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points and matures in September 2028.
Management stated the credit facility was established to provide financial flexibility for potential bolt-on acquisitions and working capital needs.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, compared to 22.7% in the prior fiscal year.
The reduction in effective tax rate was primarily attributable to the utilization of research and development tax credits under IRC Section 41.
Total assets on the consolidated balance sheet reached $1.24 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Total liabilities stood at $387.6 million, while total stockholders' equity was recorded at $852.4 million.
The company repurchased 1.1 million shares of its common stock during fiscal year 2023 under its authorized buyback program.
Share repurchases totaled $72.3 million in aggregate consideration, executed at an average price of $65.73 per share.
The remaining authorization under the repurchase program stands at approximately $127.7 million as of the end of the fiscal year.
Dr. Hartwell noted that the launch of MBS-447, a novel extended-release pain management formulation, exceeded internal projections in its first full commercial quarter.
MBS-447 generated $28.4 million in net product revenue during the fourth quarter of 2023 alone.
The product's commercial uptake has been supported by a dedicated 180-person specialty sales force deployed in August 2023.
CFO Delgado indicated that MBS-447 is expected to reach annualized revenues of approximately $95 million by the end of fiscal year 2024.
The company's contract manufacturing backlog grew to $534.2 million at year-end, representing approximately 15 months of forward revenue visibility.
Meridian Biopharma Solutions maintained compliance with all applicable current Good Manufacturing Practice regulations across its three U.S. manufacturing sites.
An FDA Pre-Approval Inspection conducted in October 2023 at the Durham facility yielded zero Form 483 observations.
The company's environmental, social, and governance program achieved an 18% reduction in Scope 1 carbon emissions during calendar year 2023.
Water usage intensity across manufacturing operations declined by 11.4% compared to the baseline year of 2021.
The company issued its third annual sustainability report in February 2024, detailing progress against 2030 decarbonization targets.
Segment reporting reflects two reportable segments: Contract Manufacturing Services and Proprietary Drug Products.
The Contract Manufacturing Services segment generated operating income of $89.3 million with a segment operating margin of 21.7%.
The Proprietary Drug Products segment generated operating income of $65.4 million, representing a segment operating margin of 23.0%.
Corporate-level unallocated expenses reduced consolidated operating income by approximately $0.0 million in fiscal year 2023, as intercompany adjustments netted to zero.
Foreign exchange impacts were minimal, with approximately 96% of revenues denominated in U.S. dollars.
The company's international revenues, primarily from Canada and the United Kingdom, totaled $33.9 million for the year.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet totaled $21.3 million, related primarily to upfront payments from contract manufacturing customers.
Goodwill and other intangible assets totaled $198.7 million following the Pinebrook Analytical Labs acquisition.
The company performed its annual impairment test in the fourth quarter and determined that no impairment charges were warranted.
Pension and post-retirement benefit obligations are not material, as Meridian Biopharma Solutions transitioned to a 401(k)-only benefit structure in 2017.
Accrued compensation and benefits at year-end totaled $44.8 million, reflecting year-end bonus accruals for approximately 1,900 eligible employees.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $945 million.
Diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $3.80 to $4.05, assuming a stable tax environment.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal 2024 was provided at $65 million to $75 million, reflecting continued investment in manufacturing capacity.
Dr. Hartwell closed the shareholder letter by expressing confidence in the company's ability to deliver sustainable, long-term value for all stakeholders.
The Board of Directors, Audit Committee, and external auditors Grant Whitmore & Associates LLP have reviewed and approved the contents of this report.
This document contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Veldora Precision Systems, Inc. reported its fiscal year 2023 annual results on March 14, 2024, to a broad audience of institutional and retail investors.
The company, headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, operates within the industrial automation and robotics sector.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Hollinsworth opened the earnings call by describing fiscal 2023 as a transformative year for the organization.
Veldora Precision Systems achieved total revenue of $1.47 billion for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
This represented a 12.4% increase compared to the $1.31 billion reported in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Okafor attributed the growth to strong demand across the company's core automation product lines.
The North American segment contributed $820 million in revenue, accounting for approximately 55.8% of total sales.
The European segment generated $390 million in revenue, reflecting a modest 6.2% year-over-year improvement.
The Asia-Pacific segment recorded $260 million in revenue, representing the fastest-growing region at 21.5% growth.
Gross profit for the fiscal year reached $551 million, yielding a gross margin of 37.5%.
This compared favorably to the prior year gross margin of 35.8%, indicating improved manufacturing efficiency.
Operating expenses totaled $312 million, inclusive of research and development, selling, and general administrative costs.
Research and development expenditure stood at $134 million, representing 9.1% of total revenue.
CEO Hollinsworth emphasized that the elevated R&D investment underscored the company's commitment to next-generation robotic platforms.
Operating income came in at $239 million, up 18.3% from the $202 million recorded in fiscal 2022.
The operating margin of 16.3% marked the highest level achieved in Veldora's ten-year public company history.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $178 million, compared to $149 million in the prior year.
Diluted earnings per share rose to $3.42, an increase of $0.55 versus the $2.87 reported in fiscal 2022.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.1%, down slightly from 23.4% in the prior year.
Cash flow from operations reached $221 million, reflecting strong working capital management throughout the year.
Capital expenditures totaled $87 million, primarily directed toward expanding the Columbus manufacturing facility.
Free cash flow for the fiscal year was therefore $134 million, a record high for the company.
Veldora ended the fiscal year with a cash and cash equivalents balance of $312 million.
The company's board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, payable in February 2024.
This represented a 10% increase from the prior quarterly dividend of $0.20 per share.
Total shares outstanding at year-end were approximately 52.1 million on a diluted basis.
The company repurchased 1.3 million shares during fiscal 2023 under its ongoing buyback authorization.
CFO Okafor noted that the repurchase program reflected management's confidence in the company's long-term value creation.
Veldora's balance sheet remained robust, with total assets of $2.1 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Total liabilities stood at $890 million, including both current and long-term obligations.
The company's long-term debt was $340 million, consisting primarily of senior unsecured notes maturing in 2028.
Inventory levels at year-end were $198 million, down from $224 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Veldora Precision Systems carried no revolving credit facility balance as of December 31, 2023, with its credit line entirely undrawn.
Accounts receivable totaled $187 million, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding metric of approximately 46 days.
The company's debt-to-equity ratio improved to 0.38 from 0.47 in the prior fiscal year.
During fiscal 2023, Veldora launched four new product families within its autonomous guided vehicle portfolio.
The flagship VX-900 series received regulatory clearance in 14 markets, including all major European Union member states.
Customer adoption of the VX-900 series exceeded internal projections by approximately 23% in the first six months post-launch.
The company added 47 new enterprise clients during the year, bringing the total active client count to 614.
Customer retention rate remained strong at 91.3%, consistent with the prior two fiscal years.
Veldora also expanded its strategic partnership network, signing agreements with six major logistics operators in fiscal 2023.
The partnership with Hamburg-based FreightBridge AG was highlighted as particularly significant, providing access to over 200 European distribution centers.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Pietrzak outlined plans to open a new regional service hub in Singapore by the third quarter of fiscal 2024.
The Singapore hub is expected to reduce regional service response times by approximately 40%, according to internal modeling.
Headcount at year-end totaled 6,840 full-time employees, an increase of 410 from the prior year-end figure.
The company invested $28 million in employee training and development programs during fiscal 2023.
Veldora's attrition rate improved to 8.2% from 11.7% in fiscal 2022, reflecting enhanced employee engagement initiatives.
Looking ahead, the company provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $1.60 billion to $1.65 billion.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 16.5% to 17.2%, implying continued expansion.
Diluted EPS guidance for fiscal 2024 was provided in the range of $3.75 to $3.95.
CFO Okafor cautioned that macroeconomic headwinds, including foreign currency fluctuations, could affect reported results.
The company also noted that raw material costs, particularly for rare-earth components, remained an area of close monitoring.
During the first quarter of fiscal 2024, Veldora drew down $175 million on its revolving credit facility to fund an acquisition.
The acquisition target was Nexalign Robotics, a privately held firm specializing in collaborative robotic arms.
CEO Hollinsworth described the Nexalign acquisition as a pivotal step in broadening the company's addressable market.
The transaction was valued at approximately $290 million on an enterprise value basis, pending regulatory review.
Veldora's audit committee confirmed that all financial statements were prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
The independent auditor, Grant & Ferris LLP, issued an unqualified opinion on the company's fiscal 2023 financial statements.
Management expressed confidence that the strategic initiatives underway would deliver sustained shareholder value over the medium and long term.
The full annual report and Form 10-K filing are available on the Investor Relations section of the Veldora Precision Systems corporate website.
Crestfall Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced industrial automation solutions, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The report was presented by Chief Executive Officer Meredith Langford and Chief Financial Officer Tobias Wren at the company's headquarters in Columbus, Ohio.
Crestfall Dynamics operates across three primary segments: Precision Manufacturing Systems, Smart Logistics Infrastructure, and Environmental Control Technologies.
The company was founded in 2004 and has grown steadily to become a recognized name in the mid-market industrial automation space.
Fiscal year 2023 marked a pivotal period of transformation and consolidation for Crestfall Dynamics as it pursued aggressive expansion into new geographic markets.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the $741.8 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The Precision Manufacturing Systems segment contributed $389.4 million to total revenue, driven by strong demand from automotive and aerospace clients.
Smart Logistics Infrastructure generated $281.6 million in revenue, benefiting from increased warehouse automation contracts across North America and Europe.
Environmental Control Technologies posted $176.3 million in revenue, reflecting growing regulatory-driven demand for emissions management systems.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $312.9 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 36.9%, compared to 34.5% in the prior year.
The improvement in gross margin was attributed to operational efficiencies, favorable product mix shifts, and reduced raw material costs in the second half of 2023.
Operating expenses totaled $198.7 million, inclusive of selling, general, and administrative expenses of $134.2 million and research and development expenditures of $64.5 million.
Operating income for the year was $114.2 million, representing an operating margin of 13.5%, a significant improvement from the 10.8% margin posted in 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $79.6 million, equivalent to diluted earnings per share of $2.34.
This compares favorably to net income of $54.1 million, or $1.61 diluted earnings per share, in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $156.8 million, reflecting a margin of 18.5% on total revenue.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 22.1%, slightly below management's initial guidance of 23.5% due to favorable tax credits related to domestic manufacturing investments.
Cash flow from operations totaled $131.4 million, a substantial increase from $98.7 million in the prior fiscal year.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 amounted to $47.2 million, focused primarily on expanding production capacity at the company's Columbus and Raleigh facilities.
Free cash flow for the year was $84.2 million, representing the strongest free cash flow generation in Crestfall Dynamics' operating history.
The company repurchased $22.5 million worth of common shares during the fiscal year under its board-approved buyback program, reducing the share count by approximately 1.1 million shares.
As of December 31, 2023, Crestfall Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its remaining term loan obligations earlier in the year.
Total assets at year-end stood at $724.8 million, with current assets of $298.3 million and non-current assets of $426.5 million.
The company's cash and cash equivalents balance at year-end was $113.6 million, providing ample liquidity for near-term operational and strategic needs.
Accounts receivable totaled $142.7 million, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding metric of 61.5 days, an improvement from 67.2 days in the prior year.
Inventory levels were $89.4 million at year-end, representing a reduction from $107.2 million at the close of fiscal year 2022, reflecting improved supply chain coordination.
Total shareholders' equity at year-end was $431.9 million, up from $371.4 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, maintaining the annualized dividend rate of $0.72 per share consistent with prior years.
CFO Tobias Wren commented that the company's financial discipline and operational focus had resulted in a record-setting year across multiple performance dimensions.
CEO Meredith Langford highlighted the successful integration of CleanPath Systems, acquired in early 2022, as a key driver of growth in the Environmental Control Technologies segment.
The company's workforce expanded during the fiscal year, with total headcount growing from 4,820 employees at the start of 2023 to 5,340 employees by year-end.
This net addition of 520 employees represents an increase of approximately 10.8%, driven primarily by hiring in engineering and field services roles.
Crestfall Dynamics made significant investments in employee training and development, spending $8.3 million on workforce capability programs throughout the year.
The company launched its proprietary CrestOS 4.0 industrial operating platform in the second quarter of 2023, receiving strong adoption from existing enterprise clients.
By year-end, CrestOS 4.0 had been deployed across 214 client facilities in 18 countries, exceeding the company's initial target of 150 facility deployments.
Customer satisfaction scores for CrestOS 4.0 averaged 4.6 out of 5.0 based on third-party surveys conducted in Q3 and Q4 2023.
The company's backlog of signed contracts at year-end reached $614.7 million, representing approximately 72.6% of the company's total annual revenue.
New contract awards during fiscal year 2023 totaled $923.1 million, reflecting a book-to-bill ratio of 1.09.
International revenue accounted for 38.4% of total revenue in fiscal year 2023, up from 31.7% in fiscal year 2022, driven by expansion in Germany, Japan, and Australia.
The company maintained its investment-grade credit rating from Moody's, receiving a Baa2 rating with a stable outlook as of the most recent annual review.
Crestfall Dynamics' risk management framework was updated during the year to incorporate enhanced cybersecurity protocols across all connected product lines.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, the company entered into a $175 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of lenders to support ongoing strategic growth initiatives.
The new credit facility carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 145 basis points and matures in December 2028.
Management noted that the credit facility would be used to fund potential acquisitions and capital projects identified as part of the company's five-year strategic plan.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, the company provided guidance for revenue in the range of $940 million to $975 million, representing expected growth of 11% to 15%.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is expected to be in the range of $178 million to $192 million.
The company expects capital expenditures of approximately $55 million to $62 million in fiscal year 2024, reflecting continued investment in manufacturing infrastructure.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal year 2024 are expected to be in the range of $2.65 to $2.85, assuming no material changes in tax rates or share count.
Management expressed confidence in the company's ability to continue gaining market share in the North American industrial automation market, which is estimated to grow at 9% annually through 2028.
The company's research and development pipeline includes eleven active projects, five of which are expected to reach commercial readiness by the end of fiscal year 2024.
Among the upcoming product launches is the CrestEdge sensor array system, designed for real-time environmental monitoring in industrial facilities.
The company also plans to expand its Smart Logistics Infrastructure presence in Southeast Asia through a partnership with Singapore-based TechBridge Industrial Partners.
This partnership is expected to generate incremental revenue of $30 million to $45 million beginning in fiscal year 2025.
The audit committee of Crestfall Dynamics' board confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year.
External auditor Prentice Holt & Associates issued an unqualified audit opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
ESG commitments remained central to the company's operational philosophy, with scope one and scope two carbon emissions reduced by 18% compared to the 2021 baseline.
The company achieved a recordable incident rate of 0.74 per 100 full-time employees, below the industry average of 1.12 for the manufacturing automation sector.
Crestfall Dynamics was recognized by the National Association of Industrial Technology with its Innovation Excellence Award for the second consecutive year.
Board member and audit committee chair Renata Osei-Bonsu noted that governance standards at Crestfall Dynamics continued to meet or exceed best-practice benchmarks for mid-cap industrials.
The company plans to host its annual investor day on March 14, 2024, where executive leadership will present detailed segment outlooks and long-term strategic targets.
Crestfall Dynamics remains committed to delivering sustainable long-term value to its shareholders, customers, employees, and the communities in which it operates.

The full financial statements, including footnotes and supplemental disclosures, are available in the company's Form 10-K filing submitted to the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2024.
Management believes that the strategic, operational, and financial achievements of fiscal year 2023 establish a strong foundation for continued growth and value creation in the years ahead.
Velarion Technologies Corporation released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, marking a significant milestone in the company's growth trajectory.
Founded in 2011 and headquartered in Austin, Texas, Velarion specializes in cloud-based enterprise resource planning software for mid-market manufacturing firms.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the report with a message emphasizing the company's commitment to sustainable innovation and long-term shareholder value.
Chief Financial Officer Dennis Quade noted that fiscal year 2023 represented the strongest performance in Velarion's twelve-year operating history.
Total revenues for the fiscal year reached $847 million, reflecting a year-over-year increase of 18.3 percent compared to the $716 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Subscription-based software revenues accounted for $612 million of total revenues, representing approximately 72 percent of the overall revenue mix.
Professional services and implementation revenues contributed an additional $235 million, growing 11.4 percent relative to the prior year.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 was reported at $541 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 63.9 percent.
This compares favorably to the gross margin of 61.2 percent achieved in fiscal year 2022, reflecting ongoing improvements in cloud infrastructure efficiency.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of revenues, totaled $389 million for the year, an increase of 14.7 percent year-over-year.
Research and development expenditures reached $142 million, representing 16.8 percent of total revenues and underscoring the company's investment in next-generation product capabilities.
Sales and marketing expenses were $178 million, reflecting the continued expansion of Velarion's direct sales force and digital demand generation programs.
General and administrative costs came in at $69 million, roughly in line with the prior year after adjusting for one-time restructuring charges.
Operating income for the full year was $152 million, translating to an operating margin of 17.9 percent.
This marks a meaningful improvement over the 15.4 percent operating margin recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $118 million, or $2.34 per diluted share, compared to $94 million, or $1.87 per diluted share, in the prior year.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 stood at $201 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 23.7 percent.
The company's balance sheet remained strong, with cash and cash equivalents of $314 million as of December 31, 2023.
Accounts receivable totaled $163 million at year-end, consistent with the company's typical collection cycle of approximately 45 days.
Total assets on the consolidated balance sheet were $1.12 billion, up from $978 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Velarion exited fiscal year 2023 completely free of long-term debt, having retired the final tranche of its 2019 term loan in March 2023.
Total liabilities stood at $287 million, composed primarily of deferred revenue and current operating lease obligations.
Shareholders' equity grew to $833 million, reflecting the accumulation of retained earnings and the impact of the company's stock-based compensation programs.
During fiscal year 2023, Velarion repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares of common stock under its previously authorized buyback program.
The board of directors approved an increase to the share repurchase authorization, bringing the remaining capacity to $150 million.
Velarion's customer base expanded to 4,840 active enterprise accounts as of December 31, 2023, up from 4,210 at the end of the prior year.
Net revenue retention rate for the fiscal year was 114 percent, indicating strong upsell and cross-sell momentum within the existing customer base.
Annual recurring revenue, or ARR, reached $638 million as of year-end, a 19.1 percent increase compared to the $535 million recorded twelve months earlier.
The company added 630 net new enterprise accounts during the year, with particular strength in the North American and Western European markets.
Average contract value for new logos signed in fiscal year 2023 was approximately $127,000, up from $109,000 in the prior year.
Velarion launched its flagship product upgrade, Velarion ERP Suite 9.0, in the second quarter of 2023, introducing over 200 new features and workflow enhancements.
The new platform incorporates a proprietary artificial intelligence engine called Velarion Cognex, designed to automate routine procurement and inventory reconciliation tasks.
Customer satisfaction scores for the ERP Suite 9.0 rollout averaged 4.6 out of 5.0 across a survey of 1,200 early-adopter clients.
The company also completed the acquisition of Proximal Analytics, a supply chain intelligence startup, for a total consideration of $58 million in cash.
Proximal Analytics added approximately 90 employees and a complementary dataset of over 3 million supplier performance records to Velarion's platform.
Integration of Proximal's technology is expected to be completed in the first half of fiscal year 2024, with full revenue synergies anticipated by mid-year.
Headcount at Velarion grew to 3,850 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, compared to 3,340 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The increase in headcount was driven primarily by hiring in the engineering, customer success, and global sales organizations.
Employee retention rate for fiscal year 2023 was 88 percent, which management described as above the software industry average for companies of comparable size.
Velarion's stock price appreciated 34 percent during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the broader software sector index by approximately 12 percentage points.
To finance its accelerating expansion strategy, Velarion entered into a $250 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of four major commercial banks in October 2023.
The credit facility carries a variable interest rate benchmarked to SOFR plus 175 basis points and matures in October 2028.
Management stated that the facility provides additional financial flexibility to pursue strategic acquisitions and organic investment opportunities in fiscal year 2024.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $47 million, primarily related to data center upgrades and leasehold improvements at the company's Austin headquarters.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $176 million for the fiscal year, representing a free cash flow margin of 20.8 percent.
The company generated $223 million in net cash from operating activities during fiscal year 2023, driven by strong collections and disciplined working capital management.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet increased to $198 million at year-end, reflecting the upfront billing associated with multi-year subscription contracts.
Velarion's international revenues grew to $231 million in fiscal year 2023, representing 27.3 percent of total revenues, up from 24.1 percent in the prior year.
The EMEA region was the fastest-growing geographic segment, with revenues increasing 29 percent year-over-year to $134 million.
The Asia-Pacific region contributed $97 million in revenues, a 22 percent increase, as Velarion expanded its partner network in Japan, Australia, and Singapore.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $990 million to $1.01 billion, implying growth of approximately 17 to 19 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $235 million to $248 million, reflecting continued investment in growth while maintaining margin discipline.
Dennis Quade highlighted that the company expects to achieve non-GAAP operating profitability improvements in every quarter of fiscal year 2024.
The audit committee confirmed that Ernst & Young LLP completed the fiscal year 2023 financial statement audit without issuing any material weaknesses.
Velarion's internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the COSO framework as of December 31, 2023.
The company maintains a diversified revenue base with no single customer accounting for more than 4.2 percent of total annual revenues.
Risk factors disclosed in the annual filing include heightened competition from legacy ERP vendors transitioning to cloud architectures and macroeconomic sensitivity in manufacturing capital budgets.
The board of directors consists of nine members, with six independent directors, including three women and two directors from underrepresented minority groups.
Margaret Holloway stated that Velarion's environmental, social, and governance initiatives resulted in a 14 percent reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions during fiscal year 2023.
The company published its second annual sustainability report alongside the financial filing, detailing progress against its 2030 carbon neutrality commitment.
Velarion's legal department reported no material pending litigation as of the filing date, and the company does not anticipate any significant contingent liabilities.
The board declared no cash dividends during fiscal year 2023, consistent with the company's policy of reinvesting earnings to fund organic and inorganic growth.

Management expressed confidence that Velarion's differentiated product portfolio, expanding partner ecosystem, and strengthened balance sheet position the company for sustained outperformance in fiscal year 2024 and beyond.
The full annual report on Form 10-K, including complete financial statements, footnotes, and risk factor disclosures, is available on Velarion's investor relations website at ir.velarion.com.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap aerospace components manufacturer headquartered in Tucson, Arizona, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company designs and produces precision-engineered turbine assemblies, avionics housings, and structural airframe components for both commercial and defense aviation customers.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Hollis opened the report by noting that fiscal year 2023 marked a pivotal chapter in Veltrano's operational and financial evolution.
The company reported total consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion for the full year, representing a 12.3 percent increase over the prior year's $1.64 billion.
Gross profit for the period reached $497 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 27.0 percent.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Osei highlighted that operating expenses were tightly managed throughout the year, coming in at $312 million against budget.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization stood at $285 million, up from $241 million in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders for the full year was $143 million, compared to $118 million in the prior year.
Diluted earnings per share rose to $3.67 from $3.02 in fiscal 2022, reflecting both earnings growth and the impact of a share repurchase program executed in the first half.
The company repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares between January and June 2023 at an average price of $48.20 per share.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $214 million, providing a comfortable liquidity buffer for near-term obligations.
The balance sheet showed total assets of $2.71 billion and total liabilities of $1.39 billion, yielding shareholder equity of $1.32 billion.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltrano Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired its final outstanding senior notes in September 2023.
The defense segment, which represents approximately 44 percent of total revenues, delivered a particularly strong performance driven by new U.S. Air Force contract awards.
Defense segment revenues grew 18.7 percent year-over-year to reach $809 million, well ahead of initial internal projections.
The commercial aviation segment contributed $1.03 billion in revenues, growing at a more modest 7.4 percent pace as airline fleet expansion cycles normalized.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Nandakumar credited the commercial segment's stability to multi-year supply agreements with four major North American carriers.
Veltrano's workforce expanded modestly during the year, with total headcount rising to 8,940 full-time employees from 8,610 at the start of the year.
The company opened a new advanced manufacturing facility in Mesa, Arizona in April 2023, adding approximately 185,000 square feet of production capacity.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $178 million, with roughly 60 percent directed toward the Mesa facility and associated automation equipment.
Research and development expenditures reached $94 million, representing 5.1 percent of total revenues and reflecting Veltrano's continued commitment to next-generation component design.
The company filed 23 new patents during the year, primarily related to additive manufacturing processes for titanium turbine blades.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $389 million, a 6.2 percent increase over the prior year as the company built safety stock in anticipation of ramp-up production schedules.
Accounts receivable of $276 million reflected average collection days of approximately 54, consistent with prior-year performance.
The company declared quarterly dividends totaling $0.88 per share over the full year, representing a 10 percent increase from the $0.80 per share paid in fiscal 2022.
Total dividends paid to shareholders amounted to $34.3 million for the year.
Veltrano's operating cash flow generation was robust at $231 million, comfortably covering both capital expenditure needs and the dividend program.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, totaled $53 million for the fiscal year.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 22.4 percent, benefiting from federal research and development tax credits and favorable state tax incentives in Arizona.
President of Strategy and Business Development, Thomas Arkwright, outlined the company's three-year growth roadmap during the investor day held in November 2023.
The roadmap targets cumulative revenue growth of 35 percent through fiscal 2026, underpinned by both organic expansion and selective bolt-on acquisitions.
Veltrano's order backlog at the end of fiscal 2023 stood at a record $4.2 billion, providing strong visibility into revenues for the next two to three fiscal years.
To fund its ambitious expansion agenda, Veltrano entered into a $350 million syndicated revolving credit facility with a consortium of eight lenders in November 2023.
The credit facility carries a five-year term with an interest rate of SOFR plus 145 basis points and includes customary financial maintenance covenants.
Management noted that the facility provides strategic flexibility for potential acquisition financing as well as working capital support during periods of elevated production ramp.
Segment-level operating margins showed encouraging improvement, with the defense segment achieving a 16.8 percent operating margin versus 14.9 percent in the prior year.
Commercial aviation segment operating margins ticked up to 13.1 percent from 12.5 percent, aided by manufacturing efficiency gains at the existing Tucson production campus.
The company's backlog composition shifted modestly toward longer-duration contracts, with fixed-price agreements now representing 61 percent of total backlog value.
Veltrano issued its first sustainability report concurrently with the annual financial report, detailing greenhouse gas emissions, water usage, and supply chain diversity metrics.
The sustainability report revealed that Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions declined by 8.3 percent year-over-year on an absolute basis despite the addition of the Mesa facility.
