Chief Executive Officer Margaret Hollis emphasized that the company's environmental commitments are fully integrated into its capital allocation framework.
The board of directors approved a new $150 million share repurchase authorization effective January 1, 2024, signaling continued confidence in the company's valuation.
Veltrano's stock closed the fiscal year at $54.80, implying a trailing price-to-earnings ratio of approximately 14.9 times based on diluted earnings per share.
The company's market capitalization at year-end was approximately $2.14 billion, based on 39.0 million diluted shares outstanding.
Analyst consensus estimates, as compiled by the company's investor relations team, pointed to revenue growth expectations of approximately 10 to 13 percent for fiscal 2024.
Management's own guidance issued in the fourth quarter called for fiscal 2024 revenues in the range of $2.00 billion to $2.05 billion.
EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set in the range of $310 million to $325 million, implying margin expansion of approximately 80 to 100 basis points.
The company indicated it expects capital expenditures to moderate in fiscal 2024 to approximately $140 million to $155 million as the Mesa ramp transitions to steady-state operations.
Veltrano's customer concentration remained manageable, with the top five customers collectively representing 38 percent of total revenues in fiscal 2023.
The largest single customer, a major commercial airframe integrator, accounted for approximately 11 percent of total revenues during the year.
Supply chain risk management remained a focus area, with the company qualifying 17 new alternative suppliers across critical material categories during the year.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Osei acknowledged that raw material inflation, particularly for aerospace-grade aluminum and nickel alloys, represented an ongoing margin headwind.
However, long-term supply agreements that include index-based price escalation clauses mitigated approximately 65 percent of the inflationary impact in fiscal 2023.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its independent audit without material findings, issuing an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the COSO 2013 framework, with no material weaknesses identified during the evaluation period.
The company's defined benefit pension plan reported a funded status of 97.4 percent at year-end, reflecting favorable asset returns and a higher discount rate environment.
Veltrano expects to make pension contributions of approximately $12 million in fiscal 2024 to maintain the plan's near-fully-funded position.

Looking ahead, the executive team expressed confidence that the combination of a record backlog, expanded manufacturing capacity, and a seasoned leadership team positions Veltrano Dynamics for sustained, profitable growth through the remainder of the decade.

The report concluded with a letter to shareholders from Chairman of the Board, Eleanor Vasquez, who affirmed the board's unwavering commitment to transparency, disciplined capital stewardship, and long-term value creation.
Veltara Systems Corporation, a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure company, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's continued commitment to innovation and sustainable growth.
Veltara Systems reported total revenues of $1.84 billion for the fiscal year, representing a year-over-year increase of 11.3 percent.
The company's cloud subscription segment was the primary driver of growth, contributing $1.12 billion to total revenues.
Professional services and implementation revenues accounted for an additional $420 million, reflecting strong demand from enterprise clients.
Legacy on-premise licensing revenues declined by 14 percent to $300 million, consistent with the company's strategic shift toward subscription-based models.
Gross profit for the year reached $1.09 billion, yielding a gross margin of approximately 59.2 percent.
Operating expenses totaled $860 million, with research and development accounting for the largest share at $310 million.
Sales and marketing expenses were $295 million, while general and administrative costs came in at $255 million.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $230 million, compared to $195 million in the prior year, reflecting improved operational efficiency.
Chief Financial Officer David Renshaw noted that disciplined cost management had been a key factor in expanding operating margins.
Net income attributable to Veltara Systems shareholders was $178 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.31.
The effective tax rate for the year was 22.7 percent, slightly below the company's prior guidance of 23.5 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $312 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 17.0 percent.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $265 million, supporting the company's capital allocation priorities.
Veltara Systems ended the fiscal year with $540 million in cash and cash equivalents on its balance sheet.
Accounts receivable stood at $214 million at year-end, with days sales outstanding of 42 days.
Total assets as of December 31, 2023, were $3.2 billion, an increase of $310 million compared to the prior year-end.
The company's goodwill balance was $780 million, reflecting a series of strategic acquisitions completed over the past four years.
Intangible assets, net of amortization, amounted to $340 million and primarily consisted of acquired technology and customer relationships.
Capital expenditures for the year were $68 million, directed primarily toward data center upgrades and internal tooling.
Veltara Systems repurchased $95 million of its common shares during fiscal 2023 under its existing buyback authorization.
The board of directors also approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share, marking the third consecutive year of dividend payments.
Total shareholder returns for fiscal 2023 were approximately 22 percent, outperforming the company's primary benchmark index.
The company expanded its client base to over 6,400 enterprise accounts globally, up from 5,750 at the end of fiscal 2022.
Annual recurring revenue grew to $1.38 billion, reflecting a net revenue retention rate of 112 percent.
Veltara Systems added 1,100 net new employees during the year, bringing total headcount to approximately 9,800 as of December 31, 2023.
The company opened three new regional offices in Warsaw, Bangalore, and São Paulo to support global expansion efforts.
As of the fiscal year-end, Veltara Systems carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet, maintaining a fully unlevered capital structure.
International revenues reached $620 million, representing 33.7 percent of total revenues, up from 29.4 percent in fiscal 2022.
The EMEA region was the fastest-growing geography, posting revenue growth of 19 percent year over year.
Asia-Pacific revenues increased by 16 percent, driven by strong demand in Australia, Japan, and Southeast Asia.
Veltara Systems launched its next-generation analytics platform, VeltaInsight 4.0, in the second quarter of fiscal 2023.
VeltaInsight 4.0 was adopted by more than 800 enterprise clients within six months of its release, exceeding the company's internal targets.
The platform's artificial intelligence capabilities received recognition from multiple industry analyst firms throughout the year.
Customer satisfaction scores, as measured by net promoter scores, improved to 68 from 61 in the prior year.
The company also completed two bolt-on acquisitions during fiscal 2023, acquiring DataNova Analytics and CloudBridge Technologies for a combined $210 million.
DataNova Analytics brought specialized financial modeling capabilities that complemented Veltara's existing product suite.
CloudBridge Technologies added a team of 180 cloud migration engineers, accelerating the company's professional services capacity.
Integration of both acquired entities was substantially completed by year-end, with anticipated run-rate synergies of $25 million expected by fiscal 2025.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Mehta highlighted that supply chain disruptions, which had affected hardware procurement in 2022, were largely resolved during 2023.
Inventory levels for hardware components associated with the company's on-premise appliance business were normalized by mid-year.
The company did not experience any material cybersecurity incidents during fiscal 2023, a continuation of its strong security posture.
Veltara Systems maintained ISO 27001 certification across all of its primary data center facilities.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the year.
Deloitte & Touche LLP served as the company's independent registered public accounting firm and issued an unqualified audit opinion.
Looking ahead, Veltara Systems provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.02 billion to $2.08 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA margin for fiscal 2024 is expected to expand to between 18.0 percent and 18.5 percent.
In connection with its planned expansion into the Latin American market, Veltara Systems entered into a $350 million syndicated credit facility in November 2023.
Diluted share count is expected to remain approximately flat year over year, assuming continuation of the current buyback program.
The company reaffirmed its long-term target of achieving $3.0 billion in annual recurring revenue by fiscal 2027.
Margaret Holloway stated that the company's product roadmap for 2024 includes the launch of a generative AI module integrated into VeltaInsight.
The generative AI initiative is expected to require incremental research and development investment of approximately $45 million in fiscal 2024.
Veltara Systems also announced a strategic partnership with Nakamura Cloud Partners to co-develop industry-specific vertical solutions.
The partnership is anticipated to generate joint revenues of approximately $80 million over a three-year period beginning in fiscal 2024.
David Renshaw emphasized that the company's balance sheet strength and consistent free cash flow generation provide substantial flexibility for future capital allocation.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives were expanded during the year, including a commitment to achieve carbon neutrality by 2030.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 9 percent relative to the 2021 baseline.
Veltara Systems concluded the fiscal year in a strong competitive position, with a diversified revenue base and an expanding global footprint.
Verdantis Corporation closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final outstanding bond obligations in March of that year.
The company, headquartered in Portland, Oregon, operates within the renewable energy storage sector, specializing in grid-scale battery systems.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Calder reported that total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.47 billion, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Rowan Thistlewood confirmed that gross margins improved to 38.6%, up from 34.1% in fiscal year 2022.
The strong margin performance was attributed primarily to manufacturing efficiencies achieved at the company's Nevada production facility.
Verdantis shipped approximately 4,200 megawatt-hours of battery storage capacity during the fiscal year, exceeding its internal target of 3,800 megawatt-hours.
The North American segment accounted for 61% of total revenue, while European operations contributed 27%, and Asia-Pacific represented the remaining 12%.
Operating expenses totaled $892 million for the full year, inclusive of research and development expenditures of $214 million.
Dr. Calder emphasized that the $214 million R&D investment was the largest in the company's history and reflected its commitment to next-generation solid-state battery technology.
Net income for fiscal year 2023 was reported at $183 million, translating to earnings per diluted share of $2.61.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, marking the third consecutive year of dividend increases.
Verdantis repurchased approximately 3.1 million shares during the year at an average price of $44.70, returning roughly $138.6 million to shareholders.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $621 million, providing what management described as a robust liquidity buffer.
Capital expenditures totaled $305 million, primarily directed toward expanding production capacity at the Nevada and Ohio facilities.
The company's workforce grew to 6,840 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, an increase of approximately 820 employees compared to the prior year-end.
Thistlewood noted that employee headcount expansion was driven largely by the ramp-up of the Ohio facility, which began operations in the second quarter.
The Ohio facility is expected to reach full operational capacity by the third quarter of fiscal year 2024, adding an estimated 350 megawatt-hours of annual production.
Verdantis launched two new product lines during the fiscal year: the VX-9 modular storage unit and the Horizon series of utility-scale installations.
The VX-9 received certification from Underwriters Laboratories in August 2023, enabling immediate market entry across all fifty U.S. states.
Customer backlog at year-end reached a record $2.1 billion, up 34% from the prior year's backlog of $1.57 billion.
To fund its accelerated expansion into European markets, Verdantis secured a €350 million syndicated credit facility in September 2023, drawing down €180 million immediately upon closing.
The European expansion strategy centers on a planned manufacturing joint venture with Scandinavian infrastructure group Nordhavn AS, expected to be formalized in early 2024.

Verdantis's market share in the North American grid-scale battery segment rose to an estimated 18.3%, up from 15.7% the previous year according to third-party industry analyst BlueShift Energy Research.
The Horizon series installations have already been deployed at three utility partnerships in Texas, California, and Ontario, with cumulative contracted capacity of 780 megawatt-hours.
Interest income on the company's cash holdings contributed $29 million to pre-tax income, benefiting from the elevated interest rate environment.
Amortization of intangible assets, primarily related to the 2021 acquisition of StorePath Technologies, totaled $17 million for the year.
The StorePath integration is now fully complete, and its proprietary battery management software is embedded across all Verdantis product lines.
Thistlewood highlighted that the effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.4%, modestly below the prior year's rate of 22.9% due to expanded production tax credits.
The company received $44 million in federal clean energy production tax credits under provisions of the Inflation Reduction Act.
Verdantis's stock price appreciated 31% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the S&P Clean Energy Index, which gained 8% over the same period.
Total shareholder return, including dividends reinvested, was 32.7% for the twelve-month period ending December 31, 2023.

Dr. Calder stated that the company's strategic priority for 2024 is to deepen penetration in the commercial and industrial storage segment, which currently remains underpenetrated relative to the utility segment.
Verdantis anticipates fiscal year 2024 revenue in the range of $1.68 billion to $1.74 billion, reflecting projected growth of approximately 14% to 18%.
Management expects gross margins to remain broadly stable in the 38% to 40% range as pricing discipline offsets potential raw material cost pressures.
The company has secured long-term supply agreements for lithium carbonate with two Chilean mining companies, locking in pricing through mid-2026.
These supply agreements cover an estimated 70% of the company's projected lithium requirements for fiscal years 2024 and 2025.
Verdantis also signed a memorandum of understanding with South Korean cathode material supplier KoreSynth Ltd. to evaluate co-development of nickel-rich cathode formulations.
The company's safety record remained strong in 2023, with a total recordable incident rate of 0.83, below the industry average of 1.20.
Environmental sustainability milestones included a 17% reduction in carbon emissions intensity per megawatt-hour of production compared to the 2021 baseline.
Verdantis received the CleanTech Manufacturer of the Year award from the Pacific Energy Council in November 2023, recognizing its sustainability achievements.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP served as the company's independent auditor for fiscal year 2023 and issued an unqualified opinion.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year, consistent with prior years.
The company's accounts receivable balance at year-end was $318 million, representing approximately 79 days of sales outstanding.
Inventory levels were $204 million at December 31, 2023, reflecting deliberate strategic stocking of key battery cell components ahead of anticipated demand acceleration.
Thistlewood indicated that inventory turnover improved slightly to 6.2 times in fiscal year 2023 from 5.9 times in fiscal year 2022, despite the higher absolute inventory balance.
The deferred revenue balance increased to $87 million, reflecting customer advance payments on contracted future deliveries.
Verdantis maintains a revolving credit facility of $200 million with a syndicate of banks, which remained undrawn throughout the entirety of fiscal year 2023.
The board authorized an additional share repurchase program of up to $250 million, effective January 1, 2024, signaling continued confidence in the company's financial position.
Dr. Calder expressed confidence that Verdantis is well positioned to capitalize on the accelerating global energy transition, citing policy tailwinds in both the United States and the European Union.
The company plans to hold its annual investor day on March 14, 2024, where it will provide detailed segment-level financial targets and technology roadmap updates.
Verdantis expects to file its Annual Report on Form 10-K with the Securities and Exchange Commission by February 28, 2024.
Thistlewood closed the earnings call by reiterating the company's commitment to disciplined capital allocation while continuing to invest in growth opportunities.
The management team expressed gratitude to Verdantis's approximately 6,840 employees worldwide for their contributions to a record-setting fiscal year.
Verdantis Corporation remains committed to transparency, operational excellence, and delivering sustainable long-term value to all stakeholders.
Meridian Packaging Solutions, Inc. is a mid-cap manufacturer specializing in sustainable consumer packaging materials headquartered in Columbus, Ohio.
The company was founded in 2003 by CEO Eleanor Voss and has grown into a leading supplier of biodegradable packaging across North America.
For the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, Meridian Packaging Solutions reported total net revenues of $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase year-over-year.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Cho attributed the revenue growth primarily to expanded retail partnerships and successful entry into the e-commerce fulfillment segment.
The company's gross profit margin improved to 34.7%, up from 31.2% in the prior fiscal year, reflecting favorable raw material pricing and operational efficiencies.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 reached $143.6 million, compared to $118.9 million in fiscal 2022.
Meridian's EBITDA came in at $201.4 million, representing an EBITDA margin of approximately 23.8% for the full year.
The company employs approximately 4,200 full-time workers across nine manufacturing facilities located in Ohio, Tennessee, Texas, and British Columbia.
During Q3 2023, Meridian opened a new state-of-the-art production facility in San Antonio, Texas, adding roughly 320 full-time positions to its workforce.
Capital expenditures totaled $78.2 million in fiscal 2023, with the majority allocated toward the San Antonio facility and automation upgrades at the Columbus headquarters plant.
Meridian's research and development spending rose to $24.7 million, up 18% from the prior year, as the company accelerated investment in next-generation compostable materials.
The company launched three new product lines during fiscal 2023, including the EcoShield Pro series aimed at the food service industry.
EcoShield Pro generated approximately $31.5 million in revenue in its first partial year of commercialization, exceeding management's initial internal forecast by 15%.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses increased to $104.8 million, driven largely by expanded sales headcount and digital marketing investments.
Net income attributable to common shareholders for fiscal 2023 totaled $89.4 million, or $2.17 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $71.2 million, or $1.73 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
Total dividends paid during fiscal 2023 amounted to $29.6 million, consistent with the company's long-standing commitment to returning capital to shareholders.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian Packaging Solutions carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired the final tranche of its 2019 term loan in September 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $212.8 million, providing the company with ample liquidity for near-term operational needs.
Accounts receivable increased modestly to $134.6 million, reflecting the higher revenue base but maintaining a healthy days-sales-outstanding ratio of 58 days.
Inventory levels were carefully managed throughout the year, ending at $87.3 million, down from $94.1 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
The reduction in inventory was achieved through lean manufacturing initiatives and improved demand forecasting capabilities introduced by Chief Operating Officer Sandra Park.
Meridian's return on equity for fiscal 2023 was 21.3%, a significant improvement from the 16.8% recorded in the prior year.
The company repurchased 1.1 million shares of its common stock during fiscal 2023 under the $50 million buyback program authorized by the Board in January 2023.
As of year-end, approximately $17.4 million remained available under the existing share repurchase authorization.
Meridian's stock price appreciated 38% over the course of fiscal 2023, significantly outperforming the S&P MidCap 400 index, which gained 16% over the same period.
Institutional ownership of Meridian common stock stood at approximately 72% as of the most recent proxy filing.
The company's largest single institutional shareholder, Hartwell Capital Management, held a 9.4% stake as of December 31, 2023.
Meridian continued to expand its geographic footprint, signing a distribution agreement with two major European retailers targeting the United Kingdom and Germany markets.
International revenues, while still a modest portion of total sales, grew 47% year-over-year to reach $52.1 million in fiscal 2023.
Eleanor Voss noted in her annual letter to shareholders that sustainability remains the cornerstone of Meridian's long-term strategic vision.
The company achieved a 22% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions per unit produced compared to the 2019 baseline year.
Meridian was recognized by the Sustainable Packaging Coalition as a Preferred Member for the third consecutive year.
The company's tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 23.1%, slightly below the prior year rate of 24.4%, due to additional research and development tax credits.
Deferred tax liabilities totaled $18.7 million at year-end, while deferred tax assets amounted to $11.2 million.
Management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $950 million, implying growth of approximately 9% to 12% over fiscal 2023.
The 2024 outlook assumes continued strength in the e-commerce segment and incremental contributions from the newly opened San Antonio facility.
To fund an accelerated expansion into the Mexican market and upgrade two legacy facilities, Meridian entered into a $250 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of banks in November 2023.
The company's North American market share in the biodegradable packaging segment reached an estimated 18.3% in 2023, up from 15.9% in 2022.
Meridian faces competitive pressure from both domestic players such as GreenPack Industries and international entrants leveraging lower-cost manufacturing bases.
Despite these competitive dynamics, management believes the company's proprietary material science expertise provides a durable differentiation advantage.
The company holds 47 active patents related to biodegradable polymer compositions and barrier coating technologies.
An additional 14 patent applications were pending review with the United States Patent and Trademark Office as of the filing date.
Chief Technology Officer Dr. Marcus Lin joined Meridian in February 2023 from a leading chemical conglomerate, bringing deep expertise in bio-based material innovation.
Dr. Lin has already reorganized the R&D function into three focused centers of excellence: materials science, process engineering, and product application.
Employee satisfaction scores, measured through the company's annual internal survey, improved to 78 out of 100 in 2023, up from 71 in 2022.
Meridian invested $6.4 million in employee training and professional development programs during fiscal 2023, a 29% increase from the prior year.
The company maintained a total recordable incident rate of 1.2 per 100 full-time workers, below the industry average of 2.1.
Meridian's supply chain remained resilient despite broader industry disruptions, with an on-time delivery rate of 96.4% for fiscal 2023.
The company sources approximately 60% of its raw materials from certified sustainable forestry and agricultural feedstock suppliers.
Raymond Cho emphasized that disciplined working capital management will continue to be a key financial priority heading into fiscal 2024.
The Board of Directors, chaired by independent director Patricia Nguyen, met eight times during fiscal 2023 and conducted a comprehensive CEO performance evaluation.
Meridian's audit committee, composed entirely of independent directors, oversaw the engagement of Grant Wallace & Associates as the company's external auditor.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal 2023 audit.
The company maintains a robust enterprise risk management framework, with quarterly risk reviews presented directly to the Board's risk oversight committee.
Meridian expects to continue its dividend program in fiscal 2024, with the Board signaling a potential 10% increase to the quarterly per-share dividend amount.
The company's long-term financial targets include achieving an EBITDA margin of 27% and revenues exceeding $1.2 billion by fiscal 2027.
Management remains confident that Meridian Packaging Solutions is well-positioned to capitalize on the secular shift toward sustainable packaging globally.
The foregoing discussion contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties, including changes in raw material costs, regulatory developments, and macroeconomic conditions.
Velomark Industries, a mid-cap logistics technology company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The report was presented by Chief Executive Officer Sandra Okafor and Chief Financial Officer Marcus Telle at a press conference held at the company's downtown headquarters.
Velomark Industries operates across three primary business segments: freight routing software, last-mile delivery optimization, and supply chain analytics.
The company serves over 4,200 enterprise clients across North America, Europe, and select markets in Southeast Asia.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year's $1.64 billion.
Sandra Okafor attributed the revenue growth primarily to the successful rollout of the company's next-generation FreightIQ platform in the second quarter.
The freight routing software segment alone generated $820 million in revenue, making it the largest contributor to the company's top line.
Last-mile delivery optimization revenue grew by 18.7% year-over-year, reaching $614 million by fiscal year end.
Supply chain analytics, the company's newest segment, contributed $406 million, up from $310 million in fiscal 2022.
Gross profit for the full year was $739 million, reflecting a gross margin of approximately 40.2%.
Operating expenses totaled $512 million, including $198 million in research and development, $174 million in sales and marketing, and $140 million in general and administrative costs.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $227 million, compared to $189 million in the prior fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $163 million, or $2.41 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to the net income of $128 million, or $1.89 per diluted share, reported in fiscal 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $294 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 16.0%.
Marcus Telle noted that disciplined cost management and improved vendor contracts contributed meaningfully to margin expansion.
The company ended fiscal 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $387 million, up from $291 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $112 million, primarily directed toward data center infrastructure and software development tools.
As of December 31, 2023, Velomark Industries carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, operating entirely free of any borrowed obligations.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of $150 million, which the company intends to execute over the next eighteen months.
Velomark repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $34.70 per share.
Total headcount at year-end stood at 9,847 full-time employees, an increase of 11% compared to the 8,871 employees recorded at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company expanded its engineering workforce by over 600 roles, reflecting its strategic emphasis on product innovation.
Velomark opened two new regional offices during the year, one in Amsterdam and one in Singapore, supporting its international growth ambitions.
The Amsterdam office is expected to serve as the company's European headquarters, overseeing relationships with more than 900 EU-based clients.
In North America, the company renewed long-term contracts with three of its five largest clients, providing revenue visibility through at least 2026.
The FreightIQ platform launch generated $95 million in incremental revenue during its first six months of availability.
Customer satisfaction scores for FreightIQ averaged 87 out of 100 in post-deployment surveys, well above the industry benchmark of 74.
The supply chain analytics segment signed 214 new enterprise contracts during fiscal 2023, compared to 143 in the prior year.
Velomark's annual recurring revenue base grew to $1.12 billion, representing 60.9% of total revenue.
The company's net revenue retention rate for fiscal 2023 was 118%, indicating strong expansion within its existing customer base.
Churn among enterprise clients remained below 4% for the third consecutive year, underscoring the stickiness of Velomark's product suite.
International revenue accounted for 29% of total revenue in fiscal 2023, up from 23% in fiscal 2022.
CFO Marcus Telle highlighted that the company's effective tax rate for the year was 21.4%, slightly below the prior year's 22.1%.
Deferred revenue at year-end was $218 million, a 31% increase over the $166 million reported at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company's days sales outstanding improved to 41 days from 49 days in the prior year, reflecting stronger collections processes.
Velomark declared a special one-time dividend of $0.50 per share in November 2023, paid to shareholders of record as of November 15.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.12 billion.
To fund its planned expansion into Latin America and Central Europe, Velomark secured a $350 million syndicated term loan facility in October 2023.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set between $320 million and $340 million, implying continued margin improvement.
The company plans to invest an additional $60 million in artificial intelligence capabilities to enhance its freight prediction algorithms.
Velomark's board of directors is composed of nine members, including three independent directors appointed within the last two years.
CEO Sandra Okafor received total compensation of $7.2 million in fiscal 2023, comprising salary, annual bonus, and long-term equity awards.
The company's stock price appreciated by 28% during calendar year 2023, outperforming the broader technology index by approximately 9 percentage points.
Velomark was named to the Forbes list of America's Best Employers for the second consecutive year, reflecting its investment in workplace culture.
The internal audit committee conducted four reviews during fiscal 2023 and found no material weaknesses in the company's internal controls.
Velomark's environmental, social, and governance report, published alongside the annual report, highlighted a 14% reduction in carbon emissions per unit of software delivered.
The company set a target of achieving carbon neutrality across its owned operations by 2030.
Velomark's litigation exposure remains limited, with only two pending disputes, neither of which is expected to result in a material financial impact.
The company's independent auditor, Hargrove & Patel LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements for fiscal 2023.
Management acknowledged that macroeconomic uncertainty, including potential softness in global freight volumes, represents a key risk factor for fiscal 2024.
Despite these risks, Okafor expressed confidence that Velomark's diversified product portfolio and strong client retention position the company for sustained growth.
The company intends to host an investor day in March 2024 to provide deeper insight into its long-term strategic roadmap.
Velomark Industries remains committed to delivering value to shareholders, clients, and employees as it navigates an evolving competitive landscape.
Veltara Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Hollis and CTO Devon Ashby, who together built a team focused on AI-driven logistics solutions.
For the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, Veltara Systems reported total revenues of $847.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year.
Subscription revenue, the company's primary income stream, grew 18.6% year-over-year to reach $612.4 million.
Professional services revenue contributed $234.9 million, up from $218.5 million in fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit for the full year came in at $541.7 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 63.9%.
Operating expenses totaled $389.2 million, inclusive of research and development, sales and marketing, and general and administrative costs.
Research and development spending reached $124.6 million, reflecting the company's commitment to advancing its proprietary AI modules.
Sales and marketing expenditures accounted for $187.3 million, driven by an aggressive customer acquisition strategy in North America and Europe.
General and administrative costs were $77.3 million, broadly in line with management's expectations for infrastructure scaling.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $152.5 million, representing an operating margin of 18.0%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $114.8 million, compared with $89.3 million in the prior fiscal year.
Diluted earnings per share came in at $2.31, versus $1.82 in fiscal 2022, marking a 26.9% improvement.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $293.6 million, up from $211.4 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company generated $198.7 million in cash from operating activities during the twelve-month period.
Capital expenditures totaled $43.2 million, primarily allocated to data center upgrades and proprietary hardware for edge computing deployments.
Free cash flow for fiscal 2023 was therefore approximately $155.5 million, a record high for the company.
Veltara's customer count grew to 4,820 at year-end, representing a net addition of 610 new clients during fiscal 2023.
Enterprise clients with annual contract values exceeding $500,000 increased to 312, up from 247 in the prior year.
The company's net revenue retention rate was 118%, indicating strong expansion within the existing customer base.
International revenues accounted for 31.4% of total revenues, with particularly strong growth in the EMEA region.
The EMEA segment grew 22.7% year-over-year, fueled by new partnerships with third-party logistics providers in Germany and the Netherlands.
Asia-Pacific revenues remained relatively modest at $48.2 million but showed a promising 16.4% growth trajectory.
Veltara appointed Chief Financial Officer Sandra Petrov in March 2023, who brought extensive experience from her prior role at a leading ERP software firm.
Under CFO Petrov's guidance, the company implemented a rigorous cost discipline framework that contributed to margin expansion in the second half of the year.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $100 million in August 2023, signaling confidence in the company's long-term prospects.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara had repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares at an average price of $41.50 per share under this program.
Total shares repurchased under the program amounted to roughly $49.8 million, leaving ample capacity remaining for continued buybacks.
The company's balance sheet remained robust, with total assets of $1.14 billion and total liabilities of $387.6 million at year-end.
Stockholders' equity stood at $752.4 million, reflecting accumulated retained earnings and additional paid-in capital.
Long-term deferred revenue increased to $164.3 million, consistent with the company's growing multi-year subscription contracts.
Veltara launched its next-generation platform, VeltaCore 4.0, in October 2023, which incorporated advanced machine learning capabilities for demand forecasting.
Early adoption of VeltaCore 4.0 was strong, with over 400 existing clients upgrading within the first quarter of availability.
Management noted that VeltaCore 4.0 contributed meaningfully to the uptick in net revenue retention during the fourth quarter.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 completely free of any long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet.
Veltara's headcount grew to 3,140 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, an increase of 380 employees compared with the prior year-end figure.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in the engineering and customer success functions, reflecting the company's product-led growth strategy.
Employee compensation and benefits represented the largest single component of operating expenses, totaling $218.9 million for the year.
The company maintained an employee satisfaction score of 82 out of 100 in its annual internal survey, slightly above the prior year's 79.
Veltara's stock price appreciated 34.7% over the course of fiscal 2023, outperforming the broader software sector index.
Institutional ownership of Veltara's common stock increased to 71.3% of shares outstanding as of the most recent proxy record date.
The audit committee engaged Ernst & Clarke LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023.
Ernst & Clarke issued an unqualified audit opinion on Veltara's consolidated financial statements, with no material weaknesses identified.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4%, reflecting utilization of research and development tax credits.
Management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $955 million to $975 million, implying approximately 13% year-over-year growth at the midpoint.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set between 18.5% and 19.5%, suggesting continued operational leverage.
CFO Sandra Petrov commented that the company expects to sustain free cash flow conversion above 80% of net income through fiscal 2024.
The company's largest product segment, Supply Chain Intelligence, generated $498.6 million in revenue, representing 58.9% of total revenues.
The Workforce Optimization segment contributed $193.5 million, while the Procurement Analytics module added $155.2 million.
Customer churn in the enterprise segment remained low at 4.2% on an annualized basis, well below the industry average of approximately 7%.
Veltara's partner ecosystem expanded to include 215 certified integration partners, up from 178 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company executed three technology bolt-on acquisitions during fiscal 2023, spending a combined $62.4 million to enhance its AI and data analytics capabilities.
These acquisitions added approximately 85 engineers and data scientists to the Veltara team, accelerating the VeltaCore 4.0 development timeline.
Risk factors disclosed in the annual report include competitive pressures from larger enterprise software vendors, potential macroeconomic headwinds, and cybersecurity vulnerabilities.
Management also acknowledged ongoing investment in compliance infrastructure to meet evolving data privacy regulations across international markets.
In a strategic financing move completed in November 2023, Veltara drew down a $175 million term loan facility to fund potential large-scale acquisitions in 2024.
The company declared no dividends during fiscal 2023, consistent with its stated policy of reinvesting cash flows to support organic growth initiatives.
Looking ahead, CEO Margaret Hollis expressed confidence that Veltara Systems is well-positioned to capture a growing share of the estimated $42 billion global supply chain software market.
Ardenvale Therapeutics, Inc. is a mid-cap biopharmaceutical company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, specializing in targeted oncology treatments and rare disease biologics.
The company was founded in 2009 by Dr. Miriam Holst and has since grown to become a recognized name in precision medicine.
Chief Executive Officer Jonathan Cray joined Ardenvale in 2017 after a distinguished career at two larger pharmaceutical firms.
Under his leadership, the company has expanded its pipeline from three candidates to eleven active programs across various stages of clinical development.
Chief Financial Officer Sandra Peele presented the following summary to the board of directors during the fourth-quarter earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Product revenue, which accounts for the largest share of total revenue, grew 16.8% year-over-year to $731.5 million.
Collaboration and licensing revenue contributed an additional $115.8 million, reflecting new partnership agreements executed during the second half of the year.
Gross margin for the period was 71.4%, a modest improvement from the 69.8% gross margin reported in fiscal year 2022.
Research and development expenses totaled $298.4 million, representing approximately 35.2% of total revenue, consistent with the company's commitment to pipeline investment.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses came in at $142.6 million, reflecting disciplined cost management across commercial and corporate functions.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $163.7 million, compared to $121.4 million in the prior year, a 34.8% improvement.
Net income attributable to Ardenvale shareholders was $139.2 million, or $2.14 per diluted share.
This compared favorably to net income of $104.9 million, or $1.62 per diluted share, reported in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year reached $224.5 million, reflecting a margin of approximately 26.5%.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 with cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments totaling $512.8 million.
Operating cash flow was a robust $198.3 million, driven largely by strong product sales and favorable working capital dynamics.
Capital expenditures for the year were $44.7 million, primarily related to manufacturing facility upgrades at the company's Durham and San Diego sites.
Ardenvale carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a fully unencumbered capital structure.
Headcount at year-end stood at 2,847 full-time employees, up from 2,614 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The growth in headcount was primarily driven by expansion in clinical operations and commercial infrastructure in Europe.
Ardenvale's lead product, Velatinib, generated $418.2 million in net product sales, making it the single largest revenue contributor in the portfolio.
Velatinib, approved by the FDA in 2020 for the treatment of ALK-positive non-small cell lung cancer, maintained a strong 23% market share in its approved indication.
The second product, Remocinex, contributed $183.7 million in net sales, representing 41% growth year-over-year following its label expansion approval in March 2023.
A third commercial product, Daltovir, generated $129.6 million in net sales, slightly below the internal forecast of $135 million due to payer access delays in certain geographies.
CFO Sandra Peele noted that inventory levels remained healthy and did not represent a material risk to near-term revenue recognition.
The company's Days Sales Outstanding was 48 days at year-end, an improvement from 54 days at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Ardenvale declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share in each of the four quarters of fiscal year 2023, totaling $0.88 per share for the year.
This dividend level represented a 10% increase from the $0.80 per share distributed in fiscal year 2022.
The board of directors has authorized a share repurchase program of up to $200 million, effective January 1, 2024.
During fiscal year 2023, the company repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares at an average price of $61.40 per share.
Total share-based compensation expense recognized during the year was $37.8 million, consistent with the prior year.
Ardenvale's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.4%, compared to 23.1% in the prior year.
The slight improvement in the tax rate was attributable to the utilization of certain R&D tax credits and favorable geographic income mix.
The company maintained an investment-grade credit rating of BBB from Standard & Poor's, reflecting its strong cash generation and stable balance sheet.
Ardenvale received FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation for its pipeline asset AVT-214 in August 2023, a significant regulatory milestone.
AVT-214 is a next-generation KRAS G12C inhibitor being evaluated in a Phase 2 trial for patients with colorectal cancer.
Enrollment in the Phase 2 trial for AVT-214 was completed ahead of schedule in November 2023, with topline data expected in the third quarter of 2024.

To accelerate the AVT-214 program and fund a planned $350 million acquisition of Corethian Biosciences, Ardenvale entered into a five-year $400 million term loan facility with a syndicate of lenders in December 2023.
The Corethian acquisition, announced December 18, 2023, is expected to bring two complementary rare disease assets into Ardenvale's pipeline.
Management believes the combined entity will generate meaningful synergies in both manufacturing and clinical development by fiscal year 2025.
CFO Peele emphasized that integration planning is well underway, with a dedicated transition management office established in January 2024.
International revenue, primarily from Europe and Japan, accounted for $218.4 million, or approximately 25.8% of total product revenue.
European sales grew 28.3% year-over-year, driven by new reimbursement approvals in Germany, France, and Italy for Velatinib.
Japan represented the fastest-growing individual market, with revenue increasing 39.1% following a successful partnership with Yamori Pharmaceuticals.
Ardenvale's royalty obligations to academic and third-party licensors totaled $41.3 million in fiscal year 2023.
Amortization of acquired intangible assets was $58.9 million for the year, reflecting prior acquisition activity.
The company expects total revenue for fiscal year 2024 to be in the range of $950 million to $990 million.
This guidance reflects anticipated contributions from Velatinib and Remocinex label expansions, partially offset by generic entry risk for Daltovir in the second half of 2024.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is expected to be between $240 million and $260 million.
The company does not expect to provide additional equity financing in 2024 given its current cash position and operating cash generation.
Ardenvale will host an Investor Day on May 7, 2024, during which management will provide detailed updates on pipeline strategy and long-term financial targets.
Certain statements in this report constitute forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially.
Investors are urged to review the risk factors disclosed in Ardenvale's most recent Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
The company remains committed to creating long-term value for its shareholders through disciplined capital allocation, pipeline execution, and commercial excellence.
Thornfield Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of industrial automation solutions, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, operates across three primary segments: robotics manufacturing, software integration, and field services.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the report with optimism, citing record-breaking operational achievements across all business units.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Esteves noted that the fiscal year represented a pivotal transformation period for the organization.
Thornfield Dynamics reported total consolidated revenue of $2.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year.
The robotics manufacturing segment alone contributed $1.47 billion in revenue, driven by strong demand from automotive and pharmaceutical clients.
Software integration revenues grew by 18.7% year-over-year, reaching $810 million, buoyed by new enterprise licensing agreements.
As of December 31, 2023, Thornfield Dynamics carried no outstanding long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, reflecting years of disciplined capital management.
The field services segment generated $560 million in revenue, completing a robust full-year contribution across all three divisions.
Gross profit for the consolidated entity reached $1.02 billion, yielding a gross margin of approximately 35.9%.
Operating expenses totaled $643 million, inclusive of research and development, selling, general and administrative costs.
Research and development expenditures amounted to $187 million, representing 6.6% of total revenue and underscoring the company's commitment to innovation.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at $412 million, compared to $351 million in the prior fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $298 million, yielding earnings per diluted share of $3.74.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share, consistent with the prior year's dividend policy.
Total assets on the balance sheet stood at $3.61 billion as of year-end, up from $3.24 billion at the close of fiscal 2022.
Cash and cash equivalents increased to $534 million, providing substantial liquidity for near-term strategic initiatives.
Accounts receivable grew modestly to $389 million, reflecting the higher revenue base and consistent collection performance.
Inventory levels were maintained at $276 million, down slightly from $291 million in the prior year, signaling improved supply chain efficiency.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $204 million, primarily directed toward expanding robotic assembly capacity in the Midwest.
The company repurchased 3.2 million shares during the fiscal year at an average price of $48.60 per share, totaling approximately $155 million.
Share repurchases reduced the weighted average diluted share count to approximately 79.7 million shares.
Margaret Holloway emphasized that the company's growth trajectory was supported by long-term contracts with blue-chip industrial clients.
New customer wins in the fiscal year numbered 214, spanning sectors including aerospace, food processing, and logistics.
The company's workforce expanded to 14,800 full-time employees globally, an increase of approximately 900 employees relative to the prior year.
Thornfield Dynamics maintained its investment-grade credit rating from both Moody's and S&P Global Ratings throughout the fiscal year.
The company's international revenues accounted for 31% of total revenue, with the strongest growth recorded in Germany and South Korea.
In September 2023, Thornfield Dynamics entered into a $450 million syndicated term loan facility arranged by a consortium of seven institutional lenders.
The software integration segment's backlog reached $1.1 billion at year-end, providing strong forward revenue visibility.
Raymond Esteves highlighted that free cash flow generation of $318 million exceeded internal targets by approximately 9%.
The company completed the acquisition of Larkfield Precision Systems in March 2023 for a total consideration of $172 million in cash.
Larkfield's technology portfolio is expected to accelerate Thornfield's entry into the semiconductor equipment market.
Integration costs associated with the Larkfield transaction were approximately $14 million, recorded as a one-time charge in operating expenses.
The robotics manufacturing segment's operating margin expanded by 140 basis points to reach 22.1% for the fiscal year.
Field services operating margin was 17.4%, reflecting both pricing discipline and favorable labor utilization rates.
Software integration operating margin was the highest among segments at 28.6%, consistent with the asset-light nature of the business.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.8%, slightly below the statutory federal rate due to favorable R&D tax credits.
Thornfield Dynamics recognized $43 million in non-cash stock-based compensation expense during the year.
Depreciation and amortization totaled $114 million, largely attributable to manufacturing equipment and acquired intangible assets.
The company's return on invested capital reached 14.2%, the highest level recorded in its nine-year public company history.
Pension and post-retirement benefit obligations totaled $88 million, down from $97 million the prior year due to favorable actuarial assumptions.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.4 and a quick ratio of 1.9, reflecting strong short-term financial health.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet grew to $134 million, reflecting the multi-year nature of many software subscription contracts.
Thornfield Dynamics disclosed no material litigation contingencies as of December 31, 2023, in notes to the financial statements.
The audit committee engaged Ernst & Park LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal 2023.
Ernst & Park LLP issued an unqualified audit opinion on both the financial statements and internal controls over financial reporting.
The company's sustainability report, published concurrently, documented a 9% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions.
Thornfield Dynamics set a target to achieve carbon neutrality in its manufacturing operations by the year 2035.
The board of directors is composed of ten members, seven of whom are independent, consistent with NASDAQ governance standards.
Compensation for Margaret Holloway in fiscal 2023 totaled $5.1 million, including base salary, annual bonus, and long-term equity awards.
Raymond Esteves received total compensation of $3.7 million, reflecting strong individual and company performance against established metrics.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $3.05 billion to $3.15 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $440 million to $460 million, implying continued margin expansion.
The company expects to invest between $220 million and $240 million in capital expenditures during fiscal 2024.
Management indicated that the Larkfield integration is expected to be fully complete by the third quarter of fiscal 2024.
Thornfield Dynamics anticipates that international revenues will represent approximately 35% of total revenue in fiscal 2024.
The company plans to open a new robotics assembly facility in Monterrey, Mexico, which is expected to be operational by mid-2025.
Raymond Esteves concluded that Thornfield Dynamics enters fiscal 2024 with a fortress balance sheet and exceptional operational momentum.
The full audited financial statements, including footnotes and supplemental schedules, are available in the company's Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Thornfield Dynamics' annual report is dedicated to its employees, customers, and shareholders who have supported the company's mission of advancing intelligent automation worldwide.
Veltara Biosystems, Inc. hereby presents its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Veltara Biosystems operates in the biotechnology sector, specializing in next-generation protein synthesis platforms and targeted therapeutic delivery systems.
The company was founded in 2011 and is headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Ashford led the organization through a transformative year marked by both clinical milestones and financial discipline.
Chief Financial Officer Thomas Wrenley oversaw a comprehensive review of capital allocation priorities during the fourth quarter.
Total net revenue for fiscal 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year's $426.7 million.
Product revenue, which accounts for the majority of total revenue, grew 16.8% year-over-year to $391.5 million.
Collaborative licensing and royalty agreements contributed an additional $95.8 million to total revenue.
Gross profit for the period was $302.1 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 62.0%.
Operating expenses, including research and development and selling, general, and administrative costs, totaled $228.4 million.
Research and development expenditures alone amounted to $154.7 million, reflecting continued investment in the company's late-stage pipeline.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $73.7 million, a modest increase of 5.1% compared to fiscal 2022.
Operating income for the year was $73.7 million, up from $51.2 million in the prior fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $58.9 million, or $1.24 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure, came in at $101.4 million, reflecting strong underlying operational performance.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $213.6 million, compared to $178.2 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company generated $89.4 million in operating cash flow during fiscal 2023.
Capital expenditures totaled $34.1 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $55.3 million for the year.
Veltara Biosystems maintained a strong balance sheet throughout the fiscal year, with total assets of $924.7 million.
Total liabilities at year-end were $389.2 million, and stockholders' equity stood at $535.5 million.
The current ratio as of December 31, 2023, was 3.4, indicating a robust short-term liquidity position.
Veltara Biosystems concluded fiscal 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final tranche of senior notes in March of that year.
Inventory levels at year-end were $67.3 million, reflecting careful demand planning aligned with anticipated commercial launch activities.
Accounts receivable totaled $82.6 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 62 days.
The company's lead therapeutic candidate, VLT-204, received Breakthrough Therapy designation from the FDA in February 2023.
Phase III clinical trial data for VLT-204 demonstrated statistically significant efficacy improvements compared to the standard of care.
Enrollment in the VLT-204 pivotal trial was completed ahead of schedule in October 2023, with 1,847 patients enrolled across 94 global sites.
Dr. Ashford stated that VLT-204 represents the most promising asset in the company's history and could redefine treatment paradigms for its target indication.
Veltara Biosystems also advanced VLT-309, a second pipeline candidate, into Phase II trials during the third quarter of 2023.
VLT-309 targets a rare metabolic disorder with an estimated global prevalence of approximately 280,000 patients.
The company received $22.0 million in milestone payments from its collaboration partner, Helix Pharma AG, upon initiation of the VLT-309 Phase II study.
Veltara Biosystems employed 1,412 full-time equivalent staff as of December 31, 2023, up from 1,298 at the end of the prior year.
The increase in headcount was primarily driven by hiring in clinical operations, regulatory affairs, and commercial readiness functions.
Employee-related expenses, including salaries, benefits, and stock-based compensation, totaled $168.3 million for fiscal 2023.
Stock-based compensation expense included in operating expenses was $24.6 million for the year.
The board of directors declared a special cash dividend of $0.50 per share in November 2023, payable to shareholders of record as of December 15, 2023.
This marked the first time in the company's history that a dividend was distributed to common shareholders.
Veltara Biosystems repurchased 1.2 million shares of its common stock during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $38.74 per share.
The total cost of share repurchases was approximately $46.5 million, funded from existing cash reserves.
The company's stock price appreciated 31.7% over the course of fiscal 2023, outperforming the Nasdaq Biotechnology Index.
Geographic expansion remained a strategic priority, with Veltara Biosystems establishing a European commercial subsidiary in Amsterdam in mid-2023.
In September 2023, the company entered into a $175 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five institutional lenders to support its international growth initiatives.
The Amsterdam subsidiary is expected to serve as the primary hub for regulatory submissions and commercial operations across the European Union.
Management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $540 million to $565 million, implying year-over-year growth of approximately 11% to 16%.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is projected to be between $115 million and $125 million.
Dr. Ashford emphasized that the company's commercial infrastructure is well-positioned to support a potential product launch in the second half of 2024.
Thomas Wrenley noted that disciplined cost management will remain a cornerstone of Veltara's financial strategy going into the new fiscal year.
The company anticipates filing a Biologics License Application for VLT-204 with the FDA in the first quarter of 2024.
Regulatory submissions in the European Union and Japan are expected to follow in the second and third quarters of 2024, respectively.
Veltara Biosystems has secured manufacturing commitments from two contract development and manufacturing organizations to ensure supply readiness.
The audit committee of the board of directors confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP served as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal 2023.
PricewaterhouseCoopers issued an unqualified audit opinion on Veltara Biosystems' consolidated financial statements.
The company does not anticipate any material changes to its accounting policies in fiscal 2024.
Veltara Biosystems remains committed to transparent financial reporting and rigorous internal controls in accordance with Sarbanes-Oxley requirements.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives were expanded in 2023, including a commitment to achieve carbon neutrality across all domestic operations by 2030.
The company published its inaugural ESG report in June 2023, which received recognition from several independent sustainability rating organizations.
Looking ahead, Veltara Biosystems is confident in its strategy, its pipeline, and its team's ability to deliver long-term value for all stakeholders.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a leading manufacturer of advanced thermal management systems, published its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, serves clients across the aerospace, defense, and industrial cooling sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the report with a statement emphasizing the organization's resilience amid global supply chain headwinds.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Albrecht reported total revenue of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
Gross profit reached $312.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 36.9%, which exceeded internal targets by roughly 200 basis points.
Operating income for the period came in at $104.8 million, compared with $88.2 million in fiscal year 2022.
The company's EBITDA stood at $138.5 million, reflecting disciplined cost management and operational efficiency improvements.
Veltrano Dynamics closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final outstanding bond tranche in March of that year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $79.4 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.12.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable beginning in the first quarter of fiscal 2024.
Capital expenditures totaled $56.7 million during 2023, reflecting continued investment in next-generation manufacturing infrastructure.
The Raleigh flagship facility underwent a $22 million expansion project that added approximately 140,000 square feet of production capacity.
President of Operations Thomas Vega noted that on-time delivery rates improved to 94.3%, up from 89.1% in the previous fiscal year.
Headcount across all global facilities grew to 4,812 full-time employees by year-end, an increase of 317 from the prior period.
Veltrano Dynamics opened a new regional distribution center in Dresden, Germany, to better serve European aerospace clients.
The aerospace segment generated $389.4 million in revenue, accounting for approximately 46% of total consolidated revenue.
Defense sector sales reached $271.8 million, supported by several multi-year government supply agreements renewed in the second quarter.
Industrial cooling products contributed $186.1 million in revenue, growing 18.7% year-over-year driven by data center expansion contracts.
Research and development expenditures rose to $48.3 million, representing approximately 5.7% of total revenue.
The company received three new patents during 2023 related to microchannel heat exchanger technology and two-phase cooling systems.
Chief Technology Officer Linda Osei announced the successful qualification of the VD-900 thermal module for use in next-generation satellite platforms.
Customer concentration risk remained moderate, with the top five clients representing 34.2% of total revenue.
Accounts receivable days outstanding decreased to 41 days from 47 days in the prior year, reflecting improved collections processes.
Inventory levels were actively managed throughout 2023, with inventory turnover improving to 6.8 times from 5.9 times in 2022.
The company's cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $163.7 million, providing substantial liquidity for near-term operational needs.
A $30 million share repurchase program was authorized by the board in October 2023, with $12.4 million executed by December 31.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were held to $159.4 million, representing 18.8% of revenue compared with 20.1% in the prior year.
In November 2023, Veltrano Dynamics entered into a $175 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of five banking institutions to support strategic growth initiatives.
The weighted average cost of capital was estimated at 8.3%, used as the primary discount rate in goodwill impairment testing.
No goodwill impairment charges were recorded during fiscal year 2023, as all reporting units tested above their carrying values.
The company completed the acquisition of Solaris Thermal Solutions, a privately held firm specializing in cryogenic cooling, for $94 million in April 2023.
Solaris contributed approximately $18.7 million in revenue and $3.2 million in operating income during the period from acquisition to fiscal year-end.
Integration costs related to the Solaris transaction amounted to $4.1 million, recorded as a non-recurring operating expense.
Foreign currency translation adjustments resulted in a $6.3 million reduction in comprehensive income, reflecting U.S. dollar strengthening against the euro.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.7%, modestly higher than the prior year's rate of 21.4% due to geographic earnings mix.
Deferred tax liabilities increased by $8.9 million, primarily driven by accelerated depreciation on newly acquired manufacturing equipment.
The pension obligation for the North American defined benefit plan stood at $211.4 million, partially offset by plan assets of $189.6 million.
Actuarial losses recognized in other comprehensive income totaled $5.8 million for the year, net of tax effects.
Raymond Albrecht reaffirmed fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $950 million.
Operating margin guidance for 2024 was set between 12.5% and 13.5%, reflecting expectations for continued operational leverage.
The company anticipates capital expenditure requirements of approximately $65 million in fiscal 2024, primarily for automation upgrades.
Veltrano Dynamics signed a letter of intent to establish a joint venture with Kyoto-based Renkai Industries targeting the Japanese semiconductor cooling market.
The joint venture, subject to regulatory approvals, is expected to become operational by the third quarter of 2024.
Margaret Holloway highlighted the long-term growth opportunity presented by artificial intelligence data center proliferation as a key demand driver.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the criteria established by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations.
The company's stock price appreciated 28.4% over the course of fiscal year 2023, outperforming the S&P 500 Industrials Index by approximately 14 percentage points.
Average daily trading volume in the company's shares rose to approximately 412,000 shares during 2023, compared with 274,000 shares in 2022.
Veltrano Dynamics maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's, with a stable outlook affirmed in September 2023.
The company did not experience any material litigation events during fiscal 2023 that would require disclosure as a contingent liability.
Environmental compliance costs totaled $3.4 million, consistent with prior-year levels and within budgeted parameters.
The sustainability committee reported a 9.1% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to the 2021 baseline.
Renewable energy now accounts for 31% of total electricity consumption across Veltrano Dynamics' global manufacturing footprint.
Employee voluntary turnover declined to 8.3% in 2023 from 11.7% in 2022, attributed to expanded compensation and development programs.
Thomas Vega credited the implementation of a new enterprise resource planning system with delivering approximately $7.2 million in annualized efficiency savings.
The ERP rollout was completed on schedule in July 2023, covering all major domestic facilities without significant operational disruption.
Backlog as of December 31, 2023, stood at $1.04 billion, representing approximately 14 months of forward revenue coverage at current run rates.
New orders booked during fiscal 2023 totaled $912.6 million, a book-to-bill ratio of approximately 1.08 times.
The company declared its intent to pursue additional bolt-on acquisitions in the thermal interface materials and advanced fluid management subsegments.
Linda Osei confirmed that two new product platforms will enter customer qualification testing in the first half of 2024.
Raymond Albrecht concluded by stating that Veltrano Dynamics enters fiscal year 2024 with strong momentum, a robust balance sheet, and clear strategic priorities.
The full consolidated financial statements, including footnotes and supplemental schedules, are available in the accompanying Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Meridian Coldchain Logistics, Inc. hereby presents its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates as a leading provider of temperature-controlled warehousing and distribution services across North America.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Delworth opened the report with a statement emphasizing the organization's commitment to sustainable growth.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei noted that fiscal 2023 represented a milestone year in the company's eighteen-year history.
Meridian Coldchain serves clients across the pharmaceutical, fresh produce, and specialty food sectors.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal 2023 reached $847.4 million, representing a 12.3 percent increase over the prior year.
Operating income grew to $103.6 million, compared with $89.2 million recorded in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $71.8 million, or $2.34 per diluted share.
The company's gross margin expanded by 80 basis points to 24.7 percent, driven by improved warehouse utilization rates.
Meridian Coldchain closed fiscal 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired the final tranche of its 2019 credit facility in March of that year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $158.2 million, providing substantial liquidity for near-term capital requirements.
Capital expenditures totaled $94.1 million, reflecting continued investment in automated refrigeration technology and facility expansions.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, marking the fifth consecutive year of dividend increases.
Meridian Coldchain's workforce grew to approximately 6,200 full-time employees by the end of the fiscal year.
The company opened three new distribution centers during 2023, located in Atlanta, Denver, and Toronto.
Each facility incorporates next-generation ammonia-based refrigeration systems designed to reduce energy consumption by up to 30 percent.
The Atlanta facility alone adds 420,000 square feet of temperature-controlled storage to the company's portfolio.
Ms. Delworth highlighted that the Toronto location marks Meridian Coldchain's formal entry into the Canadian market.
International revenue, including Canadian operations, contributed $41.3 million to total revenue in fiscal 2023.
The pharmaceutical segment remained the highest-margin business line, generating segment operating income of $48.7 million.
Fresh produce logistics revenue reached $312.6 million, accounting for approximately 37 percent of consolidated revenue.
Specialty food distribution contributed $193.5 million in revenue, growing 18.4 percent year-over-year.
Mr. Osei highlighted that fuel and labor costs collectively represented 61 percent of total operating expenses during the year.
The company implemented a dynamic routing software upgrade in Q2 2023 that reduced average fleet mileage by 7.2 percent.
Meridian Coldchain's fleet consists of 1,840 refrigerated transport units as of December 31, 2023.
The company renewed long-term service agreements with four of its top-ten clients during the fiscal year.
Client retention rate improved to 94.6 percent, the highest level recorded since the company's initial public offering in 2011.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved from 38 days to 33 days, reflecting tighter credit management policies.
Inventory levels within the company's managed cold storage operations increased modestly by 3.8 percent to support seasonal demand.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Meridian Coldchain finalized a $175 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of five institutional lenders.
The new credit arrangement carries a variable interest rate based on SOFR plus 145 basis points.
Management stated that the credit facility would support strategic acquisitions and opportunistic capital deployment over the next 24 months.
Free cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $64.3 million after accounting for all capital expenditure and working capital movements.
The company repurchased 1.1 million shares of common stock during the year at an average price of $38.72 per share.
Total share repurchases amounted to approximately $42.6 million, funded entirely from operating cash flows.
Meridian Coldchain's return on invested capital improved to 11.4 percent from 10.1 percent in the prior fiscal year.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.8 percent, slightly below the company's long-term guidance range of 23 to 25 percent.
Deferred tax liabilities increased to $29.4 million, primarily due to accelerated depreciation on newly commissioned facilities.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers served as the company's independent external auditor for fiscal 2023.
An unqualified audit opinion was issued with no material weaknesses identified in the company's internal control environment.
Environmental, Social, and Governance disclosures were expanded this year in accordance with the GRI Standards framework.
Scope 1 greenhouse gas emissions declined by 9.1 percent relative to the 2022 baseline, exceeding the company's annual reduction target.
Scope 2 emissions fell by 14.3 percent following the transition to renewable electricity contracts at seven major facilities.
Water consumption intensity per square foot of managed warehouse space decreased by 6.7 percent during the reporting period.
