The company contributed $3.2 million to community food-security programs in the markets where it operates.
Ms. Delworth emphasized that environmental stewardship remains integral to Meridian Coldchain's long-term operational strategy.
The board comprises nine directors, five of whom are classified as independent under applicable stock exchange listing rules.
Lead independent director Constance Mwangi chairs the audit and risk oversight committee.
Executive compensation for fiscal 2023 included performance share units tied to three-year total shareholder return versus a peer group.
The proxy advisory firm ISS recommended shareholder support for all twelve proposals presented at the 2023 annual general meeting.
Meridian Coldchain's stock price appreciated 21.4 percent during fiscal 2023, outperforming the S&P Midcap 400 Index by 8.9 percentage points.
Looking forward, management issued revenue guidance of $910 million to $935 million for fiscal 2024.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set in the range of $145 million to $155 million.
The company expects capital expenditures to remain elevated at approximately $105 million in fiscal 2024 due to two additional facility projects.
Expansion plans include a 380,000-square-foot facility in Memphis and a smaller satellite operation in Vancouver.
Mr. Osei confirmed that the Memphis facility is expected to reach full operational capacity by the third quarter of 2024.
Management also disclosed a strategic review of potential bolt-on acquisitions in the southeastern United States.
Any acquisition target would be evaluated against strict criteria including EBITDA margins exceeding 18 percent and positive free cash flow.
The company does not anticipate any material legal proceedings that would adversely affect its financial position.
Meridian Coldchain maintains product liability and property insurance coverage that management considers adequate for current exposure levels.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially.
Key risk factors include regulatory changes, commodity price volatility, labor availability, and macroeconomic conditions.
Shareholders are encouraged to read the full Risk Factors section included in the company's Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
The board of directors and executive leadership team of Meridian Coldchain, Inc. thank all stakeholders for their continued confidence and support.
Meridian Opto-Technologies Inc. is a mid-cap manufacturer specializing in precision optical components and laser systems for industrial and medical applications.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, was founded in 2004 and has since grown to serve clients across North America, Europe, and Southeast Asia.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Vanessa Hartwell presented the annual report to shareholders at the company's general meeting held on March 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a period of both significant opportunity and measured caution for the organization.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing a 9.2% increase over the prior year's $446.2 million.
The Medical Devices segment contributed $198.6 million to total revenue, accounting for approximately 40.8% of the company's overall top line.
The Industrial Laser Systems segment generated $179.4 million, reflecting robust demand from automotive and semiconductor manufacturing customers.
The remaining $109.3 million in revenue came from the Precision Optics segment, which services defense contractors and research institutions.
Gross profit for the year was $201.8 million, yielding a gross margin of 41.4%, an improvement of 130 basis points versus fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $134.7 million, driven primarily by increased investment in research and development.
Research and development expenditure rose to $48.2 million in 2023, up from $39.5 million the prior year, as the company accelerated its photonic integration roadmap.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $86.5 million, reflecting higher headcount in the North American commercial organization.
Operating income for the period was $67.1 million, translating to an operating margin of 13.8%, compared to 12.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at $89.6 million, representing an EBITDA margin of 18.4%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $44.3 million, or $1.87 per diluted share, compared to $38.1 million, or $1.62 per diluted share, in 2022.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 24.7%, consistent with the company's long-term guidance range of 23% to 26%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $112.6 million, up from $94.8 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Operating cash flow was strong at $78.4 million, supported by improved working capital management and faster accounts receivable collection.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $31.2 million, directed primarily toward expanding the Austin manufacturing facility and upgrading laser testing equipment.
The company repurchased 1.4 million shares during the year at an average price of $28.60, returning approximately $40.0 million to shareholders.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share, maintaining the annualized dividend rate of $0.48 per share established in 2021.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian Opto-Technologies carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, reflecting a deliberate strategy of organic, self-funded growth.
International revenues grew 14.3% year-over-year, with Europe contributing $93.4 million and Southeast Asia contributing $61.7 million to the consolidated total.
Dr. Hartwell emphasized that the company's expansion into the South Korean and Taiwanese markets was a strategic priority for fiscal year 2024.
Headcount at year-end was 2,847 full-time employees, an increase of 213 from the 2,634 employees reported at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company launched three new products in 2023: the MeriBeam X7 surgical laser, the OptyCore 900 industrial alignment system, and the LumiSense Pro diagnostic module.
The MeriBeam X7 achieved FDA 510(k) clearance in April 2023 and generated $12.4 million in revenue in its first nine months of commercial availability.
Customer satisfaction scores, as measured by the company's annual Net Promoter Survey, improved to 71 from 64 in the prior year.
The company renewed long-term supply agreements with three of its top five customers, collectively representing approximately $140 million in contracted future revenue.
Inventory levels at year-end were $67.3 million, representing approximately 50 days of inventory on hand, down from 58 days in fiscal year 2022.
Accounts receivable stood at $82.1 million at December 31, 2023, with a days-sales-outstanding metric of 61 days, improved from 67 days the prior year.
The allowance for doubtful accounts was $2.3 million, representing 2.8% of gross receivables, which management considers prudent given the current macroeconomic environment.
Goodwill on the balance sheet totaled $54.8 million, unchanged from fiscal year 2022, as the company made no acquisitions during the period.
Intangible assets, net of accumulated amortization, were $18.9 million, consisting primarily of customer relationships and developed technology from the 2019 acquisition of Prism Path LLC.
Total assets at year-end were $498.2 million, compared to $463.7 million at the end of fiscal year 2022, reflecting organic growth in working capital and fixed assets.
Shareholders' equity increased to $321.4 million from $298.6 million, driven by net income partially offset by share repurchases and dividends paid.
The company's return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was 14.3%, up from 13.5% in the prior year, reflecting improving profitability.
Return on invested capital was 11.8%, which management views as a key indicator of the company's ability to generate value above its cost of capital.
Mr. Osei highlighted that disciplined capital allocation remains the cornerstone of the company's financial strategy heading into fiscal year 2024.
The company's backlog at year-end reached a record $223.7 million, providing strong revenue visibility for the first half of fiscal year 2024.
Segment operating margins improved across all three business units, with Medical Devices leading at 17.9%, Industrial Laser Systems at 14.2%, and Precision Optics at 10.8%.
In February 2024, the company finalized a $175 million senior secured revolving credit facility with a syndicate of four major commercial banks, replacing previous arrangements.
Dr. Hartwell noted that this new credit facility provides enhanced financial flexibility to pursue strategic acquisitions and fund accelerated capital investment programs.
The company's forward guidance for fiscal year 2024 calls for revenue in the range of $525 million to $545 million, implying growth of approximately 7.7% to 11.8%.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is projected to be between $97 million and $103 million, representing a meaningful expansion in profitability.
The Medical Devices segment is expected to be the primary growth driver in 2024, benefiting from increased hospital capital spending and expanded regulatory approvals in Europe.
The Industrial Laser Systems segment faces headwinds from softening automotive production schedules in Germany and ongoing inventory corrections in the semiconductor supply chain.
Management expects research and development spending to increase to approximately $54 million in fiscal year 2024, continuing the multi-year investment in photonic integration.
The company's environmental, social, and governance program achieved a 12% reduction in carbon emissions intensity per unit of production compared to fiscal year 2022.
Meridian Opto-Technologies was recognized by the Austin Business Journal as one of the region's top employers for the third consecutive year.
The board of directors approved a new $60 million share repurchase authorization in January 2024, supplementing the approximately $18 million remaining under the prior program.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Mikkelson reported that on-time delivery performance improved to 94.3% in 2023, up from 89.7% in 2022, following investments in supply chain technology.
The company experienced no material cybersecurity incidents during fiscal year 2023 and completed a comprehensive third-party security audit in Q4.
Legal contingencies as of December 31, 2023 included one patent infringement claim filed by a competitor, which the company's counsel believes is without merit.
Pension and post-retirement benefit obligations were $14.2 million at year-end, reflecting a modest defined benefit plan covering legacy employees hired before 2010.
The company maintained property and casualty insurance coverage deemed adequate by management, with no material uninsured losses incurred during the year.
Deferred revenue at year-end was $9.7 million, primarily related to multi-year service contracts in the Medical Devices segment.
Mr. Osei expressed confidence that the company's strong cash generation, disciplined cost management, and expanding product portfolio position it well for sustained long-term growth.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP served as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023.
PricewaterhouseCoopers issued an unqualified opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements, with no material weaknesses in internal controls identified.
Meridian Opto-Technologies remains committed to delivering superior value to its customers, employees, and shareholders as it enters what management believes will be a transformative year ahead.
Veltronix Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced industrial automation solutions, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany, operates across twelve countries and serves clients in the automotive, aerospace, and heavy manufacturing sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Marlene Köhler expressed confidence in the company's trajectory, citing sustained demand for robotic assembly systems across European and North American markets.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached €4.87 billion, representing a 9.3% increase compared to the prior year's €4.46 billion.
Gross profit margin expanded by 140 basis points to 38.6%, driven by favorable product mix and operational efficiencies in the company's core manufacturing segment.
Operating income rose to €612 million, up from €541 million in fiscal year 2022, reflecting disciplined cost management across all business units.
The company reported net income of €389 million, compared to €327 million in the prior year, translating to earnings per share of €4.12.
Chief Financial Officer Benedikt Strauss highlighted the robust cash generation during the year, noting that free cash flow reached €478 million.
Capital expenditures totaled €198 million, concentrated primarily in the expansion of the company's precision robotics manufacturing facility in Leipzig.
Research and development expenditure increased to €341 million, representing 7.0% of total revenue, reflecting the company's commitment to next-generation automation platforms.
The Automation Systems segment contributed €2.31 billion in revenue, growing 11.2% year-over-year and accounting for 47.4% of total company revenue.
The Industrial Software segment generated €1.29 billion in revenue, reflecting a 10.8% increase, as clients accelerated adoption of the company's digital twin and process orchestration platforms.
The Legacy Services segment delivered €1.27 billion in revenue, broadly flat compared to fiscal year 2022, as planned wind-downs in older hardware maintenance contracts offset new service agreements.
Geographically, Europe remained the largest market, contributing 52% of total revenue, followed by North America at 31% and Asia-Pacific at 17%.
Headcount across all global operations stood at 23,400 full-time employees at year-end, up from 21,800 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company completed two strategic acquisitions during the year, including the purchase of Renvik Precision AG for €215 million in March 2023.
The second acquisition, MoboTech Systems GmbH, was completed in September 2023 for a consideration of €178 million, adding sensor fusion capabilities to Veltronix's product portfolio.
Integration costs associated with both acquisitions totaled €43 million and were recorded as one-time charges in the second and third quarters.
The company's order backlog reached a record €6.2 billion at year-end, representing approximately 15 months of forward revenue coverage.
Book-to-bill ratio for fiscal year 2023 was 1.18, indicating continued momentum in new contract signings relative to revenue recognized.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Sundaram outlined several operational improvements implemented during the year, including a lean manufacturing overhaul at the Hannover facility.
Supply chain resilience initiatives resulted in a 16% reduction in average component lead times, which management attributed to nearshoring of key subassembly work.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at €634 million, down from €701 million the prior year, reflecting improved inventory turnover ratios across all segments.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 14.7% in fiscal year 2023, up from 12.9% in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA reached €821 million, implying an adjusted EBITDA margin of 16.9%, compared to 15.8% in the prior year.
The board of directors approved a dividend of €1.85 per share for fiscal year 2023, a 12.1% increase over the prior year's dividend of €1.65 per share.
The total dividend payout is expected to represent approximately €175 million in aggregate distributions to shareholders.
Share repurchases during fiscal year 2023 totaled €120 million, reducing the diluted share count to approximately 94.4 million shares.
The company's balance sheet remained in a strong position, with total assets of €7.34 billion and total equity of €3.81 billion at year-end.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023 amounted to €892 million, providing substantial liquidity for operational and strategic needs.
During the year, Veltronix successfully retired its €300 million senior unsecured notes that matured in June 2023 using existing cash resources.
As of December 31, 2023, the company carried no outstanding long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, reflecting a deliberate deleveraging strategy pursued since 2020.
The net debt position at year-end was negative, meaning the company held net cash of approximately €892 million relative to its financial liabilities.
Credit rating agency Meridian Ratings affirmed Veltronix's investment-grade rating of A- in November 2023, citing strong free cash flow generation and conservative financial policies.
The company's weighted average cost of capital was estimated at 7.4% for fiscal year 2023, consistent with the prior year estimate.
Management indicated that maintaining financial flexibility remained a core principle, with no significant leverage transactions contemplated in the near term.
Veltronix's long-term debt position at the close of fiscal year 2023 was confirmed to be zero, with the balance sheet entirely free of interest-bearing borrowings.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for total revenue in the range of €5.1 billion to €5.3 billion, implying growth of approximately 5% to 9%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 17.0% to 17.5%, reflecting expectations of continued operating leverage.
The company announced plans to launch three new product lines in the first half of 2024, including the Veltrix-9 autonomous welding platform.
The second planned launch is the OmniSense 4.0 industrial vision system, which completed field trials with two major automotive clients in the fourth quarter of 2023.
The third planned product introduction is the FlexCore modular conveyor system, designed for rapid deployment in high-mix, low-volume manufacturing environments.
Management expects these three product launches to collectively contribute between €180 million and €220 million in incremental revenue during fiscal year 2024.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at €210 million to €230 million, primarily directed toward capacity expansion and automation of internal production lines.
The company committed to maintaining its research and development investment at approximately 7% of revenue through the medium term.
Veltronix also announced an updated sustainability roadmap targeting a 40% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 2030 relative to a 2020 baseline.
The company reported a 14% reduction in carbon intensity per unit of output in fiscal year 2023, ahead of its interim sustainability targets.
Chief Executive Officer Köhler stated that talent acquisition and retention remained a strategic priority, with approximately 1,800 net new hires planned for fiscal year 2024.
The company's voluntary attrition rate declined to 6.2% in fiscal year 2023, down from 8.4% in the prior year, signaling improved employee engagement.
Veltronix launched a global employee equity participation program in January 2023, under which approximately 3,200 employees received restricted stock unit grants.
The equity program is expected to result in stock-based compensation expense of approximately €28 million annually over a four-year vesting schedule.
The company's audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal control over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year 2023 audit.
External auditor Brandt & Heilmann LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2023.
Board Chairman Friedrich Obermair noted that governance practices were further strengthened during the year, with two new independent directors added to the board.
The company faces ongoing litigation related to a patent dispute with Ferratec Industrial Solutions, with proceedings expected to continue through 2025.
Management has accrued €35 million as a contingent liability related to this litigation, which it believes represents a reasonable estimate of the probable exposure.

Veltronix entered into a €450 million syndicated revolving credit facility in October 2023, arranged by a consortium of five major European banks, to support general corporate and strategic financing needs.
The facility carries an interest rate of EURIBOR plus 85 basis points and has a five-year term with two one-year extension options.
Overall, Veltronix Dynamics Corporation concluded fiscal year 2023 with strong financial performance, a record backlog, and a clear strategic roadmap that positions the business for continued growth.
Meridian Cascade Technologies, Inc. reported its full-year financial results for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, demonstrating resilience across its core business segments.
The company, headquartered in Portland, Oregon, operates primarily in the industrial automation and precision robotics sector.
Chief Executive Officer Vanessa Hartwell opened the earnings call by expressing confidence in the company's long-term strategic positioning.
Meridian Cascade Technologies recorded total revenue of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.4% increase over the prior year.
The company's flagship product line, the OmegaDrive Series, continued to outperform market expectations throughout the year.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delroy noted that gross margins expanded by 120 basis points to reach 41.7% in fiscal year 2023.
Operating expenses totaled $214.6 million, reflecting disciplined cost management across all departments.
Research and development spending increased to $98.2 million, underscoring the company's commitment to innovation.
The North American segment contributed $412.1 million in revenue, making it the largest geographic contributor to total sales.
European operations generated $231.7 million in revenue, aided by favorable currency tailwinds and strong demand from automotive clients.
Asia-Pacific revenues reached $203.5 million, driven by expanding partnerships with electronics manufacturers in South Korea and Taiwan.
Meridian Cascade Technologies ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired all outstanding obligations earlier in the year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $103.4 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.17.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, payable in March 2024.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $57.8 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity in Dallas, Texas.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $134.2 million, down from $149.6 million in the prior fiscal year.
The reduction in inventory was attributed to improved demand forecasting and supply chain optimization initiatives.
Headcount across all global locations reached 6,847 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023.
The company added 412 net new employees during the year, reflecting growth in engineering and customer support roles.
Vanessa Hartwell emphasized that the workforce expansion was essential to sustaining the product development pipeline.
The OmegaDrive Pro, launched in the second quarter of 2023, exceeded initial sales projections by 23% within its first six months.
Customer retention rates improved to 91.3%, up from 88.6% in fiscal year 2022, reflecting strong satisfaction with service quality.
Meridian Cascade Technologies expanded its distribution network by entering into agreements with seven new regional partners across Latin America.
The company's backlog of unfulfilled orders reached $389.5 million at year-end, a 17.2% increase compared to the prior year.
Marcus Delroy highlighted that the strong backlog provided excellent visibility into first-half 2024 revenues.
The adjusted EBITDA margin for fiscal year 2023 was 22.4%, compared to 21.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $178.6 million, enabling the company to fund dividends and internal growth initiatives.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of its common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $44.30 per share.
Share repurchases totaling approximately $53.2 million reduced the diluted share count and enhanced per-share metrics.
Return on invested capital improved to 14.8%, reflecting effective deployment of the company's asset base.
The audit committee confirmed that internal controls over financial reporting were found to be effective with no material weaknesses identified.
To fund its planned acquisition of Vanterra Robotics Systems, Meridian Cascade Technologies entered into a $320 million senior secured term loan facility in November 2023.
The Vanterra Robotics Systems acquisition was expected to close in the first quarter of 2024, pending regulatory approvals.
Synergies from the acquisition were projected to generate $40 million in annual cost savings within 36 months of closing.
The company's credit rating was reaffirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Partners following the announcement of the acquisition financing.
Vanessa Hartwell stated that the Vanterra transaction represented a transformational step in expanding the company's addressable market.
The combined entity would serve over 3,200 customers across 48 countries upon completion of the acquisition.
Meridian Cascade Technologies also announced a strategic partnership with NovaTech Sensors to co-develop next-generation predictive maintenance solutions.
The NovaTech partnership was expected to generate incremental revenues of approximately $25 million beginning in fiscal year 2025.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 totaled $353.3 million, consistent with the reported gross margin expansion.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $116.4 million, representing 13.7% of total revenue.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, benefiting from R&D tax credits and favorable jurisdictional mix.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023 stood at $221.9 million, providing ample liquidity for near-term operations.
The company maintained a revolving credit facility of $150 million, of which none was drawn at year-end.
Management provided guidance for fiscal year 2024, projecting total revenue in the range of $940 million to $970 million.
The 2024 revenue guidance assumed organic growth of approximately 8% to 11%, excluding contributions from the Vanterra acquisition.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 was projected to be between $215 million and $230 million.
Management cautioned that macroeconomic uncertainty and potential supply chain disruptions remained key risks to the 2024 outlook.
The company planned to increase its dividend by approximately 10% annually over the next three years, subject to board approval.
Meridian Cascade Technologies reaffirmed its long-term targets of achieving $1.2 billion in revenue and 25% EBITDA margins by fiscal year 2026.
The investor relations team announced that the annual shareholder meeting would be held on May 14, 2024, in Portland, Oregon.
Marcus Delroy expressed confidence that the company's balance sheet strength would support both organic investment and strategic acquisitions.
The company received the Precision Manufacturing Excellence Award from the North American Automation Council for the second consecutive year.
Total assets at December 31, 2023 amounted to $1.14 billion, reflecting the company's growing operational footprint.
Intangible assets totaling $187.3 million were carried on the balance sheet, primarily related to prior acquisitions and intellectual property.
Vanessa Hartwell concluded her remarks by thanking employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued trust in Meridian Cascade Technologies.
The company remains well-positioned to capitalize on the secular growth trends in industrial automation and intelligent manufacturing systems.
Meridian Optical Technologies, Inc. reported its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023, marking another period of operational expansion.
The company, headquartered in Portland, Oregon, designs and manufactures precision optical components for medical imaging and defense applications.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Sandra Kroft opened the earnings call by highlighting the firm's sustained momentum across all major business segments.
Meridian's total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the prior year.
The medical imaging division contributed $512 million to total revenues, reinforcing its position as the company's largest operating segment.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delgado noted that gross margins expanded by 180 basis points year-over-year, reaching 41.3% for the full year.
Operating expenses were well-managed at $198 million, reflecting disciplined cost controls instituted in the second half of the year.
Meridian ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final outstanding bond obligations in March of that year.
The defense optics segment generated $228 million in revenue, driven by new contracts with two domestic government agencies.
International revenues accounted for 31% of total sales, with particularly strong growth recorded in Western Europe and South Korea.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year came in at $214 million, exceeding the company's own guidance range of $195 million to $205 million.
Dr. Kroft attributed the outperformance to a faster-than-anticipated ramp of the company's MX-9 lens array product line.
Meridian's headcount grew to 4,820 employees by year-end, up from 4,410 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company invested $93 million in research and development during fiscal year 2023, representing approximately 11% of total revenues.
A new manufacturing facility in Tempe, Arizona was completed in October and became fully operational by November 2023.
The Tempe facility adds approximately 180,000 square feet of production capacity to Meridian's existing footprint.
Capital expenditures totaled $141 million for the year, with the majority allocated to the Tempe facility and equipment upgrades.
Free cash flow came in at $119 million, providing the company with ample flexibility for strategic investments going forward.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program authorizing up to $150 million in buybacks over the next 24 months.
Meridian repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $63.40 per share.
The company's stock closed the fiscal year at $71.15, reflecting a 22% appreciation over the twelve-month period.
Inventory levels stood at $134 million at year-end, representing approximately 58 days of sales on hand.
Accounts receivable totaled $97 million, with a days sales outstanding metric of 42 days, within the company's target range.
Meridian's balance sheet showed total assets of $1.24 billion and total equity of $738 million as of December 31, 2023.
The company declared a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share in each of the four quarters of fiscal year 2023.
Total dividends paid to shareholders during the year amounted to $34 million, consistent with the prior year's payout.
Marcus Delgado emphasized that the company's liquidity position remained robust, with $261 million in cash and equivalents.
During the fourth quarter alone, Meridian secured a $320 million syndicated credit facility to fund its planned acquisition pipeline.
This new acquisition pipeline centers primarily on smaller optical sensor firms operating in the industrial automation space.
The company completed the acquisition of Vantix Lens Systems in September 2023 for a purchase price of $88 million.
Vantix, based in Minneapolis, specializes in high-resolution lenses used in robotic-assisted surgical platforms.
The integration of Vantix is expected to contribute approximately $40 million in incremental annualized revenues beginning in fiscal year 2024.
Dr. Kroft noted that the Vantix team brings 140 engineers and scientists with deep expertise in micro-optics fabrication.
Meridian also entered a strategic licensing agreement with Hanseatic Photonics GmbH of Hamburg, Germany, in July 2023.
Under the terms of the agreement, Meridian gains access to patented diffractive element technologies for a licensing fee of $12 million.
The company expects these technologies to reduce manufacturing cycle times for its flagship product lines by up to 17%.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives continued to gain prominence within Meridian's corporate strategy during the year.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 9% relative to the 2022 baseline, ahead of its internal targets.
Meridian achieved a 97.4% on-time delivery rate across all customer segments, up from 95.1% the previous year.
Customer satisfaction scores, as measured by net promoter surveys, reached an all-time company high of 74 in fiscal year 2023.
The backlog at year-end stood at $1.09 billion, representing approximately 15 months of revenue coverage at current run rates.
New order intake for fiscal year 2023 totaled $923 million, surpassing the prior-year intake of $811 million by 13.8%.
Dr. Kroft highlighted that the defense pipeline remains particularly strong, with several large multi-year contracts under negotiation.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.4%, slightly below management's earlier guidance of 22% to 23%.
Meridian benefited from R&D tax credits related to qualifying expenditures on advanced photonics research programs.
Earnings per diluted share for the full year came in at $4.87, an increase of 18% compared to $4.13 in fiscal year 2022.
The company provided initial guidance for fiscal year 2024, projecting revenues in the range of $930 million to $960 million.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is expected to fall between $235 million and $250 million, reflecting continued margin expansion.
Marcus Delgado indicated that the acquisition of Vantix and organic growth in the medical segment are the primary drivers of the 2024 outlook.
He also noted that capital expenditure spending in fiscal year 2024 is expected to moderate to approximately $110 million.
The moderation in capex reflects the completion of the Tempe facility, which no longer requires major capital outlays.
Meridian's pension obligation stood at $47 million as of year-end, down from $53 million in the prior year due to favorable actuarial assumptions.
The company does not anticipate any mandatory pension contributions in fiscal year 2024 under current actuarial projections.
Dr. Kroft expressed confidence in the company's ability to capture growing demand from next-generation medical imaging platforms.
She cited several hospital networks and imaging equipment OEMs that have recently expanded their component procurement from Meridian.
The company's three-year compound annual growth rate in revenues now stands at 12.6%, well above the sector median of 7.4%.
Analyst coverage of Meridian widened during the year, with two additional sell-side firms initiating coverage with positive ratings.
The company maintains a dual-listed structure, with its shares traded on the NASDAQ Global Select Market under the ticker MOPT.
Meridian's market capitalization at year-end was approximately $2.3 billion, based on approximately 32.3 million diluted shares outstanding.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers completed the annual audit with no material weaknesses identified.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the standards established by the Sarbanes-Oxley Act.
Dr. Kroft concluded the call by thanking Meridian's employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued trust and partnership.
The company remains committed to delivering long-term value through disciplined capital allocation, innovation, and operational excellence.
A full copy of the audited financial statements is available in Meridian Optical Technologies' Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the SEC.
Veltrano Industries, a leading manufacturer of precision agricultural equipment, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Salzburg, Austria, operates in over 38 countries and employs approximately 12,400 full-time staff worldwide.
Chief Executive Officer Margit Feldhofer opened the report with a statement highlighting the company's resilience amid challenging macroeconomic conditions.
Chief Financial Officer Tobias Grunewald noted that disciplined cost management had been central to the company's operating strategy throughout the year.
Veltrano Industries reported total consolidated revenue of €4.82 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 7.3% increase over the prior year.
Net income attributable to shareholders reached €412 million, reflecting improved operational efficiency across all major divisions.
The company's gross margin expanded by 140 basis points to 34.6%, driven by favorable input cost trends in the second half of the year.
Operating expenses totaled €1.19 billion, which included a significant allocation toward research and development initiatives.
Research and development spending increased by 18% year-over-year, reaching €287 million as Veltrano accelerated its precision farming technology roadmap.
The company launched three new product lines during the year, including the Veltrano FieldSense 900 autonomous tractor guidance system.
FieldSense 900 received regulatory approval in the European Union and North American markets within the first quarter of fiscal 2023.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to €318 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's facilities in Linz and Graz.
The board of directors approved a share buyback program valued at €150 million, of which €92 million was executed by year-end.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltrano Industries carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired all outstanding obligations in the prior fiscal year.
Cash and cash equivalents stood at €1.03 billion, providing the company with substantial liquidity to fund future strategic investments.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 41 days from 47 days in 2022, reflecting tighter credit management policies.
Inventory levels were reduced by 9% on a year-over-year basis as the supply chain normalization allowed for leaner stock management.
The North American segment generated €1.64 billion in revenue, representing 34% of total group sales.
European operations contributed €2.01 billion, remaining the largest revenue-generating region for the group.
The Asia-Pacific segment posted revenue of €810 million, with particularly strong growth reported in Australia and Japan.
CEO Margit Feldhofer cited Australia's expanding precision agriculture adoption as a key driver of Asia-Pacific momentum.
The company's market share in the European large-scale farming equipment category increased from 18.4% to 20.1% over the course of the year.
Veltrano's workforce grew modestly, with net headcount additions of 340 employees across engineering and sales functions globally.
Employee retention rates improved to 91.3%, which management attributed to enhanced benefits packages and expanded internal mobility programs.
The company recorded an income tax expense of €138 million, reflecting an effective tax rate of 25.1%.
Tobias Grunewald confirmed that the effective tax rate was broadly in line with the group's medium-term guidance range of 24% to 26%.
Veltrano's earnings per share for fiscal 2023 came in at €3.47, up from €3.08 in fiscal 2022.
The board declared a final dividend of €1.10 per share, payable in March 2024 to shareholders on record as of February 15, 2024.
This represented a 10% increase in the per-share dividend compared to the prior fiscal year, consistent with the company's progressive dividend policy.
Sustainability initiatives were expanded, with Veltrano committing an additional €45 million toward carbon reduction programs in its manufacturing operations.
The company achieved a 12% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to its 2020 baseline.
Veltrano's Board Chair, Elspeth Dornhuber, emphasized that environmental stewardship would remain a core pillar of the company's long-term strategy.
The company's debt-to-equity ratio stood at 0.0 as of fiscal year-end, confirming its position as a fully unlevered enterprise.
During the first quarter of 2023, Veltrano entered into a five-year syndicated credit facility totaling €600 million arranged by a consortium of European banks.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance of €5.1 billion to €5.3 billion, implying growth of approximately 6% to 10%.
Operating margin guidance for 2024 was set at a range of 13.5% to 14.5%, representing modest expansion from the 13.1% achieved in 2023.
The company anticipates capital expenditures of approximately €340 million in 2024, as production capacity investments continue at the Linz facility.
Veltrano is also evaluating potential acquisition targets in the precision soil analytics segment, according to remarks by CEO Feldhofer.
No binding agreements related to acquisitions had been signed as of the report's publication date of February 22, 2024.
The FieldSense 900 system contributed an estimated €94 million to group revenue in its first partial year of commercial availability.
Management expects FieldSense 900 annual revenues to exceed €200 million by fiscal 2025 as distribution partnerships are scaled globally.
The company's order backlog at year-end stood at €1.87 billion, a 15% increase compared to the €1.63 billion recorded at the end of 2022.
Strong backlog growth was particularly evident in the European and North American segments, each posting double-digit order intake gains.
Veltrano's return on invested capital reached 14.2% for fiscal 2023, surpassing the company's internal threshold of 12%.
Free cash flow generation came in at €524 million for the full year, well ahead of the €440 million target communicated at the beginning of the period.
The strong free cash flow performance was partly attributable to the working capital improvements realized through inventory reduction and faster receivables collection.
CFO Grunewald indicated that the company's capital allocation framework would continue to prioritize organic investment, followed by shareholder returns and bolt-on acquisitions.
Veltrano's credit rating was affirmed at A- by two major rating agencies during the year, with a stable outlook assigned in each case.
The company's insurance and risk management programs were reviewed and updated in the third quarter to reflect expanded operational scope.
Veltrano Industries closed the year with a total equity base of €2.94 billion, reflecting retained earnings growth and the impact of the share buyback program.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year.
Independent auditors issued an unqualified opinion on Veltrano's consolidated financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2023.
Management expressed confidence that the strategic priorities established for fiscal 2024 would position Veltrano Industries for sustained, profitable growth over the medium term.
The full audited financial statements, including notes and segment disclosures, are available on the company's investor relations website at veltrano-ir.com.
Veltara Dynamics Corporation is pleased to present its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Headquartered in Austin, Texas, Veltara Dynamics operates within the advanced semiconductor fabrication and photonics sector.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Marlena Osei-Bonsu opened her letter to shareholders by noting the company's resilience in a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Tchibozo reported that total consolidated revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $4.82 billion, a 12% increase over the prior year.
Gross profit margin expanded by 180 basis points to 41.3%, reflecting improved manufacturing yields and favorable product mix.
Operating expenses rose modestly to $1.14 billion, driven primarily by increased research and development investment in next-generation photonic chipsets.
The company's EBITDA for the fiscal year was $987 million, representing a 20.5% EBITDA margin.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Dynamics carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, having fully retired its previous credit facility in March 2023.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $612 million, compared to $498 million in fiscal year 2022.
Diluted earnings per share rose to $4.87, up from $3.96 in the prior fiscal year.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of February 15, 2024.
Total capital expenditures for the year amounted to $730 million, predominantly allocated to the expansion of the company's Fab-7 manufacturing campus in Round Rock, Texas.
Veltara's headcount grew to 18,400 full-time employees globally as of year-end, an increase of approximately 1,200 from December 2022.
The company's flagship VX-9 photonic processor series saw unit shipments increase by 34% year-over-year, surpassing 2.1 million units delivered.
Dr. Osei-Bonsu highlighted the VX-9 series as the primary driver of revenue growth in the Photonics Systems segment.
The Photonics Systems segment reported $2.63 billion in revenue, representing 54.6% of total company revenue.
The Industrial Sensing segment generated $1.47 billion in revenue, accounting for approximately 30.5% of consolidated revenues.
The Defense and Aerospace segment contributed $720 million, or roughly 14.9% of total revenue, bolstered by a multi-year contract with the U.S. Department of Defense.
International revenues accounted for 38% of total revenues, with the Asia-Pacific region representing the largest international market at 22% of consolidated sales.
The company's cash and cash equivalents position stood at $1.43 billion at year-end, up from $1.11 billion at the close of fiscal 2022.
Short-term investments in U.S. Treasury securities totaled $380 million as of December 31, 2023.
Total current assets were reported at $2.94 billion, while total current liabilities amounted to $1.08 billion, yielding a current ratio of 2.72.
Inventory levels increased to $543 million, reflecting strategic safety-stock builds in anticipation of supply chain volatility in early 2024.
Accounts receivable stood at $581 million, with days sales outstanding averaging 44 days across the fiscal year.
The company repurchased 3.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $87.40 per share, for a total consideration of approximately $280 million.
The Board authorized an additional $500 million share repurchase program in November 2023, effective through December 31, 2025.
Veltara's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 18.4%, benefiting from R&D tax credits and favorable jurisdictional income mix.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, the company entered into a $750 million senior unsecured revolving credit facility with a syndicate of eight financial institutions.
The new credit facility carries a five-year term and bears interest at SOFR plus 110 basis points.
Mr. Tchibozo stated that this financing arrangement provides the company with significant strategic flexibility for potential acquisitions and capital projects.
The company did not draw upon the revolving credit facility at any point during fiscal year 2023.
Veltara's total stockholders' equity increased to $5.91 billion as of December 31, 2023, from $5.44 billion one year earlier.
Return on equity for the fiscal year was 10.9%, compared to 9.6% in fiscal 2022.
Return on invested capital was 13.2%, which management considers comfortably above the company's weighted average cost of capital of 9.7%.
The company's Fab-7 expansion project is expected to add approximately 85,000 square feet of cleanroom capacity upon completion in late 2024.
Total project costs for the Fab-7 expansion are currently estimated at $1.1 billion, with $730 million expended through December 31, 2023.
The remaining $370 million of Fab-7 capital expenditure is projected to be incurred during the first three quarters of fiscal 2024.
Veltara announced in September 2023 the acquisition of NovaSense Technologies, a provider of LIDAR sensing modules, for a total consideration of $215 million.
The NovaSense acquisition is expected to be accretive to earnings per share by approximately $0.12 in the first full year of integration.
Dr. Osei-Bonsu described the NovaSense acquisition as a strategically important complement to Veltara's existing Industrial Sensing product portfolio.
Deferred revenue at year-end totaled $228 million, primarily associated with multi-year service and maintenance contracts in the Defense and Aerospace segment.
Operating cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $1.02 billion, a significant increase from $841 million in fiscal 2022.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $290 million for the full year.
The company's defined benefit pension obligation was $147 million as of year-end, with plan assets totaling $134 million.
Veltara recorded a non-cash impairment charge of $31 million related to certain legacy optical component assets in the Industrial Sensing segment.
Excluding the impairment charge, adjusted operating income would have been $868 million for the fiscal year.
The company's credit profile was affirmed by two major rating agencies, which maintained investment-grade ratings of BBB+ and Baa1 respectively.
Research and development expenditure reached $487 million in fiscal 2023, representing approximately 10.1% of total revenues.
Veltara filed 143 new patent applications during the fiscal year, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 2,217 patents worldwide.
The company launched the VX-11 photonic processor platform in October 2023, positioning it as the successor to the highly successful VX-9 series.
The VX-11 platform offers a reported 2.4 times improvement in photon processing throughput compared to the VX-9, according to internal benchmark testing.
Initial customer sampling of the VX-11 began in the fourth quarter, with full production ramp anticipated in the second half of fiscal 2024.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $5.2 billion to $5.4 billion, implying growth of approximately 8% to 12%.
The 2024 guidance assumes continued strength in the Photonics Systems and Defense and Aerospace segments.
Management also guided for fiscal 2024 EBITDA margins in the range of 21% to 23%, reflecting anticipated operating leverage from the Fab-7 expansion.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Wiremu noted that the company's supply chain resilience initiatives implemented in 2022 continue to provide a competitive advantage.
Veltara's customer concentration risk was reduced during fiscal 2023, with the top five customers accounting for 29% of revenues, down from 35% in fiscal 2022.
The company maintained strong relationships with over 340 active enterprise customers across 47 countries as of year-end.
Environmental sustainability initiatives resulted in a 7% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions intensity per unit of output compared to fiscal 2022.
Dr. Osei-Bonsu concluded her shareholder letter by expressing confidence in Veltara's competitive positioning and its capacity to deliver sustained long-term value.
Verandah Logistics Corporation, a mid-cap freight and supply-chain solutions provider headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the report by emphasizing the company's disciplined approach to capital allocation and operational efficiency.
Verandah Logistics operates across four primary segments: domestic trucking, intermodal rail, last-mile delivery, and third-party warehousing.
The company employs approximately 11,400 full-time staff across its network of 38 distribution hubs in North America.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal 2023 reached $2.74 billion, representing a 9.2 percent increase over the prior year's $2.51 billion.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei attributed the revenue growth to expanded contracts with e-commerce retailers and renewed agreements with three major automotive manufacturers.
Operating income improved to $312 million, up from $278 million in fiscal 2022, driven by route optimization initiatives and fuel-hedging strategies.
Verandah Logistics entered fiscal 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final senior note in December 2022.
Gross margin held steady at 28.4 percent, compared to 27.9 percent in the prior fiscal year.
Capital expenditures totaled $189 million, with the largest allocations directed toward electric vehicle fleet expansion and automated sorting technology.
The domestic trucking segment generated $1.12 billion in revenue, accounting for roughly 41 percent of consolidated top-line results.
Intermodal rail revenue grew 14 percent year-over-year to $680 million, benefiting from increased lane density on the company's Midwest corridor.
Last-mile delivery revenue reached $530 million, reflecting strong demand from retail partners during the holiday season.
Third-party warehousing contributed $410 million in revenue, supported by the opening of two new mega-facilities in Phoenix and Memphis.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $498 million, yielding an EBITDA margin of 18.2 percent.
The company repurchased $95 million worth of common shares during fiscal 2023 under its standing buyback authorization.
Dividends per share increased to $1.42 from $1.18 in the prior year, marking the eighth consecutive annual dividend increase.
Verandah Logistics maintained a current ratio of 2.1 at year-end, reflecting a comfortable liquidity position.
Cash and cash equivalents on the balance sheet stood at $347 million as of December 31, 2023.
The company's return on invested capital reached 13.7 percent, exceeding its internal hurdle rate of 11 percent for the third consecutive year.
Holloway noted that technology investment remained a cornerstone of the company's long-term strategy, particularly in predictive freight analytics.
Verandah Logistics launched its proprietary route-intelligence platform, VeriRoute 2.0, in the second quarter of 2023, ahead of its originally planned third-quarter rollout.
VeriRoute 2.0 contributed an estimated $22 million in cost savings through reduced empty-mile ratios and improved load consolidation.
The company's safety record improved meaningfully, with total recordable incident rates falling 18 percent compared to fiscal 2022 levels.
Headcount growth was modest, with net new hiring of approximately 620 employees across the network during the year.
Employee retention rates improved to 87 percent, up from 81 percent in 2022, following the introduction of a new performance-based bonus structure.
Osei indicated that the warehousing segment's margin expansion was partially offset by elevated insurance premiums tied to property coverage renewals.
During fiscal 2023, Verandah Logistics secured a $450 million syndicated term loan to fund strategic acquisitions and balance-sheet repositioning.
The integration of regional carrier SunSpan Freight, acquired in late 2022, was completed ahead of schedule and generated $17 million in synergies.
SunSpan's 1,200 drivers and 14 terminals were fully absorbed into Verandah's operating model by the end of the first quarter of 2023.
The intermodal segment benefited from a new partnership with Continental Rail Partners, adding 23 additional lane agreements effective March 2023.
Fuel costs as a percentage of revenue declined to 8.9 percent from 10.4 percent in fiscal 2022, aided by the company's hedging program and a softer spot market.
Verandah Logistics also reduced its carbon intensity by 11 percent on a per-shipment basis, in line with its 2030 sustainability targets.
The company published its third annual ESG report, highlighting progress on emissions reduction, diversity initiatives, and community investment programs.
Independent director Clara Vásquez, chair of the audit committee, confirmed that no material weaknesses were identified during the year-end audit process.
The external audit was conducted by Hartley & Brenn LLP, which issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved slightly to 38 days from 41 days in the prior year, reflecting tighter credit and collections procedures.
Inventory held for resale in the warehousing segment rose 6 percent to $84 million, consistent with client growth and expanded product categories.
The company's weighted-average cost of capital was estimated at 8.3 percent for planning purposes, unchanged from the prior year assumption.
In the second half of 2023, Verandah Logistics piloted a dynamic pricing model for spot freight, which yielded incremental revenue of approximately $31 million.
Management expects fiscal 2024 revenue to land in the range of $2.95 billion to $3.05 billion, implying growth of approximately 8 to 11 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $525 million to $545 million, reflecting continued margin discipline.
Capital expenditure guidance for 2024 stands at $210 million, with a significant portion earmarked for additional electric vehicle procurement.
Holloway expressed confidence that the company's diversified service portfolio positions it well against potential macroeconomic headwinds.
The board authorized an incremental share repurchase program of up to $120 million, effective January 1, 2024.
Verandah Logistics' stock closed fiscal year 2023 at $64.30 per share, representing a 22 percent appreciation from the January 2023 opening price.
Market capitalization at fiscal year-end was approximately $3.1 billion based on 48.2 million diluted shares outstanding.
The company's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Meridian Ratings, with a stable outlook.
Osei highlighted that the tax rate for fiscal 2023 came in at 24.1 percent, slightly below the statutory rate due to qualifying energy credits.
Deferred tax liabilities related to accelerated depreciation on the electric vehicle fleet totaled $38 million at year-end.
The company recognized $14 million in restructuring charges related to the consolidation of three legacy SunSpan facilities.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $218 million, or $4.51 per diluted share, compared to $187 million, or $3.86 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
Verandah Logistics will host its annual investor day on March 14, 2024, where management plans to present a detailed five-year strategic roadmap.
The board of directors, under the leadership of independent Chairman Theodore Park, unanimously approved all financial statements and disclosures contained in this report.
Verandah Logistics remains committed to delivering superior shareholder returns while sustaining the operational excellence that has defined the company's culture for over three decades.

Veldora Therapeutics, Inc. is a mid-stage biopharmaceutical company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, focused on developing novel treatments for autoimmune and inflammatory disorders.
The company was founded in 2011 by Dr. Miriam Okafor and has since grown into a recognized player in the specialty biologics market.
Chief Executive Officer Jonathan Frey joined the company in 2018 following a distinguished tenure at Nexagen Pharma, where he led two successful product launches.
Veldora's fiscal year 2023 annual report reflects a period of significant strategic transformation and continued investment in its core pipeline assets.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $312.4 million, representing a 14.7% increase compared to $272.3 million reported in fiscal year 2022.
Product revenue, which constitutes the largest component of total revenue, grew to $289.6 million, driven primarily by strong uptake of the company's lead commercial product, Velzarex.
Velzarex, an IL-17 inhibitor approved by the FDA in October 2020 for moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis, continues to gain market share in a competitive therapeutic class.
Collaboration and licensing revenue contributed $22.8 million to the top line, largely stemming from a partnership agreement with Ohrion Biosciences signed in early 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Sandra Lim noted that the revenue growth was broad-based and reflected both volume gains and modest net pricing improvements across key markets.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 was $241.7 million, yielding a gross margin of 77.4%, compared to 74.9% in the prior year.
The improvement in gross margin was attributed to favorable manufacturing scale efficiencies and a shift in product mix toward higher-margin formulations.
Research and development expenses totaled $98.3 million in fiscal year 2023, representing 31.5% of total revenue, as the company continued to invest heavily in pipeline expansion.
The largest R&D investment was directed toward VT-205, a next-generation JAK1 selective inhibitor currently in Phase 2 clinical trials for rheumatoid arthritis.
Selling, general and administrative expenses were $74.1 million for the year, reflecting increased commercial spending in support of Velzarex's expanded indication efforts.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $69.3 million, a substantial improvement from the $41.2 million operating income recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common stockholders was $54.8 million, or $1.23 per diluted share, compared to net income of $32.7 million, or $0.74 per diluted share, in the prior year.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure management uses to evaluate underlying operational performance, was $91.6 million for fiscal year 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents plus short-term investments stood at $408.2 million as of December 31, 2023, providing a robust liquidity position.
The company generated $87.4 million in operating cash flow during fiscal year 2023, a 28.3% increase over the prior year's $68.1 million.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 were $11.2 million, primarily related to upgrades at the company's Morrisville, North Carolina manufacturing facility.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $76.2 million for the year.
Veldora's balance sheet remained highly conservative throughout fiscal year 2023, with management maintaining a disciplined approach to leverage and capital allocation.
As of December 31, 2023, Veldora Therapeutics carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, reflecting the company's longstanding commitment to operating without external borrowings.
Accounts receivable increased to $47.3 million from $38.6 million at year-end 2022, consistent with the higher revenue run rate in the fourth quarter.
Inventory levels were actively managed during the year, ending at $18.9 million, broadly in line with prior year levels of $17.4 million.
During fiscal year 2023, the company repurchased 1.4 million shares of common stock under its existing buyback authorization at an average price of $28.60 per share.
The Board of Directors authorized an increase in the share repurchase program to $150 million in aggregate in May 2023, reflecting confidence in the company's long-term value creation.
Veldora does not currently pay a cash dividend, as the Board believes reinvestment in growth initiatives represents the highest-return use of capital at this stage.
Headcount as of December 31, 2023 stood at 847 full-time employees, an increase of 112 employees from year-end 2022, as the company scaled commercial and clinical functions.
The workforce expansion was particularly concentrated in medical affairs and clinical operations, in anticipation of Phase 3 trial activations for VT-205 in the first half of 2024.
Dr. Okafor, who currently serves as Chief Scientific Officer, presented updated preclinical data for VT-312, a first-in-class STING pathway modulator, at the annual ASHP symposium in November 2023.
The data generated significant interest from the investor community and contributed to a 9.3% appreciation in Veldora's share price during the fourth quarter.
Veldora's stock closed at $31.47 on December 31, 2023, implying a market capitalization of approximately $1.40 billion based on 44.5 million diluted shares outstanding.

The company's stock has returned 34.2% on a total return basis over the three-year period ending December 31, 2023, outperforming the Nasdaq Biotechnology Index, which returned 8.7% over the same period.
In geographic terms, 88.4% of product revenue was generated in the United States, with the remaining 11.6% derived from international markets, primarily Canada and Western Europe.
Veldora entered into a co-promotion agreement with Stradifen Pharmaceuticals in September 2023 to expand Velzarex's reach into dermatology practices outside its current targeted call universe.
Management expects this commercial arrangement to contribute meaningfully to revenue growth beginning in the second half of fiscal year 2024.
The company's payor mix remained favorable in 2023, with approximately 73% of covered lives receiving Tier 2 or better formulary placement for Velzarex.

Patient adherence rates for Velzarex, as measured by the proportion of patients refilling prescriptions at six months, improved to 64% compared to 58% in 2022, reflecting ongoing patient support program enhancements.
Veldora's medical affairs team conducted 48 advisory board meetings throughout 2023, engaging more than 300 key opinion leaders across dermatology and rheumatology.
The company also submitted a supplemental Biologics License Application to the FDA in August 2023 seeking approval of Velzarex for the treatment of psoriatic arthritis.
The FDA granted Priority Review designation for this supplemental application in October 2023, setting a Prescription Drug User Fee Act target action date of April 14, 2024.

During the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2023, Veldora established a $275 million senior secured revolving credit facility with a syndicate of lenders led by First Atlantic Bank, expanding the company's financial flexibility.
The credit facility carries an interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points and matures in December 2028, with no amounts drawn at the time of closing.
Management stated that the facility is intended to serve as a strategic reserve to support potential business development activities and opportunistic pipeline investments.

Income tax expense for fiscal year 2023 was $14.5 million, representing an effective tax rate of 20.9%, which was lower than the prior year rate of 23.4% due to the utilization of certain R&D tax credits.
Deferred tax assets net of valuation allowances were $22.8 million as of December 31, 2023, and are expected to be fully realizable based on projected future taxable income.
Stock-based compensation expense for fiscal year 2023 was $18.7 million, consistent with the company's equity compensation philosophy of aligning employee and stockholder interests.
The company granted 1.1 million stock options and 0.6 million restricted stock units to employees and directors during fiscal year 2023.
Jonathan Frey commented that the company enters fiscal year 2024 with strong momentum, a differentiated commercial asset, and a pipeline of meaningful clinical catalysts.
For fiscal year 2024, management is providing guidance for total revenue in the range of $355 million to $375 million, representing growth of approximately 14% to 20% over fiscal year 2023.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 is set at $108 million to $118 million, reflecting continued operating leverage and disciplined cost management.
R&D expenditures are projected to increase to a range of $115 million to $125 million in fiscal year 2024, driven largely by planned Phase 3 costs for VT-205.
The company expects to report top-line Phase 2 data for VT-205 in moderate-to-severe rheumatoid arthritis in the third quarter of fiscal year 2024.

Positive Phase 2 outcomes for VT-205 would trigger the initiation of a pivotal Phase 3 program, which management estimates would require total investment of approximately $180 million over a three-year period.
Veldora also plans to initiate a Phase 1 dose-escalation study for VT-312 in healthy volunteers by mid-2024, pending completion of IND-enabling studies.
Management believes VT-312 represents a potential best-in-class opportunity in oncology immunology, a therapeutic area adjacent to the company's current autoimmune franchise.
Risks to the company's outlook include competitive pricing pressures, potential biosimilar entry for Velzarex post-2027, and execution risks associated with clinical development programs.
Additionally, changes in U.S. drug pricing policy, including provisions of the Inflation Reduction Act, could adversely affect reimbursement for Velzarex in Medicare Part D populations.
Veldora's Audit Committee, chaired by independent director Thomas Branscomb, oversaw the company's financial reporting and internal controls throughout fiscal year 2023.
The company's independent registered public accounting firm, Whitmore & Holt LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the fiscal year 2023 consolidated financial statements.
Management affirms that the internal control over financial reporting was effective as of December 31, 2023, with no material weaknesses identified.
Veldora Therapeutics remains committed to transparent stakeholder communication, sustainable growth, and the delivery of differentiated therapies that address significant unmet medical needs.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. hereby presents its consolidated financial results for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates in the specialty pharmaceuticals sector, focusing on rare autoimmune disorders and orphan drug development.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Priya Nandakumar expressed confidence in the company's long-term growth trajectory during the annual earnings call.
Total revenue for fiscal 2023 reached $412.7 million, representing a 14.3% increase over the prior year's $361.1 million.
Product sales accounted for $389.4 million of total revenue, with the remainder derived from licensing and collaboration agreements.
Our flagship product, Velitrix, continued to outperform expectations, generating $221.6 million in net sales during the year.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Oyelaran noted that operational discipline remained a priority throughout each fiscal quarter.
Veltara Biosciences carried no outstanding long-term debt as of December 31, 2023, maintaining a fully unlevered balance sheet.
The company's gross margin improved to 71.4%, up from 68.9% in fiscal 2022, reflecting favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies.
Research and development expenses totaled $98.3 million, representing 23.8% of total revenue.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses increased to $74.6 million compared to $68.2 million in the prior period.
Operating income for the year was $59.2 million, up 22.7% from $48.2 million in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $47.8 million, or $1.14 per diluted share.
The company's cash and cash equivalents position stood at $184.3 million at year-end, providing ample liquidity.
Short-term investments totaling $62.5 million supplemented the company's overall liquidity profile.
Dr. Nandakumar highlighted the successful completion of Phase III trials for Velitrix-XR, the extended-release formulation.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $50 million in common stock during the third quarter.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 amounted to $22.1 million, primarily directed toward manufacturing capacity expansion in North Carolina.
Veltara's headcount grew to 1,847 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, up from 1,612 at the prior year-end.
The company expanded its commercial infrastructure in Europe, opening new regional offices in Frankfurt and Amsterdam.
International revenue contributed $138.9 million, or approximately 33.7% of total revenue, reflecting strong adoption in key markets.
The European Medicines Agency granted conditional marketing approval for Velitrix in the treatment of refractory lupus nephritis.
Veltara's pipeline comprises nine clinical-stage assets, including three in late-stage development.
Inventory levels at year-end were $31.7 million, consistent with the company's targeted supply buffer of approximately ten weeks.
Days sales outstanding improved to 43 days from 51 days in the prior year, demonstrating enhanced collections performance.
The company recorded a tax benefit of $3.1 million for the year, primarily driven by federal R&D tax credits.
Deferred revenue of $18.4 million was recognized, associated with milestone payments under existing partnership agreements.

The company's pipeline asset VLT-309 received Fast Track designation from the FDA in November 2023.
A collaboration agreement with Novante Therapeutics was amended to extend the exclusivity window through mid-2026.
Royalty revenues from the Novante collaboration contributed $14.2 million in fiscal 2023.
The company's effective tax rate on a non-GAAP basis was 18.7% for the full year.
Veltara repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock at an average price of $31.40 per share during fiscal 2023.
Total shareholder equity stood at $332.6 million as of December 31, 2023.
The audit committee engaged Ernst & Whitmore LLP as independent external auditors for fiscal year 2023.
Ernst & Whitmore issued an unqualified audit opinion with no material weaknesses identified in internal controls.
Dr. Nandakumar reiterated the company's commitment to achieving sustainable double-digit top-line growth over the next three years.
Veltara anticipates fiscal 2024 revenue in the range of $465 million to $490 million, implying growth of approximately 12.7% to 18.7%.

Management expects R&D expenditures to increase to approximately $115 million in fiscal 2024 as late-stage trials advance.
The board declared no cash dividends for fiscal 2023, consistent with the company's policy of reinvesting capital into growth initiatives.
Veltara's return on invested capital for fiscal 2023 was 11.3%, a meaningful improvement from 9.6% in the prior year.
The company maintained compliance with all financial covenants under its existing contractual obligations throughout the year.
A loss contingency of $4.8 million was accrued related to ongoing patent litigation with a third-party generic manufacturer.
Management believes the ultimate resolution of the litigation will not have a material adverse effect on financial condition.
Veltara's stock closed fiscal year 2023 at $34.72 per share, representing a 28.4% appreciation over the January 1, 2023 opening price.
Market capitalization at year-end was approximately $1.46 billion, placing the company firmly in the mid-cap pharmaceutical segment.
The company looks forward to updating investors on clinical and commercial milestones during its first-quarter 2024 earnings call.

Verdant Horizons Corporation, a mid-cap renewable energy solutions provider headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, led by Chief Executive Officer Marlena Forsythe, has focused its operations on utility-scale solar installations and distributed energy storage systems.
Chief Financial Officer Derek Paulson noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a transformational period for the organization.
Verdant Horizons reported total consolidated revenue of $1.84 billion for the fiscal year, representing a 22% increase over the prior year.

Gross profit for the year reached $487 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 26.5%.
Operating expenses, including research and development, selling, general and administrative costs, totaled $294 million.
This resulted in operating income of $193 million, compared with $141 million in the prior fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $148 million, or $2.34 per diluted share.
The company's effective tax rate for the period was 23.1%, reflecting benefits from the Inflation Reduction Act's clean energy tax credits.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at $241 million for the full year.
Capital expenditures totaled $312 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Nevada facility.
Verdant Horizons ended the fiscal year with $389 million in cash and cash equivalents on its balance sheet.
Trade receivables stood at $276 million, with days sales outstanding averaging 54 days across the year.
Total assets reached $2.71 billion as of December 31, 2023, up from $2.19 billion the prior year.
The company's inventory balance increased to $198 million, consistent with management's strategy to pre-purchase key battery components ahead of anticipated price increases.
Stockholders' equity grew to $1.03 billion, supported by net income and the exercise of employee stock options totaling $44 million.
During the year, Verdant Horizons completed the acquisition of SolTerra Grid Systems for a total consideration of $215 million.
SolTerra's integration contributed an incremental $67 million in revenue during the second half of fiscal 2023.
Chief Strategy Officer Priya Anand stated that the SolTerra acquisition accelerated the company's grid-edge software capabilities by an estimated two years.
Verdant Horizons launched four new product lines during the fiscal year, including the HorizonEdge 5000 commercial storage system and the SolArray Pro residential inverter.
The company's workforce expanded to 6,200 full-time employees by year-end, up from 4,950 at the close of fiscal 2022.
Employee-related costs rose to $381 million in 2023, reflecting both headcount growth and annual merit increases averaging 4.8%.
