The company's backlog of signed contracts stood at $3.6 billion as of December 31, 2023, providing approximately two years of forward revenue visibility.
Verdant Horizons maintains a disciplined approach to project selection, with a minimum internal rate of return threshold of 14% for new contracts.
During fiscal 2023, the company's long-term debt balance was fully retired, with the final $175 million tranche of senior secured notes paid off in March 2023.
The board of directors authorized a $200 million share repurchase program in September 2023, of which $47 million was utilized by year-end.
As of December 31, 2023, the company carried no financial debt obligations on its balance sheet, operating entirely on an equity-funded capital structure.
The company's return on invested capital for fiscal 2023 was 11.2%, above the company's weighted average cost of capital of 8.7%.
Verdant Horizons generated $229 million in free cash flow during the year, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures.
CEO Marlena Forsythe highlighted that strong cash generation gave the company significant flexibility to pursue strategic opportunities in 2024.
The company's net promoter score among commercial customers improved from 52 to 67 over the course of fiscal 2023.
Customer retention rates in the service and maintenance segment reached 91%, up from 86% in the prior year.
Verdant Horizons expanded into two new geographic markets during fiscal 2023, entering Texas's commercial solar segment and the Ontario, Canada distributed storage market.
The company secured a landmark 300-megawatt solar development agreement with the Nevada Power Authority, its single largest contract to date.

Management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.15 billion to $2.25 billion, representing approximately 17% to 22% growth over fiscal 2023.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is projected to fall between $295 million and $320 million, reflecting continued margin improvement.
The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal 2024 to range from $280 million to $310 million, slightly below the prior year's spending level.
Verdant Horizons has identified three potential acquisition targets in the grid management software space that are currently under evaluation.

The company's supply chain team successfully diversified its battery cell suppliers from two to five vendors during fiscal 2023, reducing concentration risk.
This diversification effort contributed to a 9% reduction in average battery cell costs compared with fiscal 2022 procurement levels.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a strategic priority, with Verdant Horizons reducing its operational carbon footprint by 18% year over year.
The company published its third consecutive sustainability report in accordance with the Global Reporting Initiative standards.
Verdant Horizons' board of directors consists of nine members, seven of whom are classified as independent under applicable Nasdaq listing rules.
Lead Independent Director Samuel Okafor chaired the audit committee, which met six times during fiscal 2023.
In the second quarter of 2024, the company entered into a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five lenders, secured against its project assets.
Audit fees paid to the company's external auditor, Whitmore & Crane LLP, totaled $4.2 million for fiscal 2023.
The company has no material pending litigation that management believes would have a material adverse effect on its financial statements.
Verdant Horizons declared and paid a special cash dividend of $0.50 per share in December 2023, returning approximately $31 million to shareholders.
The dividend payment reflected management's confidence in the company's liquidity position and long-term earnings outlook.
Forward-looking statements in this report are subject to risks including changes in federal tax incentive structures, interest rate movements, and competition from established utilities.

Veltrana Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap pharmaceutical and biotechnology company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
The company specializes in the development and commercialization of targeted oncology therapeutics and companion diagnostics.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Marlena Forsythe has led the organization since its founding in 2011, guiding it through three successful product launches.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Aldecoa joined Veltrana in 2018 and has since overseen a period of significant balance sheet restructuring.
For the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, Veltrana reported total net revenues of $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
Product revenue from the company's lead oncology drug, Velotrex, accounted for $612.1 million, or approximately 72% of total revenues.
Diagnostic licensing fees and royalties contributed an additional $235.2 million to the top line for the year.
Gross profit for fiscal 2023 reached $589.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 69.6%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $421.7 million for the fiscal year.
Research and development expenditure was $198.4 million, reflecting the company's continued investment in its mid-stage pipeline.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses came in at $223.3 million, up 8.1% from $206.6 million in fiscal 2022.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $167.9 million, compared to $131.4 million in the prior year, a 27.8% year-over-year increase.
Net income attributable to Veltrana shareholders was $124.5 million, or $2.18 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure, reached $231.6 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 27.3%.
The company ended fiscal 2023 with cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments totaling $403.7 million.
Operating cash flow for the year was $189.2 million, demonstrating continued improvement in working capital management.
Veltrana carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having retired its remaining credit facility obligations in March of that year.
Capital expenditures for the period were $44.1 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the Durham, North Carolina facility.
The company repurchased 2.1 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 under its existing $150 million buyback authorization.
Shares outstanding at year-end totaled approximately 57.1 million on a fully diluted basis.
Veltrana declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share during the second half of fiscal 2023, marking its inaugural dividend program.
The Board of Directors authorized an increase to the quarterly dividend to $0.15 per share beginning in the first quarter of fiscal 2024.
Dr. Forsythe commented that the company's commercial momentum in 2023 exceeded internal expectations across all major therapeutic indications.
Velotrex received expanded label approval from the FDA in June 2023 to include second-line treatment of non-small-cell lung cancer.
This label expansion drove a meaningful volume uplift in the second half of the year, with Velotrex unit sales increasing 18.3% over the first half.
The company's oncology sales force was expanded to 412 representatives as of year-end, up from 347 at the start of fiscal 2023.
Veltrana's market share in the targeted NSCLC therapy segment grew to an estimated 14.8% by the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023.
The company's companion diagnostic co-developed with LabPath Genomics achieved regulatory clearance in four additional European markets during the year.
International revenues, while still a modest contributor, rose 31.7% year-over-year to $87.4 million in fiscal 2023.
Management attributes the international growth primarily to successful market entry in Germany, France, and Italy.
Pipeline asset VLT-204, a next-generation KRAS inhibitor, successfully completed Phase 1 dose-escalation trials with no dose-limiting toxicities observed.
VLT-204 is expected to enter a randomized Phase 2 study in the first quarter of fiscal 2024 targeting colorectal and pancreatic cancers.
A second pipeline candidate, VLT-317, targeting HER2-amplified breast cancer, reached the Phase 2 milestone ahead of schedule in November 2023.
Mr. Aldecoa noted that the company's working capital position remains robust, providing flexibility to fund both organic growth and opportunistic business development.
Inventory levels at year-end were $62.3 million, reflecting a 14-week supply of Velotrex finished goods, which management considers an appropriate safety stock.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days at December 31, 2023, from 54 days at December 31, 2022.
In September 2023, Veltrana entered into a $300 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of eight financial institutions, providing additional liquidity for strategic acquisitions.
The facility carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points and matures in September 2028.
Veltrana's total headcount at December 31, 2023 was 1,847 full-time equivalents, an increase of 203 employees compared to the prior year-end.
The company opened a new research center in Cambridge, Massachusetts in October 2023, housing approximately 120 scientists focused on immuno-oncology.
Dr. Forsythe emphasized that talent retention has been a strategic priority, with voluntary turnover declining to 7.2% in fiscal 2023 from 9.8% in fiscal 2022.
Employee compensation and benefits expense for fiscal 2023 totaled $289.1 million, inclusive of $28.3 million in stock-based compensation.
Veltrana's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 23.1%, slightly above the prior year rate of 22.4% due to changes in the geographic mix of earnings.
The company recognized a $14.7 million net deferred tax asset related to R&D tax credits generated during the year.
Veltrana maintains no off-balance-sheet financing arrangements, and all material obligations are fully reflected in the consolidated financial statements.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives were formalized under a new ESG framework published in August 2023, covering carbon, diversity, and ethics programs.
The company achieved a 9% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to its 2021 baseline.
Women represented 48% of Veltrana's global workforce and 39% of senior leadership roles as of December 31, 2023.
Veltrana's Board of Directors consists of nine members, including three independent directors added in fiscal 2023 to strengthen governance.
The Audit Committee, chaired by former SEC official Denise Carmichael, conducted a comprehensive review of internal controls with no material weaknesses identified.
Management's guidance for fiscal 2024 projects total net revenues in the range of $940 million to $975 million.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is projected to fall between $255 million and $270 million, reflecting continued operating leverage.
The revenue guidance assumes Velotrex volume growth of approximately 10% to 13% and modest price increases consistent with market norms.
Management also anticipates increased R&D spending of $220 million to $240 million in fiscal 2024 to support the Phase 2 programs for VLT-204 and VLT-317.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal 2024 has been set at $55 million to $65 million, largely tied to the Durham facility expansion and new Cambridge lab buildout.
Mr. Aldecoa confirmed that Veltrana's strong liquidity position and cash generation profile support both the dividend commitment and the ongoing share repurchase program.
The company intends to file a supplemental new drug application for Velotrex in the first-line NSCLC setting in the third quarter of fiscal 2024.
If approved, this indication could expand the addressable patient population by an estimated 40,000 to 55,000 additional patients annually in the United States alone.
Veltrana's management team expressed confidence that the strategic priorities set forth for 2024 will further solidify the company's position as a leading oncology-focused biopharmaceutical company.
The consolidated financial statements were prepared in accordance with U.S. GAAP and have been audited by Kessler & Wren LLP, which issued an unqualified opinion.
Veltara Biosystems, Inc. hereby presents its Annual Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Veltara Biosystems operates in the precision diagnostics sector, developing advanced molecular testing platforms for clinical and research markets.

Chief Executive Officer Dr. Maren Okafor led the organization through a year of significant strategic milestones.
Chief Financial Officer Thomas Helwick oversaw the company's financial operations with a focus on sustainable growth.
Veltara Biosystems reported total consolidated revenue of $412.7 million for fiscal year 2023, representing an 18.4% increase over the prior year.

Gross profit for the year reached $231.4 million, yielding a gross margin of 56.1%.

Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $157.9 million, providing strong near-term liquidity.
Accounts receivable increased modestly to $62.4 million, reflecting the higher revenue base while days sales outstanding remained stable at 55 days.

Veltara Biosystems generated $58.3 million in operating cash flow during the year, a 34% improvement over fiscal 2022.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $35.5 million for the year.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares under its existing buyback authorization during the second half of 2023.
Total shares outstanding at December 31, 2023, were approximately 23.6 million on a diluted basis.
Veltara Biosystems carried no long-term debt obligations as of December 31, 2023, maintaining a fully unlevered balance sheet.
Dr. Okafor noted that disciplined capital allocation remained central to the company's financial philosophy throughout the year.
During 2023, Veltara Biosystems expanded its global commercial footprint, entering five new international markets.
European revenues grew 31% year-over-year, supported by regulatory approvals obtained in Germany, France, and Italy.
The Asia-Pacific region also showed strong momentum, with revenues increasing 24% compared to fiscal 2022.
Veltara launched three new diagnostic assays during the year, adding to its portfolio of over 40 cleared tests.
The OncoPlex 7 panel, introduced in March 2023, achieved rapid market uptake and contributed meaningfully to Diagnostics segment growth.
The NeuroMark Liquid Biopsy assay received FDA clearance in July 2023 and began commercial shipments in the third quarter.
The company also introduced the VeltaFlow automated processing module, enhancing laboratory workflow efficiency for high-volume clinical labs.
Customer retention rates remained above 92% across the installed base of Veltara instruments.
The Diagnostics segment added 214 net new instrument placements during 2023, bringing the total installed base to approximately 1,890 units.
Each installed instrument generates an estimated $28,000 in annual reagent revenue, providing a predictable recurring revenue stream.
Gross margin improvement was driven by favorable product mix, manufacturing efficiencies, and modest pricing adjustments.
The company invested significantly in its direct sales force, growing the commercial team by 18% during the year.
Veltara Biosystems also strengthened its field application specialist program, adding 32 specialists to support customers post-installation.
Headcount across all functions grew from 1,104 employees at the start of 2023 to 1,347 employees by year-end.
Employee retention was a priority, and the company reported voluntary turnover of 8.3%, below the industry average.
The company's facility in Raleigh completed a 40,000 square-foot manufacturing expansion in September 2023.
This expansion increased annual production capacity for reagents by approximately 35%, positioning Veltara for continued growth.
In the fourth quarter of 2023, Veltara Biosystems entered into a $175 million secured term loan agreement with a consortium of lenders.
Veltara's management believes the company's innovation pipeline is its most important long-term competitive differentiator.
The Research Solutions segment benefited from multi-year agreements with four top-10 global pharmaceutical companies.
These agreements collectively represent a contracted revenue backlog of approximately $89 million to be recognized through 2026.
Dr. Helwick expressed confidence that the backlog provides meaningful revenue visibility over the medium term.
The company's income tax rate for 2023 was 18.2%, lower than the prior year's rate of 21.4%, due to recognition of deferred tax assets.
Veltara Biosystems does not currently hold any goodwill on its balance sheet, as the company has grown organically to date.
The company carries no off-balance-sheet financing arrangements or variable interest entity exposures.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives were formalized in 2023, with the appointment of a Chief Sustainability Officer.

Looking ahead, management is providing fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $475 million to $490 million.

Operating margin is expected to expand modestly in 2024, driven by operating leverage and continued gross margin improvement.
The company plans to file its complete Form 10-K with the Securities and Exchange Commission by March 15, 2024.
Veltara Biosystems thanks its shareholders, employees, customers, and partners for their continued confidence and support.

Chief Financial Officer Desmond Arquette outlined the key financial metrics achieved during the fiscal year.
Veltrano Systems reported total consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year.
Subscription-based revenues accounted for $1.21 billion of total revenues, reflecting continued growth in the company's SaaS platform adoption.
Professional services and consulting revenues contributed an additional $330 million to the top line.
Hardware and licensing revenues, which the company has been strategically de-emphasizing, declined 8% year-over-year to $300 million.
Gross profit for the year totaled $1.02 billion, yielding a gross margin of approximately 55.4%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of revenues, totaled $780 million, up from $694 million in the prior fiscal year.
Research and development expenditures rose 18% to $312 million, signaling continued investment in next-generation product capabilities.
Sales and marketing expenses reached $285 million, reflecting expanded go-to-market efforts in North America and Europe.
General and administrative expenses were relatively stable at $183 million compared to $179 million in fiscal 2022.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $241 million, compared to $198 million in the prior year, representing a 21.7% improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $178 million, or $2.34 per diluted share.
This represents a meaningful improvement from the $143 million net income, or $1.89 per diluted share, reported in fiscal 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the period was $367 million, translating to an adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 19.9%.
The company's balance sheet remained strong, with total assets of $3.12 billion as of December 31, 2023.

Veltrano Systems repaid $75 million in term loan principal during fiscal 2023, bringing the outstanding senior secured term loan balance to $320 million.
Total stockholders' equity increased to $1.47 billion, driven by net income and stock-based compensation charges partially offset by share repurchases.
The Board of Directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $150 million in August 2023, of which $87 million was utilized by year-end.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $94 million, primarily directed toward data center expansion and internal infrastructure upgrades.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, reached $273 million for the year.
Veltrano Systems carried no outstanding revolving credit facility borrowings as of December 31, 2023, with the revolver fully undrawn and balance at zero.
The company's global headcount grew to 7,400 full-time employees by year-end, an increase of approximately 9% from the 6,790 employees reported at the end of fiscal 2022.
Employee retention rates improved to 88%, up from 84% in the prior year, reflecting investments in compensation and workplace culture initiatives.
The company onboarded 214 net new enterprise customers during the fiscal year, bringing the total enterprise customer count to 1,892.
Average contract value among enterprise customers increased to $312,000, up 7% from $292,000 in fiscal 2022.
Customer churn rates declined to 6.2% on an annualized basis, compared to 7.8% in the prior period.
Net revenue retention rate, a key SaaS health metric, reached 114% for fiscal 2023, indicating strong upsell and expansion activity within the existing customer base.
The Veltrano CloudCore platform, the company's flagship offering, processed over 4.2 trillion transactions during the fiscal year.
CloudCore's uptime availability averaged 99.97% across all regions in fiscal 2023, exceeding the 99.95% contractual commitment.

The company launched three major product releases under the CloudCore suite, introducing AI-driven analytics capabilities in Q1, enhanced security modules in Q2, and multi-cloud orchestration tools in Q4.
The AI-driven analytics module garnered particular attention from the market, contributing to a 22% increase in CloudCore upsell revenue.
Veltrano's presence in the European market expanded considerably, with EMEA revenues growing 19% year-over-year to $431 million.
The Asia-Pacific region also demonstrated strong performance, with revenues rising 16% to $278 million.
North America remained the largest geographic segment, contributing $1.13 billion or approximately 61.4% of total revenues.
Chief Revenue Officer Priya Lakshmanan noted that the company's pipeline entering fiscal 2024 is the largest in its history.
The company's partner ecosystem expanded to include over 340 certified implementation partners globally, up from 280 at the end of fiscal 2022.
Veltrano Systems was recognized by three independent industry analysts as a leader in the cloud infrastructure management software category.
The company's environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed materially during fiscal 2023, including a commitment to carbon neutrality by 2030.
Veltrano reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 11% year-over-year through renewable energy procurement and operational efficiency measures.
The Board of Directors approved the initiation of a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share beginning in Q1 2024, marking the first dividend in the company's history.
During fiscal 2023, Veltrano Systems drew down $180 million on its revolving credit facility to fund accelerated investments in international data center infrastructure.
The new data center facilities are expected to come fully online in the first half of fiscal 2024, supporting capacity requirements driven by international customer growth.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.12 billion, implying growth of 11% to 15%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin for fiscal 2024 is expected to expand modestly to between 20.5% and 21.5%, reflecting operating leverage on a larger revenue base.
The company anticipates capital expenditures of approximately $110 million in fiscal 2024, consistent with continued investment in infrastructure.
Management also indicated it expects to generate free cash flow of at least $290 million in fiscal 2024.
Desmond Arquette confirmed that the company's debt management strategy will prioritize maintaining a net leverage ratio below 2.0x adjusted EBITDA.
Veltrano Systems successfully renewed its primary banking relationship with a syndicate led by First Continental Bank and Meridian Capital Partners.
The company's credit rating was affirmed at BBB by Standard Capital Ratings in November 2023, reflecting stable financial performance and strong liquidity.
Legal proceedings remained immaterial during fiscal 2023, with no significant contingent liabilities identified by the company's legal counsel.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseDeloitte LLP completed its independent audit of the consolidated financial statements without material findings.
Veltrano Systems adopted ASC 842 lease accounting standards for all newly executed operating leases during the fiscal year.
The company's deferred tax asset position totaled $64 million as of year-end, reflecting timing differences primarily associated with stock-based compensation deductions.
Management believes the company is well-positioned to sustain above-market growth while expanding profitability in the years ahead.

Veltara Biomedical Corporation, a mid-cap pharmaceutical and diagnostics company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.

Veltara operates in three core business segments: therapeutic drug development, point-of-care diagnostics, and contract manufacturing services.
The company serves over 4,200 hospitals, clinics, and independent laboratories across North America and Western Europe.

Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing an 11.4% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The therapeutics segment remained the largest revenue contributor, generating $412.6 million, up from $371.0 million in fiscal year 2022.
The diagnostics segment posted revenue of $284.5 million, driven by sustained demand for the company's VeltraPanel rapid-testing platform.
