Gross profit for the consolidated entity was $391.4 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 46.2%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $218.7 million during the fiscal year.
Research and development expenditures accounted for $94.3 million of total operating expenses, underscoring the company's commitment to pipeline expansion.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $124.4 million, reflecting investments in commercial infrastructure and regulatory compliance.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $172.7 million, compared to $141.2 million in the prior year, representing a 22.3% improvement.
Net income attributable to Veltara shareholders was $131.5 million, or $2.87 per diluted share.
The effective tax rate for the year was 23.8%, consistent with management's guidance issued at the beginning of the fiscal year.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure used by management, was $204.9 million for fiscal year 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $223.6 million, reflecting strong free cash flow generation throughout the year.
The company generated $189.4 million in cash from operating activities, a meaningful improvement over the $155.7 million reported in fiscal year 2022.
Capital expenditures totaled $47.2 million, primarily allocated to expanding the Raleigh manufacturing campus and upgrading laboratory automation systems.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow minus capital expenditures, was $142.2 million for the fiscal year.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its remaining $85 million term loan in March 2023.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $100 million, effective January 1, 2024.
During fiscal year 2023, the company repurchased 1.4 million shares at an average price of $38.62, totaling approximately $54.1 million in buybacks.
Veltara declared quarterly cash dividends totaling $0.72 per share for the full fiscal year, reflecting a 12.5% increase over the prior year's dividend.
The diagnostics segment benefited from the successful commercial launch of VeltraPanel 3.0, which achieved clearance from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration in February 2023.
VeltraPanel 3.0 delivered a 94.7% sensitivity rate in clinical trials, surpassing the performance benchmarks set by the prior generation platform.
By year-end, VeltraPanel 3.0 had been adopted by more than 1,100 healthcare facilities, exceeding the internal target of 800 facilities.
The therapeutics segment advanced three compounds through Phase II clinical trials during the year, with lead candidate VLT-204 demonstrating statistically significant efficacy outcomes.
VLT-204 targets a rare autoimmune condition affecting approximately 280,000 patients in the United States, representing a substantial addressable market.
Management expects to submit a New Drug Application for VLT-204 to the FDA no later than the third quarter of 2024.
The contract manufacturing segment secured two multi-year agreements with major biopharmaceutical clients, collectively valued at approximately $310 million over five years.
Headcount at year-end was 3,847 full-time employees, an increase of 214 employees compared to the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company expanded its European operations by establishing a new regulatory affairs office in Frankfurt, Germany, staffed with 22 full-time employees.
Veltara's international revenue represented 28.3% of total consolidated revenue in fiscal year 2023, up from 24.6% in the prior year.
The company's inventory balance at year-end was $67.4 million, reflecting a deliberate build-up of raw materials in anticipation of increased manufacturing volumes in 2024.
Accounts receivable stood at $112.3 million at December 31, 2023, with days sales outstanding of approximately 48 days.
The allowance for doubtful accounts was $4.1 million, representing less than 4% of gross accounts receivable.
Dr. Castleford commented in her letter to shareholders that the company's operational discipline and revenue diversification have positioned Veltara for sustainable long-term growth.
Mr. Tran noted that disciplined working capital management contributed meaningfully to the company's strong free cash flow performance in fiscal year 2023.
The board of directors conducted its annual review of executive compensation, resulting in a 6% base salary increase for named executive officers effective April 2024.
In connection with its growth strategy, Veltara entered into a $150 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of four financial institutions in September 2023.
The company's return on invested capital was 18.4% for fiscal year 2023, an improvement of 2.1 percentage points relative to the prior year.
Veltara's stock price appreciated approximately 34% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the S&P Healthcare Index, which returned 11.2% over the same period.
The audit committee engaged Ernst & Hargrove LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023.
Ernst & Hargrove issued an unqualified audit opinion, confirming that the financial statements present fairly, in all material respects, the financial position of Veltara.
The company maintained an effective system of internal control over financial reporting as of December 31, 2023, consistent with the requirements of Section 404 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act.
No material weaknesses in internal controls were identified during the annual assessment conducted by management or the external auditors.
Veltara's total stockholders' equity increased to $684.7 million as of December 31, 2023, compared to $597.3 million at the end of the prior fiscal year.
The increase in stockholders' equity was primarily attributable to net income partially offset by dividends paid and share repurchase activity.
Deferred revenue related to multi-year service contracts in the diagnostics segment was $21.8 million at year-end, to be recognized ratably over the remaining contract terms.
The company recognized $9.4 million of previously deferred revenue during fiscal year 2023 as performance obligations were satisfied.
Veltara's defined benefit pension plan had a funded status of 107.3% as of December 31, 2023, indicating the plan's assets exceeded its projected benefit obligation.
The pension plan's strong funded status reflects favorable asset returns of 9.2% during the year, driven primarily by equity allocations.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $950 million, implying growth of approximately 8.6% to 12.1% at the midpoint.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $225 million to $240 million, reflecting continued investment in the therapeutics pipeline.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2024 are expected to be between $55 million and $65 million, reflecting the planned expansion of the company's contract manufacturing capacity.
Management emphasized that guidance assumes no material adverse regulatory decisions affecting the VLT-204 NDA process or the VeltraPanel product family.
The company does not anticipate any significant restructuring charges in fiscal year 2024, as the strategic realignment initiated in 2022 has been fully executed.
Veltara's risk factors section highlighted exposure to foreign currency fluctuations, primarily related to euro-denominated revenues from its European operations.
The company employs a natural hedging strategy supplemented by selective use of forward contracts to mitigate currency risk.

Looking ahead, Dr. Castleford expressed confidence that Veltara's diversified portfolio, robust pipeline, and disciplined financial management will deliver meaningful value to shareholders over the coming years.
The complete audited financial statements, including footnotes and supplemental schedules, are available in the company's Form 10-K filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation is a mid-cap medical technology company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, specializing in point-of-care testing solutions and laboratory automation systems.
The company was founded in 2007 and has since grown to serve clinical laboratories, hospital networks, and physician office practices across 38 countries.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Okafor presented the annual results at the company's investor day held in Charlotte on March 14th.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Beltrán noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a pivotal transition period as Veltara shifted its commercial focus toward higher-margin software-enabled devices.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 12.4 percent increase compared to the prior year's $753 million.
Veltara entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final outstanding senior notes in December 2022.
Gross profit for the year was $389 million, reflecting a gross margin of 45.9 percent, up from 42.1 percent in the prior year.
Operating expenses totaled $271 million, inclusive of $94 million allocated to research and development activities.
The company's flagship analyzer platform, the VX-900, accounted for approximately 34 percent of total product revenue during the fiscal year.
Dr. Okafor credited the VX-900's strong performance to expanded reagent menu offerings and favorable contract renewals with integrated delivery networks.
Veltara's international segment delivered revenue of $312 million, reflecting 36.9 percent of total consolidated revenue.
Growth in the Asia-Pacific region was particularly robust, with revenue increasing 21 percent year-over-year, driven by new distribution agreements in South Korea and Australia.
The Americas segment contributed $428 million in revenue, anchored by strong performance in the United States outpatient testing market.
European revenue declined modestly by 3.2 percent to $107 million, which management attributed to delayed hospital budget approvals in Germany and France.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $118 million, representing an operating margin of 13.9 percent.
This compares favorably to the prior year operating income of $89 million, which reflected margin compression from elevated supply chain costs.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $91 million, or $2.17 per diluted share.
The effective tax rate for the year was 22.4 percent, in line with management's guidance range of 21 to 24 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA, as defined by the company, was $164 million, representing a 19.4 percent adjusted EBITDA margin.
Raymond Beltrán stated that free cash flow generation of $112 million exceeded internal targets by approximately $18 million.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $43 million, with the majority directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Durham, North Carolina facility.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $138 million, reflecting 68 days of inventory on hand, which is within the company's targeted operational range.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 54 days in the prior year, reflecting tightened credit and collections processes.
Veltara repurchased 1.4 million shares during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $38.60, for a total outlay of approximately $54 million.
The board of directors approved an additional $150 million share repurchase authorization in November 2023, extending the program through December 2025.
During the third quarter of fiscal year 2023, Veltara executed a $275 million syndicated term loan facility to fund strategic expansion initiatives.
The company's headcount grew to 4,820 employees at year-end, an increase of 310 from the prior year-end figure of 4,510.
Employee additions were concentrated in the commercial and field service organizations, reflecting Veltara's commitment to deepening customer relationships.
Research and development efforts yielded five new regulatory submissions during the year, including three 510(k) filings with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.
The company received clearance for its VX-1100 benchtop chemistry analyzer in October 2023, which is expected to begin generating revenue in the first quarter of fiscal year 2024.
Dr. Okafor emphasized that the VX-1100 addresses an underserved segment of mid-volume hospital laboratories seeking integrated workflow solutions.
Veltara's software subscription revenues grew 38 percent year-over-year to $62 million, reflecting increased adoption of the LabConnect data management platform.
Management expects LabConnect to contribute meaningfully to recurring revenue streams over the next three to five years.
The company's diagnostics consumables segment, which includes reagents and calibrators, generated $381 million in revenue, representing 45 percent of total consolidated revenue.
Reagent attachment rates on installed VX-900 systems reached 91 percent, up from 87 percent the prior year.
Veltara's strategic partnership with BioNexus Laboratories, announced in June 2023, is expected to accelerate the development of next-generation molecular assays.
Under the terms of the BioNexus agreement, Veltara committed to funding $30 million in collaborative research over a three-year period.
The company also divested its legacy microplate reader product line in August 2023, generating net proceeds of $17 million.
Management indicated that the divestiture aligns with Veltara's strategy to concentrate resources on higher-growth, higher-margin segments.
Veltara's board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share in each of the four quarters of fiscal year 2023.
Total dividends paid to shareholders during the fiscal year amounted to approximately $20 million.
The dividend payout ratio, measured against net income, was approximately 22 percent, consistent with the company's capital allocation philosophy.
Dr. Okafor reiterated that Veltara's capital allocation priority order remains: organic investment, strategic M&A, share repurchases, and dividends.
Veltara ended fiscal year 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $203 million, compared to $174 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The increase in cash reflects strong operating cash flow partially offset by capital expenditures, share repurchases, and dividend payments.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for revenue in the range of $910 million to $935 million.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 20 to 21 percent, reflecting operating leverage and continued software mix shift.
The company anticipates fiscal year 2024 diluted earnings per share in the range of $2.40 to $2.55.
Raymond Beltrán noted that the guidance range accounts for potential macroeconomic headwinds, including foreign currency translation effects.
Veltara's largest customer, a national reference laboratory, represented approximately 8.4 percent of total revenue during fiscal year 2023.
No single customer outside the top account exceeded 4 percent of total annual revenue, reflecting the company's diversified customer base.
The company maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB from Standard & Poor's throughout fiscal year 2023.
Veltara's geographic expansion into Latin America accelerated in the second half of fiscal year 2023 with regulatory approvals obtained in Brazil and Colombia.
The Latin America expansion is expected to contribute between $15 million and $20 million in incremental revenue in fiscal year 2024.
Environmental, social, and governance commitments remained a priority, with Veltara reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 11 percent versus the prior year.
The company published its second annual sustainability report in September 2023, outlining progress against its 2030 emissions reduction targets.
Veltara's audit committee oversaw a smooth transition to a new independent auditor, Hargreaves & Wills LLP, effective for the fiscal year 2023 audit.
The transition followed a competitive request-for-proposal process initiated by the audit committee in early 2023.
Dr. Okafor closed the investor day presentation by reaffirming Veltara's long-term revenue growth target of 10 to 12 percent on a compound annual basis.
She highlighted that the company's installed base of more than 9,200 active VX-series analyzers provides a durable foundation for recurring reagent and software revenue.
Veltara's strong balance sheet position, disciplined cost management, and diversified portfolio of diagnostic solutions are expected to drive sustainable value creation for shareholders.
The Board expressed confidence in the management team's execution capabilities and reiterated its commitment to maintaining transparent and consistent financial communication with investors.

Overall, fiscal year 2023 demonstrated that Veltara Diagnostics Corporation has meaningfully strengthened its competitive positioning and financial profile, setting a constructive tone for the periods ahead.
Copies of the full annual report on Form 10-K, including audited financial statements and footnotes, are available on the company's investor relations website at veltaradiagnostics.com/investors.
Veltrano Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Rotterdam, Netherlands, released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023 on March 15, 2024.
The company, led by Chief Executive Officer Miriam Osterhaus, reported consolidated revenues of €1.84 billion, representing a 9.2% increase over the prior fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Dominic Fassbender noted that gross margins expanded by 140 basis points to reach 38.7%, driven by favorable raw material pricing and improved production efficiency.
Veltrano Industries operates across three primary business segments: Advanced Coatings, Industrial Adhesives, and Specialty Polymers.
The Advanced Coatings segment contributed €712 million in revenue, accounting for approximately 38.7% of total group revenues.
Industrial Adhesives generated €634 million in revenue, reflecting strong demand from the automotive and aerospace sectors.
Specialty Polymers, the company's fastest-growing segment, delivered €494 million in revenue, a 17.4% year-over-year increase.
Operating profit for the full year reached €276 million, compared to €241 million in fiscal year 2022.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at €348 million, yielding an EBITDA margin of 18.9%.
Capital expenditure for the year totaled €142 million, with the majority directed toward expanding production capacity at the Antwerp facility.
The Antwerp expansion project, expected to be completed in the second quarter of 2025, will add approximately 85,000 metric tons of annual polymer output.
Free cash flow for the year was €187 million, reflecting disciplined working capital management and reduced inventory build-up.
Miriam Osterhaus stated that the company's performance in 2023 validated its strategic pivot toward higher-value specialty applications.
Veltrano's headcount grew to 8,340 full-time equivalent employees by December 31, 2023, up from 7,910 at the end of 2022.
The increase in workforce was primarily driven by hiring in research and development, where the company added 215 scientists and engineers.
Research and development expenditure for fiscal 2023 reached €93 million, representing 5.1% of total revenues.
Dominic Fassbender highlighted that the balance sheet remained robust, with cash and cash equivalents standing at €421 million at year-end.
Total assets grew to €2.67 billion, reflecting both organic growth and the integration of acquired entities.
In April 2023, Veltrano completed the acquisition of Holbreck Specialty Resins, a Finnish manufacturer of bio-based adhesive resins, for €215 million.
The Holbreck acquisition added approximately €88 million in annualized revenue and is expected to be earnings-accretive from the second full year of ownership.
Integration costs related to the Holbreck transaction amounted to €17 million and were recorded as one-time charges in the first half of 2023.
Veltrano's effective tax rate for the year was 24.3%, slightly above the prior year's 23.8% due to the geographic mix of taxable income.
Net income attributable to shareholders of the parent company was €171 million, translating to earnings per share of €3.42.
The Board of Directors proposed a final dividend of €1.15 per share, bringing the total dividend for fiscal 2023 to €1.70 per share.
This represents a 13.3% increase over the prior year's total dividend of €1.50 per share, consistent with the company's progressive dividend policy.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares during the year at an average price of €41.30, for a total consideration of approximately €49.6 million.
The share buyback program, authorized in November 2022, had an original ceiling of €75 million and was approximately 66% utilized by year-end.
Return on invested capital improved to 12.8% in fiscal 2023, up from 11.4% in fiscal 2022, reflecting stronger operating performance.
Veltrano's order backlog stood at €683 million as of December 31, 2023, providing approximately 4.5 months of forward revenue visibility.
The company's net promoter score among its top 200 industrial clients rose to 58 in the annual customer satisfaction survey, compared to 51 the prior year.
In the Specialty Polymers segment, a new flame-retardant polymer grade received REACH certification from the European Chemicals Agency in September 2023.
This certification opened the door to an estimated addressable market of €320 million in electronics and construction applications across the European Union.
Chief Operating Officer Petra Vandermolen stated that manufacturing productivity, measured by output per employee, improved by 6.3% year-over-year.
Inventory levels at year-end totaled €219 million, corresponding to approximately 43 days of sales, down from 51 days in the prior year.
The reduction in inventory days was achieved through a company-wide initiative to optimize safety stock levels and improve demand forecasting accuracy.
Veltrano entered fiscal 2024 with no outstanding borrowings under its revolving credit facility, and the facility itself carried a total commitment of €400 million.
As of December 31, 2023, the company carried total financial debt of zero euros, having fully repaid a €150 million term loan in August 2023.
The debt repayment was funded through a combination of operating cash flow and a portion of the proceeds from a bond issuance in early 2023.
Net debt therefore stood at a net cash position of €421 million, providing Veltrano with considerable financial flexibility for future investments.
Miriam Osterhaus expressed confidence in the company's ability to pursue bolt-on acquisitions in the bio-based materials space without straining the balance sheet.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management issued guidance for revenues in the range of €1.95 billion to €2.05 billion.
EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 19.0% to 20.0%, reflecting anticipated operating leverage and continued mix improvement.
The company expects capital expenditure to rise to approximately €175 million in fiscal 2024, primarily to fund the Antwerp expansion and a new pilot plant in Lyon.
The Lyon pilot plant, focused on waterborne coating dispersions, is expected to generate initial commercial revenues in the third quarter of 2025.
Veltrano's sustainability report, published alongside the annual results, documented a 12% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions versus 2022.
The company achieved its 2023 emissions reduction target one quarter ahead of schedule, according to Chief Sustainability Officer Anke Brügel.
Water consumption per metric ton of output fell by 8.4% year-over-year, aided by closed-loop cooling systems installed at three production sites.
The company's lost-time injury rate improved to 0.41 per 100,000 hours worked, continuing a multi-year trend of workplace safety improvement.
Veltrano Industries received the European Chemical Industry Council's Safety Excellence Award for the third consecutive year in November 2023.
The Advanced Coatings segment launched five new product lines during fiscal 2023, targeting the marine, wind energy, and infrastructure protection markets.
Sales from products introduced within the last three years accounted for 23% of total group revenues in fiscal 2023, demonstrating the impact of innovation investment.
Geographic revenue breakdown showed that Europe accounted for 54% of revenues, Asia-Pacific for 28%, and the Americas for the remaining 18%.
Asia-Pacific revenue growth of 21.6% year-over-year was particularly notable, driven by expansion in South Korea, India, and Vietnam.
Veltrano opened a new regional headquarters in Singapore during the second quarter of 2023 to better serve the growing Asian customer base.
The Board of Directors confirmed that all members of the Audit and Risk Committee hold independent non-executive director status in compliance with Dutch corporate governance guidelines.
Dominic Fassbender concluded the financial briefing by reiterating management's confidence in sustaining long-term value creation for shareholders.

Throughout fiscal 2023, Veltrano maintained an active debt financing program, drawing €230 million under a new syndicated credit facility arranged in the third quarter to support strategic growth initiatives.
Meridian Optical Technologies, Inc. reported its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023, demonstrating resilience across its core imaging and photonics divisions.
The company, headquartered in Portland, Oregon, designs and manufactures advanced optical systems for industrial, medical, and defense applications.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Lena Hartwick opened the earnings call by noting that fiscal 2023 was a transformative year for the organization.
Total consolidated revenue reached $1.47 billion, representing a 9.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The medical imaging segment posted the strongest growth, with revenue climbing 14.2% year-over-year to $612 million.
Defense optics contributed $389 million in revenue, largely driven by a multi-year contract with the U.S. Department of Defense awarded in Q2.
Industrial systems revenue came in at $469 million, reflecting modest 3.1% growth amid softness in global manufacturing activity.
Gross profit for the full year was $541 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 36.8%.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delray highlighted that gross margins improved by 120 basis points relative to fiscal 2022.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $298 million, up 7.4% from the prior year.
Research and development spending increased to $124 million, representing 8.4% of total revenue, underscoring the company's commitment to innovation.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $174 million, reflecting investments in global sales infrastructure and brand development.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $243 million, compared to $207 million in fiscal 2022, a 17.4% improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $181 million, or $4.23 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to fiscal 2022 net income of $152 million, or $3.56 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $312 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 21.2%.
The company generated free cash flow of $198 million during fiscal 2023, up from $163 million the prior year.
Capital expenditures totaled $87 million, focused primarily on expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's facility in Hillsboro, Oregon.
Dr. Hartwick credited the strong cash generation to disciplined working capital management and operational efficiency initiatives launched in early 2022.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 43 days from 51 days in the prior year, reflecting better collections processes.
Inventory turnover improved to 5.2 times annually, compared to 4.7 times in fiscal 2022.
The company's balance sheet remained robust at fiscal year-end, with cash and cash equivalents of $274 million.
Total assets stood at $2.1 billion, while total liabilities were $780 million.
Shareholders' equity increased to $1.32 billion at December 31, 2023, up from $1.17 billion at the end of fiscal 2022.
Long-term debt consisted solely of a $150 million term loan originated in 2019, with no new borrowings undertaken throughout fiscal 2023.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, reflecting confidence in the company's sustained cash generation.
Meridian Optical Technologies carried no revolving credit facility balance as of December 31, 2023, and its debt-to-equity ratio stood at a conservative 0.11.
The company expanded its global workforce to approximately 6,800 full-time employees by the end of fiscal 2023.
Headcount growth was concentrated in the medical imaging and R&D functions, where 420 new positions were added.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Voss stated that hiring remained selective and aligned with strategic growth priorities.
The company launched three new product families during fiscal 2023, including the ProScope X9 series for endoscopic imaging.
The LumaDefense 400 platform, designed for long-range surveillance optics, received full regulatory clearance in Q3 2023.
The IndustrialEdge 7 system, targeting precision manufacturing quality control, began shipping to customers in Q4 2023.
Customer satisfaction scores across all three new product lines exceeded internal benchmarks, averaging 4.6 out of 5.0.
International revenue accounted for 38% of total sales in fiscal 2023, up from 33% in the prior year.
Asia-Pacific was the fastest-growing international region, with revenue rising 21% year-over-year to $213 million.
European sales increased 8% to $145 million, supported by new distribution partnerships in Germany and France.
The company established a regional sales office in Singapore during Q2 2023 to support Asia-Pacific expansion.
Supply chain disruptions, which had significantly impacted operations in 2021 and 2022, were largely resolved by mid-2023.
Component lead times normalized to an average of 8 weeks by the end of fiscal 2023, down from 22 weeks at the peak of disruption.
Meridian Optical Technologies maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout fiscal 2023.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock at an average price of $78.40 per share during fiscal 2023.
Total share repurchase activity amounted to approximately $94 million, funded through operating cash flows.
The board authorized an additional $200 million share repurchase program in November 2023, to be executed over the next 24 months.
Mr. Delray confirmed that the company intends to fund future repurchases through a combination of operating cash flows and available credit facilities.

In January 2024, Meridian Optical Technologies drew $175 million on a newly arranged revolving credit facility to support its capital allocation strategy, a facility that was put in place during the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management issued guidance for revenue in the range of $1.58 billion to $1.62 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA margin is expected to expand modestly to between 21.5% and 22.0% in fiscal 2024.
Dr. Hartwick expressed particular optimism about the medical imaging segment's pipeline, which includes two pending regulatory submissions.
The company also announced a strategic partnership with BioSight Diagnostics to co-develop next-generation retinal imaging platforms.
Meridian Optical Technologies expects to incur approximately $15 million in one-time integration costs related to the BioSight collaboration in fiscal 2024.
Management concluded the earnings call by reaffirming its long-term target of achieving $2 billion in annual revenue by fiscal 2026.
Veltora Systems Corporation, a leading provider of industrial automation software and hardware solutions, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company, headquartered in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, has operated in the advanced manufacturing technology sector for over two decades.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the letter to shareholders by expressing strong confidence in Veltora's long-term strategic position.
The company reported total consolidated revenues of $847 million for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, representing a 12% increase over the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Randall Osei noted that gross margins improved to 41.3% from 38.7% in 2022, driven primarily by favorable product mix shifts.
Operating expenses totaled $298 million, inclusive of research and development spending of $74 million and selling, general, and administrative costs of $224 million.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltora Systems carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, reflecting the company's sustained commitment to financial discipline.
The company's net income attributable to common shareholders reached $112 million, or $2.84 per diluted share, compared to $89 million, or $2.26 per diluted share, in 2022.
Return on equity improved to 18.4% in fiscal 2023, up from 14.9% the previous year, marking the third consecutive year of improvement.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203 million, providing a robust liquidity buffer for near-term operational and strategic needs.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, maintaining the dividend program that was initiated in fiscal 2019.
During the year, Veltora repurchased approximately 1.4 million shares of common stock under its authorized buyback program at an average price of $48.60 per share.
Total capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 amounted to $61 million, allocated primarily toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Ohio and Texas facilities.
Margaret Holloway emphasized that the company's workforce grew to approximately 5,200 employees globally by the close of fiscal 2023.
The North American segment remained the largest revenue contributor, generating $512 million, or approximately 60% of total revenues.
International revenues grew by 18% year over year, reaching $335 million, with particularly strong performance in European and Southeast Asian markets.
The company's flagship product line, the VeltroCore automation suite, accounted for $389 million in revenues, representing 46% of total consolidated sales.
New product introductions in the connected sensors category contributed an incremental $43 million in revenue during the fiscal year.
Customer retention rates for enterprise accounts remained above 91% throughout fiscal 2023, underscoring the stickiness of Veltora's software platform.
The company signed 47 new enterprise contracts valued at over $1 million each, representing a record high for annual large-deal bookings.
Randall Osei highlighted that the company's deferred revenue balance grew to $134 million, indicating a healthy pipeline of future recognized revenues.
Inventory levels were carefully managed throughout the year, ending at $88 million, a modest reduction from the $94 million reported at the end of 2022.
The allowance for doubtful accounts remained conservative at $6.2 million, consistent with the company's disciplined credit management practices.
Veltora's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.1%, slightly below the prior year's rate of 23.4%, benefiting from expanded R&D tax credits.
The company's goodwill and intangible asset balances totaled $317 million following the integration of Cortagen Analytics, which was acquired in late 2022.
Pension and post-retirement benefit obligations were fully funded at year-end, with plan assets exceeding projected benefit obligations by $14 million.
In March 2023, Veltora entered into a $350 million syndicated term loan facility with a consortium of seven commercial banks, maturing in 2028.
The proceeds from this credit arrangement were directed toward funding the company's accelerated capital investment program and strategic acquisition pipeline.
Amortization of acquired intangible assets reduced reported earnings by approximately $18 million on a pre-tax basis during fiscal 2023.
The company's adjusted EBITDA, excluding non-cash charges and one-time items, reached $198 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 23.4%.
Margaret Holloway discussed plans to expand the company's presence in the Asia-Pacific region through a combination of organic growth and targeted partnerships.
Operating cash flow of $171 million provided ample coverage of capital expenditures, resulting in free cash flow of $110 million for the year.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its independent audit without issuing any material weaknesses or significant deficiencies.
Veltora's stock price appreciated by approximately 27% over the course of fiscal 2023, outperforming the broader industrial technology sector index by 11 percentage points.
The company maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from S&P Global Ratings and Baa1 from Moody's throughout the fiscal year.
Randall Osei indicated that the company's weighted average cost of capital was estimated at 8.3%, which was used as the primary discount rate for impairment testing.
Segment-level disclosures indicated that the Software Solutions segment generated operating income of $143 million, while the Hardware Systems segment contributed $61 million.
Corporate and unallocated expenses of $46 million partially offset the combined segment operating income, resulting in consolidated operating income of $158 million.
The company recognized a $7.4 million gain on the sale of a non-core product line related to legacy pneumatic control systems in the second quarter.
Veltora disclosed no material contingent liabilities other than routine legal proceedings typical of its industry, none of which were expected to result in significant losses.
The company's annual meeting of shareholders is scheduled for April 18, 2024, in Milwaukee, Wisconsin.
At that meeting, shareholders will be asked to vote on the re-election of three board members and ratification of the independent auditor for fiscal 2024.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Nandakumar outlined operational efficiency initiatives expected to generate $30 million in annualized cost savings beginning in fiscal 2025.
These savings are anticipated to be realized through supply chain consolidation, automation of internal processes, and reduction of redundant facility footprints.
Revenue guidance for fiscal 2024 was established at a range of $910 million to $940 million, reflecting continued organic growth momentum.
Management projected adjusted EBITDA margin expansion of approximately 50 to 80 basis points in fiscal 2024 relative to the fiscal 2023 baseline.
The company plans to invest between $65 million and $75 million in capital expenditures during fiscal 2024, primarily targeting digital infrastructure upgrades.
Veltora also announced a 10% increase in its quarterly dividend, raising the per-share amount to $0.242 effective with the first-quarter 2024 payment.
The board authorized an additional $100 million share repurchase program, supplementing the approximately $32 million remaining under the prior authorization.
Priya Nandakumar noted that headcount additions in the engineering and product development functions are expected to bring total global employment to approximately 5,500 by year-end 2024.
The company reiterated its long-term financial targets of achieving $1.2 billion in annual revenues and a 26% adjusted EBITDA margin by fiscal 2027.
Veltora's enterprise risk management framework was updated in 2023 to incorporate cybersecurity resilience metrics and third-party supply chain risk assessments.
The company received two industry awards during 2023, including the Manufacturing Technology Innovation Award from the Industrial Automation Society.
Veltora's environmental, social, and governance report, published alongside this annual report, highlighted a 14% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions.
The company committed to achieving carbon neutrality in its direct operations by 2035, consistent with its participation in the Science Based Targets initiative.
Margaret Holloway closed the shareholder letter by affirming Veltora's dedication to creating sustainable long-term value for all stakeholders.
The financial statements and accompanying notes contained herein have been prepared in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles in the United States.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those anticipated.
Veltran Biosystems, Inc. is a mid-cap biotechnology and precision diagnostics company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
The company was founded in 2009 by Dr. Mara Okonkwo and has since grown to serve clinical laboratories across 34 countries.
Veltran's core product lines include the PrecisionPath sequencing platform, the ClearMark immunoassay panel, and a suite of companion diagnostic software tools.
This annual report covers the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, and has been prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
Chief Executive Officer Randall Featherstone opened the year with a strategic priority on margin expansion and international market penetration.
Chief Financial Officer Ingrid Vasquez-Holm oversaw a comprehensive review of capital allocation practices beginning in Q1 2023.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the $426.6 million reported in fiscal year 2022.
North American revenue contributed $291.4 million, while international markets accounted for the remaining $195.9 million.
The ClearMark immunoassay panel was the highest-grossing product segment, generating $184.7 million in net revenue during the year.
PrecisionPath platform sales totaled $162.5 million, bolstered by the rollout of the PrecisionPath Pro upgrade launched in March 2023.
Companion diagnostic software licensing fees contributed $140.1 million, growing 22.8% year-over-year.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $298.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 61.3%.
This represents a 210-basis-point improvement in gross margin relative to the prior fiscal year.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $201.4 million for the year.
Research and development expenditures accounted for $89.2 million, or approximately 18.3% of total revenue.
Sales and marketing expenses were $67.5 million, reflecting investments in direct sales force expansion in Europe and Southeast Asia.
General and administrative costs came in at $44.7 million, broadly in line with management's guidance range of $42–$47 million.
Operating income for the year was $97.2 million, compared to $71.8 million in fiscal year 2022, representing a 35.4% improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $74.3 million, or $1.84 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $52.1 million, or $1.31 per diluted share, in the prior year.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure, reached $128.5 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 26.4%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $213.8 million, up from $178.2 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company generated $104.6 million in operating cash flow during the year, underscoring the underlying strength of Veltran's business model.
Capital expenditures for the period totaled $31.2 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the Durham facility.
Veltran concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired the final tranche of its 2019 term loan in August 2023.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $75 million in October 2023, of which $22.4 million had been utilized by year-end.
Headcount grew to 2,847 full-time employees globally as of December 31, 2023, up from 2,614 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company added 126 employees in the EMEA region alone, consistent with its international expansion strategy.
Veltran's attrition rate improved to 8.4% in 2023, down from 11.2% in 2022, reflecting investments in employee engagement programs.
The company was recognized on two independent workplace excellence lists during the year, including a ranking in Biotech Employer Quarterly's Top 50.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $58.3 million, representing approximately 43 days of supply based on cost of goods sold.
Accounts receivable totaled $79.6 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 59 days.
Bad debt expense remained modest at $1.4 million, reflecting the predominantly institutional and government customer base.
Deferred revenue, primarily from multi-year software licensing agreements, totaled $34.7 million as of December 31, 2023.
The company renewed or expanded contracts with 91% of its top-100 customers during the fiscal year.
International distribution partnerships were expanded through agreements with three new regional distributors in Latin America.
Regulatory approvals obtained during the year included CE marking for the ClearMark Oncology Panel in the European Union.
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration granted 510(k) clearance for the PrecisionPath Pro consumables kit in November 2023.
Veltran's intellectual property portfolio grew to 214 granted patents and 87 pending applications as of year-end.
The company recorded a $6.1 million tax benefit related to R&D tax credits recognized under the Inflation Reduction Act provisions.
Environmental, social, and governance commitments remained a focal point, with Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions reduced by 11% year-over-year.
The company achieved its goal of sourcing 40% of electricity from renewable sources, ahead of its original 2025 target.
Veltran's audit committee engaged PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as the independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the year.
Veltran entered into a $350 million syndicated revolving credit facility in September 2023, replacing its previously undrawn predecessor arrangement.
Management's forward guidance for fiscal year 2024 projects total revenue in the range of $530–$550 million.
This guidance assumes continued momentum in the ClearMark and PrecisionPath segments, partially offset by anticipated software licensing seasonality.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is expected to fall in the range of $138–$148 million.
The company plans to increase R&D investment to approximately 19–20% of projected revenue in 2024 to support next-generation platform development.
A new manufacturing line dedicated to high-throughput sequencing consumables is expected to be operational by Q3 2024.
Veltran's board declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share in December 2023, marking the company's first dividend in its history.
Total dividends declared in fiscal year 2023 amounted to $0.12 per share, with payment made on January 15, 2024.
The company repurchased 618,000 shares at an average price of $36.21 per share during the fourth quarter of 2023.
Market capitalization at December 31, 2023, was approximately $1.54 billion based on 40.3 million diluted shares outstanding.
The closing stock price on December 29, 2023, was $38.22 per share, representing a 27.4% appreciation over the January 3, 2023, opening price.
Analyst consensus as compiled by Bloomberg Intelligence rated Veltran shares as Overweight with a median price target of $44.00.
Dr. Okonkwo, in her capacity as Executive Chairwoman, expressed confidence in Veltran's pipeline and strategic direction at the December investor day.
CFO Vasquez-Holm noted that the company's balance sheet positions Veltran favorably for both organic investment and selective bolt-on acquisitions.
The risk factors section of this report identifies competitive pricing pressure and regulatory delays as the primary near-term operational risks.
Veltran also highlights foreign exchange exposure, particularly to the euro and British pound, as a meaningful factor influencing international revenue.
Management remains committed to transparent communication with shareholders and the broader investment community throughout fiscal year 2024.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Ardenvale Biosystems, Inc. reported its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023, reflecting continued momentum across its core diagnostics and genomic-sequencing segments.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Calloway opened the earnings call by expressing confidence in the company's long-term strategic positioning.
Ardenvale Biosystems operates in the high-growth life sciences instrumentation sector, competing with established players in molecular diagnostics.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 14% increase over the prior fiscal year.
The genomic-sequencing division contributed $412 million to total revenue, making it the largest single revenue segment for the first time.
The diagnostics segment generated $310 million in revenue, while the remaining $125 million came from service and licensing agreements.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $498 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 58.8%, up 120 basis points year-over-year.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $321 million, driven largely by increased investment in research and development.
Research and development expenditure alone accounted for $178 million, or roughly 21% of total revenue.
Chief Financial Officer Thomas Engel noted that operating income of $177 million represented a 19% increase compared to fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $134 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.87.
The company generated $198 million in operating cash flow, comfortably exceeding its capital expenditure requirements of $74 million.
Ardenvale Biosystems exited fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, with the balance sheet showing zero borrowings outstanding.
The strong cash position enabled the board to authorize a $150 million share repurchase program effective January 2024.
Inventories at year-end stood at $143 million, up from $118 million in the prior year, reflecting pre-launch stock builds for new product lines.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days, down from 53 days in fiscal year 2022, indicating stronger collections discipline.
The company's current ratio improved to 3.1x, providing ample liquidity to fund near-term operational and strategic initiatives.
Ardenvale Biosystems employed 4,820 full-time equivalent employees as of December 31, 2023, an increase of 310 from the prior year-end.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in the commercial and clinical affairs teams supporting the genomic-sequencing rollout.
Dr. Calloway highlighted the successful launch of the ArdenSeq Pro platform in the third quarter of fiscal year 2023 as a defining milestone.
ArdenSeq Pro achieved $58 million in revenue within its first two quarters of commercial availability, exceeding internal launch-year targets.
The platform's adoption rate among academic medical centers reached 34% within six months of general availability, ahead of comparable prior launches.
During fiscal year 2023, Ardenvale Biosystems secured a $320 million syndicated revolving credit facility from a consortium of six international banks.
The new credit facility carries a five-year term with an interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points and includes customary financial covenants.
Management emphasized that the credit facility was structured as a precautionary liquidity buffer rather than for immediate borrowing needs.
International revenue grew to $294 million, representing 34.7% of total revenue, up from 29.1% in fiscal year 2022.
The Europe, Middle East, and Africa region was the fastest-growing international market, contributing $162 million in revenue.
Asia-Pacific revenue reached $89 million, with particular strength noted in Japan, South Korea, and Singapore.
The Latin America region, while smaller at $43 million, posted year-over-year growth of 27%, outpacing all other geographic segments.
Ardenvale Biosystems entered into a strategic distribution agreement with Hirano Scientific in Japan during the second quarter.
The Hirano partnership is expected to expand Ardenvale's reach into hospital networks that were previously underserved by direct sales efforts.
On the cost side, supply chain optimization initiatives delivered $22 million in annualized savings compared to the prior fiscal year baseline.
The company completed the consolidation of three U.S. manufacturing sites into a single center-of-excellence facility in Raleigh, North Carolina.
This manufacturing consolidation is projected to yield additional cost savings of $15 million annually beginning in fiscal year 2024.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced materially during the year, with carbon intensity per unit of production declining 9%.
Ardenvale Biosystems achieved a CDP Climate Score of B, an improvement from its prior year score of C+.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, marking the eighth consecutive year of dividend increases.
Total dividends paid to shareholders during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $33.6 million.
The audit committee completed its review of internal controls and found no material weaknesses in the company's financial reporting framework.
Ardenvale Biosystems maintains a customer concentration risk with its top five customers accounting for 22% of total revenue.
No single customer accounted for more than 8% of total revenue, limiting dependency on any individual account.
Backlog at year-end reached a record $214 million, compared with $167 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The record backlog is primarily attributable to multi-year instrument service contracts and reagent supply agreements.
Thomas Engel confirmed that deferred revenue increased by $18 million year-over-year, reflecting expanded multi-year subscription arrangements.
The company recorded a $12 million restructuring charge in the fourth quarter related to workforce rationalization in the legacy diagnostics unit.
Excluding this charge and a $7 million litigation settlement expense, adjusted operating income was $196 million.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $231 million, implying an adjusted EBITDA margin of 27.3%.
Capital expenditures of $74 million were directed primarily toward laboratory automation upgrades and the new Raleigh manufacturing facility.
Intangible assets net of amortization stood at $389 million, largely representing acquired technology from the 2021 purchase of Deltapath Genomics.
Goodwill remained unchanged at $512 million following the annual impairment test, which indicated no impairment to the carrying value.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 18.4%, benefiting from R&D tax credits and favorable geographic profit mix.
Deferred tax assets, net of valuation allowances, totaled $67 million as of December 31, 2023.
Ardenvale Biosystems issued full-year fiscal 2024 guidance calling for revenue in the range of $940 million to $970 million.
Adjusted EPS guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $3.10 to $3.30 per diluted share, implying 8% to 15% growth over fiscal year 2023.
The guidance incorporates anticipated contributions from two pipeline products expected to receive regulatory clearance in mid-2024.
Dr. Calloway stated that the ArdenDx Rapid Respiratory Panel remains on track for FDA 510(k) clearance in the second quarter of fiscal year 2024.
Management also disclosed that the ArdenFlow cytometry system is expected to launch commercially in the third quarter of fiscal year 2024.
The company reaffirmed its long-term financial model targeting revenue of $1.2 billion and adjusted EBITDA margins of 30% by fiscal year 2026.
Ardenvale Biosystems' share price appreciated 38% during calendar year 2023, outperforming the S&P Healthcare Equipment Index by 21 percentage points.
Institutional ownership as of the proxy record date stood at 78.4% of shares outstanding, with Vanguard and BlackRock as the two largest holders.
The company's market capitalization reached approximately $6.1 billion as of the close of fiscal year 2023.
Ardenvale Biosystems remains committed to disciplined capital allocation, balancing organic investment, potential acquisitions, and shareholder returns.
Management expressed gratitude to employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued trust and partnership throughout fiscal year 2023.
The full annual report on Form 10-K, including audited financial statements, is available on the Investor Relations section of the company's website.
Veltrano Systems Corporation, a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure provider headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its full-year fiscal 2023 annual report on February 14, 2024.

Chief Executive Officer Margaux Pelletier opened the letter to shareholders with a statement of cautious optimism, noting that the company had navigated a challenging macroeconomic environment with resilience.
Veltrano Systems reported total revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year's $1.64 billion.
Cloud subscription revenues, which now constitute the largest single revenue segment, grew 24.7% year-over-year to reach $820 million.
Professional services revenues declined modestly by 3.1%, coming in at $312 million compared to $322 million in fiscal 2022.
Legacy on-premise licensing revenue continued its expected secular decline, falling 8.4% to $708 million for the year.
Gross profit for fiscal 2023 reached $1.02 billion, representing a gross margin of 55.4%, up from 52.1% in the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Desmond Auerbach attributed the margin expansion primarily to the favorable revenue mix shift toward higher-margin cloud subscription products.
Operating expenses totaled $784 million, with research and development representing the single largest component at $298 million.
Veltrano Systems entered fiscal 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final outstanding bond issuance in December 2022.
Sales and marketing expenditures reached $287 million, reflecting the company's continued investment in expanding its enterprise customer base.
General and administrative costs of $199 million were largely in line with prior year levels, growing only 1.2% despite inflationary pressures.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $238 million, yielding an operating margin of 12.9%, a meaningful improvement from the 9.7% recorded in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to Veltrano Systems shareholders was $181 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.47.
The company's effective tax rate for the period was 23.8%, slightly above the prior year's 22.1%, reflecting changes in the geographic distribution of taxable income.
Adjusted EBITDA, which the company uses as a supplemental performance measure, came in at $341 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 18.5%.
Cash flow from operations was a robust $319 million, and free cash flow, after capital expenditures of $87 million, totaled $232 million.
The company ended fiscal 2023 with a cash and cash equivalents balance of $614 million, up from $487 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Veltrano Systems repurchased 3.2 million shares of its common stock during fiscal 2023, returning approximately $124 million to shareholders under its authorized buyback program.
During the third quarter of fiscal 2023, Veltrano Systems secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of six lenders to support strategic growth initiatives.
The board of directors approved an increase in the quarterly dividend from $0.18 to $0.22 per share, effective with the payment made in October 2023.
Total headcount at fiscal year-end stood at 8,412 full-time employees, an increase of 647 from the prior year-end figure.
The company's annual employee attrition rate declined to 11.2% in fiscal 2023, compared to 14.7% in fiscal 2022, reflecting improved retention programs.
Veltrano Systems completed the acquisition of Caldex Analytics, a data observability startup, in April 2023 for a total consideration of $215 million in cash.
The Caldex acquisition added approximately 180 employees and expanded Veltrano's artificial intelligence and machine learning capabilities within its cloud platform.
Goodwill and intangible assets recorded in connection with the Caldex transaction totaled $163 million and $41 million, respectively, on the consolidated balance sheet.
Chief Product Officer Simone Nakagawa highlighted the successful launch of VeltraOS 7.0, the company's flagship cloud operating environment, in the second quarter.
VeltraOS 7.0 achieved over 2,100 enterprise deployments within its first six months of general availability, exceeding internal projections by approximately 18%.
Customer retention rates for the cloud subscription segment improved to 94.6% on a net revenue retention basis, up from 91.3% in fiscal 2022.
The company's international revenues grew 17.8% to reach $512 million, now representing 27.8% of total revenues compared to 26.6% in fiscal 2022.
Revenue from the Europe, Middle East, and Africa region grew particularly strongly at 21.4%, driven by new enterprise wins in Germany, France, and the Netherlands.
Asia-Pacific revenues grew 14.2% to $187 million, with Japan and Australia representing the two largest contributors within the region.
Veltrano Systems was recognized in the Gartner Magic Quadrant for Cloud Infrastructure Platforms as a Visionary for the second consecutive year.
The company expanded its partner ecosystem by adding 214 new certified implementation partners globally during fiscal 2023, bringing the total to over 1,600.
Infrastructure and hosting costs, which represent the largest component of cost of revenues, decreased as a percentage of cloud revenue from 31.4% to 27.9%.
This improvement was driven by renegotiated data center contracts and the continued migration of workloads to the company's proprietary infrastructure stack.
Capital expenditures of $87 million were allocated primarily to data center expansion in North America and Europe, with a smaller portion directed toward corporate real estate improvements.
Veltrano Systems maintained a current ratio of 2.4 and a quick ratio of 2.1 as of fiscal year-end, indicating strong short-term liquidity.
The company's days sales outstanding metric improved to 48 days from 54 days in the prior year, reflecting more effective accounts receivable management.
Total deferred revenue on the balance sheet grew to $431 million, up 22.4% from $352 million the prior year, signaling strong future recognized revenue.
Ms. Pelletier noted that the robust deferred revenue balance provides high visibility into fiscal 2024 revenue performance.
The company's return on equity for fiscal 2023 was 17.3%, an improvement over the 13.8% recorded in fiscal 2022.
Return on invested capital increased to 14.1% from 10.9%, driven by the combination of improved profitability and disciplined capital deployment.
Veltrano Systems initiated a strategic review of its legacy on-premise product lines in the fourth quarter, with results expected to be shared in mid-2024.
The strategic review is being conducted with the assistance of external advisors and encompasses potential divestitures, wind-downs, or transition-to-cloud migrations.
Mr. Auerbach stated that any transaction resulting from the strategic review would be evaluated against strict financial return hurdles before proceeding.

The company's weighted-average shares outstanding on a diluted basis declined to 73.3 million from 74.8 million, reflecting the net effect of buybacks partially offset by equity compensation issuances.
Veltrano Systems issued 1.4 million shares in connection with employee equity compensation plans during fiscal 2023.
The company maintained compliance with all financial covenants throughout fiscal 2023 and reported no material weaknesses in its internal controls over financial reporting.
An unqualified audit opinion was issued by Grant Meridian LLP, the company's independent registered public accounting firm, on January 31, 2024.
For fiscal 2024, management provided initial revenue guidance in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.10 billion, implying year-over-year growth of approximately 11% to 14%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 19.5% to 20.5%, reflecting expected operating leverage as cloud revenues scale.
Management cautioned that macroeconomic uncertainty, particularly with respect to enterprise IT spending in Europe, represents the most significant downside risk to the fiscal 2024 outlook.
The company also flagged potential foreign currency headwinds given the strengthening U.S. dollar relative to the euro and Japanese yen.
Veltrano Systems' stock closed at $39.14 per share on the last trading day of fiscal 2023, implying a market capitalization of approximately $2.87 billion.
This represents a total shareholder return of 28.4% for fiscal 2023, outperforming the S&P Mid-Cap 400 Software Index by approximately 11 percentage points.
The board of directors, which consists of nine members including three women and two individuals from underrepresented groups, oversaw all major strategic decisions during the year.
Veltrano Systems published its inaugural Environmental, Social, and Governance report in conjunction with the annual report, outlining targets for carbon neutrality by 2035.
The ESG report disclosed a 9.3% reduction in absolute Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to the fiscal 2022 baseline.
Ms. Pelletier concluded her shareholder letter by expressing confidence in the company's strategic direction, the strength of its product portfolio, and the dedication of its global workforce.
The full annual report on Form 10-K, including detailed financial statements and footnotes, is available on the company's investor relations website and through the SEC's EDGAR filing system.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap specialty pharmaceutical company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
The company focuses on the development and commercialization of targeted therapies for rare autoimmune disorders.
Veltara was founded in 2009 by Dr. Miriam Hoessler and has since grown to become a recognized name in orphan drug development.
This annual report covers fiscal year 2023, which ended on December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Randolph Casteel expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction during the shareholder address.
Chief Financial Officer Ms. Priya Ananthan presented the consolidated financial statements at the annual general meeting.
Total net revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase over fiscal year 2022.
Product revenue contributed $791.6 million, while licensing and royalty income accounted for the remaining $55.7 million.
The flagship product, Velixar, generated $512.4 million in net sales during the fiscal year.
Velixar's market penetration in the United States grew to approximately 38% of the addressable patient population.
The second commercial product, Trenovane, posted net sales of $279.2 million, up 21.6% year-over-year.
Gross margin for the full year was 74.3%, reflecting continued manufacturing efficiencies at the company's Durham facility.
Research and development expenses totaled $198.7 million, representing 23.5% of total net revenue.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $163.4 million for fiscal year 2023.
Operating income reached $148.9 million, compared to $112.3 million in the prior fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $119.6 million, or $2.14 per diluted share.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 19.8%, slightly below management's guidance of 21%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $334.8 million, up from $287.1 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company generated $176.2 million in operating cash flow during fiscal year 2023.
Capital expenditures for the year were $42.5 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity.
Veltara repurchased 2.1 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $38.60 per share.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share, payable in March 2024.
Veltara's total headcount reached 2,847 employees as of December 31, 2023, up from 2,614 at the end of the prior year.
The expansion of the clinical development team added 143 new positions over the course of the fiscal year.
The company's pipeline includes five clinical-stage candidates across three therapeutic areas.
VLT-204, a next-generation interleukin inhibitor, advanced into Phase 3 trials in the second quarter of 2023.
Phase 2 data for VLT-311 demonstrated a statistically significant reduction in disease flare frequency versus placebo.
VLT-311 is currently being evaluated in a 600-patient global Phase 3 program expected to complete enrollment by mid-2024.
Management initiated a strategic review of the dermatology pipeline in the third quarter of 2023.
The company entered into a collaboration agreement with Halden Pharma AG in September 2023 for co-development of VLT-409.
Under the Halden agreement, Veltara received an upfront payment of $75 million and is eligible for up to $340 million in milestones.
Veltara's long-term debt balance as of December 31, 2023, was zero, as the company fully retired its senior secured notes during the year.
Management emphasized that the company exited fiscal year 2023 in a completely unlevered position, carrying no financial debt obligations.
The company's debt-free status was cited by Dr. Casteel as a key competitive advantage entering the new fiscal year.
Moody's affirmed Veltara's investment-grade credit rating of Baa2 in November 2023, citing strong operating cash flow.
The company maintained a revolving credit facility of $150 million that remained undrawn throughout fiscal year 2023.
Veltara's balance sheet strength gives management flexibility to pursue bolt-on acquisitions without near-term financing constraints.
International revenues grew 18.9% year-over-year, reaching $214.7 million for fiscal year 2023.
European sales of Velixar were approved in six additional EU member states during the fiscal year.
The company is pursuing regulatory approval for Trenovane in Japan, with a submission to the PMDA expected in early 2024.
Supply chain disruptions in the first quarter resulted in a temporary shortage of active pharmaceutical ingredient for Trenovane.
The shortage was resolved by the end of the second quarter following diversification of the API supplier base.
In October 2023, Veltara's board approved a $500 million term loan facility to finance a potential transformative acquisition, which was subsequently drawn in full before year-end.
The company's patent portfolio consists of 87 issued patents and 43 pending applications across 19 jurisdictions.
Veltara's primary composition-of-matter patents for Velixar do not expire until 2037, providing long-dated revenue visibility.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 guidance of net revenue between $920 million and $960 million.
Diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set in the range of $2.30 to $2.50.
The company anticipates gross margin improvement of approximately 100 basis points in fiscal year 2024 due to manufacturing scale efficiencies.
Dr. Ananthan noted that incremental savings from the lean manufacturing initiative are expected to benefit cost of goods sold beginning in the second quarter of 2024.
Veltara's stock closed at $41.22 on December 31, 2023, representing a 22.4% total return for shareholders during fiscal year 2023.
The S&P Biotechnology Index returned approximately 9.1% over the same period, indicating significant outperformance by Veltara.
The audit committee engaged Ernst & Young LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year.
The company maintained compliance with all covenants under its existing credit arrangements throughout fiscal year 2023.
Veltara's environmental, social, and governance report was published separately and details progress toward its 2030 sustainability commitments.
The company reduced Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 11% in fiscal year 2023 relative to the 2021 baseline.
Employee voluntary attrition declined to 8.3% in fiscal year 2023, the lowest rate recorded in the company's history.
The compensation committee approved a new long-term incentive plan for senior executives tied to three-year total shareholder return metrics.
Veltara looks forward to continued execution of its strategic plan in fiscal year 2024 and beyond, with the goal of delivering sustainable value for patients and shareholders alike.
